title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: nlmixr2lib model library nlmixr2. ships curated pharmacometric models (PK, PD, endogenous, therapeutic-area-specific) users can load name combine modify.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: inst/modeldb/ — every distributed model, organized category (specificDrugs/, endogenous/, pharmacokinetics/, pharmacodynamics/, therapeuticArea/). function name inside .R file must match filename. R/modeldb.R — registry-building machinery (buildModelDb(), readModelDb(), modellib()). vignettes/ — validation vignettes, one per published model. vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd — canonical naming conventions document.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: Repo-scoped skills live .claude/skills/: extract-literature-model — guided workflow extracting pharmacometric model scientific source (journal article, supplement, poster, regulatory document) package. Use user provides paper asks add model. See .claude/skills/extract-literature-model/SKILL.md. skill’s references/ folder contains templates standards enforces. authoritative covariate-column register inst/references/covariate-columns.md — consult introducing new covariate column. register installed package checkModelConventions() can parse runtime; update file (R code) ratifying new canonical covariate.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: full details see vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd .claude/skills/extract-literature-model/references/naming-conventions.md. Compartments: depot, central, peripheral1, peripheral2, effect. Observation: Cc. PK parameters (log-scale): lka, lcl, lvc, lvp, lq, lfdepot; derived ka, cl, vc, vp, q, kel, k12, etc. IIV: eta + transformed name (etalcl, etalvc). Use etalcl even paper used etacl. Residual error: propSd, addSd; multi-output prefixes output (CcpropSd). Covariate columns: canonical names inst/references/covariate-columns.md. Standardized choices include SEXF (1 = female), ADA_POS (1 = ADA-positive), RACE_<GROUP> prefix race indicators.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: Never push directly main. Always branch open PR. regenerating data/modeldb.rda / inst/modeldb.qs2, sync origin/main upstream additions aren’t clobbered. nlmixr2lib::buildModelDb() regenerates registry; commit regenerated artifacts alongside model file vignette.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: devtools::check() must pass. Model files validated via buildModelDb() (filename ↔︎ function-name match, parseable ini() / model()). Vignettes must build cleanly; use PKNCA (Suggests) NCA validation.

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: Replicate population pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic model reported Aguiar Zdovc et al. (2021) ustekinumab adults Crohn’s disease. Ustekinumab IgG1k monoclonal antibody binds p40 subunit shared interleukin-12 interleukin-23. structural model two-compartment quasi-equilibrium target-mediated drug disposition (QE-TMDD) model unbound drug unbound IL-12/IL-23 p40 target, distributing two-compartment systems binding central compartment, linked fecal calprotectin via indirect-response model unbound target stimulates FC production. Citation: Aguiar Zdovc J, Hanzel J, Kurent T, Sever N, Smrekar N, Kozelj M, Novak G, Stabuc B, Drobne D, Grabnar . Ustekinumab Dosing Individualization Crohn’s Disease Guided Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Model. Pharmaceutics. 2021;13(10):1587. doi:10.3390/pharmaceutics13101587 Article: https://doi.org/10.3390/pharmaceutics13101587

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: Aguiar 2021 cohort comprised 57 adults active Crohn’s disease starting ustekinumab single Slovenian tertiary referral center, followed 32 weeks. Baseline demographics (Aguiar 2021 Table 1): 56% female; median age 49 years (IQR 32-56); median weight 70 kg (IQR 59-84); median fat-free mass 45 kg (IQR 39-62) derived per subject via Janmahasatian 2005 model; median disease duration 14 years (IQR 7-22); 66.7% exposed prior biologic (38/57 prior anti-TNF; 10/57 prior vedolizumab); 77.2% endoscopically active luminal disease baseline; median serum CRP 3 mg/L (IQR 3-11), albumin 43 g/L (IQR 41-44), fecal calprotectin 134 mg/kg (IQR 53-213). FCGR3A rs396991 genotype distribution: V/V 8.8% (5/57), V/F 54.4% (31/57), F/F 36.8% (21/57). Standard ustekinumab dosing (simulations use) weight-tiered IV induction (260 mg WT ≤\le 55 kg, 390 mg 55 < WT ≤\le 85 kg, 520 mg WT > 85 kg) week 0, followed 90 mg subcutaneous maintenance every 8 weeks. demographics available programmatically via model’s metadata (readModelDb("Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab")$population).

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab.R. table collects one place. Concentrations nmol/L; source paper converted ustekinumab concentrations nmol/L using molecular weight 149 kDa (Aguiar 2021 Methods section 2.3).

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: FFM, PRIOR_BIO, FCGR3A_VV, ENDO_ULCER added canonical register inst/references/covariate-columns.md alongside model. ALB CRP pre-existing canonical columns.

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: Original subject-level data publicly available. virtual cohort uses covariate distributions approximating published Table 1 demographics. Covariates sampled independently (paper publish joint distributions); FCGR3A genotype frequencies match cohort’s Hardy-Weinberg distribution (V/V 8.8%, V/F + F/F 91.2%).
code: set.seed(20260425)  n_subj <- 200 mw_ust <- 149  # ustekinumab molecular weight in kDa, per Aguiar 2021 Methods.  # 1 mg ustekinumab = 1000 / mw_ust nmol = 6.711 nmol. mg_to_nmol <- function(mg) mg * 1000 / mw_ust  # Build a virtual Crohn's-disease cohort approximating Aguiar 2021 Table 1. cohort <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_subj),   # Weight: median 70 kg, IQR 59-84; lognormal covers the right skew.   WT  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(70), 0.22), 40), 130) ) |>   mutate(     # FFM: median 45 kg, IQR 39-62; correlated with weight in the source cohort.     # Approximated as 0.65 * WT for males (44%) and 0.55 * WT for females (56%).     SEXF = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.56),     FFM  = pmin(pmax(WT * if_else(SEXF == 1, 0.55, 0.65) +                        rnorm(n_subj, 0, 3), 25), 90),     # Albumin: median 43 g/L, IQR 41-44; tight distribution in CD remission/active.     ALB = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 42.5, 3.5), 28), 52),     # CRP in CD skews right; median 3 mg/L, IQR 3-11; log-normal shape.     CRP = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(4), 1.0), 0.3), 80),     # Prior biologic exposure: 66.7% in cohort (Table 1).     PRIOR_BIO = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.667),     # FCGR3A genotype: 8.8% V/V (Table 1).     FCGR3A_VV = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.088),     # Endoscopically active disease: 77.2% at baseline (Table 1).     ENDO_ULCER = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.772)   )

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: Aguiar 2021 simulated licensed regimen: weight-tiered IV induction week 0 (260 mg WT ≤\le 55 kg, 390 mg 55 < WT ≤\le 85 kg, 520 mg WT > 85 kg) followed 90 mg subcutaneous every 8 weeks (Methods section 2.4 scenario ). dosing dataset nmol model parameterized nmol / nmol/L; helper mg_to_nmol converts.
code: week  <- 7  # days ind_t <- 0 sc_times <- c(8, 16, 24) * week  # SC maintenance at 8, 16, 24 weeks  induction_dose_mg <- function(wt) {   ifelse(wt <= 55, 260,          ifelse(wt <= 85, 390, 520)) }  iv_induction <- cohort |>   mutate(     TIME = ind_t,     AMT  = mg_to_nmol(induction_dose_mg(WT)),     EVID = 1L,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_,     phase = "induction_IV"   )  sc_maintenance <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = sc_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = mg_to_nmol(90),     EVID = 1L,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA_real_,     phase = "maintenance_SC"   )  # Observation grid: dense early, weekly through 32 weeks. obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   c(0, 0.05, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7),   seq(7, 32 * week, by = 3.5) )))  obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_days) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0L,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_,     phase = NA_character_   )  # Use the variable-name CMT for observations: "Cc" for ustekinumab # concentration, "fc" for fecal calprotectin. Aguiar 2021's PD output is on # the fc compartment; observing only "Cc" plus "fc" gives both endpoints. obs_cc <- obs |> mutate(CMT = "Cc") obs_fc <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = c(0, 8, 16, 24, 32) * week) |>   mutate(AMT = 0, EVID = 0L, CMT = "fc", DV = NA_real_, phase = NA_character_)  events <- bind_rows(iv_induction, sc_maintenance, obs_cc, obs_fc) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV,          FFM, ALB, CRP, PRIOR_BIO, FCGR3A_VV, ENDO_ULCER, phase)  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID", "CMT")])))

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: default rxSolve() samples -subject random effects per subject model defines omega-style etas; stochastic simulation VPC plots use. published-value comparison uses rxode2::zeroRe() remove -subject variability typical-value trajectory can compared head--head Aguiar 2021’s reported point estimates.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
code: sim_cc <- sim |> filter(CMT == 7L)  # CMT 7 = Cc observations  sim_summary <- sim_cc |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(Cc_ugmL_05 = Q05 * mw_ust / 1000,          Cc_ugmL_50 = Q50 * mw_ust / 1000,          Cc_ugmL_95 = Q95 * mw_ust / 1000)  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / week, y = Cc_ugmL_50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Cc_ugmL_05, ymax = Cc_ugmL_95),               alpha = 0.25, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 32, by = 4)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = expression("Free ustekinumab ("*mu*"g/mL)"),     title = "Standard ustekinumab regimen: weight-tiered IV induction + 90 mg SC Q8W",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ", n_subj,                       " virtual CD patients)"),     caption = "Replicates Aguiar 2021 Figure 2A (free ustekinumab VPC structure)."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: tfree (unbound target central, nmol/L) fc (fecal calprotectin, mg/kg) emitted model alongside Cc can read directly simulation output.
code: sim_fc <- sim |> filter(CMT == 6L)  # CMT 6 = fc observations  sim_pd <- sim_fc |>   filter(time >= 0) |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     target_Q05 = quantile(tfree, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     target_Q50 = quantile(tfree, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     target_Q95 = quantile(tfree, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     fc_Q05 = quantile(fc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     fc_Q50 = quantile(fc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     fc_Q95 = quantile(fc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  p_target <- ggplot(sim_pd, aes(x = time / week, y = target_Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = target_Q05, ymax = target_Q95),               alpha = 0.25, fill = "darkorange") +   geom_line(colour = "darkorange", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 32, by = 4)) +   labs(x = "Time (weeks)", y = "Free target (nmol/L)",        title = "Unbound IL-12/23 p40 target") +   theme_bw()  p_fc <- ggplot(sim_pd, aes(x = time / week, y = fc_Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = fc_Q05, ymax = fc_Q95),               alpha = 0.25, fill = "firebrick") +   geom_line(colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 32, by = 4)) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (weeks)", y = "FC (mg/kg)",        title = "Fecal calprotectin",        caption = "Replicates Aguiar 2021 Figure 2B (FC VPC structure).") +   theme_bw()  if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   patchwork::wrap_plots(p_target, p_fc, ncol = 1) } else {   print(p_target)   print(p_fc) }

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: PKNCA run steady-state SC dosing interval (last 8-week interval, weeks 24-32) IV induction dose (week 0 - week 8, first SC maintenance dose week 8 closes interval). Aguiar 2021 reports typical terminal half-life 17 days induction dose (Section 3.3). Simulated NCA: IV induction (0-8 weeks) SC steady-state interval (weeks 24-32).
code: ind_window_start <- 0 ind_window_end   <- 8 * week  ss_window_start  <- 24 * week ss_window_end    <- 32 * week  # Pull only Cc observations from the simulation. sim_cc_only <- sim |> filter(CMT == 7L)  iv_conc <- sim_cc_only |>   filter(time >= ind_window_start, time <= ind_window_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(ID = id, time_rel = time - ind_window_start, Cc,             treatment = "IV_induction")  sc_ss_conc <- sim_cc_only |>   filter(time >= ss_window_start, time <= ss_window_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(ID = id, time_rel = time - ss_window_start, Cc,             treatment = "SC_90mg_Q8W_ss")  nca_conc <- bind_rows(iv_conc, sc_ss_conc)  iv_doses <- iv_induction |>   transmute(ID, time_rel = TIME - ind_window_start, AMT,             treatment = "IV_induction")  sc_ss_dose <- sc_maintenance |>   filter(TIME == 24 * week) |>   transmute(ID, time_rel = TIME - ss_window_start, AMT,             treatment = "SC_90mg_Q8W_ss")  nca_dose <- bind_rows(iv_doses, sc_ss_dose) conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = 8 * week,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   cmin      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■                             15% |  ETA:  9s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                   46% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         81% |  ETA:  2s  knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   digits  = 3,   caption = "Simulated NCA: IV induction (0-8 weeks) and SC steady-state interval (weeks 24-32)." )

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: Aguiar 2021 reports typical terminal half-life ustekinumab induction dose 17 days (Section 3.3) median trough steady-state concentration standard regimen 1.5 ug/mL (Section 4 discussion Wang et al. comparison). head--head comparison published point estimates, run typical-value simulation (zeroRe()) single subject reference covariates result deterministic. Typical-value simulation vs published values (Aguiar 2021 Section 3.3 Discussion comparison Wang et al.). simulated terminal half-life SC steady-state trough agree published values within ~20%. Differences greater 20% indicate either covariate-distribution mismatch virtual cohort model-parameter transcription bug; tune parameters match.
code: ref_subj <- tibble::tibble(   ID = 1L,   FFM = 45, ALB = 43, CRP = 3,   PRIOR_BIO = 1L, FCGR3A_VV = 0L, ENDO_ULCER = 1L )  ref_iv <- ref_subj |>   mutate(TIME = 0, AMT = mg_to_nmol(390), EVID = 1L,          CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_) ref_sc <- ref_subj |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = c(8, 16, 24) * week) |>   mutate(AMT = mg_to_nmol(90), EVID = 1L,          CMT = "depot", DV = NA_real_) ref_obs <- ref_subj |>   tidyr::crossing(     TIME = c(0.5, 1, 7, 14, 21, 28, 42, 56,              seq(56, 32 * week, by = 7))   ) |>   mutate(AMT = 0, EVID = 0L, CMT = "Cc", DV = NA_real_)  ref_events <- bind_rows(ref_iv, ref_sc, ref_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID))  mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ref_events,                                returnType = "data.frame") |>   filter(CMT == 7L) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalksyn', 'etalfc0'  # Terminal half-life from the IV induction phase (days 7-56 after dose): typical_late <- sim_typical |>   filter(time >= 7, time <= 56) fit_thalf <- lm(log(Cc) ~ time, data = typical_late) typical_thalf <- as.numeric(log(2) / -coef(fit_thalf)[2])  # Steady-state trough at week 32 (typical value, single reference subject): typical_trough <- sim_typical |>   filter(time == 32 * week) |>   pull(Cc) typical_trough_ugmL <- typical_trough * mw_ust / 1000  comparison <- tibble::tribble(   ~Metric,                                          ~Published, ~Simulated,   "Terminal half-life after IV induction (days)",   17,         round(typical_thalf, 1),   "SC SS trough free ustekinumab (ug/mL)",          1.5,        round(typical_trough_ugmL, 2) )  knitr::kable(   comparison,   caption = "Typical-value simulation vs published values (Aguiar 2021 Section 3.3 and Discussion comparison with Wang et al.)." )

title: Aguiar_2021_ustekinumab
text: PK assay unbound (free) drug. Aguiar 2021 ELISA (ImmunoGuide, AybayTech) measures free ustekinumab; model’s Cc therefore set free-drug concentration (cfree), total drug. matters total drug (free + complex) free drug diverge rapid binding events; assays read total ustekinumab back-calculated model without re-deriving Cc <- ctot. FFM derivation. source paper derives FFM per subject height, weight, sex via Janmahasatian 2005 semi-mechanistic model (Aguiar 2021 Methods 2.2). virtual cohort approximates FFM sex-dependent fraction WT (0.55 women, 0.65 men) plus jitter, rather reproducing Janmahasatian exactly; sufficient population-level VPC checks individual prediction. Joint covariate distributions published. WT, ALB, CRP, FCGR3A, PRIOR_BIO, ENDO_ULCER marginals sampled independently. cohort median FFM (45 kg) achieved linear WT-FFM relationship, joint correlations across covariates (e.g., albumin CRP, FFM prior biologic exposure) preserved. CRP treated time-fixed baseline simulations even though fitted ustekinumab response lower active-disease CRP time. Aguiar 2021 covariate model uses baseline CRP (Table 1: median 3 mg/L), model’s CRP covariate intended baseline column. Initial target distribution SS. target_central(0) target_peripheral(0) set T0 = Ksyn/Kdeg, steady-state unbound target concentration absence drug. peripheral target sees free target (binding central), SS without drug peripheral free-target concentration equals central total target, initial value correct compartments. model-derived helper Janmahasatian FFM exported. Users height + weight + sex columns compute FFM upstream supply FFM directly; future versions nlmixr2lib may export helper. CV--omega convention. %CV values Tables 2 3 converted log-scale variance via omega^2 = log(CV^2 + 1). IIV F reported logit scale SD (Aguiar 2021 Table 2 footnote e: 17.3); value squared directly without log transformation. Non-canonical compartment names. total_target matches canonical QSS / MM TMDD compartment name naming-conventions.md. companion target_peripheral (free peripheral target) fc (fecal calprotectin) compartments canonical list; checkModelConventions() emits two warnings . Aguiar 2021 distributes unbound target peripheral compartment (non-standard QE-TMDD extension) links unbound target FC via indirect response, pre-existing canonical name fits. names kept descriptive rather forcing drug-side peripheral2 / effect slots, mislead reader thinking drug-disposition compartments.

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: Citation: Almquist J, Kuruvilla D, Mai T, Tummala R, White WI, Tang W, Roskos L, Chia YL. Nonlinear Population Pharmacokinetics Anifrolumab Healthy Volunteers Patients Systemic Lupus Erythematosus. J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;62(9):1106-1120. Article: doi:10.1002/jcph.2055 Trial registries: NCT02601625 (phase 1), NCT01559090 (phase 2 Japan), NCT01438489 (MUSE), NCT02446912 (TULIP-1), NCT02446899 (TULIP-2). Anifrolumab fully human IgG1-kappa monoclonal antibody type interferon receptor subunit 1 (IFNAR1), approved moderate-- severe systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). paper integrated population PK analysis pooling 6049 anifrolumab serum concentrations 670 subjects across one phase 1 healthy-volunteer study, one phase 2 Japanese SLE study, phase 2b MUSE trial, two phase 3 TULIP trials. structural model two-compartment model parallel linear nonlinear elimination: first-order linear clearance plus target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD) quasi-steady-state (QSS) approximation Mager & Krzyzanski (2005). receptor pool held constant — supplement appendix carries explicit total- receptor compartment linear turnover rate kdeg, fixed 77.4 day⁻¹ confocal imaging (supplement reference 1, Wang 2013).

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: popPK dataset pooled 6 healthy adult volunteers (single-dose IV) 664 adult patients moderate--severe SLE across four interventional trials: phase 2 dose-escalation study Japanese patients (n = 17), phase 2b MUSE trial (n = 200), two phase 3 TULIP trials (TULIP-1 n = 267, TULIP-2 n = 180). pooled population 92.5 % female, median age 41 years (range 18-69), median body weight 68.6 kg (range 42.0-144.8), race distribution 57.2 % White, 13.6 % Black African American, 11.0 % Asian, 17.0 % . Across SLE studies, 75.5 - 88.2 % patients classified IFNGS-high validated 4-gene test (IFI27, IFI44, IFI44L, RSAD2). ADA prevalence visit 6.9 % (46/670) overall significant impact PK post-hoc TULIP analysis (Almquist 2022 Tables S2-S4). information available programmatically via model’s population metadata (readModelDb("Almquist_2022_anifrolumab")$population).

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Almquist_2022_anifrolumab.R. table collects one place review.

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: Body weight power effect CL (exponent 0.601) Vc (exponent 0.764). IFNGS status retained linear CL , R0; paper’s stepwise analysis (Section “Effect Baseline Type IFNGS Status PK”) found stronger objective-function drop IFNGS tested linear CL (ΔOFV = -272) R0 (ΔOFV = -199). Age, sex, race, ethnicity, region, renal/hepatic function, SLEDAI-2K score, dsDNA antibody status, complement levels, ADA status, concomitant medications tested retained.

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. figures use virtual populations whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics. Three cohorts constructed: single_dose — single 300 mg IV bolus, sampled densely 24 weeks, IFNGS-high typical subject — used Cc(t) shape PKNCA calculations. dose_panel — single doses 100, 150, 300, 1000 mg covering trial dose range, used replicate Figure 2’s dose-proportionality comparison. q4w_year — 13 doses 300 mg IV every 4 weeks (52 weeks) IFNGS-high cohort sampled IIV, plus 12 weeks washout — used replicate Figure 3 quantify time-varying CL.
code: set.seed(20220401)  mod <- readModelDb("Almquist_2022_anifrolumab")  build_single_dose <- function(amt_mg, n_sub, ifngs_high_pct = 100,                               wt_mean = 69.1, wt_cv = 0.20,                               max_day = 168, id_offset = 0L) {   ids <- id_offset + seq_len(n_sub)   wt  <- pmax(40, wt_mean * exp(rnorm(n_sub, 0, sqrt(log(1 + wt_cv^2)))))   bgene <- as.integer(runif(n_sub) < (ifngs_high_pct / 100))    obs_grid <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, 14,  by = 1),     seq(15, 56, by = 2),     seq(60, max_day, by = 7)   )))    doses <- tibble(     id    = ids,     time  = 0,     amt   = amt_mg,     evid  = 1L,     cmt   = "central",     WT    = wt,     BGENE21_HIGH = bgene   )   obs <- tibble(id = ids, WT = wt, BGENE21_HIGH = bgene) |>     cross_join(tibble(time = obs_grid)) |>     mutate(amt = NA_real_, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc")   bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     arrange(id, time, evid) |>     mutate(dose_grp = paste0(amt_mg, " mg")) } dose_levels <- paste0(c(100, 150, 300, 1000), " mg")  # Single 300 mg dose, IFNGS-high typical subject (no IIV) for the Figure 2 shape single_dose <- build_single_dose(amt_mg = 300, n_sub = 1, ifngs_high_pct = 100,                                  max_day = 168, id_offset = 0L)  # Dose-comparison panel: 25 subjects each, IFNGS-high, sampled IIV via stochastic sim later dose_panel <- bind_rows(   build_single_dose(100,  25, 100, max_day = 168, id_offset = 1000L),   build_single_dose(150,  25, 100, max_day = 168, id_offset = 2000L),   build_single_dose(300,  25, 100, max_day = 168, id_offset = 3000L),   build_single_dose(1000, 25, 100, max_day = 168, id_offset = 4000L) )  # Q4W 300 mg cohort: 60 subjects, 81 % IFNGS-high (TULIP-1/2 average), 13 doses + washout build_q4w <- function(n_sub, dose_mg = 300, n_doses = 13, ifngs_high_pct = 81,                       wt_mean = 69.1, wt_cv = 0.20, washout_weeks = 16,                       id_offset = 5000L) {   ids <- id_offset + seq_len(n_sub)   wt  <- pmax(40, wt_mean * exp(rnorm(n_sub, 0, sqrt(log(1 + wt_cv^2)))))   bgene <- as.integer(runif(n_sub) < (ifngs_high_pct / 100))    dose_times <- seq(0, by = 28, length.out = n_doses)   end_day <- max(dose_times) + 7 * washout_weeks   obs_grid <- sort(unique(c(     dose_times,     dose_times + 1,     dose_times + 14,     seq(0, end_day, by = 7)   )))    doses <- tibble(id = ids, WT = wt, BGENE21_HIGH = bgene) |>     cross_join(tibble(time = dose_times, amt = dose_mg, evid = 1L,                       cmt = "central"))   obs <- tibble(id = ids, WT = wt, BGENE21_HIGH = bgene) |>     cross_join(tibble(time = obs_grid, amt = NA_real_, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc"))   bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     arrange(id, time, evid) |>     mutate(dose_grp = paste0(dose_mg, " mg Q4W")) } q4w_year <- build_q4w(n_sub = 60)

code: # Typical-value (no IIV) for the Figure 2 single-dose shape sim_single_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(   rxode2::zeroRe(mod), events = single_dose,   keep = c("WT", "BGENE21_HIGH", "dose_grp") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalr0', 'etatmax'  # Stochastic dose panel (IIV included) sim_dose_panel <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = dose_panel,   keep = c("WT", "BGENE21_HIGH", "dose_grp") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  # Q4W steady-state cohort (IIV) sim_q4w <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = q4w_year,   keep = c("WT", "BGENE21_HIGH", "dose_grp") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure 2 — single-dose PK by IFNGS status (typical subject)
title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: Almquist 2022 Figure 2 plots model-predicted single-dose anifrolumab concentration-time profiles typical 70 kg patient, comparing IFNGS-high vs IFNGS-low. QSS-TMDD model produces characteristic multiphasic decay: early linear distribution phase, near-linear-CL phase drug concentration much greater Kss, steep accelerated decline Cc approaches Kss target-mediated pathway dominates. QSS denominator 1 + R_total · Kss / (Kss + Ab)² rises near 1.14 low concentrations, reflecting partitioning drug target-bound state. Replicates Figure 2 Almquist 2022: typical-value 300 mg single-IV-dose PK shape (IIV). IFNGS-low typical subject shows multiphasic decay scaled linear-CL factor 0.793.
code: sim_single_typ |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 168) |>   mutate(Cc_high = pmax(Cc, 1e-4),          Cc_low  = pmax(Cc * 0.793, 1e-4)) |>  # F_IFNGS-low scales linear CL only   select(time, Cc_high, Cc_low) |>   pivot_longer(-time, names_to = "ifngs", values_to = "Cc") |>   mutate(ifngs = recode(ifngs, Cc_high = "IFNGS-high",                                 Cc_low  = "IFNGS-low (linear-CL × 0.793)")) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Cc, colour = ifngs)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Anifrolumab Cc (µg/mL)",        colour = "",        title = "Figure 2 — Typical 300 mg single-dose PK by IFNGS status",        caption = "QSS-TMDD multiphasic decay; LLOQ 0.02 µg/mL.")

what: Figure 2 panel — nonlinear PK across dose levels
title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: Figure 2 Almquist 2022 also illustrates anifrolumab exposure increases dose-proportionally 100 mg 1000 mg target-mediated pathway saturates dose increases. stochastic dose-panel simulation reproduces : Replicates Figure 2 Almquist 2022: VPC-style 5-50-95 % bands single-dose PK across 100, 150, 300, 1000 mg IV cohorts. Nonlinear elimination shows steeper terminal slope lower doses.
code: sim_dose_panel |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 168) |>   mutate(Cc = pmax(Cc, 1e-4),          dose_grp = factor(dose_grp, levels = dose_levels)) |>   group_by(time, dose_grp) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ dose_grp) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.02, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Anifrolumab Cc (µg/mL)",        title = "Figure 2 panel — VPC by single-dose level",        caption = "Dashed line: assay LLOQ 0.02 µg/mL.")

what: Figure 3 — washout from steady state (300 mg IV Q4W)
title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: Figure 3 Almquist 2022 shows simulated washout steady-state 300 mg IV Q4W dosing. reproduce q4w_year cohort plotting post-final-dose tail. Replicates Figure 3 Almquist 2022: 5-50-95 % VPC anifrolumab washout steady state (300 mg IV Q4W × 13 doses). Dashed line assay LLOQ; dotted vertical marker median time LLOQ reported paper (8.4 weeks ≈ 59 days post-final-dose).
code: last_dose_day <- max(seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 13))  sim_q4w |>   filter(time >= last_dose_day, time <= last_dose_day + 16 * 7) |>   mutate(Cc = pmax(Cc, 1e-4),          t_post = time - last_dose_day) |>   group_by(t_post) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(t_post, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.02, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_vline(xintercept = 8.4 * 7, linetype = "dotted", colour = "firebrick") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Days after last dose", y = "Anifrolumab Cc (µg/mL)",        title = "Figure 3 — Washout from steady state",        caption = "Cohort: 60 subjects, 81 % IFNGS-high; 13 × 300 mg IV Q4W then 16 wk follow-up.")

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: empirical F_emp(t) = exp((Tmax + η_Tmax) · t / (TC50 + t)) factor gives asymptotic CL ratio exp(Tmax) = exp(-0.155) = 0.857, .e. 14.3 % asymptotic decrease linear CL relative baseline. t = 365 days, typical-value reduction baseline
code: t_yr <- 365 tmax_typ <- -0.155 tc50 <- 380 red_1y <- 1 - exp(tmax_typ * t_yr / (tc50 + t_yr)) cat(sprintf("Predicted typical-value CL reduction at 1 year: %.1f %% (paper reports 8.4 %% median).\n",             100 * red_1y)) #> Predicted typical-value CL reduction at 1 year: 7.3 % (paper reports 8.4 % median). cat(sprintf("Predicted typical-value CL reduction asymptotically: %.1f %% (paper reports 16.4 %%).\n",             100 * (1 - exp(tmax_typ)))) #> Predicted typical-value CL reduction asymptotically: 14.4 % (paper reports 16.4 %).

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: 1-year typical-value CL reduction reported (~7.3 %) slightly lower paper’s reported 8.4 % median paper’s value median simulated population η_Tmax IIV (variance 0.146, CV 38.2 %) included; typical-value calculation zeros η_Tmax. Including IIV virtual population yields comparable median.

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: QSS structural form retained, full Mager-Jusko TMDD ODE. main paper Methods supplement appendix explicitly adopt quasi-steady-state approximation (Mager & Krzyzanski 2005). supplement’s NONMEM control stream QSS form denominator 1 + C4 · KSS/(KSS + C2)². paper report kon koff separately — derived combination Kss = (koff + kint)/kon. full Mager-Jusko TMDD ODE reconstructed without inventing two binding rate constants, packaged model implements QSS form exactly published, augmented dynamic-receptor-pool ODE supplement appendix specifies (d/dt(total_target) = kdeg · (R0 · Vc - R_total) kint = kdeg). earlier extraction attempt (task 119, never merged main) main paper available — JCPH supplement subscription-blocked time. supplement disk, implementation can carry explicit receptor pool rather holding R_total = R0 constant. kdeg kint provenance. receptor degradation rate kdeg = 77.4 day⁻¹ constraint kint = kdeg recorded supplement appendix (line 140 supplement trim used ) ultimately reference Wang et al. 2013 (Clin Pharmacol Ther 93:483-492) — confocal imaging studies IFNAR1 internalization. main paper Table 1 lists parameter k_int , kdeg, value 77.4 day⁻¹ marked “(fixed)”. packaged model uses supplement’s mechanistic interpretation (receptor-turnover parameter also drives complex internalisation) kdeg <- fixed(77.4) declaration carries inline non-paper-source note pointing supplement appendix. Time-varying-CL Hill exponent fixed 1. supplement appendix writes empirical CL factor Hill function exponent LAM = THETA(15), main paper’s Methods text reduces simple Emax--time form t / (TC50 + t). value THETA(15) reported -disk source (neither Table 1 main paper supplementary figure). packaged model uses LAM = 1 per main paper text; typical-value 1-year CL reduction predicts (~7.3 %) close identical paper’s population median 8.4 %. Anifrolumab molecular weight 148 kDa, used convert drug mass (mg, Vc/CL/Q unit) drug amount (nmol, binding- kinetics unit). paper state MW explicitly; 148 kDa standard value IgG1-kappa anifrolumab’s reported composition. 5 % error MW propagates 5 % shift computed binding-pathway amount-rates — drug-mass dynamics unaffected. document rather tune MW match published target. Reference body weight 69.1 kg. supplement’s NONMEM code centers BW power effect 69.1 kg (BLWGHT/69.1); matches population median Table S2 (68.6 kg) within 0.7 % taken control stream rather re-computed demographics table. Diagonal Ω matrix. Almquist 2022 Table 1 reports diagonal ω² lcl, lvc, lr0, tmax; correlations among ETAs published. packaged model uses diagonal Ω. Race / ethnicity / region retained. Tested significant per SCM. None model covariates; population metadata records demographic distribution context .

title: Almquist_2022_anifrolumab
text: author correspondence needed; parameter values present -disk either Table 1 main paper supplement appendix.

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: Citation: Bajaj G, Wang X, Agrawal S, Gupta M, Roy , Feng Y. Model-based population pharmacokinetic analysis nivolumab patients solid tumors. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2017;6(1):58-66. doi:[10.1002/psp4.12143](https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12143) Description: Two-compartment population PK model nivolumab (anti-PD-1 IgG4) time-varying clearance (sigmoid Emax) patients advanced solid tumors. Modality: Therapeutic monoclonal antibody (IgG4), IV infusion. Nivolumab fully human anti-PD-1 IgG4 monoclonal antibody. Bajaj 2017 presents final population PK model supporting clinical development prescriber information, pooling 1,895 patients across 11 trials. revision earlier stationary-clearance analysis added time-varying clearance term motivated FDA-identified temporal trend nivolumab CL. Structure: linear two-compartment IV model time-varying CL via sigmoid-Emax function time since first dose: CLt,=CLi⋅exp⁡(Emax⁡,itγT50γ+tγ),Emax⁡,=Emax⁡,TV+ηEmax,\mathrm{CL}_{t,} \;=\; \mathrm{CL}_i \cdot   \exp\!\left( \dfrac{E_{\max,}\, t^{\gamma}}                      {T_{50}^{\gamma} + t^{\gamma}} \right), \qquad E_{\max,} = E_{\max,\mathrm{TV}} + \eta_{E_{\max},} Emax=−0.295E_{\max} = -0.295 (fractional decrease CL t≫T50t \gg T_{50}) T50=1,410T_{50} = 1{,}410 h = 58.75 days. Covariate effects: body weight (power) eGFR (CKD-EPI, power) CL; ECOG performance status ≥1\geq 1, male sex, Asian race (exponential indicators) CL; body weight (power) male sex (exponential) Vc.

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: final-model population comprised 1,895 patients across 11 trials (Bajaj 2017 Table 2 Table 3): 3 phase studies (MDX1106-01, ONO-4538-01, MDX1106-03). 3 phase II studies (CA209010, CA209063, ONO-4538-02). 5 phase III studies (CA209017, CA209037, CA209025, CA209057, CA209066). Dose range 0.3-10.0 mg/kg IV infusion (1-hour) every 2 weeks (Q2W) every 3 weeks (Q3W). Baseline demographics (Bajaj 2017 Table 3): Age 61.1 (SD 11.1) years; weight 79.1 (SD 19.3) kg. Sex: 66.7% male, 33.3% female. Race: 88.92% White, 6.44% Asian, 2.80% Black / African American, 1.74% . ECOG performance status: 38.73% = 0, 58.52% = 1, 2.74% = 2. Baseline CKD-EPI eGFR: 78.5 (SD 21.6) mL/min/1.73 m^2. Tumor type: melanoma 29.82%, NSCLC 34.78%, RCC 31.93%, 3.48%. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Bajaj_2017_nivolumab")$population.

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Bajaj_2017_nivolumab.R. table collects one place review. Equations: structural two-compartment micro-constant form; Eqs. 7, 8, 10 Bajaj 2017 express CL(t) VC terms reference values, covariate exponents, sigmoid-Emax time function listed . Reference covariates (Bajaj 2017 Table 1 footnote ): white female, 80 kg, eGFR 90 mL/min/1.73 m^2, ECOG performance status = 0.

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose demographics approximate pooled Bajaj 2017 population (Table 3). Continuous covariates drawn log-normal / normal distributions around reported means; binary / categorical covariates match reported marginal distributions. Two reference dosing regimens (approved highest-tested regimens Bajaj 2017) compared: 3 mg/kg Q2W (pivotal dose across indications) 10 mg/kg Q2W (highest dose PK analysis).
code: set.seed(2017) n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble(   ID         = seq_len(n_subj),   WT         = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(79.1), 0.24), 34.1), 168.2),   CRCL       = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 78.5, 21.6), 30), 180),   SEXF       = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.333),   RACE_ASIAN = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.0644),   ECOG_GE1   = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.6126) # ECOG 1 (58.52%) + ECOG 2 (2.74%) ) dose_interval_d <- 14 n_doses         <- 12 dose_times_d    <- seq(0, by = dose_interval_d, length.out = n_doses) obs_times_d     <- sort(unique(c(dose_times_d, seq(0, 168, by = 1))))  build_events <- function(pop, mgkg) {   amt_per_subject <- pop$WT * mgkg   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(AMT = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times_d) |>     mutate(EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 1 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = paste0(mgkg, " mg/kg Q2W"))   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_d) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,            DV = NA_real_,            treatment = paste0(mgkg, " mg/kg Q2W"))   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events_3  <- build_events(cohort, 3) events_10 <- build_events(cohort, 10)

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Bajaj_2017_nivolumab") sim_3  <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_3,  returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_10 <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_10, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_3,  treatment = "3 mg/kg Q2W"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_10, treatment = "10 mg/kg Q2W") )

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: Bajaj 2017 Figure 3 shows visual predictive check 3.0 10.0 mg/kg Q2W. figure reproduces median 5-95% prediction interval packaged model, analogous shaded bands paper’s VPC.
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Nivolumab concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     title = "Simulated nivolumab PK: 3 mg/kg vs 10 mg/kg Q2W",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ",                       n_subj, " virtual patients per arm). Replicates Bajaj 2017 Figure 3."),     caption = "Model: Bajaj 2017 CPT:PSP 6(1):58-66"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: Bajaj 2017 reports sigmoid decrease CL baseline CLbase⋅exp⁡(−0.295)\mathrm{CL}_\mathrm{base} \cdot \exp(-0.295) = 74.5% baseline steady state (paper reports mean maximal reduction ~24.5%). typical-value CL(t) / CL(0) profile reproduces time course white female, 80 kg, eGFR 90, ECOG 0, non-Asian reference subject (deterministic, etas = 0):
code: t_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5) events_cl <- data.frame(   ID         = 1,   WT         = 80,   CRCL       = 90,   SEXF       = 1,   RACE_ASIAN = 0,   ECOG_GE1   = 0,   TIME       = c(0, t_grid),   AMT        = c(80 * 3, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   EVID       = c(1, rep(0, length(t_grid))),   CMT        = "central",   DUR        = c(1 / 24, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   DV         = NA_real_ ) mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_cl  <- rxSolve(mod_typ, events = events_cl, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etacl_emax' sim_cl  <- sim_cl[sim_cl$time > 0, ]  ggplot(sim_cl, aes(time, cl / cl_base)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1,           linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = exp(-0.295), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "CL(t) / CL(0)",     title = "Time-varying clearance in Bajaj 2017",     subtitle = "Typical reference subject; asymptote at exp(Emax) = 0.745 (24.5% reduction)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: Compute NCA parameters 12th (near steady-state) dosing interval 3 mg/kg Q2W 10 mg/kg Q2W. paper report dedicated NCA table (reports model-based exposure metrics), comparison within-simulation consistency check packaged model behaves linear 2-compartment PK time-varying clearance: () 10 mg/kg exposure ~3.33x higher 3 mg/kg (dose-proportional CL concentration-independent); (b) apparent terminal half-life steady state range Bajaj 2017 reports t_{1/2}(β) (geometric mean 25 days; 77.5% CV). Simulated NCA parameters steady state (12th dosing interval, days 154-168)
code: # Use the 12th (last simulated) dosing interval as the steady-state # approximation. Time-since-first-dose of 154 to 168 days spans this # interval. interval_start <- dose_times_d[12] interval_end   <- interval_start + dose_interval_d  sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc),                 time >= interval_start,                 time <= interval_end) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - interval_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, Cc)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  dose_df <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time == interval_start, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(.groups = "drop") |>   dplyr::left_join(cohort |> dplyr::select(id = ID, WT), by = "id") |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt      = ifelse(treatment == "3 mg/kg Q2W", WT * 3, WT * 10),     time_rel = 0   ) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, amt)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = dose_interval_d,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   cmin      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                  50% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      90% |  ETA:  1s knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   caption = "Simulated NCA parameters at steady state (12th dosing interval, days 154-168)" )

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: Bajaj 2017 publish pooled NCA table. paper report population-level PK descriptors (Results Table 1 footnotes) can cross-checked packaged model: Differences within 20% expected; anything larger indicate coding error. PKNCA table’s half.life column compared reported geometric mean 25 days (77.5% CV). large -subject CV published terminal t_{1/2}(β) reflects IIV VP (omega^2 = 0.258, corresponding 50.8% CV VP) combined log-normal CL variability.

title: Bajaj_2017_nivolumab
text: Time-varying clearance parameterization. Bajaj 2017 Eq. 8 expresses CL(t) CL_base * exp(Emax_i * t^gamma / (T50^gamma + t^gamma)), additive IIV Emax (Emax_i = Emax_TV + eta_Emax, Bajaj 2017 Eq. 3). packaged model preserves form verbatim (including additive, non-log-normal IIV Emax). stochastic simulation published omega^2_EMAX = 0.0719 (SD 0.268), small fraction individuals draw Emax > 0 show slight CL increase time — feature additive parameterization, coding change. Time units. Bajaj 2017 reports CL Q mL/h T50 h; VC, VP L; time hours throughout Table 1. packaged model keeps time days consistency mAb half-life reporting (nlmixr2lib mAb models), CL Q converted via x24/1000 T50 via /24. Sex encoding. Bajaj 2017’s SEX column male-indicator (1 = male, 0 = female) female reference category (Table 1 footnote : “white female reference”). packaged model stores sex canonical SEXF column (1 = female, 0 = male) derives male-indicator inside model() (1 - SEXF), preserving paper’s reference values CL_REF VC_REF. model applied dataset SEXF already present, transformation required. Performance-status encoding. Bajaj 2017’s PS column binary: 1 baseline ECOG >= 1, else 0. packaged model stores canonical ECOG_GE1 column. Bajaj 2017 Methods notes one constituent study (CA209025) used Karnofsky Performance Status values mapped ECOG scale per Oken 1982 crosswalk binarization. Renal function encoding. Bajaj 2017’s eGFR column estimated CKD-EPI equation mL/min/1.73 m^2. packaged model stores canonical CRCL column CKD-EPI method documented covariateData[[CRCL]]$notes. Virtual cohort. Demographics simulated match marginal distributions reported Bajaj 2017 Table 3. Continuous covariates (WT, CRCL) assume lognormal / normal shape anchored reported mean SD; binary / categorical covariates (SEXF, RACE_ASIAN, ECOG_GE1) match reported proportions. Joint covariate structure (e.g., correlation WT SEXF, PS ALB noted paper’s discussion) simulated. --scope covariates. paper’s full-model results sensitivity analyses also examined baseline ALB, LDH, age, race (non-Asian), tumor type, tumor burden, PD-L1 expression, mild hepatic impairment, time-varying ADA status. five covariates retained final model (BW, eGFR, ECOG PS >= 1, sex, Asian race) implemented . ALB flagged paper potentially clinically relevant covariate sensitivity analysis (>20% effect low ALB); part published final model included . ADA covariate. Bajaj 2017 reports ADA--CL estimate ~114% (modest 14% increase CL ADA-positive) retained final model. implemented . IV infusion duration. simulations use 1-hour infusion (DUR = 1/24 day), matching regimens summarized Bajaj 2017 Table 2.

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: vignette exercises mechanistic model (Bender 2014 Table II), tracks drug--antibody-ratio moiety DAR0, DAR1, …, DAR7 individually. moiety three-compartment distribution shares central volume V1, peripheral volumes V2/V3, distributional clearances CLd2/CLd3 every DAR species. first-order deconjugation chain carries DAR_n toward DAR_(n-1) rates k_7->6 = k_6->5 = k_5->4 = k_4->3 = k_3->2 — single shared upper-chain rate estimated species (k_7->3 model file); k_2->1 k_1->0 — two separate first-order rate constants. See companion Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_reduced model / vignette simpler two-species (T-DM1 + DAR0) lumping (Bender 2014 Table III).

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Cynomolgus monkey estimates populate default ini() block. rat parameter set documented model’s population$species_parameters metadata model-file comments.

To switch to r"| __truncated__ str(mod$meta$population$dose_products, max.level = 2) #> List of 3 #>  $ note        : chr "Paper reports two preclinical dose products. The DAR-specific fractions below are seeded into the DAR0_central."| __truncated__ #>  $ T_DM1_DAR3_1:List of 8 #>   ..$ DAR7: num 0.02 #>   ..$ DAR6: num 0.05 #>   ..$ DAR5: num 0.1 #>   ..$ DAR4: num 0.19 #>   ..$ DAR3: num 0.26 #>   ..$ DAR2: num 0.23 #>   ..$ DAR1: num 0.13 #>   ..$ DAR0: num 0.02 #>  $ T_DM1_DAR1_5:List of 8 #>   ..$ DAR7: num 0 #>   ..$ DAR6: num 0 #>   ..$ DAR5: num 0.01 #>   ..$ DAR4: num 0.04 #>   ..$ DAR3: num 0.13 #>   ..$ DAR2: num 0.26 #>   ..$ DAR1: num 0.35 #>   ..$ DAR0: num 0.21

what: Cynomolgus 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 replication
title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender 2014 Figure 4 (cynomolgus panel) shows TT per-DAR concentration profiles single 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 dose. Simulating mechanistic model requires seeding dar*_central compartment DAR-3.1 dose-product fraction multiplied total dose amount.   per-DAR profiles reproduce expected qualitative features paper: DAR7 DAR6 decline monotonically (higher species feed ); DAR3–DAR5 peak early decay; DAR1 DAR0 build time fed deconjugation higher moieties.
code: dose_mg <- 120  # 30 mg/kg * 4 kg body weight dar_fracs <- list(   dar7_central = 0.02, dar6_central = 0.05,   dar5_central = 0.10, dar4_central = 0.19,   dar3_central = 0.26, dar2_central = 0.23,   dar1_central = 0.13, dar0_central = 0.02 )  events <- rxode2::et() for (cmt in names(dar_fracs)) {   events <- rxode2::et(events,                        amt = dose_mg * dar_fracs[[cmt]],                        cmt = cmt, time = 0) } events <- rxode2::et(events, seq(0.05, 42, length.out = 150), cmt = "Cc")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = events) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' sim_long <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Ctt), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc  = "T-DM1 (DAR1-7)",                           Ctt = "Total trastuzumab"))  ggplot(sim_long, aes(time, conc, colour = analyte)) +   geom_line(size = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = NULL,        title  = "Cynomolgus 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 (Bender 2014 mechanistic)",        caption = "Replicates the composite analytes in Bender 2014 Figure 4.") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated. dar_cols <- paste0("Cc_dar", 0:7) sim_dar <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   select(time, all_of(dar_cols)) |>   pivot_longer(-time, names_to = "moiety", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(moiety = factor(moiety, levels = dar_cols, labels = paste0("DAR", 0:7)))  ggplot(sim_dar, aes(time, conc, colour = moiety)) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.01, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = "Moiety",        title  = "Per-DAR concentration profiles (cynomolgus 30 mg/kg DAR 3.1)",        caption = "Replicates the per-moiety panels in Bender 2014 Figure 4.") #> Warning: Removed 18 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical mechanistic DAR PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Cynomolgus body weight fixed 4 kg convert paper’s mg/kg doses absolute mg; model treats volumes absolute (mL paper, L ini() block). Residual error applied Cc (T-DM1) Ctt (TT) ; per-DAR concentrations exposed deterministic derived outputs (Cc_dar0 … Cc_dar7) VPC-style comparison. model’s k_plasma parameter retained future -vitro plasma stability simulations, even though -vivo ODE sum CL_in_vivo/V1 + k_plasma simplifies CL_TT/V1.

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender et al. (2014) developed two preclinical PK models trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1), antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) trastuzumab maytansine derivative DM1 linked via SMCC. dataset combined rat (n = 34) cynomolgus monkey (n = 18) PK, plus vitro plasma stability. vignette exercises reduced model (Bender 2014 Table III), collapses conjugated species single lumped “T-DM1” compartment tracks naked trastuzumab (DAR0) separately. T-DM1 deconjugates DAR0 via single first-order deconjugation clearance CL_DEC. forms share central / two peripheral volumes distributional clearances. See companion Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_mechanistic model / vignette full DAR0–DAR7 catenary chain (Bender 2014 Table II).

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: rat cynomolgus monkey parameter sets documented model’s population$species_parameters metadata. ini() block populated cynomolgus estimates (Table III, cyno column). rat alternates noted -file comments can applied updating fixed effects (converting published mL/day values L/day via /1000).
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_reduced")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' str(mod$meta$population$species_parameters, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ cynomolgus:List of 9 #>   ..$ source : chr "Bender 2014 Table III, cynomolgus columns" #>   ..$ CL_TT  : chr "19.9 mL/day (IIV CV 19.8%)" #>   ..$ V1     : chr "154 mL (IIV CV 7.65%)" #>   ..$ CLd2   : chr "56.8 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V2     : chr "50.0 mL (no IIV)" #>   ..$ CLd3   : chr "60.4 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V3     : chr "84.7 mL (IIV CV 27.3%)" #>   ..$ CL_DEC : chr "22.0 mL/day (IIV CV 11.9%)" #>   ..$ res_err: chr "9.64% proportional" #>  $ rat       :List of 10 #>   ..$ source : chr "Bender 2014 Table III, rat columns" #>   ..$ CL_TT  : chr "2.37 mL/day (IIV CV 24.6%)" #>   ..$ V1     : chr "10.7 mL (IIV CV 19.8%)" #>   ..$ CLd2   : chr "59.7 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V2     : chr "2.52 mL (IIV CV 68.0%)" #>   ..$ CLd3   : chr "13.9 mL/day (no IIV)" #>   ..$ V3     : chr "15.5 mL (IIV CV 16.5%)" #>   ..$ CL_DEC : chr "2.24 mL/day (IIV CV 15.3%)" #>   ..$ res_err: chr "10.9% proportional" #>   ..$ notes  : chr "Rat fit adds IIV on V2; to switch from the cynomolgus default to the rat parameter set, call ini() on the resul"| __truncated__
