title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender 2014 Figure 3 (bottom panel) displays total trastuzumab (TT) T-DM1 concentration–time profiles single 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 DAR 3.1 dose four cynomolgus monkeys. Reference body weight cynomolgus monkeys ~4 kg, giving nominal 120 mg absolute dose.
code: dose_mg <- 120  # 30 mg/kg * 4 kg body weight events  <- rxode2::et(amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = 0) |>   rxode2::et(seq(0.1, 42, length.out = 120), cmt = "Cc")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = events) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp2', 'etalcldec' sim_long <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Ctt), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc  = "T-DM1",                           Ctt = "Total trastuzumab"))  ggplot(sim_long, aes(time, conc, colour = analyte)) +   geom_line(size = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = NULL,        title  = "Cynomolgus monkey 30 mg/kg IV T-DM1 (Bender 2014 reduced)",        caption = "Typical-value replication of Bender 2014 Figure 3 (cynomolgus panel).") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Switching ini() fixed effects Table III rat estimates two-step update: convert published mL mL/day L L/day (divide 1000), pass ini() model object. IIV V2 reported rat cyno default ini etalvp term, illustrate typical-value simulation .
code: mod_rat <- mod |>   ini(     lcl    = log(0.00237),     lvc    = log(0.0107),     lqd2   = log(0.0597),     lvp    = log(0.00252),     lqd3   = log(0.0139),     lvp2   = log(0.0155),     lcldec = log(0.00224),     propSd   = 0.109,     propSd_Ctt = 0.109   ) #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lcl` to `-6.0448653238351` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lvc` to `-4.53751153751428` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lqd2` to `-2.81842325858358` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lvp` to `-5.98349637745881` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lqd3` to `-4.27586643884549` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lvp2` to `-4.16691525505694` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `lcldec` to `-6.10127941311519` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `propSd` to `0.109` #> ℹ change initial estimate of `propSd_Ctt` to `0.109`  rat_dose <- 3  # 10 mg/kg * 0.3 kg typical rat BW events_rat <- rxode2::et(amt = rat_dose, cmt = "central", time = 0) |>   rxode2::et(seq(0.05, 28, length.out = 120), cmt = "Cc")  sim_rat <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_rat |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = events_rat) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp2', 'etalcldec' sim_rat_long <- as.data.frame(sim_rat) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Ctt), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc  = "T-DM1",                           Ctt = "Total trastuzumab"))  ggplot(sim_rat_long, aes(time, conc, colour = analyte)) +   geom_line(size = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration (ug/mL)",        colour = NULL,        title  = "Rat 10 mg/kg IV T-DM1 (Bender 2014 reduced, rat parameter set)",        caption = "Typical-value replication of Bender 2014 Figure 2 (rat panel).")

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Bender 2014 Table III reports derived terminal half-lives total trastuzumab T-DM1 species. Computing simulated log-linear terminal phase provides end--end sanity check published values.
code: late <- as.data.frame(sim) |>   filter(time >= 25)  t12 <- function(t, c) log(2) / -coef(lm(log(c) ~ t))[2] data.frame(   analyte = c("T-DM1", "Total trastuzumab"),   simulated_t12_days = c(t12(late$time, late$Cc),                          t12(late$time, late$Ctt)),   paper_t12_days = c(5.21, 10.5) ) #>             analyte simulated_t12_days paper_t12_days #> 1             T-DM1           5.207294           5.21 #> 2 Total trastuzumab          10.465739          10.50

title: Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) preclinical reduced PK model (Bender 2014)
text: Cynomolgus body weight fixed 4 kg rat body weight fixed 0.3 kg convert paper mg/kg doses absolute mg inputs; model scale body weight, matching paper’s absolute-volume parameterization. Residual error treated pure proportional linear space; Bender 2014 reports residual error additive log-scale, equivalent proportional nlmixr2’s linear space. BSV zeroed rxode2::zeroRe() typical-value replication; Table III IIV estimates remain available ini() stochastic simulation.

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Citation: Berends SE, Strik , Van Selm S, Lowenberg M, Ponsioen CY, D’Haens GR, Mathot RAA. Tumor necrosis factor-mediated disposition infliximab ulcerative colitis patients. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2019;46(6):543-551. doi:10.1007/s10928-019-09652-5 Description: Two-compartment TMDD-QSS population PK/target-dynamics model infliximab free TNF adults moderate--severe ulcerative colitis (Berends 2019) Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-019-09652-5 Berends et al. (2019) developed target-mediated drug disposition model infliximab (IFX, IgG1 anti-TNF monoclonal antibody) adults moderate--severe ulcerative colitis. final structural model two-compartment quasi-steady-state (QSS) approximation full Mager-Jusko TMDD framework: free IFX rapid binding equilibrium free TNF, IFX-TNF complex internalized rate kint (= paper’s ke(P)), distinct first-order linear elimination free IFX CL/Vc. Covariates retained CL anti-drug-antibody (ADA) status (categorical, multiplicative factor 2.15 ADA-positive subjects) serum albumin (continuous, power form (ALB/38)^-1.13). TNF degradation rate kdeg fixed 5.12/day sensitivity analysis (Supplementary Table 1) data simultaneously identify Kss, kint, kdeg.

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Single-center prospective cohort 20 anti-TNF naive adults moderate--severe ulcerative colitis (Amsterdam, Netherlands), median age 36 years (range 19-69), median body weight 70 kg (range 47-90), 35 % female, median serum albumin 38 g/L (range 23-45), median CRP 25.3 mg/L, 55 % concomitant thiopurines, 35 % hospitalized baseline. received standard 5 mg/kg IFX induction weeks 0, 2, 6 (one subject also received additional dose day 5). 214 IFX serum concentrations 214 TNF serum concentrations used fitting; antibodies--infliximab detected 7/20 patients follow-(Berends 2019 Methods + Table 1). full population descriptor available programmatically:
code: str(rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Berends_2019_infliximab"))$meta$population) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> List of 14 #>  $ n_subjects                 : int 20 #>  $ n_studies                  : int 1 #>  $ age_range                  : chr "19-69 years (median 36)" #>  $ weight_range               : chr "47-90 kg (median 70)" #>  $ sex_female_pct             : num 35 #>  $ race_ethnicity             : chr "Not reported (single-center Netherlands cohort)" #>  $ disease_state              : chr "Adults with moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis (95% endoscopic Mayo score 3, 95% corticosteroid-refractory, "| __truncated__ #>  $ dose_range                 : chr "5 mg/kg IV infliximab induction at weeks 0, 2, and 6 (one subject also received an additional dose at day 5)." #>  $ regions                    : chr "Single-center prospective cohort, Amsterdam, Netherlands." #>  $ albumin_median             : chr "38 g/L (range 23-45)" #>  $ crp_median                 : chr "25.3 mg/L (range 0.6-196.2)" #>  $ concomitant_thiopurines_pct: num 55 #>  $ sccai_median               : chr "10 (range 1-15)" #>  $ notes                      : chr "Anti-TNF naive patients with active disease at baseline. The dataset comprises 214 IFX serum and 214 TNF serum "| __truncated__

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Berends_2019_infliximab.R. table collects equation parameter provenance one place. Kss value 13.6 nM taken Results “Final model” paragraph (“Population steady state dissociation constant (Kss) 13.6 nM”), matches paper’s abstract consistent Table 2 bootstrap median (13.7 nM, 95 % CI 6.92-23.1). Table 2’s integer-rounded “14” treated display rounding.

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Original individual data publicly available. simulate typical 70 kg, ADA-negative subject 38 g/L albumin (cohort median) figure replication, self-consistent NCA single-dose run. paper’s figures show typical-value predictions, figure-replication simulations turn -subject variability via rxode2::zeroRe().
code: MW_IFX_kDa <- 149     # paper Methods MW_TNF_kDa <- 52      # paper Methods mg_per_kg  <- 5 wt_kg      <- 70      # cohort median (Table 1) alb_med    <- 38      # cohort median (Table 1) dose_mg    <- mg_per_kg * wt_kg  # Induction schedule used in the paper: induction_days <- c(0, 14, 42)   # week 0, 2, 6

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Berends_2019_infliximab") mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  obs_t <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 7, by = 0.25),                        seq(7.5, 90, by = 0.5),                        induction_days,                        induction_days + 0.001)))  build_events <- function(ada_status, alb_value) {   ev <- et(id = 1)   for (td in induction_days) {     ev <- et(ev, amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = td, id = 1)   }   ev <- et(ev, time = obs_t, cmt = "Cc",   id = 1)   ev <- et(ev, time = obs_t, cmt = "Rtot", id = 1)   list(ev = ev,        iCov = data.frame(id = 1, ALB = alb_value, ADA_POS = ada_status)) }  run_typical <- function(ada_status, alb_value) {   pkg <- build_events(ada_status, alb_value)   s <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, pkg$ev, iCov = pkg$iCov,                        returnType = "data.frame")   s[!duplicated(s$time), ] }  sim_neg_med <- run_typical(0, 38)   # ADA-, median ALB #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalBmax' sim_pos_med <- run_typical(1, 38)   # ADA+, median ALB #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalBmax' sim_neg_lo  <- run_typical(0, 23)   # ADA-, low ALB (cohort min) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalBmax' sim_neg_hi  <- run_typical(0, 45)   # ADA-, high ALB (cohort max) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalBmax'

what: Figure 2 — Predicted total TNF concentration over time
title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Figure 2 Berends 2019 shows model-predicted total TNF concentration rising induction (driven IFX-TNF complex accumulation, since internalization kint = 0.984/day much slower free-TNF degradation kdeg = 5.12/day) subsequently declining IFX clears Kss.
code: tnf_df <- bind_rows(   sim_neg_med |> transmute(time, Rtot_pgml,                             scenario = "ADA-, ALB 38 g/L (typical)"),   sim_pos_med |> transmute(time, Rtot_pgml,                             scenario = "ADA+, ALB 38 g/L"),   sim_neg_lo  |> transmute(time, Rtot_pgml,                             scenario = "ADA-, ALB 23 g/L (low)") )  ggplot(tnf_df, aes(time, Rtot_pgml, colour = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 19.8, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   labs(     x      = "Time (days)",     y      = "Total TNF concentration (pg/mL)",     colour = NULL,     title  = "Replicates Figure 2 of Berends 2019",     caption = paste("Dashed line = baseline TNF (Bmax = 19.8 pg/mL).",                     "Vertical accumulation reflects IFX-TNF complex carry-over",                     "while drug exceeds Kss.")   ) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: Figure 3 — Effect of ADA and albumin on infliximab clearance
title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Berends 2019 Figure 3 shows impact () serum albumin (b) anti-drug antibodies simulated IFX trough concentrations. reproduce equivalent comparison: trough week 6 (day 42, immediately third induction dose). Predicted week-6 trough IFX concentration covariate scenario. clearance ratios implied Table 2 : (ALB/38)^-1.13 -> ALB = 23 g/L gives 1.74 x typical CL; ALB = 45 g/L gives 0.81 x typical CL. ADA-positive multiplies CL 2.15. Lower albumin ADA-positive status reduce trough exposure, consistent Figure 3 Berends 2019.
code: trough_t <- 42 - 0.001  # immediately pre-dose at week 6  trough_at <- function(s, scenario_label) {   s |>     filter(abs(time - trough_t) < 0.5) |>     summarise(Cc_ugml = mean(Cc), Cc_nM = mean(Cc_nM),               scenario = scenario_label, .groups = "drop") }  trough_df <- bind_rows(   trough_at(sim_neg_med, "ADA-, ALB 38 g/L"),   trough_at(sim_neg_lo,  "ADA-, ALB 23 g/L"),   trough_at(sim_neg_hi,  "ADA-, ALB 45 g/L"),   trough_at(sim_pos_med, "ADA+, ALB 38 g/L") )  knitr::kable(trough_df, digits = 2,              caption = "Predicted week-6 trough IFX concentration under each covariate scenario.")

what: Figure 4 — Concentration-time profiles for IFX and TNF (typical patient)
title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Figure 4 Berends 2019 shows typical-value model-predicted IFX TNF time courses overlaid observed data induction period. reproduce equivalent typical-value lines 70 kg / 38 g/L albumin / ADA-negative subject.
code: plot_df <- sim_neg_med |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Rtot_pgml, free_TNF_pgml),                names_to = "panel", values_to = "value") |>   mutate(panel = recode(panel,                         Cc            = "IFX serum concentration (ug/mL)",                         Rtot_pgml     = "Total TNF concentration (pg/mL)",                         free_TNF_pgml = "Free TNF concentration (pg/mL)"))  ggplot(plot_df, aes(time, value)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, colour = "steelblue") +   facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = NULL,     title  = "Replicates Figure 4 of Berends 2019",     caption = "Typical 70 kg, ADA-negative, ALB 38 g/L. Doses at weeks 0, 2, 6."   )

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: paper publish formal NCA table IFX. perform self-consistency NCA simulated single 5 mg/kg IV dose typical ADA-negative subject (major TMDD-induced terminal-slope deviation since Bmax = 19.8 pg/mL far Kss = 13.6 nM = 2.0 ug/mL, target saturated duration measurable IFX). Dosing formula grouped treatment per skill’s PKNCA recipe. PKNCA summary single 5 mg/kg IV bolus (ADA-negative typical-value simulation; IFX ug/mL).
code: sd_obs_t <- sort(unique(c(0, 0.04, 0.083, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7,                           seq(10, 56, by = 2))))  ev_sd <- et(id = 1) |>   et(amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = 0, id = 1) |>   et(time = sd_obs_t, cmt = "Cc", id = 1)  icov_sd <- data.frame(id = 1, ALB = 38, ADA_POS = 0) sim_sd <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, ev_sd, iCov = icov_sd,                           returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalBmax' sim_sd <- sim_sd[!duplicated(sim_sd$time), ]  nca_conc <- sim_sd |>   filter(time > 0, Cc > 0) |>   transmute(id = 1L, time, Cc,             treatment = "5 mg/kg IV SD, ADA-, ALB 38")  dose_df <- data.frame(id = 1L, time = 0, amt = dose_mg,                       treatment = "5 mg/kg IV SD, ADA-, ALB 38")  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(start = 0, end = Inf,                         cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                         aucinf.obs = TRUE, half.life = TRUE)  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.04) is not allowed knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_res), digits = 3,              caption = "PKNCA summary for a single 5 mg/kg IV bolus (ADA-negative typical-value simulation; IFX in ug/mL).")

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
code:
# Hand-checked Cmax: dose / Vc gives mg/L = ug/mL directly. expected_cmax_ugml <- dose_mg / 3.18 cat(sprintf("Expected Cmax: %.2f ug/mL (= %.1f nM)
",             expected_cmax_ugml,             expected_cmax_ugml * 1000 / MW_IFX_kDa)) #> Expected Cmax: 110.06 ug/mL (= 738.7 nM)  # Linear half-life ignoring TMDD. t_half_linear <- log(2) * 3.18 / 0.404 cat(sprintf("Linear t1/2 (= ln(2) * Vc / CL): %.2f days
", t_half_linear)) #> Linear t1/2 (= ln(2) * Vc / CL): 5.46 days  # Bmax dimensional spot-check. bmax_ugml <- 5.662e-5 cat(sprintf("Bmax check: %.4e ug/mL x 52/149 x 1e6 = %.2f pg/mL (paper 19.8)
",             bmax_ugml, bmax_ugml * MW_TNF_kDa / MW_IFX_kDa * 1e6)) #> Bmax check: 5.6620e-05 ug/mL x 52/149 x 1e6 = 19.76 pg/mL (paper 19.8)

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Working concentration unit. Drug central mg, Cc = central / Vc natively mg/L = ug/mL. TMDD-QSS arithmetic requires drug, target, Kss molar scale; paper’s nM values Kss pM/pg/mL values TNF stored IFX-mass-equivalent ug/mL using MW_IFX = 149 kDa: Kss = 13.6 nM × 149/1000 = 2.0264 ug/mL, Bmax = 0.38 pM × 149/1000 = 5.662e-5 ug/mL. display variables Rtot_pgml free_TNF_pgml apply inverse conversion (using MW_TNF = 52 kDa) recover paper’s TNF units. convention follows Valenzuela_2025_nipocalimab.R. Kss value. Table 2 reports integer-rounded Estimate 14 nM, Results “Final model” paragraph abstract quote 13.6 nM, bootstrap median 13.7 nM (95 % CI 6.92-23.1). use 13.6 nM (text bootstrap-consistent value). CL-Vc covariance. Table 2’s “Cov. CL-Vc (%) 12.3” interpreted correlation coefficient (rho = 0.123) rather literal covariance, reading 12.3 directly covariance gives Cauchy-Schwarz violation (|rho| > 1). -diagonal etalcl + etalvc therefore rho * sqrt(omega2_CL * omega2_Vc) = 0.123 * sqrt(0.08185 * 0.05022) = 0.007884. kdeg fixed. paper fixes kdeg = 5.12 /day sensitivity analysis (Supplementary Table 1) data jointly identify Kss, kint, kdeg. model wraps parameter fixed() downstream re-estimation respects constraint. race / body-weight covariate. paper retained ADA albumin CL; body weight, age, sex, CRP, calprotectin, disease extent, hospitalization, concomitant thiopurines retained PK parameter. impose allometric scaling. Single-center cohort. data come single Amsterdam center (n = 20). Predictions outside demographic envelope (children, high body weights, patients non-UC IBD) extrapolations fitted parameters constrained . Dosing regimen. vignette uses paper’s induction-schedule (weeks 0, 2, 6); maintenance dosing (q8w week 14) included analysis dataset therefore validated . Free-TNF readout. model exposes total TNF (Rtot, Rtot_pgml) free TNF (free_TNF_pgml = (Rtot - complex) × MW_TNF/MW_IFX × 1e6). paper measured free TNF using Singulex ultrasensitive immunoassay; Rtot state total (free + bound) pool definition (Eq. 13).

title: Berends_2019_infliximab
text: Berends SE, Strik , Van Selm S, Lowenberg M, Ponsioen CY, D’Haens GR, Mathot RAA. Tumor necrosis factor-mediated disposition infliximab ulcerative colitis patients. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2019;46(6):543-551. doi:10.1007/s10928-019-09652-5

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: Citation: Brillac C, Semiond D, Oprea C, Baruchel , Zwaan CM, Nguyen L. Selection isatuximab dosing regimen pediatric patients leukemia using population pharmacokinetics. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2025;95:116. doi:[10.1007/s00280-025-04832-2](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00280-025-04832-2) Description: Two-compartment population PK model linear elimination central compartment isatuximab pediatric adult patients relapsed/refractory acute leukemias. Modality: Therapeutic monoclonal antibody (anti-CD38 IgG), IV infusion 20 mg/kg. Brillac 2025 develops pooled adult+pediatric population PK model isatuximab support dose selection phase 2 ISAKIDS study (NCT03860844, pediatric R/R /AML) combining pediatric data adult data phase 2 ISLAY study (NCT02999633, adult R/R T-T-cell lymphoblastic lymphoma). Body weight covariate retained final model; allometric power scaling applied clearance volumes distribution, exponent intercompartmental clearance Q held fixed 0.85 poorly estimated freed. structural ODE system reproduced paper’s Methods : dA1dt=K⋅Dose+k21A2−(CLV1+k12)A1dA2dt=k12A1−k21A2 \frac{dA_1}{dt} \;=\; K \cdot \mathrm{Dose} \;+\; k_{21} A_2 \;-\; \left(\frac{\mathrm{CL}}{V_1} + k_{12}\right) A_1 \qquad \frac{dA_2}{dt} \;=\; k_{12} A_1 \;-\; k_{21} A_2 A1A_1, A2A_2 central- peripheral-compartment amounts, CL linear clearance, V1V_1 central volume, k12k_{12}, k21k_{21} inter-compartment rate constants, KK infusion rate constant. packaged model carries infusion via event-table RATE (DUR) column central-compartment dose row.

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: final population PK dataset comprised 79 patients pooling 65 pediatric patients ISAKIDS 14 adult patients ISLAY, contributing 674 plasma concentrations (555 pediatric + 119 adult, ~8 observations per patient). Pediatric subset (Brillac 2025 Results / Patients): Median age 8.0 years (range 1.4-17.0); one patient younger 24 months. Median weight 32.5 kg (range 8.8-108.0). 61.5% male. Cohort split: AML n = 26, B-n = 27, T-n = 12. Adult subset (Brillac 2025 Results / Database): 14 patients, age 16-74 years, weight 46-93 kg, R/R T-T-cell lymphoblastic lymphoma. Pooled-dataset covariates summarized Brillac 2025 Figure 1 (weight, age, weight-vs-age scatter); population median weight 38 kg reference allometric scaling final model. patients received isatuximab 20 mg/kg IV infusion. schedule QW first 5 doses (D1, D8, D15, D22, D29) followed Q2W (D43, D57); AML schedule QW × 3 cycle 1 optional cycle 2. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Brillac_2025_isatuximab")$population.

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Brillac_2025_isatuximab.R. table collects one place review. Reference covariates: body weight 38 kg (population median pooled adult+pediatric dataset; Brillac 2025 Table 1 footnote Results “patient median weight 38 kg”).

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use four virtual weight cohorts matching groupings used Brillac 2025 Figure 4 (9-12 kg, 13-18 kg, 19-50 kg, 51-100 kg). Within cohort, body weights drawn uniform distribution across cohort’s weight band; covariate- distribution approximation, literal reproduction individual study patient. dosing schedule uses design (Brillac 2025 Methods): 20 mg/kg IV infusion (1-hour) D1, D8, D15, D22, D29 induction, D43 D57 consolidation. Observation grid daily day 70.
code: set.seed(2025) n_per_group <- 100  make_cohort <- function(n, wt_lo, wt_hi, label, id_offset) {   tibble(     id   = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT   = stats::runif(n, wt_lo, wt_hi),     cohort = label   ) }  cohorts <- bind_rows(   make_cohort(n_per_group,  9,  12, "9-12 kg",   id_offset = 0L),   make_cohort(n_per_group, 13,  18, "13-18 kg",  id_offset = 1L * n_per_group),   make_cohort(n_per_group, 19,  50, "19-50 kg",  id_offset = 2L * n_per_group),   make_cohort(n_per_group, 51, 100, "51-100 kg", id_offset = 3L * n_per_group) ) infusion_d  <- 1 / 24 dose_times_d <- c(0, 7, 14, 21, 28, 42, 56) obs_times_d  <- sort(unique(c(dose_times_d, seq(0, 70, by = 1))))  build_events <- function(pop) {   amt_per_subject <- pop$WT * 20   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(amt = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times_d) |>     mutate(evid = 1L,            cmt  = "central",            dur  = infusion_d,            dv   = NA_real_)   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times_d) |>     mutate(amt  = NA_real_,            evid = 0L,            cmt  = "central",            dur  = NA_real_,            dv   = NA_real_)   bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events <- build_events(cohorts) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: Deterministic typical-value (etas zeroed) profiles useful reproducing model-implied summary curves:
code: mod <- readModelDb("Brillac_2025_isatuximab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = c("cohort"),                        returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = events, keep = c("cohort"),                            returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: figure summarises simulated isatuximab concentrations four Brillac 2025 weight groups induction + consolidation period (D1-D70). simulation-side analogue Brillac 2025 Supplementary Fig. 6 (visual predictive checks weight group): heaviest cohort (51-100 kg) sits lowest weight- normalized exposure lightest (9-12 kg) highest.
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, cohort) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  cohort_order <- c("9-12 kg", "13-18 kg", "19-50 kg", "51-100 kg") sim_summary <- sim_summary |>   dplyr::mutate(cohort = factor(cohort, levels = cohort_order))  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = cohort, fill = cohort)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Isatuximab concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     title = "Simulated isatuximab PK by weight group (ALL design)",     subtitle = paste0(       "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ", n_per_group,       " virtual patients per group). Replicates Brillac 2025 Suppl. Fig. 6 layout."     ),     caption = "Model: Brillac 2025 Cancer Chemother Pharmacol 95:116"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Replicates Figure 4: cumulative AUC by weight group
title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: Brillac 2025 Figure 4 shows median cumulative AUC treatment period decreases approximately 30% 9-12 kg weight group relative 51-100 kg reference adult group, substantial overlap distributions. figure reproduces qualitative shape: cumulative AUC drops monotonically body weight WT-power model, lightest band sitting ~30% heaviest band linear scale.
code: auc_window_d <- 70  auc_by_subject <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= auc_window_d, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, cohort) |>   dplyr::summarise(     auc_total = sum((Cc + dplyr::lag(Cc)) / 2 * (time - dplyr::lag(time)),                     na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(cohort = factor(cohort, levels = cohort_order))  ref_median <- auc_by_subject |>   dplyr::filter(cohort == "51-100 kg") |>   dplyr::pull(auc_total) |>   stats::median(na.rm = TRUE)  auc_by_subject <- auc_by_subject |>   dplyr::mutate(rel_to_ref = auc_total / ref_median)  ggplot(auc_by_subject, aes(cohort, auc_total)) +   geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.4) +   labs(     x = "Body weight group",     y = "Cumulative AUC over ALL treatment (ug*day/mL)",     title = "Cumulative AUC by weight group (ALL design, D1-D70)",     subtitle = "Replicates Figure 4(a) of Brillac 2025: ~30% lower median in 9-12 kg vs reference 51-100 kg",     caption = paste0(       "Median ratios vs 51-100 kg: ",       paste(         sprintf("%s = %.0f%%",                 levels(auc_by_subject$cohort),                 100 * tapply(auc_by_subject$rel_to_ref,                              auc_by_subject$cohort,                              stats::median,                              na.rm = TRUE)),         collapse = "; "       )     )   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: Compute first-dose NCA parameters (Cmax, Tmax, AUClast, half-life) weight group. first dose interval days 0-7 (single-dose window second QW dose); PKNCA’s auclast integrates trapezoidally last quantifiable concentration. treatment grouping (cohort) included per-group summary can compared Brillac 2025 Supplementary Fig. 9 (boxplots first-dose Cmax / AUC weight group). Simulated single-dose NCA parameters weight group (days 0-7)
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 0, time <= 7) |>   dplyr::select(id, cohort, time, Cc)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | cohort + id)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, cohort, time, amt)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | cohort + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = 7,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■               60% |  ETA:  2s  knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   caption = "Simulated single-dose NCA parameters by weight group (days 0-7)" )

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: Brillac 2025 tabulate per-cohort NCA summary, paper provide several model-derived population statistics can cross-checked packaged model. pediatric individual-EBE half-life 23-28 days reported paper agrees typical-value calculation packaged parameters 38 kg (~22 days). adult mean CL reported paper (~0.365 L/day) EBE-derived sample mean incorporates -subject variability; model’s typical-value CL 70 kg 0.224 L/day -predicts observed mean ~40%, consistent high reported ω(CL) 62.3% small adult sample size (n = 14). Differences within ~20% typical value expected; larger discrepancies trace per-subject EBE variability rather coding error.

title: Brillac_2025_isatuximab
text: Time units. Brillac 2025 reports clearance intercompartmental clearance L/h; packaged model keeps time days consistency half-life convention used elsewhere nlmixr2lib’s mAb library, CL Q converted via × 24. IIV interpretation. Brillac 2025 Methods state “ω thus approximate coefficient variation” exponential random-effects model parameter_i = TV * exp(eta_i). packaged model interprets percentages reported Table 1 SD eta log scale stores omega² = (%/100)² ini(), line Monolix reports ω. IIV structure. Brillac 2025 Methods state Ω modelled diagonal (covariance etas); packaged model uses four independent eta parameters accordingly. Single covariate. Body weight covariate retained published final model; paper’s Methods note age, albumin, sex tested found statistically significant weight included. None covariates implemented . depot compartment. Isatuximab administered IV infusion ; packaged model depot compartment dose enters central directly, consistent Brillac 2025’s published ODE system (input K * Dose supplied via event-table RATE/DUR column central-compartment dose row). Virtual cohort weight bands. four cohorts approximate weight bands used Brillac 2025 Figure 4. Within band weights sampled uniformly; paper’s actual within-cohort weight distribution reported. Adult sex distribution. paper reports 61.5% male 65 pediatric patients evaluable PK; separate adult-cohort sex breakdown given. population sex_female_pct field (38.5%) reflects pediatric figure; assumption recorded population$notes. Race / ethnicity. reported Brillac 2025; recorded “reported” population$race_ethnicity. Race / ethnicity tested covariate published model.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Simulate tislelizumab (anti-PD-1 IgG4) concentration-time profiles using final three-compartment population PK model Budha et al. (2023). pooled analysis included 2596 patients advanced tumors 12 studies (14,473 serum concentration observations). structural model three-compartment IV model linear elimination. Final covariates: body weight, albumin, baseline tumor size, ADA status, tumor-type indicators classical Hodgkin lymphoma gastric cancer CL; body weight, age, sex Vc. Tislelizumab PK linear across 0.5–10 mg/kg dose range, approved dose 200 mg IV every 3 weeks.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: See inst/references/covariate-columns.md canonical register.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Approximate Budha 2023 Table 2 distributions pooled 2596-patient cohort (medians ranges — paper publish individual covariates covariate correlations).
code: set.seed(2023) n_subj <- 500  # Weight and age: log-normal / normal fits to reported medians and ranges WT  <- pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(65), 0.20), 31.9), 130) AGE <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 60, 12), 18), 90)  # Albumin (g/L): normal around median, clipped to observed range ALB <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 41, 5), 17), 61.3)  # Tumor size (mm): log-normal around median 63.3, clipped to observed range TUMSZ <- pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(63), 0.75), 10), 408)  # Sex: 26.0% female SEXF <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.26)  # ADA: 16.6% positive (of the 98.9% non-missing; treat missing as negative here) ADA_POS <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.166)  # Tumor type: sample from Table 2 proportions; only cHL and GC get indicators tumtp_p <- c(   cHL   = 0.027,   CRC   = 0.031,   EC    = 0.144,   GC    = 0.039,   HCC   = 0.122,   NPC   = 0.008,   NSCLC = 0.443,   OC    = 0.020,   UC    = 0.058,   Other = 0.108 ) TUMTP <- sample(names(tumtp_p), n_subj, replace = TRUE, prob = tumtp_p) TUMTP_CHL <- as.integer(TUMTP == "cHL") TUMTP_GC  <- as.integer(TUMTP == "GC")  pop <- data.frame(   ID        = seq_len(n_subj),   WT, AGE, ALB, TUMSZ,   SEXF, ADA_POS,   TUMTP, TUMTP_CHL, TUMTP_GC,   # Per-subject grouping label carried through to rxSolve via `keep`   treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative") )

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Approved flat regimen: 200 mg IV q3w. Simulate 10 cycles (≈ 30 weeks) followed observation tail capture terminal disposition.
code: cycle_days <- 21 n_cycles   <- 10 dose_times <- seq(0, (n_cycles - 1) * cycle_days, by = cycle_days)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 21, by = 0.5),                     # intensive profile through cycle 1   seq(21, n_cycles * cycle_days, by = 3),   # pre/post dose through multi-dose   seq(n_cycles * cycle_days, 365, by = 7)   # washout tail )))  d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(     AMT  = 200,          # flat 200 mg     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     DUR  = 30 / (60 * 24), # 30-minute IV infusion in days     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DUR  = NA_real_,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>   as.data.frame()

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Budha_2023_tislelizumab") set.seed(20230331) # `keep = "treatment"` carries the ADA grouping label through to the # simulation output so the NCA blocks below do not need a post-hoc # left_join from `pop`. sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame", keep = "treatment") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Tislelizumab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Simulated tislelizumab PK, 200 mg IV q3w x 10 cycles",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual patients)",     caption = "Model: Budha et al. (2023) CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Effect of ADA status on PK
title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
code: sim_ada <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(ADA_label = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, ADA_label) |>   dplyr::summarise(median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  ggplot(sim_ada, aes(x = time / 7, y = median, color = ADA_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("ADA-negative" = "steelblue", "ADA-positive" = "tomato")) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Tislelizumab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Tislelizumab median concentrations by ADA status",     subtitle = "ADA-positive patients have ~11.7% higher CL (exp(0.111) - 1)",     color = "Immunogenicity",     caption = "Model: Budha et al. (2023)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Run PKNCA first dosing interval (day 0–21) pseudo-steady-state interval (cycle 10, days 189–210), stratified ADA status. Terminal half-life expected approximate paper’s reported ≈ 23.8 days (Budha 2023 Methods) ADA-negative arm. Cycle 1 NCA summary (days 0-21), stratified ADA status Cycle 10 NCA summary (days 189-210, pseudo-steady state), stratified ADA status
code: # `treatment` was carried through rxSolve via `keep = "treatment"` above; # no left_join needed here.  # Cycle 1 interval conc1 <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= 21, Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::rename(ID = id) |>   dplyr::transmute(ID, time_rel = time, Cc, treatment, interval = "Cycle 1")  dose1 <- pop |>   dplyr::transmute(ID, time_rel = 0, AMT = 200, treatment, interval = "Cycle 1")  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(conc1, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(dose1, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) nca_data <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj, dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 21,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range not allowed knitr::kable(   summary(nca_results),   digits  = 2,   caption = "Cycle 1 NCA summary (days 0-21), stratified by ADA status" ) # Steady-state-ish interval at cycle 10 (day 189-210) # `treatment` already on `sim` via rxSolve's `keep` argument. conc_ss <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 189, time <= 210, Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::rename(ID = id) |>   dplyr::transmute(ID, time_rel = time - 189, Cc, treatment)  dose_ss <- pop |>   dplyr::transmute(ID, time_rel = 0, AMT = 200, treatment)  conc_obj_ss <- PKNCAconc(conc_ss, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj_ss <- PKNCAdose(dose_ss, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) nca_data_ss <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj_ss, dose_obj_ss,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 21,     cmax      = TRUE,     cmin      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE   ) ) nca_results_ss <- pk.nca(nca_data_ss) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                58% |  ETA:  2s knitr::kable(   summary(nca_results_ss),   digits  = 2,   caption = "Cycle 10 NCA summary (days 189-210, pseudo-steady state), stratified by ADA status" )

what: Volume of distribution at steady state
title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: paper reports Vss = 6.42 L (Budha 2023 Results). nlmixr2 model, Vss simply sum central peripheral volumes typical covariates:
code: vss <- 3.05 + 1.27 + 2.10 cat(sprintf("Typical-subject Vss = %.2f L (Budha 2023 reports 6.42 L)\n", vss)) #> Typical-subject Vss = 6.42 L (Budha 2023 reports 6.42 L)

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Budha 2023 publish individual PK covariate tables, virtual population approximates Table 2 medians ranges: Weight: log-normal, median 65 kg, clipped 31.9–130 kg. Age: normal, median 60 y, clipped 18–90 y. Albumin: normal, median 41 g/L, clipped 17–61.3 g/L. paper uses g/L (SI), g/dL. Tumor size: log-normal, median 63.3 mm (matched Budha 2023 Table 2 median 63.3 mm), clipped 10–408 mm. Sex: 26% female (Budha 2023 Table 2). ADA status: 16.6% positive (Budha 2023 Table 2; 1.1% missing re-coded negative simulation). Tumor-type mix: sampled independently Table 2 proportions; cHL (2.7%) GC (3.9%) get indicator columns — types reference. covariate correlations imposed (paper report ). Dose regimen uses approved 200 mg q3w flat dose (Budha 2023 Methods label); model also valid 0.5–10 mg/kg per PopPK dataset. Infusion duration set 30 min (clinical administration); final PopPK model treats infusions bolus deriving concentration-time profiles study EDC.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Structure: three-compartment IV linear elimination. Reference covariates: WT 65 kg, AGE 60 y, ALB 41 g/L, TUMSZ 63 mm, male, ADA-negative, non-cHL / non-GC tumor. Strongest covariates CL (Budha 2023 Figures 6–7, clinically meaningful): ±20% range WT (0.565 exponent) ALB (-0.457 exponent). cHL patients ~19% lower CL (exp(-0.216) = 0.806) reference tumor mix. Shrinkage V2 V3 IIV high (55.8%, 44.4%). paper retains etas; implementation follows paper.

title: Budha_2023_tislelizumab
text: Budha N, Wu CY, Tang Z, et al. Model-based population pharmacokinetic analysis tislelizumab patients advanced tumors. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2023;12(1):95-109. doi:10.1002/psp4.12880

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Citation: Carlsson Petri KC, Hale PM, Hesse D, Rathor N, Mastrandrea LD. Liraglutide pharmacokinetics exposure-response adolescents obesity. Pediatric Obesity. 2021;16(10):e12799. doi:10.1111/ijpo.12799 Description: Liraglutide PK model adolescents (Carlsson Petri 2021) Article: Pediatric Obesity 2021;16(10):e12799 Open-access PMC copy: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8519033/

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: published analysis pooled 176 subjects across four Novo Nordisk trials liraglutide (Saxenda, 3.0 mg maintenance -daily subcutaneous): 121 adolescents phase 3a trial NN2211-4180 (primary population), 13 adolescents phase 1 trial NN2211-3967, 13 children NN2211-4181, 29 adults NN2211-3630 (Carlsson Petri 2021 Methods). adolescent phase 3a population (Table 1) 55.4% female (67/121), 84.3% White / 10.7% Black / 1.7% Asian / 3.3% , mean body weight 99.4 kg (SD 19.7, range 62.1-178.2 kg) mean age 14.6 years (SD 1.6, range 12-17). 107/121 adolescents reached 3.0 mg maintenance dose weekly escalation (0.6-1.2-1.8-2.4-3.0 mg). Age cutoffs used define CHILD / ADOLESCENT indicators children 7-11 y, adolescents 12-17 y, adults >=18 y. information available programmatically via readModelDb("CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide")$population.

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide.R. table collects review.

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Carlsson Petri 2021 Table 1 adolescent phase 3a (trial NN2211-4180) demographics: 55.4% female, body weight ~ Normal(99.4, 19.7) truncated reported 62.1-178.2 kg range, CHILD = 0 ADOLESCENT = 1. subjects receive 3.0 mg -daily SC maintenance dose (escalation, match 107/121 “maintenance” regimen). Liraglutide molar mass 3751.2 g/mol (C172H265N43O51); doses entered nmol simulated Cc = central / Vc directly nmol/L (matching paper’s reported concentration units). event table -daily SC dosing four weeks (sampling every hour last dosing interval capture steady-state shape) constructed .
code: set.seed(20210609) # publication date reference n_subj <- 400  # Liraglutide molar mass (g/mol). 3 mg = 3e-3 / 3751.2 mol = 7.997e-7 mol = 799.7 nmol. lira_mw <- 3751.2 dose_mg <- 3.0 dose_nmol <- dose_mg * 1e6 / lira_mw # convert mg to nmol: 1 mg = (1 / MW) * 1e6 nmol  cohort <- tibble(   id          = seq_len(n_subj),   SEXF        = as.integer(runif(n_subj) < 0.554),   WT          = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 99.4, sd = 19.7), 62.1), 178.2),   CHILD       = 0L,   ADOLESCENT  = 1L,   treatment   = factor("3 mg QD (adolescent)") ) sim_days <- 28 tau <- 24 # dosing interval (h) n_doses <- sim_days # one dose per day dose_times <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)  # Dense sampling on the final dosing interval (for Cmax / Tmax / AUCtau), # plus coarse sampling earlier (for visual steady-state approach). final_dose_time <- dose_times[n_doses] obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, final_dose_time, by = 6), # every 6 h across the run-in   final_dose_time + c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24) )))  dose_rows <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_nmol, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)  obs_rows <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, SEXF, WT, CHILD, ADOLESCENT, treatment) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' conc_unit <- mod$units[["concentration"]] sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = events,   keep = c("SEXF", "WT", "CHILD", "ADOLESCENT", "treatment") )

what: Typical steady-state concentration profile over the last 24 h
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 publish concentration-time figure 3.0 mg adolescent dose, report exposure metric Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24 h) steady state. deterministic (“typical”) profile zeros random effects median trajectory reference 100 kg female adolescent visible.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cohort <- tibble(   id = 1,   SEXF = 1L, WT = 100, CHILD = 0L, ADOLESCENT = 1L,   treatment = factor("Typical adolescent, 100 kg female, 3 mg QD") )  typical_doses <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_nmol, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)  typical_obs <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = seq(0, final_dose_time + tau, by = 0.5)) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  typical_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(typical_doses, typical_obs) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, SEXF, WT, CHILD, ADOLESCENT, treatment) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = typical_events,   keep = c("SEXF", "WT", "CHILD", "ADOLESCENT", "treatment") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc'  sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = paste0("Liraglutide concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Typical adolescent: 3 mg QD SC, 100 kg female",     caption = "Deterministic typical-value prediction.") +   theme_minimal()

what: VPC-style median and 5-95 percentiles over the 28-day run-in
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: stochastic cohort summary (median 5th 95th percentiles across 400 simulated adolescents) replicates type summary shown paper’s Figure 3 (exposure metrics vs. body weight / age group).
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = paste0("Liraglutide concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles across adolescent cohort (3 mg QD SC)",     caption = "Cohort covariates approximate Carlsson Petri 2021 Table 1 (trial NN2211-4180).")

what: Exposure vs. body weight at steady state
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 Figure 3A shows individual steady-state average concentration decreases increasing body weight (consistent positive allometric exponents CL V, greater effect CL). replicate relationship computing simulated subject’s Cavg final dosing interval.
code: cavg_by_id <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= final_dose_time, time <= final_dose_time + tau,     !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, WT, SEXF) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Cavg_nmol_L = mean(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(Sex = ifelse(SEXF == 1L, "Female", "Male"))  ggplot(cavg_by_id, aes(WT, Cavg_nmol_L, colour = Sex)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +   labs(x = "Body weight (kg)", y = paste0("C_avg,ss (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Steady-state Cavg vs. body weight",     caption = "Replicates the shape of Carlsson Petri 2021 Figure 3A (adolescents, 3 mg QD).") +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, C_tau, C_avg, AUClast using PKNCA final dosing interval steady state (recipe 3). treatment grouping variable (treatment) placed id formula per project convention. Steady-state NCA final 24-h dosing interval (3 mg QD SC adolescent cohort).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= final_dose_time, time <= final_dose_time + tau) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - final_dose_time) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_rel, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == final_dose_time) |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = tau,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),   caption = "Steady-state NCA at the final 24-h dosing interval (3 mg QD SC adolescent cohort).")

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 reports steady-state Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24) individual (Figure 3A), tabulated population mean; however, reference female adult (CL/F typical = 1.01 L/h), predicted Cavg 3.0 mg dose : Cavg_ref = 3 mg / (1.01 L/h * 24 h) = 0.1238 mg/L = 33.0 nmol/L (liraglutide MW = 3751.2 g/mol) typical 100 kg female adolescent, model adds 1.06x age factor CL (adolescent/adult), typical CL = 1.01 * (100/100)^0.762 * 1.12^0 * 1.06 = 1.0706 L/h, giving predicted typical Cavg 3 / (1.0706 * 24) * 1e6 / 3751.2 = 31.1 nmol/L. Predicted steady-state Cavg 3 mg QD adolescent cohort.
code: typical_cl <- 1.01 * (100 / 100)^0.762 * 1.12^(1 - 1) * 1.06^1 # female (SEXF=1), 100 kg, ADOLESCENT typical_cavg_mg_L <- dose_mg / (typical_cl * 24) typical_cavg_nmol_L <- typical_cavg_mg_L * 1e6 / lira_mw  # Median simulated Cavg from the stochastic cohort sim_cavg <- cavg_by_id |>   dplyr::summarise(median_Cavg_nmol_L = median(Cavg_nmol_L),     q05 = quantile(Cavg_nmol_L, 0.05),     q95 = quantile(Cavg_nmol_L, 0.95))  compare_tbl <- tibble::tibble(   Source = c("Typical-value closed form",     "Simulated cohort (median)",     "Simulated cohort (90% range)"),   Cavg_nmol_L = c(sprintf("%.1f", typical_cavg_nmol_L),     sprintf("%.1f", sim_cavg$median_Cavg_nmol_L),     sprintf("%.1f - %.1f", sim_cavg$q05, sim_cavg$q95)) )  knitr::kable(compare_tbl,   caption = "Predicted steady-state Cavg for the 3 mg QD adolescent cohort.")

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: published analysis covariate SEXM (1 = male, 0 = female) translated canonical SEXF (1 = female). sex effect CL encoded e_sex_cl^(1 - SEXF) males retain 1.12x factor Table 3 females reference (factor = 1). previous implementation (1 - SEXF)^e_sex_cl bug: females (SEXF = 1), 0^1.12 = 0 zeroed CL. 100, correct transformation isomega^2 = log(1 + CV^2). fixed model file; see -file comment onetalcl/etalvc`. age-effect encoding previously CHILD^e_age_child_cl * ADOLESCENT^e_age_adolescent_cl, evaluates 0^1.11 * 0^1.06 = 0 adults (indicators 0). corrected form e_age_child_cl^CHILD * e_age_adolescent_cl^ADOLESCENT, correctly evaluates 1 adults, 1.06 adolescents, 1.11 children. simulated cohort uses NN2211-4180 adolescent phase 3a demographics (weight, sex). Race/ethnicity model covariate therefore reproduced. simulated subjects “adolescents 3.0 mg maintenance” match dominant 107/121 regimen; weekly dose escalation reproduced paper’s steady-state exposure metric concerns maintenance dose. Liraglutide molar mass (3751.2 g/mol) used convert mg dose input nmol amount needed Cc nmol/L. source paper’s LLOQ 0.03 nmol/L. deterministic typical-value check agrees closed-form Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24) within rounding; stochastic cohort median tracks typical-value prediction within ~10% across sampled weight distribution.

what: Nimotuzumab semi-mechanistic QSS TMDD population PK model (Castro-Suárez 2020)
title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: de Castro-Suárez et al. (2020) developed semi-mechanistic two-compartment population pharmacokinetic model nimotuzumab (humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody EGFR, intermediate affinity Kd ≈ 2.1 × 10⁻⁸ M) 20 adults autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease (ADPKD) following single 30-min intravenous infusion one four fixed dose levels (50, 100, 200, 400 mg, n = 5 per cohort). non-linear PK described quasi-steady-state target-mediated drug disposition (QSS TMDD) framework EGFR binding represented central (Rtot) peripheral (Rtotp) compartments turnover mediator stimulates non-specific clearance via sigmoid Emax free central nimotuzumab. Covariates retained final model. vignette documents parameter provenance, replicates typical PK profiles shown Figure 4 paper across experimental extrapolated dose range (50–1200 mg), runs PKNCA virtual cohort provide dose-stratified Cmax / AUC / half-life summaries.

previous_headings: Nimotuzumab semi-mechanistic QSS TMDD population PK model (Castro-Suárez 2020)
title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Castro-Suárez 2020 Table 1 (single-center Cuban phase trial, n = 20): Body weight, height, age, BSA, CrCL, serum creatinine, total kidney volume (TKV), total cyst volume (TCV), sex, race tested covariates stepwise covariate-search procedure (forward p < 0.05, backward p < 0.01); none retained statistically significant PK parameter. Programmatically: readModelDb("Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab") carries metadata meta$population field returned rxode2 UI object (via model’s population element model function called directly).

previous_headings: Nimotuzumab semi-mechanistic QSS TMDD population PK model (Castro-Suárez 2020)
title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Every numeric value inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab.R comes following locations de Castro-Suárez N, Trame MN, Mangas-Sanjuan V, et al., Pharmaceutics 2020;12(12):1147 (doi:10.3390/pharmaceutics12121147). errata located PubMed, MDPI landing page, Google Scholar (search dates 2026-04-25). ODE system implemented inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab.R mathematically equivalent paper’s Eqs. (1)–(3): model file tracks total drug amount uses closed-form QSS algebraic solution cfree = ½·((C_total − R − Kss) + √((C_total − R − Kss)² + 4·Kss·C_total)) (Gibiansky et al. 2008) free concentration compartment. paper’s published form (A1 free amount dilution factor (1 + R·Kss/(Kss + A1/V)²) denominator) implemented form (total amount implicit free-conc solution) yield identical observable total nimotuzumab concentrations dilution factor exactly d(C_total)/d(C_free) QSS.

previous_headings: Nimotuzumab semi-mechanistic QSS TMDD population PK model (Castro-Suárez 2020)
title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Castro-Suárez trial enrolled n = 5 patients per dose cohort; stable PKNCA validation simulate larger virtual ADPKD cohort (n = 100 per dose level) drawing body weight log-normal distribution anchored published median weight (65.7 kg, SD 14.69 kg → CV ≈ 22%) clipped clinically plausible adult range. Body weight enters model via population metadata (final model carries allometric covariate effect), weight variability propagate Cc; included realism cohort summary. Inter-individual variability flows two random effects retained final model, η Rtotp (135% CV) η Kout (197% CV).
code: set.seed(20201201) n_per_dose <- 100 dose_levels <- c(50, 100, 200, 400, 800, 1200)  make_cohort <- function(dose_mg, n, id_offset = 0L) {   # Body weight: log-normal, median 65.7 kg, CV 22%, clipped to 38-110 kg.   wt <- pmin(110, pmax(38, exp(log(65.7) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.22))))   tibble(     id   = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT   = wt,     DOSE = dose_mg   ) }  pop <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_along(dose_levels), function(k) {   make_cohort(dose_levels[k], n_per_dose, id_offset = (k - 1L) * n_per_dose) }))  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(pop$id)) table(pop$DOSE) #>  #>   50  100  200  400  800 1200  #>  100  100  100  100  100  100

previous_headings: Nimotuzumab semi-mechanistic QSS TMDD population PK model (Castro-Suárez 2020)
title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Single 30-min IV infusion cohort dose; observation grid spans 0 1000 h mirror simulation window Castro-Suárez Figure 4.
code: infusion_h <- 0.5 obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 0.5),   seq(24, 168, by = 2),   seq(168, 1000, by = 6) )))  d_dose <- pop |>   mutate(     TIME = 0,     AMT  = DOSE,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     RATE = DOSE / infusion_h,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     RATE = 0,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(id, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(id, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, RATE, DV, WT, DOSE)  stopifnot(sum(d_sim$EVID == 1) == nrow(pop))

title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Two passes: stochastic simulation full IIV (η Rtotp Kout) plus 48% proportional residual error VPC-style summaries PKNCA, typical-value simulation rxode2::zeroRe() deterministic profiles Figure 4.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab")  set.seed(20201202) sim_full <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalrtotp', 'etalkout' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 4 — typical PK profiles by dose (50–1200 mg)
title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Castro-Suárez Figure 4 plots typical (zero-RE) concentrations nimotuzumab central (panel ), peripheral (panel b), mediator (panel c) compartments single dose 50, 100, 200, 400, 800, 1200 mg. reproductions use dose grid 1000-h horizon.  simulated 50 mg curve shows faster early decline visible published Figure 4A, providing visual support V1 × (1 − 0.53) interpretation Table 2 row “V1 change (D = 50 mg) [%] = 53” (see Errata section rationale remaining ambiguity).   dose-dependent mediator activation reproduces Castro-Suárez §3.3: maximum effect mediator approached 100 mg (S50 = 8.57 mg/L), diminishing additional return 200–1200 mg.
code: sim_typ_one <- sim_typ |>   group_by(DOSE) |>   filter(id == min(id)) |>   ungroup() |>   mutate(dose_label = factor(paste(DOSE, "mg"), levels = paste(dose_levels, "mg")))  ggplot(sim_typ_one, aes(time, Cc, colour = dose_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (h)",     y = "Total nimotuzumab Cc (mg/L)",     colour = "Dose",     title = "Figure 4(a)-equivalent — central concentration vs. time",     caption = "Replicates Figure 4A of Castro-Suárez 2020 — central compartment concentration following a single IV infusion of 50, 100, 200, 400, 800, or 1200 mg nimotuzumab in an ADPKD patient."   ) +   theme_bw() #> Warning in scale_y_log10(): log-10 transformation introduced #> infinite values. ggplot(sim_typ_one, aes(time, peripheral1 / 9.92e-3, colour = dose_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (h)",     y = "Peripheral total nimotuzumab (mg/L)",     colour = "Dose",     title = "Figure 4(b)-equivalent — peripheral compartment",     caption = "Replicates Figure 4(b) of Castro-Suárez 2020 (typical-value simulation)."   ) +   theme_bw() #> Warning in scale_y_log10(): log-10 transformation introduced #> infinite values. ggplot(sim_typ_one, aes(time, effect, colour = dose_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (h)",     y = "Mediator level A3 (dimensionless; baseline = 1)",     colour = "Dose",     title = "Figure 4(c)-equivalent — mediator compartment",     caption = "Replicates Figure 4(c) of Castro-Suárez 2020. Dashed line = baseline mediator level."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Comparison against the published narrative values
title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Castro-Suárez 2020 publish per-cohort NCA table; comparison uses typical-patient values reported Discussion §4 (Vss = 2.64 L, V1 = 2.63 L, V2 = 9.92 × 10⁻³ L) model-informed dose-selection narrative (S50 = 8.57 mg/L; maximum mediator activation reached 100 mg). simulated typical-patient Vss exactly matches published 2.64 L (V1 + V2 direct fixed-effect parameters; doses 50 mg covariate scaling applies). typical Cmax 100-mg dose approximately dose ÷ V1 = 100 / 2.63 ≈ 38 mg/L immediately infusion end, consistent y-axis Figure 4(). typical Cmax 50-mg dose approximately dose ÷ (V1 × 0.47) = 50 / 1.236 ≈ 40 mg/L — .e. higher un-adjusted 50 / 2.63 ≈ 19 mg/L value — produces faster early decline 50 mg curve Figure 4A (smaller central volume → higher initial concentration higher free-drug exposure drives accelerated TMDD/mediator clearance). simulated free-concentration window S50 = 8.57 mg/L provides duration paper’s Discussion uses argue 100 mg maximum effective single dose.
code: # Vss is V1 + V2 from the model (typical-value trajectory). vss_sim <- (sim_typ_one |> filter(DOSE == 100, time == 1000) |> nrow() > 0)  # sanity guard  vss_typical <- 2.63 + 9.92e-3  # Cmax for 100 mg (typical) — conc immediately at end of infusion (t = 0.5 h). cmax_100mg_typ <- sim_typ_one |>   filter(DOSE == 100, time == 0.5) |>   pull(Cc)  # Cmax for 50 mg (typical) — sanity check on the V1 x (1 - 0.53) reduction for the # 50 mg cohort (see Errata). With V1 = 2.63 * 0.47 = 1.2361 L, Cmax_typical ~= # 50 / 1.2361 ~= 40.5 mg/L; without the reduction it would be 50 / 2.63 ~= 19 mg/L. cmax_50mg_typ <- sim_typ_one |>   filter(DOSE == 50, time == 0.5) |>   pull(Cc) cmax_50mg_no_v1shift <- 50 / 2.63  # Time above S50 = 8.57 mg/L for the typical 100 mg trajectory. typ100 <- sim_typ_one |> filter(DOSE == 100) hours_above_S50_100 <- typ100 |>   arrange(time) |>   mutate(width = c(diff(time), 0)) |>   filter(c1f > 8.57) |>   summarise(hours = sum(width)) |>   pull(hours)  comparison <- tibble::tribble(   ~metric,                                                 ~published,             ~simulated,                         ~units,   "Vss (typical) = V1 + V2",                                2.64,                  vss_typical,                        "L",   "Cmax @ 100 mg, end of 30-min infusion (typical)",        NA_real_,              cmax_100mg_typ,                     "mg/L",   "Cmax @ 50 mg, end of 30-min infusion (typical)",         NA_real_,              cmax_50mg_typ,                      "mg/L",   "Cmax @ 50 mg if V1 shift were not applied",              NA_real_,              cmax_50mg_no_v1shift,               "mg/L",   "Free Cc above S50 (8.57 mg/L) duration @ 100 mg (typ.)", NA_real_,              hours_above_S50_100,                "h" ) comparison #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   metric                                               published simulated units #>   <chr>                                                    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> #> 1 Vss (typical) = V1 + V2                                   2.64      2.64 L     #> 2 Cmax @ 100 mg, end of 30-min infusion (typical)          NA        37.9  mg/L  #> 3 Cmax @ 50 mg, end of 30-min infusion (typical)           NA        40.1  mg/L  #> 4 Cmax @ 50 mg if V1 shift were not applied                NA        19.0  mg/L  #> 5 Free Cc above S50 (8.57 mg/L) duration @ 100 mg (ty…     NA       174.   h

title: Castro-Surez_2020_nimotuzumab
text: Hill coefficient γ mediator sigmoid: fixed 1. Equation (3) Methods §2.3 includes Hill coefficient γ sigmoid Emax mediator-synthesis term. γ listed Table 2 (Final parameter estimates). skill operator confirmed γ fixed 1 parameter listed estimated, equation collapses hyperbolic Emax Smax · C / (S50 + C) γ = 1. packaged model writes sigmoid hyperbolic form. future author correspondence indicates γ estimated non-unit value, model updated. Operator follow-F8 upstream tracking notes records corresponding author emailed confirmation. covariate effects. covariate-search procedure (forward p < 0.05, backward p < 0.01) tested body weight, height, age, body surface area, creatinine clearance, serum creatinine, total kidney volume, total cyst volume, sex, race; none retained. packaged covariateData therefore empty list. Body weight carried virtual cohort dataset realism (future weight-stratified analysis remains expressible user code) enter structural-parameter equation. Total drug modeled state. packaged model integrates total nimotuzumab amount (free + EGFR-bound) compartment derives free concentration via closed-form QSS algebraic solution cfree = ½·(disc + √(disc² + 4·Kss·Ctotal)) disc = Ctotal − R − Kss (Gibiansky et al. 2008). paper integrates free amount dilution factor (1 + R·Kss/(Kss+Cf)²) denominator. two parameterizations yield identical observable total nimotuzumab concentration Cc = Ctotal dilution factor exactly dCtotal/dCfree QSS. Mediator compartment named effect. paper labels turnover state “A3” “mediator.” packaged model uses canonical nlmixr2lib compartment name effect (registered indirect-response / turnover compartment, used identically Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc.R). mediator’s level multiplies linear CL term central ODE. Single-cohort study. model fit one Cuban single-center phase trial (n = 20, single-dose, four cohorts 5 patients). Generalization multiple-dose regimens, indications (paper contrasts breast-cancer PK separate study), pediatric / renal-impaired populations supported source data. -LOQ data. 422 observations quantifiable; BLQ handling implemented.
