title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Citation: Chen N, Kassir N, Laadem , Giuseppi AC, Shetty JK, Maxwell SE, Sriraman P, Ritland S, Linde PG, Budda B, Reynolds J, Ramji P, Palmisano M, Zhou S. Population Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Luspatercept, Erythroid Maturation Agent, Anemic Patients Myelodysplastic Syndromes. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2020 Oct;9(10):395-404. doi:10.1002/psp4.12515 Description: One-compartment population PK model luspatercept (activin receptor type IIB / IgG1 Fc-fusion) adults anemia due myelodysplastic syndromes (Chen 2020), first-order subcutaneous absorption, first-order linear elimination parameterised CL/F V1/F, body weight + age + baseline albumin power covariates CL/F, body weight + baseline albumin power covariates V1/F. Article: CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2020;9(10):395-404

title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Chen 2020 population PK analysis pooled 260 adult patients anemia due lower-risk myelodysplastic syndromes (MDS) two studies: phase II A536-03 (“PACE-MDS”, NCT01749514, n = 107) pivotal phase III ACE-536-MDS-001 (“MEDALIST”, NCT02631070, n = 153). patients received luspatercept subcutaneously every 3 weeks (q3w) doses ranging 0.125 1.75 mg/kg. patients (91.0%) started 1.0 mg/kg stepwise titration 1.33 1.75 mg/kg needed inadequate hemoglobin response persistent transfusion need. Baseline demographics (Chen 2020 Table 1): 38.8% female; median age 72 years (range 27-95); median weight 76.3 kg (range 46-124); 82.3% White; median baseline albumin 44 g/L (range 31.0-52.6); median eGFR 73.1 mL/min/1.73 m^2 (range 29.6-150). 26.9% renal impairment, 51.5% mild, 21.5% moderate; 59.2% hepatic impairment, 31.5% mild, 8.8% moderate, 0.4% severe. 38.5% received concurrent iron chelation therapy. 2,403 quantifiable luspatercept concentrations collected 4-784 days first dose using validated ELISA assay range 50-600 ng/mL. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Chen_2020_luspatercept")$population.

title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Chen_2020_luspatercept.R. table collects review.

title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Chen 2020 Table 1 demographics: weight, age, baseline albumin sampled log-normal-like distributions matched published medians ranges, sex sampled published 38.8% female prevalence.
code: set.seed(20260425) n_subj <- 500  # Truncated-normal-style sampler that approximates a population with a given # median and a published min-max range. Bounded so simulated subjects sit # inside the trial inclusion envelope. sample_in_range <- function(n, median, lo, hi, sd) {   out <- rnorm(n, mean = median, sd = sd)   pmin(pmax(out, lo), hi) }  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id   = seq_len(n_subj),   WT   = sample_in_range(n_subj, median = 76.3, lo = 46,   hi = 124,  sd = 17),   AGE  = sample_in_range(n_subj, median = 72,   lo = 27,   hi = 95,   sd = 9),   ALB  = sample_in_range(n_subj, median = 44,   lo = 31,   hi = 52.6, sd = 4),   SEXF = rbinom(n_subj, 1, prob = 0.388) )  # Two dose groups bracketing the recommended titration range (1.0 mg/kg # starting and 1.75 mg/kg maximum). Chen 2020 reports mean AUC_ss between # 151 day*ug/mL (at 1.0 mg/kg) and 264 day*ug/mL (at 1.75 mg/kg). build_events <- function(cohort, dose_mgkg, n_doses = 10, tau = 21,                          id_offset = 0L, label) {   base <- dplyr::mutate(cohort, id = id + id_offset)   obs_times <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, n_doses * tau, by = 1),                              (n_doses - 1) * tau + c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 14, 17, 21))))   doses <- base |>     tidyr::crossing(time = seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = WT * dose_mgkg, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L,                   treatment = label)   obs <- base |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L,                   treatment = label)   dplyr::bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, AGE, ALB, SEXF, treatment) }  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   build_events(cohort, dose_mgkg = 1.00, id_offset =     0L, label = "1.0 mg/kg q3w"),   build_events(cohort, dose_mgkg = 1.75, id_offset = 1000L, label = "1.75 mg/kg q3w") )  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: deterministic replication typical-subject behaviour (-subject variability), zero random effects:
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Chen_2020_luspatercept")) sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events,                        keep = c("WT", "AGE", "ALB", "SEXF", "treatment")) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  # Reference subject from Chen 2020: 70 kg, 72 yr, 44 g/L albumin. typical_scenarios <- tibble::tibble(   id = c(1L, 2L),   scenario = c("Reference 70 kg @ 1.0 mg/kg", "Reference 70 kg @ 1.75 mg/kg"),   WT = 70, AGE = 72, ALB = 44, SEXF = 0L,   dose_mg = c(70 * 1.00, 70 * 1.75) )  ev_typical <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(typical_scenarios)), function(i) {   ev_i <- rxode2::et(amt = typical_scenarios$dose_mg[i], cmt = "depot",                      ii = 21, addl = 9) |>     rxode2::et(seq(0, 210, by = 0.5)) |>     as.data.frame()   ev_i$id <- typical_scenarios$id[i]   ev_i }))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical,   params = typical_scenarios[, c("id", "WT", "AGE", "ALB", "SEXF")],   returnType = "tibble" ) |>   dplyr::left_join(typical_scenarios[, c("id", "scenario")], by = "id") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 1a — visual predictive check by dose group
title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Chen 2020 Figure 1a population VPC luspatercept serum concentration time. simulated cohort summarises 5th, 50th, 95th percentiles concentration 10 q3w doses (treatment window evaluated paper primary efficacy endpoint, weeks 1-24).
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Luspatercept concentration (ug/mL)",        title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles by dose group",        caption = "Replicates the population VPC structure in Chen 2020 Figure 1a.") +   theme_minimal()

what: Figure 1b — clinical relevance of covariates on AUC_ss
title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Chen 2020 Figure 1b reports percentage difference AUC_ss / Cmax_ss extreme covariate values relative “normal” (10th-90th percentile) covariate values. Body weight + 1.0 mg/kg weight-based dosing produces < 10% AUC_ss difference heavy light patients, age albumin produce < 20% AUC_ss differences. chunk reproduces sensitivity using simulated typical-subject AUC_ss across covariate extremes (weight 46-124 kg, age 27-95 years, albumin 31-53 g/L). Predicted AUC_ss across covariate extremes vs. reference subject (replicates structure Chen 2020 Figure 1b AUC_ss ).
code: cov_grid <- tibble::tibble(   scenario = c("WT low (46 kg)", "WT high (124 kg)",                "AGE low (27 yr)", "AGE high (95 yr)",                "ALB low (31 g/L)", "ALB high (53 g/L)",                "Reference (76.3 kg, 72 yr, 44 g/L)"),   WT  = c(46, 124, 76.3, 76.3, 76.3, 76.3, 76.3),   AGE = c(72, 72,    27,   95, 72,   72,   72),   ALB = c(44, 44,    44,   44, 31,   53,   44),   SEXF = 0L ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     id = dplyr::row_number(),     dose_mg = WT * 1.0,     # CL/F at the simulated covariate combination per Chen 2020 Results equation     cl_pred = 0.469 * (WT/70)^0.769 * (AGE/72)^(-0.534) * (ALB/44)^(-1.17),     aucss_pred = dose_mg / cl_pred  # day*ug/mL over a 21-day interval   ) ref_aucss <- cov_grid$aucss_pred[cov_grid$scenario == "Reference (76.3 kg, 72 yr, 44 g/L)"] cov_grid |>   dplyr::mutate(pct_diff = 100 * (aucss_pred - ref_aucss) / ref_aucss) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 2,     caption = "Predicted AUC_ss across covariate extremes vs. the reference subject (replicates the structure of Chen 2020 Figure 1b for AUC_ss only).")

title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, AUC last (steady-state) dosing interval, terminal half-life using PKNCA. Stratify dose group results can compared paper’s reported AUC_ss range ~13-day half-life.
code: # Restrict to the last (10th) dosing interval as the steady-state interval. ss_start <- 9 * 21    # day 189 ss_end   <- 10 * 21   # day 210  sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_ss = time - ss_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, time_ss, Cc, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time_ss | treatment + id)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == ss_start) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_ss = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time_ss, amt, treatment)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time_ss | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = 21,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>  ■■■■■■                            18% |  ETA: 18s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■                       32% |  ETA: 14s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                   46% |  ETA: 11s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■               60% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           74% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      89% |  ETA:  2s summary(nca_res) #>  start end      treatment   N    auclast        cmax              tmax #>      0  21  1.0 mg/kg q3w 500 149 [40.3] 9.48 [29.9] 4.00 [3.00, 5.00] #>      0  21 1.75 mg/kg q3w 500 262 [40.9] 16.7 [31.4] 4.00 [3.00, 5.00] #>    half.life #>  14.9 [6.64] #>  15.1 [7.22] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; half.life: arithmetic mean and standard deviation; N: number of subjects

title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Chen 2020 reports mean elimination half-life ~13 days mean AUC_ss values 151-264 day*ug/mL 1.0-1.75 mg/kg dose range (Results, “Luspatercept population PK model” “Exposure-response efficacy”). IIV AUC_ss 38.0%. Simulated steady-state AUC terminal t1/2 vs. Chen 2020 Results.
code: nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "half.life")) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     cv_pct = 100 * sd(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = c(median, cv_pct))  published <- tibble::tribble(   ~treatment,         ~published_aucss,        ~published_thalf, ~published_cv_aucss,   "1.0 mg/kg q3w",    "151 day*ug/mL (mean)",  "~13 days",       "38.0%",   "1.75 mg/kg q3w",   "264 day*ug/mL (mean)",  "~13 days",       "38.0%" )  dplyr::left_join(published, nca_tbl, by = "treatment") |>   knitr::kable(digits = 2,     caption = "Simulated steady-state AUC and terminal t1/2 vs. Chen 2020 Results.")

title: Chen_2020_luspatercept
text: Original individual-level data public. virtual cohort uses truncated-normal sampling match Chen 2020 Table 1 medians ranges weight, age, baseline albumin. Race distribution (82.3% White) documented population metadata used model: race tested candidate covariate Chen 2020 retained. Chen 2020 fitted log-transformed luspatercept concentrations additive residual error model NONMEM. equivalent proportional residual error linear (concentration) space; reported 22.4% maps directly propSd = 0.224 nlmixr2. IIV reported square root omega^2 expressed percentage (standard NONMEM exponential-IIV reporting). cross-check AUC_ss IIV 38.0% reported Results: sqrt(omega^2_CL) = 0.364, predicted log-normal CV% AUC 100 * sqrt(exp(0.364^2) - 1) = 37.7%, agrees published 38.0%. IIV Ka. Chen 2020 Methods state “Inclusion IIV absorption rate constant led large shrinkage”, random effect excluded original authors. model parameterised apparent CL/F V1/F. Absolute bioavailability estimated; full subcutaneous dose enters depot compartment apparent volume / clearance absorb F. Steady-state AUC computed 10th dosing interval (days 189-210) 10-dose q3w regimen. typical CL/F 0.469 L/day apparent half-life ~13 days, accumulation q3w modest steady state essentially reached 5th-6th dose. Figure 1b reproduction uses analytical AUC_ss prediction (dose / CL/F) typical-subject level clarity; calculation Chen 2020 used Monte Carlo covariate sensitivity analysis without additional sources variability (IIV, residual error, random sampling) contribute published Figure 1b error bars.

what: Guselkumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Simulate guselkumab serum concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Chen et al. (2022), built pooled phase 3 data patients active psoriatic arthritis (DISCOVER-1 DISCOVER-2). Guselkumab human IgG1 lambda monoclonal antibody targeting p19 subunit interleukin-23. structural model one-compartment linear PK model first-order subcutaneous absorption first-order elimination. final covariate set body weight apparent clearance (CL/F) apparent volume distribution (V/F), diabetes-mellitus comorbidity indicator CL/F. Typical parameters 84 kg non-diabetic reference subject CL/F = 0.596 L/day, V/F = 15.5 L, Ka = 0.572 1/day, give typical terminal half-life approximately 18.1 days. Citation: Chen Y, Miao X, Hsu CH, Zhuang Y, Kollmeier , Xu Z, Zhou H, Sharma . Population pharmacokinetics exposure-response modeling analyses guselkumab patients psoriatic arthritis. Clin Transl Sci. 2022;15(3):749-760. doi:10.1111/cts.13197 Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/cts.13197

title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Per Chen 2022 (Demographic characteristics section Table S1 supplement): analysis pooled data DISCOVER-1 (NCT03162796) DISCOVER-2 (NCT03158285), two pivotal phase 3 trials adults active psoriatic arthritis. Median baseline body weight 84 kg (25th-75th percentile 71.0-97.3 kg), diabetes comorbidity present approximately 9% patients, majority patients White, approximately 11% previously received anti-tumor necrosis factor alpha therapy. Antidrug-antibody positivity 2.0% population PK analysis dataset. full demographic table (age, sex, race, region) lives Table S1 supplement reproduced verbatim main-text trim. metadata available programmatically:
code: readModelDb("Chen_2022_guselkumab")$meta$population

previous_headings: Guselkumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Chen 2022 publish individual-level data; virtual cohort approximates demographics main-text Demographic characteristics paragraph. continuous-covariate distribution log-normal centered population median weight (84 kg). Diabetes sampled Bernoulli reported 9% prevalence. cohort sized 500 subjects make per-time-point quantiles smooth.
code: set.seed(2022) n_subj <- 500L  pop <- tibble(   ID   = seq_len(n_subj),   WT   = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(84), 0.20), 45), 200),  # kg, clipped to a plausible adult range   DIAB = as.integer(rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.09))                   # ~9% diabetic per Results )

previous_headings: Guselkumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Two phase 3 SC regimens simulated: q8w (approved clinical regimen): 100 mg SC weeks 0 4, every 8 weeks (weeks 12, 20, 28, …). q4w: 100 mg SC every 4 weeks (weeks 0, 4, 8, 12, …). regimens simulated week 52 reach approximate steady state. depot compartment SC dosing compartment (cmt = 1); central compartment sampling compartment (cmt = 2).
code: weeks_q4w <- seq(0, 52, by = 4)              # 0, 4, 8, ... 52 weeks_q8w <- c(0, 4, seq(12, 52, by = 8))    # 0, 4, 12, 20, 28, 36, 44, 52  dose_times_q4w <- weeks_q4w * 7 dose_times_q8w <- weeks_q8w * 7  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,   28,    by = 1),     # daily through week 4   seq(28, 52 * 7, by = 3.5)    # twice weekly out to week 52 )))  make_events <- function(pop_df, dose_times, regimen, id_offset = 0L) {   pop_df <- pop_df %>% mutate(ID = ID + id_offset, regimen = regimen)   d_dose <- pop_df %>%     crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%     mutate(AMT = 100, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DV = NA_real_)   d_obs <- pop_df %>%     crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)   bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%     as.data.frame() }  events_q4w <- make_events(pop, dose_times_q4w, "q4w", id_offset =      0L) events_q8w <- make_events(pop, dose_times_q8w, "q8w", id_offset = n_subj)  events <- bind_rows(events_q4w, events_q8w) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))

title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Median 90% prediction interval simulated serum guselkumab concentration time course q4w q8w regimens (replicates shape population PK simulation Figure S1 Chen 2022 underlies steady-state exposure simulations Figure S2).
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(regimen, time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x        = "Time (weeks)",     y        = "Guselkumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title    = "Simulated guselkumab PK in psoriatic arthritis",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual patients per regimen)",     caption  = "Underlies the simulation work in Figure S2 of Chen 2022."   ) +   theme_bw()

previous_headings: Guselkumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: paper’s Table-S2-style steady-state interval summary reports C_trough,ss AUC_tau. Approximate steady-state intervals last full inter-dose window regimen (weeks 44-52 q8w; weeks 48-52 q4w). Simulated steady-state per-interval exposures (Cmax / Ctrough ug/mL; AUCtau ug*day/mL).
code: ss_intervals <- tribble(   ~regimen, ~start_wk, ~end_wk,   "q4w",     48,        52,   "q8w",     44,        52 )  ss_summary <- ss_intervals %>%   rowwise() %>%   do({     row <- .     sub <- sim %>%       filter(regimen == row$regimen,              time >= row$start_wk * 7,              time <= row$end_wk   * 7,              Cc > 0) %>%       arrange(id, time)     per_id <- sub %>%       group_by(id) %>%       summarise(         Cmax    = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),         Ctrough = Cc[which.max(time)],         AUCtau  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),         .groups = "drop"       )     tibble(       regimen        = row$regimen,       window         = sprintf("weeks %d-%d", row$start_wk, row$end_wk),       Cmax_median    = median(per_id$Cmax),       Ctrough_median = median(per_id$Ctrough),       AUCtau_median  = median(per_id$AUCtau)     )   }) %>%   bind_rows()  knitr::kable(   ss_summary,   digits  = 3,   caption = "Simulated steady-state per-interval exposures (Cmax / Ctrough in ug/mL; AUCtau in ug*day/mL)." )

text: Run PKNCA steady-state q8w maintenance interval (weeks 44-52). expected typical terminal half-life ~18.1 days typical 84 kg subject (Results section, page 752). PKNCA summary q8w steady-state maintenance interval (weeks 44-52). Expected typical terminal half-life ~18.1 days (Chen 2022 Results, page 752).
code: nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(regimen == "q8w", time >= 44 * 7, time <= 52 * 7, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(time_rel = time - 44 * 7) %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, regimen)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   mutate(     ID        = ID + n_subj,                  # match q8w id_offset     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 100,     regimen   = "q8w"   ) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, regimen)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | regimen + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | regimen + ID) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 8 * 7,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■                      35% |  ETA:  7s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■             67% |  ETA:  3s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■    98% |  ETA:  0s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = "PKNCA summary for the q8w steady-state maintenance interval (weeks 44-52). Expected typical terminal half-life ~18.1 days (Chen 2022 Results, page 752)." )

previous_headings: Guselkumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Results section reports typical-subject CL/F = 0.596 L/day, V/F = 15.5 L, Ka = 0.572 1/day, typical terminal half-life ~18.1 days median 84 kg diabetes comorbidity. Reproduce using packaged model -subject variability zeroed :
code:
mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  typ_pop <- tibble(   ID   = 1L,   WT   = 84,   DIAB = 0L )  typ_dose <- typ_pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times_q8w) %>%   mutate(AMT = 100, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DV = NA_real_)  typ_obs <- typ_pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)  typ_events <- bind_rows(typ_dose, typ_obs) %>%   arrange(TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()  sim_typ <- rxSolve(mod_typ, typ_events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka'  cl_typ  <- 0.596 v_typ   <- 15.5 ka_typ  <- 0.572 t_half  <- log(2) * v_typ / cl_typ  cat(sprintf(   paste0(     "Typical 84 kg non-diabetic subject (vs paper Results, page 752):
",     "  CL/F = %.3f L/day  (paper: 0.596)
",     "  V/F  = %.2f L      (paper: 15.5)
",     "  Ka   = %.3f 1/day  (paper: 0.572)
",     "  t1/2 (typical, ln(2) * V/CL) = %.1f days  (paper: ~18.1)
"   ),   cl_typ, v_typ, ka_typ, t_half )) #> Typical 84 kg non-diabetic subject (vs paper Results, page 752): #>   CL/F = 0.596 L/day  (paper: 0.596) #>   V/F  = 15.50 L      (paper: 15.5) #>   Ka   = 0.572 1/day  (paper: 0.572) #>   t1/2 (typical, ln(2) * V/CL) = 18.0 days  (paper: ~18.1)  ggplot(sim_typ %>% filter(time > 0), aes(time / 7, Cc)) +   geom_line(colour = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x        = "Time (weeks)",     y        = "Guselkumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title    = "Typical 84 kg non-diabetic subject, q8w regimen (zeroed random effects)",     caption  = "100 mg SC at weeks 0 and 4, then every 8 weeks."   ) +   theme_bw()

previous_headings: Guselkumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
what: Covariate-effect simulation: heavier (>= 90 kg) and diabetic subgroups
title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: paper reports (Results, Simulation; Figure S2) q8w regimen model predicts: patients body weight >= 90 kg vs. < 90 kg C_trough lower ~33.4% AUC_tau lower ~28.8%; patients vs. without diabetes comorbidity C_trough lower ~30.3% AUC_tau lower ~18.9%. Reproduce using zeroed random effects (typical-subject predictions) steady-state q8w (weeks 44-52). body-weight contrast uses 95 kg vs. 80 kg round-number representatives straddle 90 kg cutoff used paper. Steady-state q8w typical-subject covariate-effect contrasts (Ctrough ug/mL; AUCtau ug*day/mL). Compare Chen 2022 Figure S2 / Results: -33.4% Ctrough -28.8% AUC heavier subjects; -30.3% Ctrough -18.9% AUC diabetic subjects. body-weight contrast simulated 95 vs. 80 kg (typical-subject anchors straddling 90 kg cutoff) rather median---subgroup paper used report Figure S2’s percentages, within---percent difference expected. diabetes contrast fixed 80 kg reproduce paper’s percentages closely 1.15 multiplier CL/F operates independently body weight.
code: typ_eval <- function(WT_val, DIAB_val) {   pop1   <- tibble(ID = 1L, WT = WT_val, DIAB = as.integer(DIAB_val))   d_dose <- pop1 %>% crossing(TIME = dose_times_q8w) %>%     mutate(AMT = 100, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DV = NA_real_)   d_obs  <- pop1 %>% crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)   ev <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>% arrange(TIME, desc(EVID)) %>% as.data.frame()   s  <- rxSolve(mod_typ, ev, returnType = "data.frame") %>%     filter(time >= 44 * 7, time <= 52 * 7, Cc > 0) %>% arrange(time)   list(     Ctrough = tail(s$Cc, 1),     AUCtau  = sum(diff(s$time) * (head(s$Cc, -1) + tail(s$Cc, -1)) / 2)   ) }  ref80   <- typ_eval(80, 0) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka' heavy95 <- typ_eval(95, 0) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka' diab80  <- typ_eval(80, 1) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka'  pct <- function(new, ref) 100 * (new - ref) / ref  cov_table <- tibble(   contrast        = c("WT 95 vs 80 kg, no diabetes", "Diabetes vs no diabetes, 80 kg"),   Ctrough_ref     = c(ref80$Ctrough, ref80$Ctrough),   Ctrough_new     = c(heavy95$Ctrough, diab80$Ctrough),   Ctrough_pct_chg = c(pct(heavy95$Ctrough, ref80$Ctrough),                       pct(diab80$Ctrough,  ref80$Ctrough)),   AUCtau_ref      = c(ref80$AUCtau, ref80$AUCtau),   AUCtau_new      = c(heavy95$AUCtau, diab80$AUCtau),   AUCtau_pct_chg  = c(pct(heavy95$AUCtau, ref80$AUCtau),                       pct(diab80$AUCtau,  ref80$AUCtau)) )  knitr::kable(   cov_table,   digits  = 2,   caption = "Steady-state q8w typical-subject covariate-effect contrasts (Ctrough in ug/mL; AUCtau in ug*day/mL). Compare to Chen 2022 Figure S2 / Results: -33.4% Ctrough and -28.8% AUC for heavier subjects; -30.3% Ctrough and -18.9% AUC for diabetic subjects." )

previous_headings: Guselkumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Chen 2022 publish individual-level PK per-subject covariate values, virtual population approximates main-text demographics rather reproducing : Weight: sampled log-normal around 84 kg median (Results, page 752) SD 0.20 log scale, clipped 45-200 kg. approximately matches reported 25th-75th percentile range (71-97 kg). Diabetes: sampled Bernoulli(0.09) (~9% prevalence per Results, Demographic characteristics). Sex, age, race, region, baseline PASI, DAS28, CRP, prior anti-TNF, ADA status: evaluated paper’s full covariate model exposure-response work retained final population PK model part packaged PK model. Bioavailability (F): absolute bioavailability separately identifiable SC-data; model uses apparent CL/F V/F published, depot compartment dosed directly. f(depot) term included. Residual error. combined add(addSd) + prop(propSd) form standard nlmixr2 implementation paper’s “combined additive proportional residual error model” (Methods, Base model section), addSd ug/mL matching concentration units. Exposure-response models (landmark longitudinal). Chen 2022 also reports landmark longitudinal exposure-response models ACR IGA endpoints (Tables 2-3). models scope population-PK packaged model nlmixr2lib implemented . time-varying weight. Weight treated time-fixed baseline value; paper describe time-varying weight effect.

title: Chen_2022_guselkumab
text: Chen Y, Miao X, Hsu CH, Zhuang Y, Kollmeier , Xu Z, Zhou H, Sharma . Population pharmacokinetics exposure-response modeling analyses guselkumab patients psoriatic arthritis. Clin Transl Sci. 2022;15(3):749-760. doi:10.1111/cts.13197

title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: Citation: Cheng IL, Huang ZH, Worth , Booth C, Standing JF. Pharmacokinetic modelling intravenous immunoglobulin children primary immunodeficiencies secondary antibody deficiencies. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2025;1-11. doi:10.1002/bcp.70420 Description: Two-compartment population PK model intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) replacement therapy pediatric primary-immunodeficiency secondary-antibody-deficiency patients (Cheng 2026) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/bcp.70420

title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: model developed retrospective single-centre cohort 64 children treated intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) tertiary paediatric hospital United Kingdom April 2019 April 2024 (Cheng 2026 Table 2). Forty-four children primary immunodeficiency (PID) twenty secondary antibody deficiency (SAD); fifteen SAD patients antibody deficiency following rituximab five following CAR-T cell therapy. cohort spanned wide age range (3 weeks 16.8 years; median 4.08 years) weight range (3.15 95.3 kg; median 18.6 kg). median dose 0.56 g/kg every 28 days (range 0.24 1.38 g/kg). PID patients received 0.3 g/kg every 3 weeks per local policy; SAD patients received 0.5 g/kg every 4 weeks. median baseline IgG 4 g/L (PID baselines fixed 4 g/L since established long-term IgRT patients), median absolute CD19+ B cell count 0.07 × 10^9 cells/L, median IgM 0.21 g/L. Plasma IgG samples (n = 444) predominantly trough samples; assay’s lower limit quantification 0.07 g/L. Three IVIG products used: Privigen (n = 53), Octagam (n = 9), Gamunex (n = 2) (Cheng 2026 Table 3). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin")$population model loaded.

title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: published erratum corrigendum found paper model extraction date. Two notational ambiguities noted source worth flagging readers: “study adds” highlight box abstract describe SAD patients “54% lower clearance” PID patients. actual parameter estimate (Cheng 2026 Table 4) theta_DT_CL = 0.542, multiplies CL SAD branch — .e., SAD CL 54.2% PID CL, equivalently 45.8% lower PID CL. Discussion’s phrasing (“clearance SAD patients half PID patients”) matches parameter value; highlight-box “54% lower” appears confuse multiplier percent-change. model file uses table value (0.542) directly. Results paragraph describing final model, source writes “IgM cell count” (page 7); context read “IgM level” (IgM antibody concentration, cell count). parameter continuous IgM concentration (g/L) tabulated Cheng 2026 Table 2.

title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: Original individual-level IgG data publicly available. figures use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate modelled population: weight--age sampled published Table 2 medians ranges, disease type drawn 44 PID / 20 SAD split, IgM drawn published distribution.
code: set.seed(20260428)  n_subj <- 200  # Disease split: 44 PID / 20 SAD per Table 2 (PID = reference, DIS_SAD = 0) n_pid <- round(n_subj * 44 / 64) n_sad <- n_subj - n_pid  # Body weight: sample on log scale across the reported pediatric range # (3.15-95.3 kg). Use a log-uniform distribution truncated to the published # extremes; this is conservative relative to the actual right-skewed # weight distribution but gives roughly the published 18.6 kg median. sample_wt <- function(n) {   u <- runif(n)   exp(log(3.15) + u * (log(95.3) - log(3.15))) }  # IgM: log-uniform across the reported range (0.03-5.61 g/L) sample_igm <- function(n) {   u <- runif(n)   exp(log(0.03) + u * (log(5.61) - log(0.03))) }  cohort <- data.frame(   ID      = seq_len(n_subj),   WT      = sample_wt(n_subj),   DIS_SAD = c(rep(0L, n_pid), rep(1L, n_sad)),   IGM     = sample_igm(n_subj) )  cat(sprintf(   "Cohort: n=%d, PID=%d, SAD=%d; weight median=%.1f kg, IgM median=%.2f g/L\n",   nrow(cohort), n_pid, n_sad, median(cohort$WT), median(cohort$IGM) )) #> Cohort: n=200, PID=138, SAD=62; weight median=16.5 kg, IgM median=0.42 g/L

title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: Cheng 2026 Figure 1 shows estimated IgG levels various dosing regimens (maintenance doses 0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6 g/kg) 28 days. paper simulates 1000 virtual patients using median values relevant covariates (weight 18.6 kg, IGM 0.21 g/L, DIS_SAD = 0 PID). replicate scenario cohort-median typical patient.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin")  # Typical median PID patient typical_wt  <- 18.6 typical_igm <- 0.21 typical_dt  <- 0L  # Doses to compare (g/kg) over 28 days doses_gkg <- c(0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6)  obs_grid <- seq(0, 28, by = 1)  # Build a single multi-cohort event table; one cohort per dose level. Each # cohort gets a disjoint ID range via id_offset so rxSolve does not collide # subjects across cohorts. make_cohort <- function(dose_gkg, n, id_offset) {   amt <- dose_gkg * typical_wt   data.frame(     ID       = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT       = typical_wt,     DIS_SAD  = typical_dt,     IGM      = typical_igm,     dose_gkg = dose_gkg,     amt      = amt   ) }  n_per <- 200  cohorts <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_along(doses_gkg), function(i) {   make_cohort(doses_gkg[i], n_per, id_offset = (i - 1L) * n_per) }))  # Build dosing + observation event table per subject dose_rows <- cohorts |>   transmute(     id   = ID,     time = 0,     amt  = amt,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "central",     WT, DIS_SAD, IGM, dose_gkg   )  obs_rows <- cohorts[rep(seq_len(nrow(cohorts)), each = length(obs_grid)), ] |>   mutate(     id   = ID,     time = rep(obs_grid, times = nrow(cohorts)),     amt  = 0,     evid = 0L,     cmt  = "central"   ) |>   select(id, time, amt, evid, cmt, WT, DIS_SAD, IGM, dose_gkg)  events <- bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |> arrange(id, time, desc(evid))  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")]))  set.seed(2026) sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = c("dose_gkg")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time, dose_gkg) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(dose_label = sprintf("%.1f g/kg", dose_gkg)) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50, colour = dose_label, fill = dose_label)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(6, 8), linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey30") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 28, by = 7)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Total plasma IgG (g/L)",     colour = "Dose",     fill   = "Dose",     title  = "Replicates Cheng 2026 Figure 1: simulated IgG vs. time at various IV dose levels",     caption = "Typical median PID patient (WT 18.6 kg, IGM 0.21 g/L). Dotted lines: target troughs (6 and 8 g/L)."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: Cheng 2026 Figure 2 compares dosing regimens without 1 g/kg loading dose Day 0. simulate 0.5 g/kg every 28 days two cycles, without loading dose, typical PID patient.  Simulated proportion post-dose time total IgG > 6 g/L > 8 g/L.
code: n_per <- 200 duration_days <- 56  # two 28-day cycles obs_grid_2 <- seq(0, duration_days, by = 1)  # Helper: build events for a regimen make_regimen <- function(label, dose_gkg, has_loading, n, id_offset) {   ids <- id_offset + seq_len(n)   base <- data.frame(     id    = ids,     WT    = typical_wt,     DIS_SAD = typical_dt,     IGM   = typical_igm,     regimen = label   )   amt_maint <- dose_gkg * typical_wt   amt_load  <- 1.0 * typical_wt   doses <- if (has_loading) {     bind_rows(       data.frame(id = ids, time = 0,   amt = amt_load,  evid = 1L, cmt = "central"),       data.frame(id = ids, time = 28,  amt = amt_maint, evid = 1L, cmt = "central")     )   } else {     bind_rows(       data.frame(id = ids, time = 0,   amt = amt_maint, evid = 1L, cmt = "central"),       data.frame(id = ids, time = 28,  amt = amt_maint, evid = 1L, cmt = "central")     )   }   obs <- expand.grid(id = ids, time = obs_grid_2) |>     mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "central")   bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     left_join(base, by = "id") |>     arrange(id, time, desc(evid)) }  regimens <- bind_rows(   make_regimen("0.5 g/kg q28d (no loading)", 0.5, FALSE, n_per, id_offset =     0L),   make_regimen("1 g/kg load + 0.5 g/kg q28d", 0.5, TRUE,  n_per, id_offset = n_per) )  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(regimens[, c("id", "time", "evid")]))  set.seed(20262) sim2 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = regimens, keep = c("regimen")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim2 |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time, regimen) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(6, 8), linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey30") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, duration_days, by = 14)) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Total plasma IgG (g/L)",     colour = "Regimen",     fill   = "Regimen",     title  = "Replicates Cheng 2026 Figure 2: 0.5 g/kg q28d with vs. without 1 g/kg loading dose",     caption = "Typical median PID patient (WT 18.6 kg, IGM 0.21 g/L). Dotted lines: target troughs (6 and 8 g/L)."   ) +   theme_bw() # Probability of target attainment (PTA) above 6 and 8 g/L per regimen pta <- sim2 |>   filter(time > 0, time <= duration_days) |>   group_by(regimen) |>   summarise(     pta_above_6 = mean(Cc > 6, na.rm = TRUE),     pta_above_8 = mean(Cc > 8, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(   pta,   digits  = 3,   caption = "Simulated proportion of post-dose time with total IgG > 6 g/L and > 8 g/L." )

what: PKNCA validation — replicate Cheng 2026 AUC values
title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: Cheng 2026 reports 28-day AUC 200.2 (CI 194.4-206.2), 211.5 (205.6-217.5), 222.4 (216.6-228.5) g/L·day 0.4, 0.5, 0.6 g/kg infusions respectively, median typical patient. Note model defines Cc total IgG (exogenous drug plus endogenous CBAS baseline), NCA-derived AUC includes baseline contribution directly comparable published AUC values. Simulated NCA 28-day dosing interval (total IgG, including endogenous baseline).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 0, time <= 28) |>   mutate(treatment = sprintf("%.1f g/kg", dose_gkg)) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_nca <- events |>   filter(evid == 1, time == 0) |>   mutate(treatment = sprintf("%.1f g/kg", dose_gkg)) |>   select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,                              concu = "g/L", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_nca, amt ~ time | treatment + id,                              doseu = "g")  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = 28,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■                     39% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      88% |  ETA:  1s  nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary,              caption = "Simulated NCA over the 28-day dosing interval (total IgG, including endogenous baseline).")

previous_headings: PKNCA validation — replicate Cheng 2026 AUC values
title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: Simulated vs. published 28-day AUC total plasma IgG (g/L·day) typical median PID patient. published 95% CIs reflect parameter uncertainty across 1000 NONMEM simulation replicates; simulated CIs reflect -subject residual variability across n = 200 virtual patients per dose level.
code: nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result)  simulated_aucs <- nca_tbl |>   filter(PPTESTCD == "auclast") |>   group_by(treatment) |>   summarise(     AUC_sim_median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     AUC_sim_q025   = quantile(PPORRES, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     AUC_sim_q975   = quantile(PPORRES, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups        = "drop"   )  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment      = c("0.4 g/kg", "0.5 g/kg", "0.6 g/kg"),   AUC_pub_median = c(200.2, 211.5, 222.4),   AUC_pub_lo     = c(194.4, 205.6, 216.6),   AUC_pub_hi     = c(206.2, 217.5, 228.5) )  comparison <- published |>   left_join(simulated_aucs, by = "treatment") |>   mutate(pct_diff = 100 * (AUC_sim_median - AUC_pub_median) / AUC_pub_median)  knitr::kable(   comparison,   digits  = 1,   caption = "Simulated vs. published 28-day AUC of total plasma IgG (g/L·day) for the typical median PID patient. The published 95% CIs reflect parameter uncertainty across 1000 NONMEM simulation replicates; the simulated CIs reflect between-subject and residual variability across n = 200 virtual patients per dose level." )

title: Cheng_2026_immunoglobulin
text: IIV correlation structure: Cheng 2026 reports IIV parameterised using “variance-covariance matrix” (.e., block omega -diagonal correlations etas), Table 4 lists diagonal CV% values (CL 42.7%, V2 138.6%, CBAS 49.3%). Without published -diagonal covariance terms, etas packaged model independent; downstream uncertainty joint parameter draws therefore slightly larger source. IIV V1 vs. V2: Cheng 2026 Methods describes IIV “clearance, volume distribution baseline IgG”, final-model Table 4 reports IIV V2 (peripheral) rather V1 (central). packaged model follows Table 4 (authoritative final-model column) applies IIV vp (V2), vc (V1). Allometric exponents fixed theory-based values: 0.75 (CL/Q) 1.0 (V1/V2) per Cheng 2026 Methods, page 7. base model estimated values near (0.788 CL, 0.743 V) parameter collinearity; authors fixed exponents final model. packaged model uses fixed() reflect . Endogenous IgG handling: Total observed plasma IgG = exogenous drug-derived contribution (central / vc) + endogenous baseline (cbas). CBAS value depends disease type IgM level; PID IgM median (0.21 g/L), CBAS = 5.67 g/L. means modelled Cc total assayed IgG, just drug component, NCA values include baseline contribution. Virtual cohort covariate distributions: original individual-level data publicly available. replicate-figure cohorts use published-table extremes (weight 3.15-95.3 kg, IgM 0.03-5.61 g/L) 44/20 PID/SAD ratio reported Table 2. Sex model covariate (Cheng 2026 Methods: sex contribute model improvement); race ethnicity reported source. Dosing: Modelled IV bolus central compartment. Cheng 2026 describes “intravenous Ig” without specifying infusion duration; paper’s simulations reported single-event basis. AUC interpretation: simulated 28-day AUC includes exogenous-drug contribution endogenous CBAS baseline (5.67 × 28 ≈ 158.8 g/L·day typical PID patient). published AUC values (200.2-222.4 g/L·day) consistent interpretation; comparing drug-AUC (subtracting baseline contribution) published numbers understate simulated value.

what: Mirikizumab population PK in Crohn’s disease (VIVID-1 phase 3)
title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Replicate VIVID-1 final population PK model reported Chua et al. (2025) patients moderately--severely active Crohn’s disease. Mirikizumab humanized IgG4 monoclonal antibody binds p19 subunit IL-23. induction regimen 900 mg IV every 4 weeks three doses (weeks 0, 4, 8); maintenance regimen 300 mg SC every 4 weeks week 52. Citation: Chua L, Otani Y, Lin Z, Friedrich S, Durand F, Zhang XC. Mirikizumab Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Patients Moderately--Severely Active Crohn’s Disease: Results Two Randomized Studies. Clin Transl Sci. 2025;18(8):e70320. doi:10.1111/cts.70320 Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/cts.70320 paper reports two independent final popPK models — SERENITY (phase 2, n=186) VIVID-1 (phase 3, n=711) — different covariate structures. vignette replicates VIVID-1 , supports approved Crohn’s disease dose regimen model underlying published simulations (Figure S6). SERENITY reproduced package.

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: VIVID-1 (NCT03926130) global phase 3 treat-study (July 2019 October 2023) mirikizumab versus placebo ustekinumab moderately-- severely active Crohn’s disease. popPK dataset comprised 5,318 observations 711 patients (628 mirikizumab-treated induction plus 83 placebo non-responders crossed Week 12). Baseline demographics (Chua 2025 Table 1): mean weight 68.1 kg (range 21.1-154.0), mean age 36.3 years (range 18-74), 56.5% male, 70.7% White / 25.0% Asian / 4.2% ; mean baseline SES-CD 12.9 mean baseline CDAI 319. 53.4% previous biologic therapy 47.8% failed previous biologic therapy. demographics available programmatically via model’s metadata (accessible meta$population list model evaluated).

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Chua_2025_mirikizumab.R. rate parameters converted h^-1 (published) day^-1 (nlmixr2lib time convention) multiplying 24.

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: CRP BMI canonical entries inst/references/covariate-columns.md. hsCRP column (high-sensitivity assay used e.g. Thakre 2022) separate canonical column; conflate.

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Original subject-level data VIVID-1 publicly available. virtual cohort uses covariate distributions approximating published Table 1 demographics. Covariates sampled independently (paper publish joint distributions) marginals centered scaled give simulated individual parameters bracket reference values.
code: set.seed(20250819) n_subj <- 300  cohort <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_subj),   # Weight: mean 68.1 kg, range 21.1-154; lognormal covers the skew.   WT  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(66), 0.27), 25), 150),   # Albumin: reference 44.57 g/L; VIVID-1 CD population often mildly low.   ALB = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 41, 4), 25), 52),   # CRP in CD skews right; reference 7.41 mg/L; log-normal shape.   CRP = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(6), 1.1), 0.3), 150),   # BMI: reference 24.75 kg/m^2; typical adult IBD distribution.   BMI = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 24.5, 4.5), 14), 45) )

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Approved VIVID-1 regimen: 900 mg IV every 4 weeks 3 doses (weeks 0, 4, 8), 300 mg SC every 4 weeks week 12 week 52.
code: week <- 7 # days per week iv_days <- c(0, 4, 8) * week          # 0, 28, 56 sc_days <- seq(12, 48, by = 4) * week # 84, 112, ..., 336  # Observation grid: dense early, coarser in maintenance, plus steady-state cycle obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 84, by = 1),           # induction phase, daily   seq(85, 336, by = 3.5),       # maintenance, every half-week   seq(336, 364, by = 0.5)       # last SC interval, dense for Cmax/trough )))  iv_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = iv_days) |>   mutate(AMT = 900, EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_,          phase = "induction_IV")  sc_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = sc_days) |>   mutate(AMT = 300, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot",   DV = NA_real_,          phase = "maintenance_SC")  obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_days) |>   mutate(AMT = 0, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_,          phase = NA_character_)  events <- bind_rows(iv_dose, sc_dose, obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, CRP, BMI, phase)

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Chua_2025_mirikizumab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / week, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 52, by = 8)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = expression("Mirikizumab concentration ("*mu*"g/mL)"),     title = "VIVID-1 regimen: 900 mg IV Q4W x 3, then 300 mg SC Q4W",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 300 virtual CD patients)",     caption = "Model: Chua et al. (2025) VIVID-1 final popPK."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: paper reports steady-state exposures phase regimen (Chua 2025 Section 3.1.1 Figure S6): Compute PKNCA NCA parameters steady-state IV interval (dose 3: week 8 week 12) steady-state SC interval (dose week 48 week 52). Simulated steady-state NCA regimen phase (median across N = 300).
code: tau <- 4 * week # 28 days  iv_ss_start <- 8 * week sc_ss_start <- 48 * week  # Concentrations over the two SS intervals, keyed by treatment group iv_conc <- sim |>   filter(time >= iv_ss_start, time <= iv_ss_start + tau, !is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(ID = id, time_rel = time - iv_ss_start, Cc,             treatment = "IV_900mg_Q4W_ss")  sc_conc <- sim |>   filter(time >= sc_ss_start, time <= sc_ss_start + tau, !is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(ID = id, time_rel = time - sc_ss_start, Cc,             treatment = "SC_300mg_Q4W_ss")  nca_conc <- bind_rows(iv_conc, sc_conc)  nca_dose <- bind_rows(   tibble(ID = cohort$ID, time_rel = 0, AMT = 900,          treatment = "IV_900mg_Q4W_ss"),   tibble(ID = cohort$ID, time_rel = 0, AMT = 300,          treatment = "SC_300mg_Q4W_ss") ) conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = tau,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■                        28% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■        84% |  ETA:  1s  knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   digits  = 3,   caption = "Simulated steady-state NCA by regimen phase (median across N = 300)." )

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Simulated vs published steady-state exposures (Chua 2025 Section 3.1.1). four metrics expected match within 20%. Typical-value checks (using rxode2::zeroRe() zero -subject variability) model development matched paper within ~10% IV Cmax,ss (336 vs 332) IV AUC_tau,ss (1902 vs 1820), within ~10-12% SC Cmax,ss (15.0 vs 13.6) SC AUC_tau,ss (246 vs 220). Terminal half-life typical 900 mg single IV dose 9.26 days (paper: 9.3 days, < 1% difference).
code: nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result)  simulated_sbs <- nca_tbl |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "auclast")) |>   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(sim_median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = sim_median) |>   rename(Cmax_sim = cmax, AUCtau_sim = auclast)  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment  = c("IV_900mg_Q4W_ss", "SC_300mg_Q4W_ss"),   Cmax_pub   = c(332, 13.6),   AUCtau_pub = c(1820, 220) )  comparison <- published |>   left_join(simulated_sbs, by = "treatment") |>   mutate(     Cmax_pct_diff   = 100 * (Cmax_sim   - Cmax_pub)   / Cmax_pub,     AUCtau_pct_diff = 100 * (AUCtau_sim - AUCtau_pub) / AUCtau_pub   )  knitr::kable(   comparison,   digits  = 1,   caption = "Simulated vs published steady-state exposures (Chua 2025 Section 3.1.1)." )

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: SERENITY replicated. paper reports separate phase 2 popPK analysis (SERENITY) different covariates (SES-CD power-ALB CL, linear BMI effect F linear scale). analysis reproduced package. Users needing SERENITY estimates refer Chua 2025 Table 2 directly. BMI centering logit(F). Table 2 footnote e states F parameterized logit scale linear BMI slope, report BMI centering value used VIVID-1 fit. SERENITY analysis uses centering 24.75 kg/m^2 (also Table 2 footnote e); value adopted reasonable default. affect typical-value F = 38.8% BMI = 24.75. Residual error. Chua 2025 tabulate residual error Table 2; estimates shown supplementary model-code figure (Figure S13), available structured data. combined proportional 20% additive 0.10 ug/mL error assumed reasonable default IgG mAb popPK model. Users fitting model data re-estimate. Covariate distributions. WT, ALB, CRP, BMI marginals sampled independently; paper publish joint distributions. Marginals chosen approximate VIVID-1 demographics Table 1. Time-varying albumin simplification. ALB treated time-varying source model held constant baseline draw simulation. paper’s covariate-effect magnitude modest (CL shifts ~6% per +3 g/L ALB around 44.57 g/L), minor approximation steady-state exposures shown . Unit convention. published rate parameters reported h^-1 Chua 2025 Table 2; converted day^-1 nlmixr2lib (time unit = day).

title: Chua_2025_mirikizumab
text: Chua L, Otani Y, Lin Z, Friedrich S, Durand F, Zhang XC. Mirikizumab Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Patients Moderately--Severely Active Crohn’s Disease: Results Two Randomized Studies. Clin Transl Sci. 2025;18(8):e70320. doi:10.1111/cts.70320

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Citation: Cirincione B, Mager DE. Population pharmacokinetics exenatide. British Journal Clinical Pharmacology. 2017;83(3):517-526. doi:10.1111/bcp.13135 Description: Population PK model exenatide immediate-release (Cirincione 2017): two-compartment, parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination, sequential zero-order saturable first-order absorption SC dosing. Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2017;83(3):517-526

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: published analysis pooled 195 subjects 8 clinical trials, including healthy volunteers patients type 2 diabetes mellitus. Baseline demographics Cirincione 2017 Table 1: 22% female, 67% Caucasian; age 18-76 years (mean 48, SD 12.9); body weight 52.6-162 kg (mean 86, SD 16.2); MDRD eGFR ranged 4.1 mL/min/1.73 m^2 (ESRD) >= 90 (normal), pooled mean 88.7 (SD 31.4). Renal-function distribution: 129 normal, 47 mild impairment, 10 moderate, 1 severe, 8 ESRD. Single repeated SC doses 2.5-20 ug. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Cirincione_2017_exenatide")$population.

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Cirincione_2017_exenatide.R. table collects review.

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Table 1 demographics: body weight truncated normal centered 86 kg (SD 16.2), CRCL distribution matching reported renal-function bands. non-Study-1 / non-Study-5 (pooled “”) residual group simulated NCA comparison uses largest share original cohort. fixed 10 ug single SC dose used match paper’s Figure 5 reference dose.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 500  renal_bands <- tibble::tibble(   band       = c("normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe", "ESRD"),   n_in_paper = c(129, 47, 10, 1, 8),   egfr_min   = c(90, 60, 30, 15, 4.1),   egfr_max   = c(140, 89, 59, 29, 14) ) renal_bands$frac <- renal_bands$n_in_paper / sum(renal_bands$n_in_paper)  sample_band <- sample(renal_bands$band, n_subj, replace = TRUE, prob = renal_bands$frac) egfr_draws <- vapply(sample_band, function(b) {   row <- renal_bands[renal_bands$band == b, ]   runif(1, row$egfr_min, row$egfr_max) }, numeric(1))  cohort <- tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 86, sd = 16.2), 52.6), 162),   CRCL = egfr_draws,   STUDY1 = 0L,   STUDY5 = 0L,   treatment = factor(sample_band, levels = c("normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe", "ESRD")) )  events <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 10, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, time = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, CRCL, STUDY1, STUDY5, treatment) |>   dplyr::bind_rows(     cohort |>       tidyr::crossing(time = c(seq(0, 4, 0.1), seq(4.2, 12, 0.2))) |>       dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, CRCL, STUDY1, STUDY5, treatment)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: deterministic replication (typical-value predictions without -subject variability, matching paper’s Figure 5):
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Cirincione_2017_exenatide")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' conc_unit <- mod$units[["concentration"]] sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = c("WT", "CRCL", "treatment")) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> Warning: No sigma parameters in the model  typical_scenarios <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1:6,   scenario = c("75 kg, CRCL 90", "100 kg, CRCL 90", "125 kg, CRCL 90",     "75 kg, CRCL 75", "75 kg, CRCL 45", "median (84.8 kg, CRCL 80)"),   WT = c(75, 100, 125, 75, 75, 84.8),   CRCL = c(90, 90, 90, 75, 45, 80),   STUDY1 = 0L,   STUDY5 = 0L )  ev_typical <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(typical_scenarios)), function(i) {   ev_i <- rxode2::et(amt = 10, cmt = "depot") |>     rxode2::et(seq(0, 12, 0.05)) |>     as.data.frame()   ev_i$id <- typical_scenarios$id[i]   ev_i }))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical,   params = typical_scenarios[, c("id", "WT", "CRCL", "STUDY1", "STUDY5")],   returnType = "tibble" ) |>   dplyr::left_join(typical_scenarios[, c("id", "scenario")], by = "id") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalkm' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 5 — typical concentration by body weight and CRCL
title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 shows simulated typical concentration-time profiles 10 ug SC dose across body weight CRCL scenarios. Lower body weight lower CRCL increase exposure (lower central volume lower linear clearance, respectively).
code: sim_typical |>   ggplot(aes(time, Cc, colour = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = paste0("Exenatide concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     colour = NULL,     title = "Typical concentration profiles (replicates Figure 5)",     caption = "Replicates Figure 5 of Cirincione 2017: 10 ug SC, typical values.") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: VPC-style summary simulated cohort, stratified renal-function band.
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = paste0("Exenatide concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles by renal-function band",     caption = "10 ug SC; cohort covariate distributions approximate Table 1.") #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, AUC0-last, terminal half-life using PKNCA. Groups renal-function band (treatment stratifier) summary can compared paper’s typical values.
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = 12,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #> Warning: Requesting an
