title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: paper reports typical Cmax 75 kg approximately 287, 273, 321 pg/mL CRCL 90, 75, 45 mL/min/1.73 m^2, respectively (Figure 5), typical terminal half-life ~2.4 h. deterministic (“typical”) check simulated cohort: Simulated typical values (cmax, tmax, half.life) vs. Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 / typical half-life.
code: nca_typical <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(   PKNCA::PKNCAconc(     sim_typical |>       dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>       dplyr::mutate(treatment = scenario) |>       dplyr::distinct(id, time, treatment, .keep_all = TRUE) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment),     Cc ~ time | treatment + id   ),   PKNCA::PKNCAdose(     typical_scenarios |>       dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 10, treatment = scenario) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment),     amt ~ time | treatment + id   ),   intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 12, cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE, half.life = TRUE) ))  nca_typical_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_typical$result) |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "half.life")) |>   dplyr::select(treatment, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES)  published <- tibble::tribble(   ~treatment, ~cmax_pub, ~thalf_pub,   "75 kg, CRCL 90", 287, 2.4,   "100 kg, CRCL 90", 237, 2.4,   "125 kg, CRCL 90", 189, 2.4,   "75 kg, CRCL 75", 273, 2.4,   "75 kg, CRCL 45", 321, 2.4,   "median (84.8 kg, CRCL 80)", NA, 2.4 )  dplyr::left_join(published, nca_typical_tbl, by = "treatment") |>   knitr::kable(digits = 2,     caption = "Simulated typical values (cmax, tmax, half.life) vs. Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 / typical half-life.")

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: source column DVID (character-valued: "study1" / "study5" / "otherStudy") split two binary indicators (STUDY1, STUDY5) per nlmixr2lib covariate-columns.md register. STUDY1 = STUDY5 = 0 selects pooled-residual error. Km reported paper 567 pg/mL. model encoded ng/mL (0.567) remain unit-consistent Cc = central / vc (ug/L = ng/mL); observation subsequently multiplied 1000 report pg/mL. virtual cohort approximates Table 1 demographics. Race distribution used model therefore reproduced. CRCL sampled uniformly within renal-function band using paper’s per-band counts. IIV variance encoded log(1 + CV^2) (log-normal variance CV) rather shorthand log(1 + CV) prior version file used. matches standard NONMEM → nlmixr2 interpretation paper reports CV% log-normal random effects. Typical-value Cmax agrees Figure 5 within ~10% across weight CRCL scenarios; simulated terminal half-life ~2.4 h matches published value. Differences corrected tuning parameters.

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Replicate Figures 4, 5, 6 Clegg et al. (2024): Figure 4: Prediction-corrected VPC final model, stratified trial RSV season (Phase 2b, MELODY, MEDLEY Season 1, MEDLEY Season 2). Figure 5: Prediction-corrected VPC MELODY safety cohort. Figure 6: Predicted exposure (AUC₀₋₃₆₅, C_max, Day-151 concentration) versus body weight gestational age (GA) group weight-banded dosing. original study data publicly available, figures show simulated prediction intervals virtual infant populations whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics (Table 1, Clegg 2024). Body weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0–24 months).

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0–24 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). Formula: weight (kg) = M × (1 + L × S × z)^(1/L)

what: Helper: build a virtual cohort dataset
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Builds dose + observation records virtual cohort n infants. ga_range: gestational age range weeks. pna0_range: postnatal age dosing range months. max_day: follow-duration days. season2: TRUE Season 2 (200 mg flat dose, Season 2 CL effect applied). obs_days: observation time points days. id_offset: integer added seq_len(n) multiple cohorts can bind_rows()-ed without colliding ID. rxSolve treats ID subject key; duplicate IDs across cohorts silently collapse single “Frankenstein” subject receives summed dose, non-zero offset mandatory multi-cohort simulation (see Figure 4).
code: make_cohort <- function(n, ga_range, pna0_range, max_day,                         season2 = FALSE, obs_days = seq(0, max_day, by = 7),                         id_offset = 0L) {   GA <- runif(n, ga_range[1], ga_range[2]) # weeks   PNA_0 <- runif(n, pna0_range[1], pna0_range[2]) # months at dosing   wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n, 0, 1)))   WT_0 <- who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   AMT <- if (season2) rep(200, n) else ifelse(WT_0 < 5, 50, 100)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = id_offset + seq_len(n), GA, PNA_0, wt_z, WT_0, AMT,     SEASON2 = as.integer(season2),     ADA_POS = 0L,     # All White/Nat.Haw reference race for simplicity     RACE_BLACK_OTH = 0L,     RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI = 0L   )    # Dose records   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(       TIME = 0, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA_0,       WT = WT_0     )    # Observation records with time-varying WT and PAGE   d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(obs_days)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_days, times = n),       EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA, AMT = 0,       PNA = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z)     )    bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PAGE,       RACE_BLACK_OTH, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI, SEASON2, ADA_POS, WT_0) }

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Clegg_2024_nirsevimab") conc_unit <- rxode2::rxode(mod)$units[["concentration"]] #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure 4 — Prediction-corrected VPC by trial and season
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Four panels matching published trials: vignette shows simulated prediction intervals (observed data; original data publicly available).
code:
set.seed(8897) # MEDI8897 = nirsevimab development code  # `id_offset` per cohort is mandatory: rxSolve uses ID as the subject key, # so duplicate IDs across cohorts silently merge into a single subject that # receives the summed dose. Without offsets, Figure 4's predictions came out # ~3-fold too high because IDs 1..100 appeared in all four cohorts and were # collapsed into Frankenstein subjects with ~400 mg in depot at TIME = 0. d_p2b <- make_cohort(200, c(29, 35), c(0, 3), 400, id_offset =   0L) |>   mutate(trial = "Phase 2b
(healthy infants, 29 to <35 wGA)") d_melody <- make_cohort(200, c(35, 42), c(0, 3), 500, id_offset = 200L) |>   mutate(trial = "MELODY
(healthy infants, ≥35 wGA)") d_med_s1 <- make_cohort(200, c(24, 35), c(0, 3), 400, id_offset = 400L) |>   mutate(trial = "MEDLEY Season 1
(infants ≤35 wGA, CHD/CLD)") d_med_s2 <- make_cohort(100, c(35, 42), c(12, 24), 400,   season2 = TRUE, id_offset = 600L) |>   mutate(trial = "MEDLEY Season 2
(children with CHD/CLD)")  sim_f4 <- bind_rows(d_p2b, d_melody, d_med_s1, d_med_s2) # Cheap regression guard: every (ID, TIME, EVID) triple must be unique. stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(sim_f4[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))  # Carry `trial` through rxSolve via `keep =` rather than a post-hoc left_join. # A `left_join(distinct(ID, trial))` fans out every row when an ID happens to # repeat across trials, silently re-pooling cohorts; `keep = "trial"` aligns # the trial label per output row. out_f4 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = sim_f4, keep = "trial") |>   as.data.frame() d_f4 <- out_f4 |>   group_by(trial, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(trial = factor(trial, levels = c(     "Phase 2b
(healthy infants, 29 to <35 wGA)",     "MELODY
(healthy infants, ≥35 wGA)",     "MEDLEY Season 1
(infants ≤35 wGA, CHD/CLD)",     "MEDLEY Season 2
(children with CHD/CLD)"   ))) ggplot(d_f4, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~trial, scales = "free_x", nrow = 2) +   scale_y_log10(     limits = c(0.1, NA),     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 500, by = 100)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = paste0("Nirsevimab serum concentration (", conc_unit, ")
Prediction corrected"),     title = "Figure 4 — Prediction-corrected VPC by trial and season",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated median (line) and 90% prediction interval (shaded).
",       "Original observed data not available; reference race = White, ADA negative."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 9)) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.1, NA), labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values.

what: Figure 5 — Prediction-corrected VPC for the MELODY safety cohort
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Healthy term late-preterm infants (≥35 weeks GA), Season 1, followed 360 days. paper uses 10th/50th/90th percentiles (rather 5th/95th used Figure 4).
code: set.seed(3979) # NCT03979313 = MELODY trial d_f5 <- make_cohort(500, c(35, 42), c(0, 3), 360,   obs_days = seq(0, 360, by = 7))  out_f5 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_f5) |> as.data.frame() d_f5_plot <- out_f5 |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(Cc, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(Cc, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) ggplot(d_f5_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q10), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q90), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +   scale_y_log10(     limits = c(0.5, NA),     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 360, by = 30)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = paste0("Nirsevimab serum concentration (", conc_unit, ")\nPrediction corrected"),     title = "Figure 5 \u2014 Prediction-corrected VPC: MELODY safety cohort",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated 10th/50th/90th percentiles (N = 500 virtual infants, \u226535 wGA).\n",       "Original observed data not available."     )   ) +   theme_bw() #> Warning in scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA), labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values.

what: Figure 6 — Predicted exposure versus body weight by GA group
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Simulate 3 GA groups (GA <29 weeks, ≥29 <35 weeks, ≥35 weeks), dosing birth, weight-banded dosing (50 mg ≤5 kg, 100 mg >5 kg). Compute AUC₀₋₃₆₅ (trapezoidal), C_max, Day-151 concentration. Use daily time steps accurate NCA. Adult reference lines paper (3000 mg IV dose): AUC₀₋₃₆₅ = 57.0 day·mg/mL; C_max = 1140 μg/mL. EC₉₀ (preclinical) = 6.8 μg/mL (Day-151 concentration panel).
code: set.seed(2024) n_per_ga <- 200 obs_daily <- seq(0, 365, by = 1)  ga_groups <- list(   list(label = "GA < 29 weeks", ga = c(24, 29)),   list(label = "GA \u226529 to <35 weeks", ga = c(29, 35)),   list(label = "GA \u226535 weeks", ga = c(35, 42)) )  sim_f6_list <- lapply(ga_groups, function(g) {   GA <- runif(n_per_ga, g$ga[1], g$ga[2])   PNA_0 <- rep(0, n_per_ga) # dose at birth   wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n_per_ga, 0, 1)))   WT_0 <- who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   AMT <- ifelse(WT_0 <= 5, 50, 100)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = seq_len(n_per_ga), GA, PNA_0, wt_z, WT_0, AMT,     SEASON2 = 0L, ADA_POS = 0L,     RACE_BLACK_OTH = 0L, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI = 0L   )    d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(TIME = 0, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA,       PAGE = GA / 4.35, WT = WT_0)    d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_per_ga), each = length(obs_daily)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_daily, times = n_per_ga),       EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA, AMT = 0,       PNA = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z)     )    d_events <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PAGE,       RACE_BLACK_OTH, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI, SEASON2, ADA_POS, WT_0)    out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_events) |>     as.data.frame()    # NCA by individual: AUC0_365 and Cmax via PKNCA; C_day151 as direct lookup   out_nca <- out |> filter(time >= 0, !is.na(Cc)) |> mutate(ga_group = g$label)    # Build PKNCA dose data from the per-subject dose amounts   dose_df <- pop |>     mutate(time = 0, ga_group = g$label) |>     select(id = ID, time, amt = AMT, ga_group)    conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(out_nca, Cc ~ time | ga_group + id)   dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | ga_group + id)   nca_res  <- pk.nca(PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj,     intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 365,                            auclast = TRUE, cmax = TRUE)))    nca_pknca <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>     filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "cmax")) |>     select(id, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>     tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES) |>     rename(AUC0_365 = auclast, Cmax = cmax)    # Day-151 concentration: direct lookup (not a standard NCA parameter)   c_day151 <- out |>     group_by(id) |>     summarise(C_day151 = Cc[which.min(abs(time - 151))],               WT_base  = WT_0[1], .groups = "drop")    nca <- left_join(nca_pknca, c_day151, by = "id") |>     mutate(ga_group = g$label) })  d_f6_nca <- bind_rows(sim_f6_list) |>   mutate(ga_group = factor(ga_group, levels = sapply(ga_groups, `[[`, "label"))) # 90% prediction intervals (5th-95th) by baseline weight bin wt_bins <- seq(1, 11, by = 0.5)  d_f6_long <- d_f6_nca |>   pivot_longer(c(AUC0_365, Cmax, C_day151), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") |>   mutate(wt_bin = cut(WT_base, breaks = wt_bins, right = FALSE))  d_f6_pi <- d_f6_long |>   group_by(ga_group, metric, wt_bin) |>   summarise(     wt_mid = mean(WT_base),     Q05 = quantile(value, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(value, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(value, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   filter(!is.na(wt_bin))  metric_labels <- c(   AUC0_365 = "AUC[0-365]~(day%.%mg/mL)",   Cmax     = "C[max]~(mu*g/mL)",   C_day151 = "C[day~151]~(mu*g/mL)" ) # Adult reference values (3000 mg IV) adult_refs <- data.frame(   metric = c("AUC0_365", "Cmax"),   yref   = c(57.0, 1140) ) ec90_val <- 6.8 # preclinical EC90, μg/mL  ggplot(d_f6_pi, aes(x = wt_mid)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#87ceeb", alpha = 0.6) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q50), linewidth = 0.8) +   # Individual posthoc predictions   geom_point(     data = d_f6_nca |>       pivot_longer(c(AUC0_365, Cmax, C_day151), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value"),     aes(x = WT_base, y = value),     colour = "grey50", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.4   ) +   # Adult reference lines (AUC and Cmax panels only)   geom_hline(     data = adult_refs,     aes(yintercept = yref),     linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7   ) +   # EC90 line (Day-151 panel only)   geom_hline(     data = data.frame(metric = "C_day151", yref = ec90_val),     aes(yintercept = yref),     linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7   ) +   facet_grid(     metric ~ ga_group,     scales = "free_y",     labeller = labeller(metric = as_labeller(metric_labels, label_parsed))   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 11, by = 2)) +   labs(     x = "Baseline weight (kg)",     y = NULL,     title = "Figure 6 \u2014 Predicted exposure vs body weight by GA group",     caption = paste0(       "Blue band: 90% prediction interval (5th\u201395th percentile). ",       "Black line: median. Grey dots: individual predictions.\n",       "Dotted line: adult reference (3000 mg IV). ",       "Dashed line: preclinical EC\u2090 = 6.8 \u03bcg/mL (Day-151 panel).\n",       "White race, ADA negative, Season 1 assumed."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(     strip.text.x = element_text(size = 9),     strip.text.y = element_text(size = 8)   ) #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Model: 2-compartment first-order IM absorption; published parameters 70 kg adult reference, scaled allometrically infant weights. Virtual populations: Covariate distributions match published trial demographics (Table 1). Race set White/Native Hawaiian reference ADA negative simulated subjects isolate weight maturation effects. Weight trajectories: weight--age LMS growth standards (combined sex, 0–24 months), infant’s growth percentile (z-score) held constant. IIV: Simulated using published omega² values: CL (26% CV), V2 (43% CV), Ka (44% CV; high shrinkage 83%), correlation r = 0.785 CL V2. Q V3 IIV final model. Residual error: Proportional 21%. Figure 6 NCA: AUC₀₋₃₆₅ C_max computed via PKNCA daily simulated concentrations; Day-151 concentration derived direct lookup densely sampled time course.

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Clegg L, Freshwater E, Leach , Villafana T, Wahlby Hamren U. Population Pharmacokinetics Nirsevimab Preterm Term Infants. J Clin Pharmacol. 2024;64(5):555-567. doi:10.1002/jcph.2401

what: Daclizumab HYP CD25 receptor occupancy PK/PD model
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: Daclizumab high-yield process (HYP) humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody binds alpha-subunit high-affinity interleukin-2 receptor (CD25). Diao et al. (2016) developed sigmoidal maximum-response (Emax) PK/PD model linking daclizumab HYP serum concentration CD25 occupancy peripheral CD4+ T cells subjects relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS). model output percentage CD4+ T cells staining positive unoccupied CD25 (.e., 100% means daclizumab bound 56% typical baseline value). PK backbone inherited Othman 2014 (two-compartment, first-order SC absorption lag, allometric weight scaling); Diao 2016 PD analysis fixed PK previously published RRMS population PK model added algebraic CD25 binding equation. nlmixr2lib version uses Othman 2014 healthy-volunteer PK canonical daclizumab HYP PK library coherence (see Assumptions deviations). Citation: Diao L, Hang Y, Othman AA, et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2016;82(5):1333-1342. Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.13051 PMID: 27333593

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: pooled PK/PD analysis included 1459 RRMS subjects 7622 CD25 occupancy records four daclizumab HYP clinical studies (Diao 2016 Table 2): Subject-level demographics SELECT, SELECTION, OBSERVE DECIDE PK/PD subgroups reported companion population PK analysis (Diao 2016 reference [13]) enumerated Diao 2016 . metadata available programmatically readModelDb("Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25")$population.

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comments next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25.R. table collects review.

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: Individual data public. simulation covers two regimens (150 mg SC 300 mg SC every 4 weeks), 6 dose cycles, 24 weeks washout; matches SELECTION trial design informed saturation phase (intensive substudy OBSERVE) desaturation phase (washout cohort). dosing schedule : dose days 0, 28, 56, 84, 112, 140 (6 doses 5 months), washout 24 weeks. PD sampling dense first dose (8 h, 24 h, 72 h, 120 h, 7 d, 14 d capture saturation kinetics) weekly chronic / washout phases.
code: set.seed(2016) n_per_arm <- 50 cohort <- bind_rows(   tibble(id = seq_len(n_per_arm),                 dose_mg = 150, regimen = "150 mg SC Q4W"),   tibble(id = n_per_arm + seq_len(n_per_arm),     dose_mg = 300, regimen = "300 mg SC Q4W") ) |>   mutate(WT = pmin(120, pmax(45, rnorm(n(), 71, 14))),  # SELECT/DECIDE adult RRMS weights          DOSE_50MG = 0L) dose_times <- seq(0, 140, by = 28)               # 6 doses Q4W obs_times  <- sort(unique(c(0, 8/24, 1, 3, 5, 7, 14, 21,                             seq(28, 350, by = 7))))  sim_one <- function(sub) {   ev <- rxode2::et(amt = sub$dose_mg, time = dose_times, cmt = "depot") |>     rxode2::et(obs_times, cmt = "Cc") |>     rxode2::et(obs_times, cmt = "cd25")   ev_df <- as.data.frame(ev)   ev_df$id        <- sub$id   ev_df$WT        <- sub$WT   ev_df$DOSE_50MG <- sub$DOSE_50MG   ev_df }  events <- cohort |>   dplyr::group_split(id) |>   lapply(sim_one) |>   dplyr::bind_rows() |>   dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(cohort, id, regimen, dose_mg), by = "id")  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid", "cmt")])))

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: Two parallel simulations: stochastic VPC (full IIV) deterministic typical-value run figure replication.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25")  # `regimen` is already on every row of `events` (per-id from the cohort # left_join above) — carry it through `rxSolve(keep = ...)` so we don't # need a fragile post-hoc `left_join` on the simulation output. set.seed(2016) sim_pop <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events, returnType = "data.frame",                            keep = "regimen") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events, returnType = "data.frame",                            keep = "regimen") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etacd25E0', 'etalcd25IC50' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 1A: rapid CD25 saturation after the first 150 mg SC dose
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: Diao 2016 Figure 1A shows simulated unoccupied CD25 first 24 hours 150 mg SC dose. published profile reaches near-zero unoccupied CD25 within ~7 hours.
code: fig1a <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(regimen == "150 mg SC Q4W", time <= 1, time >= 0)  ggplot(fig1a, aes(time * 24, cd25)) +   geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 60)) +   labs(     x = "Time after 1st dose (h)",     y = "Unoccupied CD25 (% of CD4+ T cells)",     title = "Figure 1A - rapid CD25 saturation after 150 mg SC",     caption = "Replicates Figure 1A of Diao 2016 (typical value, 70 kg adult)."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 1B: slow CD25 desaturation after last steady-state 150 mg SC dose
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: Diao 2016 Figure 1B shows return unoccupied CD25 baseline ~24 weeks last steady-state 150 mg SC dose. desaturation IC50 2.07 mg/L drives slow return; Cc ~1 mg/L population fraction unoccupied climbs back baseline range.
code: last_dose_t <- 140 fig1b <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(regimen == "150 mg SC Q4W", time >= last_dose_t) |>   dplyr::mutate(weeks_after_last = (time - last_dose_t) / 7)  ggplot(fig1b, aes(weeks_after_last, cd25)) +   geom_line(color = "darkorange3", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 60)) +   labs(     x = "Weeks after last dose",     y = "Unoccupied CD25 (% of CD4+ T cells)",     title = "Figure 1B - CD25 desaturation after the last 150 mg SC dose",     caption = "Replicates Figure 1B of Diao 2016 (typical value, 70 kg adult)."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: stochastic visual predictive check (VPC) regimens.
code: vpc <- sim_pop |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= 350, !is.na(cd25)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, regimen) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(cd25, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(cd25, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(cd25, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time / 7, Q50, color = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, color = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~regimen) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Unoccupied CD25 (% of CD4+ T cells)",     title = "Stochastic VPC: CD25 occupancy by daclizumab HYP",     caption = "5th-95th-percentile envelope across virtual subjects."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: PD model produces unoccupied-CD25 percentages, amenable standard non-compartmental PK metrics. PKNCA run inherited PK output confirm steady-state Cmax / Cmin / AUC 150 mg 300 mg SC Q4W regimens (one dosing-interval steady state). Steady-state (dose 5) NCA regimen.
code: sim_conc <- sim_pop |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 112, time <= 140) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_in_interval = time - 112) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = time_in_interval, Cc = Cc, regimen = regimen)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == 112) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = 0, amt = amt, regimen = regimen)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_conc, Cc ~ time | regimen + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | regimen + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start = 0, end = 28,   cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE,   tmax = TRUE, auclast = TRUE )  nca <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) knitr::kable(summary(nca, drop.group = "id"),              caption = "Steady-state (dose 5) NCA by regimen.") #> Warning: The `drop.group` argument of `summary.PKNCAresults()` is deprecated as of PKNCA #> 0.11.0. #> ℹ Please use the `drop_group` argument instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: Diao 2016 reports qualitative behavioural targets CD25 occupancy model rather tabulated NCA-style PD metrics. relevant simulated checkpoints listed ; see figures full profiles. CD25 saturation / desaturation checkpoints.
code: last_dose_t <- 140  # At Cc >= 5 mg/L during dosing, occupancy is fully maintained (Diao 2016 Discussion). # Saturation attained ~7 h after the first dose. typ_first <- dplyr::filter(sim_typ, regimen == "150 mg SC Q4W", time >= 0, time <= 1) saturation_t_h <- typ_first$time[which(typ_first$cd25 < 0.05 * 56)[1]] * 24  # After last dose, return to baseline at ~24 weeks (Diao 2016 Discussion). typ_after <- dplyr::filter(sim_typ, regimen == "150 mg SC Q4W", time >= last_dose_t) return_t_w <- (typ_after$time[which(typ_after$cd25 > 0.5 * 56)[1]] - last_dose_t) / 7  cmp <- tibble(   metric = c("Time to >95% saturation after first 150 mg SC dose (h)",              "Time to >50% baseline recovery after last 150 mg SC dose (weeks)"),   published = c("~7 h", "~24 weeks"),   simulated = c(sprintf("%.1f h", saturation_t_h),                 sprintf("%.1f weeks", return_t_w)) ) knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "CD25 saturation / desaturation checkpoints.")

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: trimmed PDF full PDF show several non-substantive transcription oddities arising PDF text extraction (mathematical operators rendered /C0, /C18, /C1; subscripts collapsed baseline). paper errata. Two model-relevant ambiguities documented future user can audit: Two parameter sets single Hill equation. Diao 2016 Table 3 reports two IC50 / Hill coefficient pairs (saturation 0.0135 mg/L, Hill = 1, fixed; desaturation 2.07 mg/L, Hill = 4.44, estimated) published Equation (1) single 1 - Cc^gamma / (Cc^gamma + IC50^gamma) Hill function. narrative explains saturation pair fixed OBSERVE intensive substudy desaturation pair estimated SELECTION washout data, operative phase-switching rule (e.g., direction change Cc, hysteresis loop, two effect compartments) specified paper appendices. library implementation uses desaturation parameters; see Assumptions deviations. Hill coefficient point estimate without precision indicator (Table 5 / Treg row). Treg Hill = 2 SE, FIXED tag, bootstrap CI Table 5. companion Treg vignette treats structurally fixed value. (Recorded cross-paper consistency; issue Table 5 Treg model, CD25 model.)

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd25
text: PK backbone Othman 2014, -paper PK summary. Diao 2016 Methods reports different population PK model (CL = 0.212 L/day 68 kg, allometric exponents 0.87/1.12, F = 0.88, t1/2,abs = 5 days, Tlag = 1.61 h) fit RRMS subjects separate publication (Diao 2016 reference [13]). packaged PD model inherits Othman 2014 healthy-volunteer PK structure parameter values (CL = 0.24 L/day 70 kg, allometric exponents 0.54/0.64, F = 0.84, t1/2,abs ~ ln(2)/0.216 = 3.2 days, Tlag = 2 h) consistency canonical daclizumab HYP PK library. effect substitution CD25 occupancy time course small clinical doses CD25 saturates almost immediately desaturation IC50 2.07 mg/L large relative typical trough; users need exact reproduction Diao 2016 PK can override relevant ini() entries calling model. specify. typical clinical concentrations (Cc ~5-15 ug/mL dosing) desaturation Hill predicts >97% occupancy, Cc ~1 ug/mL washout predicts ~4% occupancy, recapitulating published “return baseline ~24 weeks” qualitative target. Hill coefficient treated structurally fixed. Diao 2016 Table 3 reports desaturation Hill coefficient = 4.44 bootstrap 95% CI 3.19-5.19. library encodes fixed() structural value rather estimated theta model used simulation, estimation; users fitting model data can release fix ini(). Baseline-IIV additive, log-normal. Diao 2016 Table 3 labels baseline IIV “(additive) 11” (.e., SD = 11 percentage points linear scale). library encodes faithfully etacd25E0 ~ 121 cd25E0_i = cd25E0 + etacd25E0 model body. deviates usual nlmixr2lib pattern eta log-transformed parameters published form. Virtual-cohort weight distribution. Body weight sampled N(71, 14) kg truncated 45-120 kg; SELECT / DECIDE RRMS adult population summary inferred daclizumab HYP Phase 2/3 program. Sex, age, race, neutralizing-antibody status, PK covariates significant CD25 PD parameters (Diao 2016 report PD-side covariate effects) simulated.

what: Daclizumab HYP CD56 bright NK cell expansion PK/PD model
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: CD56 bright natural killer (NK) cells expand daclizumab high-yield process (HYP) treatment, hypothesized result increased availability T-cell-derived IL-2 daclizumab HYP blocks high-affinity IL-2 receptor activated T cells. Diao et al. (2016) characterized expansion using indirect-response model daclizumab HYP serum concentration stimulates zero-order production rate (Kin) CD56 bright NK cells (% total lymphocytes) via saturable Smax function; elimination rate constant Kout expressed via baseline (Kout = Kin / NK_baseline) therefore independently estimated. PK backbone inherited Othman 2014 (two-compartment first-order SC absorption lag, allometric weight scaling); see companion Othman_2014_daclizumab vignette PK details. Citation: Diao L, Hang Y, Othman AA, et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2016;82(5):1333-1342. Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.13051 PMID: 27333593

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: pooled PK/PD analysis included 1405 RRMS subjects 9630 CD56 bright NK cell records four daclizumab HYP clinical studies (Diao 2016 Table 2): median (mean) baseline CD56 bright NK percentage 0.6% (0.75%) total lymphocytes (Diao 2016 Results, NK section). library implementation uses median baseline structural value; users fitting model data can override cd56baseline ini().

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: Original-data public. simulation covers 150 mg SC every 4 weeks one year, 24 weeks washout, re-initiation 150 mg SC every 4 weeks another year (matching SELECT / SELECTION washout / re-initiation design used characterize slow expansion plateau reversibility).
code: set.seed(2016) n_subjects <- 100 cohort <- tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subjects),   WT = pmin(120, pmax(45, rnorm(n_subjects, 71, 14))),   DOSE_50MG = 0L ) # Year 1: 13 doses Q4W from t = 0 to t = 336. # Washout: 24 weeks (no doses) from t = 336 to t = 504. # Year 2: 13 doses Q4W from t = 504 to t = 840. dose_times <- c(seq(0, 336, by = 28), seq(504, 840, by = 28)) obs_times  <- sort(unique(c(0, 7, 14, 21, seq(28, 1008, by = 7))))  sim_one <- function(sub) {   ev <- rxode2::et(amt = 150, time = dose_times, cmt = "depot") |>     rxode2::et(obs_times, cmt = "Cc") |>     rxode2::et(obs_times, cmt = "cd56bright")   ev_df <- as.data.frame(ev)   ev_df$id        <- sub$id   ev_df$WT        <- sub$WT   ev_df$DOSE_50MG <- sub$DOSE_50MG   ev_df }  events <- cohort |>   dplyr::group_split(id) |>   lapply(sim_one) |>   dplyr::bind_rows()  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid", "cmt")])))

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
code: mod     <- readModelDb("Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright") mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  set.seed(2016) sim_pop <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalcd56Kin', 'etalcd56Smax' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 2: CD56 bright NK expansion + washout + re-initiation
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: Diao 2016 Figure 2 simulates 150 mg SC Q4W 48 weeks, 24 weeks treatment discontinuation, re-initiation 150 mg SC Q4W another 48 weeks. published profile reaches expansion plateau ~week 36 maximum expansion ratio ~5.2; last dose, NK percentage gradually declines baseline within ~24 weeks.
code: fig2 <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(cd56bright), time <= 1008) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::mutate(     weeks = time / 7,     expansion_ratio = cd56bright / 0.6   )  ggplot(fig2, aes(weeks, expansion_ratio, group = id)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.30, color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.4) +   stat_summary(aes(group = 1), geom = "line", fun = median,                color = "darkblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 5.2, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x = 50, y = 5.5, label = "5.2 (published plateau)",            hjust = 0, size = 3, color = "grey40") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 144, by = 24)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "CD56 bright NK expansion ratio (NK / baseline)",     title = "Figure 2 - CD56 bright NK expansion, washout, re-initiation",     caption = paste0(       "Replicates Figure 2 of Diao 2016. 150 mg SC Q4W for 48 wk; ",       "24-wk washout; re-initiation 150 mg SC Q4W for 48 wk."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
code: vpc <- sim_pop |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(cd56bright)) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(cd56bright, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(cd56bright, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(cd56bright, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time / 7, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95),               fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.20) +   geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "CD56 bright NK (% of total lymphocytes)",     title = "Stochastic VPC: CD56 bright NK by daclizumab HYP",     caption = "5th-95th-percentile envelope across the virtual cohort."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: CD56 bright NK percentage amenable standard NCA. PKNCA run inherited PK output confirm steady-state PK profile cohort. Steady-state (dose 12) NCA, 150 mg SC Q4W.
code: sim_conc <- sim_pop |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 308, time <= 336) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_in_interval = time - 308) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = time_in_interval, Cc = Cc,                    regimen = "150 mg SC Q4W")  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == 308) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = 0, amt = amt,                    regimen = "150 mg SC Q4W")  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_conc, Cc ~ time | regimen + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | regimen + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start = 0, end = 28,   cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE,   tmax = TRUE, auclast = TRUE )  nca <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) knitr::kable(summary(nca, drop.group = "id"),              caption = "Steady-state (dose 12) NCA, 150 mg SC Q4W.") #> Warning: The `drop.group` argument of `summary.PKNCAresults()` is deprecated as of PKNCA #> 0.11.0. #> ℹ Please use the `drop_group` argument instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: Diao 2016 reports qualitative behavioural targets CD56 bright NK expansion model rather tabulated PD metrics; relevant checkpoints summarised . CD56 bright NK expansion / recovery checkpoints.
code: typ <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(cd56bright)) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::mutate(weeks = time / 7,                 expansion = cd56bright / 0.6)  # Plateau between weeks 32 and 48 (just before first washout). plateau   <- typ |> dplyr::filter(weeks >= 32, weeks <= 48) peak_plat <- max(plateau$expansion, na.rm = TRUE)  # Time after week-48 last dose (week 48 = day 336) for return to ~baseline. after_w48 <- typ |> dplyr::filter(time >= 336, time <= 504) return_t_w <- (after_w48$time[which(after_w48$expansion < 1.20)[1]] - 336) / 7  cmp <- tibble(   metric    = c("Expansion plateau (week 36, ratio NK / baseline)",                 "Time to return within 20% of baseline after washout (weeks)"),   published = c("~5.2", "~24"),   simulated = c(sprintf("%.2f", peak_plat),                 sprintf("%.1f", return_t_w)) ) knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "CD56 bright NK expansion / recovery checkpoints.")

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: trimmed PDF DOCX appendices show several non-substantive extraction artifacts (operators rendered /C0, scientific notation collapsed; etc.) paper errata. Two model-relevant ambiguities documented: Baseline value used derive Kout labelled Table 4. Diao 2016 Methods states “Kout expressed function Kin using baseline information,” Table 4 list baseline value used. Results narrative gives “median (mean) CD56 bright NK cell percentage 0.6% 0.75%”. library implementation uses 0.6% (median) cd56baseline. Using 0.75% (mean) instead shift structural Kout 0.009888/0.6 = 0.01648 /day 0.009888/0.75 = 0.01318 /day, giving slightly slower turnover. EC50 IIV reported Table 4. Kin Smax carry IIV Table 4. library encodes eta lcd56EC50 (exp(lcd56EC50) typical-value EC50, random component).

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_cd56bright
text: PK backbone Othman 2014, -paper PK summary. Diao 2016 fixed PK published RRMS PopPK model (CL = 0.212 L/day 68 kg, allometric exponents 0.87 / 1.12, F = 0.88, t1/2,abs ~ 5 days, Tlag = 1.61 h). packaged PD model uses canonical daclizumab HYP PK Othman 2014 (CL = 0.24 L/day 70 kg, exponents 0.54 / 0.64, F = 0.84, ka = 0.216 /day, Tlag = 2 h). numerical PK differences (~10-20% Cc) propagate saturable Smax * Cc / (EC50 + Cc) term; clinical Cc 5-15 ug/mL function far saturated (EC50 = 18 mg/L), expansion ratios sensitive PK assumption. Users need exact reproduction Diao 2016 Figure 2 numerical values can override inherited PK ini() entries. Median baseline used Kout. noted Errata, library uses median 0.6% rather mean 0.75%. Using mean 0.75% shift simulated NK percentage curves slightly upward absolute terms leave expansion ratio (NK / baseline) close unchanged. PD covariates. Diao 2016 report covariate effects NK PD parameters; covariates packaged model inherited Othman 2014 PK backbone (WT, DOSE_50MG). Virtual-cohort weight distribution. Body weight sampled N(71, 14) kg truncated 45-120 kg (SELECT / DECIDE adult RRMS population summary).

what: Daclizumab HYP regulatory T cell (Treg) reduction PK/PD model
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: CD25 constitutively expressed high levels regulatory T cells (Treg, defined CD4+ CD127low/- Foxp3+). Daclizumab high-yield process (HYP) reduces circulating Treg counts treatment despite maintaining clinical efficacy RRMS. Diao et al. (2016) characterized reduction algebraic sigmoidal Emax model linking daclizumab HYP serum concentration directly Treg percentage CD4+ T cells; adding delay / effect compartment improve fit (Diao 2016 Discussion). PK backbone inherited Othman 2014 (two-compartment first-order SC absorption lag, allometric weight scaling); see companion Othman_2014_daclizumab vignette PK details. Citation: Diao L, Hang Y, Othman AA, et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2016;82(5):1333-1342. Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.13051 PMID: 27333593

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: pooled PK/PD analysis included 1353 RRMS subjects 8742 Treg records four daclizumab HYP clinical studies (Diao 2016 Table 2): typical baseline Treg percentage among CD4+ T cells 12.1% (Diao 2016 Table 5).

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: simulation covers 150 mg SC every 4 weeks 6 cycles, 24 weeks washout, mirroring schedule used characterise rapid Treg reduction (~4 days post-dose) ~20-week return baseline last dose.
code: set.seed(2016) n_subjects <- 100 cohort <- tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subjects),   WT = pmin(120, pmax(45, rnorm(n_subjects, 71, 14))),   DOSE_50MG = 0L ) dose_times <- seq(0, 140, by = 28)             # 6 doses Q4W obs_times  <- sort(unique(c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 10, 14, 21,                             seq(28, 350, by = 7))))  sim_one <- function(sub) {   ev <- rxode2::et(amt = 150, time = dose_times, cmt = "depot") |>     rxode2::et(obs_times, cmt = "Cc") |>     rxode2::et(obs_times, cmt = "treg")   ev_df <- as.data.frame(ev)   ev_df$id        <- sub$id   ev_df$WT        <- sub$WT   ev_df$DOSE_50MG <- sub$DOSE_50MG   ev_df }  events <- cohort |>   dplyr::group_split(id) |>   lapply(sim_one) |>   dplyr::bind_rows()  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid", "cmt")])))

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
code: mod     <- readModelDb("Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg") mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  set.seed(2016) sim_pop <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etaltregE0', 'etaltregIC50', 'etaltregEmax' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 3A: rapid Treg reduction after a 150 mg SC dose
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: Diao 2016 Figure 3A simulates Treg time course first ~14 days following 150 mg SC daclizumab HYP dose. published profile reaches maximum reduction ~60% (.e., Treg falls ~12.1% ~4.7%) approximately 4 days post-dose.
code: fig3a <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(treg), time <= 14) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE)  ggplot(fig3a, aes(time, treg)) +   geom_line(color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 12.1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 12.1 * (1 - 0.61), linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 14)) +   labs(     x = "Time after 1st dose (days)",     y = "Treg (% of CD4+ T cells)",     title = "Figure 3A - rapid Treg reduction after 150 mg SC",     caption = paste0(       "Replicates Figure 3A of Diao 2016 (typical value, 70 kg). ",       "Dashed line = baseline (12.1%); dotted line = 60% reduction (~4.7%)."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 3B: Treg recovery after the last steady-state 150 mg SC dose
title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: Diao 2016 Figure 3B shows return Treg baseline ~20 weeks last steady-state 150 mg SC dose.
code: last_dose_t <- 140 fig3b <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(treg), time >= last_dose_t) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::mutate(weeks_after_last = (time - last_dose_t) / 7)  ggplot(fig3b, aes(weeks_after_last, treg)) +   geom_line(color = "darkorange3", linewidth = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 12.1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 14)) +   labs(     x = "Weeks after last dose",     y = "Treg (% of CD4+ T cells)",     title = "Figure 3B - Treg recovery after the last 150 mg SC dose",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3B of Diao 2016. Dashed line = baseline (12.1%)."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
code: vpc <- sim_pop |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(treg)) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(treg, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(treg, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(treg, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time / 7, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95),               fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.20) +   geom_line(color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 12.1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Treg (% of CD4+ T cells)",     title = "Stochastic VPC: Treg by daclizumab HYP",     caption = "5th-95th-percentile envelope across the virtual cohort."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: Treg percentage amenable standard NCA. PKNCA run inherited PK output confirm steady-state (dose 5) Cmax / Cmin / AUC across one dosing interval. Steady-state (dose 5) NCA, 150 mg SC Q4W.
code: sim_conc <- sim_pop |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 112, time <= 140) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_in_interval = time - 112) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = time_in_interval, Cc = Cc,                    regimen = "150 mg SC Q4W")  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == 112) |>   dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = 0, amt = amt,                    regimen = "150 mg SC Q4W")  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_conc, Cc ~ time | regimen + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | regimen + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start = 0, end = 28,   cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE,   tmax = TRUE, auclast = TRUE )  nca <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) knitr::kable(summary(nca, drop.group = "id"),              caption = "Steady-state (dose 5) NCA, 150 mg SC Q4W.") #> Warning: The `drop.group` argument of `summary.PKNCAresults()` is deprecated as of PKNCA #> 0.11.0. #> ℹ Please use the `drop_group` argument instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: Diao 2016 reports two narrative checkpoints Treg reduction model: Treg reduction / recovery checkpoints.
code: typ <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(treg)) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE)  # Maximum reduction within first 14 days post first dose. first_window <- typ |> dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= 14) min_treg <- min(first_window$treg, na.rm = TRUE) t_min   <- first_window$time[which.min(first_window$treg)] max_red <- (12.1 - min_treg) / 12.1  # Time to return within 20% of baseline (10% absolute) after last dose. after_last <- typ |> dplyr::filter(time >= 140) recover_t_w <- (after_last$time[which(after_last$treg > 0.95 * 12.1)[1]] - 140) / 7  cmp <- tibble(   metric    = c("Time to maximum reduction post 150 mg SC (days)",                 "Maximum fractional Treg reduction post 150 mg SC",                 "Time to >95% baseline recovery after last dose (weeks)"),   published = c("~4 days", "~60%", "~20 weeks"),   simulated = c(sprintf("%.1f", t_min),                 sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * max_red),                 sprintf("%.1f", recover_t_w)) ) knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Treg reduction / recovery checkpoints.")

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: Several non-substantive PDF text-extraction artifacts (operators rendered /C0, etc.) appear trimmed source; paper errata. Two model-relevant ambiguities documented: Hill coefficient point estimate without precision indicator. Diao 2016 Table 5 lists gamma = 2 Treg model standard error, (FIXED) tag, bootstrap 95% CI 1.39 2.91. bootstrap CI wide enough encompass gamma = 1 (hyperbolic Emax), Table 5 row format (SE column populated alongside value) consistent value treated fixed structural parameter fitting. library encodes treggamma <- fixed(2) match. Residual additive error units. Diao 2016 Table 5 reports “Residual error (additive): 0.416” explicit unit string. Treg measurement scale “% CD4+ T cells” (per Table 5 baseline row), additive residual error interpreted 0.416 percentage points; proportional component (50.1%) taken CV scale (propSd_treg <- 0.501).

title: Diao_2016_daclizumab_treg
text: PK backbone Othman 2014, -paper PK summary. companion CD25 CD56 bright vignettes: Diao 2016 fixed PK published RRMS PopPK model (CL = 0.212 L/day 68 kg, allometric exponents 0.87 / 1.12, F = 0.88, t1/2,abs ~ 5 days, Tlag = 1.61 h) whereas packaged PD model uses Othman 2014 healthy-volunteer PK (CL = 0.24 L/day 70 kg, exponents 0.54 / 0.64, F = 0.84, ka = 0.216 /day, Tlag = 2 h). IC50 3.97 mg/L comparable typical clinical Cc range, simulated Treg curves sensitive PK assumption. Users need exact reproduction Diao 2016 Figure 3 numerical values can override inherited PK ini() entries. PD covariates. Diao 2016 report covariate effects Treg PD parameters; inherited PK covariates (WT, DOSE_50MG) present. Hill coefficient treated structurally fixed. See Errata. Virtual-cohort weight distribution. Body weight sampled N(71, 14) kg truncated 45-120 kg.

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: Citation: Faelens R, Wang Z, Bouillon T, Declerck P, Ferrante M, Vermeire S, Dreesen E. Model-Informed Precision Dosing Infliximab Induction Therapy Reduces Variability Exposure Endoscopic Improvement Patients Ulcerative Colitis. Pharmaceutics. 2021;13(10):1623. doi:10.3390/pharmaceutics13101623 Description: One-compartment IV population PK model infliximab adults moderate--severe ulcerative colitis (Faelens 2021 adapted model; baseline-covariate-re-fit Dreesen 2019) Article: https://doi.org/10.3390/pharmaceutics13101623 Supplement (PMID 34683916, “pharmaceutics-13-01623-s001.zip”; contains Table S1 parameter estimates NONMEM control stream adapted model) Faelens 2021 paper model-informed precision-dosing (MIPD) simulation study. popPK model used simulation re-fitted version previously published Dreesen 2019 popPK model (BJCP 85:782-795), time-varying covariates (CRP, serum albumin) intentionally removed increased exposures simulated 10 mg/kg dosing MIPD biased dose-dependent feedback acute-phase proteins. baseline covariates retained adapted model : baseline Mayo endoscopic subscore (MAYO_E, levels 1 / 2 / 3), baseline corticosteroid use (STEROID, 0/1), extensive colitis baseline (DISEXT_EP, 0/1), fat-free mass (FFM, kg).

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: model fit 583 PK samples 204 patients moderate--severe ulcerative colitis enrolled IBD Biobank University Hospitals Leuven, Belgium (B322201213950/S53684). original cohort full baseline demographics described Dreesen et al. 2019 (Faelens 2021 paper redescribe ). Faelens 2021 Section 2.1 reports patient count sample count notes original cohort received predominantly 5 mg/kg infliximab dosing (~90% doses) ~10% doses 10 mg/kg. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Faelens_2021_infliximab")$population.

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Faelens_2021_infliximab.R trailing comments next ini() entry. table collects one place. model reparameterised source’s (KE, V) parameterisation nlmixr2lib’s standard (CL, Vc) parameterisation via CL = KE * V. Mayo endoscopic subscore effect, acts categorically KE source, re-expressed log fold-change CL relative Mayo 2 reference category (e_mayo1_cl = log(0.0422 / 0.0463) = -0.0928, e_mayo3_cl = log(0.0570 / 0.0463) = +0.2079). independent IIV KE V source becomes 2x2 correlated block (etalcl, etalvc) induced correlation ~0.58 — see ini() block comments variance / covariance arithmetic.

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: original Dreesen 2019 / Faelens 2021 cohort dataset publicly available. cohort approximates simulation paper’s “combined 2:3 (49% : 51%)” virtual population sampling weight, sex, FFM, baseline corticosteroid use, extensive colitis plausible distributions adults moderate--severe UC, baseline Mayo endoscopic subscore fixed group. Section “Assumptions deviations” lists assumption.
code: set.seed(2021)  janmahasatian_ffm <- function(wt_kg, ht_m, sexf) {   bmi <- wt_kg / ht_m^2   ifelse(     sexf == 1,     9.27e3 * wt_kg / (8.78e3 + 244 * bmi),     9.27e3 * wt_kg / (6.68e3 + 216 * bmi)   ) }  make_cohort <- function(n, mayo_level, dose_per_kg_mg, treatment, id_offset = 0L) {   tibble(     id          = id_offset + seq_len(n),     SEXF        = rbinom(n, 1, 0.40),     WT          = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n, log(70), 0.20), 45), 130),     HT_M        = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n, mean = 1.72 - 0.13 * SEXF, sd = 0.08), 1.50), 1.95),     FFM         = janmahasatian_ffm(WT, HT_M, SEXF),     MAYO_E      = mayo_level,     STEROID     = rbinom(n, 1, 0.50),     DISEXT_EP   = rbinom(n, 1, 0.40),     treatment   = treatment,     dose_per_kg = dose_per_kg_mg   ) }  dose_times <- c(0, 14, 42) obs_times  <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 7, by = 0.5), seq(7, 84, by = 1))))  build_events <- function(cohort) {   d_dose <- cohort %>%     crossing(time = dose_times) %>%     mutate(       amt  = dose_per_kg * WT,       evid = 1,       cmt  = "central",       Cc   = NA_real_     )   d_obs <- cohort %>%     crossing(time = obs_times) %>%     mutate(amt = NA_real_, evid = 0, cmt = "central", Cc = NA_real_)   bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%     arrange(id, time, desc(evid)) %>%     select(id, time, amt, evid, cmt, Cc, MAYO_E, STEROID, DISEXT_EP, FFM,            treatment, dose_per_kg) }  n_per <- 500 cohort_5  <- bind_rows(   make_cohort(round(n_per * 0.49), mayo_level = 2L, dose_per_kg_mg = 5,  treatment = "5 mg/kg",  id_offset =     0L),   make_cohort(round(n_per * 0.51), mayo_level = 3L, dose_per_kg_mg = 5,  treatment = "5 mg/kg",  id_offset =   500L) ) cohort_10 <- bind_rows(   make_cohort(round(n_per * 0.49), mayo_level = 2L, dose_per_kg_mg = 10, treatment = "10 mg/kg", id_offset =  2000L),   make_cohort(round(n_per * 0.51), mayo_level = 3L, dose_per_kg_mg = 10, treatment = "10 mg/kg", id_offset =  2500L) )  cohort <- bind_rows(cohort_5, cohort_10) events <- build_events(cohort)  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Faelens_2021_infliximab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod,   events = events,   keep = c("treatment", "MAYO_E", "dose_per_kg") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
code: auc_d84 <- sim %>%   filter(time <= 84) %>%   group_by(id, treatment, MAYO_E) %>%   arrange(time) %>%   summarise(     AUCd84 = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(auc_d84, aes(x = AUCd84, fill = treatment)) +   geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +   facet_wrap(~ paste0("Baseline Mayo ", MAYO_E)) +   scale_x_log10() +   labs(     x = expression(AUC[d84] ~ "(mg" %.% "day/L, log scale)"),     y = "Density",     title = expression("Simulated " * AUC[d84] ~ "by dose and baseline Mayo subscore"),     caption = "Replicates Figure 2 panels of Faelens 2021 (AUCd84 distributions)."   ) +   theme_bw() conc_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time, treatment) %>%   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(conc_summary, aes(x = time, y = Q50, color = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, color = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Infliximab serum concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Infliximab concentration profile during induction therapy",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (combined Mayo 2/3 cohort)",     caption = "Replicates the qualitative shape of Faelens 2021 Figure 3."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: Faelens 2021 simulation paper reports AUCd84 (area concentration-time curve baseline endoscopy day 84) rather classical single-dose AUCinf. Compute via PKNCA [0, 84]-day interval, stratified treatment baseline Mayo subscore. PKNCA summary [0, 84]-day induction window, treatment baseline Mayo subscore.
code: sim_nca <- sim %>%   filter(!is.na(Cc), time <= 84) %>%   mutate(treatment_mayo = paste0(treatment, ", Mayo ", MAYO_E)) %>%   select(id, time, Cc, treatment_mayo)  dose_df <- events %>%   filter(evid == 1) %>%   mutate(treatment_mayo = paste0(treatment, ", Mayo ", MAYO_E)) %>%   select(id, time, amt, treatment_mayo)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(   sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment_mayo + id,   concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day" ) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(   dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment_mayo + id,   doseu = "mg" )  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = 84,   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                   46% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■        82% |  ETA:  2s nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary, caption = "PKNCA summary on the [0, 84]-day induction window, by treatment and baseline Mayo subscore.")

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: Faelens 2021 Table 1 reports per-cohort AUCd84 medians 90%-prediction intervals. Compute simulated equivalents cohort compare side--side. Note published values different virtual population (Faelens 2021 resampled original Dreesen 2019 dataset, disk); discrepancies > ~20% expected covariate distributions differ materially source dataset. Simulated vs. published AUCd84 (mg*day/L), dose baseline Mayo subscore (Faelens 2021 Table 1, ‘pub’ = published median + 90% PI).
code: auc_simulated <- auc_d84 %>%   group_by(treatment, MAYO_E) %>%   summarise(     median_sim = median(AUCd84),     q05_sim    = quantile(AUCd84, 0.05),     q95_sim    = quantile(AUCd84, 0.95),     .groups    = "drop"   )  published_table1 <- tibble::tribble(   ~treatment, ~MAYO_E, ~median_pub, ~q05_pub, ~q95_pub,   "5 mg/kg",        2L,     2455,     1215,     4805,   "5 mg/kg",        3L,     1979,      953,     3990,   "10 mg/kg",       2L,     4910,     2431,     9609,   "10 mg/kg",       3L,     3958,     1906,     7981 )  comparison <- published_table1 %>%   left_join(auc_simulated, by = c("treatment", "MAYO_E")) %>%   mutate(     pct_diff_median = round(100 * (median_sim - median_pub) / median_pub, 1)   )  knitr::kable(   comparison,   digits  = 0,   caption = "Simulated vs. published AUCd84 (mg*day/L), by dose and baseline Mayo subscore (Faelens 2021 Table 1, 'pub' = published median + 90% PI)." )

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: Faelens 2021 ran dosing simulations original Dreesen 2019 cohort dataset, synthetic resample, dataset publicly available. Replication therefore relies synthesised covariate distributions; simulated AUCd84 medians percentiles comparison table match published Table 1 exactly. Specifically: Body weight: sampled log-normal median 70 kg SD 0.20 log scale, clipped [45, 130] kg. Faelens 2021 / Dreesen 2019 publish empirical weight distribution. Sex: 40% female. original cohort sex balance reported Faelens 2021. Height: sampled normal sex-specific mean (males 1.72 m, females 1.59 m) SD 0.08 m. Used input Janmahasatian (2005) FFM formula; model uses FFM directly. Baseline corticosteroid use (STEROID): Bernoulli prevalence 0.50. simulation paper state corticosteroid prevalence virtual cohort. Extensive colitis baseline (DISEXT_EP): Bernoulli prevalence 0.40. simulation paper state prevalence virtual cohort. Baseline Mayo endoscopic subscore (MAYO_E): fixed per cohort (Mayo 2 Mayo 3) per Faelens 2021 stratified reporting convention. Mayo 1 patients existed Dreesen 2019 dataset source paper’s KE parameter model, Faelens 2021 reports Mayo 2 Mayo 3 simulation results. Excluded interoccasion variability (IOV): source model small IOV term KE (6.70% CV per occasion supplement Table S1 adapted model). nlmixr2lib portability — assume user OCC indicator event data — IOV implemented . IIV KE (33.4% CV) V (23.6% CV) implemented correlated 2x2 block (etalcl, etalvc) per (CL, Vc) reparameterisation. --domain Mayo missing (MPRE = -99): source NONMEM $THETA block carries additional KE estimate subjects missing Mayo subscore, supplement Table S1 report converged final value category. library implementation supports Mayo 1 / 2 / 3 . Dosing route: original cohort received infliximab 2-hour IV infusion. vignette uses instantaneous IV bolus dose days 0, 14, 42 simulation focus AUCd84 (identical within numerical tolerance 2-hour vs. instantaneous infusion monoclonal antibody multi-day half-life). Users wishing add 2-hour infusion explicitly can set rate accordingly dose row.

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: Model: one-compartment IV model linear elimination, absorption compartment. Dosing schedule: Faelens 2021 induction regimen days 0, 14, 42 — distinct common day-0/14/42 ACT 1/2 schedule referenced Fasanmade 2009, days 0/14/42 days expressed “weeks 0, 2, 6” elsewhere; time grid. Source’s (KE, V) → nlmixr2lib’s (CL, Vc): source’s two independent random effects KE V become single 2x2 correlated block (etalcl, etalvc) CL = KE * V reparameterisation. arithmetic documented model file’s ini() comments. Distinct Berends 2019 Hanzel 2021 infliximab models: Berends 2019 target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD) model explicit free TNF dynamics; Hanzel 2021 two-compartment SC + IV CT-P13 biosimilar model. Faelens 2021 simplest three: one-compartment IV-, baseline-covariates-, designed inducton-therapy dosing simulations moderate--severe UC.

title: Faelens_2021_infliximab
text: Faelens R, Wang Z, Bouillon T, Declerck P, Ferrante M, Vermeire S, Dreesen E. Model-Informed Precision Dosing Infliximab Induction Therapy Reduces Variability Exposure Endoscopic Improvement Patients Ulcerative Colitis. Pharmaceutics. 2021;13(10):1623. doi:10.3390/pharmaceutics13101623

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Citation: Farrell C, Schweizer C, Wustner J, Weil S, Namiki M, Nakano T, Nakai K, Phillips MD. Population pharmacokinetics farletuzumab, humanized monoclonal antibody folate receptor alpha, epithelial ovarian cancer. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2012;70(5):727-734. doi:10.1007/s00280-012-1959-y Description: Two-compartment population PK model farletuzumab (humanized IgG1 anti-folate-receptor-alpha monoclonal antibody) first-order linear elimination IV infusion women advanced epithelial ovarian cancer (Farrell 2012). Article: Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2012;70(5):727-734

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: analysis pooled 79 women advanced epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, primary peritoneal cancer two clinical trials (Farrell 2012 Table 1): Phase study MORAb-003-001 (n = 25, weekly IV farletuzumab ascending 12.5–400 mg/m² 4 weeks relapse standard chemotherapy) Phase II study MORAb-003-002 (n = 54, relapsed platinum-sensitive disease; 37.5, 62.5, 100 mg/m² weekly, majority 100 mg/m² combination chemotherapy arms later transitioned back monotherapy). Patients 31–81 years old (median 61); body weight ranged 44.5–118.2 kg (median 66.2); reported race predominantly Caucasian (~77%) smaller Asian, Hispanic, Black, groups. Farletuzumab given 1 mg/min infusion advanced 5 mg/min tolerated. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Farrell_2012_farletuzumab")$population.

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Farrell_2012_farletuzumab.R. table collects review.

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Table 1 overall demographics: body weights 44.5–118.2 kg range drawn truncated distribution around 66.2 kg median, every patient assigned PHASE2 = 1 (Phase II residual error — larger study one combined additive + proportional error). Dosing 100 mg/m² weekly IV infusion 1 hour 8 weeks, matching primary Phase II dosing schedule.
code: set.seed(20260421) n_subj <- 100  # Truncated approximation of the Table 1 overall weight distribution: # median 66.2, range 44.5–118.2. Use a log-normal-like sample then clip. wt_draws <- pmin(pmax(exp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = log(66.2), sd = 0.18)), 44.5), 118.2) # BSA derived by DuBois from weight assuming 163 cm mean height (clipped 1.37–2.24 # to match Table 1): BSA = 0.007184 * (wt^0.425) * (height_cm^0.725). bsa_draws <- pmin(pmax(0.007184 * wt_draws^0.425 * 163^0.725, 1.37), 2.24)  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = wt_draws,   BSA = bsa_draws,   PHASE2 = 1L,   dose_mgm2 = 100,   dose_mg = 100 * bsa_draws  # mg/m^2 x m^2 = mg ) cohort$treatment <- "100 mg/m2 weekly" # Build event table: 8 weekly 1-hour IV infusions + dense sampling for NCA. tau <- 24 * 7  # one week in hours n_doses <- 8 dose_times <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)  doses <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt = dose_mg,     rate = dose_mg,      # 1-hour infusion => rate = dose (mg/h)     cmt = "central",     evid = 1L   ) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  # Observation grid: dense during the first week, then per-week troughs, and # one long post-last-dose tail for terminal-phase characterization. obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 1),                  # first day, hourly   seq(28, 168, by = 12),               # rest of week 1, 12-hourly   seq(170, 8 * tau - 1, by = 24),      # dose interval troughs   seq(8 * tau, 8 * tau + 60 * 24, by = 24)  # 60-day terminal tail )))  obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(doses, obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Typical-value replication (zeroing -subject variability) useful “central” prediction curve carry wide Vp IIV:
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Farrell_2012_farletuzumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod,   events = events,   keep = c("WT", "PHASE2", "treatment") ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> Warning: No sigma parameters in the model  typical_cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 66.2, PHASE2 = 1L, treatment = "100 mg/m2 weekly (typical)" )  typical_doses <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 170, rate = 170, cmt = "central", evid = 1L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  typical_obs <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid, WT, PHASE2, treatment)  typical_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(typical_doses, typical_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = typical_events, keep = c("WT", "PHASE2", "treatment") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp'

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Farrell 2012 Figure 2 visual predictive check final model observed data. five-fifty-ninety-five percentile summary simulated cohort weekly dosing interval post-last-dose terminal phase captures structural features (peak near end infusion, slow biexponential decline).
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(day = time / 24) |>   dplyr::group_by(day) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)", y = "Farletuzumab concentration (µg/mL)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles — 100 mg/m² weekly × 8",     caption = "Replicates the structure of Figure 2 (VPC) of Farrell 2012."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Figure 3 — weekly vs. 3-weekly dosing at matched mg/kg/week
title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Farrell 2012 Figure 3 shows steady-state exposure similar weekly vs. 3-weekly dosing weekly-equivalent mg/kg dose maintained. block reproduces comparison using typical-value predictions.
code: wt_ref <- 66.2 bsa_ref <- 0.007184 * wt_ref^0.425 * 163^0.725  # ~1.70 m^2  weekly_mg  <- 100 * bsa_ref                      # 100 mg/m^2 weekly q3w_mg     <- 3 * weekly_mg                      # same mg/kg/week, given every 3 weeks  tau_w  <- 24 * 7 tau_q3 <- 24 * 21  make_regimen <- function(amt, tau, n_doses, label) {   dt <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)   obs_t <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, max(dt) + tau, by = 6),     seq(max(dt) + tau, max(dt) + tau + 60 * 24, by = 24)   )))   dplyr::bind_rows(     tibble::tibble(id = 1L, time = dt,   amt = amt, rate = amt,       cmt = "central", evid = 1L, WT = wt_ref, PHASE2 = 1L, treatment = label),     tibble::tibble(id = 1L, time = obs_t, amt = 0, rate = 0,       cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, WT = wt_ref, PHASE2 = 1L, treatment = label)   ) |> dplyr::arrange(time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  ev_weekly <- make_regimen(weekly_mg, tau_w,  12, "100 mg/m² weekly") ev_q3w    <- make_regimen(q3w_mg,    tau_q3,  4, "300 mg/m² every 3 weeks")  regimen_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   ev_weekly,   ev_q3w |> dplyr::mutate(id = 2L) )  sim_regimens <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = regimen_events, keep = c("WT", "PHASE2", "treatment") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'  sim_regimens |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(day = time / 24) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Cc, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)", y = "Farletuzumab concentration (µg/mL)",     colour = NULL,     title = "Weekly vs. every-3-week dosing at matched mg/m²/week (typical)",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3 of Farrell 2012."   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, AUC(0-τ) (one-week interval first dose), terminal half-life using PKNCA. terminal half-life estimated 60-day post-last-dose tail, β-phase dominates. Simulated NCA summaries 100 mg/m² weekly Phase II cohort.
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  # After-first-dose NCA over tau = 1 week, plus terminal-phase half-life over # the 60-day post-last-dose tail. start_last <- max(dose_df$time)  # last dose time intervals <- data.frame(   start      = c(0,   start_last + 1,    start_last + 1),   end        = c(tau, start_last + tau,  start_last + 60 * 24),   cmax       = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   tmax       = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   auclast    = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   ctrough    = c(TRUE,  TRUE,  FALSE),   cmin       = c(FALSE, TRUE,  FALSE),   half.life  = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE) )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #> Warning: Requesting an
