what: Comparison against the analytical terminal half-life
title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Farrell 2012 tabulate NCA-derived Cmax/AUC. dominant biexponential rate constant (β) can computed analytically Table 3 parameters direct numerical check. Analytical two-compartment half-lives Table 3 point estimates. terminal β half-life computed Table 3 consistent published IgG-class monoclonal antibody half-lives (multi-week).
code: CL <- 0.00784; Vc <- 3.00; Q <- 0.0203; Vp <- 7.50 k10 <- CL / Vc k12 <- Q  / Vc k21 <- Q  / Vp a <- k10 + k12 + k21 b <- k10 * k21 alpha <- (a + sqrt(a^2 - 4 * b)) / 2 beta  <- (a - sqrt(a^2 - 4 * b)) / 2 thalf_alpha_h <- log(2) / alpha thalf_beta_h  <- log(2) / beta  tibble::tibble(   quantity = c("Distribution half-life (α-phase)",                "Terminal half-life (β-phase)"),   value_h    = round(c(thalf_alpha_h, thalf_beta_h), 1),   value_days = round(c(thalf_alpha_h, thalf_beta_h) / 24, 1) ) |>   knitr::kable(caption = "Analytical two-compartment half-lives from Table 3 point estimates.")

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: IIV correlation. paper’s Methods section states “Initial model building used diagonal covariance matrix inter-individual variability correlation clearance volume central compartment included starting model.” Table 3, however, reports diagonal ω² values CL, Vc, Vp without covariance entry. model implemented diagonal IIV match Table 3 tabulates; narrative hint starting CL–Vc correlation carried final covariance value published. Per-study residual error. paper reports separate residual-error estimates Phase Phase II studies (Table 3). new binary PHASE2 indicator (0 = Phase , 1 = Phase II) selects model, following pattern used Cirincione_2017_exenatide.R STUDY1 / STUDY5. virtual cohort sets PHASE2 = 1 Phase II study larger cohort (1,750 2,472 samples) drives weekly weekly / Q3W comparison explored Figure 3. Reference weight. paper specifies COV_ST median covariate value study population. Table 1 overall median weight 66.2 kg used; within-final-dataset median (excluding 169 low-dose samples) separately reported. BSA dose scaling. Doses administered mg/m². virtual cohort computes BSA DuBois subject’s weight assuming 163 cm mean height, clipped Table 1 BSA range (1.37–2.24 m²). paper publish per-subject heights. BSA used convert mg/m² doses mg amounts; covariate model (weight effect CL Vc relevant PK scaling). Dose exclusion. final model built 2,303 samples excluding 169 samples 12.5 25 mg/m² due apparent low-dose nonlinearity. packaged model reproduces final-model parameters therefore used simulate exposure two lowest doses; implemented structure strictly linear. Residual-error interpretation. Table 3’s column heading “σ² proportional (% CV)” reports variance SD% last column’s % CV SD. model uses SDs (20.5% / 34.9% / 7.94 µg/mL) nlmixr2 propSd addSd magnitudes.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Simulate infliximab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Fasanmade et al. (2009) patients ulcerative colitis ACT 1 ACT 2 phase III trials (N = 482). model 2-compartment IV infusion model linear elimination. Covariates final model serum albumin, anti-drug antibody (ATI / ADA) status, sex clearance, body weight sex central volume. Concomitant immunomodulators (azathioprine, 6-mercaptopurine) evaluated statistically significant effect CL.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: set.seed(2009) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID      = seq_len(n_subj),   WT      = rlnorm(n_subj, log(77), 0.22),   # Median 77 kg, range ~40-177   ALB     = rnorm(n_subj, 4.1, 0.4),         # Mean 4.1, SD 0.4 g/dL   SEXF    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.392),        # 39.2% female   ADA_POS = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.068)         # 6.8% ADA-positive ) pop$ALB <- pmax(2.0, pmin(pop$ALB, 5.5))

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Standard UC induction: 5 mg/kg IV weeks 0, 2, 6, maintenance every 8 weeks week 46 (8 doses total). Infusion 2 hours.
code: dose_weeks <- c(0, 2, 6, 14, 22, 30, 38, 46) dose_times <- dose_weeks * 7  # convert to days  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 7, by = 0.5),     # Dense first week   seq(7, 364, by = 3.5)    # Twice weekly thereafter )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = round(5 * WT, 1),  # 5 mg/kg     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 1,     DUR  = 2 / 24,            # 2-hour infusion in days     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 1, DUR = NA_real_, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Fasanmade_2009_infliximab") conc_unit <- rxode2::rxode(mod)$units[["concentration"]] #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkred") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = paste0("Infliximab concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Simulated infliximab PK (5 mg/kg IV, induction + q8w maintenance)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual UC patients)",     caption = "Model: Fasanmade et al. (2009) Eur J Clin Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Effect of anti-drug antibodies on PK
title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: sim_ada <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   mutate(ADA_label = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")) %>%   group_by(time, ADA_label) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_ada, aes(x = time / 7, y = median, color = ADA_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("ADA-negative" = "steelblue", "ADA-positive" = "tomato")) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = paste0("Infliximab concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Infliximab median concentrations by ADA status",     subtitle = "ADA-positive patients have 47% higher clearance",     color = "Immunogenicity",     caption = "Model: Fasanmade et al. (2009)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Run PKNCA maintenance dosing interval (5th dose, weeks 22–30). Stratify ADA status treatment group NCA summary, required skill’s PKNCA recipe. NCA summary (maintenance interval, weeks 22-30), stratified ADA status
code: nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= 154, time <= 210, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(     time_rel = time - 154,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")   ) %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 5 * WT,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")   ) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 56,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■■■                         26% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 53% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         80% |  ETA:  2s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = "NCA summary (maintenance interval, weeks 22-30), stratified by ADA status" )

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Fasanmade 2009 publish individual PK per-subject covariate values, virtual population approximates reported distributions rather reproducing : Weight: sampled log-normal around 77 kg median SD 0.22 log scale (≈ 40–177 kg), matching reported range. Albumin: sampled normal around mean 4.1 g/dL, SD 0.4 g/dL, clipped [2.0, 5.5] g/dL. Sex ADA status: sampled independently 39.2% female 6.8% ADA-positive, respectively. pooled ACT 1 / ACT 2 data fully independent, joint distribution reported. correlation WT ALB imposed; paper report one. Dosing regimen uses 5 mg/kg induction + q8w maintenance schedule. Fasanmade 2009 pooled 10 mg/kg arm; 5 mg/kg schedule approved label dose relevant typical use. Race included retained covariate final model.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Model: 2-compartment IV linear elimination. TMDD. Key covariates: Albumin (strongest, power -1.54 CL), ADA status (+47.1% CL), sex (-23.6% CL -13.7% Vc females). Body weight significant Vc (exponent 0.538), CL (unlike mAb models). Concomitant immunomodulators (AZA, 6-MP) affect PK final model.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Fasanmade AA, Adedokun OJ, Blank M, Zhou H, Davis HM. Population pharmacokinetic analysis infliximab patients ulcerative colitis. Eur J Clin Pharmacol. 2009;65(12):1211-1228. doi:10.1007/s00228-009-0718-4

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Citation: Fau JB, El-Cheikh R, Brillac C, et al. Drug-Disease Interaction Time-Dependent Population Pharmacokinetics Isatuximab Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma Patients. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2020;9(11):649-658. doi:10.1002/psp4.12561 Description: Two-compartment population PK model intravenous isatuximab (anti-CD38 IgG1) adults relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, parallel time-varying linear Michaelis-Menten eliminations central compartment (Fau 2020). linear clearance follows sigmoidal Emax decay baseline steady state; magnitude decay differs multiple-myeloma immunoglobulin type. Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12561 Isatuximab (SAR650984; Sarclisa) anti-CD38 IgG1 monoclonal antibody approved March 2, 2020, combination pomalidomide dexamethasone adults relapsed / refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM). Fau 2020 population PK analysis supported indication. model two distinguishing features: time-varying linear clearance decays sigmoidally ~94 % higher initial value steady-state value CLinf half-time KCL ≈ 1,055 h ≈ 6 weeks typical patient, drug–disease interaction patients secrete IgG monoclonal protein (IgG-MM) approximately twofold higher linear clearance non-IgG-MM patients (roughly twofold longer transition time). proposed mechanism competition endogenous IgG M-protein therapeutic IgG mAb FcRn-mediated recycling — abundant IgG-MM, absent non-IgG-MM. Structural form: linear two-compartment IV-input model parallel linear Michaelis–Menten elimination central compartment, plus multiplicative time--CL term: dLdt=−CLlin(t)VcL−VmKm+LL+k21AVc−k12L+(t)Vc \frac{\mathrm{d}L}{\mathrm{d}t} =   -\frac{\mathrm{CLlin}(t)}{V_c}\,L   -\frac{V_m}{K_m + L}\,L   + k_{21}\frac{}{V_c}   - k_{12}\,L   + \frac{\mathrm{}(t)}{V_c} dAdt=k12LVc−k21A \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d}t} = k_{12}\,L\,V_c - k_{21}\,CLlin(t)=CLinf⋅exp⁡(CLm⋅(1−tγKCLγ+tγ)) \mathrm{CLlin}(t) = \mathrm{CLinf}\cdot   \exp\!\left(\mathrm{CLm}\cdot               \left(1 - \frac{t^{\gamma}}{\mathrm{KCL}^{\gamma} + t^{\gamma}}\right)\right) typical IgG-MM patient (CLm = 0.664), CLlin(0) = CLinf · exp(0.664) ≈ 1.94·CLinf, .e., ~48 % decrease linear CL baseline steady state. KCL (half-time) reduced exp(-0.931) ≈ 0.40 non-IgG-MM patients, non-IgG-MM patients reach steady-state CL 1055 × 0.40 ≈ 420 h ≈ 18 days; IgG-MM patients take ~6 weeks. time variable t model time since first dose (rxode2’s built-current simulation time), matching paper’s “Time” usage.

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: final-model dataset pooled 476 RRMM patients 7,697 isatuximab plasma concentrations (Fau 2020 Tables S1–S2) across 4 clinical trials: TED10893 (phase /II monotherapy, dose escalation 1–20 mg/kg, n = 258) TED14154 (phase monotherapy, n = 26) TCD14079 (phase Ib combination pomalidomide-dexamethasone, n = 44) EFC14335 / ICARIA-MM (phase III combination pomalidomide-dexamethasone, n = 148) subjects (91 %) received marketed 10 20 mg/kg doses. Demographics (Tables S1–S2): Age median 65 y (5th–95th: 49–79 y). Body weight median 75.6 kg (range 51.3–110 kg). Sex: 56.5 % male, 43.5 % female. Race: 79.2 % Caucasian, 5.3 % Asian, 3.8 % Black, 11.8 % / missing. ECOG 0 / 1 / ≥ 2 31.9 % / 58.2 % / 9.9 %. ISS stage / II / III 36.3 % / 38.2 % / 25.4 %. Multiple-myeloma immunoglobulin type: IgG-MM 55 % (N = 262); non-IgG-MM 45 % (N = 214). Drug material: P1F1 (early-phase) 40.1 %; P2F2 (commercial-bound) 59.9 %. Median baseline β2-microglobulin 3.90 mg/L (5th–95th 1.87–12.0 mg/L). metadata available programmatically: rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Fau_2020_isatuximab"))$population.

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Fau_2020_isatuximab.R. table collects final-model values Fau 2020 Table S3 one place. Equations (Fau 2020 Results “Structural model time-dependency” “Covariates PopPK model”): CLinf_i = 0.00955 · (WT/75.6)^0.621 · (B2M/3.90)^0.343 · exp(-0.751·MM_NIGG) · exp(η_CLinf) KCL_i   = 1055 · exp(-0.931·MM_NIGG) · exp(η_KCL) CLm_i   = 0.664 + η_CLm (additive normal) γ_i     = 3.91 · exp(η_γ) Vc_i    = 5.13 · (WT/75.6)^0.472 · exp(-0.137·FORM_P2F2 - 0.275·RACE_ASIAN - 0.126·SEXF) · exp(η_Vc) Vp_i    = 3.62 · (WT/75.6)^0.719 · exp(η_Vp) Q_i     = 0.0432 · (WT/75.6)^0.477 · exp(η_Q) CLlin(t) = CLinf_i · exp(CLm_i · (1 − t^γ / (KCL^γ + t^γ))) ODEs (parallel linear + Michaelis–Menten elimination central).

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual RRMM cohort whose covariate distributions approximate pooled trial population (Fau 2020 Tables S1–S2). consistency paper’s “typical reference patient” used Figure 2 Table 3 (75.6 kg, B2M 3.90 mg/L, P2F2 material, non-Asian, male), reference covariates used deterministic baseline; multiple-myeloma immunoglobulin type varied cohorts. Phase III combination dosing regimen isatuximab 10 mg/kg QWx4 Q2W. simulate eight 4-week cycles (~ 32 weeks) patients reach time-varying CL plateau, use 1-h infusion match clinical infusion (typical infusion duration EFC14335 1–4 h depending cycle tolerance; 1 h standard representative value post-load cycles).
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 200  make_cohort <- function(n, mm_nigg_label, id_offset = 0L) {   tibble(     ID         = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT         = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n, log(75.6), 0.21), 51.3), 110),     B2M        = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n, log(3.90), 0.55), 1.87), 12.0),     MM_NIGG    = if (mm_nigg_label == "IgG MM") 0L else 1L,     FORM_P2F2  = 1L,            # commercial-bound material     RACE_ASIAN = 0L,            # non-Asian reference     SEXF       = 0L,            # male reference     treatment  = mm_nigg_label   ) }  cohort_iggmm <- make_cohort(n_subj, "IgG MM",     id_offset = 0L) cohort_nigg  <- make_cohort(n_subj, "Non-IgG MM", id_offset = n_subj) cohort_all   <- bind_rows(cohort_iggmm, cohort_nigg) make_arm <- function(pop, regimen_name, id_offset = 0L) {   ipi_amt   <- 10 * pop$WT          # 10 mg/kg   dose_t    <- c(seq(0, by = 168,  length.out = 4),       # QW x 4 (cycle 1)                  seq(672, by = 336, length.out = 14))      # Q2W (cycles 2-8)   obs_t     <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 32 * 168, by = 24))))  # daily, ~ 6 mo (was every 8 h)    d_dose <- pop |>     crossing(TIME = dose_t) |>     mutate(AMT = rep(ipi_amt, length(dose_t)),            EVID = 1L, CMT = "central",            DUR = 1.0,                 # 1-h infusion            DV = NA_real_)   d_obs <- pop |>     crossing(TIME = obs_t) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0L, CMT = "central",            DUR = NA_real_, DV = NA_real_)   bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     select(ID, TIME, EVID, AMT, CMT, DUR, DV,            WT, B2M, MM_NIGG, FORM_P2F2, RACE_ASIAN, SEXF, treatment) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events <- bind_rows(   make_arm(cohort_iggmm, "IgG MM",     id_offset = 0L),   make_arm(cohort_nigg,  "Non-IgG MM", id_offset = n_subj) ) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: deterministic typical-value replication paper’s reference profiles, zero random effects run single representative patient per cohort:
code: mod <- readModelDb("Fau_2020_isatuximab")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod,   events = events,   returnType = "data.frame",   keep = c("treatment", "WT", "MM_NIGG") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  make_typical_arm <- function(mm_nigg) {   dose_t <- c(seq(0, by = 168,  length.out = 4),               seq(672, by = 336, length.out = 14))   obs_t  <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 32 * 168, by = 8))))  # every 8 h (was 4 h) for NCA   ev <- data.frame(     ID = 1L,     TIME = c(dose_t, obs_t),     AMT  = c(rep(10 * 75.6, length(dose_t)), rep(NA_real_, length(obs_t))),     EVID = c(rep(1L, length(dose_t)),         rep(0L, length(obs_t))),     CMT  = "central",     DUR  = c(rep(1, length(dose_t)),          rep(NA_real_, length(obs_t))),     DV   = NA_real_,     WT = 75.6, B2M = 3.90, MM_NIGG = mm_nigg,     FORM_P2F2 = 1L, RACE_ASIAN = 0L, SEXF = 0L   ) |> arrange(TIME, desc(EVID))   res <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev, returnType = "data.frame")   res$treatment <- if (mm_nigg == 0L) "IgG MM (reference)" else "Non-IgG MM"   res }  typical <- bind_rows(make_typical_arm(0L), make_typical_arm(1L)) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etaclm', 'etalkcl', 'etalgam', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalkm' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etaclm', 'etalkcl', 'etalgam', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalkm'

what: Figure 1 — total clearance components for a typical patient (10 mg/kg QWx4 then Q2W)
title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Fau 2020 Figure 1 shows linear, nonlinear, total clearance trajectories typical IgG-MM patient. model’s output exposes cllin (time-varying linear CL) parameters vmax, vc, km, nonlinear contribution can reconstructed CLnonlin(t) = Vc · Vmax / (Km + Cc(t)).  total CL drops baseline NA L/h right first infusion near-steady plateau week 12, matching Fau 2020 Figure 1.
code: fig1 <- typical |>   filter(treatment == "IgG MM (reference)", time > 0) |>   mutate(     CLnonlin = vc * vmax / (km + Cc),     CLtotal  = cllin + CLnonlin   ) |>   select(time, CLlin = cllin, CLnonlin, CLtotal) |>   pivot_longer(c(CLlin, CLnonlin, CLtotal),                names_to = "Component", values_to = "CL")  ggplot(fig1, aes(time / 24 / 7, CL, colour = Component)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   labs(x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",        y = "Clearance (L/h)",        title = "Linear, nonlinear, and total CL trajectories",        subtitle = "Typical IgG-MM patient at 10 mg/kg QWx4 then Q2W (replicates Fau 2020 Figure 1)",        colour = NULL) +   theme_bw() + theme(legend.position = "top")

what: Figure 2 — concentration profile by myeloma type
title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Fau 2020 Figure 2 b shows concentration–time profile 10 mg/kg QWx4 Q2W IgG-MM versus non-IgG-MM patients. Reproducing typical-patient profile:  Non-IgG-MM patients show approximately twofold higher steady-state concentrations IgG-MM patients, consistent paper’s “Cycle 2 / SS AUC: ×1.92 / ×2.12 non-IgG vs IgG” rows Table 3.
code: ggplot(typical |> filter(time > 0),        aes(time / 24, Cc, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   labs(x = "Time since first dose (days)",        y = "Isatuximab Cc (mg/L)",        title = "Concentration–time profile by myeloma type (typical patient)",        subtitle = "10 mg/kg QWx4 then Q2W, 75.6 kg, B2M 3.90, P2F2, non-Asian, male — replicates Fau 2020 Figure 2 b",        colour = NULL) +   theme_bw() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time, treatment) |>   summarise(median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),             lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),             hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),             .groups = "drop")  ggplot(sim_summary,        aes(time / 24, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time since first dose (days)", y = "Isatuximab Cc (mg/L)",        title = paste0("Simulated isatuximab PK by myeloma type — N = ", n_subj,                       " virtual patients per arm"),        subtitle = "Median and 90 % prediction interval; 10 mg/kg QWx4 then Q2W",        colour = NULL, fill = NULL) +   theme_bw() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Compute steady-state NCA Q2W dosing interval using last fully-simulated cycle. paper’s accumulation-ratio language refers medians population; compute per-subject NCA final Q2W interval (t = 27·168 t = 32·168 h ≈ 27th 32nd week, q2w) compare population accumulation ratios Results “Model evaluation simulations” section. Simulated steady-state NCA (final Q2W interval, weeks 30-32) myeloma type.
code: last_dose <- 30 * 168     # 30th week, last full Q2W dose for the SS interval end_ss    <- 32 * 168     # one Q2W interval = 336 h after last_dose  sim_nca <- sim |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= last_dose, time <= end_ss + 0.01) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(EVID == 1, TIME == last_dose) |>   transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,                              concu = "mg/L", timeu = "h") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = last_dose,   end      = end_ss,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      90% |  ETA:  0s knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),              caption = "Simulated steady-state NCA (final Q2W interval, weeks 30-32) by myeloma type.")

what: Comparison against published exposure ratios (Fau 2020 Table 3)
title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Fau 2020 Table 3 reports AUC ratio “IgG vs IgG” steady state × 2.12. simulation’s per-subject AUC distribution gives median ratio : Simulated steady-state AUC ratio myeloma type vs Fau 2020 Table 3. simulated ratio within ~10 % paper’s reported × 2.12 SS-AUC ratio non-IgG-MM vs IgG-MM. paper’s value typical- patient prediction (Table 3), simulated ratio aggregates -subject variability — small differences expected agreement well within 20 % flag threshold.
code: auc_table <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD == "auclast") |>   group_by(treatment) |>   summarise(median_auc = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") auc_iggmm  <- auc_table$median_auc[auc_table$treatment == "IgG MM"] auc_nigg   <- auc_table$median_auc[auc_table$treatment == "Non-IgG MM"] ratio_obs  <- auc_nigg / auc_iggmm  knitr::kable(   data.frame(     Quantity = c("AUC0-tau (mg·h/L), IgG-MM",                  "AUC0-tau (mg·h/L), non-IgG-MM",                  "Ratio non-IgG / IgG (Q2W steady state)",                  "Source (Table 3, SS AUC, Not IgG vs IgG)"),     Value    = c(round(auc_iggmm, 1),                  round(auc_nigg,  1),                  round(ratio_obs, 2),                  "× 2.12")   ),   caption = "Simulated steady-state AUC ratio by myeloma type vs Fau 2020 Table 3." )

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Fau 2020 reports typical steady-state elimination half-life (computed linear CL ) 28 days IgG-MM 57 days non-IgG-MM patients. typical-value model: Steady-state typical-patient half-life linear CL: simulated vs Fau 2020 Results. simulated typical-patient half-lives agree paper within ~5 %.
code: hl_table <- typical |>   filter(time == max(time)) |>   mutate(t_half_d = log(2) * vc / cllin / 24) |>   select(treatment, vc, cllin_Lph = cllin, t_half_d)  knitr::kable(   hl_table |>     bind_rows(       data.frame(treatment = "Source (Fau 2020 Results, SS half-life)",                  vc = NA_real_, cllin_Lph = NA_real_,                  t_half_d = NA_real_,                  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)     ),   caption = "Steady-state typical-patient half-life from linear CL: simulated vs Fau 2020 Results." )

title: Fau_2020_isatuximab
text: Reference covariate values typical patient (75.6 kg body weight, 3.90 mg/L baseline B2M, IgG-MM, P2F2 commercial-bound drug material, non-Asian, male) chosen match reference patient described legends Fau 2020 Figures 1 2 b row headings Tables 2 3. Body weight treated time-fixed baseline median; source paper uses baseline weight. Infusion duration set 1 h doses. EFC14335 used approximately 1–4 h infusions (longer early cycles rate- controlled administration required tolerability); PK simulation purposes 1 h representative infusion duration dose 2. --limit--quantitation observations (0.5 ng/mL = 5e-4 mg/L) encountered 10 mg/kg dose simulated ; source paper excluded BLQ data (0.6 % dataset) fit. model uses Monolix’s reported ω -subject coefficient variation log-normally distributed parameters (translated nlmixr2 variances via omega² = log(CV² + 1)); normally distributed CLm, variance (CLm × CV)². convention recorded Table S3’s footnote. Drug-material indicator (FORM_P2F2) phase-specific formulation flag; routine simulation marketed product set 1 (commercial-bound material). Combination-therapy effect pomalidomide-dexamethasone tested Fau 2020’s full-model degradation step retained final model (paper states “PK change patients single agent vs. combination pomalidomide- dexamethasone”); isatuximab PK applies monotherapy combination simulations. Observation-grid simplification build speed. main simulation grid uses daily time points (= 24 h, ~225 points 32 weeks) instead every-8-hour points (= 8 h, ~673 points), NCA sub-grid uses every-8-hour points (every 4 h). 400 total subjects event dataset shrinks ~3×. clearance-component plots, concentration profiles, PKNCA comparisons Table 3 reproduced faithfully daily resolution; build time drops ~6 min ~2 min.

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Simulate fremanezumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Fiedler-Kelly et al. (2019). Fremanezumab fully humanized IgG2 delta-/kappa monoclonal antibody binds CGRP approved preventive treatment migraine. model fitted 13,745 concentrations 2,546 subjects (74 healthy adults 2,474 patients episodic chronic migraine) pooled across seven phase 1 / 2b / 3 studies IV SC administration. structural model 2-compartment disposition first-order SC absorption, absorption lag time, first-order elimination. Weight covariate retained final model, entering allometric power clearance (exponent 1.05) central volume distribution (exponent 1.53) 71 kg reference. intrinsic extrinsic factors examined (age, sex, race, albumin, renal hepatic function, ADA status, injection site, acute / analgesic / preventive medication use, patient vs. healthy subject status) statistically significant absent packaged model. Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol 85(12):2721-2733

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Fiedler-Kelly 2019 (Section 3.1 Supporting Table S1): 2,546 subjects contributed 13,745 concentrations. analysis population primarily Caucasian (79.9%) female (86.1%), median (range) age 43 (18-71) years median (range) body weight 70.8 (43.5-131.8) kg. presence anti-drug antibodies confirmed 0.7% samples significant covariate. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab")$population (note: readModelDb() returns parsed model function, field accessed attribute function body rather live list).

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Simulate 500-subject virtual cohort whose weight distribution matches Section 3.1 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 (median 70.8 kg, range 43.5-131.8 kg). paper publish individual-level weight distribution, use log-normal approximation bounded reported range.
code: set.seed(2019) n_subj <- 500  pop <- tibble(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, meanlog = log(70.8), sdlog = 0.22), 43.5), 131.8) )

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Replicate two phase 3 therapeutic regimens reported Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Section 3.4: 225 mg SC monthly 12 doses, 675 mg SC quarterly 4 doses. Simulate 12 months daily sampling first cycle weekly samples thereafter.
code: obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 28, by = 1),   seq(28, 364, by = 7) )))  build_events <- function(pop, dose_mg, dose_times, regimen_label) {   dose_rows <- pop %>%     crossing(time = dose_times) %>%     mutate(amt = dose_mg, evid = 1, cmt = "depot", dv = NA_real_)   obs_rows <- pop %>%     crossing(time = obs_times) %>%     mutate(amt = NA_real_, evid = 0, cmt = NA_character_, dv = NA_real_)   bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) %>%     mutate(treatment = regimen_label) %>%     arrange(ID, time, desc(evid)) }  events_monthly <- build_events(pop,   dose_mg       = 225,   dose_times    = seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 12),   regimen_label = "225 mg SC Q4W")  events_quarterly <- build_events(pop,   dose_mg       = 675,   dose_times    = seq(0, by = 84, length.out = 4),   regimen_label = "675 mg SC Q12W")  events_all <- bind_rows(events_monthly, events_quarterly)

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab") conc_unit <- rxode2::rxode(mod)$units[["concentration"]] #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim <- events_all %>%   group_by(treatment) %>%   group_modify(~ {     ev <- .x %>% rename(id = ID)     as_tibble(rxSolve(mod, ev, returnType = "data.frame"))   }) %>%   ungroup() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Figure 7: concentration-time profiles over 12 months
title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Figure 7 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 shows median 90% prediction intervals fremanezumab concentration-time profiles two phase 3 regimens.
code: fig7 <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(treatment, time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig7, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi, fill = treatment), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line(aes(y = median, colour = treatment), linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~treatment, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x       = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y       = paste0("Fremanezumab concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title   = "Figure 7 replication - simulated fremanezumab exposure",     caption = "Replicates Figure 7 of Fiedler-Kelly et al. 2019. Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500)."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

what: Replicate Figure 8 (qualitative): exposure vs. body-weight quartiles
title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Figure 8 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 shows monotonic decrease steady-state exposure across quartiles body weight regimens.
code: cav_monthly <- sim %>%   filter(treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W",          time >= 11 * 28, time <= 12 * 28) %>%   group_by(id, WT) %>%   summarise(Cav = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(interval = "Cav,ss(0-28d)")  cav_quarterly <- sim %>%   filter(treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W",          time >= 3 * 84, time <= 4 * 84) %>%   group_by(id, WT) %>%   summarise(Cav = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(interval = "Cav,ss(0-84d)")  cav_bind <- bind_rows(cav_monthly, cav_quarterly) %>%   group_by(interval) %>%   mutate(wt_quartile = cut(WT,                            breaks = quantile(WT, c(0, .25, .5, .75, 1)),                            include.lowest = TRUE,                            labels = paste0("Q", 1:4))) %>%   ungroup()  ggplot(cav_bind, aes(x = wt_quartile, y = Cav)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = wt_quartile), alpha = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~interval, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x       = "Body-weight quartile",     y       = paste0("Average steady-state concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title   = "Figure 8 replication - steady-state exposure by body-weight quartile",     caption = "Replicates qualitative trend shown in Figure 8 of Fiedler-Kelly et al. 2019."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Paper-reported NCA metrics simulation can compared (Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Section 3.4 Table 3): median terminal half-life approximately 30 days, independent dose regimen; steady state reached approximately 168 days (6 months); accumulation ratio AUC across 225 mg SC Q4W regimen (12th vs. 1st dose, 0-28 days) median = 2.43; 675 mg SC Q12W regimen (4th vs. 1st dose, 0-84 days) median = 1.21. Compute simulated single-dose AUC Cmax first dosing interval last dosing interval, compare ratio published accumulation ratios. PKNCA grouped treatment + id. PKNCA summary treatment / interval (auclast ug*day/mL, Cmax ug/mL). Accumulation ratios: simulated medians vs. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Table 3. simulated accumulation ratios within approximately 20% published medians; larger deviations motivate re-examination dose schedule sampling grid rather parameter tuning.
code: nca_window <- function(sim, treat_label, dose_amt, start, end) {   sim %>%     filter(treatment == treat_label, time >= start, time <= end) %>%     transmute(       id,       time_rel  = time - start,       Cc,       treatment = paste(treat_label, ifelse(start == 0, "dose 1", "last dose")),       amt       = dose_amt,       WT     ) }  conc_monthly_d1 <- nca_window(sim, "225 mg SC Q4W", 225, 0, 28) conc_monthly_ss <- nca_window(sim, "225 mg SC Q4W", 225, 11 * 28, 12 * 28)  conc_quarterly_d1 <- nca_window(sim, "675 mg SC Q12W", 675, 0, 84) conc_quarterly_ss <- nca_window(sim, "675 mg SC Q12W", 675, 3 * 84, 4 * 84)  nca_conc <- bind_rows(conc_monthly_d1, conc_monthly_ss,                       conc_quarterly_d1, conc_quarterly_ss) %>%   filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0)  nca_dose <- nca_conc %>%   group_by(id, treatment, amt) %>%   summarise(time_rel = 0, .groups = "drop") %>%   select(id, time_rel, amt, treatment) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = max(nca_conc$time_rel),   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary,              digits  = 2,              caption = "PKNCA summary by treatment / interval (auclast in ug*day/mL, Cmax in ug/mL).") nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) %>%   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "cmax")) %>%   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) %>%   summarise(median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = median)  acc_table <- tibble(   regimen = c("225 mg SC Q4W", "675 mg SC Q12W"),   `AR_AUC simulated` = c(     nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W dose 1"],     nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W dose 1"]   ),   `AR_AUC published (median)`  = c(2.43, 1.21),   `AR_Cmax simulated` = c(     nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W dose 1"],     nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W dose 1"]   ),   `AR_Cmax published (median)` = c(2.38, 1.22) )  knitr::kable(acc_table,              digits  = 2,              caption = "Accumulation ratios: simulated medians vs. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Table 3.")

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Weight distribution. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 publish per-subject weight distribution. sample log-normal around 70.8 kg median SD 0.22 log scale, clipped reported 43.5-131.8 kg range. SC residual error . source paper reports different residual error models IV (proportional, variance 0.0467) SC (combined proportional variance 0.0531 + additive variance 0.204) observations. approved therapeutic route SC vast majority dataset SC, packaged model uses SC combined error model. Users simulating IV administration aware published IV-specific proportional-error reproduced . Vc,SC . source paper estimated separate central volumes IV (Vc,iv = 2.98 L, FIXED) SC (Vc,SC = 1.88 L) administration reconcile biphasic IV profile monophasic SC profile pooled dataset. packaged model uses Vc,SC = 1.88 L primary therapeutic use SC dosing. Simulating IV administration model slightly -represent initial central volume relative source paper. IV dosing exercised vignette. phase 1 IV arm (225 mg 900 mg, 1-hour infusion) supported structurally (omit depot dose, dose directly central) simulated . Race ADA status included simulated covariates retained significant predictors final model.

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Structural model: 2-compartment first-order SC absorption, absorption lag time, first-order elimination, allometric weight scaling CL (exponent 1.05) Vc (exponent 1.53) relative 71 kg reference. Fixed parameters: Q, Vp, F, tlag held fixed source (Section 3.2 rationale: Q Vp estimated IV phase 1 data, F tlag combined IV + SC phase 1 data, fixed pooling phase 2b / 3 trough samples). packaged model preserves wrapping fixed(). IIV: diagonal matrix CL, Vc, ka; source paper reports -diagonal terms estimated. Terminal half-life predicted structural parameters approximately 30 days, matching paper’s reported median.

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Fiedler-Kelly JB, Cohen-Barak O, Morris DN, et al. Population pharmacokinetic modelling simulation fremanezumab healthy subjects patients migraine. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2019;85(12):2721-2733. doi:10.1111/bcp.14096

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm
text: Citation: Fiedler-Kelly JB, Passarell J, Ludwig E, Levi M, Cohen-Barak O. Effect Fremanezumab Monthly Quarterly Doses Efficacy Responses. Headache. 2020 Jul;60(7):1376-1391. doi:10.1111/head.13855. PMID: 32445498. Description: Population PD exposure-response model relating fremanezumab average plasma concentration (Cav) monthly moderate--severe headache days adults chronic migraine. Placebo time-course Hill (sigmoid) function months drug effect power function Cav centered population median Cav. Fitted 5312 monthly observations 1361 chronic-migraine patients pooled across LBR-101-021 phase 2b TV48125-CNS-30049 phase 3 studies (Fiedler-Kelly 2020). Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/head.13855 vignette covers chronic-migraine (CM) exposure-response model Fiedler-Kelly 2020. companion EM model FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em. fremanezumab population PK fitted paper; per-subject Cav values taken Fiedler-Kelly 2019 popPK model (Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab library). CM model relates monthly moderate--severe (M/S) headache days fremanezumab Cav time--treatment via Hill--time placebo power--Cav drug effect, centered individual baseline: msHeadacheDays(t,Cav)=BLi+MaxP,itHiT50Hi+tHi−BLi⋅θD,(CavCavmed)θE,\text{msHeadacheDays}(t, C_{av}) = \text{BL}_i + \text{Max}_{P,}\,\frac{t^{H_i}}{T_{50}^{H_i} + t^{H_i}} - \text{BL}_i \cdot \theta_{D,}\,\left(\frac{C_{av}}{\text{Cav}_{\text{med}}}\right)^{\theta_{E,}} individual baseline piecewise-linear function baseline acute-medication days, BLi=θBL+θSmax⁡(0,ACUTE_MED_DAYS−5)+ηBL,\text{BL}_i = \theta_{BL} + \theta_{S}\,\max(0,\,\text{ACUTE\_MED\_DAYS} - 5) + \eta_{\text{BL},} (breakpoint 5 d/mo). t months (28-day periods). placebo Hill function power--Cav drug effect operator-confirmed Figure 2B; parameter values come Supplementary Table S4.

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm
text: model fitted 5,312 monthly M/S-headache-day observations 1,361 chronic-migraine patients pooled across two studies — LBR-101-021 phase 2b study (n = 251) TV48125-CNS-30049 phase 3 study (n = 1,110). Patients met ICHD-3 criteria chronic migraine: headache least 15 days/month least 8 migraine days/month. Pooled demographics (Supplementary Table S2): mean age 41.3 years (range 18–71); mean baseline weight 72.66 kg (range 43.5–131.8); 87.2% female; 79.8% White, 9.0% Black, 9.0% Asian; 9.1% Hispanic ethnicity; mean years since onset 19.73 (range 0–61); mean baseline M/S headache days 13.2 (range 0–28); mean baseline acute-medication days 5.73 d/mo (range 0–28). Concomitant analgesic use 10.5% concomitant migraine-preventive use 24.2%. Patients received fremanezumab 225 mg q1m 675 mg starting dose, 675 mg quarterly, 900 mg monthly (phase 2b ), placebo SC 3 months; observation unit one 28-day month.

code: mod <- readModelDb("FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm") ev_check <- data.frame(   id   = 1L,   time = 0:3,   CAV  = 0,   ACUTE_MED_DAYS = 0 ) sim_check <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = ev_check, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: etalogitDrugInt #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: etalogitDrugInt #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etabl_cm', 'etamaxPLC_cm', 'etalhill_PLC', 'etalogitDrugInt', 'etaldrugExp' sim_check[, c("time", "msHeadacheDays")] #>   time msHeadacheDays #> 1    0      10.200000 #> 2    1       7.505923 #> 3    2       6.983113 #> 4    3       6.677923

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm
text: original M/S-headache-day diary data publicly available. validation construct virtual CM cohort whose covariate distributions match Supplementary Table S2, simulate three Phase 3 regimens (placebo; 225 mg q1m + 675 mg starting dose; 675 mg quarterly) using fixed schedule period-mean Cav values.
code: set.seed(20260427)  n_per_arm <- 250L  # Per-period Cav values per the regimen-specific median Cavs reported in # the Results — CM section. regimen_cav <- list(   placebo            = c(0, 0, 0),   q3m_675            = c(70, 28, 28),     # Single 675 mg q3m: high mo 1, declining   q1m_225_with_LD    = c(70, 70, 70)      # 225 mg q1m + 675 mg LD: ~steady-state high )  make_arm <- function(arm_name, cav_per_month, n, id_offset) {   expand.grid(     id    = id_offset + seq_len(n),     time  = 1:3   ) |>     arrange(id, time) |>     mutate(       regimen        = arm_name,       ACUTE_MED_DAYS = pmax(0, rnorm(n(), mean = 5.73, sd = 7.01)),       CAV            = cav_per_month[time]     ) }  events <- bind_rows(   make_arm("placebo",         regimen_cav$placebo,         n_per_arm, id_offset =      0L),   make_arm("q3m_675",         regimen_cav$q3m_675,         n_per_arm, id_offset =   1000L),   make_arm("q1m_225_with_LD", regimen_cav$q1m_225_with_LD, n_per_arm, id_offset =   2000L) ) |>   mutate(evid = 0)  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm
text: Figures 3c-d 4b-5b Fiedler-Kelly 2020 plot simulated mean monthly M/S headache days percent CM responders.
code: # Replicates Figure 3C of Fiedler-Kelly 2020: mean monthly M/S headache days by regimen. sim_iiv |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(     mean_md = mean(msHeadacheDays),     sd_md   = sd(msHeadacheDays),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, mean_md, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_md - sd_md, ymax = mean_md + sd_md), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_point(size = 1.5) +   labs(     x        = "Time (months)",     y        = "Mean monthly M/S headache days",     title    = "Figure 3C — mean (±1 SD) monthly M/S headache days, virtual CM cohort",     caption  = "Replicates Figure 3C of Fiedler-Kelly 2020 in the virtual cohort."   ) +   theme_minimal() # Replicates Figure 4B: percent responders (>= 50% reduction in M/S headache days). baseline_per_id <- events |>   group_by(id, regimen) |>   summarise(BL = first(10.2 + 0.460 * pmax(0, ACUTE_MED_DAYS - 5)), .groups = "drop")  responders <- sim_iiv |>   left_join(baseline_per_id, by = c("id", "regimen")) |>   mutate(reduction_pct = 100 * (BL - msHeadacheDays) / BL,          responder     = reduction_pct >= 50)  responder_summary <- responders |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(pct_responders = mean(responder) * 100, .groups = "drop")  ggplot(responder_summary, aes(time, pct_responders, colour = regimen)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   labs(     x       = "Time (months)",     y       = "Percent responders (≥ 50% reduction)",     title   = "Figure 4B — simulated percent CM responders by month",     caption = "Replicates Figure 4B of Fiedler-Kelly 2020."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm
text: Fiedler-Kelly 2020 Results — CM section reports specific quantitative checks reproduce typical-value simulation: Typical-value month-3 M/S headache days reduction baseline regimen. placebo arm produces 1.9-day month-3 reduction (paper: “approximately 3.5 days placebo”). typical Cav 70 µg/mL (q1m + LD), model gives 3.98-day reduction, drug-contribution typical baseline 10.2 d 10.2 × 0.157 × (70/69)^0.328 ≈ 1.61 days (paper narrative: 2.7 3.6 days).
code: narrative_compare <- sim_typ |>   filter(time == 3) |>   group_by(regimen) |>   summarise(     typical_md_month3 = round(mean(msHeadacheDays), 2),     typical_reduction = round(10.2 - mean(msHeadacheDays), 2),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(   narrative_compare,   caption = "Typical-value month-3 M/S headache days and reduction from baseline by regimen." )

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm
text: Time unit months (28-day periods). EM vignette. Placebo time-course form operator-confirmed Figure 2B. Supplementary Table S4 lists Maximum response (placebo), T50 placebo, Hill coefficient placebo, FIXED. Hill--time form placebo_eff(t) = MaxPLC * t^Hill / (T50^Hill + t^Hill) visually read Figure 2B operator sidecar request 3 extraction. CavMedian = 69 µg/mL visually inferred Figure 2B. drug-effect intercept drugInt = 0.157 described S4 “fractional reduction baseline median fremanezumab Cav”, median Cav listed numerically S4 trimmed text. value 69 µg/mL read x-axis position per-regimen median markers Figure 2B (operator decision sidecar request 3, answer cm_cavmed = USER) reproduces paper’s narrative drug-effect ranges (12-16% across Cav 28-70 µg/mL; 18-19% Cav 120 µg/mL). Users can recover exact published Cav_median analysis dataset override CavMedian accordingly model consumed. Cav per-period covariate, model output. CAV column must supplied per row user, derived externally Fiedler-Kelly 2019 popPK model (Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab). Cav values virtual cohort. Hand-tuned Results section’s regimen medians; exact reproduction paper’s full simulation, drive model EB Cav Fiedler-Kelly 2019 popPK simulator. ACUTE_MED_DAYS distribution. virtual cohort draws N(5.73, 7.01) truncated 0 (Supplementary Table S2 mean SD across pooled CM cohort). actual data right-skewed median 1.75 d/mo. Observation variable msHeadacheDays, Cc. Convention waiver EM vignette. units$dosing carries explanatory string satisfy checkModelConventions() requirement; PD-model consume dose events. MU-referencing warning. EM vignette: etalogitDrugInt’s link goes inverse-logit. Affects refitting, simulation.

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em
text: Citation: Fiedler-Kelly JB, Passarell J, Ludwig E, Levi M, Cohen-Barak O. Effect Fremanezumab Monthly Quarterly Doses Efficacy Responses. Headache. 2020 Jul;60(7):1376-1391. doi:10.1111/head.13855. PMID: 32445498. Description: Population PD exposure-response model relating fremanezumab average plasma concentration (Cav) monthly migraine days adults episodic migraine. Placebo time-course exponential growth months (predicted reduction = exp(exponent * t)) drug effect Emax/EC50 Cav scaled individual baseline migraine days. Fitted 4444 monthly observations 1142 episodic-migraine patients pooled across LBR-101-022 phase 2b TV48125-CNS-30050 phase 3 studies (Fiedler-Kelly 2020). Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/head.13855 Fiedler-Kelly 2020 develops two exposure-response (E-R) models fremanezumab, one episodic migraine (EM, vignette) one chronic migraine (CM, separate vignette FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_cm). fremanezumab population PK fitted paper; per-subject Cav values drive E-R taken individual empirical-Bayes estimates Fiedler-Kelly 2019 popPK model (Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab library). EM model relates monthly migraine days fremanezumab Cav time--treatment via three additive components: migraineDays(t,Cav)=BLi−eθP,−BLi⋅Emax⁡,iCavEC50+Cav\text{migraineDays}(t, C_{av}) = \text{BL}_i - e^{\theta_{P,}\,t} - \text{BL}_i \cdot E_{\max,}\,\frac{C_{av}}{EC_{50} + C_{av}} individual baseline piecewise-linear function baseline acute-medication days, BLi=θBL+θSmax⁡(0,ACUTE_MED_DAYS−5)+ηBL,\text{BL}_i = \theta_{BL} + \theta_{S}\,\max(0,\,\text{ACUTE\_MED\_DAYS} - 5) + \eta_{\text{BL},} (breakpoint 5 d/mo per medication-overuse-headache convention). t months (28-day periods). placebo time-course follows operator-confirmed Figure 2A form (exp-decay reduction); drug-effect uses Emax/EC50 Cav reported Supplementary Table S3.

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em
text: model fitted 4,444 monthly migraine-day observations 1,142 episodic-migraine patients pooled across two studies — LBR-101-022 phase 2b study (n = 286) TV48125-CNS-30050 phase 3 study (n = 856). Patients met ICHD-3 criteria episodic migraine: headache 6–14 (phase 3) 8–14 (phase 2b) days per month least 4 migraine days/month. Pooled demographics (Supplementary Table S1): mean age 41.8 years (range 18–70); mean baseline weight 73.99 kg (range 43.1–120.0); 85.7% female; 79.9% White, 11.2% Black, 7.0% Asian; 12.3% Hispanic ethnicity; mean years since onset 19.97 (range 0–65); mean baseline migraine days 9.7 (range 3–20); mean baseline acute-medication days 3.50 d/mo (range 0–15.2). Concomitant analgesic use 6.7% concomitant migraine-preventive use 21.4%. Patients received fremanezumab 225 mg monthly, 675 mg monthly, 675 mg quarterly, 225 mg monthly 675 mg starting dose, placebo SC 3 months; observation unit one 28-day month. demographic summary exposed programmatically via model metadata:

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em
text: original migraine-day diary data publicly available. validation construct virtual EM cohort whose covariate distributions match Supplementary Table S1, simulate four trial regimens (placebo; 225 mg q1m; 675 mg q3m; 225 mg q1m 675 mg starting dose) using fixed schedule period-mean Cav values. Cav supplied per-period covariate column (set 0 placebo periods).
code: set.seed(20260427)  n_per_arm <- 250L  # Per-period (28-day) Cav values for each regimen. # Median Cav values stated in Fiedler-Kelly 2020 Results — Chronic Migraine # section: 28 ug/mL for the 675 mg q3m regimen, 70 ug/mL for the 225 mg q1m # with 675 mg starting-dose regimen. The 225 mg q1m without loading-dose # Cav is approximated at the 28 -> 70 trajectory midpoint. regimen_cav <- list(   placebo            = c(0, 0, 0),   q1m_225            = c(35, 50, 60),    # 225 mg monthly, no LD (build-up)   q3m_675            = c(70, 28, 28),    # 675 mg quarterly: high in mo 1, lower mo 2-3   q1m_225_with_LD    = c(70, 70, 70)     # 225 mg q1m + 675 mg starting dose: ~steady high )  make_arm <- function(arm_name, cav_per_month, n, id_offset) {   expand.grid(     id    = id_offset + seq_len(n),     time  = 1:3   ) |>     arrange(id, time) |>     mutate(       regimen        = arm_name,       ACUTE_MED_DAYS = pmax(0, rnorm(n(), mean = 3.50, sd = 4.17)),       CAV            = cav_per_month[time]     ) }  events <- bind_rows(   make_arm("placebo",         regimen_cav$placebo,         n_per_arm, id_offset =      0L),   make_arm("q1m_225",         regimen_cav$q1m_225,         n_per_arm, id_offset =   1000L),   make_arm("q3m_675",         regimen_cav$q3m_675,         n_per_arm, id_offset =   2000L),   make_arm("q1m_225_with_LD", regimen_cav$q1m_225_with_LD, n_per_arm, id_offset =   3000L) ) |>   mutate(evid = 0)  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))
