title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em
text: Figures 3a-b 4a Fiedler-Kelly 2020 plot mean (± 1 SD) monthly migraine days percent responders three-month follow-.
code: # Replicates Figure 3A of Fiedler-Kelly 2020: mean monthly migraine days by regimen. sim_iiv |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(     mean_md = mean(migraineDays),     sd_md   = sd(migraineDays),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, mean_md, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_md - sd_md, ymax = mean_md + sd_md), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_point(size = 1.5) +   labs(     x        = "Time (months)",     y        = "Mean monthly migraine days",     title    = "Figure 3A — mean (±1 SD) monthly migraine days, virtual EM cohort",     caption  = "Replicates Figure 3A of Fiedler-Kelly 2020 in the virtual cohort."   ) +   theme_minimal() # Replicates Figure 4A of Fiedler-Kelly 2020: percent responders (>= 50% # reduction from baseline migraine days) by month and regimen. baseline_per_id <- events |>   group_by(id, regimen) |>   summarise(BL = first(8.35 + 0.438 * pmax(0, ACUTE_MED_DAYS - 5)), .groups = "drop")  responders <- sim_iiv |>   left_join(baseline_per_id, by = c("id", "regimen")) |>   mutate(reduction_pct = 100 * (BL - migraineDays) / BL,          responder     = reduction_pct >= 50)  responder_summary <- responders |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(pct_responders = mean(responder) * 100, .groups = "drop")  ggplot(responder_summary, aes(time, pct_responders, colour = regimen)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   labs(     x       = "Time (months)",     y       = "Percent responders (≥ 50% reduction)",     title   = "Figure 4A — simulated percent responders by month",     caption = "Replicates Figure 4A of Fiedler-Kelly 2020."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em
text: Fiedler-Kelly 2020 Results — EM section reports specific narrative numbers reproduce typical-value simulation: Typical-value month-3 migraine days reduction baseline regimen. placebo arm produces 2.42-day month-3 reduction (paper: “approximately 3 days”). 225 mg q1m + 675 mg LD arm achieves 4.54-day reduction month 3. maximal Cav-driven reduction model 2.1 days, .e. ~25% typical baseline (paper: “approximately 25% additional maximal reduction (ie, approximately 2 days)”).
code: narrative_compare <- sim_typ |>   filter(time == 3) |>   group_by(regimen) |>   summarise(     typical_md_month3 = round(mean(migraineDays), 2),     typical_reduction = round(8.35 - mean(migraineDays), 2),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(   narrative_compare,   caption = "Typical-value month-3 migraine days and reduction from baseline by regimen." )

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em
text: PKNCA wrong validation target model: concentration profile integrate (Cav supplied covariate, derived PK ODE), response variable count migraine days per month rather sampled concentration. validation strategy adopted therefore narrative-comparison table immediately , mirroring operator-confirmed Figure 2A interpretation matching per-regimen reduction ranges reported Results section.

title: FiedlerKelly_2020_fremanezumab_em
text: Time unit months (28-day periods). paper aggregates outcomes monthly periods reports placebo drug-effect parameters implicit “month” time unit. model’s units$time = "month" documents ; users supplying time days need divide 28. Placebo time-course form operator-confirmed Figure 2A. Supplementary Table S3 lists Exponent placebo time-course. functional form BL - exp(exponent * t) visually read Figure 2A operator sidecar request 3 extraction. typical exponent 0.360 model gives 2.94-day placebo reduction month 3 (matching paper’s narrative “approximately 3 days”). Cav per-period covariate, model output. PD-file. CAV column must supplied per row user, derived externally Fiedler-Kelly 2019 popPK model (Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab) observed exposure data. CAV = 0 placebo periods. Cav values virtual cohort. per-period regimen Cav profiles cohort builder simplified hand-tuned values intended bracket observed median Cavs reported Results section (28 µg/mL 675 mg q3m, 70 µg/mL 225 mg q1m + LD). exact reproduction paper’s full simulation, drive model EB Cav Fiedler-Kelly 2019 popPK simulator. ACUTE_MED_DAYS distribution. virtual cohort draws ACUTE_MED_DAYS N(3.50, 4.17) truncated 0 (Supplementary Table S1 mean SD across pooled EM cohort). actual data heavily right-skewed distribution median 0.97, typical-value simulation slightly -represents high-tail. Observation variable migraineDays, Cc. checkModelConventions() warning recommending rename Cc appropriate count--days endpoint concentration. Following convention Mulyukov_2018_ranibizumab (uses bcva output). units$dosing carries explanatory string satisfy checkModelConventions() requirement; PD-model consume dose events. model’s input CAV covariate column. MU-referencing warning. nlmixr2’s parser flags etalogitEmax (analogous CM-side etalogitDrugInt) “non-mu-referenced” link typical theta individual parameter goes inverse-logit transform rather simple addition. affects estimation behaviour (model intended simulation, refitting) documented readers attempt refit model new data.

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Citation: Frey N, Grange S, Woodworth T. Population pharmacokinetic analysis tocilizumab patients rheumatoid arthritis. J Clin Pharmacol. 2010;50(7):754-766. doi:10.1177/0091270009350623 Description: Two-compartment population PK model tocilizumab adults moderate--severe rheumatoid arthritis (Frey 2010), parallel first-order linear Michaelis-Menten elimination central compartment. Article: J Clin Pharmacol. 2010;50(7):754-766

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Frey 2010 pooled data four phase III studies — OPTION (N = 396), TOWARD (N = 718), RADIATE (N = 341), AMBITION (N = 338) — final population PK dataset 1793 adults moderate--severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA). dataset contained 7415 serum tocilizumab concentrations 4 8 mg/kg one-hour intravenous infusions every 4 weeks 24 weeks (Frey 2010 Methods, p754; Results, p757). four trials together cover patients inadequate response methotrexate (OPTION), inadequate response traditional DMARDs (TOWARD), inadequate response anti-TNF therapy (RADIATE), tocilizumab monotherapy (AMBITION). Baseline demographics (Frey 2010 Table , p758): median age 52-54 years (range 18-89), 81-84% female, median body weight 67-73 kg (range 38-150), median BSA 1.7-1.8 m^2, median serum albumin 38-39 g/L (range 24-50), median creatinine clearance 104-113 mL/min, median total serum protein 73-75 g/L, median HDL cholesterol 54 mg/dL, median rheumatoid factor 92-117 U/mL (range 15-11800), 78-84% non-smokers. Race composition 72-90% White / 9-13% Asian (highest OPTION) / 0-5% Black / 2-10% American Indian Alaskan Native / 2-5% across four trials. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Frey_2010_tocilizumab")$population.

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Frey 2010 Tables II III. reference covariate values : BSA = 1.8 m^2, male sex, HDL-C = 54 mg/dL, log(RF) = 4.7 (equivalently RF ~110 U/mL), total protein = 74 g/L, albumin = 38 g/L, creatinine clearance = 106 mL/min, non-smoker.

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Two-compartment IV parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination. Frey 2010 fits 2-compartment model first-order linear clearance CL (L/day) saturable Michaelis-Menten elimination Vm (mg/day) Km (ug/mL) acting central compartment. depot compartment tocilizumab administered 1-hour IV infusion; model file dose enters central compartment directly. CV% log-normal variance. Frey 2010 Table II reports -subject variability CV% linear-parameter scale -diagonal correlations etas Pearson r. conversion omega^2 = log(1 + CV^2) gives log-normal variance; -diagonal covariances computed r_ij * sqrt(omega_i^2 * omega_j^2). Log-RF covariate. Frey 2010 fits rheumatoid-factor effect natural-log scale using power--ratio form (LRF / 4.7)^0.1 LRF = log(RF). canonical column RHEUMATOID_FACTOR carries raw RF U/mL; model equation applies log transform internally (log(RHEUMATOID_FACTOR) / log(110))^e_lrf_cl, algebraically identical paper’s form log(110) ~= 4.7. CRCL units. Frey 2010 uses raw creatinine clearance mL/min (paper’s Table III equation VM = 7.5 * (CRCL/106)^0.2), reference 106 mL/min. canonical CRCL column normally documented BSA-normalized (mL/min/1.73 m^2); Frey 2010 column carries raw CrCl mL/min BSA enters model separately linear CL. per-model covariateData[[CRCL]]$notes documents deviation.

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: published Table II (Frey 2010 p759) lists residual-error standard deviations swapped Greek-letter subscripts: row labels units (additive 2.4 ug/mL; proportional 22%) correct unambiguous. parenthetical Greek subscripts sigma_prop / sigma_add interchanged relative residual-error equation Methods (p756), eps1 proportional error (variance sigma^2_prop) eps2 additive error (variance sigma^2_add). model file uses values unambiguous rows: propSd = 0.22 addSd = 2.4 ug/mL. second, smaller notational issue: Frey 2010 Figure 1 (p760) labels y-axis panels Vm VM (mg/h), Table II Results narrative report VM = 7.5 mg/d. model file uses per-day value (mg/day), consistent table text. may figure-axis-label slip rather value error.

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Frey 2010 Table demographics. subject-level observed data released paper. Two regimens simulated parallel: 4 mg/kg 8 mg/kg one-hour IV infusions every 4 weeks (Q4W). Six Q4W doses (168 days, ~8 elimination half-lives linear range; reference t1/2 ~21 days per Frey 2010 Discussion p762) place final cycle safely steady state.
code: set.seed(20260428)  # Cohort size: 200 subjects per dose arm gives stable 5/50/95 percentile # bands for the Figure 4 / Figure 3 reproduction and the PKNCA distribution # summaries. n_subj <- 200  clamp <- function(x, lo, hi) pmin(pmax(x, lo), hi)  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id  = seq_len(n_subj),   WT  = clamp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 70,  sd = 17),   38, 150),   BSA = clamp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 1.8, sd = 0.22), 1.3, 2.7),   SEXF = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.82),   HDLC = clamp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 54, sd = 14),   23, 135),   RHEUMATOID_FACTOR = pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(110), 0.9), 15),   TPRO = clamp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 74, sd = 5),    57, 96),   ALB  = clamp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 38, sd = 4),    24, 50),   CRCL = clamp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 106, sd = 35),  27, 317),   SMOKE = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.18) ) tau     <- 28              # Q4W dosing interval (days) inf_dur <- 1 / 24          # 1-hour IV infusion duration (days) n_doses <- 6 dose_days <- seq(0, tau * (n_doses - 1), by = tau)  build_events <- function(cohort, dose_mgkg, treatment) {   ev_dose <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_mgkg * WT,         # mg/kg * kg = mg                   cmt = "central",                   evid = 1L,                   dur  = inf_dur,                   treatment = treatment)   ss_start <- tau * (n_doses - 1)   ss_end   <- ss_start + tau   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, 56, by = 1),                # daily over the first 2 cycles     seq(56, ss_start, by = 3),         # every 3 days through the build-up     seq(ss_start, ss_end, by = 0.5),   # half-day grid for the SS cycle / NCA     dose_days + inf_dur,               # peak-near-end-of-infusion     dose_days + 1, dose_days + 7, dose_days + 14   )))   ev_obs <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L,                   dur = NA_real_, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, dur, treatment,                   WT, BSA, SEXF, HDLC, RHEUMATOID_FACTOR,                   TPRO, ALB, CRCL, SMOKE) }  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   build_events(cohort, 4, "4 mg/kg Q4W"),   build_events(cohort |> dplyr::mutate(id = id + n_subj), 8, "8 mg/kg Q4W") ) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Frey_2010_tocilizumab")) conc_unit <- mod$units[["concentration"]] keep_cols <- c("WT", "BSA", "SEXF", "HDLC", "RHEUMATOID_FACTOR",                "TPRO", "ALB", "CRCL", "SMOKE", "treatment")  sim <- lapply(split(events, events$treatment), function(ev) {   out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = ev, keep = keep_cols)   as.data.frame(out) }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Frey 2010 Figure 4 (p762) shows mean 5th/95th-percentile simulated tocilizumab serum concentrations 24 weeks treatment, separately 4 mg/kg Q4W (panels C) 8 mg/kg Q4W (panels B D); panels /B use linear y-axis C/D log y-axis. block replicates linear-scale panels B.  simulation log y-axis reproduces dynamic range shown Frey 2010 Figure 4 panels C D, 4 mg/kg trough drops well 8 mg/kg trough nonlinear (Vm/Km) clearance pathway saturates less completely lower dose.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0, time <= tau * n_doses) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~treatment, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = paste0("Tocilizumab Cc (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Replicates Frey 2010 Figure 4 (panels A, B): linear-axis VPC",     caption = "Simulated 5/50/95 percentile profiles over 6 Q4W cycles."   ) +   theme_minimal() ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pmax(Q05, 0.1), ymax = Q95),               alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = paste0("Tocilizumab Cc (", conc_unit, ", log scale)"),     title = "Replicates Frey 2010 Figure 4 (panels C, D): log-axis VPC"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis final (steady-state) Q4W dosing interval gives Cmax, Cmin (Ctrough), AUC0-tau per simulated subject dose group. per-subject results summarised mean +/- SD, format reported Frey 2010 Table IV.
code: ss_start <- tau * (n_doses - 1) ss_end   <- ss_start + tau  nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  # One representative dose per subject per arm at the start of the SS cycle. nca_dose <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == ss_start) |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = tau,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      89% |  ETA:  0s summary(nca_res) #>  start end   treatment   N     auclast        cmax        cmin #>      0  28 4 mg/kg Q4W 200  477 [55.7] 82.2 [53.0] 0.672 [180] #>      0  28 8 mg/kg Q4W 200 1240 [49.1]  165 [46.9]  3.95 [240] #>                     tmax         cav #>  0.0417 [0.0417, 0.0417] 17.0 [55.7] #>  0.0417 [0.0417, 0.0417] 44.2 [49.1] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Table IV Frey 2010 (p762) reports simulated steady-state mean (SD) AUC, Cmax, Cmin 48 weeks treatment. values mean per-subject NCA results vignette’s 6-cycle simulation; accumulation small Q4W dosing (~1.1-1.2x AUC Cmax, ~2x Cmin per Table IV) 6-cycle vs 12-cycle comparison dominated within-cycle PK rather residual accumulation. Simulated vs. Frey 2010 Table IV mean steady-state AUC (10^3 h*ug/mL), Cmax (ug/mL), Cmin (ug/mL). Published values: 4 mg/kg AUC 13 (5.8), Cmax 88 (41), Cmin 1.5 (2.1); 8 mg/kg AUC 35 (16), Cmax 183 (86), Cmin 9.7 (11).
code: nca_long <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result)  per_subj <- nca_long |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "cmin", "auclast")) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(id_cols = c(treatment, id),                      names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES) |>   dplyr::mutate(auclast_h = auclast * 24)   # day*ug/mL -> h*ug/mL  ss_summary <- per_subj |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Cmax_sim_mean = mean(cmax, na.rm = TRUE),     Cmax_sim_sd   = sd(cmax,   na.rm = TRUE),     Cmin_sim_mean = mean(cmin, na.rm = TRUE),     Cmin_sim_sd   = sd(cmin,   na.rm = TRUE),     AUC_sim_mean  = mean(auclast_h, na.rm = TRUE) / 1000,  # in 10^3 h*ug/mL     AUC_sim_sd    = sd(auclast_h,   na.rm = TRUE) / 1000,     .groups = "drop"   )  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment     = c("4 mg/kg Q4W", "8 mg/kg Q4W"),   AUC_pub_mean  = c(13,  35),    # 10^3 h*ug/mL   AUC_pub_sd    = c(5.8, 16),   Cmax_pub_mean = c(88,  183),   # ug/mL   Cmax_pub_sd   = c(41,  86),   Cmin_pub_mean = c(1.5, 9.7),   # ug/mL   Cmin_pub_sd   = c(2.1, 11) )  comparison <- ss_summary |>   dplyr::left_join(published, by = "treatment") |>   dplyr::mutate(     Cmax_pct_diff = 100 * (Cmax_sim_mean - Cmax_pub_mean) / Cmax_pub_mean,     Cmin_pct_diff = 100 * (Cmin_sim_mean - Cmin_pub_mean) / Cmin_pub_mean,     AUC_pct_diff  = 100 * (AUC_sim_mean  - AUC_pub_mean)  / AUC_pub_mean   ) |>   dplyr::select(treatment,                 AUC_pub_mean,  AUC_sim_mean,  AUC_pct_diff,                 Cmax_pub_mean, Cmax_sim_mean, Cmax_pct_diff,                 Cmin_pub_mean, Cmin_sim_mean, Cmin_pct_diff)  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,   caption = paste("Simulated vs. Frey 2010 Table IV mean steady-state",                   "AUC (10^3 h*ug/mL), Cmax (ug/mL), and Cmin (ug/mL).",                   "Published values: 4 mg/kg AUC 13 (5.8), Cmax 88 (41),",                   "Cmin 1.5 (2.1); 8 mg/kg AUC 35 (16), Cmax 183 (86),",                   "Cmin 9.7 (11)."))

title: Frey_2010_tocilizumab
text: Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Body weight, BSA, HDL-C, RF (log-normal), total protein, albumin, creatinine clearance drawn independent truncated-normal log-normal distributions parameters approximating Frey 2010 Table medians observed ranges. Smoking drawn Bernoulli p=0.18 match ~18% smoker prevalence Frey 2010 Results (p759). simulator treats covariates independent, whereas source data BSA, weight, BMI, total protein, albumin correlated; approximation acceptable resulting marginal distributions match Table summaries within 1 SD. subject-level observed data. Frey 2010 release subject-level concentrations; validation reproduces Table IV summary statistics Figure 4’s VPC bands rather overlaying observed points. Simulation horizon. vignette simulates 6 Q4W doses (168 days) instead 12-dose / 48-week horizon Frey 2010 Table IV. Accumulation ratios reported Table IV (~1.1-1.2x AUC Cmax, ~2x Cmin) small dosing interval exceeds three linear-range half-lives; difference dose 6 dose 12 steady state therefore negligible mean comparison. CRCL units. canonical CRCL covariate column carries raw creatinine clearance mL/min model (Frey 2010’s parameterization), BSA-normalized mL/min/1.73 m^2 form used models package. See per-model covariateData[[CRCL]]$notes. Residual-error label swap. model uses propSd = 0.22, addSd = 2.4 ug/mL; Frey 2010 Table II header swaps sigma_prop / sigma_add Greek subscripts row labels values unambiguous. See Errata section . Dosing assumption. Doses administered 1-hour IV infusions dur = 1/24 day, matching Frey 2010 Methods (p754). difference bolus 1-hour infusion sampling times beyond infusion negligible given ~21-day terminal half-life.

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: Citation: Levi M, Grange S, Frey N. Exposure-response relationship tocilizumab, anti-IL-6 receptor monoclonal antibody, large population patients rheumatoid arthritis. J Clin Pharmacol. 2013;53(2):151-159. doi:10.1177/0091270012437585. PMID 23436260. PK backbone: Frey N, Grange S, Woodworth T. Population pharmacokinetic analysis tocilizumab patients rheumatoid arthritis. J Clin Pharmacol. 2010;50(7):754-766. doi:[10.1177/0091270009350623](https://doi.org/10.1177/0091270009350623); packaged separately Frey_2010_tocilizumab library. file extracts indirect-response DAS28 PD model. library function name Frey_2013_tocilizumab follows package’s senior-author convention (Frey senior popPK / PD author tocilizumab program); actual paper’s first author Levi.

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: Frey 2013 fitted PD model pool two four phase III tocilizumab studies — OPTION (methotrexate-inadequate responders) TOWARD (traditional-DMARD-inadequate responders) — yielding 1703 patients 12,618 DAS28 observations week 24. 80% patients female majority (~74%) White; remaining race groups (Asian, American Indian/Alaska Native, ) pooled paper Asian--Others composite drives RACE_ASIAN_OTH covariate effect Kout. Tocilizumab administered 4 8 mg/kg 1-hour IV infusion every 4 weeks 24 weeks, plus placebo--DMARD-background arm. Baseline biomarkers (Supplementary Table S1) gave median IL-6 around 20 pg/mL, median HAQ-DI 1.5-1.6, median PAIN VAS 60-62, median Physician’s Global VAS 65 — median values reference covariate values used model. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Frey_2013_tocilizumab")$population.

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: Every PD parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term comes Frey 2013 Tables 1 2 (final-model column). PK backbone reproduced Frey 2010 Table II typical reference-covariate values; PK covariate effects, PK IIV, PK residual error Frey 2010 reproduced (exposure-response overlay file, popPK file). full covariate-aware tocilizumab popPK, use standalone Frey_2010_tocilizumab library model.

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: Indirect-response inhibition DMARD background. drug effect uses sum tocilizumab concentration DMARD background (expressed tocilizumab concentration units): CeffP = Cc + DMARD. DMARD = 0.30 ug/mL typical drug-effect parameters (EC50 = 3.7, Emax = 0.73, gamma = 0.64), fractional effect zero tocilizumab `0.73 * 0.30^0.64 / (3.7^0.64 0.30^0.64) ≈ 0.124`, placebo+DMARD arm shows 12% reduction DAS28 steady state (decrease 0.80 units typical baseline 6.8) — figure stated Frey 2013 Discussion. Initial condition matches typical observed baseline. DAS28 compartment initialized Base, typical observed baseline DAS28 6.8 reported Frey 2013 Table 1. Kin = Kout * Base, placebo---DMARD---tocilizumab steady state Base; constant DMARD background plus tocilizumab drives trajectory Base time. Sex covariate encoded female canonical reference. Frey 2013 Table 2 reports Emax = 0.72 * 1.0 females Emax = 0.72 * 1.1 males. library uses canonical SEXF column (1 = female, 0 = male) applies equation Emax = Emax_typ * (1 + 0.11 * (1 - SEXF)), females (SEXF = 1) get unmodified typical Emax males (SEXF = 0) get +11% bump. typical population value Emax = 0.73 reported Table 1 consistent parameterization given ~80% female cohort. Race covariate composite Asian/AmInd/indicator. Frey 2013 pools smaller-N race groups single Asian--others composite (RACE = 1) White + Black reference (RACE = 0). library introduces new canonical RACE_ASIAN_OTH composite (registered alongside existing RACE_BLACK_OTH RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI per-paper composites). Effect form: Kout = Kout_typ * (1 + (-0.25) * RACE_ASIAN_OTH), Kout 25% lower Asian/AmInd/composite group. IL-6 log-transformed via (log(IL6 * 1000) / 9.9) ratio. log-IL-6 ratio appears three places final model: EC50 (exponent -4.4), BASE (exponent +0.13), DMARD background (exponent -6.4). constant 9.9 = log(20000) corresponds reference IL-6 ~20 pg/mL (OPTION/TOWARD median per Supplementary Table S1). library carries IL6 raw pg/mL column applies log transform inside model(). PAIN HAQ floored 0.010 inside model(). Frey 2013 Table 2 reports paper-side covariate ranges PAIN HAQ starting 0.010 (0), reflecting explicit floor used keep power form well-defined patient reports zero score. library applies floor inside model() via max(0.010, PAIN) max(0.010, BLHAQ). affects small fraction subjects zero score VAS-style components. CV% log-normal variance. IIV reported CV% linear-parameter scale (Frey 2013 Table 1). nlmixr2 convention log-normal IIV log-transformed parameter; conversion omega^2 = log(CV^2 + 1) applied ini().

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: Two minor numerical inconsistencies detected extracting parameter values: HAQ--BASE exponent: Table 1 reports 0.040, Table 2 formula uses 0.043. Working backwards Table 2 stated percent-change range (-20% HAQ = 0.010, +2.6% HAQ = 3.0), exponent must 0.043 (giving -19.6% +2.7%); 0.040 value Table 1 produces -18.4% reproduce stated -20%. model uses 0.043 (Table 2’s value). PAIN--BASE exponent: Table 1 reports 0.060, Table 2 formula uses 0.062. Working backwards Table 2 stated percent-change range (-42% PAIN = 0.010, +3.2% PAIN = 100), exponent must 0.062 (giving -41.7% +3.2%); 0.060 value Table 1 produces -40.6%. model uses 0.062 (Table 2’s value). cases Table 1 entry appears rounded--two-decimal display; Table 2 formula values (0.043 0.062) actual estimates reproduce stated ranges exactly. third minor display inconsistency exists Table 1 Table 2 Emax (Table 1: 0.73; Table 2 formula: 0.72 * 1.0 female, 0.72 * 1.1 male). two reconciled SEX covariate: Table 2’s 0.72 female-reference NONMEM theta, Table 1’s 0.73 typical-population value cohort’s ~80%-female mix (0.72 * 0.80 + 0.72 * 1.1 * 0.20 = 0.736 ≈ 0.73). library uses Table 1 typical 0.73 ini() applies +11% bump males via (1 + 0.11 * (1 - SEXF)); slightly overstates Emax sexes ~1% relative strict Table 2 parameterization keeps model anchored Table 1’s reported typical estimate.

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: cohort-level simulations use small virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate OPTION/TOWARD pool per Supplementary Table S1. Subject-level observed data released paper. Three regimens simulated: placebo (zero tocilizumab dose, DMARD background still active constant DMARD term), 4 mg/kg IV q4w, 8 mg/kg IV q4w 24 weeks. Per-subject doses calculated assuming 70-kg typical body weight (PK backbone embedded typical Frey 2010 PK reference values, per-individual weight scaling applied ).
code: set.seed(20260429) n_subj <- 40  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id             = seq_len(n_subj),   IL6            = pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(20) - 0.5 * 1.0^2, 1.0), 0.7),   SEXF           = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.82),   RACE_ASIAN_OTH = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.24),   BLHAQ          = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 1.55, sd = 0.65), 0, 3)),   PAIN           = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 60,   sd = 20),  0, 100)),   BLPHYVAS       = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 65,   sd = 18),  10, 100)) ) tau       <- 28                          # Q4W dosing interval (days) week24    <- 24 * 7                      # day 168 n_doses   <- ceiling(week24 / tau)       # 6 doses through 24 weeks dose_days <- seq(0, tau * (n_doses - 1), by = tau) # Trim observation grid to keep the vignette under the 5-min wall-time gate. obs_days  <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, week24, by = 7), dose_days, dose_days + 1))) infusion_dur <- 1 / 24                   # 1-hour infusion in days

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: model declares single observation equation (das28 ~ add(...)), rxSolve() accepts straightforward multi-subject event tables. deterministic typical-patient comparison paper’s week-24 statistics, zero random effects use reference-covariate medians (IL6 20 pg/mL, SEXF = 1 (female), RACE_ASIAN_OTH = 0 (White Black), BLHAQ = 1.6, PAIN = 60, BLPHYVAS = 65):
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Frey_2013_tocilizumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim_one <- function(subj_row, dose_per_kg, treatment, body_kg = 70) {   amt_mg <- dose_per_kg * body_kg   if (amt_mg > 0) {     ev_dose <- subj_row |>       tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>       dplyr::mutate(         amt  = amt_mg,         rate = amt_mg / infusion_dur,         cmt  = "central",         evid = 1L       )   } else {     ev_dose <- subj_row[0, ] |>       dplyr::mutate(time = numeric(0), amt = numeric(0),                     rate = numeric(0), cmt = character(0), evid = integer(0))   }   ev_obs <- subj_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = "das28", evid = 0L)   events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid,                   IL6, SEXF, RACE_ASIAN_OTH, BLHAQ, PAIN, BLPHYVAS)   out <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events))   out$id        <- subj_row$id   out$treatment <- treatment   out }  simulate_cohort <- function(cohort, dose_per_kg, treatment) {   lapply(seq_len(nrow(cohort)), function(i) {     sim_one(cohort[i, , drop = FALSE], dose_per_kg, treatment)   }) |> dplyr::bind_rows() }  sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   simulate_cohort(cohort, 0, "Placebo"),   simulate_cohort(cohort, 4, "TCZ_4mgkg_q4w"),   simulate_cohort(cohort, 8, "TCZ_8mgkg_q4w") ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L,   IL6 = 20,   SEXF = 1L,   RACE_ASIAN_OTH = 0L,   BLHAQ = 1.6,   PAIN = 60,   BLPHYVAS = 65 )  ev_typ <- function(dose_per_kg, body_kg = 70) {   amt_mg <- dose_per_kg * body_kg   if (amt_mg > 0) {     ev_dose <- typical_cov |>       tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>       dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_mg, rate = amt_mg / infusion_dur,                     cmt = "central", evid = 1L)   } else {     ev_dose <- typical_cov[0, ] |>       dplyr::mutate(time = numeric(0), amt = numeric(0),                     rate = numeric(0), cmt = character(0), evid = integer(0))   }   ev_obs <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = "das28", evid = 0L)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, rate, cmt, evid,                   IL6, SEXF, RACE_ASIAN_OTH, BLHAQ, PAIN, BLPHYVAS) }  sim_typ <- dplyr::bind_rows(   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(0))) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "Placebo"),   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(4))) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "TCZ_4mgkg_q4w"),   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(8))) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "TCZ_8mgkg_q4w") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalEC50', 'etalEmax', 'etalKout', 'etalBase', 'etalDMARD' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalEC50', 'etalEmax', 'etalKout', 'etalBase', 'etalDMARD' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalEC50', 'etalEmax', 'etalKout', 'etalBase', 'etalDMARD'
