title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: Frey 2013 reports model-based simulation results DAS28 remission (DAS28 < 2.6) EULAR good-response rates 24 weeks treatment virtual RA cohort 108,500 patients (Discussion narrative, Figure 3 PPC): 4 mg/kg → 24% DAS28 remission, 32% EULAR good-response 8 mg/kg → 38% DAS28 remission, 48% EULAR good-response small virtual-cohort simulation (N = 40) reach 108,500-patient resolution paper’s full simulation, section validates typical-patient trajectories paper’s Discussion narrative reports cohort remission rates directional check. Week-24 DAS28 summary. Typical-patient column IIV-zeroed; cohort_remission_pct uses small N = 40 virtual cohort. paper_remission_pct paper_eular_good_pct Frey 2013 Discussion values 108,500-patient full simulation.
code: week24_typ <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(das28), abs(time - week24) < 1e-6) |>   dplyr::transmute(treatment, das28_typ = das28)  cohort_remission <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(das28), abs(time - week24) < 1e-6) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     cohort_remission_pct = 100 * mean(das28 < 2.6, na.rm = TRUE),     cohort_median_das28  = median(das28, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment            = c("Placebo", "TCZ_4mgkg_q4w", "TCZ_8mgkg_q4w"),   paper_remission_pct  = c(NA, 24, 38),   paper_eular_good_pct = c(NA, 32, 48) )  comparison <- published |>   dplyr::left_join(week24_typ, by = "treatment") |>   dplyr::left_join(cohort_remission, by = "treatment") |>   dplyr::arrange(match(treatment, c("Placebo", "TCZ_4mgkg_q4w", "TCZ_8mgkg_q4w")))  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,   caption = paste0(     "Week-24 DAS28 summary. Typical-patient column is IIV-zeroed; ",     "cohort_remission_pct uses the small N = ", n_subj,     " virtual cohort. paper_remission_pct and paper_eular_good_pct ",     "are Frey 2013 Discussion values from the 108,500-patient full ",     "simulation."   ))

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: reference covariates (IL6 20 pg/mL, SEXF = 1, RACE_ASIAN_OTH = 0, BLHAQ = 1.6, PAIN = 60, BLPHYVAS = 65) model’s typical Base must equal exp(log(6.8)) = 6.8. DAS28 state initialized Base, t = 0 value DAS28 compartment 6.8 regardless regimen. Typical-patient DAS28 t = 0 (arms; equal 6.8).
code: baseline_check <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(das28), time == 0) |>   dplyr::distinct(treatment, baseline = das28) knitr::kable(baseline_check, digits = 3,   caption = "Typical-patient DAS28 at t = 0 (all arms; should equal 6.8).")

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: Frey 2013 reports placebo + DMARD-background arm reaches roughly 12% reduction DAS28 steady state (decrease 0.80 DAS28 units typical baseline 6.8). consequence constant DMARD term CeffP = Cc + DMARD. block extracts typical-patient placebo trajectory compares week-24 DAS28 paper. Typical-patient placebo-arm week-24 DAS28 reduction.
code: placebo_check <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(treatment == "Placebo", !is.na(das28),                 abs(time - week24) < 1e-6) |>   dplyr::transmute(     das28_week24    = das28,     pct_reduct_sim  = 100 * (6.8 - das28) / 6.8,     pct_reduct_pub  = 12   ) knitr::kable(placebo_check, digits = 2,   caption = "Typical-patient placebo-arm week-24 DAS28 reduction.")

title: Frey_2013_tocilizumab
text: PK backbone embedded typical covariate values. Frey 2013 fed individual empirical-Bayes PK estimates Frey 2010 PD fit. keep library model self-contained single file embed Frey 2010 typical PK reference covariate values (BSA 1.8 m^2, HDL-C 54 mg/dL, log RF 4.7, total protein 74 g/L, albumin 38 g/L, creatinine clearance 106 mL/min, non-smoker, male) PK IIV PK covariate effects — file exposure-response overlay, popPK file. Users need covariate-aware PK compose PD model standalone Frey_2010_tocilizumab library model (simulate two steps: Frey 2010 PK generate individual Cc(t), simplified indirect-response PD block consumes Cc input). Sex-effect parameterization choice. anchor Emax_typ = 0.73 Frey 2013 Table 1’s typical-population value rather Table 2’s female-reference 0.72. keeps typical-patient simulation aligned published typical-population estimate slightly overstates Emax (~1%) sex categories relative strict Table 2 parameterization. See Errata section. Use Table 2 exponents 0.043 (HAQ) 0.062 (PAIN) instead Table 1’s 0.040 0.060. Table 1 entries rounded display values; Table 2 formula values reproduce stated percent-change ranges. See Errata. PAIN HAQ floor 0.010. Reproduces Frey 2013 Table 2’s paper-side floor covariates. Affects subjects zero VAS scores; floor power form otherwise collapse 0 model predict unphysical zero baseline. Composite race indicator RACE_ASIAN_OTH. Frey 2013 pools smaller-N race groups (Asian, American Indian/Alaska Native, ) single Asian--others category White + Black reference. library introduces new specific-scope canonical RACE_ASIAN_OTH composite. composite mutually exclusive decomposed RACE_ASIAN, RACE_OTHER, etc. indicators model — combine . Convention-checker warnings (compartment / observation naming). nlmixr2lib::checkModelConventions("Frey_2013_tocilizumab") flags two warnings: (1) PD-state compartment das28 canonical compartment list (canonical list covers PK generic effect compartment, disease-score states); (2) observation variable das28 match canonical Cc single-output PK models. warnings inherent disease-score PKPD models non-PK observation; warnings appear Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp indirect-response disease- activity models. PK side still expose Cc derived quantity; observation hooked residual error DAS28 state rather Cc Frey 2013 fitted DAS28 observations. typical-patient DAS28 trajectories analogous Frey 2013 Figure 4; (ii) cohort-level DAS28 VPC analogous Supplementary Figure S2; (iii) baseline reproduction (DAS28 t = 0 must equal Base); placebo+DMARD-arm 12% reduction week 24; (v) cohort remission rate directional check paper’s 108,500-patient simulation values (24% / 38% remission 4 / 8 mg/kg). Cohort N small (40 subjects) cohort percentages wide intrinsic Monte-Carlo error; directional comparison validation, exact-percent agreement. DAS28-ESR vs DAS28-CRP. Frey 2013 used DAS28-ESR (DAS28 erythrocyte-sedimentation-rate component). companion sarilumab paper (Ma 2020) used DAS28-CRP. two scales similar ranges clinical interpretations identical; numerical comparison model’s DAS28 Ma 2020 DAS28-CRP requires care. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. IL6 drawn log-normal median 20 pg/mL GSD ~2.7; SEXF Bernoulli(0.82); RACE_ASIAN_OTH Bernoulli(0.24); BLHAQ N(1.55, 0.65) truncated [0, 3]; PAIN N(60, 20) truncated [0, 100]; BLPHYVAS N(65, 18) truncated [10, 100]. ranges approximate Supplementary Table S1 drawn observed subject-level data, publicly released. Body weight fixed 70 kg cohort dose calculation. Frey 2013 doses tocilizumab mg/kg, PK backbone file fixed Frey 2010 typical-patient PK reference; therefore use uniform 70 kg body weight convert mg/kg mg IV-infusion event records. Patient-level body-weight scaling PK available via standalone Frey_2010_tocilizumab library model.

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Citation: Gandhi V, Sun H, Subramanian K, Lon HK, Roy . Model-Based Selection Recommendation Subcutaneous Abatacept Dose Patients Polyarticular Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis. J Clin Pharmacol. 2021 May;61(5):651-661. doi:10.1002/jcph.1781 Description: Two-compartment population PK model abatacept (CTLA4-Ig Fc-fusion) pooled across adults rheumatoid arthritis patients aged 2-17 years polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis (Gandhi 2021), first-order SC absorption, zero-order IV infusion support, first-order linear elimination, logit-scale SC bioavailability disease/age/weight covariates, KA parameterisation enforces KA > k_el. Article: J Clin Pharmacol. 2021;61(5):651-661 (open access via PMC8359474)

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Gandhi 2021 pooled 13 phase 2/3 abatacept clinical studies final population PK dataset 2 616 patients (12 759 abatacept serum concentrations): 2 213 adults rheumatoid arthritis (RA) contributing 9 420 samples, 403 patients aged 2-17 years polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis (pJIA) contributing 3 339 samples. Concentrations quantified validated ELISA (LLOQ 1.0 ng/mL); 8.7% samples LLOQ excluded. Gandhi 2021 used pooled population PK model select weight-tiered weekly subcutaneous (SC) abatacept regimen pJIA: 50 mg patients < 25 kg, 87.5 mg 25 < 50 kg, 125 mg >= 50 kg, designed deliver steady-state trough concentration (Cminss) >= 10 µg/mL > 90 % patients across 2-17 year age range. selected regimen subsequently confirmed phase 3 pJIA trial (130 131 evaluable patients met Cminss target). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Gandhi_2021_abatacept")$population.

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Gandhi 2021 Table 2 (final population PK model fitted expanded RA + pJIA dataset). Reference covariate values come Figure 1 caption: 49-year-old reference patient, baseline body weight 68 kg, albumin 4.1 g/dL, calculated GFR 99.18 mL/min/1.73 m², swollen joint count 15, NSAIDs. Male reference category sex per Methods text “sex, male used reference”; reference patient’s female designation Figure 1 visualization baseline used covariate-effect forest plot.

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Time-unit conversion. Gandhi 2021 reports CL Q L/h KA 1/h (published Table 2 row header “KA (L/h)” units typo rate constant). nlmixr2lib convention time days, values multiplied 24 inside log(...) ini(). Logit-F parameterisation. Gandhi 2021 constrains absolute bioavailability (0, 1) via inverse-logit link (Methods Eq. S1): F_abs = 1 / (1 + exp(-F_TV)) F_TV = logitfdepot + etalogitfdepot + DIS_PJIA * e_jia_f + e_wt_f * log(WT/68) + e_age_f * log(AGE/49). reference value logitfdepot = 1.20831 gives F_abs ≈ 0.770 adult RA reference patient (68 kg, 49 yr). typical pJIA patient +3.08 disease shift drives logit-F ≈ 4.288, .e. F_abs ≈ 0.987. KA > kel constraint. Gandhi 2021 Methods Eq. S2 reparameterises absorption KA_i = KA_TV * exp(etaKA) + k_el,k_el,= CL_i / VC_i, prevent flip-flop parameter estimates fitting. model file implements verbatim. typical-value parameters (KA_TV * 24 = 0.12504 1/day, k_el = 0.4296 / 3.29 ≈ 0.131 1/day), effective absorption rate constant ≈ 0.256 1/day (half-life ≈ 2.7 days), giving SC Tmax near 5 days matches abatacept SC label values. Independent IIVs. paper report full IIV variance-covariance block (unlike Li 2019, ). IIVs treated independent , matching Table 2 layout ETA carries variance shrinkage value. Residual error reported variance. Gandhi 2021 Table 2 reports SIGMA matrix variances “Estimate” column IIV variances (see Errata rationale). proportional additive variances converted standard deviations sqrt() nlmixr2’s add() / prop() conventions: propSd = sqrt(0.0615) ≈ 0.248 addSd = sqrt(0.00134) ≈ 0.0366 mg/L.

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Gandhi 2021 study populations (Table S1 listing 13 phase 2/3 contributing studies embedded PMC full text). Subject-level observed data released paper. Three regimens simulated: Adult RA, 125 mg SC QW — approved adult SC regimen (DIS_PJIA = 0). pJIA weight-tiered SC QW — 50 mg < 25 kg, 87.5 mg 25 < 50 kg, 125 mg >= 50 kg (Gandhi 2021 Tables; DIS_PJIA = 1). Adult RA weight-tiered ~10 mg/kg IV Q4W — 500 mg < 60 kg, 750 mg 60-100 kg, 1000 mg > 100 kg (approved IV dosing recommendation).
code: set.seed(20260425)  # Adult RA cohort n_ra <- 300 ra <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq_len(n_ra),   WT              = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 70, sd = 18),  40, 160)),   AGE             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 49, sd = 13),  18,  90)),   ALB             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 4.1, sd = 0.35), 2.5, 5.0)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 99,  sd = 25), 30, 180)),   SWOL_28JOINT    = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 15, sd = 6)), 0, 28)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_ra, 1, 0.78),       # RA cohorts ~75-80% female   CONMED_NSAID    = rbinom(n_ra, 1, 0.55),   DIS_PJIA        = 0L,   cohort          = "RA" )  # Pediatric pJIA cohort spanning 2-17 years and the 3 weight-tier groups. n_pjia <- 200 pjia <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq.int(from = n_ra + 1, length.out = n_pjia),   AGE             = runif(n_pjia, 2, 17),   WT              = pmax(pmin(8 + (AGE - 2) * 4 + rnorm(n_pjia, 0, 6), 100), 8),   ALB             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_pjia, mean = 4.1, sd = 0.4), 2.5, 5.0)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_pjia, mean = 110, sd = 25), 60, 200)),   SWOL_28JOINT    = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_pjia, mean = 6, sd = 4)), 0, 28)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_pjia, 1, 0.70),     # pJIA cohorts skew female   CONMED_NSAID    = rbinom(n_pjia, 1, 0.40),   DIS_PJIA        = 1L,   cohort          = "pJIA" )  pjia <- pjia |>   dplyr::mutate(     pjia_dose = dplyr::case_when(       WT <  25 ~ 50,       WT <  50 ~ 87.5,       TRUE     ~ 125     )   ) tau_sc <- 7    # SC QW tau_iv <- 28   # IV Q4W n_sc   <- 26   # 26 weekly doses -> 182 days, deeply into SS n_iv   <- 7    # 7 q4w doses -> 196 days  dose_days_sc <- seq(0, tau_sc * (n_sc - 1), by = tau_sc) dose_days_iv <- seq(0, tau_iv * (n_iv - 1), by = tau_iv)  ra_iv <- ra |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt_iv = dplyr::case_when(       WT <  60  ~ 500,       WT <= 100 ~ 750,       TRUE      ~ 1000     )   )  build_sc_events <- function(cohort_df, dose_days, treatment, dose_col = NULL,                             fixed_amt = NULL) {   ev_dose <- cohort_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(       amt = if (!is.null(fixed_amt)) fixed_amt else .data[[dose_col]],       cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment     )   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau_sc, by = 1),                             dose_days + 1, dose_days + 3)))   ev_obs <- cohort_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  build_iv_events <- function(cohort_iv, dose_days, treatment) {   ev_dose <- cohort_iv |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_iv, cmt = "central", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment)   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau_iv, by = 1),                             dose_days + 1, dose_days + 7)))   ev_obs <- cohort_iv |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  events_ra_sc   <- build_sc_events(ra,   dose_days_sc, "RA_SC_125_QW",                                   fixed_amt = 125) events_pjia_sc <- build_sc_events(pjia, dose_days_sc, "pJIA_SC_weight_tiered",                                   dose_col = "pjia_dose") events_ra_iv   <- build_iv_events(ra_iv, dose_days_iv, "RA_IV_weight_tiered_Q4W")

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Gandhi_2021_abatacept")) keep_cols <- c("WT", "AGE", "ALB", "CRCL", "SWOL_28JOINT",                "SEXF", "CONMED_NSAID", "DIS_PJIA", "cohort", "treatment")  sim_ra_sc   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ra_sc,                                              keep = keep_cols)) sim_pjia_sc <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_pjia_sc,                                              keep = keep_cols)) sim_ra_iv   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ra_iv,                                              keep = keep_cols)) sim         <- dplyr::bind_rows(sim_ra_sc, sim_pjia_sc, sim_ra_iv)

what: SC absorption Tmax in a typical adult RA patient
title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Gandhi 2021 Methods Eq. S2 reparameterises KA ensure KA > kel. effective absorption rate constant typical adult RA patient KA_TV + k_el = 0.00521 * 24 + 0.0179 * 24 / 3.29 ≈ 0.256 1/day, giving SC absorption half-life ≈ 2.7 days single-dose Tmax near 5 days — consistent abatacept SC label.
code: mod_typ <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() typ_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 68, AGE = 49, ALB = 4.1, CRCL = 99.18,   SWOL_28JOINT = 15, SEXF = 0L, CONMED_NSAID = 0L, DIS_PJIA = 0L ) ev_single_sc <- typ_cov |>   tidyr::crossing(time = c(0, seq(0.25, 28, by = 0.25))) |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt  = ifelse(time == 0, 125, 0),     cmt  = ifelse(time == 0, "depot", NA_character_),     evid = ifelse(time == 0, 1L, 0L)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) sim_single_sc <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = ev_single_sc)) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalvc', 'etalcl', 'etalvp', 'etalogitfdepot'  tmax_day  <- sim_single_sc$time[which.max(sim_single_sc$Cc)] cmax_mgL  <- max(sim_single_sc$Cc)  ggplot(sim_single_sc, aes(time, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = tmax_day, linetype = "dashed", colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time after single SC dose (days)",     y = "Abatacept Cc (mg/L = µg/mL)",     title = sprintf("Typical adult RA single 125 mg SC: Tmax = %.1f days, Cmax = %.2f mg/L",                     tmax_day, cmax_mgL)   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: pJIA Cminss by age category (replicates Figure 3)
title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Gandhi 2021 Figure 3 plots predicted distribution steady-state trough concentration (Cminss) following SC IV abatacept pJIA patients, stratified age category (2-<6, 6-<12, 12-17 years). paper states SC Cminss pJIA approximately 3-fold higher IV Cminss, Cminss exceeds 10 µg/mL near-maximal efficacy target.
code: ss_start_sc <- tau_sc * (n_sc - 1) ss_end_sc   <- ss_start_sc + tau_sc  trough_pjia <- sim_pjia_sc |>   dplyr::filter(time == ss_start_sc + tau_sc) |>   dplyr::mutate(age_grp = cut(AGE, breaks = c(2, 6, 12, 17.001),                               right = FALSE,                               labels = c("2 to <6", "6 to <12", "12-17"),                               include.lowest = TRUE))  ggplot(trough_pjia, aes(age_grp, Cc)) +   geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.6) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 10, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Age category (years)",     y = "Steady-state trough Cc (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Replicates Gandhi 2021 Figure 3 (pJIA SC weight-tiered Cminss)",     caption = "Red dashed line = 10 µg/mL near-maximal-efficacy target."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: SC vs IV concentration-time profiles in adult RA
title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
code: vpc_ra <- dplyr::bind_rows(   sim_ra_sc |> dplyr::mutate(regimen = "SC 125 mg QW"),   sim_ra_iv |> dplyr::mutate(regimen = "IV weight-tiered Q4W") ) |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0, time <= 84) |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc_ra, aes(time, Q50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Abatacept Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profiles in adult RA: SC vs IV",     caption = "Virtual RA cohort (N = 300); first 12 weeks."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Non-compartmental analysis steady-state SC IV dosing intervals. PKNCA computes per-subject Cmax, Cmin, Cavg, AUCtau. Two PKNCA blocks run (one per regimen-cohort grouping) formula carries id/treatment per skill’s PKNCA recipe.
code: nca_conc_pjia <- sim_pjia_sc |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start_sc, time <= ss_end_sc, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start_sc) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_pjia <- pjia |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = pjia_dose, treatment = "pJIA_SC_weight_tiered") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_pjia <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_pjia, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_pjia <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_pjia, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_sc <- data.frame(start = 0, end = tau_sc,                            cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                            auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE) nca_pjia <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_pjia, dose_obj_pjia,                                            intervals = intervals_sc)) summary(nca_pjia) #>  start end             treatment   N    auclast        cmax        cmin #>      0   7 pJIA_SC_weight_tiered 200 250 [33.4] 40.8 [29.1] 29.0 [43.2] #>               tmax         cav #>  2.00 [1.00, 3.00] 35.7 [33.4] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects nca_conc_ra <- sim_ra_sc |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start_sc, time <= ss_end_sc, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start_sc) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_ra <- ra |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 125, treatment = "RA_SC_125_QW") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_ra <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_ra, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_ra <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_ra, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  nca_ra <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_ra, dose_obj_ra,                                          intervals = intervals_sc)) summary(nca_ra) #>  start end    treatment   N     auclast        cmax        cmin #>      0   7 RA_SC_125_QW 300 95.9 [35.4] 15.4 [31.7] 11.1 [45.5] #>               tmax         cav #>  2.00 [1.00, 3.00] 13.7 [35.4] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against the Gandhi 2021 Cminss >= 10 µg/mL benchmark
title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Gandhi 2021 reports selected weight-tiered subcutaneous regimen delivered Cminss >= 10 µg/mL 130 131 evaluable pJIA patients across 2-17 year age range — .e. > 99 %. cohort-fraction check direct numerical replicate claim virtual pJIA cohort. Fraction virtual pJIA subjects achieving steady-state Cmin >= 10 mg/L weight-tiered SC regimen (Gandhi 2021: 130/131 = 99.2%). Fraction virtual adult RA subjects achieving steady-state Cmin >= 10 mg/L 125 mg SC QW (Gandhi 2021: comparable IV ~10 mg/kg Q4W).
code: pct_above_pjia <- trough_pjia |>   dplyr::group_by(age_grp) |>   dplyr::summarise(     N             = dplyr::n(),     pct_ge_10_mgL = 100 * mean(Cc >= 10, na.rm = TRUE),     median_Cmin   = median(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(pct_above_pjia, digits = 1,   caption = "Fraction of virtual pJIA subjects achieving steady-state Cmin >= 10 mg/L on the weight-tiered SC regimen (Gandhi 2021: 130/131 = 99.2%).") trough_ra <- sim_ra_sc |>   dplyr::filter(time == ss_start_sc + tau_sc) |>   dplyr::mutate(wt_grp = cut(WT, breaks = c(-Inf, 60, 100, Inf),                              labels = c("<60 kg", "60-100 kg", ">100 kg")))  pct_above_ra <- trough_ra |>   dplyr::group_by(wt_grp) |>   dplyr::summarise(     N             = dplyr::n(),     pct_ge_10_mgL = 100 * mean(Cc >= 10, na.rm = TRUE),     median_Cmin   = median(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(pct_above_ra, digits = 1,   caption = "Fraction of virtual adult RA subjects achieving steady-state Cmin >= 10 mg/L on 125 mg SC QW (Gandhi 2021: comparable to IV ~10 mg/kg Q4W).")

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: Time-unit rescaling. Gandhi 2021 reports CL, Q, KA per-hour units; model file converts per-day factor 24 match nlmixr2lib units$time = "day" convention. VC, VP, F, IIV variances, residual-error magnitudes carry unchanged. KA “L/h” units typo. Gandhi 2021 Table 2 lists KA’s units “L/h”, KA first-order absorption rate constant units 1/time. model file treats KA 1/h (converts 1/day) consistent rest Methods (Eq. S2 expresses KA sum rate constants). SJC 28-joint scale assumed. Gandhi 2021 reports swollen joint count covariate (SJC) reference value 15 explicitly identify joint-count scale. Per operator decision, canonical SWOL_28JOINT used (1) author group used 28-joint count prior Li 2019 RA-analysis, (2) reference value 15 consistent 28-joint scale’s 0-28 range, (3) pediatric pJIA patients minority pooled cohort (403/2616 ≈ 15 %). Sex covariate direction. Gandhi 2021 Methods state “sex, male used reference”; model codes SEXF = 1 female applies published Table 2 coefficient +0.0674 exp(SEXF * 0.0674), females ≈ 7 % higher CL males. sign opposite Li 2019 (-0.0722 SEX RA-cohort); dataset expansion pJIA Gandhi 2021 produced sign flip. Convention follows existing Li 2019 implementation nlmixr2lib. Independent IIVs (full block). Gandhi 2021 Table 2 reports ETA’s variance shrinkage independently, variance-covariance block reported paper text. model treats IIVs independent. Li 2019 (group’s prior model) report 4×4 full block etalcl + etalvc + etalq + etalvp, users want closer match Li 2019 covariance structure can override IIV block downstream. pJIA--CL retained. Gandhi 2021 explicitly states “Disease (pJIA vs RA) clinically relevant effect abatacept CL” final-model Table 2 include JIA effect CL. pJIA covariate enters bioavailability submodel . Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Table S1 (per-study demographic summary) referenced paper embedded PMC full text, virtual-cohort distributions approximate. Adult RA: WT ~ N(70, 18), AGE ~ N(49, 13), ALB ~ N(4.1, 0.35), CRCL ~ N(99, 25), SWOL_28JOINT ~ rounded N(15, 6), SEXF = 78 % female, CONMED_NSAID = 55 %. pJIA: AGE uniform [2, 17], WT linearly scaled age plus noise (8 + 4·(AGE-2) ± 6 kg), SEXF = 70 % female. reference covariate values match Gandhi 2021 Figure 1 caption; dispersions approximate typical phase 3 RA / pJIA cohort. IV instantaneous bolus. Gandhi 2021 administers IV abatacept 30-minute infusion. simulation treats IV doses instantaneous bolus central, slightly overstates early post-dose Cmax effect AUC, trough, half-life estimates. Users wanting exact infusion kinetics set rate (dur) rxode2 event table. subject-level observed data. Gandhi 2021 release individual subject data; figures reported pure forward simulation published final-model parameters virtual cohort.

title: Gandhi_2021_abatacept
text: published Gandhi 2021 paper contains one ambiguity reviewer re-reading source alongside model aware : Residual error scale (Table 2). Gandhi 2021 Table 2 reports proportional additive residual errors (0.0615 0.00134) “Estimate” column carries IIV variances ETA. IIV column entries unambiguously variances (var(etalka) = 1.11 large SD), symmetry residual-error entries likely SIGMA (variance) values . Per operator decision, model treats residual errors variances converts SDs via sqrt(): propSd = sqrt(0.0615) ≈ 0.248 (24.8 % proportional error) addSd = sqrt(0.00134) ≈ 0.0366 mg/L (≈ 37 ng/mL additive). alternative interpretation — SDs directly, matching Li 2019 nlmixr2lib convention author group — give propSd = 0.0615 (6.15 %) addSd = 0.00134 mg/L (1.34 ng/mL), unusually tight biologic plausible given Gandhi 2021’s larger richly-covariated dataset. paper explicitly label residual-error column variance vs SD; without supplement (control stream) disk disambiguated beyond column-symmetry argument used . future reader obtains supplement residual-error parameterisation conflicts interpretation, propSd / addSd lines ini() updated. formal erratum. search PubMed J Clin Pharmacol corrections feed 2026-04-25 returned published corrections.

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Citation: Grimm HP, Schumacher V, Schafer M, et al. Delivery Brainshuttle amyloid-beta antibody fusion trontinemab non-human primate brain projected efficacious dose regimens humans. mAbs. 2023;15(1):2261509. doi:10.1080/19420862.2023.2261509 Description (trontinemab): Trontinemab PK model non-human primates (Grimm 2023): two-compartment plasma PK Michaelis-Menten elimination brain-region effect-compartment distribution (cerebellum, hippocampus, striatum, cortex, choroid plexus, CSF). Description (gantenerumab): Gantenerumab PK model cynomolgus monkeys (Grimm 2023): two-compartment plasma PK brain extracellular distribution across six brain regions (cerebellum, hippocampus, striatum, cortex, choroid plexus, CSF). Article: https://doi.org/10.1080/19420862.2023.2261509

what: Simulate trontinemab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 10 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 10, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5 / 60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Tront <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_trontinemab") conc_unit <- rxode2::rxode(Grimm2023Tront)$units[["concentration"]] #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimTront <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Tront |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimTront$Analyte <- "Trontinemab"

what: Simulate gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 20 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 20, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5 / 60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Gant <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_gantenerumab") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimGant <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Gant |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimGant$Analyte <- "Gantenerumab"

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 2 paper.
code: dSim <- bind_rows(dSimTront, dSimGant) dSim$Analyte <- factor(dSim$Analyte, levels = c("Trontinemab", "Gantenerumab"))  ggplot(dSim, aes(x = time, y = sim)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = paste0("Concentration (", conc_unit, ")")   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 336, by = 48)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1e3, NA)) +   facet_grid(~Analyte)

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 3 paper.
code: d_plot_brain <-   dSim |>   select(time, Analyte, starts_with("C", ignore.case = FALSE)) |>   select(-starts_with("Cbrain"), -Cc) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -c("time", "Analyte"), names_to = "ASPEC", values_to = "AVAL") |>   mutate(     ASPEC =       factor(         gsub(x = ASPEC, pattern = "^C", replacement = ""),         levels = c("hippocampus", "cerebellum", "choroid_plexus", "cortex", "striatum", "csf"),         labels = c("Hippocampus", "Cerebellum", "Choroid Plexus", "Cortex", "Striatum", "Cerebrospinal Fluid")       )   )  ggplot(d_plot_brain, aes(x = time, y = AVAL, colour = Analyte)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Concentration (ng/g)"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 72, 168, 240, 336)) +   facet_wrap(~ASPEC)

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta et al. (2016) characterised amatuximab (MORAb-009) pharmacokinetics 199 patients across four clinical studies (two Phase dose-escalation studies advanced mesothelin-expressing tumours, Phase II pancreatic cancer study, Phase II malignant pleural mesothelioma [MPM] study). final PK model two-compartment structure parallel linear Michaelis-Menten (saturable) elimination central compartment. Covariate analysis retained body weight central volume (power effect, reference 70 kg) antidrug-antibody (ADA) positivity titer threshold > 64 linear clearance (multiplicative effect 1.49). vignette documents parameter provenance source-trace table, builds virtual cohort matching Gupta 2016 Table 1, simulates 5 mg/kg weekly (without interruption) regimen evaluated paper’s dose selection analysis, validates simulated NCA observed Study 003 median Cmin published PK characteristics. Citation: Gupta , Hussein Z, Hassan R, Wustner J, Maltzman JD, Wallin BA. Population pharmacokinetics exposure-response relationship amatuximab, anti-mesothelin monoclonal antibody, patients malignant pleural mesothelioma application dose selection. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2016;77(4):733-743. Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s00280-016-2984-z

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta 2016 Table 1 (pharmacokinetic-analysis database, N = 199): information available programmatically via readModelDb("Gupta_2016_amatuximab")$population.

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Every numeric value model file inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Gupta_2016_amatuximab.R comes Gupta et al. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol 2016;77(4):733-743 (doi:10.1007/s00280-016-2984-z). CV% values converted log-normal variances via ω2=log⁡(CV2+1)\omega^2 = \log(CV^2 + 1), CL-VcV_c covariance cov=r⋅ωCL2⋅ωVc2=0.411⋅0.056443=0.023198\mathrm{cov} = r \cdot \sqrt{\omega^2_{CL}\cdot\omega^2_{V_c}} = 0.411 \cdot 0.056443 = 0.023198.

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: N = 200 virtual adult cancer patients. Body weight drawn truncated normal distribution matching published mean (75.1 kg) SD (16.4 kg), clipped observed range (35-134 kg). ADA positivity (>64 titer threshold) drawn Bernoulli(0.10) plausible approximation; Gupta 2016 reports ADA reactions hypersensitivity events main driver dose discontinuation publish exact ADA-prevalence distribution PK dataset.
code: set.seed(2016) n_subj <- 200  # Body weight — truncated normal matching Gupta 2016 Table 1 (mean 75.1 kg, # SD 16.4, range 35-134). wt <- rnorm(n_subj, mean = 75.1, sd = 16.4) wt <- pmin(pmax(wt, 35), 134)  # ADA titer > 64 indicator, approximate 10% positivity for illustration. ada_pos <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.10)  pop <- tibble(   ID      = seq_len(n_subj),   WT      = wt,   ADA_POS = ada_pos )  summary(pop$WT) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   35.00   63.82   74.91   75.45   86.55  123.03 mean(pop$ADA_POS) #> [1] 0.115

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: paper’s dose-selection simulation (Results “Simulations dose evaluation”, p. 739) administered amatuximab 5 mg/kg weekly 1-h constant-rate IV infusion six 21-day cycles (interruption), giving 18 doses ~126 days. reproduce schedule, sampling pre-dose trough, end--infusion, 24 h, 72 h, mid-interval NCA.
code: week_h <- 7 * 24 n_cycles <- 6 cycle_h <- 21 * 24 dose_times <- sort(unique(c(   outer((seq_len(n_cycles) - 1) * cycle_h,         c(0, 7 * 24, 14 * 24),         `+`) ))) tmax_h <- max(dose_times) + 14 * 24  # follow-up through 2 wk post last dose  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 1),   dose_times + 1, dose_times + 24, dose_times + 72,   dose_times + 168,   seq(0, tmax_h, by = 24) )))  # Build dosing records: 5 mg/kg as 1-h infusion (mg dose = 5 * WT) d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 5 * WT,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     RATE = AMT / 1,   # 1-h infusion     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     RATE = 0,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, RATE, DV, WT, ADA_POS)  stopifnot(sum(d_sim$EVID == 1) == n_subj * length(dose_times))

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Stochastic simulation full IIV (VPC-style plots NCA) typical-value reference pass via rxode2::zeroRe().
code: mod <- readModelDb("Gupta_2016_amatuximab")  set.seed(20160222) sim_full <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvmax' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta 2016 Figure 2 shows observed-vs-predicted concentration-time profile pooled cohort. panel shows first-dose profile (0-168 h) virtual cohort overlaid typical-value trajectory.
code: first_dose <- sim_full |> filter(time <= 168, time > 0) first_typ  <- sim_typ  |> filter(time <= 168, time > 0, id == 1)  ggplot() +   geom_line(     data = first_dose, aes(time, Cc, group = id),     colour = "grey70", alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.25   ) +   geom_line(     data = first_typ, aes(time, Cc), colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 1   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time after first dose (h)",     y       = expression(Amatuximab~concentration~(mu*g/mL)),     title   = "Single 5 mg/kg IV 1-h infusion - virtual cohort vs typical patient",     caption = "Gupta 2016 Figure 2-equivalent (pre-infusion pcVPC, Phase I panel)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta 2016 (Results “Simulations dose evaluation”, p. 739) simulated weekly 5 mg/kg regimen without interruption. paper reports without ADA, simulated median steady-state CminC_\min 83.1 µg/mL, approximately 80% patients achieved CminC_\min exposure-response threshold 38.2 µg/mL.
code: trough <- sim_full |>   filter(time %in% dose_times) |>   mutate(ADA_label = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA titer > 64", "ADA-negative"))  trough_summary <- trough |>   group_by(time_day, ADA_label) |>   summarise(     Q05 = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(trough_summary, aes(time_day, Q50, colour = ADA_label, fill = ADA_label)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 38.2, linetype = "dashed", colour = "firebrick") +   annotate("text", x = 10, y = 42,            label = "Exposure-response threshold 38.2 ug/mL",            colour = "firebrick", hjust = 0, size = 3) +   labs(     x       = "Time (days)",     y       = expression(Amatuximab~pre-dose~trough~(mu*g/mL)),     colour  = NULL, fill = NULL,     title   = "Simulated amatuximab trough profile, 5 mg/kg weekly without interruption",     caption = "Gupta 2016 Figure 3-equivalent (dose-selection simulation)"   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: PKNCA validation at the final-week steady state
title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Run NCA final 168-h (weekly) dosing interval 18-dose course compare simulated CmaxC_\max, CminC_\min, AUC0−τAUC_{0-\tau}, terminal half-life published values. PKNCA formula includes treatment grouping variable (ADA status) results can rolled separately ADA-negative ADA titer > 64 subjects, per library’s PKNCA-recipe convention.
code: tau_h   <- week_h start_ss <- max(dose_times) end_ss   <- start_ss + tau_h  sim_nca <- sim_full |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= start_ss - 1, time <= end_ss + 1) |>   transmute(     id        = id,     time      = time,     Cc        = Cc,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA titer > 64", "ADA-negative")   )  dose_nca <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 1) |>   transmute(     id        = ID,     time      = TIME,     amt       = AMT,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA titer > 64", "ADA-negative")   )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_nca, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start       = start_ss,   end         = end_ss,   cmax        = TRUE,   tmax        = TRUE,   cmin        = TRUE,   auclast     = TRUE,   cav         = TRUE,   half.life   = TRUE )  res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           74% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- as.data.frame(res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "cmin", "auclast", "cav", "half.life")) |>   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     median = stats::median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     p05    = stats::quantile(PPORRES, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     p95    = stats::quantile(PPORRES, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) nca_summary #> # A tibble: 12 × 5 #>    treatment      PPTESTCD   median     p05     p95 #>    <chr>          <chr>       <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 ADA titer > 64 auclast    6732.  3575.    9789.  #>  2 ADA titer > 64 cav          40.1   21.3     58.3 #>  3 ADA titer > 64 cmax        102.    67.2    151.  #>  4 ADA titer > 64 cmin         12.9    2.35    24.0 #>  5 ADA titer > 64 half.life    68.6   24.1     91.1 #>  6 ADA titer > 64 tmax          1      1        1   #>  7 ADA-negative   auclast   10346.  5658.   20712.  #>  8 ADA-negative   cav          61.6   33.7    123.  #>  9 ADA-negative   cmax        117.    79.6    208.  #> 10 ADA-negative   cmin         32.1    7.95    81.8 #> 11 ADA-negative   half.life   108.    54.4    168.  #> 12 ADA-negative   tmax          1      1        1

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Interpretation: model reproduces directionality ADA effect magnitude (simulated CminC_\min ratio ADA-positive : ADA-negative closely matches paper’s reported ratio ~26.9 / 83.1 = 0.32, consistent 1/1.49 fold clearance increase). absolute simulated CminC_\min values systematically lower paper’s published stochastic simulation values — see “Assumptions deviations” likely cause (published Manly eta transformation VmaxV_\max re-implemented , paper’s stochastic median may influenced heavy upper tail log-normal VmaxV_\max distribution ω=1.24\omega = 1.24).
code: sim_cmin_ss <- sim_full |>   filter(time == start_ss) |>   group_by(ADA_POS) |>   summarise(median_cmin = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  sim_cmin_negative <- sim_cmin_ss |> filter(ADA_POS == 0) |> pull(median_cmin) sim_cmin_positive <- sim_cmin_ss |> filter(ADA_POS == 1) |> pull(median_cmin)  comparison <- tibble::tribble(   ~metric,                                                ~published, ~simulated,       ~units,   "SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA-negative (paper simulation)", 83.1,       sim_cmin_negative, "ug/mL",   "SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA titer > 64 (paper simulation)", 26.9,    sim_cmin_positive, "ug/mL",   "Observed median Cmin, Study 003 MPM (5 mg/kg D1+D8 of 21-day cycle)", 38.2, NA_real_, "ug/mL",   "Exposure-response threshold for OS",                    38.2,      NA_real_,           "ug/mL" ) comparison #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   metric                                               published simulated units #>   <chr>                                                    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> #> 1 SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA-negative (paper simulation)      83.1      32.1 ug/mL #> 2 SS Cmin, 5 mg/kg QW, ADA titer > 64 (paper simulati…      26.9      12.9 ug/mL #> 3 Observed median Cmin, Study 003 MPM (5 mg/kg D1+D8 …      38.2      NA   ug/mL #> 4 Exposure-response threshold for OS                        38.2      NA   ug/mL

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Manly transformation ηVmax\eta_{V_\max}. Gupta 2016 applied Manly transformation shape factor λ=−0.181\lambda = -0.181 (95% CI -0.450 0.0875, .e., statistically distinguishable zero / log-normal) eta VmaxV_\max. vignette uses standard log-normal form nlmixr2 without Manly reshaping. Since λ\lambda’s CI includes zero, first-order-equivalent approximation; simulated marginal VmaxV_\max distribution differs upper tail. paper’s Table 2 CV 124% treated log-normal CV converted via ω2=log⁡(CV2+1)\omega^2 = \log(CV^2 + 1). Absolute CminC_\min offset vs paper’s dose-evaluation simulation. paper’s dose-evaluation sim reports median weekly-regimen CminC_\min 83.1 µg/mL (ADA) 26.9 µg/mL (ADA >64). vignette’s typical-value simulation lower (around 30 µg/mL -ADA), stochastic medians virtual cohort track typical value closely. Possible contributors: paper’s Manly transformation (produces heavier-tailed VmaxV_\max distribution pure log-normal) can raise median predicted CminC_\min due right-tail mass low-VmaxV_\max subjects; paper report residual baseline demographic distribution (weight, ADA prevalence) used inside 199-patient dose-evaluation stochastic cohort, vignette supplies plausible defaults. observed median CminC_\min MPM Study 003 (38.2 µg/mL, intermittent D1+D8 21-day cycle dosing) closer magnitude simulation . ADA prevalence. exact ADA-positivity prevalence inside 199-patient PK cohort reported; vignette assumes 10% prevalence illustration. Replace Bernoulli probability virtual-pop exact prevalence applying specific population. Intermittent vs continuous weekly dosing. paper distinguishes observed data (D1 + D8 21-day cycle MPM patients) simulated weekly-without-interruption regimen used dose-selection analysis. vignette implements continuous weekly regimen (match paper’s Figure 3 dose-evaluation simulation). Race, age, ECOG, albumin, baseline mesothelin. Gupta 2016 tested covariates retain final PK model (Results, “Effect covariates”). simulated . pcVPC replication (Gupta 2016 Figure 1). reproduced exactly published figure uses observed (irregular) sampling design across four studies prediction-correction individual-covariate-predicted concentration. Figure 1-equivalent panel shows analogous single-dose PK shape axes.

title: Gupta_2016_amatuximab
text: Gupta , Hussein Z, Hassan R, Wustner J, Maltzman JD, Wallin BA. Population pharmacokinetics exposure-response relationship amatuximab, anti-mesothelin monoclonal antibody, patients malignant pleural mesothelioma application dose selection. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2016;77(4):733-743. doi:10.1007/s00280-016-2984-z

title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Citation: Hanzel J, Bukkems LH, Gecse KB, D’Haens GR, Mathot RAA. Population pharmacokinetics subcutaneous infliximab CT-P13 Crohn’s disease ulcerative colitis. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2021;54(10):1309-1319. Article: doi:10.1111/apt.16609 Open access full text: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/apt.16609 CT-P13 infliximab biosimilar (chimeric anti-TNF-alpha IgG1 monoclonal antibody). Hanzel et al. characterised pharmacokinetics subcutaneous formulation jointly modelling intravenous (5 mg/kg) induction variable-dose subcutaneous maintenance phases CT-P13 1.6 phase study (NCT02883452) 175 adults active Crohn’s disease ulcerative colitis. final model two-compartment structure first-order subcutaneous absorption, linear elimination, bioavailability < 1, body weight CL, Vc, Vp, Q; serum albumin CL; antidrug-antibody (ATI) positivity CL.

title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Hanzel 2021 Table 2 summarises 175-subject pooled cohort: Sex: 54% female, 46% male. Age: median 36 years (IQR 29-48, range 18-70). Disease: Crohn’s disease 55% (n = 97), ulcerative colitis 45% (n = 78). Moderate--severe activity baseline. Body weight: median 69 kg (IQR 60-80, range 43-118). Albumin: median 44 g/L (IQR 40-46, range 28-54). Note SI units (g/L), distinct g/dL convention used infliximab popPK papers (e.g., Fasanmade 2009). C-reactive protein: median 3.2 mg/L (IQR 1.2-7.9, range 0.2-89.4). Concomitant immunomodulators: 84% pre-treatment, 46% treatment. Concomitant corticosteroids: 61% pre-treatment, 41% treatment. Treatment-emergent antibodies: 33% developed neutralising antibodies treatment. Per-cohort design (Hanzel 2021 Methods, “Clinical study design patients”): Weight-based SC dose: 120 mg <= 80 kg, 240 mg > 80 kg. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Hanzel_2021_infliximab")$population.

title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: per-parameter origin recorded next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hanzel_2021_infliximab.R. table collects one place review.

title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Original per-subject data publicly available. virtual population constructed match paper’s Monte Carlo simulation setup (Methods, “Monte Carlo simulations”): three weight strata (50, 70, 120 kg) 1000 patients , albumin sampled distribution roughly matching Table 2 (median 44 g/L, IQR 40-46, range 28-54), subjects modelled ADA-negative.
code: set.seed(2021) n_per_stratum <- 200L  # smaller than the paper's 1000 for vignette runtime  make_pop <- function(weight, n, id_offset) {   tibble(     ID      = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT      = weight,     ALB     = pmax(28, pmin(rnorm(n, 44, 4), 54)),  # match Table 2 distribution     ADA_POS = 0L,     weight_kg = paste0(weight, " kg")   ) }  pop <- bind_rows(   make_pop(50,  n_per_stratum, id_offset =     0L),   make_pop(70,  n_per_stratum, id_offset =  1000L),   make_pop(120, n_per_stratum, id_offset =  2000L) )

what: Replicating Figure 3: IV induction + SC maintenance
title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Hanzel 2021 Figure 3 simulates IV 5 mg/kg days 0 14 followed subcutaneous 120 mg every 2 weeks starting day 42, 50, 70, 120 kg virtual patients. reproduce regimen.  simulated profiles reproduce qualitative pattern Figure 3: three weight strata reach 5-7 mg/L trough plateau ~12 weeks, heavier patients sitting lighter ones steady state per-kg subcutaneous maintenance dose lower heavier patients fixed 120 mg regimen.
code: maintenance_days <- seq(42, 42 + 14 * 26, by = 14)   # ~52 weeks of q2w SC dosing  dose_iv <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = c(0, 14)) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 5 * WT,           # 5 mg/kg     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, ADA_POS, weight_kg)  dose_sc <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = maintenance_days) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 120,              # 120 mg SC fixed dose     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, ADA_POS, weight_kg)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 14, by = 1),   seq(14, 42, by = 1),   seq(42, max(maintenance_days) + 14, by = 1) )))  obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, ADA_POS, weight_kg)  events_f3 <- bind_rows(dose_iv, dose_sc, obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID))  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(events_f3[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])) mod <- readModelDb("Hanzel_2021_infliximab") sim_f3 <- rxSolve(mod, events_f3, keep = "weight_kg", returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_f3 %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time, weight_kg) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = time, color = weight_kg, fill = weight_kg)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, linetype = 0) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(3, 7), linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +   scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, 200)) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "CT-P13 concentration (mg/L)",     color = "Body weight",     fill = "Body weight",     title = "Replicates Hanzel 2021 Figure 3",     subtitle = "5 mg/kg IV at days 0 and 14, then 120 mg SC q2w from day 42",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval; dotted lines at 3 and 7 mg/L."   ) +   theme_bw() #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

what: Replicating Figure 2 (VPC) for the IV 5 mg/kg arm
title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Figure 2 Hanzel 2021 shows prediction-corrected VPC route / dose. reproduce IV 5 mg/kg arm . Subjects receive IV doses days 0, 14, 42 (week 6), 98 (week 14), 154 (week 22), part-2 IV arm trial.
code: n_vpc <- 300L  set.seed(202101) pop_iv <- tibble(   ID      = 5000L + seq_len(n_vpc),   WT      = pmax(43, pmin(rlnorm(n_vpc, log(69), 0.22), 118)),   ALB     = pmax(28, pmin(rnorm(n_vpc, 44, 4), 54)),   ADA_POS = rbinom(n_vpc, 1, 0.10),   weight_kg = "VPC" )  iv_days <- c(0, 14, 42, 98, 154)  dose_vpc <- pop_iv %>%   crossing(TIME = iv_days) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 5 * WT,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, ADA_POS)  obs_vpc_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,   14, by = 0.5),   seq(14,  42, by = 1),   seq(42, 220, by = 2) )))  obs_vpc <- pop_iv %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_vpc_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, ADA_POS)  events_vpc <- bind_rows(dose_vpc, obs_vpc) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID))  sim_vpc <- rxSolve(mod, events_vpc, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_vpc %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = time)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.20, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 0.8, colour = "steelblue") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "CT-P13 concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated PI for IV 5 mg/kg arm of CT-P13 1.6 part 2",     subtitle = "5 mg/kg IV on days 0, 14, 42, 98, 154; replicates Hanzel 2021 Fig. 2 (upper-left panel) qualitatively",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Run PKNCA Cmax, Tmax, AUClast, half-life IV induction phase (single 5 mg/kg dose, day 0 day 14) Figure 3 cohort. Stratification weight_kg treatment grouping per-weight results can compared. PKNCA summary 5 mg/kg IV induction (day 0 day 14), weight stratum.
code: single_dose_window <- 14 sim_nca <- sim_f3 %>%   filter(time >= 0, time <= single_dose_window, Cc > 0) %>%   transmute(ID = id, time, Cc, treatment = weight_kg)  dose_nca <- pop %>%   transmute(ID, time = 0, amt = 5 * WT, treatment = weight_kg)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(sim_nca,  Cc  ~ time | treatment + ID,                       concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(dose_nca, amt ~ time | treatment + ID,                       doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = single_dose_window,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE ) nca_data    <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_results <- pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: Too few points for half-life calculation what: Comparison against the published typical-value half-life
title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Hanzel 2021 Abstract: “half-life typical 70 kg patient serum albumin 44 g/L 10.8 days.” typical-value terminal half-life model parameters CL, Vc, Vp, Q. compute analytically packaged model: Typical-value terminal half-life (days). analytic value identical paper’s reported 10.8 days, confirming structural-parameter set transcribed correctly.
code: pars <- list(CL = 0.355, Vc = 3.10, Vp = 1.93, Q = 0.598) k10 <- pars$CL / pars$Vc k12 <- pars$Q  / pars$Vc k21 <- pars$Q  / pars$Vp disc <- (k10 + k12 + k21)^2 - 4 * k10 * k21 lambda_z <- ((k10 + k12 + k21) - sqrt(disc)) / 2  tibble(   source        = c("Hanzel 2021 Abstract (typical 70 kg, ALB 44 g/L)", "Computed from model parameters"),   half_life_d   = c(10.8, log(2) / lambda_z) ) %>%   knitr::kable(     digits  = 2,     caption = "Typical-value terminal half-life (days)."   )

what: Comparison against the published trough-coverage Monte Carlo
title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Hanzel 2021 reports Monte Carlo results steady-state SC trough coverage (Results, “Monte Carlo simulations”): 50 / 70 / 120 kg strata, percentage trough concentrations exceeding 7 mg/L maintenance dosing 96.4% / 90.3% / 71.9%, respectively. reproduce Monte Carlo end--interval troughs Figure 3 simulation (every-2-weeks SC 120 mg phase, week 30 capture near-steady-state behaviour): Trough coverage 120 mg SC q2w maintenance, simulation vs Hanzel 2021. Differences published values driven sample size (vignette uses fewer subjects per stratum paper’s 1000) simplified albumin distribution; qualitative ordering (50 kg > 70 kg > 120 kg) reproduced.
code: trough_days <- seq(42 + 14 * 14, max(maintenance_days), by = 14)  # day 238 onward troughs <- sim_f3 %>%   filter(time %in% trough_days) %>%   group_by(weight_kg) %>%   summarise(     n     = n(),     pct_above_3  = mean(Cc > 3) * 100,     pct_above_5  = mean(Cc > 5) * 100,     pct_above_7  = mean(Cc > 7) * 100,     .groups = "drop"   )  published <- tribble(   ~weight_kg, ~paper_pct_above_3, ~paper_pct_above_5, ~paper_pct_above_7,   "50 kg",   99.9, 99.0, 96.4,   "70 kg",   99.9, 98.3, 90.3,   "120 kg",  99.5, 90.6, 71.9 )  troughs %>%   left_join(published, by = "weight_kg") %>%   knitr::kable(     digits  = 1,     caption = "Trough coverage during 120 mg SC q2w maintenance, simulation vs Hanzel 2021."   )

title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Inter-occasion variability (IOV) CL implemented. Hanzel 2021 Table 3 reports IOV CL 17.5% dosing event defined new occasion. Library models conventionally carry subject-level IIV; IOV term documented dropped simulation. Subject-level variability model therefore somewhat lower published combined IIV+IOV total. ATI treated binary, time-varying. source Methods Table 1 lists ATI time-dependent /covariate; map canonical ADA_POS column document rename covariateData[[ADA_POS]]$source_name. Albumin distribution. Per-subject albumin trajectories publicly available; sample ALB ~ N(44, 4) g/L clipped [28, 54] g/L range reported Table 2. Time-variation modelled. Race / ethnicity reported. source paper provide race / ethnicity demographics, population metadata records “reported.” PKNCA half-life vs typical-value half-life. Per vignette template guidance, PKNCA half-life 14-day IV phase reflects whatever sampling window given; analytic typical-value half-life 10.8 days CL/Vc/Vp/Q model parameters meant reproduce, exactly. -diagonal IIV interpretation. Table 3 reports -diagonal IIV elements column header “Correlation …”; take face value correlation coefficients [-1, 1] convert covariances via cov_ij = corr_ij * sqrt(var_i * var_j) rxode2 block-eta specification.

title: Hanzel_2021_infliximab
text: Hanzel J, Bukkems LH, Gecse KB, D’Haens GR, Mathot RAA. Population pharmacokinetics subcutaneous infliximab CT-P13 Crohn’s disease ulcerative colitis. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2021;54(10):1309-1319. doi:10.1111/apt.16609

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: Omalizumab humanized anti-IgE IgG1 monoclonal antibody used moderate--severe atopic asthma. interrupts allergic cascade binding free serum IgE, lowering concentration, downmodulating FcεRI basophils mast cells. Hayashi et al. (2007) developed mechanism-based population PK/PD model 202 Japanese subjects (single- multiple-dose studies 1101 1305) describes three serum entities — free omalizumab, free IgE, omalizumab–IgE complex — clearance volume distribution, coupled instantaneous-equilibrium binding step (law mass action) concentration-dependent dissociation constant. Body weight modifies omalizumab CL Vd; baseline IgE modifies IgE CL IgE production rate. model externally validated 531 White patients (studies 007/008/009). Citation: Hayashi N, Tsukamoto Y, Sallas WM, Lowe PJ. mechanism-based binding model population pharmacokinetics pharmacodynamics omalizumab. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2007;63(5):548-561. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2125.2006.02803.x (PMID 17096680). Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-2125.2006.02803.x PMID: 17096680

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: model-building dataset comprises 202 Japanese subjects two clinical studies (Hayashi 2007 Table 1): total 3192 observation records (1037 omalizumab, 1191 total IgE, 964 free IgE) included; 275 free-IgE values 150 ng/mL upper limit quantification excluded. metadata available programmatically readModelDb("Hayashi_2007_omalizumab")$population.

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comments next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hayashi_2007_omalizumab.R. table collects review.

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort reproduces four single-dose groups study 1101 (75, 150, 300, 375 mg SC) — design dense sampling schedule (Hayashi 2007 Methods, page 549). Body weight baseline IgE sampled study-1101 distribution (mean 62.5 kg ± 6.4 kg; mean 811 ng/mL ± 473 ng/mL, log-normal).
code: set.seed(20070501L) # paper publication month YYYYMM01  study_1101_doses <- c(75, 150, 300, 375) n_per_dose       <- 12L  make_cohort <- function(n, dose_mg, id_offset = 0L) {   data.frame(     id    = id_offset + seq_len(n),     dose  = dose_mg,     WT    = pmax(45, pmin(80, rnorm(n, mean = 62.5, sd = 6.4))),     IGE   = pmax(200, pmin(2200,               exp(rnorm(n, mean = log(811) - 0.5 * log(1 + (473 / 811)^2),                           sd = sqrt(log(1 + (473 / 811)^2))))))   ) }  cohorts <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_along(study_1101_doses), function(i) {   make_cohort(n_per_dose, study_1101_doses[i],               id_offset = (i - 1L) * n_per_dose) }))  obs_times <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 7, 10, 14, 28, 42, 56, 70, 84) # days, study 1101 schedule  events <- cohorts |>   rowwise() |>   do({     row <- .     et_obj <- rxode2::et(time = 0, amt = row$dose, cmt = "depot") |>       rxode2::et(obs_times, cmt = "Cc")     et_obj$id    <- row$id     et_obj$WT    <- row$WT     et_obj$IGE   <- row$IGE     et_obj$dose  <- row$dose     et_obj   }) |>   ungroup() |>   as.data.frame()  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hayashi_2007_omalizumab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events,                        keep = c("dose", "WT", "IGE"),                        returnType = "data.frame", addDosing = FALSE) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim$dose_label <- factor(paste0(sim$dose, " mg"),                          levels = paste0(study_1101_doses, " mg"))

what: Replicate published Figure 3 — single-dose profiles by dose level
title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: Hayashi 2007 Figure 3 shows individual observed serum concentrations omalizumab, total IgE, free IgE study 1101 84 days, 80% prediction intervals. reproduce cohort means 80% prediction intervals simulated population. Replicates Figure 3 Hayashi 2007 (top row, omalizumab vs time single-dose group, study 1101). Replicates Figure 3 Hayashi 2007 (free IgE vs time single-dose group, study 1101). Replicates Figure 3 Hayashi 2007 (total IgE vs time single-dose group, study 1101).
code: sim |>   group_by(time, dose_label) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(Cc, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(Cc, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ dose_label, scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Total omalizumab (µg/mL)",        title = "Hayashi 2007 Figure 3, omalizumab panel",        caption = "Lines: median; ribbon: 10th–90th percentile from 12 simulated subjects per dose.") sim |>   group_by(time, dose_label) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(freeIgE, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(freeIgE, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(freeIgE, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ dose_label) +   scale_y_log10() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 12, lty = 2, colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 21, lty = 2, colour = "grey40") +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Free IgE (ng/mL, log scale)",        title = "Hayashi 2007 Figure 3, free-IgE panel",        caption = "Dashed grey lines indicate the 12–21 ng/mL clinical-efficacy band cited in Hayashi 2007.") sim |>   group_by(time, dose_label) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(totalIgE, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(totalIgE, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(totalIgE, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ dose_label) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Total IgE (ng/mL)",        title = "Hayashi 2007 Figure 3, total-IgE panel")

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: PKNCA computes Cmax, Tmax, AUC(0,∞), terminal half-life simulated total-omalizumab profile. Hayashi 2007 reports noncompartmental terminal half-life 18.2 days total omalizumab study 1101 (Discussion, page 558) model-derived half-life 23 days free omalizumab. compartmental Cc output total omalizumab (free + complex), NCA t½ expected land two values. Simulated NCA parameters single-dose group (study 1101).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   select(id, time, Cc, dose_label)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(evid == 1) |>   mutate(dose_label = factor(paste0(amt, " mg"),                              levels = paste0(study_1101_doses, " mg"))) |>   select(id, time, amt, dose_label)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | dose_label + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | dose_label + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary, caption = "Simulated NCA parameters by single-dose group (study 1101).")

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: Hayashi 2007 reports single global noncompartmental t½ total omalizumab study 1101 (18.2 days; Discussion, page 558) model-derived half-lives free omalizumab (23 days) IgE (2.4 days). simulated terminal t½ total omalizumab typical patient sit two model-derived bounds (23 days free omalizumab; faster complex due ΔCL_C = 5.86 mL/h top CL_X = 7.32 mL/h). per-dose NCA table published, fully quantitative side--side comparison possible.

what: Mechanism check — typical-value pre-treatment steady state
title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: Without drug, X_TE evolves dX_TE/dt = P_E − CL_E·C_fE. model’s typical-value pre-treatment steady-state free-IgE concentration (P_E / CL_E) · MW_IgE = 426.8 ng/mL. covariate reference value 482.4 ng/mL — small (~12%) intentional offset P_E CL_E independently estimated constrained reproduce IgE0 steady state. model’s initial condition X_TE(0) = (IGE / MW_IgE)·V_E, free IgE starts observed baseline drifts toward model SS first days drug; dosing begins binding kinetics dominate small drift.
code: mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' ev_nodose <- rxode2::et(seq(0, 30, by = 1), cmt = "Cc") sim_nodose <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = ev_nodose,                               params = c(WT = 61.1, IGE = 482.4),                               returnType = "data.frame", addDosing = FALSE) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etaldcl_complex', 'etalvc', 'etalvc_complex', 'etalcl_ige', 'etalp_ige' ggplot(sim_nodose, aes(time, freeIgE)) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 482.4, lty = 2, colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = (0.1595 / 0.071) * 190, lty = 3, colour = "grey40") +   labs(x = "Time (days, no drug)", y = "Free IgE (ng/mL)",        title = "Pre-treatment steady-state drift toward the model SS",        caption = "Dashed: observed IgE0 = 482.4 ng/mL (initial). Dotted: model SS = P_E/CL_E·MW_IgE ≈ 426.8 ng/mL.")

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
text: Bioavailability encoded explicitly. paper reports apparent parameters (CL_X/f, V_X/f, etc.) notes f = 0.62 separate source (Discussion, page 559). model file uses apparent values directly f scaling dose, faithfully reproduces predicted measured concentrations original NONMEM fit. Absolute clearances volumes can recovered multiplying apparent values 0.62. Time unit conversion h → d. paper reports CL ka 1/h, consistent NONMEM convention. model file converts 1/day multiplying rate 24, conversion shown ini() source-trace comment every rate parameter. Half-lives derived converted values match paper (23 days free omalizumab, 2.4 days IgE). Residual error written proportional rather exact log-normal. Hayashi 2007 uses Y = F · exp(ε); σ 0.17–0.22 range encountered , Y ≈ F·(1 + ε), proportional error model propSd = σ faithful within third-order ε terms. nlmixr2’s prop() keyword maps approximation; alternative lnorm() parameterization used future revisions. V_E/f shared V_X/f. Footnote ‡ Table 3 states V_E/f fixed equal V_X/f separate estimation converge. model file enforces setting v_ige <- vc (individual parameter, including etalvc IIV). Concentration-dependent Kd t = 0. central (paper X_TX) = 0 t = 0, Kd = Kd0·(0/total_target)^α = 0 α > 0, S = total_target, negative root yields X_C = 0. matches physical expectation (drug, complex). dosing begins central becomes strictly positive expression evaluates without numerical issues. Compartment names. paper’s X_TX (total omalizumab amount serum, free + complex) maps canonical central compartment; X_TE (total IgE amount, free + complex) maps total_target. follows canonical TMDD compartment names registered references/naming-conventions.md QSS-style total-amount parameterizations (Gibiansky 2008). complex species algebraically derived equilibrium relation rather carried separate ODE state. External validation cohort modelled. paper validates predictions 531 White patients studies 007/008/009 using model fit Japanese subjects . packaged model contains Japanese-fit parameters; encode separate White-cohort estimates none estimated authors. Race recorded Asian = 100%. Hayashi 2007 Table 1 enumerates 202 Japanese subjects studies 1101 1305 race groups model-building cohort; population metadata records cohort 100% Asian (Japanese) accordingly. Initial total_target uses observed IGE0, model SS. described Mechanism-check section, initialising IGE causes small pre-treatment drift binding dominates. Users wanting steady-state initialisation can override inits = c(total_target = p_ige / cl_ige * v_ige) computing p_ige, cl_ige, v_ige cohort interest.

title: Hayashi_2007_omalizumab
