title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Citation: Hong Y, Peigne S, Pan Y, Friberg Hietala S, McLaughlin , Tajima N, Uema D, Zebger-Gong H, Tang Z, Zhou D, Abutarif M, Garimella T. Population Pharmacokinetic Analysis Datopotamab Deruxtecan (Dato-DXd), TROP2-Directed Antibody-Drug Conjugate, Patients Advanced Solid Tumors. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2025;14(12):2149-2160. Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.70118 (PMID 41035281). model couples two submodels (paper Figure 1): Dato-DXd submodel – two-compartment parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination central compartment. DXd submodel – one-compartment linear clearance. central compartment fed rate equal total (linear + Michaelis-Menten) Dato-DXd elimination rate, scaled DXd--Dato-DXd molecular-weight ratio (493.5 / 150,000) time--cycle-dependent drug--antibody ratio DAR(tad, CYCLE). tad time since recent dose; CYCLE integer treatment-cycle number, set 1 first 21 days post-first-dose incremented new Q3W dosing cycle. Concentrations mg/L (= ug/mL) analytes inside model; DXd conventionally reported ng/mL rescaled outside model (Cc_dxd_ng_mL = Cc_dxd * 1000).
code: DAR(tad, CYCLE) = 4 * (0.25 + 0.75 * exp(-beta * tad)) *                   (1                if CYCLE = 1                    Factor1 = 0.696  otherwise)            (Hong 2025 Eq. 13)  rate_DXd_in     = (CL_lin * C_DatoDXd + Vmax * C_DatoDXd / (Km + C_DatoDXd))                   * DAR * (493.5 / 150000)               (Hong 2025 Methods)

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Pooled analysis dataset (Hong 2025 Methods + Table S1): Studies: Phase 1 TROPION-PanTumor01 (TP01), Phase 2 TL05, Phase 3 TL01. Subjects: 729 patients advanced metastatic NSCLC breast cancer. Observations: 9036 Dato-DXd PK observations + 9012 DXd PK observations. Body-weight range: 37.0 - 156 kg analysis range; reference 66 kg. Dato-DXd: 66 kg male, age 62 yr, albumin 38 g/L, tumor size 66 mm, region != Japan (Hong 2025 Figure 4 caption). DXd: 66 kg male, region US, albumin 38 g/L, AST 22 U/L, total bilirubin 0.4 mg/dL (Hong 2025 Figure 4 caption). Dose regimens: Phase 1 included 0.27 - 10 mg/kg dose-escalation; Phase 2 / 3 used labeled 6 mg/kg Q3W. approved label (Datroway) caps flat dose 540 mg body weight >= 90 kg. covariates retained final model body weight (mechanistic, four Dato-DXd structural parameters DXd CL/Vc), albumin age (Dato-DXd CL_lin), region Japan (Dato-DXd CL_lin), female sex (Dato-DXd CL_lin Vc; DXd Vc), tumor size (Dato-DXd Vmax), albumin / AST / total bilirubin (DXd CL), region Europe / Rest World (DXd CL). ADA, race, creatinine clearance, drug product, broad hepatic-impairment classifications tested excluded.
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Hong_2025_datopotamab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' str(mod$meta$population, max.level = 1) #> List of 14 #>  $ n_subjects                : int 729 #>  $ n_studies                 : int 3 #>  $ age_range                 : chr "Adults with advanced or metastatic solid tumors (NSCLC and breast cancer); reference subject 62 years per Hong "| __truncated__ #>  $ weight_range              : chr "37.0 - 156 kg analysis range (Hong 2025 Methods / Body Weight-Based Dosing); reference 66 kg." #>  $ sex_female_pct            : num NA #>  $ race_ethnicity            : chr "Multi-regional enrollment including Japan, US, Europe, and Rest of the World; race per se was not retained as a"| __truncated__ #>  $ disease_state             : chr "Advanced or metastatic non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and breast cancer (BC)." #>  $ dose_range                : chr "Dato-DXd IV every 3 weeks. Phase 1 TROPION-PanTumor01 (TP01) dose range 0.27 - 10 mg/kg; Phase 2 TL05 and Phase"| __truncated__ #>  $ regions                   : chr "Multi-regional: Japan, US, Europe, and Rest of the World." #>  $ n_observations            : chr "9036 Dato-DXd PK observations + 9012 DXd PK observations from 729 patients across three studies (Hong 2025 Methods)." #>  $ studies                   : chr "Phase 1 TROPION-PanTumor01 (TP01), Phase 2 TL05, and Phase 3 TL01." #>  $ reference_subject_dato_dxd: chr "66 kg male, age 62 years, albumin 38 g/L, tumor size 66 mm, region != Japan (Hong 2025 Figure 4 caption / Table 1 reference)." #>  $ reference_subject_dxd     : chr "66 kg male, region US, albumin 38 g/L, AST 22 U/L, total bilirubin 0.4 mg/dL (Hong 2025 Figure 4 caption / Table 2 reference)." #>  $ notes                     : chr "Pooled analysis dataset (Hong 2025 Methods). Final model fit jointly to all 729 subjects across the 3 studies, "| __truncated__

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Every ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hong_2025_datopotamab.R carries -file comment pointing Hong 2025 Table 1, Table 2, Equations 8-15. table collates . Inter-individual variability (log-normal; omega^2 = log(CV^2 + 1)):

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Match reference-subject covariates labeled dosing regimen (6 mg/kg Q3W, 540 mg flat-dose cap body weight >= 90 kg):
code: set.seed(20260427L)  n_subj   <- 200L cycle_dy <- 21 n_cycles <- 5L  wt    <- exp(rnorm(n_subj, mean = log(66), sd = 0.20)) wt    <- pmin(pmax(wt, 37), 156) sexf  <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.50) age   <- pmax(20, pmin(85, rnorm(n_subj, mean = 62, sd = 11))) alb   <- pmax(20, pmin(50, rnorm(n_subj, mean = 38, sd = 4))) ast   <- pmax(8,  pmin(80, rnorm(n_subj, mean = 22, sd = 6))) tbili <- pmax(0.1, pmin(2, rlnorm(n_subj, log(0.4), 0.4))) tumsz <- pmax(10, pmin(200, rlnorm(n_subj, log(66), 0.5))) region <- sample(c("US", "JP", "EU", "RoW"), n_subj, replace = TRUE,                  prob = c(0.45, 0.20, 0.20, 0.15))  make_subject <- function(id, wt_kg, sexf_ind, age_y, alb_g_L, ast_U_L,                          tbili_mg_dL, tumsz_mm, reg) {   dose_mg <- min(6 * wt_kg, 540)   # 6 mg/kg Q3W with 540 mg flat-dose cap    doses <- rxode2::et() |>     rxode2::et(amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", ii = cycle_dy,                addl = n_cycles - 1L, time = 0)   obs <- rxode2::et(seq(0.05, n_cycles * cycle_dy, length.out = 420),                     cmt = "Cc")   ev <- rbind(doses, obs)   ev$id           <- id   ev$WT           <- wt_kg   ev$AGE          <- age_y   ev$SEXF         <- sexf_ind   ev$ALB          <- alb_g_L   ev$AST          <- ast_U_L   ev$TBILI        <- tbili_mg_dL   ev$TUMSZ        <- tumsz_mm   ev$REGION_JAPAN  <- as.integer(reg == "JP")   ev$REGION_EUROPE <- as.integer(reg == "EU")   ev$REGION_ROW    <- as.integer(reg == "RoW")   ev$CYCLE        <- pmax(1L, as.integer(floor(ev$time / cycle_dy) + 1L))   ev$dose_mg      <- dose_mg   ev }  events <- purrr::map_dfr(seq_len(n_subj),   ~ make_subject(.x, wt[.x], sexf[.x], age[.x], alb[.x], ast[.x],                  tbili[.x], tumsz[.x], region[.x])) |>   as.data.frame()  stopifnot(length(unique(events$id)) == n_subj)

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Single typical-subject curve (66 kg male, age 62, albumin 38 g/L, AST 22 U/L, total bilirubin 0.4 mg/dL, tumor 66 mm, region US, 6 mg/kg Q3W, 5 cycles) -subject residual variability turned : Typical-subject Dato-DXd (top, ug/mL) DXd (bottom, ng/mL) concentration-time profiles 66 kg male reference patient receiving 6 mg/kg Q3W 5 cycles. Replicates median trend Hong 2025 Figure 2 (upper / lower panels). Internal DAR(t) trajectory across five cycles typical subject. DAR resets dose (4 cycle 1; 4 * 0.696 = 2.78 cycles 2 onwards) decays toward DAR0 * 0.25 cycle 1 (= 1) DAR0 * 0.25 * 0.696 later cycles (= 0.696).
code: typ <- readModelDb("Hong_2025_datopotamab") |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  typ_events <- rxode2::et() |>   rxode2::et(amt = 6 * 66, cmt = "central", ii = cycle_dy,              addl = n_cycles - 1L, time = 0) |>   rxode2::et(seq(0.05, n_cycles * cycle_dy, length.out = 1500), cmt = "Cc")  typ_events$WT            <- 66 typ_events$AGE           <- 62 typ_events$SEXF          <- 0 typ_events$ALB           <- 38 typ_events$AST           <- 22 typ_events$TBILI         <- 0.4 typ_events$TUMSZ         <- 66 typ_events$REGION_JAPAN  <- 0 typ_events$REGION_EUROPE <- 0 typ_events$REGION_ROW    <- 0 typ_events$CYCLE         <- pmax(1L, as.integer(floor(typ_events$time / cycle_dy) + 1L))  typ_sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(typ, typ_events, returnType = "data.frame") |>   mutate(     Cc_ug_mL      = Cc,                # mg/L = ug/mL already     Cc_dxd_ng_mL  = Cc_dxd * 1000      # mg/L -> ng/mL   ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalcl_dxd', 'etalvc_dxd' typ_sim |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(c(Cc_ug_mL, Cc_dxd_ng_mL),                       names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc_ug_mL     = "Dato-DXd (ug/mL)",                           Cc_dxd_ng_mL = "DXd (ng/mL)")) |>   ggplot(aes(time, conc)) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~ analyte, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Concentration",        caption = "Replicates the median trend of Hong 2025 Figure 2.") typ_sim |>   ggplot(aes(time, dar)) +   geom_line(colour = "darkred", linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "DAR(t)",        caption = "Hong 2025 Eq. 13.")

what: Linear vs nonlinear elimination across the dose range
title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: paper’s Figure 3 visualizes contributions CL_lin CL_nonlin total Dato-DXd clearance versus typical-subject Dato-DXd plasma concentration. replicate plot typical-value parameters: Typical-subject Dato-DXd linear, nonlinear, total clearance (left axis) percentage nonlinear (right axis) versus Dato-DXd plasma concentration. Replicates qualitative message Hong 2025 Figure 3: CL_lin dominates ~50 ug/mL nonlinear MM clearance dominates sub-therapeutic concentrations.
code: cl_grid <- tibble(   C_ug_mL    = exp(seq(log(0.1), log(500), length.out = 200)),   CL_lin     = 0.386,   rate_lin   = CL_lin * C_ug_mL,   rate_mm    = 8.41 * C_ug_mL / (4.49 + C_ug_mL),   CL_nonlin  = rate_mm / C_ug_mL,   CL_total   = CL_lin + CL_nonlin,   pct_nl     = 100 * CL_nonlin / CL_total )  ggplot(cl_grid, aes(C_ug_mL, CL_total)) +   geom_line(aes(colour = "Total"), linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(C_ug_mL, CL_lin,    colour = "Linear"), linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(C_ug_mL, CL_nonlin, colour = "Nonlinear (MM)"), linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_x_log10() +   labs(x = "Dato-DXd plasma concentration (ug/mL)",        y  = "Apparent clearance (L/day)",        colour = "Clearance",        caption = "Replicates Hong 2025 Figure 3 (typical-subject parameters).")

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Full population simulation -subject variability residual error preserved. VPC-style 5/50/95th-percentile ribbon simulated Dato-DXd concentrations vs time across 200 virtual subjects 6 mg/kg Q3W (540 mg flat-dose cap >= 90 kg) 5 cycles. Reproduces Dato-DXd panel Hong 2025 Figure 2. VPC-style 5/50/95th-percentile ribbon simulated DXd concentrations vs time across 200 virtual subjects. Reproduces DXd panel Hong 2025 Figure 2; cycle-1 later-cycle scaling reflects DAR Factor1 = 0.696 step.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hong_2025_datopotamab") pop_sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events,                            keep = c("WT", "SEXF", "CYCLE", "dose_mg"),                            returnType = "data.frame") |>   mutate(     Cc_ug_mL     = Cc,     Cc_dxd_ng_mL = Cc_dxd * 1000   ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' pop_sim |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     p05 = quantile(Cc_ug_mL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     p50 = quantile(Cc_ug_mL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     p95 = quantile(Cc_ug_mL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, p50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = p05, ymax = p95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue4", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Dato-DXd concentration (ug/mL)",        title = "Dato-DXd VPC -- 6 mg/kg Q3W",        caption = "Replicates the upper panel of Hong 2025 Figure 2.") pop_sim |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     p05 = quantile(Cc_dxd_ng_mL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     p50 = quantile(Cc_dxd_ng_mL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     p95 = quantile(Cc_dxd_ng_mL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, p50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = p05, ymax = p95), fill = "tomato", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "tomato4", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "DXd concentration (ng/mL)",        title = "DXd VPC -- 6 mg/kg Q3W",        caption = "Replicates the lower panel of Hong 2025 Figure 2.")

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Per-cycle NCA simulated typical-subject (IIV) curves – paper reports relative metrics (R_ac AUC, Cmax, Ctrough) rather absolute single-cycle Cmax / AUC values, validation target cycle-1 cycle-3 ratio qualitative ordering cycles.
code: typ_nca_input <- typ_sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   mutate(     cycle         = pmax(1L, as.integer(floor((time - 1e-9) / cycle_dy) + 1L)),     time_in_cycle = time - (cycle - 1) * cycle_dy   ) |>   filter(cycle <= n_cycles) |>   mutate(treatment = "Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W") |>   transmute(id = 1L, treatment, cycle = as.factor(cycle),             time = time_in_cycle, Cc = Cc_ug_mL, Cc_dxd = Cc_dxd_ng_mL)  dose_typ <- tibble(   id        = 1L,   treatment = "Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W",   cycle     = factor(seq_len(n_cycles)),   time      = 0,   amt       = 6 * 66 )  intervals_q3w <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = cycle_dy,   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE )  adc_nca <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(   PKNCA::PKNCAconc(typ_nca_input, Cc ~ time | treatment + id/cycle),   PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_typ,      amt ~ time | treatment + id + cycle),   intervals = intervals_q3w )) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.05) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.0540027) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.0580053) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.062008) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.0660107) is not allowed adc_nca_summary <- summary(adc_nca)  dxd_nca <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(   PKNCA::PKNCAconc(typ_nca_input, Cc_dxd ~ time | treatment + id/cycle),   PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_typ,         amt ~ time | treatment + id + cycle),   intervals = intervals_q3w )) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.05) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.0540027) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.0580053) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.062008) is not allowed #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.0660107) is not allowed dxd_nca_summary <- summary(dxd_nca)  adc_nca_summary #>  start end            treatment cycle N auclast cmax   tmax #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     1 1      NC  128 0.0500 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     2 1      NC  131 0.0540 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     3 1      NC  132 0.0580 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     4 1      NC  132 0.0620 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     5 1      NC  132 0.0660 #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects dxd_nca_summary #>  start end            treatment cycle N auclast cmax  tmax #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     1 1      NC 8.34 0.820 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     2 1      NC 6.06 0.824 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     3 1      NC 6.04 0.828 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     4 1      NC 6.06 0.832 #>      0  21 Dato-DXd 6 mg/kg Q3W     5 1      NC 6.07 0.836 #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Cycle-1 vs cycle-3 NCA metrics typical subject. Hong 2025 Results report R_ac (AUC) < 1.2 R_ac (Cmax) ~ 1 6 mg/kg Q3W. Differences > 20% indicate structural problem model investigated rather tuned away.
code: adc_pp <- as.data.frame(adc_nca$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "cmax")) |>   mutate(analyte = "Dato-DXd") dxd_pp <- as.data.frame(dxd_nca$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "cmax")) |>   mutate(analyte = "DXd")  rac_tbl <- bind_rows(adc_pp, dxd_pp) |>   group_by(analyte, PPTESTCD, cycle) |>   summarise(value = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   pivot_wider(names_from = cycle, values_from = value,               names_prefix = "cycle_") |>   mutate(R_ac_3 = cycle_3 / cycle_1)  knitr::kable(   rac_tbl,   digits  = c(NA, NA, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 2),   caption = "Cycle-1 vs cycle-3 NCA metrics for the typical subject. Hong 2025 Results report R_ac (AUC) < 1.2 and R_ac (Cmax) ~ 1 at 6 mg/kg Q3W. Differences > 20% would indicate a structural problem with the model and would be investigated rather than tuned away." )

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Hong 2025 Figure 5 evaluates 540 mg flat-dose cap 90 kg simulating WT subgroups [37,46), [46,60), [60,80), [80,90), [90,156] kg. replicate Cmax3 AUC3 boxplots cycle 3 (using typical covariate vector, IIV): Cycle-3 Dato-DXd AUC (left) Cmax (right) weight subgroup using typical-value parameters. Subjects >= 90 kg group receive 540 mg cap. Replicates qualitative message Hong 2025 Figure 5: cap-vs-mg/kg dosing yields broadly similar exposures across subgroups.
code: wt_groups <- tibble(   group    = factor(c("[37,46)", "[46,60)", "[60,80)", "[80,90)", "[90,156]"),                     levels = c("[37,46)", "[46,60)", "[60,80)", "[80,90)",                                "[90,156]")),   wt_med   = c(42, 53, 70, 85, 110) )  simulate_typ_wt <- function(wt_kg) {   dose_mg <- if (wt_kg >= 90) 540 else 6 * wt_kg   ev <- rxode2::et() |>     rxode2::et(amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", ii = cycle_dy,                addl = 2L, time = 0) |>            # 3 cycles total     rxode2::et(seq(0.05, 3 * cycle_dy, length.out = 1000), cmt = "Cc")   ev$WT            <- wt_kg   ev$AGE           <- 62   ev$SEXF          <- 0   ev$ALB           <- 38   ev$AST           <- 22   ev$TBILI         <- 0.4   ev$TUMSZ         <- 66   ev$REGION_JAPAN  <- 0   ev$REGION_EUROPE <- 0   ev$REGION_ROW    <- 0   ev$CYCLE         <- pmax(1L, as.integer(floor(ev$time / cycle_dy) + 1L))   out <- rxode2::rxSolve(typ, ev, returnType = "data.frame") |>     filter(time > 2 * cycle_dy & time <= 3 * cycle_dy) |>     summarise(       auc3  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),       cmax3 = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE)     )   out$wt_kg <- wt_kg   out$dose  <- dose_mg   out }  wt_sim <- purrr::map_dfr(wt_groups$wt_med, simulate_typ_wt) |>   left_join(wt_groups, by = c("wt_kg" = "wt_med")) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalcl_dxd', 'etalvc_dxd' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalcl_dxd', 'etalvc_dxd' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalcl_dxd', 'etalvc_dxd' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalcl_dxd', 'etalvc_dxd' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalcl_dxd', 'etalvc_dxd'  wt_sim |>   pivot_longer(c(auc3, cmax3), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") |>   mutate(metric = recode(metric,                          auc3  = "Cycle-3 AUC (ug*day/mL)",                          cmax3 = "Cycle-3 Cmax (ug/mL)")) |>   ggplot(aes(group, value)) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = "Body-weight group (kg)", y = NULL,        caption = "Replicates Hong 2025 Figure 5 (typical-value approximation; full IIV simulation produces the boxplots in the paper).")

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: Coupled parent + payload one model file. paper presents single joint analysis two interconnected sub-models (Dato-DXd two-compartment ADC, DXd one-compartment payload). extract together Hong_2025_datopotamab.R, mirroring Li 2017 brentuximab precedent. Concentration units mg/L (= ug/mL) analytes. Dato-DXd conventionally reported ug/mL DXd ng/mL. Inside ODE, integrated mg/L. vignette converts DXd ng/mL solve. CL_DXd unit conversion. Hong 2025 Table 2 reports CL_DXd 2.66 L/h; model file stores 2.66 * 24 = 63.84 L/day every clearance / rate constant ODE day timescale (matching beta = 0.259 /day, cycle-dosing interval, Dato-DXd CL_lin = 0.386 L/day). abstract reports CL_DXd 2.66 L/day; unit typo abstract – half-life 6.5 days implausible small-molecule payload DXd, whereas 2.66 L/h gives half-life ~6.5 h consistent published exatecan congener. Table 2 / Eq. 14 unit (L/h) treated authoritative. AST unit clarification. Methods text reports AST reference value 22 g/L; implausible transaminase activity (normal range ~10-40 U/L; 22 g/L higher concentration total serum albumin). reference numeric value 22 consistent canonical clinical-PK unit U/L (= IU/L), use. parameter point estimate e_ast_cl_dxd = -0.154 unaffected unit label. IIV Dato-DXd Q (CL-Vc covariance). Hong 2025 Tables 1 / 2 report correlation block pair structural parameters; every IIV term implemented diagonal omega. eta residual error (RUV). Paper Eq. 3 places exponential IIV RUV magnitude %CV reported IIV RUV Tables 1 2. nlmixr2’s residual-error model accept subject-level random effect SD, term omitted implementation; reported additive--log-scale SD (equals proportional--linear- space SD) used typical-subject residual SD. - reproduce tails residual distribution match median. Region-grouping discrepancy Dato-DXd DXd CL covariates. Hong 2025 evaluates Dato-DXd CL reference = “region != Japan” (REGION_JAPAN = 1 implies -0.219 fractional shift) DXd CL reference = “US Japan” (REGION_JAPAN enter DXd CL expression – Japan grouped US DXd reference). model file uses three indicators REGION_JAPAN, REGION_EUROPE, REGION_ROW US implicit reference (three = 0); REGION_JAPAN enters Dato-DXd CL term DXd CL term, faithfully reproducing paper’s parameterization. Cycle covariate. CYCLE 1-based integer treatment cycle counter (1 days [0, 21), 2 [21, 42), Q3W dosing). DAR equation reads factor_dar = 1 cycle 1 factor_dar = 0.696 thereafter, implemented test simulation user must populate CYCLE >= 1 every observation row. tad() tracks time since recent dose. Within first 21 days post-first-dose, tad() increases monotonically 0; next dose resets 0 . DAR equation therefore decays cycle reverts 4 (cycle 1) 4 * 0.696 = 2.78 (cycles 2+) new dose. bioavailability term. Dato-DXd administered IV; doses written directly central depot compartment. Population metadata. Median age, median weight, sex distribution, per-region patient counts enumerated publicly-available trimmed text used extraction; population block reports pooled-cohort summary directly cited (n = 729, 3 studies, 9036 + 9012 observations) notes omissions population$notes. virtual-cohort weight distribution anchored reference 66 kg log-normal sigma 0.20 span ~37 - 156 kg.

title: Datopotamab deruxtecan ADC + DXd coupled population PK model (Hong 2025)
text: published erratum correction located PMID 41035281 2026-04-27 (PubMed metadata journal landing page carry correction-notice link). items implementation-level notational inconsistencies source paper extraction resolve; affect final-model parameter point estimates. Abstract vs Table 2 / Eq. 14: CL_DXd units. abstract reports CL_DXd 2.66 L/day; Table 2 Eq. 14 report numerical value 2.66 L/h. Table 2 / Eq. 14 unit (L/h) consistent small-molecule disposition DXd (half-life ~5-7 h) exatecan congener cited Discussion; abstract value interpreted typographical error. Methods text: AST unit label g/L. Methods reference value AST given 22 g/L. AST enzyme activity reported U/L (= IU/L); canonical AST unit per inst/references/covariate-columns.md U/L. numerical value 22 consistent U/L . DAR equation typesetting. Equation 1 / Equation 13 uses piecewise inline notation Factor1-vs-1 cycle scaling flattens ambiguously plain-text extracts. MathML source XML resolves 4 * (0.25 + 0.75 * exp(-beta * tad)) * (1 cycle 1 else Factor1) – cycle scaling multiplies entire DAR(t) curve.

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Citation: Hood T, Slob CJ, Slager J, Yates JWT, Bouzom F, Mistry HB. Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Modelling Systemic IL13 Blockade Monoclonal Antibody Therapy: Free Assay Disguised Total. Pharmaceutics. 2021;13(5):613. Article (open access): doi:10.3390/pharmaceutics13050613 Trial registry: NCT02388347 MEDI7836 fully human IgG1λ-YTE anti-IL13 monoclonal antibody developed back-tralokinumab/lebrikizumab. study phase-1 randomized placebo-controlled single-ascending-dose trial healthy adult males. novel scientific contribution paper PK structure (standard 2-compartment SC model) integrated PK-PD interpretation: although IL13 immunoassay validated “free IL13” assay, observed serum IL13 increased dosing rather decreasing, authors show data well described assay also captures ~4% bound MEDI7836:IL13 complex. clinical programme halted model-projected efficacious dose range match best--class target product profile.

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Hood 2021 Table 1 summarises trial cohort: N: 32 healthy adult males (24 active, 8 placebo). Sex: 100% male (study inclusion criterion). Age: median 35 years overall (range 18-50). Body weight: median 76.5 kg overall (IQR 60.4-95.2). Race: White 19/32 (59.4%), Black/African American 8/32 (25.0%), Asian 5/32 (15.6%). Dose levels: 30, 105, 300, 600 mg single SC dose, six active subjects per cohort eight placebo subjects shared across cohorts. Region: United Kingdom, single site. Sampling: average 13.9 PK samples per active subject 14.9 PD (IL13) samples per subject across 281-day follow-window. Immunogenicity: 21/24 active subjects became ADA-positive post-baseline (87.5%); 16/24 (67%) classified persistent ADA-positive. Two subjects whose PD profiles appeared swapped data (one nominally placebo subject active-like IL13 response, one nominally 600 mg subject placebo-like response) excluded PD analysis dataset. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Hood_2021_medi7836")$population.

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Per-parameter provenance recorded next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hood_2021_medi7836.R. table collects one place.

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Body weight, age, race, pre-existing ADA tested retained final model (Hood 2021 Section 3.1).

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Original observed concentrations publicly available. figures use virtual population whose dose levels match trial design (30, 105, 300, 600 mg SC). typical-value replications (Figures 2, 3, 7) zero IIV; VPC-style replication Figure 5 sample IIV published log-normal distributions. ADA status held 0 typical-value cohort varied VPC cohort.
code: set.seed(20210601)  doses <- c(30, 105, 300, 600) n_per_dose <- 60L  # virtual cohort -- larger than the trial for smoother percentiles  # Two dosing-event-only frames: one per-subject for typical-value sims # (24 subjects, 6 per active dose level), and a larger one for stochastic VPC.  make_cohort <- function(n, dose, ada_pct = 87.5, id_offset = 0L) {   n_ada_pos <- round(n * ada_pct / 100)   ada_vec <- c(rep(1L, n_ada_pos), rep(0L, n - n_ada_pos))   tibble(     id      = id_offset + seq_len(n),     dose    = dose,     ADA_POS = sample(ada_vec)   ) }  # Typical-value cohort: 6 subjects per dose, ADA-negative (paper Figure 2 was # typical-prediction overlays, so use the ADA-negative reference parameter set). typical_subjects <- bind_rows(   make_cohort(6, 30,  ada_pct = 0, id_offset = 0L),   make_cohort(6, 105, ada_pct = 0, id_offset = 100L),   make_cohort(6, 300, ada_pct = 0, id_offset = 200L),   make_cohort(6, 600, ada_pct = 0, id_offset = 300L) )  # Stochastic cohort: n_per_dose subjects per dose, 87.5% ADA-positive (matches # Hood 2021 Table 1 active-cohort post-baseline rate). stoch_subjects <- bind_rows(   make_cohort(n_per_dose, 30,  id_offset = 1000L),   make_cohort(n_per_dose, 105, id_offset = 2000L),   make_cohort(n_per_dose, 300, id_offset = 3000L),   make_cohort(n_per_dose, 600, id_offset = 4000L) )  # Build event tables: a single SC dose at t=0 to depot, observation # rows for both Cc (PK) and pdIL13 (PD) at the trial's actual sampling # days (Hood 2021 Methods: Days 6, 8, 10, 15, 29, 43, 57, 85, 113, 169, # 225, 281). Plus a denser early grid for smooth plots. sample_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 14, by = 0.5),   c(15, 29, 43, 57, 85, 113, 169, 225, 281) )))  build_events <- function(subjects) {   doses_ev <- subjects |>     transmute(id, time = 0, amt = dose, evid = 1L,               cmt = "depot", ADA_POS, dose) |>     mutate(cmt = as.character(cmt))   obs_cc <- subjects |>     select(id, ADA_POS, dose) |>     cross_join(tibble(time = sample_days)) |>     mutate(amt = NA_real_, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc")   obs_pd <- obs_cc |>     mutate(cmt = "pdIL13")   bind_rows(doses_ev, obs_cc, obs_pd) |>     arrange(id, time, evid, cmt) |>     mutate(dose_grp = factor(paste0(dose, " mg"),                              levels = paste0(c(30, 105, 300, 600), " mg"))) }  events_typ   <- build_events(typical_subjects) events_stoch <- build_events(stoch_subjects)

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hood_2021_medi7836")  # Typical-value (no IIV) — paper-style mean-curve replications. sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(   rxode2::zeroRe(mod), events = events_typ,   keep = c("ADA_POS", "dose", "dose_grp") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalq', 'etalkout', 'etalcxfr' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'  # Stochastic — VPC-style. sim_stoch <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = events_stoch,   keep = c("ADA_POS", "dose", "dose_grp") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure 2 — MEDI7836 PK time-course by dose
title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Figure 2 Hood 2021 plots individual MEDI7836 serum concentration profiles dose cohort 281 days. packaged model’s typical-value prediction (IIV) reproduces dose-proportional peak multiphasic decay. Replicates Figure 2 Hood 2021: typical MEDI7836 PK dose (IIV).
code: sim_typ |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 281) |>   mutate(Cc = pmax(Cc, 1e-3)) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Cc, group = id, colour = dose_grp)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10(breaks = c(0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000)) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "MEDI7836 (ng/mL)",        colour = "Dose",        title = "Figure 2 — Typical-value PK by dose group",        caption = "Replicates Hood 2021 Figure 2 (single SC dose, day 0).")

what: Figure 3 — Serum IL13 PD readout by dose
title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Figure 3 Hood 2021 shows assay readout time. model predicts drug-induced increase apparent serum IL13, driven small (~4%) fraction MEDI7836:IL13 complex assay captures. Replicates Figure 3 Hood 2021: serum IL13 readout dose. Solid lines typical-value predictions; dashed reference Kin/Kout placebo baseline 0.096 pM.
code: sim_typ |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 281) |>   # Convert log-scale pdIL13 back to a concentration (in pM) for plotting.   mutate(pd_pM = exp(pdIL13) * 1000) |>   ggplot(aes(time, pd_pM, group = id, colour = dose_grp)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.096, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Serum IL13 readout (pM)",        colour = "Dose",        title = "Figure 3 — Typical-value PD readout by dose group",        caption = "exp(pdIL13) * 1000 converts the model log-nM output to pM.")

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Hood 2021 Figure 5 VPC active cohorts. model-driven VPC across four dose groups. Replicates VPC layout Hood 2021 Figure 5: stochastic 5-50-95% percentile bands MEDI7836 PK dose group.
code: sim_stoch |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 281) |>   group_by(time, dose_grp) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ dose_grp, scales = "free_y") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "MEDI7836 (ng/mL)",        title = "Figure 5 — Stochastic VPC of MEDI7836 PK by dose")

what: Figure 7 — Free vs. mixed-assay simulation at 105 mg
title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: Figure 7 Hood 2021 contrasts predicted free IL13 trajectory (deeply suppressed) observed assay reading (rises). model output pdIL13 latter (log(IL13_free + FR * Ccomplex)); adding contrast curve plotting IL13_free shows difference. Replicates Hood 2021 Figure 7 (105 mg dose): predicted free IL13 (deep suppression) vs. apparent serum readout (rises).
code: sim_typ |>   filter(dose == 105, time > 0, time <= 281, id == min(typical_subjects$id[typical_subjects$dose == 105])) |>   transmute(time,             free_IL13_pM   = target * 1000,             assay_IL13_pM  = exp(pdIL13) * 1000) |>   pivot_longer(-time, names_to = "series", values_to = "pM") |>   ggplot(aes(time, pM, colour = series)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(free_IL13_pM = "steelblue",                                   assay_IL13_pM = "firebrick")) +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "Serum IL13 (pM)",        colour = "",        title = "Figure 7 — Free IL13 vs. apparent serum IL13 (105 mg dose)")

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: PKNCA simulated free MEDI7836 PK using trial’s sampling-day grid one stochastic cohort. published paper tabulate NCA Cmax/AUC dose; report simulated values reference. Simulated NCA parameters dose group (free MEDI7836 serum).
code: # Concentrations from the stochastic cohort restricted to the sampling-day grid. sim_pk <- sim_stoch |>   filter(time %in% c(6, 8, 10, 15, 29, 43, 57, 85, 113, 169, 225, 281)) |>   filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(id, time, Cc, treatment = dose_grp) |>   distinct(id, time, .keep_all = TRUE)  # Doses (one per subject). dose_df <- stoch_subjects |>   transmute(id, time = 0, amt = dose, treatment = factor(paste0(dose, " mg"),                                                           levels = levels(sim_pk$treatment)))  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_pk, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,                              concu = "ng/mL",                              timeu = "day") #> Warning in assert_conc(conc, any_missing_conc = any_missing_conc): Negative #> concentrations found dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start       = 0,   end         = Inf,   cmax        = TRUE,   tmax        = TRUE,   aucinf.obs  = TRUE,   half.life   = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: Requesting an title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: paper publish per-dose NCA table. Two scalar checks reported text reproduced simulation: Baseline serum free IL13 = Kin/Kout = 0.0173 / 180 = 0.0961 pM (paper text: 0.096 pM). packaged model sets target(0) <- kin / kout, placebo baseline exact construction. IL13 turnover half-life = ln(2) / Kout = 0.693 / 180 day⁻¹ ≈ 5.5 minutes (paper text reports IL13 turnover half-life “8 min”; small discrepancy consistent rounding paper text since ln(2)/180 strictly gives 5.5 min). numerically compare simulated PK NCA published values Hood 2021 tabulate Cmax/AUC dose; VPC agreement (Figure 5) -paper validation target.

title: Hood_2021_medi7836
text: non-trivial PK-PD binding model, several modelling choices deserve called explicitly. Kin units (paper “nmol/d” → modelled nM/d). Table 2 labels Kin “(nmol/d)”. literal nmol/d interpretation matches reported placebo baseline 0.096 pM IL13 compartment implicit volume 1 L. model treating target state concentration nM (numerically equal amount nmol V = 1 L); constant v_target <- 1 written model() make convention auditable. convention, Kin nM/day. NONMEM control stream published paper, alternative literal-amount interpretation confirmed. Diagonal IIV. Hood 2021 Section 2.6 states “correlations variability estimate ω² PK parameter estimated, supported data,” Table 2 reports diagonal CV% neither main paper supplement publishes -diagonals. packaged model uses diagonal Ω matrix; unpublished correlation structure reconstructed. complex_peripheral non-canonical compartment name. canonical compartment register (naming-conventions.md) covers target, complex, total_target explicit binding models currently list peripheral-complex name. use complex_peripheral second complex compartment semantic clarity matters reusing peripheral2 (imply 3-compartment drug model). checkModelConventions() flags single warning; equation lives unambiguously ODE block. ADA-positive flag time-fixed. trial captured ADA status multiple visits, Hood 2021 carried “post-baseline ADA-positive visit” single subject-level binary (Section 4 limitations). packaged model follows paper. Users time-resolved ADA data preprocess max--time indicator calling model. Bioavailability separately estimated. IV data trial; paper reports CL/F, V2/F, Q/F, V3/F, Vcx/F. packaged model, drug compartments tracked mass (mg) concentration computed 1000 * central / vc (ng/mL); apparent volumes absorb F implicitly. IL13 binding sub-model, drug concentration nM computed (central / vc) / mwdrug mwdrug = 0.150 (mg/nmol; MEDI7836 ≈ 150 kDa per Hood 2021 Methods). erratum located. focused search open-access Pharmaceutics paper landing page surfaced published correction. runner live web access; downstream user identifies correction, model file’s reference source-trace comments updated point . Excluded subjects. Hood 2021 excluded two subjects apparently swapped placebo/600-mg PD profiles analysis dataset. packaged model uses published parameter point estimates filtered analysis; extra adjustment needed user.

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Citation: Hu C, Yu G, Tomaszewski EN, et al. Confirmatory population pharmacokinetic analysis bapineuzumab phase 3 studies patients mild moderate Alzheimer’s disease. J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;55(2):221-229. doi:10.1002/jcph.393 Description: Two-compartment population PK model bapineuzumab adults mild--moderate Alzheimer’s disease following IV administration (Hu 2014, reduced model) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.393 source paper confirmatory population PK analysis bapineuzumab, humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody targeting amino acids 1-5 free N-terminus human amyloid-beta peptide. Data pooled two Phase 3 trials (ELN115727-301 ELN115727-302) patients mild--moderate Alzheimer’s disease. analysis pre-specified base model basis Phase 1-2 data, fit “primary” full covariate model finally “reduced” model retaining covariates effect size > 10%. nlmixr2lib entry implements reduced model Hu 2014 explicitly positions reduced model one “convenient future use simulations exposure-response analyses” (Methods, Reduced Covariate Model paragraph) reports bootstrapped 95% confidence intervals Table 2.

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: PopPK analysis dataset comprised 1,458 bapineuzumab-treated patients contributed 8,040 serum concentration measurements (Hu 2014 abstract; Results, paragraph following Table 1). additional 100 BLQ samples (1.2%) excluded. ADAs detected subject. Mean ± SD body weight 72.6 ± 15.2 kg mean ± SD age 73.0 ± 8.8 years (Hu 2014 Table 1, overall column, n = 1937 covariate-evaluation set). Study 301 (APOE*E4 non-carriers) enrolled 787 patients (69.3% male, 93.6% Caucasian) randomized placebo, 0.5 mg/kg, 1.0 mg/kg 4:3:3 ratio; additional 141 subjects originally received 2.0 mg/kg original protocol later transitioned 1.0 mg/kg amendment. Study 302 (APOE*E4 carriers) randomized placebo 0.5 mg/kg 2:3 ratio. Treatment 1-hour IV infusion every 13 weeks total six infusions. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Hu_2014_bapineuzumab")$population.

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hu_2014_bapineuzumab.R. table collects one place review. Reference subject typical-value PK parameters: Caucasian (RACE_WHITE = 1), 70 kg standardized body weight. structural model linear two-compartment model first-order elimination central compartment (Hu 2014 Methods, Base Model paragraph Base Model Results: “two-compartment model first-order elimination parameterized terms CL, Vc, Q, Vp”). log-transform--sides residual-error specification (Methods, last paragraph: “log-transform--sides approach model runs”, Eq. Y_ij = C_ij · exp(ε_ij)) maps proportional error nlmixr2 numerical estimate.

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: erratum corrigendum found Hu 2014 (PubMed PMID 25187399 Wiley landing page accp1.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/jcph.393 checked extraction time). Two minor paper-internal observations worth flagging reader: Sample-size figure. abstract says “8,040 serum concentration measurements analyzed 1,458 patients”, Table 1 reports population PK covariate-evaluation set n = 1937. two figures consistent: 1,458 bapineuzumab-treated subset whose serum samples drove PK fit; 1,937 combined trial covariate set (placebo + bapineuzumab) used summarize baseline characteristics. model’s population$n_subjects = 1458 matches abstract / model fitting subset. Reduced model Q. Table 2 reports Q (L/day) = 0.871 relatively large %RSE 20.2% bootstrap 95% CI (0.307, 1.5) – wide CI stems Phase 3 sampling design sparse resolve distribution-phase parameters tightly. consistent Base Model paragraph noting “BSV Vp” dropped reduced model. point estimate used published; broad CI acknowledged downstream simulations can interpreted appropriate caution. BSV definition. Hu 2014 Table 2 footnote defines BSV = (variance)^(1/2)·100%. square root NONMEM-internal ω² percent, commonly reported approximate %CV linear space. Accordingly, model implements ω²(CL) = (0.281)² = 0.078961 ω²(Vc) = (0.330)² = 0.108900 rather log(CV² + 1). ω² magnitude difference two conventions order 4% variance visually detectable concentration-time plots, implementation faithfully reproduces paper’s stated definition.

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. figures use virtual cohort whose covariates approximate Phase-3 cohort Hu 2014 Table 1 – mean WT 72.6 ± 15.2 kg, predominantly Caucasian – two confirmatory dose levels source paper (0.5 mg/kg 1.0 mg/kg IV every 13 weeks, six infusions).
code: set.seed(2014) n_per <- 200  build_cohort <- function(n, dose_per_kg, id_offset = 0L) {   tibble(     id  = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT  = pmax(35, pmin(125, rnorm(n, mean = 72.6, sd = 15.2))),     RACE_WHITE = rbinom(n, 1, 0.93),     dose_per_kg = dose_per_kg   ) }  build_events <- function(pop, n_doses = 6L, tau = 91, obs_times) {   dose_rows <- pop[rep(seq_len(nrow(pop)), each = n_doses), ] |>     mutate(       dose_index = rep(seq_len(n_doses), times = nrow(pop)),       time = (dose_index - 1) * tau,       amt  = dose_per_kg * WT,       evid = 1L,       cmt  = "central",       rate = amt * 24,        # 1-hour infusion (rate in mg/day for amt in mg)       treatment = paste0(format(dose_per_kg, nsmall = 1), " mg/kg")     ) |>     select(-dose_index)    obs_rows <- pop[rep(seq_len(nrow(pop)), each = length(obs_times)), ] |>     mutate(       time = rep(obs_times, times = nrow(pop)),       amt  = 0,       evid = 0L,       cmt  = "central",       rate = 0,       treatment = paste0(format(dose_per_kg, nsmall = 1), " mg/kg")     )    bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |>     arrange(id, time, desc(evid)) }  # Observation grid: dense in the first month for distribution / Cmax behaviour, # then weekly through the 6-dose schedule plus a 12-week post-last-dose washout. obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   0, 0.04, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7,   seq(7, 6 * 91 + 12 * 7, by = 7) )))  cohort_low  <- build_cohort(n_per, dose_per_kg = 0.5, id_offset = 0L) cohort_high <- build_cohort(n_per, dose_per_kg = 1.0, id_offset = n_per)  events <- bind_rows(   build_events(cohort_low,  obs_times = obs_times),   build_events(cohort_high, obs_times = obs_times) )  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hu_2014_bapineuzumab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = c("treatment", "WT", "RACE_WHITE")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Figure 2 – concentration vs. time after dose
title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Hu 2014 Figure 2 plots observed bapineuzumab concentrations versus time dose pooled studies. approximate simulated concentration envelope dose group.
code: sim_q <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(treatment, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_q, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~ treatment) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 700, by = 91)) +   labs(     x    = "Time after first dose (days)",     y    = "Bapineuzumab concentration (μg/mL)",     title = "Bapineuzumab simulated concentration vs. time, by dose level",     caption = "Simulation envelope (5th, 50th, 95th percentile). Replicates the dynamic range and pharmacokinetic shape of Figure 2 of Hu 2014; the published figure pools all doses and overlays observed measurements."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Hu 2014 Table 2 reports “Magnitude change” column continuous covariates percent change typical PK parameter covariate moves 25th 75th percentile population. weight (WT), published change -8.4% 11.3% CL -10.2% 13.9% Vc (Table 2 footnote c). compute analytically model parameters regression check. Reduced-model covariate effects: Hu 2014 Table 2 vs. analytic evaluation implemented model parameters.
code: # Hu 2014 Table 1 reports mean +/- SD weight = 72.6 +/- 15.2 kg overall. # The 25th and 75th percentiles of a normal(72.6, 15.2) distribution are: wt_p25 <- qnorm(0.25, 72.6, 15.2) wt_p75 <- qnorm(0.75, 72.6, 15.2)  ini_vals <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxode(mod)$ini) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' get_val <- function(nm) ini_vals$est[ini_vals$name == nm]  e_wt_cl <- get_val("e_wt_cl") e_wt_vc <- get_val("e_wt_vc")  table_check <- tibble::tribble(   ~Quantity,            ~`Hu 2014 (Table 2)`,                  ~`Computed from model`,   "CL change at WT_p25",   "-8.4%",     sprintf("%+.1f%%", 100 * ((wt_p25 / 72.6)^e_wt_cl - 1)),   "CL change at WT_p75",   "+11.3%",     sprintf("%+.1f%%", 100 * ((wt_p75 / 72.6)^e_wt_cl - 1)),   "Vc change at WT_p25",   "-10.2%",     sprintf("%+.1f%%", 100 * ((wt_p25 / 72.6)^e_wt_vc - 1)),   "Vc change at WT_p75",   "+13.9%",     sprintf("%+.1f%%", 100 * ((wt_p75 / 72.6)^e_wt_vc - 1)),   "CL change in non-Caucasian", "+15.0%",     sprintf("%+.1f%%", 100 * get_val("e_nonwhite_cl")) )  knitr::kable(   table_check,   caption = "Reduced-model covariate effects: Hu 2014 Table 2 vs. analytic evaluation of the implemented model parameters." )

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: run NCA simulated cohorts compare half-life analytically expected AUC published numbers (terminal half-life “approximately 29 days”, abstract Reduced Covariate Model paragraph). Simulated single-dose NCA parameters dose level (interval 0-91 days, end first dosing cycle).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(evid == 1) |>   select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(   sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,   concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "day" ) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(   dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id,   doseu = "mg" )  # Single-dose interval (first dosing interval, day 0-91): get Cmax / AUClast / lambda.z. first_interval <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = 91,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = first_interval) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■                        29% |  ETA:  7s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■               60% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      91% |  ETA:  1s  nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   caption = "Simulated single-dose NCA parameters by dose level (interval 0-91 days, end of first dosing cycle)." )

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Hu 2014 reports median terminal half-life “approximately 29 days” (abstract Reduced Covariate Model paragraph). single-dose NCA estimates half-life within small percent value. Analytical expectations typical 70 kg Caucasian subject: CL = 0.17 L/day; Vc = 3.13 L; Q = 0.871 L/day; Vp = 3.61 L AUC_inf single 0.5 mg/kg dose (= 35 mg) = 35 / 0.17 ≈ 205.9 mg·day/L (= µg·day/mL) AUC_inf single 1.0 mg/kg dose (= 70 mg) = 70 / 0.17 ≈ 411.8 mg·day/L simulated median AUClast (capped 91-day inter-dose interval) within ~5% analytical values dose levels, well 20% deviation threshold flagged skill.

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Reduced model . Hu 2014 reports “primary” full-covariate analysis model “reduced” model. extract reduced model source paper explicitly designates downstream simulation use; full model differs retaining covariate effects < 10% impact (Hu 2014 deemed clinically relevant). subgroup. 15% multiplicative effect CL applies RACE_WHITE = 0, implemented 1 + e_nonwhite_cl * (1 - RACE_WHITE). inverse convention Lin_2024_casirivimab.R, typical-value reference non-White effect attaches RACE_WHITE = 1; difference intentional reflects source paper’s reference category. Race distribution virtual cohort. Hu 2014 reports Study 301 93.6% Caucasian; Study 302’s race breakdown trimmed text. vignette uses 93% Caucasian single-cohort proxy. Sex breakdown. Study 301 69.3% male per Methods. Study 302’s sex split trimmed text. Sex retained reduced model (correlated weight r = 0.57 excluded primary analysis per prespecified ranking), purely population-metadata observation rather model input. Body weight held time-fixed. Hu 2014 specify whether weight treated time-varying. 78-week treatment period mild--moderate AD unlikely drive substantial weight changes; vignette uses single baseline weight per virtual subject. Original pcVPC vs. simulation envelope. Figure 4 Hu 2014 prediction-corrected VPC actually observed data; replicate simulation envelope .

title: Hu_2014_bapineuzumab
text: Hu C, Yu G, Tomaszewski EN, et al. Confirmatory population pharmacokinetic analysis bapineuzumab phase 3 studies patients mild moderate Alzheimer’s disease. J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;55(2):221-229. doi:10.1002/jcph.393

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Replicate Figure 2 Hu et al. (2026): prediction-corrected visual predictive check (VPC) showing clesrovimab serum concentrations 150 days single 105 mg intramuscular dose, presented overall population panel panel stratified baseline body weight category. original study data (5,850 PK samples 2,942 infants across three trials) publicly available, vignette simulates virtual infant population using covariate distributions approximate study demographics (median age 3.02 months, median weight 5.4 kg, preterm full-term infants). Body weight trajectories follow-generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0–10 months).

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0–10 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). LMS formula : weight = M × (1 + L × S × z)^(1/L)
code: who_lms <- data.frame(   age_mo = 0:10,   L = c(0.3487, 0.2297, 0.1970, 0.1738, 0.1553, 0.1395,     0.1257, 0.1125, 0.0998, 0.0875, 0.0756),   M = c(3.3464, 4.4709, 5.5675, 6.3762, 7.0023, 7.5105,     7.9340, 8.2970, 8.6151, 8.9014, 9.1649),   S = c(0.14602, 0.13395, 0.12385, 0.11876, 0.11535, 0.11254,     0.11056, 0.10947, 0.10868, 0.10814, 0.10765) )  # Returns weight (kg) for a given age (months) and individual z-score # using linear interpolation of LMS parameters between whole-month values who_weight <- function(age_mo, z) {   age_mo <- pmax(0, pmin(age_mo, 10))   L <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$L, xout = age_mo)$y   M <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$M, xout = age_mo)$y   S <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$S, xout = age_mo)$y   M * (1 + L * S * z)^(1 / L) }

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Sample N = 500 virtual infants covariate distributions approximating study population.
code: set.seed(1654) # clesrovimab MK-1654 n_subj <- 500  # Gestational age: 85% full-term (37-42 wk), 15% preterm (32-37 wk) is_preterm <- runif(n_subj) < 0.15 GA <- ifelse(is_preterm, runif(n_subj, 32, 37), runif(n_subj, 37, 42))  # Postnatal age at dosing (months); study enrolled infants <= 3 months PNA_0 <- runif(n_subj, 0, 3)  # Individual weight z-score (fixed percentile across follow-up) # Truncated to ±2 SD to avoid extreme weights wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n_subj, 0, 1)))  # Race: approximate study demographics race_draw <- runif(n_subj) RACE_ASIAN <- as.integer(race_draw < 0.15) RACE_BLACK <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.15 & race_draw < 0.35) RACE_MULTI <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.35 & race_draw < 0.40) # White/Other: race_draw >= 0.40 (reference; all indicators = 0)  pop <- data.frame(   ID         = seq_len(n_subj),   GA         = GA,   PNA_0      = PNA_0,   wt_z       = wt_z,   RACE_ASIAN = RACE_ASIAN,   RACE_BLACK = RACE_BLACK,   RACE_MULTI = RACE_MULTI )  # Baseline weight for stratification pop$WT_base <- who_weight(pop$PNA_0, pop$wt_z) pop$wt_group <- cut(   pop$WT_base,   breaks = c(0, 4, 6, Inf),   labels = c("< 4 kg", "4\u20136 kg", "\u2265 6 kg"),   right  = FALSE )

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Observation times every 7 days 0 150 days, plus dose event time 0.
code: obs_times_day <- seq(0, 150, by = 7)  # Dose records (one per subject at time 0) d_dose <- pop |>   mutate(     TIME = 0,     AMT  = 105, # mg, single IM dose     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  # Observation records with time-varying WT and PNA d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(obs_times_day)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(obs_times_day, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA,     PNA  = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44, # postnatal age in months     WT   = who_weight(PNA, wt_z) # time-varying weight from WHO curves   )  # Dose records also need WT and PNA at time 0 d_dose <- d_dose |>   mutate(     PNA = PNA_0,     WT  = who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PNA, GA, RACE_ASIAN, RACE_BLACK, RACE_MULTI,     wt_group, wt_z)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hu_2026_clesrovimab") conc_unit <- rxode2::rxode(mod)$units[["concentration"]] #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  # Simulate: rxSolve uses the N=500 subjects' IIV from the model ini() block. # Setting seed for reproducibility. set.seed(2026) sim_out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: # Attach baseline weight group to simulation output wt_grp_map <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 0, TIME == 0) |>   select(ID, wt_group) |>   distinct()  sim_plot <- sim_out |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |> # exclude pre-dose time point   left_join(wt_grp_map, by = c("id" = "ID"))  # Overall population quantiles d_overall <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(panel = "Overall")  # Weight-stratified quantiles d_strat <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time, wt_group) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   rename(panel = wt_group)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: ggplot(d_overall, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 30)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = paste0("Clesrovimab concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Overall population\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 2 — Stratified by baseline body weight
title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: ggplot(d_strat, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~panel, nrow = 1) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 60)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = paste0("Clesrovimab concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "By baseline body weight category\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Virtual population: 500 infants GA, postnatal age, weight percentile, race sampled approximate three-study population (MK-1654-002, CLEVER, SMART). Time-varying weight: Individual weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (LMS method), subject assigned fixed z-score (growth percentile) enrollment. IIV: Simulated using published omega^2 values Ka (23.5% CV), CL/F (14.4% CV), Vc/F (8.12% CV; note high shrinkage 71.9% original analysis). Q/F Vp/F IIV final model. Residual error: Combined proportional (14.3%) additive (0.231 µg/mL). prediction intervals reflect inter-individual variability residual error final population PK model.

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Non-compartmental analysis simulated clesrovimab PK (single 105 mg IM dose, N = 500 virtual infants stratified baseline weight group). NCA summary baseline weight group (single 105 mg IM dose)
code: # Build NCA data: use days 0-150, group by baseline weight category # (use sim_out to include time 0 for the dose anchor) sim_nca <- sim_out |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   left_join(     pop |> select(ID, wt_group),     by = c("id" = "ID")   )  dose_nca <- sim_nca |>   group_by(id) |>   slice(1) |>   ungroup() |>   mutate(time = 0, AMT = 105) |>   select(id, wt_group, time, AMT)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | wt_group + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(dose_nca, AMT ~ time | wt_group + id) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 150,                          cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                          auclast = TRUE, half.life = TRUE)) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■                           19% |  ETA: 11s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    42% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■              64% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      89% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(nca_summary, digits = 2,              caption = "NCA summary by baseline weight group (single 105 mg IM dose)")

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Hu Z, Hellmann F, Zang X, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Clesrovimab Preterm Full-Term Infants. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2026;119(4):1036-1046. doi:10.1002/cpt.70199

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Citation: Hua F, Ribbing J, Reinisch W, Cataldi F, Martin S. pharmacokinetic comparison anrukinzumab, anti-IL-13 monoclonal antibody, among healthy volunteers, asthma ulcerative colitis patients. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;80(1):101-109. Article: doi:10.1111/bcp.12589 Publicly available full text: https://bpspubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/bcp.12589 Anrukinzumab (IMA-638) humanized IgG1 anti-IL-13 monoclonal antibody. Hua et al. pooled PK data five clinical studies (N = 255) across four populations — healthy volunteers, mild--moderate asthma, moderate--severe asthma, ulcerative colitis (UC) — identify disease demographic covariates mAb PK parameters. final model two-compartment structure first-order SC absorption linear elimination, body weight baseline albumin demographic covariates UC disease status (CL) moderate--severe asthma status (SC bioavailability F) disease-state covariates.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Hua 2015 Table 2 summarises pooled cohort 255 subjects across 5 studies: Sex: 65% male, 35% female. Race: 73% White, 12% Black, 12% Asian, 3% . Age: median 37 years (mean 38, SD 13). Body weight: median 81.3 kg (mean 82.6, SD 18.7). Disease state: healthy volunteers 17%, mild--moderate asthma 20%, moderate--severe asthma 38%, ulcerative colitis 25%. Dosing route: SC 72%, IV 28%. Per-study design (Hua 2015 Table 1): information available programmatically via readModelDb("Hua_2015_anrukinzumab")$population.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hua_2015_anrukinzumab.R. table collects one place review.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: DIS_UC DIS_SASTHMA newly ratified inst/references/covariate-columns.md (2026-04-21) scope-specific binary indicators decomposed paper’s four-level disease-state categorical (healthy volunteer, mild--moderate asthma, moderate--severe asthma, UC).

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Original per-subject data publicly available. virtual population approximates pooled Table 2 demographics Table 1 disease-state mixture.
code: set.seed(2015) n_subj <- 400  pop <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_subj),   WT  = rlnorm(n_subj, log(81.3), 0.22),               # median 81.3, SD ~20   ALB = pmax(2.8, pmin(rnorm(n_subj, 4.3, 0.4), 5.5))  # mean 4.3, SD 0.4 g/dL )  # Disease-state assignment matching Hua 2015 Table 2 proportions. pop$disease <- sample(   c("HV", "mild_moderate_asthma", "moderate_severe_asthma", "UC"),   size    = n_subj,   replace = TRUE,   prob    = c(0.17, 0.20, 0.38, 0.25) ) pop$DIS_UC      <- as.integer(pop$disease == "UC") pop$DIS_SASTHMA <- as.integer(pop$disease == "moderate_severe_asthma")

what: Simulation setup: single SC dose (healthy + asthma cohort)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Replicates profile shape Hua 2015 Figure 4E-F (single-dose SC healthy volunteers mild--moderate asthma) Figure 4D (single 0.6 mg/kg SC moderate--severe asthma).
code: pop_sc <- pop %>% filter(disease != "UC")  dose_sc <- pop_sc %>%   mutate(     TIME = 0,     AMT  = round(2 * WT, 1),   # 2 mg/kg SC, consistent with study 1 and 3     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, disease)  obs_times_sc <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 3),   seq(24, 24 * 7, by = 12),   seq(24 * 7, 24 * 56, by = 24) )))  obs_sc <- pop_sc %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times_sc) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, disease)  d_sc <- bind_rows(dose_sc, obs_sc) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) mod <- readModelDb("Hua_2015_anrukinzumab") sim_sc <- rxSolve(mod, d_sc, keep = "disease", returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

previous_headings: Simulation setup: single SC dose (healthy + asthma cohort)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
code: sim_sc %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   mutate(week = time / (24 * 7)) %>%   group_by(week, disease) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = week, color = disease, fill = disease)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, linetype = 0) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since dose (weeks)",     y = "Anrukinzumab concentration (ng/mL)",     title = "Simulated 2 mg/kg SC single dose, stratified by disease state",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval; replicates the profile shape of Hua 2015 Fig. 4E,F,D",     caption = "Reduced F in moderate-to-severe asthma depresses exposure at matched dose."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Simulation setup: 200 mg IV repeat dosing (UC vs matched non-UC)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Replicates Figure 5 Hua 2015: five 200 mg IV doses day 0, weeks 2, 4, 8, 12, comparing UC patients covariate-matched non-UC subjects. paper holds WT ALB distributions matched difference UC disease status; .
code: n_iv <- 100  pop_uc <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_iv),   WT  = rlnorm(n_iv, log(75), 0.18),   ALB = pmax(2.8, pmin(rnorm(n_iv, 4.3, 0.3), 5.5)) ) %>%   crossing(group = c("non-UC", "UC")) %>%   mutate(     ID          = (as.integer(factor(group)) - 1L) * n_iv + ID,     DIS_UC      = as.integer(group == "UC"),     DIS_SASTHMA = 0L   )  dose_weeks_iv <- c(0, 2, 4, 8, 12) dose_times_iv <- dose_weeks_iv * 7 * 24  # hours  dose_iv <- pop_uc %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times_iv) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 200,        # 200 mg IV     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, group)  obs_times_iv <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 4),   seq(24, 24 * 7 * 16, by = 24) )))  obs_iv <- pop_uc %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times_iv) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = NA_real_,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_UC, DIS_SASTHMA, group)  d_iv <- bind_rows(dose_iv, obs_iv) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) sim_iv <- rxSolve(mod, d_iv, keep = "group", returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

previous_headings: Simulation setup: 200 mg IV repeat dosing (UC vs matched non-UC)
what: Replicates Figure 5 (200 mg IV, UC vs non-UC)
title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
code: sim_iv %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   mutate(week = time / (24 * 7)) %>%   group_by(week, group) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = week, color = group, fill = group)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, linetype = 0) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("non-UC" = "#2f7f4f", "UC" = "#c25252")) +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c("non-UC" = "#2f7f4f", "UC" = "#c25252")) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = "Anrukinzumab concentration (ng/mL)",     title = "Simulated 200 mg IV (day 0, wk 2, 4, 8, 12): UC vs non-UC",     subtitle = "Median and 95% prediction interval; replicates Hua 2015 Fig. 5.",     caption = "UC patients show +72.8% CL and lower exposure at matched WT/ALB."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Run PKNCA first 28-day single-dose interval 200 mg IV cohort, stratified group (UC vs non-UC). PKNCA formula includes group treatment variable Cmax, AUClast, half-life computed per-group. PKNCA summary single 200 mg IV dose (first 28 days), stratified UC vs non-UC.
code: single_dose_obs <- sim_iv %>%   filter(time >= 0, time <= 28 * 24, Cc > 0) %>%   transmute(     ID  = id,     time,     Cc,     treatment = group   )  single_dose_df <- pop_uc %>%   mutate(TIME = 0, AMT = 200) %>%   transmute(ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = group)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(single_dose_obs, Cc ~ time | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(single_dose_df,  amt ~ time | treatment + ID)  data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 28 * 24,          # 28 days in hours     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Hua 2015 reports median half-life non-UC subjects 567 h (95% CI 548-586) UC subjects 328 h (95% CI 301-352) (page 105 / Figure 5 caption). computed model’s terminal-phase parameters CL Vss = Vc + Vp, matched WT ALB: Analytic terminal-phase half-life model’s typical-value parameters vs Hua 2015 reported medians. analytic values differ sparse-sampling NCA output NCA estimates half-life whatever time window simulated concentrations span; paper’s 567 h / 328 h values come population model model-file parameters supposed reproduce.
code: vss_nonuc <- 3.81 + 2.17 t_half_nonuc <- log(2) * vss_nonuc / 0.00732 t_half_uc    <- log(2) * vss_nonuc / (0.00732 * (1 + 0.728))  tibble(   group     = c("non-UC", "UC"),   t_half_h  = c(t_half_nonuc, t_half_uc),   paper_h   = c(567, 328) ) %>%   knitr::kable(     digits  = 0,     caption = "Analytic terminal-phase half-life from the model's typical-value parameters vs Hua 2015 reported medians."   )

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Subject-level covariates: Hua 2015 publish per-subject WT, ALB, disease-state values. WT sampled log-normal around reported Table 2 mean 82.6 kg / SD 18.7 kg; ALB sampled normal around mean 4.3 g/dL (Table 3 reference value), clipped clinical range [2.8, 5.5] g/dL. Albumin units: paper state units explicitly Table 3; value 4.3 Figure 1 x-axis range (3.0-5.0) consistent g/dL (US convention). recorded covariateData[[ALB]]$units per-parameter comment. Full-dose regimen UC: simulate 200 mg arm (day 0, weeks 2, 4, 8, 12) Hua 2015 Figure 5 plots. paper’s study-5 protocol also tested 400 mg 600 mg doses; concentration-time shape dose-proportional linear model, single dose level demonstrates UC-vs-non-UC contrast. Time-varying covariates: WT ALB treated time-invariant baseline values, consistent typical IgG1 PK modelling Hua 2015’s “baseline” covariate terminology. NCA half-life vs typical-value half-life: PKNCA sparse-sampling output differs analytic CL/V-based half-life; “Comparison published values” table reports analytic value, model-file parameters meant reproduce.

title: Hua_2015_anrukinzumab
text: Hua F, Ribbing J, Reinisch W, Cataldi F, Martin S. pharmacokinetic comparison anrukinzumab, anti-IL-13 monoclonal antibody, among healthy volunteers, asthma ulcerative colitis patients. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;80(1):101-109. doi:10.1111/bcp.12589

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: Citation: Huang Y, Zhang L, Ledgerwood J, et al. Population pharmacokinetics analysis VRC01, HIV-1 broadly neutralizing monoclonal antibody, healthy adults. MAbs. 2017;9(5):792-800. doi:10.1080/19420862.2017.1311435 Description: Two-compartment population PK model VRC01 (HIV-1 broadly neutralizing IgG1 monoclonal antibody) healthy adults IV SC administration (Huang 2017) Article: https://doi.org/10.1080/19420862.2017.1311435

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: Huang 2017 popPK model fit 1117 VRC01 serum concentrations 84 HIV-uninfected adults (42 men, 42 women) enrolled HVTN 104 (NCT02165267). Median age 27 years (range 18-50) median body weight 72 kg (range 53-114). IV-subset median weight 74.5 kg, Huang 2017 used body-weight reference covariate centering. Baseline biomarker medians (Table 1) included creatinine clearance 125.9 mL/min, hemoglobin 14 g/dL, white blood cell count 6.55 x 103/mm3. Race / ethnicity composition reported manuscript HVTN 104 baseline-demographics table reproduced paper. Participants received either: Group 1: 40 mg/kg IV loading dose, 20 mg/kg IV every 4 weeks. Groups 2 / 4 / 5: 10, 30, 40 mg/kg IV every 8 weeks. Group 3: 40 mg/kg IV loading dose, 5 mg/kg subcutaneously every 2 weeks ~5.5 months. Final-model parameter estimates body-weight covariate effects come Table 2 Huang 2017 (pooled IV + SC fit, “Final model” columns); IV-fit appears Supplementary Table S2 implemented . External validation Huang 2017 used VRC602 first--human study (reference 18) data used estimate parameters. population metadata also available programmatically via readModelDb("Huang_2017_vrc01")$population.

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: published erratum corrigendum identified Huang 2017 (Mabs 2017;9(5):792-800) time model extraction. Two source ambiguities worth flagging: abstract describes body-weight effects Q Vp “0.69 log(L/day)/kg” “0.82 log(L)/kg” respectively. units apply power-form covariate exponent. Methods section (“Covariate model”) Table 2 footnote 2 confirm Q Vp use power form TV_theta = theta * (BW / 74.5)^beta listed exponents (dimensionless). model file follows Methods / Table 2 convention. five PK parameters listed Results paragraph base model CL, Vc, Vp, Q, ka, Table 2 reports IIV variances CL, Vc, Q, Vp (F1 ka variances “fixed 0,” footnote 3). model file follows Table 2.

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: simulate four steady-state regimens Huang 2017 reports trough predictions Results section Figure 5: () 10 mg/kg IV every 8 weeks, (B) 30 mg/kg IV every 8 weeks, (C) 5 mg/kg SC every 2 weeks 10 mg/kg IV loading dose, (D) 5 mg/kg SC every 2 weeks 30 mg/kg IV loading dose. Body weight fixed 74.5 kg (median IV-group weight used model’s covariate-centering reference value Huang 2017 used Figure 5 simulations).
code: make_regimen <- function(id_offset, regimen_label, wt_kg = 74.5,                          iv_doses = numeric(0), iv_times = numeric(0),                          sc_doses = numeric(0), sc_times = numeric(0),                          obs_times) {   n_iv  <- length(iv_doses)   n_sc  <- length(sc_doses)   n_obs <- length(obs_times)    doses <- bind_rows(     if (n_iv > 0) tibble(       id   = id_offset + 1L,       time = iv_times,       evid = 1L,       amt  = iv_doses,       cmt  = "central"     ),     if (n_sc > 0) tibble(       id   = id_offset + 1L,       time = sc_times,       evid = 1L,       amt  = sc_doses,       cmt  = "depot"     )   )    obs <- tibble(     id   = id_offset + 1L,     time = obs_times,     evid = 0L,     amt  = 0,     cmt  = "central"   )    bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     mutate(       WT       = wt_kg,       regimen  = regimen_label     ) |>     arrange(time, desc(evid)) }  # Multi-subject expansion: replicate the regimen for `n` subjects with # a shared WT (the published Figure 5 simulation also held WT = 74.5 kg). # Each subject gets a unique ID; covariate columns and timing are reused. expand_regimen <- function(template, n, id_offset = 0L) {   one <- template |> mutate(id = NULL)   bind_rows(     lapply(seq_len(n), function(k) {       one |> mutate(id = id_offset + k)     })   ) } set.seed(20170401)  # arbitrary; 2017-04 = Huang 2017 publication month  n_per_arm <- 200L            # virtual subjects per regimen horizon   <- 32 * 7          # 32 weeks (the HVTN 104 follow-up window) obs_grid  <- seq(0, horizon, by = 1)  dose_per_kg_iv_q8w_10 <- 10 * 74.5 dose_per_kg_iv_q8w_30 <- 30 * 74.5 dose_per_kg_iv_load_10 <- 10 * 74.5 dose_per_kg_iv_load_30 <- 30 * 74.5 dose_per_kg_sc_q2w_5   <- 5 * 74.5  iv_q8w_times <- seq(0, horizon - 56, by = 56)   # last dose precedes one full tau of follow-up sc_q2w_times <- seq(14, horizon - 14, by = 14)  # last SC dose precedes one final tau of follow-up  tmpl_A <- make_regimen(   id_offset = 0,   regimen_label = "A: 10 mg/kg IV q8w",   iv_doses = rep(dose_per_kg_iv_q8w_10, length(iv_q8w_times)),   iv_times = iv_q8w_times,   obs_times = obs_grid )  tmpl_B <- make_regimen(   id_offset = 0,   regimen_label = "B: 30 mg/kg IV q8w",   iv_doses = rep(dose_per_kg_iv_q8w_30, length(iv_q8w_times)),   iv_times = iv_q8w_times,   obs_times = obs_grid )  tmpl_C <- make_regimen(   id_offset = 0,   regimen_label = "C: 10 mg/kg IV load + 5 mg/kg SC q2w",   iv_doses = dose_per_kg_iv_load_10,   iv_times = 0,   sc_doses = rep(dose_per_kg_sc_q2w_5, length(sc_q2w_times)),   sc_times = sc_q2w_times,   obs_times = obs_grid )  tmpl_D <- make_regimen(   id_offset = 0,   regimen_label = "D: 30 mg/kg IV load + 5 mg/kg SC q2w",   iv_doses = dose_per_kg_iv_load_30,   iv_times = 0,   sc_doses = rep(dose_per_kg_sc_q2w_5, length(sc_q2w_times)),   sc_times = sc_q2w_times,   obs_times = obs_grid )  events <- bind_rows(   expand_regimen(tmpl_A, n_per_arm, id_offset =   0L),   expand_regimen(tmpl_B, n_per_arm, id_offset =   n_per_arm),   expand_regimen(tmpl_C, n_per_arm, id_offset = 2*n_per_arm),   expand_regimen(tmpl_D, n_per_arm, id_offset = 3*n_per_arm) )  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
code: mod <- readModelDb("Huang_2017_vrc01")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod,   events = events,   keep   = c("regimen") ) |> as.data.frame() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: Huang 2017 Figure 5 displays simulated VRC01 serum concentrations four regimens ; lines medians shaded areas span 2.5th-97.5th percentiles 1000 simulated trials.
code: fig5_summary <- sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(     Q025 = quantile(Cc, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50  = quantile(Cc, 0.500, na.rm = TRUE),     Q975 = quantile(Cc, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig5_summary, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q025, ymax = Q975), alpha = 0.25, fill = "#4682b4") +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~regimen, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "VRC01 serum concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     title = "Figure 5 - simulated steady-state regimens",     caption = paste0(       "Replicates Figure 5 of Huang 2017. ",       "Lines are medians; shaded bands are 2.5th-97.5th percentiles ",       "across ", n_per_arm, " virtual subjects per regimen at WT = 74.5 kg."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 9))

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: compute steady-state NCA final 8-week dosing interval IV regimens (, B) final 2-week dosing interval SC regimens (C, D). Steady-state NCA last dosing interval. AUC Cav tau (8 weeks IV, 2 weeks SC); concentrations ug/mL, AUC ug*day/mL.
