code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, regimen)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, regimen)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(   sim_nca, Cc ~ time | regimen + id,   concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "day" )  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(   dose_df, amt ~ time | regimen + id,   doseu = "mg" ) last_dose_per_id <- dose_df |>   group_by(regimen, id) |>   summarise(t_last = max(time), .groups = "drop")  iv_q8w_last <- last_dose_per_id |>   filter(grepl("^A:|^B:", regimen)) |>   pull(t_last) |>   unique()  sc_q2w_last <- last_dose_per_id |>   filter(grepl("^C:|^D:", regimen)) |>   pull(t_last) |>   unique()  # IV q8w interval = [last dose, last dose + 56]; SC q2w interval = [last dose, last dose + 14] intervals <- bind_rows(   tibble(     start = iv_q8w_last, end = iv_q8w_last + 56,     cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE,     auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE   ) |>     mutate(regimen = "A: 10 mg/kg IV q8w") |>     bind_rows(       tibble(         start = iv_q8w_last, end = iv_q8w_last + 56,         cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE,         auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE       ) |>         mutate(regimen = "B: 30 mg/kg IV q8w")     ),   tibble(     start = sc_q2w_last, end = sc_q2w_last + 14,     cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE,     auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE   ) |>     mutate(regimen = "C: 10 mg/kg IV load + 5 mg/kg SC q2w") |>     bind_rows(       tibble(         start = sc_q2w_last, end = sc_q2w_last + 14,         cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE,         auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE       ) |>         mutate(regimen = "D: 30 mg/kg IV load + 5 mg/kg SC q2w")     ) )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) nca_tbl  <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) nca_summary <- nca_tbl |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "cmin", "auclast", "cav")) |>   group_by(regimen, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     Q025  = quantile(PPORRES, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     Q975  = quantile(PPORRES, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 3,   caption = paste0(     "Steady-state NCA on the last dosing interval. ",     "AUC and Cav are over tau (8 weeks for IV, 2 weeks for SC); ",     "concentrations in ug/mL, AUC in ug*day/mL."   ) )

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: Huang 2017 (Results paragraph, Figure 5, Discussion) reports median (95% prediction interval) steady-state trough levels : 10 mg/kg IV q8w: 5.54 (1.69, 14.5) ug/mL 30 mg/kg IV q8w: 15.9 (5.29, 46.63) ug/mL 5 mg/kg SC q2w IV loading dose: 34.10 (18.27, 65.34) ug/mL. published numbers simulated WT = 74.5 kg, value used . compare simulated cmin steady-state NCA. Steady-state trough (Cmin last tau). Published values come Results paragraph Figure 5 Huang 2017; SC trough applies 10 mg/kg 30 mg/kg IV loading Huang 2017 collapsed SC predictions across loading doses. IV-regimens (, B) reproduce published median trough within ~5% (well 20% flag threshold). SC regimens (C, D) reach slightly different median Huang 2017’s published SC prediction pools unspecified mix loading-dose conditions uses identical 34.1 ug/mL trough arms; simulated values track published 95% prediction interval.
code: published <- tibble::tribble(   ~regimen,                                       ~median_pub, ~lo_pub, ~hi_pub,   "A: 10 mg/kg IV q8w",                                   5.54,    1.69,   14.50,   "B: 30 mg/kg IV q8w",                                  15.90,    5.29,   46.63,   "C: 10 mg/kg IV load + 5 mg/kg SC q2w",                34.10,   18.27,   65.34,   "D: 30 mg/kg IV load + 5 mg/kg SC q2w",                34.10,   18.27,   65.34 )  simulated_trough <- nca_summary |>   filter(PPTESTCD == "cmin") |>   select(regimen, median_sim = median, lo_sim = Q025, hi_sim = Q975)  trough_compare <- published |>   left_join(simulated_trough, by = "regimen") |>   mutate(     pct_diff_median = 100 * (median_sim - median_pub) / median_pub   )  knitr::kable(   trough_compare,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste0(     "Steady-state trough (Cmin over the last tau). Published values come from ",     "the Results paragraph and Figure 5 of Huang 2017; the SC trough applies to ",     "both 10 mg/kg and 30 mg/kg IV loading because Huang 2017 collapsed the SC ",     "predictions across loading doses."   ) )

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: Huang 2017 reports estimated terminal half-life 15 days. check single 30 mg/kg IV simulation typical 74.5 kg subject random effects zeroed (typical-value half-life).
code: mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  ev_typ <- bind_rows(   tibble(id = 1L, time = 0,                  evid = 1L,          amt = 30 * 74.5, cmt = "central", WT = 74.5),   tibble(id = 1L, time = seq(0.01, 200, by = 0.5), evid = 0L,          amt = 0,         cmt = "central", WT = 74.5) )  sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, ev_typ) |> as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalvc'  # Fit terminal slope on the last 60 days of the typical-value profile late <- sim_typ |> filter(time >= 140, Cc > 0) fit  <- lm(log(Cc) ~ time, data = late) lambda_z <- -coef(fit)[["time"]] t_half_typ <- log(2) / lambda_z  cat(sprintf("Typical-value terminal half-life: %.1f days (Huang 2017 reports 15 days).\n",             t_half_typ)) #> Typical-value terminal half-life: 15.0 days (Huang 2017 reports 15 days).

title: Huang_2017_vrc01
text: Body weight held constant 74.5 kg. Huang 2017’s Figure 5 simulations used single typical weight; replicate choice. model supports weight distribution via WT covariate column, realistic virtual cohort draw distribution roughly bracketing 53-114 kg (HVTN 104 range). Race / ethnicity modeled. paper’s covariate selection identified body weight significant covariate final model; race / ethnicity covariate carried forward. Race composition also reported Table 1. F1 ka without IIV. Table 2 footnote 3 states inter-subject variance F1 ka fixed 0 due data sparseness SC absorption phase. model preserves constraint omitting etalka / etalfdepot. Vc covariance CL, Q, Vp. Table 2 reports covariances (CL, Q), (CL, Vp), (Q, Vp) pairs. model uses 3x3 correlated block etalcl/etalq/etalvp separate diagonal variance etalvc. Pooled-data parameters used. “Final model” parameters Table 2 pool IV + SC arms; IV-fit (Table S2) gives slightly different point estimates used . Inter-occasion variability modeled. Huang 2017 explored IOV 8-weekly groups concluded IIV affected IOV; final pooled model omits IOV (paper Results, paragraph 3 “popPK Modeling”). Residual error scale. Variances reported sigma1^2 = 0.042 sigma2^2 = 0.456 converted standard deviations (propSd, addSd) nlmixr2 add() + prop() parameterisation. External validation (VRC602) reproduced. vignette focuses final HVTN 104 model steady-state regimens shown Huang 2017 Figure 5. VRC602 individual predictions Figure 4 paper use --sample dataset packaged .

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: Citation: Hwang M, Chia YL, Zheng Y, Chen CC-K, J, Song X, Zhou D, Goldberg SB, Siu LL, Planchard D, Peters S, Mann H, Krug L, Even C. Population pharmacokinetic modelling tremelimumab patients advanced solid tumours impact disease status time-varying clearance. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2023;89(5):1601-1616. doi:[10.1111/bcp.15622](https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.15622) Description: Two-compartment population PK model tremelimumab (anti-CTLA-4 IgG2 kappa) regimen-dependent sigmoidal time-varying clearance adults advanced solid tumours, dosed monotherapy combination durvalumab. Modality: Therapeutic monoclonal antibody (IgG2 kappa), IV infusion. Tremelimumab fully human anti-CTLA-4 IgG2 kappa monoclonal antibody. Hwang 2022 pooled PK data five Phase 1 / 2 studies (956 patients, 4,043 PK records exclusions) externally validated four additional Phase 2 / 3 studies (554 patients). final model linear two-compartment IV model first-order elimination sigmoidal time-varying clearance term whose direction depends therapy regimen: clearance increases time monotherapy decreases time tremelimumab co-administered durvalumab. Baseline body weight (power) baseline serum albumin (power) retained continuous covariates clearance, body weight also retained central volume. structural model : CLi(t)=0.276L/day⋅exp⁡((Tmax⁡,r+ηTmax,)tλrTC50λr+tλr)⋅(ALBi39)−0.996⋅(WTi71)0.638⋅exp⁡(ηCL,) \mathrm{CL}_{}(t) \;=\; 0.276\;\mathrm{L/day} \cdot   \exp\!\left( \dfrac{(T_{\max,r} + \eta_{T_{\max},})\, t^{\lambda_{r}}}                      {\mathrm{TC}_{50}^{\lambda_{r}} + t^{\lambda_{r}}} \right)   \cdot   \left(\dfrac{\mathrm{ALB}_{}}{39}\right)^{-0.996}   \cdot   \left(\dfrac{\mathrm{WT}_{}}{71}\right)^{0.638}   \cdot   \exp(\eta_{\mathrm{CL},}) Vc,=3.64L⋅(WTi71)0.606⋅exp⁡(ηVc,),Q=0.357L/day,Vp=2.40L V_{c,} \;=\; 3.64\;\mathrm{L} \cdot   \left(\dfrac{\mathrm{WT}_{}}{71}\right)^{0.606}   \cdot   \exp(\eta_{V_{c},}), \qquad Q = 0.357\;\mathrm{L/day}, \qquad V_{p} = 2.40\;\mathrm{L} regimen-specific (Tmax⁡,mono,λmono)=(0.151,14.5)(T_{\max,\,\mathrm{mono}}, \lambda_{\mathrm{mono}}) = (0.151, 14.5) (Tmax⁡,combo,λcombo)=(−0.187,3.20)(T_{\max,\,\mathrm{combo}}, \lambda_{\mathrm{combo}}) = (-0.187, 3.20) common TC50=95.1days\mathrm{TC}_{50} = 95.1\;\mathrm{days} (Hwang 2022 Table 3 final-model equations page 1609).

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: final-model population comprised 956 patients (development dataset; Table 2) pooled five trials: Study 02 (NCT01938612, n = 122 enrolled / 110 included): combination durvalumab biliary tract cancer, oesophageal cancer, squamous cell carcinoma head neck. Study 06 (NCT02000947, n = 351 / 197): combination durvalumab non-small-cell lung cancer. Study 10 (NCT02261220, n = 372 / 279): combination durvalumab urothelial cancer solid tumours. DETERMINE (NCT01843374, n = 374 / 326): tremelimumab monotherapy pleural / peritoneal malignant mesothelioma. D4884C00001 (NCT02527434, n = 61 / 46): tremelimumab monotherapy (750 mg flat) combination durvalumab (75 mg flat) urothelial, triple-negative breast, pancreatic ductal cancers. Baseline demographics (Hwang 2022 Table 2): Age 65 (range 22-87) years; weight 71.6 (35.5-149.0) kg; BMI 25.2 (13.8-50.9). Sex: 63.2% male, 36.8% female. Race: 75.5% White, 19.3% Asian, 1.4% Black, 3.9% . Country: Japan 6.7%, Korea 7.7%, 85.6%. Treatment: tremelimumab monotherapy 38.8%, combination durvalumab 61.2%. ECOG performance status: 0 = 38.9%, 1 = 60.9%, 2 = 0.1%. Median tumour size 73 mm (10-668); median baseline serum albumin 39 g/L (15-52); median baseline LDH 209.5 U/L. Postbaseline ADA-positive: 6.1% overall (2.8% monotherapy, 7.8% combination). External validation (n = 554) used four phase 2 / 3 studies (ARCTIC, EAGLE, CONDOR, MYSTIC) part parameter estimation; vignette therefore simulates development-dataset regimens. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Hwang_2022_tremelimumab")$population.

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hwang_2022_tremelimumab.R. table collects one place review. Equations: structural two-compartment micro-constant form (NM-TRAN ADVAN6); full final-model equations CL_i Vc_i reproduced verbatim page 1609 Hwang 2022, time-varying multiplier EMPIR = (Tmax + ETA) * TIME^lambda / (TC50^lambda + TIME^lambda) taken supplement’s NONMEM control stream (Supplementary Results). Reference covariates (Hwang 2022 page 1609 final-model equations): WT = 71 kg, ALB = 39 g/L. two regimen-specific (Tmax, lambda) estimates share common TC50.

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: published errata corrigenda located Hwang 2022 (PubMed search, Crossref relations, OpenAlex retraction status; query 2026-04-27). One minor narrative-vs-table discrepancy noted Assumptions deviations: paper’s prose page 1609 cites Tmax = 0.157 monotherapy regimen, Table 3 reports 0.151 ± 0.00405. Table 3 value used per skill’s preference results-table point estimates narrative summaries.

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: Original observed PK data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose demographics approximate pooled Hwang 2022 development population (Table 2). Continuous covariates drawn log-normal / normal shapes anchored reported median range; binary / categorical covariates match reported marginal distributions. Two reference dosing regimens (predominant regimens Hwang 2022 development dataset, Table 1) simulated: 1 mg/kg Q4W combination durvalumab 4 doses (combination regimen used Studies 02, 06, 10, ARCTIC, EAGLE, MYSTIC, CONDOR). 10 mg/kg Q4W monotherapy 7 doses (monotherapy regimen used DETERMINE, ARCTIC, CONDOR).
code: set.seed(2022) n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id          = seq_len(n_subj),   WT          = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(71.6), 0.22), 35.5), 149.0),   ALB         = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 39, sd = 5), 15), 52),   COMBO_DURVA = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.612) ) summary(cohort[, c("WT", "ALB", "COMBO_DURVA")]) #>        WT              ALB         COMBO_DURVA   #>  Min.   : 37.82   Min.   :26.73   Min.   :0.00   #>  1st Qu.: 59.51   1st Qu.:35.37   1st Qu.:0.00   #>  Median : 72.33   Median :38.44   Median :1.00   #>  Mean   : 72.94   Mean   :38.70   Mean   :0.61   #>  3rd Qu.: 84.35   3rd Qu.:41.75   3rd Qu.:1.00   #>  Max.   :135.14   Max.   :52.00   Max.   :1.00 make_events <- function(pop, regimen) {   if (regimen == "1 mg/kg + durva") {     pop$COMBO_DURVA <- 1L     dose_times <- seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 4)     mg_per_kg  <- 1   } else if (regimen == "10 mg/kg mono") {     pop$COMBO_DURVA <- 0L     dose_times <- seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 7)     mg_per_kg  <- 10   } else stop("unknown regimen")    amt_per_subj <- pop$WT * mg_per_kg   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(AMT = amt_per_subj) |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>     mutate(EVID = 1L, CMT = "central", DUR = 1 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = regimen)   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = sort(unique(c(dose_times,                                          seq(0, 365, by = 3))))) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0L, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,            DV = NA_real_, treatment = regimen)   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events_combo <- make_events(cohort, "1 mg/kg + durva") events_mono  <- make_events(cohort, "10 mg/kg mono")

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hwang_2022_tremelimumab")  sim_combo <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_combo,                              returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_mono  <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_mono,                              returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_combo, treatment = "1 mg/kg + durva"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_mono,  treatment = "10 mg/kg mono") )

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: Hwang 2022 Figure 3 shows model-based simulations stratified regimen: panel 1 mg/kg combination arm panel B 10 mg/kg monotherapy arm, 95% prediction interval. plot reproduces median 5-95% prediction interval packaged model.
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary,        aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Tremelimumab concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     title = "Simulated tremelimumab PK by regimen",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ",                       n_subj, " virtual patients per arm). ",                       "Replicates Figure 3 of Hwang 2022."),     caption = "Model: Hwang 2022 Br J Clin Pharmacol 89(5):1601-1616"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: Hwang 2022 reports , one year dosing, typical-value CL increases ~16% monotherapy decreases ~17% combination arm (page 1609, Discussion time-varying CL). typical-value CL(t) / CL(0) curves reproduce trends reference subject (WT = 71 kg, ALB = 39 g/L, etas = 0).  horizontal asymptotes (exp(Tmax_mono) = 1.163 exp(Tmax_combo) = 0.829) match Hwang 2022’s reported ~16% increase / ~17% decrease one year (page 1609).
code: typ_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5)  build_typ_events <- function(combo) {   data.frame(     id          = 1L,     WT          = 71,     ALB         = 39,     COMBO_DURVA = combo,     TIME        = c(0, typ_grid),     AMT         = c(71, rep(NA_real_, length(typ_grid))),     EVID        = c(1L, rep(0L, length(typ_grid))),     CMT         = "central",     DUR         = c(1 / 24, rep(NA_real_, length(typ_grid))),     DV          = NA_real_   ) }  mod_typ  <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ_mono  <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = build_typ_events(0),                                  returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etacl_tmax' sim_typ_combo <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = build_typ_events(1),                                  returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etacl_tmax'  cl_curves <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_typ_mono,  regimen = "Monotherapy"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_typ_combo, regimen = "Combination with durvalumab") ) |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen) |>   dplyr::mutate(cl_ratio = cl / cl[1]) |>   dplyr::ungroup()  ggplot(cl_curves, aes(time, cl_ratio, colour = regimen)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "CL(t) / CL(0)",     title = "Time-varying clearance in Hwang 2022",     subtitle = paste0("Typical reference subject (WT = 71 kg, ALB = 39 g/L). ",                       "Asymptotes: monotherapy exp(0.151) = 1.163 (+16.3%); ",                       "combination exp(-0.187) = 0.829 (-17.1%).")   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: NCA computed first dosing interval regimen. Hwang 2022 Figure 1 reports dose-normalized Cmax Cmin Cycle 1; simulated values overlap medians dose-normalized 75 mg. Simulated Cycle-1 NCA parameters regimen (Hwang 2022)
code: nca_window <- 28  # days; one Q4W cycle  # Concentrations: Cycle-1 only (TIME within [0, 28]) sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time <= nca_window) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,                              concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "day")  dose_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(   events_combo |> dplyr::filter(EVID == 1, TIME == 0) |>     dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = TIME, amt = AMT,                      treatment = "1 mg/kg + durva"),   events_mono  |> dplyr::filter(EVID == 1, TIME == 0) |>     dplyr::transmute(id = id, time = TIME, amt = AMT,                      treatment = "10 mg/kg mono") )  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = nca_window,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           74% |  ETA:  1s  knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   caption = "Simulated Cycle-1 NCA parameters by regimen (Hwang 2022)" )

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: Hwang 2022 reports Cycle-1 dose-normalized Cmax Cmin medians 75 mg dose-equivalent (Figure 1 narrative summary, page 1607). table converts simulated medians 75 mg-equivalent basis (.e., Cmax_75 = Cmax_observed * 75 / dose_observed) places next paper’s reported medians. Hwang 2022 also reports typical-value half-life approximately 18 days (page 1609 narrative, derived baseline CL = 0.276 L/day total V_ss = Vc + Vp = 6.04 L). packaged-model implied half-life typical reference subject :
code: nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result)  med_by <- nca_tbl |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "cmin")) |>   dplyr::left_join(     dplyr::bind_rows(       events_combo |> dplyr::filter(EVID == 1, TIME == 0) |>         dplyr::transmute(id, treatment = "1 mg/kg + durva", dose_mg = AMT),       events_mono  |> dplyr::filter(EVID == 1, TIME == 0) |>         dplyr::transmute(id, treatment = "10 mg/kg mono",  dose_mg = AMT)     ),     by = c("id", "treatment")   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(value_75 = PPORRES * 75 / dose_mg) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   dplyr::summarise(median_75 = median(value_75, na.rm = TRUE),                    .groups = "drop") |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = median_75)  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment   = c("1 mg/kg + durva", "10 mg/kg mono"),   cmax_pub    = c(21.8, 21.8),  # Hwang 2022 Figure 1B caption: dose-normalized Cmax median (across regimens, dose-normalized to 75 mg)   cmin_pub    = c(3.3,  3.3)    # Hwang 2022 Figure 1A caption: dose-normalized Cmin median )  comparison <- dplyr::left_join(med_by, published, by = "treatment") |>   dplyr::transmute(     treatment,     `Median Cmax (75 mg-eq, ug/mL) - simulated` = round(cmax, 2),     `Median Cmax (75 mg-eq, ug/mL) - Hwang 2022 Fig 1B` = cmax_pub,     `Median Cmin (75 mg-eq, ug/mL) - simulated` = round(cmin, 2),     `Median Cmin (75 mg-eq, ug/mL) - Hwang 2022 Fig 1A` = cmin_pub   )  knitr::kable(comparison) vss <- 3.64 + 2.40   # Vc + Vp at WT = 71 kg reference cl0 <- 0.276 t_half <- log(2) * vss / cl0 cat(sprintf("Typical-subject terminal half-life = %.1f days\n", t_half)) #> Typical-subject terminal half-life = 15.2 days

title: Hwang_2022_tremelimumab
text: Time-varying clearance parameterization. Hwang 2022 page 1609 reports EMPIR = (Tmax + eta_Tmax) / ((TC50 / TIME)^lambda + 1), supplement’s NONMEM control stream uses algebraically identical form EMPIR = (Tmax + eta_Tmax) * TIME^lambda / (TC50^lambda + TIME^lambda). packaged model uses control-stream form avoid division TIME t = 0. TIME -> 0, EMPIR -> 0 CL(t) -> CL_base; TIME -> Inf, EMPIR -> Tmax + eta_Tmax. two regimens share common TC50 single shared additive eta Tmax (NONMEM control stream: ETA(3) added whichever THETA active per (COMB.EQ.0) / (COMB.EQ.1) switch). IIV correlation CL Vc. Hwang 2022’s NONMEM control stream declares ETA(1) CL ETA(2) Vc $OMEGA BLOCK(2) (correlated random effects), Hwang 2022 Table 3 report final -diagonal estimate (diagonal omega^2 values CL Vc). packaged model uses diagonal IIV CL Vc omits -diagonal correlation avoid hard-coding unpublished initial-estimate value. Reviewers fitting individual data may want add -diagonal back free parameter. CV% reporting. Hwang 2022 Table 3 reports IIV two columns: omega^2 estimate (variance internal log-normal scale) “CV%” value equals sqrt(omega^2) * 100 (common short-hand log-normal IIV exact small variances; formula CV = sqrt(exp(omega^2) - 1) give 34.6% / 23.5% / 40.4% rather 33.6% / 23.2% / 38.9% reported). packaged model uses omega^2 values verbatim; “CV%” column reproduced cross-reference paper’s table. Tmax (monotherapy) value. Hwang 2022 page 1609 narrative reports Tmax = 0.157 monotherapy, Table 3 reports 0.151 ± 0.00405. Table 3 point estimate used per skill’s preference results-table values narrative summaries. typical reference subject, resulting one-year typical-value CL increase exp(0.151) - 1 = 16.3%, consistent Hwang 2022’s reported “approximately 16%” (page 1609). Convention deviation: etacl_tmax IIV. shared additive eta regimen-active Tmax pair single fixed-effect parameter named cl_tmax ini(); instead, two regimen-specific cl_tmax_mono cl_tmax_combo fixed effects mixed runtime COMBO_DURVA. nlmixr2lib::checkModelConventions() flags parameter_naming warning. structure faithfully encodes Hwang 2022’s NONMEM control stream changed. Convention info: dosing vs. concentration units. units declares dosing mg concentration ug/mL. model file uses central (mg) divided vc (L) produce Cc mg/L = ug/mL, scaling factor needed. checkModelConventions() raises informational note magnitude difference; expected. Race / ethnicity covariate. Hwang 2022 reports race / ethnicity Table 2 retain race significant covariate final model (page 1609); per paper, “Asian patients tended lower bodyweight; however, weight-adjusted clearance patients similar overall population, indicating difference based race/ethnicity.” Race therefore implemented packaged model. ECOG performance status. Hwang 2022 reports ECOG PS Table 2 retain ECOG significant covariate final model (page 1609). implemented packaged model. ADA covariate. Postbaseline ADA-positive incidence reported Hwang 2022 (page 1607: 6.1% overall, 2.8% monotherapy, 7.8% combination), ADA tested excluded final model (“covariates … found significant impact”, page 1609). implemented packaged model. Albumin imputation. Hwang 2022 Table 2 footnote e notes physiologically infeasible baseline albumin values imputed population median. packaged model uses ALB face value; user datasets include imputation flag map typical population median (39 g/L) passing . Virtual cohort. Demographics simulated match marginal distributions reported Hwang 2022 Table 2. WT log-normal anchored median 71.6 kg shape chosen approximate IQR; ALB normal anchored median 39 g/L SD 5 g/L (reasonable anchor 15-52 g/L range); COMBO_DURVA matches 61.2% combination prevalence. Joint covariate structure (WT--sex, WT--region, ALB--ECOG) simulated. External validation cohorts. Four phase 2 / 3 studies (ARCTIC, EAGLE, CONDOR, MYSTIC) used Hwang 2022 external validation development-dataset model. contributed parameter estimates, vignette simulate . population summary model file documents development-dataset population (956 patients). IV infusion duration. simulations use 1-hour infusion (DUR = 1/24 day). Hwang 2022 state infusion duration explicitly methods pooled-analysis paper; Q4W IV schedule taken constituent trials.

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: Citation: Hwang M, Fan C, Yue MS, Zhou D, Paturel C, Andre P, Cheng L-Y, Mitchell P, Kourtesis P, Ruscica D, Das M, Morsli N, Ren S, Gibbs M, Phipps , Song X. Population Pharmacokinetics Monalizumab Patients Advanced Solid Tumors. J Clin Pharmacol. 2023;63(7):818-829. doi:[10.1002/jcph.2220](https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.2220) Description: Two-compartment population PK model monalizumab (anti-CD94/NKG2A IgG4) linear (first-order) elimination patients advanced solid tumors squamous cell carcinoma head neck. Modality: Therapeutic monoclonal antibody (humanized IgG4), IV infusion. Monalizumab first--class humanized anti-CD94/NKG2A IgG4 immune checkpoint inhibitor. Hwang 2023 reports population PK analysis supporting clinical development across solid-tumor head--neck indications. Structure: linear two-compartment IV model first-order elimination central compartment. Five covariate effects retained final model (Hwang 2023 Table 2): power-form effects baseline body weight (BLWT) baseline serum albumin (BALB) CL V1, proportional-shift effects female sex, current-smoker status, never-smoker status V1. Smoking 3-level categorical (never / former / current) former smoker implicit reference; 3-level encoding realised paired SMOKE_CURRENT / SMOKE_NEVER indicator pair (= 0 yields former-smoker reference). covariate equations Hwang 2023 Methods ‘Covariate Considerations’ (p. 820) : Pki=θk⋅(XijM(Xj))θj(continuous),Pki=θk⋅(1+θj)Xij(categorical) P_{ki} \;=\; \theta_k \cdot \left(\dfrac{X_{ij}}{M(X_j)}\right)^{\theta_j} \quad \text{(continuous)}, \qquad P_{ki} \;=\; \theta_k \cdot \left(1 + \theta_j\right)^{X_{ij}} \quad \text{(categorical)} reference values BLWT median = 70.6 kg BALB median = 3.80 g/dL (Hwang 2023 Table 1, p. 821).

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: final-model dataset comprised 507 patients (2,842 PK observations) pooled two studies (Hwang 2023 Methods ‘Pharmacokinetic Data Set’, p. 823): Study D419NC00001 (NCT02671435; N = 369): phase 1/2 dose-escalation / expansion / exploration monalizumab 22.5-750 mg IV Q2W 750 mg IV Q4W advanced MSS colorectal cancer, ovarian cancer, MSS endometrial cancer, NSCLC, cervical cancer, pancreatic adenocarcinoma, alone combination durvalumab (chemotherapy part 3). Study IPH2201-203 (NCT02643550; N = 138): phase 1b/2 dose-escalation / expansion monalizumab 0.4-10 mg/kg Q2W 750 mg / 1500 mg flat dose pretreated recurrent / metastatic squamous cell carcinoma head neck, combination cetuximab / durvalumab. Baseline demographics (Hwang 2023 Table 1, pp. 821-822): Age: median 60.0 years (range 23.0-91.0). Body weight: median 70.6 kg (range 37.4-154). Albumin: median 3.80 g/dL (range 1.80-4.90). Sex: 63.1% male, 36.9% female. Race: 66.9% White, 11.0% Asian, 5.1% Black African American, 3.2% , 0.2% American Indian Alaskan Native, 13.6% missing. Smoking status: 62.9% former, 28.4% current, 6.5% never, 2.2% missing. ECOG performance status: 0 (43.2%), 1 (56.4%), missing (0.4%). Tumor type: MSS colorectal 48.1%, head--neck SCC 27.2%, ovarian 8.5%, MSS endometrial 8.5%, NSCLC 3.9%, cervical 3.2%, prostate 0.6%. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Hwang_2023_monalizumab")$population.

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Hwang_2023_monalizumab.R. table collects one place review. values come Hwang 2023 Table 2 (p. 821, “Summary Final Model Pharmacokinetic Parameters Monalizumab”). Equations (Hwang 2023 Methods, p. 820): Structural: 2-compartment, first-order elimination, IV input (Methods ‘Base Model Development’ Results ‘Base Structural Model’, p. 823). IIV: log-normal theta_ki = theta_k * exp(eta_ki) (Methods, p. 820). Residual error: combined additive + proportional, Y_ij = C_ij * (1 + eps1_ij) + eps2_ij (Methods ‘Base Model Development’, p. 820). Covariate forms: continuous power categorical proportional-shift (Methods ‘Covariate Considerations’, p. 820, two equations reproduced ).

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate pooled Hwang 2023 population (Table 1). two reference dosing regimens compared 750 mg flat IV Q2W (dominant regimen across studies; Supplementary Table S1 Hwang 2023) 10 mg/kg IV Q2W (highest weight-based dose IPH2201-203 expansion cohort 1). regimen simulated six 1-hour infusions 12 weeks (steady-state approached IgG mAb PK duration).
code: set.seed(2023) n_subj <- 200  # Smoking status: former (62.9%), current (28.4%), never (6.5%); the 2.2% # missing in the source dataset is reassigned to the most-common 'former' # category, matching the convention described in Methods 'Handling of # Missing and Erroneous Data' (p. 819) for missing categorical covariates. smoke_status <- sample(   c("former", "current", "never"),   size = n_subj, replace = TRUE,   prob = c(0.629 + 0.022, 0.284, 0.065) )  cohort <- tibble(   ID            = seq_len(n_subj),   WT            = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(73.3) - 0.5 * 0.249^2, 0.249), 37.4), 154),   ALB           = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 3.76, 0.507), 1.80), 4.90),   SEXF          = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.369),   SMOKE_CURRENT = as.integer(smoke_status == "current"),   SMOKE_NEVER   = as.integer(smoke_status == "never") ) stopifnot(!any(cohort$SMOKE_CURRENT == 1 & cohort$SMOKE_NEVER == 1)) dose_interval_d <- 14 n_doses         <- 6 dose_times_d    <- seq(0, by = dose_interval_d, length.out = n_doses) obs_times_d     <- sort(unique(c(dose_times_d, seq(0, 84, by = 1))))  build_events <- function(pop, regimen) {   amt_per_subject <- if (regimen == "750 mg Q2W") {     rep(750, nrow(pop))   } else {     pop$WT * 10   }   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(AMT = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times_d) |>     mutate(EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 1 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = regimen)   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_d) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,            DV = NA_real_, treatment = regimen)   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events_750  <- build_events(cohort, "750 mg Q2W") events_10mg <- build_events(cohort, "10 mg/kg Q2W")

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hwang_2023_monalizumab") sim_750  <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_750,  returnType = "data.frame") sim_10mg <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_10mg, returnType = "data.frame") sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_750,  treatment = "750 mg Q2W"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_10mg, treatment = "10 mg/kg Q2W") )

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: Hwang 2023 Figure 2 shows prediction-corrected VPC final model across pooled study population. figure reproduces typical-value median 5-95% prediction interval packaged model two reference regimens, analogous shaded bands paper’s VPC.
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Monalizumab concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Simulated monalizumab PK: 750 mg flat vs 10 mg/kg Q2W",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ", n_subj,                       " virtual patients per arm)."),     caption = "Replicates the dose-stratified prediction structure of Hwang 2023 Figure 2."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: Hwang 2023 Figure 4 forest plot showing change steady-state AUC Cmax 5th 95th percentiles significant covariate, relative typical patient. covariate magnitudes reported Discussion (p. 826): Body weight: 95th percentile (108 kg) gives -24% AUC; 5th percentile (49.1 kg) gives +24% AUC. Albumin: 5th percentile (2.8 g/dL) gives -27% AUC. table reproduces typical-value AUC ratios packaged model. Replicates Figure 4 Hwang 2023: steady-state AUC Cmax sensitivity baseline covariates. 95th-percentile WT (108 kg) result approximately -24% AUC 5th-percentile ALB (2.8 g/dL) result approximately -27% AUC reproduce magnitudes reported Hwang 2023 Discussion (p. 826: “maximum change -24% -27% monalizumab AUC steady state 95th percentile observed body weight (108 kg) 5th percentile observed albumin value (2.8 g/dL), respectively”).
code: mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod)  # Linear-trapezoidal AUC (kept inline to avoid an extra package dependency). trapz_auc <- function(x, y) {   sum(diff(x) * (utils::head(y, -1) + utils::tail(y, -1)) / 2) }  eval_steady_state <- function(WT, ALB, SEXF, SMOKE_CURRENT, SMOKE_NEVER,                               regimen_amt = 750) {   events_typ <- data.frame(     ID            = 1L,     WT            = WT, ALB = ALB, SEXF = SEXF,     SMOKE_CURRENT = SMOKE_CURRENT, SMOKE_NEVER = SMOKE_NEVER,     TIME          = c(dose_times_d, obs_times_d),     AMT           = c(rep(regimen_amt, length(dose_times_d)),                        rep(NA_real_, length(obs_times_d))),     EVID          = c(rep(1L, length(dose_times_d)),                        rep(0L, length(obs_times_d))),     CMT           = "central",     DUR           = c(rep(1 / 24, length(dose_times_d)),                        rep(NA_real_, length(obs_times_d))),     DV            = NA_real_   ) |>     dplyr::arrange(TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID))   s <- rxSolve(mod_typ, events = events_typ, returnType = "data.frame")   ss_window <- s[s$time >= dose_times_d[length(dose_times_d) - 1] &                    s$time <= dose_times_d[length(dose_times_d) - 1] + 14, ]   list(     auc_ss  = trapz_auc(ss_window$time, ss_window$Cc),     cmax_ss = max(ss_window$Cc, na.rm = TRUE)   ) }  ref <- eval_steady_state(WT = 70.6, ALB = 3.80, SEXF = 0,                          SMOKE_CURRENT = 0, SMOKE_NEVER = 0) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp'  scenarios <- tibble::tribble(   ~scenario,                  ~WT,   ~ALB, ~SEXF, ~SMOKE_CURRENT, ~SMOKE_NEVER,   "WT = 49.1 kg (5th pctl)",  49.1,  3.80, 0,     0,              0,   "WT = 108 kg (95th pctl)",  108,   3.80, 0,     0,              0,   "ALB = 2.8 g/dL (5th pctl)",70.6,  2.8,  0,     0,              0,   "ALB = 4.6 g/dL (95th pctl)",70.6, 4.6,  0,     0,              0,   "Female",                   70.6,  3.80, 1,     0,              0,   "Current smoker",           70.6,  3.80, 0,     1,              0,   "Never smoker",             70.6,  3.80, 0,     0,              1 ) |>   rowwise() |>   mutate(     sim       = list(eval_steady_state(WT, ALB, SEXF, SMOKE_CURRENT, SMOKE_NEVER)),     auc_ss    = sim$auc_ss,     cmax_ss   = sim$cmax_ss,     auc_pct   = 100 * (auc_ss  / ref$auc_ss  - 1),     cmax_pct  = 100 * (cmax_ss / ref$cmax_ss - 1)   ) |>   ungroup() |>   select(scenario, auc_pct, cmax_pct) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp'  knitr::kable(   scenarios, digits = 1,   col.names = c("Scenario vs. typical (70.6 kg, 3.80 g/dL, male, former smoker)",                 "AUC change (%)", "Cmax change (%)"),   caption = "Replicates Figure 4 of Hwang 2023: steady-state AUC and Cmax sensitivity to baseline covariates." )

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: Compute NCA parameters 5th--6th dosing interval 750 mg Q2W 10 mg/kg Q2W. Hwang 2023 publish head--head NCA table, report (Discussion, p. 818) Cmax monalizumab “increased approximately dose-proportional manner” “1.4- 2-fold accumulation monalizumab administration 4 IV doses every 2 weeks”, consistent half-life 2-3 weeks seen single IV dose. PKNCA output provides simulated Cmax, AUC, half-life cross-checking textual reports. Simulated NCA parameters near-steady-state (5th dosing interval, days 56-70).
code: # Use the 5th dosing interval (days 56-70) as a steady-state approximation. interval_start <- dose_times_d[5] interval_end   <- interval_start + dose_interval_d  sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc),                 time >= interval_start,                 time <= interval_end) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - interval_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, Cc)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id,                              concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day")  dose_df <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time == interval_start, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(.groups = "drop") |>   dplyr::left_join(cohort |> dplyr::select(id = ID, WT), by = "id") |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt      = ifelse(treatment == "750 mg Q2W", 750, WT * 10),     time_rel = 0   ) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, amt)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = dose_interval_d,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   cmin      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■                        30% |  ETA:  6s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■            69% |  ETA:  3s knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   caption = "Simulated NCA parameters at near-steady-state (5th dosing interval, days 56-70)." )

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: Hwang 2023 publish pooled NCA table; comparisons relate simulated steady-state metrics qualitative statements paper. packaged model pure linear / first-order disposition; target-mediated saturable clearance included, line Hwang 2023’s finding “inclusion nonlinear PK incorporation time-varying CL also evaluated applied final model” (Discussion, p. 826).

title: Hwang_2023_monalizumab
text: Reference values continuous covariates. Hwang 2023 Methods (p. 820) specifies median value covariate reference. Table 1 reports overall medians 70.6 kg (BLWT) 3.80 g/dL (BALB); model file uses medians explicitly. 3-level smoking-status encoding. Hwang 2023 Table 2 retains two smoking-status coefficients V1: current-smoker effect (+0.0484) never-smoker effect (-0.141), former smoker (n = 319/507) implicit reference. packaged model represents categorical paired SMOKE_CURRENT / SMOKE_NEVER indicator pair following RACE_<GROUP> convention; indicators = 0 means subject former smoker, reference category. Setting indicators 1 simultaneously invalid (subject exactly one never / former / current). new canonical entries documented inst/references/covariate-columns.md. Sex encoding. Hwang 2023 Table 2 reports V1 coefficient women (0.111). packaged model stores sex canonical SEXF column (1 = female, 0 = male). paper’s reference category male, matches canonical convention; value transformation coefficient sign-flip needed. Albumin units. Hwang 2023 reports albumin g/dL throughout Table 1 (range 1.80-4.90 g/dL). packaged model uses g/dL reference 3.80 g/dL. nlmixr2lib models use albumin store either g/dL g/L; per-model covariateData[[ALB]]$units field load-bearing set "g/dL" . Residual error storage. Hwang 2023 Table 2 reports residual-error variances (Variance proportional error = 0.0772, Variance additive error = 0.00766); packaged model uses standard deviations expected nlmixr2’s add() / prop() syntax, .e., propSd = sqrt(0.0772) = 0.278 addSd  = sqrt(0.00766) = 0.0875 mg/L. IIV Q. Hwang 2023 Results (p. 823) explicitly states IIV intercompartmental clearance supported data; Q therefore fixed-effect-parameter packaged model. --scope covariates. paper screened many additional covariates (baseline ALP, ALT, AST, total bilirubin, CrCl, LDH, age, ECOG, ADA, race) retain final model (Results ‘Covariate Selection’, p. 823). five covariates reported Table 2 implemented . ADA covariate. Hwang 2023 reports large fraction missing ADA data (~38% study IPH2201-203, still ongoing data cutoff). “missing” separate category, ADA tested retained final-model covariate (Discussion, p. 826). Race covariate. Hwang 2023 explicitly examined East-Asian vs. non-East-Asian patients (Discussion, Figure 5) found largely overlapping trough end--infusion concentrations; race final model implemented . Virtual cohort. Continuous covariates (WT, ALB) drawn log-normal / normal distributions anchored reported mean SD; binary / categorical covariates (SEXF, smoking status) match reported marginals. Joint covariate structure simulated. 2.2% smoking-status missing fraction reassigned -common ‘former’ category, matching paper’s convention missing categorical covariates (Methods ‘Handling Missing Erroneous Data’, p. 819). IV infusion duration. simulations use 1-hour infusion (DUR = 1/24 day). Hwang 2023 specify infusion duration population-PK methods; 1 hour standard IgG mAbs listed clinical trials. Errata. errata corrigenda identified Hwang 2023 via PubMed eLink (pubmed_pubmed_corrections) PubMed search Hwang monalizumab erratum 2026-04-28.

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: Citation: Ide T, Roy , Imai Y, Vezina . Model-Based Determination Elotuzumab Pharmacokinetics Japanese Patients Multiple Myeloma Incorporating Time-Varying M Protein. J Clin Pharmacol. 2021;61(1):64-73. doi:10.1002/jcph.1698 Description: Two-compartment population PK model elotuzumab (anti-SLAMF7 humanized IgG1) Japanese non-Japanese patients multiple myeloma (Ide 2020); parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination central compartment plus second-order target-mediated elimination peripheral compartment driven non-renewable target pool, time-varying serum M protein Vmax. Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.1698 Elotuzumab humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody SLAMF7 (signaling lymphocytic activation molecule F7), used combination lenalidomide dexamethasone (Ld) — pomalidomide dexamethasone — relapsed refractory multiple myeloma. Ide 2020 refines previously published elotuzumab population PK model (Gibiansky 2014, Berdeja 2016) two changes: Time-varying serum M-protein replaces previous baseline-M-protein covariate. M-protein decreases treatment response, time-varying form better captures diminishing target-mediated- elimination component tumor burden regresses (Δ-OFV > 300 points relative baseline-M-protein model). Inclusion newly-diagnosed previously-untreated Japanese patients (n = 40 NCT02272803) extends prior R/R-dataset first-line cohort, enabling separate test “prior therapy” (LINE_1L) nonspecific clearance Vmax Michaelis-Menten target-mediated elimination. Structural form: linear two-compartment IV-input model parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination central compartment, plus additional second-order target-mediated elimination peripheral compartment driven non-renewable target pool (initial concentration RMAX). Non-renewable means target depletes monotonically drug binds — role pathway diminishes treatment time, consistent declining M-protein tumor burden. Mathematically (state variables: central [mg], peripheral1 [mg], target [μg/mL] peripheral target concentration): dcentraldt=−kelcentral−k12central+k21peripheral1−VmaxcentralCc+KM \frac{\mathrm{d}\,\mathrm{central}}{\mathrm{d}t}   = -\,k_{el}\,\mathrm{central}     -\,k_{12}\,\mathrm{central}     +\,k_{21}\,\mathrm{peripheral1}     -\frac{V_{\max}\,\mathrm{central}}{C_c + K_M} dperipheral1dt=k12central−k21peripheral1−kintperipheral1⋅target \frac{\mathrm{d}\,\mathrm{peripheral1}}{\mathrm{d}t}   =  k_{12}\,\mathrm{central}     -\,k_{21}\,\mathrm{peripheral1}     -\,k_{\mathrm{int}}\,\mathrm{peripheral1}\cdot\mathrm{target} dtargetdt=−kintperipheral1VPtarget,target(0)=Rmax \frac{\mathrm{d}\,\mathrm{target}}{\mathrm{d}t}   = -\,k_{\mathrm{int}}\,\frac{\mathrm{peripheral1}}{V_P}\,\mathrm{target}, \qquad \mathrm{target}(0) = R_{\max} Cc=central/VCC_c = \mathrm{central}/V_C, kel=CL/VCk_{el} = CL/V_C, k12=Q/VCk_{12} = Q/V_C, k21=Q/VPk_{21} = Q/V_P. mixed-units convention (drug amount, target concentration) one source paper specifies (supplement S2: “Division V_P required A_2 amount A_3 concentration”); rxode2 implementation preserves . covariate model includes 14 covariate-parameter relationships: 13 retained prior model plus new prior-line--therapy effect CL Vmax. Full equations parameter values model file (inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Ide_2020_elotuzumab.R); source-trace table collects one place.

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: model fit 8,125 elotuzumab serum concentrations 420 patients multiple myeloma (Ide 2020 Table 1) pooled five clinical studies (Ide 2020 supplement Table S1): NCT02272803 (phase 2; Japanese newly-diagnosed previously-untreated; 10 mg/kg weekly cycles 1-2 → 10 mg/kg Q2W cycles 3-18 → 20 mg/kg Q4W cycle 19; n = 40). Ld-arm cohort enriching analysis. NCT01239797 (ELOQUENT-2 phase 3; 10 mg/kg weekly cycles 1-2 → 10 mg/kg Q2W cycle 3). NCT01241292 (phase 1; 10 20 mg/kg weekly cycles 1-2 → Q2W cycle 3). NCT01393964 (phase 1; 10 mg/kg single dose cycle 1 day 1 → weekly cycles 2-3 → Q2W cycle 4). NCT01441973 (phase 2; elotuzumab monotherapy: Cohort 1 20 mg/kg Q4W cycle 2, Cohort 2 10 mg/kg weekly cycles 1-2 → Q2W cycle 3). Ld-free arm (COMBO_LEN_DEX = 0); provides data identifying 35 % CL-elevation 10-fold-KINT-reduction effects -Ld co-administration. 420 patients, 77 (18.3 %) Japanese; 80 (19.0 %) Asian. Sex 57.9 % male / 42.1 % female. Age 37-88 years (median 67). Body weight 33.8-150 kg (median 74.0). Coadministration Ld covered 92.6 % patients; residual 7.4 % NCT01441973 Ld-free monotherapy cohort. ECOG performance status 0 (51.7 %), 1 (41.0 %), 2 (7.4 %); hepatic impairment present 8.6 %; baseline eGFR mean 73.5 (SD 23.5) mL/min/1.73 m². Prior lines therapy: 0 (16.4 %), 1 (39.3 %), ≥ 2 (44.3 %). full population metadata available programmatically readModelDb("Ide_2020_elotuzumab")$population.

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Ide_2020_elotuzumab.R. table collects one place. Source-paper values Ide 2020 Table 2 unless noted; covariate-equation reference values Ide 2020 supplement S2 (PMID_32656777_supplement_6_trimmed.md); ODE structure Ide 2020 supplement 7 NONMEM control stream (PMID_32656777_supplement_7_trimmed.md).

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual reference patient virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Ide 2020 Table 1.
code: # Reference patient = supplement S2 reference covariate values (the values at # which the paper's typical CL_REF / VC_REF / etc. apply). Identical to the # Figure 1 reference-patient covariate panel. ref_pt <- list(   WT          = 75,   AGE         = 65,   SEXF        = 0,    # male   RACE_ASIAN  = 0,    # non-Asian   CRCL        = 100,  # mL/min/1.73 m^2   LDH         = 200,  # U/L   ALB         = 3.5,  # g/dL   B2M         = 1.0,  # < 2.0 mg/L (reference; paper uses thresholded indicators)   HEPIMP      = 0,    # normal liver function   ECOG_GE1    = 0,    # ECOG = 0   ECOG_GE2    = 0,   LINE_1L     = 0,    # >= 1 prior line of therapy   COMBO_LEN_DEX    = 1,    # with Ld coadministration   MCPROT      = 2.0   # 2.0 g/dL — Figure 1 reference (note: Vmax scaling reference is MCPROT = 0) ) set.seed(20260428L)  # Virtual cohort: 200 subjects with Table-1 baseline distributions. # Continuous covariates drawn from log-normals matched to reported mean/SD; # categoricals drawn at the reported prevalences. n_sub <- 200L  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id         = seq_len(n_sub),   WT         = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_sub, 74.3, 17.6), 33.8), 150),   AGE        = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_sub, 66.1, 9.66)), 37), 88),   SEXF       = rbinom(n_sub, 1, 0.421),   RACE_ASIAN = rbinom(n_sub, 1, 0.190),   # eGFR via truncated normal at the Table-1 mean/SD   CRCL       = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_sub, 73.5, 23.5), 4.58), 124),   # LDH skewed (Table 1: median 194 << mean 239; SD 146); use a log-normal   # parametrized by log-mean = log(239) - 0.5*sigma^2 with CV ~ 0.6   LDH        = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_sub, log(194), 0.5), 54), 1900),   ALB        = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_sub, 3.78, 0.581), 1.9), 5.0),   # B2M reported in Table 1 in mg/dL; convert to mg/L for the canonical column   B2M        = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_sub, log(0.330), 0.6) * 10, 0.4), 35),   HEPIMP     = rbinom(n_sub, 1, 0.086),   ECOG_score = sample(c(0L, 1L, 2L), n_sub, replace = TRUE,                       prob = c(0.517, 0.410, 0.074)),   LINE_1L    = rbinom(n_sub, 1, 0.164),   # Most patients (92.6%) have Ld coadministration   COMBO_LEN_DEX   = rbinom(n_sub, 1, 0.926) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     ECOG_GE1 = as.integer(ECOG_score >= 1),     ECOG_GE2 = as.integer(ECOG_score >= 2)   ) |>   dplyr::select(-ECOG_score)

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
code: mod         <- readModelDb("Ide_2020_elotuzumab") mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> Warning: No sigma parameters in the model  # Standard ELOQUENT-2 regimen for relapsed/refractory MM: #   10 mg/kg weekly for cycles 1-2 (8 doses on days 1, 8, 15, 22 of two #   28-day cycles), then 10 mg/kg every 2 weeks from cycle 3 onward. # Reference patient (75 kg) -> dose amount 750 mg. mg_per_kg     <- 10 weight_kg     <- ref_pt$WT dose_mg       <- mg_per_kg * weight_kg weeks_observe <- 24L         # to ~cycle 7 — covers steady-state attainment  # Construct dosing schedule manually so weekly cycles 1-2 transition cleanly # to Q2W cycles 3+. weekly_days <- c(0, 7, 14, 21, 28, 35, 42, 49)        # 8 weekly doses q2w_days    <- seq(56, weeks_observe * 7, by = 14)    # Q2W from week 8 dose_days   <- c(weekly_days, q2w_days)  events <- rxode2::et() for (d in dose_days) {   events <- events |> rxode2::et(amt = dose_mg, time = d, evid = 1L) } events <- events |>   rxode2::et(seq(0, weeks_observe * 7, by = 0.5))  # Reference-patient simulation - typical PK trajectory events_ref <- as.data.frame(events) for (nm in names(ref_pt)) events_ref[[nm]] <- ref_pt[[nm]]  sim_ref <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events_ref) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalrmax', 'etalkint', 'etalvmax', 'etalkm'

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: reference patient’s simulated concentration-time profile shows expected mAb elimination signature: initial peaks around 300-500 μg/mL, fast distribution peripheral compartment, slow elimination, accumulation across weekly doses (cycles 1-2) followed Q2W maintenance phase (cycle 3+). Reference-patient simulated concentration-time profile elotuzumab standard 10 mg/kg weekly cycles 1-2 -> Q2W cycles 3+ regimen. Vertical dashed lines mark cycle 1-2 / cycle 3+ boundary.
code: ggplot(sim_ref, aes(time / 7, Cc)) +   geom_line(color = "navy") +   geom_vline(xintercept = 8, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Elotuzumab Cc (μg/mL)") +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Time since first dose (weeks)",                      breaks = seq(0, weeks_observe, by = 4)) +   ggtitle("Reference patient — 10 mg/kg weekly cycles 1-2 then Q2W") +   theme_minimal()

what: Effect of time-varying M-protein on Vmax
title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: Time-varying serum M-protein central novelty Ide 2020. multiplicative factor exp(0.277 · MCPROT) Vmax means high baseline M-protein elevates target-mediated elimination, M-protein declines treatment response, Vmax decreases — slowing M-M elimination component time. figure replicates qualitative content Ide 2020 Figure 1 (covariate-effect forest plot, panel MCPROT) showing typical Cc trajectory three constant M-protein levels: 0 g/dL (Vmax reference), 2 g/dL (figure-1 reference patient ~ population median), 5 g/dL (high tumor burden, near population maximum 7.7 g/dL Table 1). Effect constant time-invariant M-protein elotuzumab Cc trajectory. High M-protein (5 g/dL) elevates Vmax via exp(0.277 × MCPROT) ≈ 4.0-fold MCPROT = 0, accelerating Michaelis-Menten elimination producing visibly lower Cc steady state.
code: mcprot_levels <- c(0, 2, 5) sim_mcprot <- lapply(mcprot_levels, function(mp) {   ev2 <- events_ref   ev2$MCPROT <- mp   rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev2) |>     as.data.frame() |>     dplyr::mutate(MCPROT = mp) }) |> dplyr::bind_rows() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalrmax', 'etalkint', 'etalvmax', 'etalkm' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalrmax', 'etalkint', 'etalvmax', 'etalkm' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalrmax', 'etalkint', 'etalvmax', 'etalkm'  ggplot(sim_mcprot, aes(time / 7, Cc, color = factor(MCPROT))) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_color_brewer("MCPROT (g/dL)", palette = "Set1") +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Time since first dose (weeks)",                      breaks = seq(0, weeks_observe, by = 4)) +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Elotuzumab Cc (μg/mL)") +   ggtitle("Effect of M-protein on elotuzumab exposure (typical-value)") +   theme_minimal()

what: Comparative steady-state exposures across covariate strata
title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: Figure 3 Ide 2020 reports model-predicted steady-state exposures (Cavg, Cmax, Cmin) cycle 12 prior line therapy, ethnicity, dosing regimen. simulated cohort approximates panel Asian vs non-Asian standard 10 mg/kg Q2W maintenance regimen; qualitative finding Ide 2020 (similar exposure Asian / non-Asian patients body weight differences accounted ) reproduced subject dosed weight. Steady-state Cavg, Cmin, Cmax cycle-12 dosing interval (weeks 22-24) Asian / non-Asian race. Replicates qualitative pattern Ide 2020 Figure 3 panel (ethnicity row): exposures similar across race body-weight accounted .
code: # Build dosing for the cohort: weight-based dose for each subject. cohort_events <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(cohort)), function(i) {   cov_i <- as.list(cohort[i, ])   ev_i  <- rxode2::et()   d_i   <- mg_per_kg * cov_i$WT   for (d in dose_days) ev_i <- ev_i |> rxode2::et(amt = d_i, time = d, evid = 1L)   ev_i <- ev_i |> rxode2::et(seq(0, weeks_observe * 7, by = 1))   ev_df <- as.data.frame(ev_i)   ev_df$id     <- cov_i$id   ev_df$MCPROT <- 2.0  # held constant at population median (2.1 -> rounded)   for (nm in setdiff(names(cov_i), "id")) ev_df[[nm]] <- cov_i[[nm]]   ev_df }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()  sim_cohort <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = cohort_events,                               keep = c("RACE_ASIAN", "COMBO_LEN_DEX",                                        "LINE_1L", "WT")) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalrmax', 'etalkint', 'etalvmax', 'etalkm' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega' ss_window_lo <- (24L - 2L) * 7  # cycle 12 dosing interval ~ weeks 22-24 ss_window_hi <- 24L * 7  ss <- sim_cohort |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_window_lo, time <= ss_window_hi) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, RACE_ASIAN) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Cavg = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     Cmax = max(Cc,  na.rm = TRUE),     Cmin = min(Cc,  na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(c(Cavg, Cmax, Cmin), names_to = "metric")  ggplot(ss, aes(factor(RACE_ASIAN, labels = c("non-Asian", "Asian")),                value)) +   geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5) +   facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Cc (μg/mL)") +   scale_x_discrete(name = "Race") +   ggtitle("Steady-state Cavg, Cmin, Cmax (cycle 12, virtual cohort)") +   theme_minimal()

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: PKNCA computes Cmax, Tmax, AUC cycle-12 dosing interval (standard maintenance interval — tau = 14 days Q2W; reported Cavg = AUC0-tau / tau paper). formula groups RACE_ASIAN per-stratum results directly comparable Ide 2020 Figure 3 panel pattern. Simulated steady-state NCA cycle-12 Q2W interval, race.
code: sim_nca <- sim_cohort |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, RACE_ASIAN) |>   dplyr::mutate(treatment = ifelse(RACE_ASIAN == 1L, "Asian", "non-Asian"))  dose_df <- cohort_events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1L) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, RACE_ASIAN) |>   dplyr::mutate(treatment = ifelse(RACE_ASIAN == 1L, "Asian", "non-Asian"))  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,                              concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  # Steady-state interval: cycle-12 Q2W dose interval (last full Q2W interval # in the simulation window). last_dose_t <- max(dose_df$time) intervals <- data.frame(   start    = last_dose_t,   end      = last_dose_t + 14,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data))  nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     q05    = stats::quantile(PPORRES, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     q95    = stats::quantile(PPORRES, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) knitr::kable(nca_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = "Simulated steady-state NCA over the cycle-12 Q2W interval, by race.")

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: Ide 2020 tabulate numeric steady-state exposure values, Figure 3 panel reports box-plot medians qualitatively approximately: simulated cycle-12 medians PKNCA table fall within ranges Asian non-Asian strata, consistent paper’s finding exposures similar across race body-weight accounted .

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: 2020-07-24 publisher correction: originally-published Figure 2 panel B title caption misnamed time axis “previous dose”; correction replaces “previous dose” “first dose” (acknowledged abstract footer published article). concerns figure presentation — model parameter, equation, covariate effect affected. B2M unit notation slip supplement S2. Ide 2020 Table 1 reports baseline beta-2-microglobulin mg/dL (median 0.330; thresholds 0.20 / 0.35 used binary indicators). Supplement S2 abbreviation list reports thresholds mg/L (2.0 3.5). two unit conventions describe numerical cutpoints (1 mg/dL = 10 mg/L); canonical B2M column model uses mg/L (matching supplement S2 abbreviation list canonical B2M register entry). eGFR unit notation slip supplement S2. Ide 2020 Table 1 reports baseline eGFR mL/min/1.73 m² (BSA-normalized; values 4.58-124 range). Supplement S2 reference-patient text states “GFR = 100 mL/min” without /1.73 m² qualifier. treat eGFR model BSA-normalized (.e., canonical CRCL column mL/min/1.73 m²) Table 1’s units unambiguous reference value 100 matches healthy adult eGFR BSA-normalized scale.

title: Ide_2020_elotuzumab
text: library model implements Ide 2020’s full final model (Table 2) faithfully structural ODEs, 14 covariate-parameter relationships, parameter point estimates. following items deviate source simulation-library reasons; noted future user may need re-introduce deviation fitting: Study-specific residual error multiplier omitted. Ide 2020’s residual error form W = (sdL - (sdL - sdH) · Cc / (sd50 + Cc)) · THETA(16)^STOTHER, THETA(16) = 0.707 (Table 2: SDphase1,2) multiplicative scalar applied STOTHER = 1 (.e., non-NCT01393964 studies; STUDY != 204004 per supplement-7 control stream). library model uses un-multiplied form (STOTHER = 0), corresponds larger-magnitude residual error phase 1-2 NCT01393964 cohort. conservative choice prediction-interval simulations. IIV residual-error magnitude omitted. Ide 2020 Table 2 reports ω²_EPS = 0.164 — inter-individual log-normal variability residual-SD magnitude. Library models nlmixr2lib propagate ETA--residual structure; typical-value W form used. ω²_VMAX = 0.0001 retained. Ide 2020 Table 2 footnote d notes IIV Vmax set near-zero fixed value numerical requirement IMPMAP estimator retaining parameter; preserve value document effectively Vmax variability propagated simulation. Time-varying M-protein supplied covariate column. Ide 2020 analysis, time-varying M-protein observations supplied per subject linearly interpolated observation times, last-observation- carried-forward beyond last sample. library-simulation purposes, user must supply MCPROT every event row input dataset (rxode2 convention); simulation holds MCPROT constant approximates steady-state response. dynamically-decaying MCPROT (modelling tumor response), build column piecewise-linear time series input data rely rxode2’s last-value-carried-forward semantics event rows. Reference-patient inversions COMBO_LEN_DEX. paper’s Ld-related reference (CL KINT) LENDEX = 1 (Ld). canonical COMBO_LEN_DEX uses 0 reference category (-Ld), model encodes effects exp(theta · (COMBO_LEN_DEX - 1)) preserve paper’s reference. subject COMBO_LEN_DEX = 1 (Ld coadministration) correctly receives factor 1; subject COMBO_LEN_DEX = 0 activates published 35 %-CL-elevation 10-fold-KINT-reduction effects. Supplied covariate columns must include ECOG_GE1 ECOG_GE2. paper retains separate effects ECOG = 1 versus ECOG ≥ 2. reproduce effects, user supplies binary columns (subject ECOG = 2 indicators = 1; subject ECOG = 1 ECOG_GE1 = 1; subject ECOG = 0 = 0).

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Simulate ixekizumab serum concentration–time profiles using final paediatric population PK model Jackson et al. (2022) patients aged 6 <18 years moderate--severe plaque psoriasis enrolled IXORA-PEDS (NCT03073200). model two-compartment linear PK model first-order subcutaneous absorption, allometric body-weight effect CL, Q, V2, V3, log-linear ADA-titre effect CL. Citation: Jackson K, Chua L, Velez de Mendizabal N, et al. Population pharmacokinetic exposure-efficacy analysis ixekizumab paediatric patients moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (IXORA-PEDS). Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;88(3):1074-1086. doi:10.1111/bcp.15034 Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.15034

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: IXORA-PEDS Phase 3, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial conducted paediatric patients aged 6 <18 years moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (PASI ≥ 12, sPGA ≥ 3, BSA ≥ 10% screening baseline). population PK analysis dataset comprised 558 measurable ixekizumab serum concentrations 184 patients: 4 patients <25 kg (2.2%), 45 patients 25–50 kg (24.5%), 135 patients >50 kg (73.4%). Overall baseline age ranged 6–17 years (median 15 years >50 kg group, 10 years 25–50 kg group, 7 years <25 kg group), baseline weight 21.5–136 kg, 56.5% female. majority white (81.5%), 3.26% Black/African American, 3.26% Asian, 1.63% American Indian Alaska Native, 7.61% , 2.72% missing race. Regions: US 37.5%, Europe 41.3%, Rest World 21.2%. Baseline demographics reproduced Jackson 2022 Table 1. approved weight-based Q4W regimens 20 mg (<25 kg), 40 mg (25–50 kg), 80 mg (>50 kg), initial 40/80/160 mg loading dose respectively. Programmatic access: readModelDb("Jackson_2022_ixekizumab")$population.

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: IXORA-PEDS patient-level data publicly available, figures use virtual paediatric cohort whose weight, weight-group, ADA distributions approximate Jackson 2022 Table 1 ADA incidence reported Results section 3.1. Simulated cohort summary (compare Jackson 2022 Table 1 weight-group means Results 3.1 ADA incidence).
code: set.seed(20220301) # IXORA-PEDS paper month n_subj <- 184 # match dataset size  # Weight-group proportions from Jackson 2022 Table 1 (overall column) # <25 kg: 4/184 = 2.2%; 25-50 kg: 45/184 = 24.5%; >50 kg: 135/184 = 73.4% u <- runif(n_subj) weight_group <- cut(   u,   breaks = c(-Inf, 4 / 184, 49 / 184, Inf),   labels = c("<25 kg", "25-50 kg", ">50 kg"),   right  = TRUE )  # Sample body weights inside each group to match the reported mean +/- SD # from Jackson 2022 Table 1. Clip to the reported per-group ranges. simulate_weight <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group),     "<25 kg"   = pmin(22.6, pmax(21.5, rnorm(1, 21.9, 0.520))),     "25-50 kg" = pmin(50.0, pmax(25.0, rnorm(1, 37.7, 8.10))),     ">50 kg"   = pmin(136.0, pmax(50.1, rnorm(1, 71.3, 19.5)))   ) } WT <- vapply(weight_group, simulate_weight, numeric(1))  # ADA titre: 85.8% ADA-negative (ADA_TITER = 1); 14.2% TE-ADA-positive with # titres in three bands per Results section 3.1: #   - Low    (<1:160)        : 52.6% of ADA+ samples #   - Moderate (>=1:160, <1:1280): 38.5% #   - High     (>=1:1280)    :  9.0% ada_pos <- runif(n_subj) < 0.142 ada_titre <- rep(1, n_subj) n_pos <- sum(ada_pos) if (n_pos > 0) {   band <- sample(c("low", "mod", "high"),     size    = n_pos,     replace = TRUE,     prob    = c(0.526, 0.385, 0.090)   )   titre_sample <- function(b) {     switch(b,       low  = sample(c(10, 20, 40, 80), 1),          # 1:10 to <1:160       mod  = sample(c(160, 320, 640), 1),           # 1:160 to <1:1280       high = sample(c(1280, 2560), 1)               # >=1:1280     )   }   ada_titre[ada_pos] <- vapply(band, titre_sample, numeric(1)) }  cohort <- tibble(   ID           = seq_len(n_subj),   weight_group = weight_group,   WT           = WT,   ADA_TITER    = ada_titre )  summary_tbl <- cohort |>   group_by(weight_group) |>   summarise(     n           = dplyr::n(),     mean_WT     = mean(WT),     median_WT   = median(WT),     pct_ada_pos = 100 * mean(ADA_TITER > 1),     .groups     = "drop"   ) knitr::kable(summary_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = "Simulated cohort summary (compare with Jackson 2022 Table 1 weight-group means and Results 3.1 ADA incidence).")

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Weight-based Q4W regimen per IXORA-PEDS: loading dose Week 0 (40/80/160 mg <25/25–50/>50 kg), maintenance Q4W (20/40/80 mg). Simulate Week 24 (6 maintenance doses) trough Week 12 (co-primary endpoint timepoint) reflects steady state. Times hours (model unit).
code: # Weeks -> hours conversion hours_per_week <- 7 * 24  maint_doses <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group), "<25 kg" = 20, "25-50 kg" = 40, ">50 kg" = 80) } load_doses <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group), "<25 kg" = 40, "25-50 kg" = 80, ">50 kg" = 160) }  dose_weeks    <- c(0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20) dose_times_hr <- dose_weeks * hours_per_week obs_weeks     <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 24, by = 0.5), 1, 9))) # dense early + the Week 1 and Week 9 PK addendum timepoints obs_times_hr  <- obs_weeks * hours_per_week  d_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(dose_idx = seq_along(dose_times_hr)) |>   mutate(     TIME = dose_times_hr[dose_idx],     AMT  = ifelse(dose_idx == 1,                   vapply(weight_group, load_doses, numeric(1)),                   vapply(weight_group, maint_doses, numeric(1))),     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA_real_   ) |>   select(-dose_idx)  d_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_hr) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   as.data.frame()

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Jackson_2022_ixekizumab") set.seed(42) # `weight_group` and `WT` are per-subject columns already on every row of # `d_sim` (built from `cohort |> tidyr::crossing(...)`). Carrying them # through `rxSolve(keep = ...)` removes the need for post-rxSolve # left_joins from `cohort` in every downstream chunk. sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim, returnType = "data.frame",                        keep = c("weight_group", "WT")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure 1 — Mean ixekizumab concentration vs time by weight group (Weeks 0–12)
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson 2022 Figure 1 plots mean (±SD) trough-observed concentrations 25–50 kg >50 kg groups Week 12. simulated curves show mean (5th/95th percentile ribbon) simulated ixekizumab concentrations window, stratified baseline weight group.
code: sim_plot <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 12 * hours_per_week, !is.na(Cc)) |>   mutate(week = time / hours_per_week)  fig1 <- sim_plot |>   group_by(weight_group, week) |>   summarise(     mean_Cc = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     Q05     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig1, aes(x = week, y = mean_Cc, colour = weight_group, fill = weight_group)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_continuous() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 4)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Ixekizumab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Figure 1 replication: mean concentration by weight group",     subtitle = "Simulated Weeks 0\u201312 (loading + Q4W maintenance); mean and 5\u201395th percentile",     caption = "Replicates Figure 1 of Jackson 2022."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 3A — Week 12 trough concentration vs body weight
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
code: wk12_time <- 12 * hours_per_week sim_wk12 <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(abs(time - wk12_time) < 1e-6)  ggplot(sim_wk12, aes(x = WT, y = Cc, colour = weight_group)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Baseline body weight (kg)",     y = "Week 12 trough concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Figure 3A replication: Week 12 model-predicted trough vs body weight",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3A of Jackson 2022."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Steady-state trough by weight group (Weeks 8 and 12)
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson 2022 Results section 3.1 reports mean trough concentrations predicted steady state (Weeks 8 12) 25–50 kg >50 kg groups range 3.20–3.33 µg/mL. Insufficient PK data available summarise <25 kg group. Simulated mean trough concentrations Weeks 8 12. Compare Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported means 3.20–3.33 μg/mL 25–50 kg >50 kg groups; insufficient data <25 kg group.
code: trough_weeks <- c(8, 12) trough_times <- trough_weeks * hours_per_week  trough_tbl <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time %in% trough_times) |>   group_by(weight_group, week = time / hours_per_week) |>   summarise(     mean_trough  = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     sd_trough    = sd(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     median_trough = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups      = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(trough_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = paste(                "Simulated mean trough concentrations at Weeks 8 and 12.",                "Compare with Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported means 3.20\u20133.33 \u03bcg/mL",                "in the 25\u201350 kg and >50 kg groups; insufficient data in the <25 kg group."              ))

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Run PKNCA steady-state dosing interval Week 8 Week 12 doses (.e., Week 8 dose Week 12 dose, 4-week window). interval Jackson 2022 reports steady-state trough concentrations. Simulated steady-state (Week 8–Week 12) NCA weight group. Cmin trough Week 12; compare Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported mean trough 3.20–3.33 μg/mL 25–50 >50 kg groups.
code: ss_start <- 8  * hours_per_week ss_end   <- 12 * hours_per_week  nca_conc <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, Cc > 0) |>   mutate(time_rel = time - ss_start,          treatment = paste0("IXE_Q4W_", weight_group)) |>   rename(ID = id) |>   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   mutate(time_rel = 0,          AMT      = vapply(weight_group, maint_doses, numeric(1)),          treatment = paste0("IXE_Q4W_", weight_group)) |>   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID)  data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start   = 0,     end     = 4 * hours_per_week,     cmax    = TRUE,     tmax    = TRUE,     cmin    = TRUE,     auclast = TRUE,     cav     = TRUE   ) )  nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste(     "Simulated steady-state (Week 8\u2013Week 12) NCA by weight group.",     "Cmin is the trough at Week 12; compare with Jackson 2022 Results 3.1:",     "reported mean trough 3.20\u20133.33 \u03bcg/mL in the 25\u201350 and >50 kg groups."   ) )

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Week 12 mean trough: simulated vs Jackson 2022 Results 3.1. Simulated Week 12 mean troughs track published 3.20–3.33 µg/mL range within reasonable margin; residual difference driven differences virtual cohort’s weight distribution actual IXORA-PEDS cohort.
code: pub <- tibble(   weight_group = c("<25 kg", "25-50 kg", ">50 kg"),   published_mean_trough = c(     NA_real_,  # insufficient data in <25 kg group     mean(c(3.20, 3.33)),     mean(c(3.20, 3.33))   ),   published_range = c(     "Insufficient data",     "3.20-3.33",     "3.20-3.33"   ) ) sim_trough <- trough_tbl |>   filter(week == 12) |>   select(weight_group, simulated_mean_trough = mean_trough)  comparison <- pub |>   left_join(sim_trough, by = "weight_group") |>   mutate(pct_diff = 100 * (simulated_mean_trough - published_mean_trough) / published_mean_trough)  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,              caption = "Week 12 mean trough: simulated vs Jackson 2022 Results 3.1.")

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Patient-level data unavailable. Weight distributions within group drawn Jackson 2022 Table 1 mean ± SD clipped reported range; actual IXORA-PEDS per-subject weights published. ADA-titre sampling. 14.2% patients flagged TE-ADA positive per Results section 3.1; per-band split (52.6% low / 38.5% moderate / 9.0% high titre) applies paper’s band-level percentages uniformly patient level, whereas paper reports sample level. ADA-negative patients (85.8%) carry ADA_TITER = 1 log-linear effect zero. Time-varying ADA modelled. paper uses time-matched ADA titre publish per-subject longitudinal trajectories; virtual subject carries single titre full follow-. Baseline weight . Jackson 2022 used baseline weight (mean percent change 1.6% first 12 weeks; 23% ≥±10% change 108 weeks). vignette uses single baseline weight per subject, matching paper. Residual error central compartment. paper reports one proportional residual error (27.7%); additive residual error reported added . Site injection modelled. paper evaluated injection site covariate retain final paediatric model. Dosing regimen. IXORA-PEDS used approved Q4W regimens (loading dose followed Q4W maintenance). Ongoing open-label periods beyond Week 12 simulated beyond Week 24.

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson K, Chua L, Velez de Mendizabal N, et al. Population pharmacokinetic exposure-efficacy analysis ixekizumab paediatric patients moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (IXORA-PEDS). Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;88(3):1074-1086. doi:10.1111/bcp.15034

title: Kielbasa_2020_galcanezumab
text: Simulate galcanezumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Kielbasa Quinlan (2020). Galcanezumab humanized IgG monoclonal antibody binds calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) approved prevention migraine. structural model one-compartment disposition model first-order subcutaneous absorption linear elimination, fit 15,770 serum concentrations 1,889 healthy adults adults episodic chronic migraine pooled across seven phase 1, phase 2, phase 3 clinical studies. Body weight covariate retained final model, entering allometric power apparent clearance (exponent 0.601) 73.6 kg reference. factors examined (age, sex, race, ethnicity, healthy volunteer vs. patient status, ADA positivity, ADA titer, treatment-emergent ADA, creatinine clearance, bilirubin, injection site) retained. Article: J Clin Pharmacol 60(2):229-239

title: Kielbasa_2020_galcanezumab
text: Kielbasa 2020 (Results paragraph 1 Supplemental Table S1): 1,889 individuals contributed 15,770 serum galcanezumab concentrations PK model development dataset. Baseline demographics mean (SD) age 41 (12) years range 17-65 years, mean (SD) body weight 75.8 (16.8) kg range 40-135.5 kg, population median body weight 73.6 kg. majority individuals non-Hispanic (74%), white (72%), female (80%). Patients migraine comprised 89% dataset (60% episodic, 29% chronic) healthy volunteers 11%. Doses spanned 5-300 mg SC (single Q4W); phase 3 therapeutic regimens used 120 mg QM without 240 mg loading dose, 240 mg QM. Study I5Q-MC-CGAJ contributed 1,448 PK observations 270 patients external validation dataset part development sample. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Kielbasa_2020_galcanezumab")$population.

title: Kielbasa_2020_galcanezumab
