title: Brentuximab vedotin ADC + MMAE coupled population PK model (Li 2017)
text: Median MMAE AUC0-21 across cycles 1-5, normalized cycle 1. 50-80% cycle-1--subsequent decline reported Li 2017 (Results, MMAE Final Model paragraph) reproduced Fmc = CYCLE^Fm effect attenuates proteolytic MMAE formation successive cycles.
code: mmae_cycle1 <- as.data.frame(mmae_nca_res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD == "auclast") |>   group_by(cycle) |>   summarise(median_auclast = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") mmae_cycle1 |>   mutate(ratio_to_cycle1 = median_auclast / median_auclast[cycle == "1"]) |>   knitr::kable(     digits  = c(NA, 2, 2),     caption = "Median MMAE AUC0-21 across cycles 1-5, normalized to cycle 1. The 50-80% cycle-1-to-subsequent decline reported by Li 2017 (Results, MMAE Final Model paragraph) is reproduced as the Fmc = CYCLE^Fm effect attenuates proteolytic MMAE formation in successive cycles."   )

title: Brentuximab vedotin ADC + MMAE coupled population PK model (Li 2017)
text: Molar units throughout ODE. Modeling nmol amounts, L volumes, nmol/L = pmol/mL concentrations match paper’s stated convention (“Modeling done molar units”). Users dosing mg must convert: dose_nmol = dose_mg * 1000 / MW_ADC_kDa (= dose_mg * 6.536 brentuximab vedotin, MW 153 kDa). checkModelConventions() flags “info” dosing/concentration unit magnitudes canonical pattern dosing--mg, concentration--ug/mL (mg -> mg/L). coupled ADC+MMAE model two molecular weights differing ~200x, single shared molar unit way keep mass balance 1 ADC -> DAR molecules MMAE exact inside ODE. Compartment naming. seven ODE states central, peripheral1, peripheral2, central_mmae, peripheral1_mmae, pathway_proteolytic, pathway_deconjugation. Parent (ADC) compartments use canonical names; MMAE compartments use canonical metabolite-suffix convention (central_mmae, peripheral1_mmae). two pathway_* integrators bookkeeping compartments retained supplement’s “~13% deconjugation steady state” derivation; checkModelConventions() flags canonical compartment list. Body-weight reference 75 kg. paper’s Table 2 pooled median 74.8 kg abstract covariate-equation definitions describe reference subject 75 kg, model file uses WT / 75 match paper’s reported typical values (CL = 1.56 L/d, V1 = 4.29 L). Sex reference = male. Paper Eq. 2 takes IND = 1 females IND = 0 males, e_sexf_vc = 0.873 female:male V1 ratio. Q5 V5 without IIV. BSV terms reported “0 fixed” Table 4; model omits etalq5 etalv5 rather fixing small nonzero value. Fm stored sign preserved. Paper reports Fm = -0.261 BSV 130% CV. model parameterises Fm = -exp(lfm + etalfm) lfm = log(0.261) omega^2_lfm = log(1.3^2 + 1) = 0.9895 magnitude log-normally distributed individual scale sign remains negative. Virtual-cohort race distribution. Table 2 reports 85% White remaining 15% grouped “”; race tested excluded final model, race used simulation 200-subject virtual cohort carries WT, SEXF, CYCLE. Cycle numbering. CYCLE set 1 first dose onwards 21 days, 2 next 21 days, . weekly regimens (Study 2) CYCLE increments start 28-day cycle, weekly dose. Dose cap. Li 2017 caps per-dose amounts 180 mg subjects > 100 kg. 200-subject virtual cohort applies cap; user simulating model responsible applying dosing rules data-assembly time — model implement caps.

title: Brentuximab vedotin ADC + MMAE coupled population PK model (Li 2017)
text: published erratum correction located PMID 28513851 2026-04-24 (PubMed metadata journal landing page carry correction-notice links). items implementation-level notational inconsistencies source paper supplement extraction resolve; affect final-model parameter point estimates. Table 3 σ1 units. Table 3 reports ADC additive residual 0.0125 (fixed) units “µg/mL”, surrounding Methods section states concentrations modeled pmol/mL. magnitude 0.0125 µg/mL coincides assay LLOQ (12.5 ng/mL = 0.0125 µg/mL), consistent fixing additive residual LLOQ assay-reporting scale. converted native model unit: 0.0125 µg/mL × (1000 / 153 kDa) = 0.0817 pmol/mL. Table 4 σ1 units. Analogously, MMAE additive residual reported 0.0119 ng/mL converted 0.0119 / 0.718 = 0.01658 pmol/mL molar-unit model. Fm parameterisation. Table 4 lists Fm cycle ... Estimate −0.261 ... BSV 130% CV. conventional log-normal IIV admit negative mean, paper’s Fm point estimate BSV together can interpreted consistently placing log-normal |Fm| carrying sign separately. implement Fm = -exp(lfm + etalfm) omega^2_lfm = log(1.3^2 + 1) = 0.9895 reported BSV reproduced exactly log-normal magnitude scale. Deconjugation-flux sign convention. Supplement M1 writes MMAE formation term (1) * (DAR - DAR0 * alpha) * beta. DAR0 * alpha = 1 asymptotic floor DAR(t), expression (DAR - 1) non-negative tad ≥ 0 deconjugation flux central_mmae always compartment (never ). ODE faithful supplement; note flag easy--misread sign reading equation prose. Supplement M1 DADT(6) / DADT(7) scope. two bookkeeping compartments supplement (proteolytic pathway cumulative amount, deconjugation pathway cumulative amount) written without F / flux scaling used derive steady-state ~13% deconjugation split quoted Results. retained model (pathway_proteolytic, pathway_deconjugation) reproducibility derivation used observation equation. CYCLE^Fm CYCLE = 0. CYCLE^Fm negative Fm undefined CYCLE = 0. Simulation data sets must set CYCLE >= 1 everywhere ODE integrated. virtual-cohort builder uses pmax(1L, floor(time / 21) + 1L).

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Citation: Li X, Roy , Murthy B. Population Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Relationship Intravenous Subcutaneous Abatacept Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. J Clin Pharmacol. 2019 Feb;59(2):245-257. doi:10.1002/jcph.1308 Description: Two-compartment population PK model abatacept (CTLA4-Ig Fc-fusion) adults rheumatoid arthritis (Li 2019), first-order SC absorption, zero-order IV infusion support, first-order linear elimination, logit-scale SC bioavailability, full-block IIV CL/VC/Q/VP, KA parameterisation enforces KA > k_el. Article: J Clin Pharmacol. 2019;59(2):245-257 (open access via PMC6587803)

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 pooled 11 clinical studies (4 phase 2 7 phase 3) final population PK dataset 2244 adults rheumatoid arthritis (RA). Six studies administered IV abatacept, four studies administered SC abatacept, one study (ACQUIRE) investigated routes. Doses ranged 0.5 10 mg/kg Q4W IV administration 75 200 mg QW SC administration. exclusion samples missing dose/sample information -LLOQ concentrations, analysis dataset contained 10,382 measurements (validated ELISA, LLOQ 1.0 ng/mL). approved regimens weight-tiered ~10 mg/kg IV Q4W (500 mg <60 kg, 750 mg 60-100 kg, 1000 mg >100 kg) fixed 125 mg SC QW. Li 2019 concluded regimens comparable, delivering steady-state trough concentration (Cminss) ≥10 μg/mL — exposure target associated near-maximal DAS28 response — ~90% patients across body weight strata. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Li_2019_abatacept")$population.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Li 2019 Table 1 (: final-model structural parameters, B: covariate effects; see also Errata section Table 1B’s caption issue). Reference covariate values : 50-year-old male, baseline body weight 70 kg, baseline albumin 4.0 g/dL (paper typo: “mg/dL”), calculated GFR 90 mL/min/1.73 m², swollen joint count 16, concomitant NSAIDs, treated phase-3 (commercial) SC formulation.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Time-unit conversion. Li 2019 reports CL Q L/h KA 1/h. nlmixr2lib convention time days, values multiplied 24 inside log(...) ini(). Logit-F parameterisation. Li 2019 constrains absolute bioavailability (0, 1) via inverse-logit link: F_abs = 1 / (1 + exp(-F_TV)) F_TV = logitfdepot + etalogitfdepot + FORM_ABA_PHASE2 * e_form_f. reference value logitfdepot = 1.42 gives F_abs ~= 0.805 phase-3 commercial formulation; phase-2 formulation shifts F_TV -1.16, giving F_abs ~= 0.565. KA > kel constraint. Li 2019 reports ~4-day SC Tmax ~14-day terminal half-life — .e. absorption faster elimination. prevent flip-flop parameter estimates fitting, paper reparameterises absorption KA_i = KA_TV * exp(etaKA) + k_el,k_el,= CL_i / VC_i. model file implements verbatim; simulation using typical parameters reproduces Tmax approximately 4.5 days (see ). Full-block IIV CL, VC, Q, VP. Li 2019 fits 4×4 block covariance matrix CL, VC, Q, VP etas; 10 lower-triangle values taken directly Table 1A. IIV KA logit-F independent blocks. Combined proportional + additive residual error. Li 2019 reports θPROP = 0.215 (fraction) θADD = 0.341 μg/mL; nlmixr2lib uses mg/L concentration, addSd = 0.341 carries without conversion (1 μg/mL = 1 mg/L). SWOL shifted-power form. swollen-joint-count effect ((SWOL+1)/(16+1))^0.0965; adding 1 avoids zero-count edge case origin power form. Canonical CRCL usage. Li 2019 calls renal covariate cGFR units mL/min/1.73 m²; canonical CRCL column carries units accepts either MDRD-estimated eGFR BSA-normalised measured CrCl. Reference 90. MTX dropped backward elimination. Table 1B lists CLMTX row (-0.0405, 95% CI -0.0889 0.00791, significant) paper’s final-model covariate equation p. 249 include MTX term. model implements published equation therefore excludes MTX; see Errata.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Li 2019 Methods baseline-demographic descriptors (Table S3 accessible PMC full text). Subject-level observed data released paper. Three regimens simulated: labelled 125 mg SC QW (reference); weight-tiered IV Q4W (500 mg <60 kg, 750 mg 60-100 kg, 1000 mg >100 kg); single-dose 125 mg SC Tmax / Cmax check.
code: set.seed(20260424) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq_len(n_subj),   WT              = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 70, sd = 18),   40, 160)),   AGE             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 50, sd = 13),   18,  90)),   ALB             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 4.0, sd = 0.35), 2.5, 5.0)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 90, sd = 25),   30, 180)),   SWOL_28JOINT    = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 16, sd = 6)),   0,  28)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.78),         # RA populations are ~75-80% female   CONMED_NSAID    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.55),         # Li 2019 Methods: NSAID use common in RA cohort   FORM_ABA_PHASE2 = 0L                               # Phase-3 commercial SC formulation for labelled regimen ) tau_sc <- 7         # SC QW tau_iv <- 28        # IV Q4W n_sc   <- 26        # 26 weekly doses -> ~182 days, deeply into SS n_iv   <- 7         # 7 q4w doses -> ~196 days  dose_days_sc <- seq(0, tau_sc * (n_sc - 1), by = tau_sc) dose_days_iv <- seq(0, tau_iv * (n_iv - 1), by = tau_iv)  # Weight-tiered IV amount per subject cohort_iv <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt_iv = dplyr::case_when(       WT <  60             ~ 500,       WT <= 100            ~ 750,       TRUE                 ~ 1000     )   )  build_sc_events <- function(cohort, dose_amt, dose_days, treatment) {   ev_dose <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment)   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau_sc, by = 1),     dose_days + 1, dose_days + 3   )))   ev_obs <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  build_iv_events <- function(cohort_iv, dose_days, treatment) {   ev_dose <- cohort_iv |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_iv, cmt = "central", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment)   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau_iv, by = 1),     dose_days + 1, dose_days + 7   )))   ev_obs <- cohort_iv |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  events_sc_125 <- build_sc_events(cohort, 125, dose_days_sc, "SC_125_QW") events_iv     <- build_iv_events(cohort_iv, dose_days_iv, "IV_weight_tiered_Q4W")

title: Li_2019_abatacept
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Li_2019_abatacept")) keep_cols <- c("WT", "AGE", "ALB", "CRCL", "SWOL_28JOINT",                "SEXF", "CONMED_NSAID", "FORM_ABA_PHASE2", "treatment")  sim_sc <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_sc_125, keep = keep_cols)) sim_iv <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_iv,     keep = keep_cols)) sim    <- dplyr::bind_rows(sim_sc, sim_iv)

what: SC absorption: Tmax and early-dose kinetics
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 narrates “time maximum concentration (Tmax) SC abatacept ~4 days terminal half-life ~14 days IV SC abatacept.” block confirms model reproduces Tmax simulating single 125 mg SC dose typical covariate values plotting concentration-time profile 28 days.
code: mod_typ <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() typ_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 70, AGE = 50, ALB = 4.0, CRCL = 90,   SWOL_28JOINT = 16, SEXF = 0L, CONMED_NSAID = 0L, FORM_ABA_PHASE2 = 0L ) ev_single_sc <- typ_cov |>   tidyr::crossing(time = c(0, seq(0.25, 28, by = 0.25))) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = ifelse(time == 0, 125, 0),                 cmt = ifelse(time == 0, "depot", NA_character_),                 evid = ifelse(time == 0, 1L, 0L)) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) sim_single_sc <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = ev_single_sc)) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalka', 'etalogitfdepot'  tmax_day  <- sim_single_sc$time[which.max(sim_single_sc$Cc)] cmax_mgL  <- max(sim_single_sc$Cc)  ggplot(sim_single_sc, aes(time, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = tmax_day, linetype = "dashed", colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time after single SC dose (days)",     y = "Abatacept Cc (mg/L = µg/mL)",     title = sprintf("Typical-patient single 125 mg SC: Tmax = %.1f days, Cmax = %.2f mg/L",                     tmax_day, cmax_mgL),     caption = "Li 2019 narrates 'Tmax for SC abatacept was ~4 days'."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Concentration-time profiles: SC QW vs IV Q4W
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: two approved regimens overlaid axes first 12 weeks. Percentile bands reflect cohort-level IIV.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0, time <= 84) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Abatacept Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profiles: 125 mg SC QW vs weight-tiered ~10 mg/kg IV Q4W",     caption = "Virtual RA cohort (N = 400); first 12 weeks."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Steady-state exposures by body-weight group (Figure S2 replication)
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 Figure S2 plots simulated steady-state trough (Cminss) body weight group (<60 kg, 60-100 kg, >100 kg) IV SC treatments shows () SC Cminss decreases increasing body weight (b) IV Cminss approximately constant across weight groups virtue weight-tiered dosing. block replicates body-weight-group summary using steady-state cycle virtual cohort.
code: ss_start_sc <- tau_sc * (n_sc - 1) ss_end_sc   <- ss_start_sc + tau_sc ss_start_iv <- tau_iv * (n_iv - 1) ss_end_iv   <- ss_start_iv + tau_iv  trough_by_wtgrp <- dplyr::bind_rows(   sim_sc |>     dplyr::filter(time == ss_start_sc + tau_sc) |>     dplyr::mutate(regimen = "SC 125 mg QW"),   sim_iv |>     dplyr::filter(time == ss_start_iv + tau_iv) |>     dplyr::mutate(regimen = "IV weight-tiered Q4W") ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     wt_group = cut(WT, breaks = c(-Inf, 60, 100, Inf),                    labels = c("<60 kg", "60-100 kg", ">100 kg"))   )  ggplot(trough_by_wtgrp, aes(wt_group, Cc, fill = regimen)) +   geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.size = 0.6) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 10, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Baseline body-weight group",     y = "Steady-state trough Cc (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Replicates Li 2019 Figure S2: C_minss by body-weight group, IV vs SC",     caption = "Red dashed line = 10 μg/mL near-maximal-efficacy target per Li 2019."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Non-compartmental analysis final (steady-state) SC IV dosing intervals. Computes Cmax, Cmin, Cavg, AUC per simulated subject regimen.
code: nca_conc_sc <- sim_sc |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start_sc, time <= ss_end_sc, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start_sc) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_sc <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 125, treatment = "SC_125_QW") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_sc <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_sc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_sc <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_sc, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_sc <- data.frame(start = 0, end = tau_sc,                            cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                            auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE) nca_sc <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_sc, dose_obj_sc, intervals = intervals_sc)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      89% |  ETA:  0s summary(nca_sc) #>  start end treatment   N    auclast        cmax        cmin              tmax #>      0   7 SC_125_QW 400 123 [38.1] 19.4 [35.7] 14.9 [44.0] 2.00 [1.00, 3.00] #>          cav #>  17.6 [38.1] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects nca_conc_iv <- sim_iv |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start_iv, time <= ss_end_iv, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start_iv) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_iv <- cohort_iv |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = amt_iv, treatment = "IV_weight_tiered_Q4W") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_iv <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_iv, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_iv <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_iv, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_iv <- data.frame(start = 0, end = tau_iv,                            cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                            auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE) nca_iv <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_iv, dose_obj_iv, intervals = intervals_iv)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           73% |  ETA:  1s summary(nca_iv) #>  start end            treatment   N     auclast       cmax        cmin #>      0  28 IV_weight_tiered_Q4W 400 1340 [32.3] 224 [25.7] 14.2 [56.8] #>                  tmax         cav #>  0.000 [0.000, 0.000] 47.8 [32.3] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against the Li 2019 Cminss ≥10 μg/mL benchmark
title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Li 2019 reports ~90% patients achieved steady-state trough concentration least 10 μg/mL approved regimens. cohort-fraction check direct numerical replicate claim. Fraction virtual subjects steady-state trough Cc >= 10 mg/L regimen weight group (Li 2019: ~90% across groups).
code: pct_above <- trough_by_wtgrp |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, wt_group) |>   dplyr::summarise(     N              = dplyr::n(),     pct_ge_10_mgL  = 100 * mean(Cc >= 10, na.rm = TRUE),     median_Cmin    = median(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(pct_above, digits = 1,   caption = "Fraction of virtual subjects with steady-state trough Cc >= 10 mg/L by regimen and weight group (Li 2019: ~90% across all groups).")

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: Time-unit rescaling. Li 2019 reports CL, Q, KA per-hour units; model file converts per-day factor 24 match nlmixr2lib units$time = "day" convention. VC, VP, F, IIV variances, residual-error magnitudes carry unchanged. MTX excluded final-model equation. Li 2019 Table 1B lists CLMTX = -0.0405 (95% CI crosses zero, significant 0.1% backward-elimination threshold) paper’s final-model covariate equation p. 249 include MTX term. model implements published final-model equation therefore excludes MTX; remaining 10 covariate effects Table 1B retained. FORM_ABA_PHASE2 model-specific covariate. abatacept SC phase-2-vs-phase-3 formulation indicator implemented inline covariateData rather registered canonical entry, per nlmixr2lib policy FORM_* covariates stay model-specific unless clearly generalize across multiple drugs. covariate used phase-2 trial SC data; set 0 simulations approved commercial 125 mg SC QW regimen. checkModelConventions() flags warning, accepted exception. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Li 2019 Table S3 (baseline demographics) referenced paper embedded PMC full text, virtual-cohort distributions approximate: WT ~ N(70, 18) truncated [40, 160]; AGE ~ N(50, 13) truncated [18, 90]; ALB ~ N(4.0, 0.35) truncated [2.5, 5.0] g/dL; CRCL ~ N(90, 25) truncated [30, 180]; SWOL_28JOINT ~ rounded N(16, 6) truncated [0, 28]; SEXF = 78% female; CONMED_NSAID = 55%. reference values match Li 2019 Methods narrative; dispersions approximate typical RA phase-3 cohort. IV instantaneous bolus. Li 2019 uses zero-order infusion (approved IV regimen 30-minute infusion). simulation treats IV doses instantaneous bolus central, slightly overstates early-post-dose Cmax effect AUC, trough, half-life estimates. Users wanting exact infusion kinetics set rate (dur) rxode2 event table. subject-level observed data. Li 2019 release individual subject data; figures reported pure forward simulation published final-model parameters virtual cohort.

title: Li_2019_abatacept
text: published Li 2019 paper contains two items reviewer re-reading source alongside model aware : Albumin unit typo (Methods p. 248). Li 2019 Methods states “baseline albumin 4.0 mg/dL” reference-subject covariate value. Normal adult serum albumin ~4 g/dL; value 4 mg/dL factor 1000 low physiologically impossible. treated publication unit typo: model codes ALB reference 4.0 g/dL, clinically sensible matches abatacept PK reports (e.g. Orencia label). unit recorded covariateData[[ALB]]$units g/dL explicit note. Table 1B caption vs final-model equation (Table 1 caption Results p. 249). Table 1 captioned “Parameter Estimates () Structural Part Final PPK Model Abatacept (B) Covariates Full PPK Model” — .e. Table 1A final Table 1B explicitly full model. Table 1B’s 11th row (CLMTX = -0.0405, 95% CI crossing zero) present published final-model covariate equation p. 249. operator decision extraction treat Table 1B’s 10 non-MTX estimates published final-model covariate values (matching final-model equation exactly) drop CLMTX; paper separately tabulate post-backward-elimination covariate estimates. Bristol-Myers Squibb ever publishes separate table final-model covariate estimates differ Table 1B, values model refit source.

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: Citation: Lin K-J, Turner MA, Pasoll D, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Casirivimab Imdevimab Pediatric Adult Non-Infected Individuals, Pediatric Adult Ambulatory Hospitalized Patients Household Contacts Patients Infected SARS-COV-2. Pharmaceutical Research. 2024;41(10):1933-1949. doi:10.1007/s11095-024-03764-5 Description: Two-compartment population PK model casirivimab pediatric adult subjects (non-infected, ambulatory hospitalized SARS-CoV-2-infected, household contacts) following IV SC administration (Lin 2024, casirivimab arm joint casirivimab + imdevimab popPK model) Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11095-024-03764-5 PMC full text: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11530482/ source paper develops joint population PK model casirivimab imdevimab fitted simultaneously pooled data seven clinical studies. mAb ODE chain separate structural parameters; shared two () body-weight allometric exponents, (ii) covariate effects CL Vc, (iii) η values CL, Vc, KA. model file implements casirivimab arm — imdevimab arm can added parallel Lin_2024_imdevimab.R file.

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: PopPK analysis dataset comprised 7,598 individuals seven Phase 1/1b/2/2a/3 trials (NCT04426695, NCT04425629, NCT04452318, NCT04519437, NCT04666441, NCT05092581, NCT04992273) — non-infected pediatric adult subjects, ambulatory hospitalized SARS-CoV-2-infected patients, household contacts. Median (range) weight 81.6 (8.6–235) kg median age 45 (0–98) years. cohort 50.1% female 81.8% White (Lin 2024 Table 1). dose levels analyzed, casirivimab + imdevimab administered IV (300–8000 mg single dose) SC (600–1200 mg single dose 1200 mg every 4 weeks). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Lin_2024_casirivimab")$population.

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Lin_2024_casirivimab.R. table collects one place review. Reference subject typical-value PK parameters: 45-year-old non-White male, 81.6 kg, ALB 43 g/L, baseline SARS-CoV-2 viral load 6.4 log10 copies/mL, CRP 5.48 mg/L, NLR 2.11, baseline seronegative, supplemental oxygen baseline.

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: corrigendum published Lin 2024 extraction date (verified PubMed SpringerLink). One paper-internal inconsistency identified extraction: Table 2, IIV KA row. point-estimate cell prints 72.28, row’s printed 95% confidence interval (78.05, 78.51), contain 72.28. Results narrative page 1941 explicitly states “Estimates IIV (% coefficient variation) CL, Vc KA casirivimab imdevimab 30.0%, 34.6% 78.3%, respectively.” narrative 95% CI agree value approximately 78.28%. extraction uses 78.28% treats 72.28 typesetting typo.

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: Original observed data publicly available. figures use virtual adult cohort whose covariates approximate pooled Phase-3 cohort median values Lin 2024 Table 1 (median weight 81.6 kg, median age 45, 50% female, ~82% White, ALB 43 g/L, supplemental oxygen baseline, seronegative, SARS-CoV-2 infection viral load = 0). viral load = 0 (SARS_VLOAD/6.4)^e_vload_cl term set 1 avoid 0^negative = ∞; matches population-PK convention non-infected subjects whose viral-load column zero source dataset.
code: set.seed(2024) n_subj <- 500  cohort_adult <- tibble(   id  = seq_len(n_subj),   AGE = pmax(18, pmin(98, rnorm(n_subj, mean = 45, sd = 16))),   WT  = pmax(35, pmin(180, rnorm(n_subj, mean = 81.6, sd = 22.5))),   SEXF = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.501),   RACE_WHITE = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.818),   ALB = pmax(20, pmin(60, rnorm(n_subj, mean = 43, sd = 5))),   HEPIMP_MILD  = 0L,    # all = "Normal" in the simulated cohort   SARS_VLOAD   = 6.4,   # set to reference; non-infected subjects (= 0 in source) handled below   SARS_SEROPOS = 0L,    # all = seronegative   CRP          = 5.48,  # reference (median of studies that collected CRP)   NLR          = 2.11,  # reference   OXYSUP_LOW   = 0L,   OXYSUP_HIGH  = 0L )  build_events <- function(pop, dose_mg, route, obs_times) {   cmt_dose <- if (route == "SC") "depot" else "central"   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(time = 0, amt = dose_mg, evid = 1L, cmt = cmt_dose, dv = NA_real_)   d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(nrow(pop)), each = length(obs_times)), ] |>     mutate(       time = rep(obs_times, times = nrow(pop)),       amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "central", dv = NA_real_     )   bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(id, time, desc(evid)) |>     mutate(treatment = paste0("1200 mg ", route)) }  obs_times <- c(0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 14, 21, 28, 42, 56, 84, 112, 140, 168)  events <- bind_rows(   build_events(cohort_adult,                   dose_mg = 1200, route = "SC", obs_times = obs_times),   build_events(cohort_adult |> mutate(id = id + n_subj),                                                 dose_mg = 1200, route = "IV", obs_times = obs_times) )  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Lin_2024_casirivimab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = "treatment") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: published Figure 1 displays prediction-corrected VPC casirivimab (imdevimab) route administration. approximate simulation-quantile envelope casirivimab arm.
code: sim_q <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(treatment, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.500, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_q, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~ treatment) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, by = 28)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Casirivimab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Casirivimab simulated concentration vs time, by route",     caption = "Replicates the casirivimab panel of Figure 1 of Lin 2024 (single 1200 mg dose; pcVPC in source paper)."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Replicate Figure 2 — covariate forest plot (casirivimab AUC_day28)
title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: Figure 2a Lin 2024 tabulates relative casirivimab AUC 28-day post-dose interval 5th 95th percentile significant covariate vs. reference subject. covariate enters CL known multiplicative power form (Eq. 1), AUC_day28 ≈ Dose × F / CL_typ scales inversely CL_typ ratio. compute ratio analytically model parameters covariate values reported figure caption. Casirivimab AUC_day28 ratios analytical evaluation Eq. 1 Lin 2024 5th- 95th-percentile covariate values reported Figure 2a. two largest covariates AUC_day28 ratio (weight 5th percentile ≈ 1.21 albumin 5th percentile ≈ 1.31) match published Figure 2a callouts (“body weight serum albumin largest impact, 20–34%, ~7–31% change exposures extremes”).
code: ini_vals <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxode(mod)$ini) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' get_val <- function(nm) {   ini_vals$est[ini_vals$name == nm] }  # Helper: relative CL_typ multiplier vs the reference subject when covariate # `cov` takes value `x`. All other covariates are at their reference values. rel_cl <- function(cov, x) {   switch(cov,     WT       = (x / 81.6)^get_val("e_wt_cl"),     AGE      = (x / 45  )^get_val("e_age_cl"),     ALB      = (x / 43  )^get_val("e_alb_cl"),     SARS_VLOAD = (x / 6.4)^get_val("e_vload_cl"),     CRP      = (x / 5.48)^get_val("e_crp_cl"),     NLR      = (x / 2.11)^get_val("e_nlr_cl"),     SEXF        = 1 + get_val("e_sexf_cl")    * x,     RACE_WHITE  = 1 + get_val("e_white_cl")   * x,     HEPIMP_MILD = 1 + get_val("e_hepimp_cl")  * x,     SARS_SEROPOS = 1 + get_val("e_seropos_cl") * x,     OXYSUP_LOW  = 1 + get_val("e_oxylow_cl")  * x,     OXYSUP_HIGH = 1 + get_val("e_oxyhigh_cl") * x   ) }  forest <- tribble(   ~covariate,                        ~p05,  ~p95,   "Weight (kg)",                      54.4, 126,   "Albumin (g/L)",                    30,   48.2,   "Age (y)",                          20,   75,   "Viral load (log10 copies/mL)",     0.1,  9,   "C-reactive protein (mg/L)",        0.54, 126,   "Neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio",      0.89, 9.17,   "Race (non-White, White)",          0,    1,   "Sex (male, female)",               0,    1,   "Hepatic impairment (mild, others)", 1,   0,   "Serostatus (positive, negative)",  1,    0,   "Low oxygen supplement (yes, no)",  1,    0,   "High oxygen supplement (yes, no)", 1,    0 ) |>   mutate(     covariate_key = case_when(       grepl("Weight",   covariate) ~ "WT",       grepl("Albumin",  covariate) ~ "ALB",       grepl("Age",      covariate) ~ "AGE",       grepl("Viral",    covariate) ~ "SARS_VLOAD",       grepl("C-reactive", covariate) ~ "CRP",       grepl("Neutrophil", covariate) ~ "NLR",       grepl("Race",     covariate) ~ "RACE_WHITE",       grepl("Sex",      covariate) ~ "SEXF",       grepl("Hepatic",  covariate) ~ "HEPIMP_MILD",       grepl("Serostatus", covariate) ~ "SARS_SEROPOS",       grepl("Low oxygen", covariate) ~ "OXYSUP_LOW",       grepl("High oxygen", covariate) ~ "OXYSUP_HIGH"     ),     auc_p05 = 1 / mapply(rel_cl, covariate_key, p05),     auc_p95 = 1 / mapply(rel_cl, covariate_key, p95)   )  knitr::kable(   forest |> select(covariate, "5th-pct value" = p05, "95th-pct value" = p95,                    "AUC ratio @ 5th" = auc_p05, "AUC ratio @ 95th" = auc_p95),   digits = 2,   caption = "Casirivimab AUC_day28 ratios from analytical evaluation of Eq. 1 of Lin 2024 at the 5th- and 95th-percentile covariate values reported in Figure 2a." )

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: run NCA simulated typical-adult cohort compare half-life AUC_inf values quoted Lin 2024 (page 1941: half-life = 27.6 days casirivimab). Simulated NCA parameters route (single 1200 mg dose).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(evid == 1) |>   select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■                              11% |  ETA: 19s #>  ■■■■■■■■■                         26% |  ETA: 15s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■                     41% |  ETA: 12s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                57% |  ETA:  9s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           74% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■      91% |  ETA:  2s  nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary,              caption = "Simulated NCA parameters by route (single 1200 mg dose).")

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: Lin 2024 reports casirivimab half-life 27.6 days (page 1941, narrative). Adult-typical analytical AUC_inf computed AUCinf,SC=1200×0.720.1926≈4486mg·day/L,AUCinf,IV=12000.1926≈6231mg·day/L.\mathrm{AUC}_{\mathrm{inf,SC}} = \frac{1200 \times 0.72}{0.1926} \approx 4486\ \text{mg·day/L}, \qquad \mathrm{AUC}_{\mathrm{inf,IV}} = \frac{1200}{0.1926} \approx 6231\ \text{mg·day/L}. simulated medians NCA within ~10% analytical expectations, well 20% deviation threshold flagged skill.

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: Casirivimab arm . Lin 2024 fits casirivimab imdevimab simultaneously. drug independent ODEs structural parameters, casirivimab arm extractable without loss; abstraction source’s “shared η CL/Vc/KA across casirivimab imdevimab” becomes, single-drug extraction, per-subject η casirivimab parameters variance components. IIV KA. Table 2 point-estimate cell prints 72.28, row’s 95% CI (78.05, 78.51) Results narrative states 78.3%. use 78.28% (CI midpoint, agrees narrative); see Errata / paper-internal inconsistencies section . published corrigendum exists. Pediatric switch. source paper switches fixed allometric exponents (0.75 CL, 1 Vc) higher SC bioavailability subjects AGE < 6 years. model implements .numeric(AGE < 6) indicator computed inside model(); separate PED covariate column required user. Viral load = 0 non-infected subjects. source dataset encodes baseline viral load 0 non-infected subjects. model uses power form (SARS_VLOAD/6.4)^e_vload_cl small negative exponent; SARS_VLOAD = 0 undefined (0^negative = +Inf). virtual non-infected adult cohort vignette set SARS_VLOAD = 6.4 (reference) term evaluates 1, equivalent source paper’s reported reference subject. Time-varying covariates held constant. Lin 2024 evaluates ALB, CRP, NLR time-varying. simulated cohort uses constant baseline values; appropriate comparisons vignette may capture longitudinal-cohort changes matter pcVPC late time points. Original pcVPC vs. simulation envelope. Figure 1 Lin 2024 prediction-corrected VPC actually observed data; replicate simulation envelope (observed data available outside Regeneron archive).

title: Lin_2024_casirivimab
text: Lin K-J, Turner MA, Pasoll D, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Casirivimab Imdevimab Pediatric Adult Non-Infected Individuals, Pediatric Adult Ambulatory Hospitalized Patients Household Contacts Patients Infected SARS-COV-2. Pharmaceutical Research. 2024;41(10):1933-1949. doi:10.1007/s11095-024-03764-5

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Citation: Lin K-J, Mendell J, Davis JD, Harnisch LO. Population pharmacokinetic analyses pozelimab patients CD55-deficient protein-losing enteropathy (CHAPLE disease). J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2024;51(6):905-917. doi:10.1007/s10928-024-09941-8 Description: Two-compartment two-binding-site TMDD-QE population PK model total pozelimab total C5 healthy volunteers, adults paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria, pediatric adult patients CHAPLE disease (Lin 2024) Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-024-09941-8 Open access (PMC): https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11579122/ Lin et al. (2024) developed target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD) population PK model pozelimab — fully human IgG4 anti-C5 monoclonal antibody — pooled across four phase 1-3 clinical trials healthy adult volunteers, adult patients paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH), pediatric adult patients CD55-deficient protein-losing enteropathy (CHAPLE disease). structural model two-compartment TMDD two binding sites based quasi-equilibrium (QE) approximation Gibiansky Gibiansky (2017, doi:10.1007/s10928-017-9533-1): pozelimab molecule can bind one two C5 molecules, yielding free drug Cu, pozelimab-C5 complex RC (internalized rate kint1), pozelimab-C5-C5 complex R2C (internalized rate kint2). Linear drug clearance acts free drug . Free C5 follows zero-order synthesis / first-order degradation turnover baseline correction factor theta_R0 accounts early (~1 hour post-dose) drop observed total C5. Body weight dominant covariate, retained power exponent CL, Vc, Vp; PNH disease state retains modest additive-fractional increase Vc . equilibrium dissociation constant kD fixed SPR-Biacore vitro value (0.189 nM = 0.0359 mg/L). Pozelimab approved US FDA August 2023 adult pediatric patients ≥ 1 year age CHAPLE disease (VEOPOZ, pozelimab-bbfg).

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Pooled cohort 116 participants four studies (Lin 2024 Supplementary Table S1 main-text Table 1): NCT03115996 (FIH) — phase 1 first--human single-/multiple-ascending-dose healthy adult volunteers (n = 42); single doses 1, 3, 10, 30 mg/kg IV, 300 / 600 mg SC, 15 mg/kg IV loading + 4 × 400 mg SC QW. NCT04491838 (PK comparability) — phase 1, n = 40; single 400 mg SC. NCT03946748 (PNH) — phase 2 adult PNH patients (n = 24); 30 mg/kg IV loading + 800 mg SC QW. NCT04209634 (CHAPLE) — phase 2/3 CHAPLE pediatric adult patients (n = 10; 9 children, 1 adult); 30 mg/kg IV loading + weight-tiered SC QW (125 mg < 10 kg, 200 mg 10-< 20 kg, 350 mg 20-< 40 kg, 500 mg 40-< 60 kg, 800 mg ≥ 60 kg). Demographics pooled cohort: median age 37 years (range 3-76); median body weight 66.7 kg (range 11-108); 60.3 % female; 70.7 % White, 22.4 % Asian, 3.4 % Black African American, 0.9 % American Indian Alaska Native, 2.6 % ; 0/116 ADA-positive. CHAPLE patients (n = 10) contributed median age 8.5 years (range 3-19), median body weight 25.0 kg (range 11.0-53.8), median baseline albumin 23.0 g/L (range 11-29). 2795 concentration samples (1640 total pozelimab, 1155 total C5) used; 100 post-dose BLQ samples (3.6 %) excluded Beal’s M1 method. full population descriptor available programmatically:
code: str(rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Lin_2024_pozelimab"))$meta$population) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> List of 13 #>  $ n_subjects    : int 116 #>  $ n_studies     : int 4 #>  $ age_range     : chr "3-76 years (median 37 across all subjects; pediatric and adult)" #>  $ age_median    : chr "37 years overall; 8.5 years in CHAPLE patients (range 3-19)" #>  $ weight_range  : chr "11.0-108 kg overall; 11.0-53.8 kg in CHAPLE patients" #>  $ weight_median : chr "66.7 kg overall; 25.0 kg in CHAPLE patients" #>  $ sex_female_pct: num 60.3 #>  $ race_ethnicity: chr "70.7% White, 22.4% Asian, 3.4% Black or African American, 0.9% American Indian or Alaska Native, 2.6% Other (Table 1)." #>  $ disease_state : chr "Healthy adult volunteers (n=82), adult patients with paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH; n=24), and pedia"| __truncated__ #>  $ dose_range    : chr "1-30 mg/kg single IV; 300-600 mg single SC; 400 mg SC QW; 30 mg/kg IV loading + 800 mg SC QW (PNH study); 30 mg"| __truncated__ #>  $ regions       : chr "Multi-regional phase 1-3 programme; specific regional breakdown not reported in the main text." #>  $ ada_status    : chr "All 116 subjects ADA-negative (0% positive in every cohort)." #>  $ notes         : chr "Pooled phase 1 first-in-human (NCT03115996, n=42), phase 1 PK comparability (NCT04491838, n=40), phase 2 PNH (N"| __truncated__

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Lin_2024_pozelimab.R. table collects equation parameter provenance one place. QE quadratic free C5, R=12[−(2Ctot+kD−Rtot)+(2Ctot+kD−Rtot)2+4kDRtot]R = \tfrac{1}{2}\bigl[-(2\,C_{tot}+k_D-R_{tot}) + \sqrt{(2\,C_{tot}+k_D-R_{tot})^2 + 4\,k_D\,R_{tot}}\bigr], follows conservation identity Rtot=R+RC+2R2CR_{tot} = R + RC + 2\,R2C together QE binding distribution RC=Ctot⋅2kDR/(kD+R)2RC = C_{tot}\cdot 2 k_D R / (k_D+R)^2 R2C=Ctot⋅R2/(kD+R)2R2C = C_{tot}\cdot R^2 / (k_D+R)^2. See Gibiansky Gibiansky 2017 symmetric two-binding-site TMDD-QE derivation. model file works molar (µM) units internally match Supplemental Text 1’s equations; observation outputs Cc (total pozelimab) Rtot (total C5) converted mg/L using published molecular weights (150 kDa pozelimab IgG4, 190 kDa C5; 190 kDa C5 MW reproduces paper’s footnote conversions ksyn = 0.04922 µM/day = 9.352 mg/L/day kD = 0.000189 µM = 0.0359 mg/L).

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Original individual data publicly available. simulate three typical-value scenarios spanning pooled population: HV adult — 70 kg, non-PNH, 30 mg/kg IV loading + 800 mg SC QW (matches FIH multiple-dose arm PNH study regimen, disease flag turned ). PNH adult — 70 kg, PNH-positive, 30 mg/kg IV loading + 800 mg SC QW (matches phase 2 PNH study). CHAPLE child — 25 kg, non-PNH, 30 mg/kg IV loading + 350 mg SC QW (weight-tiered regimen body weight 20-< 40 kg; 25 kg median CHAPLE body weight). figures use typical-value predictions (rxode2::zeroRe()); PKNCA section runs regimens deterministically get representative steady-state exposures.
code: mod         <- readModelDb("Lin_2024_pozelimab") mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  # Sampling grid: dense early to catch the IV peak and SC absorption, # coarser later for the QW maintenance phase out to ~6 months. obs_t <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,    1,   by = 0.05),   seq(1,    7,   by = 0.25),   seq(7,    180, by = 1) )))  # Helper to build a single-subject event table. build_events <- function(wt, pnh, iv_dose_mg, sc_dose_mg,                          iv_time = 0,                          sc_times = seq(7, 175, by = 7)) {   ev <- rxode2::et(id = 1)   ev <- rxode2::et(ev, amt = iv_dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = iv_time, id = 1)   for (t in sc_times) {     ev <- rxode2::et(ev, amt = sc_dose_mg, cmt = "depot", time = t, id = 1)   }   ev <- rxode2::et(ev, time = obs_t, cmt = "Cc",   id = 1)   ev <- rxode2::et(ev, time = obs_t, cmt = "Rtot", id = 1)   list(ev = ev, iCov = data.frame(id = 1, WT = wt, DIS_PNH = pnh)) }  run_typical <- function(wt, pnh, iv_dose_mg, sc_dose_mg) {   pkg <- build_events(wt, pnh, iv_dose_mg, sc_dose_mg)   s <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, pkg$ev, iCov = pkg$iCov,                        returnType = "data.frame")   s[!duplicated(s$time), ] }  sim_hv    <- run_typical(wt = 70, pnh = 0, iv_dose_mg = 30 * 70, sc_dose_mg = 800) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' sim_pnh   <- run_typical(wt = 70, pnh = 1, iv_dose_mg = 30 * 70, sc_dose_mg = 800) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' sim_chaple <- run_typical(wt = 25, pnh = 0, iv_dose_mg = 30 * 25, sc_dose_mg = 350) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1'

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Figure 4 Lin 2024 plots simulated total pozelimab, free pozelimab, free C5 following single IV doses 1, 3, 10, 30 mg/kg typical 70 kg adult. point figure qualitative contrast: total pozelimab profiles look linear (pozelimab-C5 complex internalized similar rate free pozelimab clearance), free pozelimab markedly nonlinear low concentrations TMDD takes .
code: fig4_doses <- c(1, 3, 10, 30)  fig4_one <- function(mg_per_kg) {   ev <- rxode2::et(id = 1) |>     rxode2::et(amt = mg_per_kg * 70, cmt = "central", time = 0, id = 1) |>     rxode2::et(time = obs_t, cmt = "Cc",   id = 1) |>     rxode2::et(time = obs_t, cmt = "Rtot", id = 1)   s <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, ev,                        iCov = data.frame(id = 1, WT = 70, DIS_PNH = 0),                        returnType = "data.frame")   s <- s[!duplicated(s$time), ]   s$dose <- paste0(mg_per_kg, " mg/kg")   s }  fig4 <- bind_rows(lapply(fig4_doses, fig4_one)) |>   mutate(dose = factor(dose, levels = paste0(fig4_doses, " mg/kg"))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1'  fig4_long <- fig4 |>   select(time, dose, Cc, free_drug, free_target) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, free_drug, free_target), names_to = "species", values_to = "conc") |>   mutate(species = recode(species,                           Cc          = "Total pozelimab (mg/L)",                           free_drug   = "Free pozelimab (mg/L)",                           free_target = "Free C5 (mg/L)"),          species = factor(species,                           levels = c("Total pozelimab (mg/L)",                                      "Free pozelimab (mg/L)",                                      "Free C5 (mg/L)")))  # Filter near-zero values so the log scale doesn't blow up. fig4_long <- fig4_long |> filter(conc > 1e-5)  ggplot(fig4_long, aes(time, conc, colour = dose, linetype = dose)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10() +   facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x       = "Time (days)",     y       = NULL,     colour  = "Single IV dose",     linetype = "Single IV dose",     title   = "Replicates Figure 4 of Lin 2024",     caption = "Typical 70 kg adult, non-PNH; single IV doses 1/3/10/30 mg/kg pozelimab."   ) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: Figure 2 / Figure 3 — Total pozelimab and total C5 over loading + maintenance
title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Figures 2 3 Lin 2024 study-level VPCs total pozelimab total C5 stratified dose. replicate underlying typical-value trajectories scenario cohort (HV adult, PNH adult, CHAPLE child) 24 weeks QW SC maintenance 30 mg/kg IV loading dose.
code: hv_pnh_chaple <- bind_rows(   sim_hv     |> transmute(time, Cc, Rtot, scenario = "HV adult, 800 mg SC QW"),   sim_pnh    |> transmute(time, Cc, Rtot, scenario = "PNH adult, 800 mg SC QW"),   sim_chaple |> transmute(time, Cc, Rtot, scenario = "CHAPLE child (25 kg), 350 mg SC QW") ) |>   mutate(scenario = factor(scenario,                            levels = c("HV adult, 800 mg SC QW",                                       "PNH adult, 800 mg SC QW",                                       "CHAPLE child (25 kg), 350 mg SC QW")))  ggplot(hv_pnh_chaple, aes(time, Cc, colour = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time (days)",     y       = "Total pozelimab (mg/L)",     colour  = NULL,     title   = "Total pozelimab — typical-value profile by scenario",     caption = "Replicates the dose-stratified trends shown in Figure 2 of Lin 2024."   ) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") ggplot(hv_pnh_chaple, aes(time, Rtot, colour = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(     x       = "Time (days)",     y       = "Total C5 (mg/L)",     colour  = NULL,     title   = "Total C5 — typical-value profile by scenario",     caption = "Replicates the dose-stratified trends shown in Figure 3 of Lin 2024."   ) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: Figure 5 — CHAPLE weight-based vs weight-tiered exposures
title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Figure 5 Lin 2024 contrasts simulated steady-state Cmin,ss AUCtau,ss CHAPLE patients receiving either 10 mg/kg weight-based maintenance dose approved weight-tiered SC QW regimen. Weight-tiered doses : 125 mg (< 10 kg), 200 mg (10-< 20 kg), 350 mg (20-< 40 kg), 500 mg (40-< 60 kg), 800 mg (≥ 60 kg). paper’s conclusion patients > 20 kg, two regimens give comparable exposures. Simulated steady-state Cmin,ss (mg/L) AUCtau,ss (mg/L*day) CHAPLE patients weight-tiered vs 10 mg/kg weight-based SC QW maintenance regimens (typical-value, 25 weekly doses).
code: chaple_tiers <- tibble::tribble(   ~tier_label,             ~wt_kg, ~tiered_mg,   "<10 kg (8 kg)",         8,      125,   "10-<20 kg (15 kg)",     15,     200,   "20-<40 kg (30 kg)",     30,     350,   "40-<60 kg (50 kg)",     50,     500,   ">=60 kg (70 kg)",       70,     800 ) |>   mutate(weight_based_mg = pmin(round(10 * wt_kg), 800))  run_ss <- function(wt, dose_mg) {   iv_load   <- 30 * wt   sc_times  <- seq(7, 175, by = 7)  # 25 weekly maintenance doses   pkg <- build_events(wt = wt, pnh = 0, iv_dose_mg = iv_load,                       sc_dose_mg = dose_mg, sc_times = sc_times)   s <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, pkg$ev, iCov = pkg$iCov,                        returnType = "data.frame")   s <- s[!duplicated(s$time), ]   # Steady-state interval = the last weekly interval [t_last, t_last + 7]   t_last <- max(sc_times)   ss     <- s |> filter(time >= t_last, time <= t_last + 7)   tibble::tibble(     Cmin_ss   = min(ss$Cc),     AUCtau_ss = with(ss, sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2))   ) }  fig5 <- chaple_tiers |>   rowwise() |>   mutate(     tiered      = list(run_ss(wt_kg, tiered_mg)),     weight_based = list(run_ss(wt_kg, weight_based_mg))   ) |>   mutate(     Cmin_tiered     = tiered$Cmin_ss,     AUC_tiered      = tiered$AUCtau_ss,     Cmin_wbased     = weight_based$Cmin_ss,     AUC_wbased      = weight_based$AUCtau_ss   ) |>   ungroup() |>   select(tier_label, wt_kg, tiered_mg, weight_based_mg,          Cmin_tiered, Cmin_wbased, AUC_tiered, AUC_wbased) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalksyn', 'etalkint1'  knitr::kable(fig5, digits = 1,              caption = "Simulated steady-state Cmin,ss (mg/L) and AUCtau,ss (mg/L*day) for CHAPLE patients under the weight-tiered vs 10 mg/kg weight-based SC QW maintenance regimens (typical-value, after 25 weekly doses).") fig5_long <- fig5 |>   pivot_longer(c(Cmin_tiered, Cmin_wbased), names_to = "regimen",                values_to = "Cmin_ss") |>   mutate(regimen = recode(regimen,                           Cmin_tiered = "Weight-tiered",                           Cmin_wbased = "10 mg/kg weight-based"),          tier_label = factor(tier_label, levels = chaple_tiers$tier_label))  ggplot(fig5_long, aes(tier_label, Cmin_ss, fill = regimen)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   labs(     x       = "CHAPLE body-weight band",     y       = "Cmin,ss total pozelimab (mg/L)",     fill    = NULL,     title   = "Replicates Figure 5 of Lin 2024 (steady-state Cmin)",     caption = "Typical-value simulation; both regimens converge for body weight > 20 kg."   ) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1),         legend.position = "bottom")

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: PKNCA computes Cmax, Tmax, AUCtau, Cmin, Cavg final maintenance dosing interval scenario. QW SC regimen yield true terminal phase within dosing interval, use auclast ctau rather aucinf.obs / half.life. PKNCA-derived steady-state pozelimab exposure metrics last weekly SC dosing interval.
code: pk_scenarios <- bind_rows(   sim_hv     |> mutate(scenario = "HV adult, 800 mg SC QW"),   sim_pnh    |> mutate(scenario = "PNH adult, 800 mg SC QW"),   sim_chaple |> mutate(scenario = "CHAPLE child (25 kg), 350 mg SC QW") )  # id is unique within scenario after the bind_rows; promote to a global id pk_scenarios <- pk_scenarios |>   mutate(id = match(scenario, unique(scenario)))  sim_nca <- pk_scenarios |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 7) |>   select(id, time, Cc, scenario)  # Build a dose frame consistent with the build_events() helper above. sc_times  <- seq(7, 175, by = 7) dose_df <- bind_rows(   tibble::tibble(scenario = "HV adult, 800 mg SC QW",            id = 1,                  time = c(0, sc_times),                  amt  = c(30 * 70, rep(800, length(sc_times)))),   tibble::tibble(scenario = "PNH adult, 800 mg SC QW",           id = 2,                  time = c(0, sc_times),                  amt  = c(30 * 70, rep(800, length(sc_times)))),   tibble::tibble(scenario = "CHAPLE child (25 kg), 350 mg SC QW", id = 3,                  time = c(0, sc_times),                  amt  = c(30 * 25, rep(350, length(sc_times)))) )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | scenario + id,                              concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | scenario + id,                              doseu = "mg")  # Steady-state interval = the last weekly interval after maintenance dosing t_last  <- max(sc_times) intervals <- data.frame(   start    = t_last,   end      = t_last + 7,   cmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   ctrough  = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)  nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   select(scenario, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES)  knitr::kable(nca_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = "PKNCA-derived steady-state pozelimab exposure metrics over the last weekly SC dosing interval.")

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Lin 2024 reports FDA-approved CHAPLE maintenance regimen (10 mg/kg SC QW 30 mg/kg IV loading dose) expected maintain total pozelimab steady-state concentrations approximately 200 mg/L (“FDA approved dosing regimen patients CHAPLE disease, total pozelimab concentrations expected maintained 200 mg/L steady-state, elimination pathway mainly via linear pathway.”; Discussion). simulated typical-value Cmin,ss values across CHAPLE weight bands (Figure 5 panel ) consistent reported floor > 20 kg patients; < 10 kg 10-< 20 kg bands sit closer linear-PK boundary, matching paper’s observation exposures begin diverge weight-tiered 10 mg/kg weight-based regimens lower body weights. paper publish numerical NCA tables main text; Figure 5 closest published comparator replicated graphically .

title: Lin_2024_pozelimab
text: Reference body weight 70 kg. Lin 2024 reports allometric exponents CL, Vc, Vp (0.9989, 0.7560, 0.7560) state reference weight main text supplement. pooled-cohort median adult body weight 66.7-68.5 kg across FIH PNH studies (Table 1) 70.2 kg PK comparability study; use 70 kg canonical NONMEM reference. exponents estimated (fixed 0.75 / 1), small mis-centering reference negligible effect simulated typical values within cohort weight range. Pozelimab molecular weight 150 kDa. paper give MW; pozelimab fully human IgG4 monoclonal (Latuszek 2020, doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0231892) 150 kDa canonical IgG4 value. C5 MW 190 kDa recovered paper’s footnote Table 2 (kD = 0.000189 µM = 0.03591 mg/L ⇒ 0.03591 / 0.000189 = 190; ksyn = 0.04922 µM/day = 9.352 mg/L/day ⇒ 9.352 / 0.04922 = 190). MWs used convert supplement’s molar-units equations model file’s mg/L observation outputs. Categorical PNH--Vc effect form. Supplemental Text 1 gives full structural ODE system spell categorical covariate equation e_pnh_vc. model uses additive-fractional form Vc = Vc_TV * (1 + e_pnh_vc * DIS_PNH), consistent Table 2’s reported estimate (0.3407, 95 % CI 0.156-0.5254 — strictly zero) standard NONMEM categorical-effect parameterizations. alternative power form theta^DIS_PNH imply 66 % decrease Vc PNH patients, biologically implausible. Adult residual variability used simulations. Table 2 reports separate proportional residual error terms adults vs. children CHAPLE disease (pozelimab: 37.22 % vs 23.17 %; total C5: 10.72 % vs 14.83 %). model file applies adult values canonical residual variability. Children--CHAPLE simulations using model file therefore -estimate spread total pozelimab slightly -estimate total C5 approximately factor 1.5; users need exact CHAPLE-pediatric residuals can override via ini() readModelDb(). Time-varying body weight implemented. Lin 2024 evaluated time-varying body weight sensitivity analysis (Table S3 / S4) found < 5 % change PK parameter estimates OFV; final model retains baseline body weight . vignette holds WT constant per subject, matching published final model. Free pozelimab free C5 exported residual-fit. bioassays measure total drug total target (Methods); free pozelimab free C5 model-derived per QE algebraic relations, surfaced free_drug free_target outputs model file downstream simulation use, assigned residual error.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Citation: Lon HK, Liu D, DuBois DC, Almon RR, Jusko WJ. Modeling pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics abatacept disease progression collagen-induced arthritic rats: population approach. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2013;40(6):701-712. doi:10.1007/s10928-013-9341-1 Description: Two-compartment population PK model linear elimination short-term zero-order SC absorption abatacept (CTLA-4Ig Fc-fusion) male Lewis rats collagen-induced arthritis (Lon 2013). Article: J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn 40(6):701-712

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Simulate abatacept plasma concentration-time profiles collagen-induced arthritic (CIA) Lewis rats using final population PK model Lon et al. 2013. Abatacept (CTLA-4Ig Fc-fusion protein; ~92 kDa) blocks CD28 co-stimulatory signal required T-cell activation. Lon 2013 characterized abatacept PK rat CIA model using IV SC single-dose SC multiple-dose regimens, fitting three arms simultaneously two-compartment linear-elimination model short-term zero-order SC absorption pattern (Eq. 3 paper).

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Lon 2013 Methods (Animals Experimental Design): fifty male Lewis rats (150-175 g arrival, 6-9 weeks old) purchased. CIA induced intradermal injection bovine type II collagen day 0 boosted day 7. day 20, twenty-three rats >= 50% paw-swelling response one two hind paws randomized four treatment arms: Vehicle control (single n = 3, multiple n = 3) IV 10 mg/kg single dose day 21 (n = 5) SC 20 mg/kg single dose day 21 (n = 6) SC 20 mg/kg day 21 followed SC 10 mg/kg days 23, 25, 27, 29 (n = 6) population PK fit used 17 abatacept-treated rats. Plasma abatacept quantified soluble CTLA-4 ELISA (LLOQ 0.16 ng/mL, inter-day CV ~15%). information available programmatically readModelDb("Lon_2013_abatacept") (returned function’s body holds population list literal).

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Lon_2013_abatacept.R. table collects one place review.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Individual body weights reported. build 17-rat virtual cohort whose arm sizes match Lon 2013 exactly (IV n = 5, SC single n = 6, SC multiple n = 6) whose weights sampled uniform distribution across published 150-175 g range arrival (weight gain modeled short post-dosing observation window).
code: set.seed(2013)  cohorts <- tribble(   ~treatment,             ~n,   "IV 10 mg/kg",           5,   "SC 20 mg/kg single",    6,   "SC multiple",           6 )  pop <- cohorts |>   group_by(treatment) |>   summarise(ID = list(seq_len(n[1])), .groups = "drop") |>   tidyr::unnest(ID) |>   mutate(     ID = dplyr::row_number(),     WT = runif(dplyr::n(), min = 0.150, max = 0.175)   )  pop #> # A tibble: 17 × 3 #>    treatment             ID    WT #>    <chr>              <int> <dbl> #>  1 IV 10 mg/kg            1 0.162 #>  2 IV 10 mg/kg            2 0.174 #>  3 IV 10 mg/kg            3 0.170 #>  4 IV 10 mg/kg            4 0.169 #>  5 IV 10 mg/kg            5 0.156 #>  6 SC 20 mg/kg single     6 0.168 #>  7 SC 20 mg/kg single     7 0.173 #>  8 SC 20 mg/kg single     8 0.170 #>  9 SC 20 mg/kg single     9 0.173 #> 10 SC 20 mg/kg single    10 0.170 #> 11 SC 20 mg/kg single    11 0.150 #> 12 SC multiple           12 0.156 #> 13 SC multiple           13 0.169 #> 14 SC multiple           14 0.171 #> 15 SC multiple           15 0.166 #> 16 SC multiple           16 0.168 #> 17 SC multiple           17 0.174

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Doses delivered depot SC arms central IV. packaged model uses podo(depot) tad(depot) drive zero-order release F * Dose / tau central first tau = 2.67 days SC dose; IV doses bypass mechanism.
code: obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,   1,   by = 1/12),           # every 2 h on day 1   seq(1,   3,   by = 1/4),            # every 6 h through day 3   seq(3,  10,   by = 0.5),            # twice daily through day 10   seq(10, 30,   by = 1)               # daily through day 30 )))  sc_multiple_times <- c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8) sc_multiple_amts  <- c(20,  10, 10, 10, 10)  # mg/kg  dose_rows_iv <- pop |>   filter(treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg") |>   transmute(     ID, treatment, WT,     time = 0,     amt  = 10 * WT,           # mg/kg * kg = mg     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "central",     dv   = NA_real_   )  dose_rows_sc_single <- pop |>   filter(treatment == "SC 20 mg/kg single") |>   transmute(     ID, treatment, WT,     time = 0,     amt  = 20 * WT,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "depot",     dv   = NA_real_   )  dose_rows_sc_multi <- pop |>   filter(treatment == "SC multiple") |>   tidyr::crossing(     dose_idx = seq_along(sc_multiple_times)   ) |>   transmute(     ID, treatment, WT,     time = sc_multiple_times[dose_idx],     amt  = sc_multiple_amts[dose_idx] * WT,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "depot",     dv   = NA_real_   )  dose_rows <- bind_rows(dose_rows_iv, dose_rows_sc_single, dose_rows_sc_multi)  obs_rows <- pop |>   select(ID, treatment, WT) |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   mutate(     amt  = NA_real_,     evid = 0L,     cmt  = NA_character_,     dv   = NA_real_   )  events <- bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |>   arrange(ID, time, desc(evid))

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Simulate -subject variability CL V1 spread across virtual cohort matches paper’s individual variability.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Lon_2013_abatacept")  events_sim <- events |> rename(id = ID) # `treatment` is already on every row of `events_sim` (each per-arm dose # builder carries it through, and the obs rows pull it from `pop`). Carry # it through rxSolve via `keep = ` rather than a fragile post-hoc # left_join from pop. sim <- rxSolve(object = mod, events = events_sim, returnType = "data.frame",                keep = "treatment") |>   as_tibble() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Figure 2: plasma concentration-time profiles by treatment
title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Figure 2 Lon 2013 shows abatacept plasma concentration (umol/L) vs. time following IV 10 mg/kg, SC 20 mg/kg single, SC multiple-dose regimens, 90% prediction intervals population model.
code: fig2 <- sim |>   filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   group_by(treatment, time) |>   summarise(     med = median(Cc),     q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig2, aes(x = time, y = med, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q05, ymax = q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~treatment, nrow = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time since first dose (days)",     y       = "Abatacept plasma concentration (umol/L, log scale)",     colour  = "Treatment",     fill    = "Treatment",     title   = "Figure 2 replication - simulated abatacept PK by treatment arm",     caption = "Replicates Figure 2 of Lon et al. 2013 (median and 90% interval across virtual cohort)."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Compute non-compartmental CL (IV arm), AUC, Cmax, Tmax, terminal half-life per subject per treatment arm. PKNCA formula groups concentrations treatment + id summaries per-arm. SC single-dose arm also derive bioavailability F comparing dose- normalized AUC(0,inf) IV arm. Simulated NCA summaries treatment arm.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   filter(time >= 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- dose_rows |>   transmute(id = ID, time, amt, treatment) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE,   cl.obs     = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  sim_nca <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "aucinf.obs", "half.life", "cl.obs")) |>   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     mean = mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     sd   = sd(PPORRES,   na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(sim_nca, digits = 3, caption = "Simulated NCA summaries by treatment arm.")

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Lon 2013 Results (Pharmacokinetics) reports following NCA-derived parameters across abatacept-treated arms: CL = 20.8 mL/day/kg Vss = 146 mL/kg F = 57.7% (SC vs. IV) model-estimated values (Table 1) CL = 21.8 mL/day/kg, V1 + V2 = 69.5 + 61.9 = 131.4 mL/kg, F = 59.2%. simulation converts PKNCA output per-kg clearance (using rat’s WT) derives F dose-normalized AUC(0,inf). Simulated NCA vs. Lon 2013 NCA / model values. simulated IV CL SC F track published NCA values within ~20% tolerance described verification checklist (differences driven two effects - structural-model CL (21.8) NCA CL (20.8) already differed ~5% source paper, finite-sample mean across 5 simulated rats variability 9.67% CV CL).
code: # Per-kg CL for the IV arm iv_cl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg", PPTESTCD == "cl.obs") |>   inner_join(pop |> select(id = ID, WT), by = "id") |>   mutate(cl_mL_per_day_per_kg = PPORRES / WT * 1000)  # L/day -> mL/day, then / kg  # Dose-normalized AUC for F calculation per_subject_auc <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD == "aucinf.obs") |>   inner_join(pop |> select(id = ID, WT), by = "id") |>   mutate(     dose_mgkg = case_when(       treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg"        ~ 10,       treatment == "SC 20 mg/kg single" ~ 20,       TRUE                              ~ NA_real_     ),     auc_per_dose = PPORRES / dose_mgkg   ) |>   filter(!is.na(dose_mgkg))  mean_iv_auc_per_dose <- per_subject_auc |>   filter(treatment == "IV 10 mg/kg") |>   summarise(mean_auc_per_dose = mean(auc_per_dose)) |>   pull(mean_auc_per_dose)  mean_sc_auc_per_dose <- per_subject_auc |>   filter(treatment == "SC 20 mg/kg single") |>   summarise(mean_auc_per_dose = mean(auc_per_dose)) |>   pull(mean_auc_per_dose)  F_sim <- mean_sc_auc_per_dose / mean_iv_auc_per_dose  comparison <- tribble(   ~parameter,                            ~source,                  ~value,   "CL (mL/day/kg), NCA IV arm",          "Lon 2013",                20.8,   "CL (mL/day/kg), NCA IV arm",          "Simulation",              round(mean(iv_cl$cl_mL_per_day_per_kg), 2),   "F (SC vs IV, fraction), NCA",         "Lon 2013",                0.577,   "F (SC vs IV, fraction), NCA",         "Simulation",              round(F_sim, 3),   "CL (mL/day/kg), model theta",         "Lon 2013 Table 1",        21.8,   "Vss (mL/kg), model V1+V2",            "Lon 2013 Table 1",        131.4,   "F (fraction), model theta",           "Lon 2013 Table 1",        0.592,   "Vss (mL/kg), NCA",                    "Lon 2013",                146.0 )  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 3,              caption = "Simulated NCA vs. Lon 2013 NCA / model values.")

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Body weight distribution. Lon 2013 reports rats weighed 150-175 g arrival. sample WT uniformly across range; weight gain modeled 8-day (SC multiple) 20-30 day observation windows. Sampling schedule. Individual sampling times published full detail. use dense early schedule (every 2-6 h 3 days, twice daily day 10, daily thereafter) supports stable NCA early-distribution late-terminal phases. Assay. Observations simulated lower limit quantification (0.16 ng/mL) construction; filtering step !.na(Cc) & Cc > 0 removes post-elimination near-zero concentrations destabilize log-linear terminal regression PKNCA. Residual error. combined prop + add error model yields additive floor 0.0365 umol/L (~ 3.4 ng/mL) plus 16.1% proportional component. Simulated concentrations include residual variability; Figure 2 replication plots median 90% interval across virtual cohort match paper’s prediction band. PD disease progression. Lon 2013 also reports PD model paw edema (Table 2) built top PK parameters. PD arm packaged model; Lon_2013_abatacept() PK-.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Structural model: 2-compartment linear elimination central compartment. SC doses go depot; zero-order rate F * Dose / tau driven depot central t < tau zero thereafter, leaving (1 - F) * Dose unabsorbed residual depot. Units. Dose mg volumes mL give central / vc mg/mL; observation equation divides MW (92,000 g/mol) multiplies 1e6 report concentrations umol/L, matching units Lon 2013 Table 1. IIV: diagonal CL V1 , per Lon 2013 Table 1; IIV reported CLD, V2, F, tau.

title: Lon_2013_abatacept
text: Lon HK, Liu D, DuBois DC, Almon RR, Jusko WJ. Modeling pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics abatacept disease progression collagen-induced arthritic rats: population approach. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2013;40(6):701-712. doi:10.1007/s10928-013-9341-1

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long et al. (2017) characterised population pharmacokinetics necitumumab (second-generation fully human IgG1 anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody) 807 cancer patients pooled across five clinical studies (three phase /II rich sampling two phase III trough-sampling squamous non-squamous non-small-cell lung cancer). final model two-compartment parallel linear Michaelis–Menten (target-mediated) elimination central compartment. Body weight covariate retained, entering power effect clearances (CL Q, shared exponent 0.768) volumes (V1 V2, shared exponent 0.498). vignette reproduces typical concentration–time profile phase III SQUIRE regimen (800 mg IV ~1 h days 1 8 21-day cycle), documents parameter provenance source-trace table, validates simulated NCA published Vss, CLtot steady state, preclinical 40 mg/L trough threshold.

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long 2017 Tables 2 3 (final pooled Pop-PK dataset): Age, sex, race, ethnicity, ECOG, liver function (AST, ALT, bilirubin), renal function (Cockcroft–Gault CrCl), concomitant gemcitabine / cisplatin tested covariates; body weight retained. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Long_2017_necitumumab")$population.

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Every numeric value model file inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Long_2017_necitumumab.R comes following locations Long , Chigutsa E, Wallin J, Clinical Pharmacokinetics 2017;56(5):505–514 (doi:10.1007/s40262-016-0452-x). errata located PubMed, Google Scholar, Springer Nature landing page (search date 2026-04-21).

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: N = 200 virtual cancer patients. Body weight drawn log-normal distribution anchored published median (71 kg, geometric CV 22%) clipped Long 2017 min/max (35–181 kg) match Table 2. Sex age simulated completeness; model sex, age, race, ethnicity effect (none retained final covariate search), WT drives -subject variability typical-value predictions.
code: set.seed(2017) n_subj <- 200  wt <- pmax(35, pmin(181, exp(log(71) + rnorm(n_subj, 0, 0.22)))) pop <- tibble(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = wt ) summary(pop$WT) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   40.13   60.47   71.31   72.94   81.41  141.58

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Four 21-day cycles (12 weeks) SQUIRE regimen: 800 mg IV 1 h days 1 8 cycle. Observation grid: dense first 72 h first dose (capture distribution), weekly troughs mid-cycle samples remaining cycles.
code: cycle_h <- 21 * 24 cycle_dose_offsets <- c(0, 7 * 24) cycles <- 4 dose_times <- as.numeric(outer(cycle_dose_offsets, (seq_len(cycles) - 1) * cycle_h, `+`)) dose_times <- sort(dose_times) tmax_h <- max(dose_times) + 21 * 24  # one full trough cycle past last dose  obs_early <- seq(0, 72, by = 1) obs_post <- sort(unique(c(   dose_times + 1,  # end of 1-h infusion   dose_times + 6,   dose_times + 24,   seq(0, tmax_h, by = 12) ))) obs_times <- sort(unique(setdiff(c(obs_early, obs_post), dose_times)))  d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 800,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 1L,     RATE = 800,  # 1-h infusion (mg/h)     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = 1L,     RATE = 0,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, RATE, DV, WT)  stopifnot(sum(d_sim$EVID == 1) == n_subj * length(dose_times))

title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Two passes: stochastic simulation full IIV residual error (VPC-style plots NCA), typical-value simulation rxode2::zeroRe() reference trajectory.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Long_2017_necitumumab")  set.seed(20170930) sim_full <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure-equivalent 1 — Single-cycle profile (virtual cohort + typical patient)
title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long 2017 Figure 2 shows observed concentrations vs. time first infusion across five studies. panel shows first-cycle profile (0–21 days) virtual cohort overlaid typical-value trajectory.
code: first_cycle <- sim_full |> filter(time <= cycle_h) first_cycle_typ  <- sim_typ  |> filter(time <= cycle_h, id == 1)  ggplot() +   geom_line(     data = first_cycle, aes(time_day, Cc, group = id),     colour = "grey70", alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.25   ) +   geom_line(     data = first_cycle_typ, aes(time_day, Cc),     colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 1   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x     = "Time since first dose (days)",     y     = "Necitumumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "First SQUIRE cycle — virtual cohort vs typical 70 kg patient",     caption = "Long 2017 Figure 2-equivalent; 800 mg IV 1-h infusion, days 1 and 8"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure-equivalent 2 — Steady-state trough across four cycles
title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Long 2017 reports steady state reached within three four cycles phase III 800 mg regimen produces trough concentrations exceed preclinical 40 mg/L threshold. plot shows simulated pre-dose end--cycle trough concentrations across four cycles 40 mg/L threshold marked.
code: trough_times <- sort(unique(c(dose_times, dose_times + 21 * 24 - 1))) trough_times <- trough_times[trough_times <= tmax_h]  trough <- sim_full |>   filter(time %in% trough_times) |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24)  trough_summary <- trough |>   group_by(time_day) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(trough_summary, aes(time_day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", colour = "firebrick") +   annotate(     "text", x = 5, y = 45,     label = "Preclinical target 40 mg/L",     colour = "firebrick", hjust = 0, size = 3   ) +   labs(     x     = "Time since first dose (days)",     y     = "Necitumumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated necitumumab trough/peak profile over 4 SQUIRE cycles",     caption = "Long 2017 Figure 4-equivalent; 800 mg IV days 1 and 8 of 21-day cycle"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: PKNCA validation over the final (cycle 4) dosing interval
title: Long_2017_necitumumab
text: Run NCA final 21-day cycle compare simulated Vss CLtot published typical-patient steady-state values (6.97 L 0.0141 L/h, Long 2017 Results narrative). PKNCA formula includes treatment grouping variable summary rolls per regimen, per library’s PKNCA-recipe convention.
code: ss_start <- dose_times[length(dose_times) - 1]  # start of cycle 4 = day 1 of cycle 4 ss_end   <- ss_start + 21 * 24                  # end of 21-day cycle  sim_nca <- sim_full |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end) |>   transmute(     id        = id,     time      = time,     Cc        = Cc,     treatment = "800 mg IV days 1 and 8 q3w"   )  dose_nca <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 1, TIME >= ss_start, TIME <= ss_end) |>   transmute(     id        = ID,     time      = TIME,     amt       = AMT,     treatment = "800 mg IV days 1 and 8 q3w"   )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_nca, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = ss_start,   end       = ss_end,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   cmin      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   cav       = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>
