Ma 2020 Table 4 prints ANC turnover rate constant > K_out, day⁻¹: Estimate 2.17 (%RSE 35.3, 95% CI 0.638–3.71); > Bootstrap median (95% CI) 211 (1.67–2.88). bootstrap median “211” almost certainly decimal-point error: accompanying 95% confidence interval (1.67–2.88) brackets 2.11 211 (100× discrepancy). neutrophil turnover rate constant ~2/day (half-life ~8 h) physiologically consistent literature neutrophil margination/demargination; 211/day (half-life ~5 min) . package implements Kout = 2.11 (corrected bootstrap median) point estimate, bootstrap CI, physiology agree. reported population-mean estimate (2.17, %RSE 35.3) close 2.11 produces indistinguishable simulated ANC profiles labelled doses. IIV %RSE Kout, also printed Table 4 “227 / bootstrap 232 (198–279)”, retained published. correction documented inline lkout entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc.R. Readers need reproduce (implausible) published bootstrap median exactly can replace log(2.11) log(211) file, though resulting ANC trajectory match Ma 2020 Figure 5. PK backbone Xu 2019, typical-value . sarilumab PK leg uses Xu 2019’s two-compartment + linear + Michaelis–Menten model reference (71 kg female, albumin 38 g/L, CrCl 100 mL/min, CRP 14.2 mg/L, ADA-negative, DP1/DP3). Xu 2019 covariate effects PK parameters (body weight, albumin, CrCl, CRP, ADA, DP2, sex) reproduced file; live companion Xu_2019_sarilumab.R model. sibling DAS28-CRP PD model (Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp.R) makes choice consistency. PK IIV retained (Xu 2019), PD IIV retained (Ma 2020). sets -subject variability carried ini() stochastic VPCs file reasonable. Use rxode2::zeroRe(mod) suppress IIV typical-value plots. Covariate distributions virtual cohort drawn independently marginal distributions Ma 2020 Table 2 (ANC dataset): 14.2% smokers, 63.8% prior corticosteroid, weight ~ N(74.1, 18.7²) truncated [40, 140]. Correlations covariates modelled. Race covariate PD model (84.8% Caucasian Ma 2020 Table 2; RACE_* column required). companion DAS28-CRP model (Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp.R, extracted separately) uses DAS28-CRP column Ma 2020 Table 3. Table 3 known decimal-point errors; typo discussion applies ANC model Table 4.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Citation: Ma L, Xu C, Paccaly , Kanamaluru V. Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Relationships Sarilumab Using Disease Activity Score 28-Joint C-Reactive Protein Absolute Neutrophil Counts Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2020;59(11):1451-1466. doi:10.1007/s40262-020-00899-7. PMID: 32451909. PK backbone Xu C, Su Y, Paccaly , Kanamaluru V. Population Pharmacokinetics Sarilumab Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(11):1455-1467. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00765-1. Description: Indirect-response PK/PD model sarilumab 28-joint disease activity score C-reactive protein (DAS28-CRP) adults rheumatoid arthritis (Ma 2020). Sarilumab inhibits DAS28-CRP production rate (kin) via sigmoid Emax function includes background DMARD placebo component (PLB). PK driver two-compartment, parallel linear + Michaelis-Menten model Xu 2019 evaluated typical covariate-reference values (adult female, 71 kg, ADA-negative, commercial drug product, ALBR = 0.78, CrCl = 100 mL/min/1.73 m^2, baseline CRP = 14.2 mg/L). Article: Clin Pharmacokinet. 2020;59(11):1451-1466 (PMID 32451909) PK backbone: Xu C et al., Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(11):1455-1467 (open access via PMC6856490); packaged separately Xu_2019_sarilumab library. model DAS28-CRP disease-activity component Ma 2020 joint PK/PD analysis sarilumab rheumatoid arthritis (RA). companion absolute-neutrophil-count (ANC) PD model paper (Ma 2020 Table 4) packaged separately Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Ma 2020 pooled three pivotal Phase II/III studies DAS28-CRP population PK/PD dataset 2082 RA patients contributing 17,229 DAS28-CRP observations week 24: NCT01061736 Part (MOBILITY Phase II dose-ranging; 12 weeks), NCT01061736 Part B (MOBILITY Phase III; 52 weeks; MTX-inadequate responders), NCT01709578 (TARGET Phase III; 24 weeks; TNF-inhibitor-inadequate responders). Baseline demographics (Ma 2020 Table 2, DAS28-CRP dataset): mean (SD) age 51.6 (12.0) years, 82.1% female, mean (SD) weight 74.6 (18.8) kg (median 72.8 kg per narrative), 83.6% Caucasian. Disease characteristics: mean (SD) baseline CRP 24.1 (25.1) mg/L (median 15.7 mg/L), mean (SD) baseline IL-6 41.8 (67.2) pg/mL, mean (SD) baseline Physician’s VAS 64.6 (16.8) (median 66), mean (SD) baseline HAQ-DI 1.68 (0.640) (median 1.75). Concomitant treatment: methotrexate 98.8%, prior biologic 39.6%, prior corticosteroid 64.6%, baseline-ACCP-positive 16.5%. Dose arms: sarilumab 100/150/200 mg SC Q2W 100/150 mg SC QW, plus placebo. paper tabulate baseline DAS28-CRP mean DAS28-CRP dataset Table 2; companion ANC dataset reported mean 6.03 (consistent modelled typical BASE 6.06 Ma 2020 Table 3). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp")$population.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Every PD parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Ma 2020 Table 3 (final-model column, DAS28-CRP). PK backbone reproduced Xu 2019 Table 3 evaluated typical reference-covariate values (adult female, 71 kg, ADA-negative, commercial drug product, ALBR = 0.78, CRCL = 100 mL/min/1.73 m^2, CRP = 14.2 mg/L); reference covariate values DAS28-CRP model, contrast, set DAS28-CRP-dataset medians per Ma 2020 narrative (CRP 15.7 mg/L, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8 kg).

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Logit-transformed Emax. Ma 2020 reports Log(Emax) 0.237 states 55.9% maximum decrease DAS28-CRP. transform consistent logit: Emax = 1 / (1 + exp(-lEmax)) → 1 / (1 + exp(-0.237)) = 0.559 (.e., 55.9%). covariate effect baseline CRP Log(Emax) therefore additive logit scale: lEmax_i = lEmax + etalEmax + theta * log(CRP / 15.7). Indirect response placebo term. inhibitory effect uses sum sarilumab concentration placebo-equivalent term: CeffP = Cc + PLB, PLB concentration units (mg/L). Ma 2020 Figure 1 caption writes Eff(C) = Emax * (C + placebo)^gamma / (IC50^gamma + (C + placebo)^gamma). baseline (drug) placebo term alone drives Eff non-zero fraction Emax, needed reproduce placebo-arm DAS28-CRP decrease reported Ma 2020 Table 3 narrative (~25% week 24). Baseline carries four continuous covariates. paper’s Table 3 lists CRP, BLPHYVAS, BLHAQ, WT covariates BASE power-form exponents 0.0564, 0.105, 0.0779, 0.0522 respectively. Reference values DAS28-CRP-dataset medians (CRP 15.7 mg/L, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8 kg) per narrative. PRICORT Kout multiplier. Table 3’s PRICORT--Kout effect 1.26. paper narrates 0.0333 vs 0.0264 day^-1 PRICORT = 1 vs 0, matches 0.0264 * 1.26 = 0.0333. CV% log-normal variance. IIV Ma 2020 Table 3 Xu 2019 Table 3 reported CV% linear-parameter scale. nlmixr2 convention log-normal IIV log-transformed parameter; conversion omega^2 = log(CV^2 + 1) applied ini(). Residual-error units DAS28. Ma 2020 Table 3 lists additive residual SD 0.647 mg/L, DAS28-CRP score unitless. paper’s CWRES VPC DAS28-CRP units, tabulated value treated additive DAS28-CRP score units ; mg/L Table 3 read copy-paste label carry-adjacent rows. PK backbone without PK covariates. Ma 2020 used sequential individual-PK-Bayes approach: individual PK parameters estimated first Xu 2019, fixed used input PD model. keep model self-contained (approach ()), Xu 2019 PK reproduced typical reference-covariate values PK IIV retained (PK covariate effects). See Assumptions section rationale trade-offs choice.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: cohort-level simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Ma 2020 Table 2 demographics DAS28-CRP dataset (n = 2082). Subject-level observed data released paper. Three regimens simulated: placebo (sarilumab zero dose delivering placebo/background-DMARD contribution PLB), 150 mg SC Q2W, 200 mg SC Q2W. horizon extends week 24 — longest follow-reported DAS28-CRP Ma 2020 Table 2 endpoint paper’s week-24 reduction statistics.
code: set.seed(20260419) n_subj <- 60  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id       = seq_len(n_subj),   WT       = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 74.6, sd = 18.8), 40, 170)),   CRP    = pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(15.7) - 0.5 * 1.0^2, 1.0), 0.5),   BLPHYVAS = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 64.6, sd = 16.8), 10, 100)),   BLHAQ    = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 1.68, sd = 0.640), 0, 3)),   PRICORT  = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.646) ) tau      <- 14                           # Q2W dosing interval (days) week24   <- 24 * 7                       # day 168 n_doses  <- ceiling(week24 / tau) dose_days <- seq(0, tau * (n_doses - 1), by = tau) obs_days  <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, week24, by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3,   dose_days + 7 )))

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: model two observation equations (Cc ~ prop(...) sarilumab concentration das28 ~ add(...) DAS28-CRP), combining cohort subjects one rxSolve() call triggers internal rxode2 segfault version used packaging time. vignette therefore simulates subjects one time stitches results together — workaround preserves correctness modest computational cost small (N = 60) validation cohort. deterministic typical-patient comparison paper’s week-24 statistics, zero random effects use reference covariate values (CRP 15.7 mg/L, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8 kg, PRICORT = 0):
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim_one <- function(subj_row, dose_amt, treatment) {   ev_dose <- subj_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   ev_cc <- subj_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "Cc", evid = 0L)   ev_das <- subj_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "das28", evid = 0L)   events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_cc, ev_das) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                   WT, CRP, BLPHYVAS, BLHAQ, PRICORT)   out <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events))   out$id        <- subj_row$id   out$treatment <- treatment   out }  simulate_cohort <- function(cohort, dose_amt, treatment) {   lapply(seq_len(nrow(cohort)), function(i) {     sim_one(cohort[i, , drop = FALSE], dose_amt, treatment)   }) |> dplyr::bind_rows() }  sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   simulate_cohort(cohort, 0,   "Placebo"),   simulate_cohort(cohort, 150, "150mg_Q2W"),   simulate_cohort(cohort, 200, "200mg_Q2W") ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 72.8, CRP = 15.7, BLPHYVAS = 66, BLHAQ = 1.75, PRICORT = 0L )  ev_typ <- function(dose) {   ev_dose <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   ev_cc <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "Cc", evid = 0L)   ev_das <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "das28", evid = 0L)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_cc, ev_das) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                   WT, CRP, BLPHYVAS, BLHAQ, PRICORT) }  sim_typ <- dplyr::bind_rows(   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(0)))   |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "Placebo"),   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(150))) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "150mg_Q2W"),   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(200))) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "200mg_Q2W") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalBase', 'etalEmax', 'etalIC50', 'etalKout', 'etalPLB' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalBase', 'etalEmax', 'etalIC50', 'etalKout', 'etalPLB' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalBase', 'etalEmax', 'etalIC50', 'etalKout', 'etalPLB'

what: DAS28-CRP over time — Figure 3 of Ma 2020
title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Ma 2020 Figure 3 plots predicted typical DAS28-CRP time course placebo, 150 mg Q2W, 200 mg Q2W 24 weeks. block reproduces figure using typical-patient deterministic simulation.
code: sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(das28)) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 7, das28, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 4)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "DAS28-CRP (typical patient)",     title = "Ma 2020 Figure 3 — typical DAS28-CRP trajectory",     caption = paste(       "Typical RA patient (reference covariates; IIV zeroed).",       "Replicates Figure 3 of Ma 2020."     )   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Ma 2020 Figure 2d prediction-corrected VPC DAS28-CRP time DAS28-CRP dataset. block reproduces figure using virtual cohort simulation (sarilumab 200 mg Q2W SC, N = 60).
code: vpc_200 <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(treatment == "200mg_Q2W", !is.na(das28)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(das28, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(das28, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(das28, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc_200, aes(time / 7, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 4)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "DAS28-CRP",     title = "Ma 2020 Figure 2d — VPC of DAS28-CRP (200 mg Q2W)",     caption = paste0(       "Virtual RA cohort (N = ", n_subj, ");",       " median and 90% prediction interval.",       " Replicates Figure 2d of Ma 2020."     )   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Sarilumab Cc over time — typical patient
title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: sarilumab concentration time course drives PD response. typical-patient sarilumab profile 24-week horizon comparison Xu 2019 Figure 3.
code: sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), treatment != "Placebo") |>   ggplot(aes(time, Cc, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Sarilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Typical-patient sarilumab Cc (embedded Xu 2019 PK)",     caption = "Typical RA patient (reference covariates; IIV zeroed)."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Ma 2020 reports typical-patient week-24 DAS28-CRP reductions two active regimens: 150 mg Q2W → 46.5% reduction 200 mg Q2W → 50.3% reduction typical baseline 6.06. correspond week-24 DAS28-CRP approximately 3.24 3.01, respectively. block extracts typical-patient simulated week-24 values compares paper. Typical-patient week-24 DAS28-CRP (IIV zeroed) vs. Ma 2020 reported values. active-arm differences within ~3%.
code: week24_vals <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(das28), abs(time - week24) < 1e-6) |>   dplyr::select(treatment, das28_sim = das28) |>   dplyr::arrange(match(treatment, c("Placebo", "150mg_Q2W", "200mg_Q2W")))  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment      = c("Placebo", "150mg_Q2W", "200mg_Q2W"),   das28_pub      = c(NA,   3.24,  3.01),   pct_reduct_pub = c(NA,   46.5,  50.3) )  comparison <- published |>   dplyr::left_join(week24_vals, by = "treatment") |>   dplyr::mutate(     pct_reduct_sim = 100 * (6.06 - das28_sim) / 6.06,     pct_diff       = 100 * (das28_sim - das28_pub) / das28_pub   )  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,   caption = paste("Typical-patient week-24 DAS28-CRP (IIV zeroed) vs.",                   "Ma 2020 reported values. All active-arm differences",                   "within ~3%."))

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: reference covariates (CRP 15.7, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8, PRICORT = 0) model’s typical BASE must equal exp(log(6.06)) = 6.06. DAS28-CRP state initialized Base, t = 0 value DAS28-CRP compartment 6.06 regardless regimen. Typical-patient DAS28-CRP t = 0 (arms; equal 6.06).
code: baseline_check <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(das28), time == 0) |>   dplyr::distinct(treatment, baseline = das28) knitr::kable(baseline_check, digits = 3,   caption = "Typical-patient DAS28-CRP at t = 0 (all arms; should equal 6.06).")

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Ma 2020 reports placebo arm DAS28-CRP decreases 25% week 24 PD model’s PLB term contributes drug effect even sarilumab concentration zero. table extracts simulated typical-patient placebo week-24 DAS28-CRP compares paper narrative. Typical-patient placebo-arm week-24 DAS28-CRP reduction.
code: placebo_check <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(treatment == "Placebo", !is.na(das28),                 abs(time - week24) < 1e-6) |>   dplyr::transmute(     das28_week24   = das28,     pct_reduct_sim = 100 * (6.06 - das28) / 6.06,     pct_reduct_pub = 25   ) knitr::kable(placebo_check, digits = 2,   caption = "Typical-patient placebo-arm week-24 DAS28-CRP reduction.")

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Approach (): PK backbone embedded typical covariate values. Ma 2020 used sequential individual-PK-Bayes estimates Xu 2019 fixed input PD fit. keep library model self-contained single file, embed Xu 2019 typical PK parameters retain Xu 2019 PK IIV (Vm/CLO/F block, Vc/F, Ka). PK covariate effects (ADA, drug product, sex, ALBR, CRCL, WT--PK, CRP--Vm) omitted. Xu_2019_sarilumab model library full-covariate PK alternative (approach (b)); users need covariate-aware PK PK/PD simulation compose two simulate two steps, using Xu 2019 generate individual sarilumab concentrations feeding input variable simplified indirect-response PD block. Reference-covariate values embedded PK. Xu 2019 typical reference values 71 kg female, ADA-negative, commercial (non-DP2) formulation, ALBR 0.78, CRCL 100 mL/min/1.73 m^2, CRP 14.2 mg/L. internal model exposed user-facing covariates PK side. DAS28-CRP-model reference covariates distinct (CRP 15.7, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8, PRICORT = 0) per Ma 2020 narrative. Emax reported Log(Emax) = 0.237 logit transform. paper doesn’t state transform explicitly, Emax = logit^-1(0.237) = 0.559 matches paper’s stated 55.9% maximum decrease DAS28-CRP, whereas exp(0.237) = 1.27 exceed unity biologically implausible fractional inhibition. Covariate--BASE functional form. Ma 2020 Table 3 lists continuous-covariate effects BASE without explicit formula. use standard power form BASE_i = BASE * (cov / ref)^theta, reproduces small % changes described paper narrative (e.g., weight effect ~0.9% 3 kg shift; phyvas effect similar magnitude). reproduce exactly every narrative percentage change single transformation; power form standard choice rheumatology PK/PD modelling matches Ma 2020 anchor values. Residual error unit DAS28. Ma 2020 Table 3 labels additive residual SD 0.647 mg/L, DAS28-CRP endpoint unitless (0-10 scale) paper’s VPC diagnostics plotted DAS28 units. treat 0.647 value additive DAS28 score units interpret mg/L copy-paste carry-adjacent rows Table 3 table cell. Companion ANC model (Ma 2020 Table 4) decimal-point typo. Ma 2020 Table 4 reports Kout bootstrap median "211 (1.67-2.88)" — leading “2” CI lower bound missing decimal. correct bootstrap median Kout 2.11 day^-1 (consistent paper’s narrative half-life bootstrap CI). companion ANC model Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc (task 005) corrects . DAS28-CRP model unaffected. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Weight drawn N(74.6, 18.8) kg truncated [40, 170]; CRP log-normal median 15.7 mg/L GSD exp(1.0) ~ 2.7x; BLPHYVAS N(64.6, 16.8) truncated [10, 100]; BLHAQ N(1.68, 0.640) truncated [0, 3]; PRICORT Bernoulli(0.646). ranges approximate Ma 2020 Table 2 drawn observed subject-level data, publicly released. reproduction Ma 2020 Figure 3 typical-patient DAS28-CRP trajectories; (ii) reproduction paper’s week-24 reduction percentages (46.5% / 50.3% 150 / 200 mg Q2W); (iii) baseline placebo-arm sanity checks. Dual observation model. model declares two observation equations: Cc ~ prop(propSd) (sarilumab concentration) das28 ~ add(addSd_das28) (DAS28-CRP score). Event tables used rxSolve() therefore need explicit cmt = "Cc" cmt = "das28" observation records disambiguate two outputs; simulation blocks . Per-subject simulation workaround. combining multi-subject event table (two unique IDs) model’s two-output residual-error block, rxode2 version packaging time triggers internal segfault rxSolveSEXP. cohort simulation therefore loops subject--subject binds rows. single-subject simulation IIV zeroed used paper-validation block, unaffected. underlying rxode2 issue fixed upstream, single-call rxSolve(mod, events = cohort_events) reproduce results fraction cost.

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: Citation: Marquez-Megias S, Nalda-Molina R, Mas-Serrano P, Ramon-Lopez . Population Pharmacokinetic Model Adalimumab Based Prior Information Using Real World Data. Biomedicines 2023;11(10):2822. Article: https://doi.org/10.3390/biomedicines11102822 PubMed: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37893195/ model one-compartment population PK model first-order subcutaneous absorption linear elimination, parameterized apparent clearance (CL/F) apparent volume (V/F). covariate model places power effect serum albumin categorical effect anti-adalimumab antibody (AAA) status CL/F. absorption rate constant ka AAA effect fixed Ternant 2015 reference-model values, remaining structural parameters IIV variances re-estimated Spanish IBD therapeutic-drug-monitoring cohort using informative priors IIV terms (Marquez-Megias 2023, Sections 2.3 3.2).

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: estimation dataset comprised 54 patients inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) treated subcutaneous adalimumab followed Dr. Balmis General University Hospital Alicante, Spain 2014 2022. Median age 43.5 years (range 11-89), 55.6% male, median weight 66.5 kg (range 34.8-94.0), median serum albumin 3.86 g/dL (range 1.97-4.96). patients Crohn’s disease (85.2%); remainder ulcerative colitis. Anti-adalimumab antibodies detected 9 patients (16.7%). 70.4% patients received originator (Humira), 27.8% biosimilars (HYRIMOZ IDACIO). analysis dataset included 148 trough serum concentrations (19 induction, 129 maintenance) measured ELISA (TheraDiag LISA TRACKER Duo) LLOQ 0.1 mg/L. See Marquez-Megias 2023 Table 1 full demographic summary. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab")$population.

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: Discussion (Section 4, paragraph albumin) states “CL/F increases 12-fold albumin rises lowest value (1.97 g/dL) highest value (4.96 g/dL).” wording internally inconsistent preceding sentence (“patients lower albumin higher CL/F”) model’s e_alb_cl = -2.33 exponent. negative exponent model predicts CL/F decreases albumin rises: implementation uses published exponent value (-2.33) documented direction effect (lower albumin -> higher clearance); 12-fold figure Discussion appears wording / arithmetic error publication (erratum located).
code: e_alb_cl <- -2.33 ratio_low_to_high <- (1.97 / 4.96)^e_alb_cl ratio_low_to_high  # ~8.6 — model-predicted CL/F ratio at low vs high albumin #> [1] 8.597378

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: simulate 200-subject cohort whose covariates approximate Final Model column Marquez-Megias 2023 Table 1.
code: set.seed(20231018)  # paper publication date  n_sub <- 200L  # Albumin: report the median (3.86) and the support (1.97-4.96). We use a # beta distribution rescaled to that range with a shape that produces a # median near 3.86 and a heavier left tail (matching IBD populations where # active disease pulls albumin downward). alb_sample <- function(n) {   # Beta(5, 2) rescaled has median ~ 3.85 g/dL on (1.97, 4.96).   pmin(pmax(1.97 + (4.96 - 1.97) * rbeta(n, shape1 = 5, shape2 = 2), 1.97), 4.96) }  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id      = seq_len(n_sub),   ALB     = alb_sample(n_sub),   ADA_POS = as.integer(runif(n_sub) < 0.167),  # 16.7% positivity, paper Table 1   treatment = "40 mg q14d SC" )  summary(cohort$ALB) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   2.690   3.790   4.176   4.114   4.493   4.922 mean(cohort$ADA_POS) #> [1] 0.21 # Time unit is hours (matching ka = 0.00625 1/h). Build a regimen consistent # with the paper: induction 160/80 mg at weeks 0/2, then 40 mg every 2 weeks # through 48 weeks (~ 12 maintenance doses). hours_per_day  <- 24 hours_per_week <- 7 * hours_per_day  induction_times <- c(0, 2 * hours_per_week) induction_doses <- c(160, 80)  maint_start    <- 4 * hours_per_week maint_end      <- 48 * hours_per_week maint_times    <- seq(maint_start, maint_end, by = 2 * hours_per_week) maint_doses    <- rep(40, length(maint_times))  dose_times <- c(induction_times, maint_times) dose_amts  <- c(induction_doses, maint_doses)  # Observation grid: one observation per day for the first 8 weeks (capture # induction + first few maintenance peaks), then daily, plus a 1-hour-before- # each-maintenance-dose sample so steady-state troughs can be measured exactly. obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 48 * hours_per_week, by = hours_per_day),   maint_times - 1   # one-hour-pre-dose samples ))) obs_times <- obs_times[obs_times >= 0]  events <- cohort |>   rowwise() |>   do({     cov <- .     bind_rows(       tibble(id = cov$id, time = dose_times, evid = 1L,              amt = dose_amts, cmt = "depot",              ALB = cov$ALB, ADA_POS = cov$ADA_POS,              treatment = cov$treatment),       tibble(id = cov$id, time = obs_times, evid = 0L,              amt = 0, cmt = "central",              ALB = cov$ALB, ADA_POS = cov$ADA_POS,              treatment = cov$treatment)     )   }) |>   ungroup() |>   arrange(id, time, desc(evid))  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = events,   keep = c("treatment", "ALB", "ADA_POS") ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: paper publish typical concentration-time profile, report 68.2% trough samples 8 mg/L, 16.2% 8 12 mg/L, 15.6% 12 mg/L (Section 3.1). median observed serum concentration 4.90 mg/L (Table 1). compute trough end maintenance interval simulation report resulting distribution.  Distribution simulated steady-state troughs vs published TSC distribution (Marquez-Megias 2023, Section 3.1). Simulated steady-state trough summary vs published median TSC (Marquez-Megias 2023, Table 1).
code: # Population concentration vs time (median + 5-95% interval) sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time / hours_per_day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(8, 12), linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)", y = "Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated adalimumab serum concentration",     subtitle = "Median and 5-95% interval; dashed lines = 8-12 mg/L therapeutic window"   ) # Compute the simulated trough (concentration just before each maintenance # dose, after steady state). Steady-state range chosen as weeks 16-48 # (after at least 6 maintenance doses). ss_dose_times <- maint_times[maint_times >= 16 * hours_per_week] trough_times  <- ss_dose_times - 1   # 1 h before next dose  troughs <- sim |>   filter(time %in% trough_times) |>   mutate(     band = cut(       Cc,       breaks = c(-Inf, 8, 12, Inf),       labels = c("<8 mg/L", "8-12 mg/L", ">12 mg/L"),       right = FALSE     )   )  trough_counts <- troughs |>   group_by(band, .drop = FALSE) |>   summarise(n_simulated = n(), .groups = "drop")  trough_summary <- data.frame(   band          = c("<8 mg/L", "8-12 mg/L", ">12 mg/L"),   pct_simulated = 100 * trough_counts$n_simulated[match(c("<8 mg/L", "8-12 mg/L", ">12 mg/L"), trough_counts$band)] / nrow(troughs),   pct_published = c(68.2, 16.2, 15.6) )  knitr::kable(   trough_summary,   digits = 1,   caption = "Distribution of simulated steady-state troughs vs published TSC distribution (Marquez-Megias 2023, Section 3.1)." ) knitr::kable(   data.frame(     statistic = c("median Cc (mg/L)", "5th percentile (mg/L)", "95th percentile (mg/L)"),     simulated = c(median(troughs$Cc),                   quantile(troughs$Cc, 0.05),                   quantile(troughs$Cc, 0.95)),     published = c(4.90, NA, NA)   ),   digits = 2,   caption = "Simulated steady-state trough summary vs published median TSC (Marquez-Megias 2023, Table 1)." )

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: Adalimumab administered chronically; relevant NCA scenario steady-state dosing interval. extract one full maintenance interval (week 36 -> week 38) per simulated subject run PKNCA steady state. Simulated steady-state NCA 14-day maintenance interval (PKNCA, n = 200 virtual subjects).
code: ss_start <- 36 * hours_per_week ss_end   <- 38 * hours_per_week tau      <- 2 * hours_per_week  # 336 h  sim_nca <- sim |>   filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   mutate(time_in_interval = time - ss_start) |>   select(id, time = time_in_interval, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(evid == 1, time == ss_start) |>   mutate(time_in_interval = time - ss_start) |>   select(id, time = time_in_interval, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(   sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,   concu = "mg/L", timeu = "h" ) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(   dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id,   doseu = "mg" )  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = tau,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE,   ctrough  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)  nca_tbl  <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result)  nca_summary <- nca_tbl |>   group_by(PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     q05    = quantile(PPORRES, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     q95    = quantile(PPORRES, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(   nca_summary, digits = 2,   caption = "Simulated steady-state NCA over a 14-day maintenance interval (PKNCA, n = 200 virtual subjects)." )

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: paper report tabulated NCA. two published quantities can cross-checked simulation median observed TSC (4.90 mg/L, Table 1) trough-band distribution (68.2 / 16.2 / 15.6%, Section 3.1). trough-band distribution qualitatively matches published one — population heavily skewed 8 mg/L target — simulated median somewhat lower (~3.1 vs 4.9 mg/L). residual discrepancy consistent two real-world features simulation include: Dose escalation. clinical cohort followed MIPD program patients sub-therapeutic troughs received dose increases; simulation holds 40 mg q14d throughout. Sample mix. published median pools induction- maintenance-phase concentrations 148 TSC samples, whereas simulation summarizes steady-state troughs fixed-dose regimen. Within bounds known differences, simulated published distributions agree: populations heavily skewed 8 mg/L therapeutic threshold, central clinical message Marquez-Megias 2023 motivating MIPD.

title: Marquez-Megias_2023_adalimumab
text: IIV interpretation. Marquez-Megias 2023 Table 3 reports IIV_CL/F = 0.667 IIV_V/F = 0.477 without explicitly stating whether CV%, omega (SD log scale), omega^2 (variance). Monolix’s default population-parameter table prints omega (SD log scale) log-normal random effects, magnitudes also consistent Ternant 2015 reference-model values. model file therefore squares obtain variances passed nlmixr2 (etalcl ~ 0.667^2, etalvc ~ 0.477^2). future review Ternant 2015 reference paper shows otherwise, variances need recomputed. Albumin distribution. paper reports median 3.86 g/dL range 1.97-4.96 g/dL (Table 1) IBD cohort. virtual cohort uses rescaled Beta(5,2) support, produces median near 3.85 g/dL slight left tail consistent active IBD. AAA prevalence. Set 16.7% match Final Model cohort (9/54 patients). simulated AAA status held fixed per subject duration simulation; source paper notes patients flagged AAA-positive ever exceeded 10 ng/mL, .e., indicator monotone-non-decreasing rather truly time-varying. Race / ethnicity. reported paper; used covariate. Body weight. used covariate paper (Section 3.2 text: “body weight, lean body weight body mass index show significant relationship”). model therefore allometric scaling, simulation simulate weight column. Bioavailability. F estimated; published CL/F V/F apparent values already absorb F. depot compartment dosed implicit F = 1, consistent Monolix pkmodel(ka, V, Cl) macro supplementary Figure S1. Discussion text vs. equation. Section 4 sentence “CL/F increases 12-fold albumin rises lowest value (1.97 g/dL) highest value (4.96 g/dL)” contradicted preceding sentence (“patients lower albumin higher CL/F”) Equation (3) exponent -2.33. correct direction implemented; see Errata section.

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Citation: Martinez JM, Brunet , Hurbin F, DiCioccio , Rauch C, Fabre D. Population Pharmacokinetic Analysis Alirocumab Healthy Volunteers Hypercholesterolemic Subjects Using Michaelis-Menten Approximation Target-Mediated Drug Disposition Model - Support Biologics License Application Submission: Part . Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(1):101-113. doi:10.1007/s40262-018-0669-y Description: Two-compartment population PK model alirocumab healthy volunteers adults hypercholesterolemia (Martinez 2019, Part ), first-order SC absorption (lag time), linear plus Michaelis-Menten (target-mediated) elimination central compartment, logit-transformed bioavailability. Article: Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(1):101-113 (open access via PMC6325993) Part II series (doi:10.1007/s40262-018-0670-5) couples PopPK model indirect-response LDL-C model; Part PK structure implemented .

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Martinez 2019 pooled 13 phase -III alirocumab trials final population PK dataset 2799 subjects 13,717 serum concentrations. dataset consisted healthy volunteers adult patients hypercholesterolemia (familial non-familial, without established coronary heart disease), including two Japanese phase /II cohorts. Dosing SC 50-300 mg single repeated (Q2W / Q4W 104 weeks, truncated 24 weeks current analysis); one phase study gave single IV doses 0.3-12 mg/kg. marketed regimens 75 mg 150 mg SC Q2W. Baseline characteristics Martinez 2019 Table 2 footnotes: median body weight 82.9 kg, median age 60 years, time-varying median free PCSK9 concentration 72.9 ng/mL (baseline median 283 ng/mL; time-varying 5th/95th percentiles 0/392 ng/mL). Concomitant statin use (rosuvastatin < 20 mg/day, atorvastatin < 40 mg/day, simvastatin dose) clinically-relevant covariate retained final model. Sex, race, renal function, BMI, ADA positivity evaluated retained. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Martinez_2019_alirocumab")$population.

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Martinez 2019 Table 2 (“Final model covariates” column) footnotes -e. Reference covariate values: 82.9 kg body weight, 60 years age, concomitant statin (STATIN = 0), 72.9 ng/mL free PCSK9.

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Time-unit conversion. Martinez 2019 reports rates h^-1; model file converts day^-1 (x 24) follow nlmixr2lib convention. Volumes (V2, V3), bioavailability factor, FPCSK9 / AGE / WT covariate normalizations time-independent carried unchanged. Additive covariate structure CLL Km. Martinez’s paper uses additive (rather multiplicative) covariate models linear clearance CLL Km, evaluated population typical value: CLL_TV = TVCLL + theta * (WT - 82.9) + theta * STATIN Km_TV = TVKM + theta * (FPCSK9 / 72.9). Individual values carry exponential -subject term: CLL_i = CLL_TV * exp(eta_CLL). preserved faithfully model() rather refactored multiplicative / power form. Power covariate V3. V3_TV = TVV3 * (AGE / 60)^0.310 (Table 2 footnote b). Individual V3 V3_i = V3_TV * exp(eta_V3). Correlated V3 / Km. paper reports omega-block covariance eta_V3 eta_Km correlation r = -0.793 (Table 2 footnote d). 2x2 block -diagonal correlation times sqrt(var_V3 * var_Km), .e. -0.793 * sqrt(0.0735 * 0.298) = -0.11738. Logit bioavailability. F IIV estimated logit space omega^2 = 1.060 (Table 2 footnote e). model parameterizes logitfdepot = logit(0.862) eta_logit_F ~ N(0, 1.060); individual F inverse logit sum. IIV Ka / Q / Vm / LAG. Martinez states interindividual term estimated four parameters; model file omits random-effects block, matching paper. Michaelis-Menten form. elimination ODE term - vmax * Cc / (km + Cc) (rate amount elimination Vmax mg/day, Km mg/L, Cc = central / V2 mg/L), matching popPK convention used Xu 2019 sarilumab -file parameter units. SC vs IV. bioavailability factor lag time applied depot compartment. IV doses (0.3-12 mg/kg single-dose phase study) bypass depot dosing directly central via cmt = "central".

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Martinez 2019 study population medians ranges. subject-level observed data released paper. Three regimens simulated parallel: 75 mg SC Q2W (lower-dose standard), 150 mg SC Q2W (higher-dose standard), 300 mg SC Q4W (equal 4-week dose 150 mg Q2W, tested phase /II). Dosing extends past alirocumab terminal half-life (~14-21 days linear range high concentrations) reach steady state NCA interval.
code: set.seed(20260424) n_subj <- 300  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id     = seq_len(n_subj),   WT     = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 82.9, sd = 18),  45, 150)),   AGE    = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 60,   sd = 12),  18,  90)),   STATIN = rbinom(n_subj, size = 1, prob = 0.80),   FPCSK9 = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 72.9, sd = 120), 0,  400)) ) tau_q2w <- 14                 # Q2W dosing interval (days) n_doses <- 26                 # 26 Q2W doses -> 350 days (~50 weeks), steady state dose_days_q2w <- seq(0, tau_q2w * (n_doses - 1), by = tau_q2w)  tau_q4w <- 28 n_doses_q4w <- 13 dose_days_q4w <- seq(0, tau_q4w * (n_doses_q4w - 1), by = tau_q4w)  make_cohort <- function(cohort, dose_amt, dose_days, treatment, tau,                         id_offset = 0L) {   coh <- cohort |> dplyr::mutate(id = id + id_offset)   ev_dose <- coh |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L,                   treatment = treatment)   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau, by = 1),     dose_days + 0.25,     dose_days + 1,     dose_days + 3,     dose_days + 7   )))   ev_obs <- coh |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L,                   treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, treatment,                   WT, AGE, STATIN, FPCSK9) }  events_75  <- make_cohort(cohort, 75,  dose_days_q2w, "75mg_Q2W",  tau_q2w,                           id_offset = 0L) events_150 <- make_cohort(cohort, 150, dose_days_q2w, "150mg_Q2W", tau_q2w,                           id_offset = 1000L) events_300 <- make_cohort(cohort, 300, dose_days_q4w, "300mg_Q4W", tau_q4w,                           id_offset = 2000L) events <- dplyr::bind_rows(events_75, events_150, events_300) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Martinez_2019_alirocumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' keep_cols <- c("WT", "AGE", "STATIN", "FPCSK9", "treatment")  sim <- lapply(split(events, events$treatment), function(ev) {   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = ev, keep = keep_cols)) }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Martinez 2019 Figure 2 shows visual predictive checks serum alirocumab concentrations per study, predictions capturing 2.5th-97.5th percentiles observed concentrations. block reproduces VPC-style plot first eight Q2W cycles 75 mg 150 mg regimens.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0, time <= 112,                 treatment %in% c("75mg_Q2W", "150mg_Q2W")) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q025 = quantile(Cc, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50  = quantile(Cc, 0.50,  na.rm = TRUE),     Q975 = quantile(Cc, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q025, ymax = Q975), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Alirocumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated 2.5-50-97.5 percentile profiles: 75 vs 150 mg SC Q2W",     caption = "Virtual hypercholesterolemic cohort (N = 300 per arm); first 8 Q2W cycles."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: final Q2W dosing interval 350-day simulation window used steady-state NCA (ss_start ss_end).
code: ss_start <- tau_q2w * (n_doses - 1) ss_end   <- ss_start + tau_q2w  ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc),                 treatment %in% c("75mg_Q2W", "150mg_Q2W")) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes(time - ss_start, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time within Q2W interval (days)",     y = "Alirocumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Steady-state Q2W cycle (final 14-day interval)",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Martinez 2019 reports CLL decreases 78% 50 kg subject increases 40% 100 kg subject versus typical 82.9 kg subject (Results §3.2). block reproduces numbers directly additive covariate equation. CLL (typical value, STATIN = 0) selected body weights. Paper: -78% 50 kg, +40% 100 kg.
code: wt_grid <- tibble::tibble(   WT_kg = c(50, 60, 70, 82.9, 90, 100, 110) ) TVCLL    <- 0.0124                              # L/h (Martinez 2019 Table 2) coef_wt  <- 2.92e-4                             # L/h/kg wt_grid <- wt_grid |>   dplyr::mutate(     CLL_Lph = TVCLL + coef_wt * (WT_kg - 82.9),     pct_vs_medianWT = 100 * (CLL_Lph - TVCLL) / TVCLL   ) knitr::kable(wt_grid, digits = 4,   caption = "CLL (typical value, STATIN = 0) at selected body weights. Paper: -78% at 50 kg, +40% at 100 kg.")

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Martinez reports V3 = 2.79 L 60 y, 2.86 L 65 y, 2.99 L 75 y. V3 (typical value) selected ages. Paper: 2.79 L @ 60 y, 2.86 L @ 65 y, 2.99 L @ 75 y.
code: age_grid <- tibble::tibble(   AGE_y = c(30, 45, 60, 65, 75, 85) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(V3_L = 2.79 * (AGE_y / 60)^0.310) knitr::kable(age_grid, digits = 3,   caption = "V3 (typical value) at selected ages. Paper: 2.79 L @ 60 y, 2.86 L @ 65 y, 2.99 L @ 75 y.")

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Martinez reports Km = 7.73 mg/L FPCSK9 = 0 Km = 4.82 mg/L FPCSK9 = 392 ng/mL (Results §3.2). Note: paper’s prose reports equation output units “mg/L” scale Km typical value; call-“7.73 ng/mL” “4.82 ng/mL” Figure 2 typographical slip — dimensional consistency requires mg/L. Km (typical value) selected free PCSK9 concentrations. Paper: 7.73 mg/L 0 ng/mL, 4.82 mg/L 392 ng/mL.
code: fp_grid <- tibble::tibble(   FPCSK9_ngml = c(0, 72.9, 200, 392) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(Km_mgL = 7.73 + (-0.541) * (FPCSK9_ngml / 72.9)) knitr::kable(fp_grid, digits = 3,   caption = "Km (typical value) at selected free PCSK9 concentrations. Paper: 7.73 mg/L at 0 ng/mL, 4.82 mg/L at 392 ng/mL.")

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis final (steady-state) Q2W dosing interval. Compute Cmax, Ctau, AUC0-tau, Cav per simulated subject treatment.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc),                 treatment %in% c("75mg_Q2W", "150mg_Q2W")) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- dplyr::bind_rows(   cohort |> dplyr::mutate(id = id,        time = 0, amt =  75, treatment = "75mg_Q2W"),   cohort |> dplyr::mutate(id = id + 1000L, time = 0, amt = 150, treatment = "150mg_Q2W") ) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,                              concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = tau_q2w,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    43% |  ETA:  3s summary(nca_res) #>  Interval Start Interval End treatment   N AUClast (day*mg/L) Cmax (mg/L) #>               0           14 150mg_Q2W 300        95.9 [52.6] 10.1 [46.0] #>               0           14  75mg_Q2W 300        42.4 [49.4] 4.67 [41.3] #>  Cmin (mg/L)        Tmax (day)  Cav (mg/L) #>  3.19 [81.0] 3.00 [1.00, 5.00] 6.85 [52.6] #>  1.32 [80.1] 3.00 [1.00, 5.00] 3.03 [49.4] #>  #> Caption: AUClast, Cmax, Cmin, Cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; Tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Martinez 2019 reports reference steady-state AUC(0-336h) 2170 mg*h/L 75 mg Q2W patients weighing 50-100 kg (Results §3.3). typical patient (IIV zeroed) paper’s STATIN-coadministration prevalence (~80% phase III pooled dataset), simulated steady-state AUC close value. typical-patient Cmax Ctrough also reasonable phase-III comparators. Typical-patient steady-state exposures (IIV zeroed) 82.9 kg / 60 y / FPCSK9 = 72.9 ng/mL / without concomitant statin. Comparison vs. Martinez 2019 Results §3.3 reference AUC(0-336h) = 2170 mg*h/L 75 mg Q2W 50-100 kg phase III patients (population-weighted 80% statin coadministration).
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cov <- function(statin) {   tibble::tibble(     id = 1L, WT = 82.9, AGE = 60, STATIN = statin, FPCSK9 = 72.9   ) }  ev_typ <- function(dose_amt, dose_days, cov_row) {   ev_dose <- cov_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   obs_times <- sort(unique(c(     seq(ss_start, ss_end, by = 0.05),     dose_days   )))   ev_obs <- cov_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, AGE, STATIN, FPCSK9) }  ss_metrics <- function(sim_df, label) {   ss <- sim_df |>     dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>     dplyr::arrange(time)   tibble::tibble(     treatment    = label,     Cmax_mgpL    = max(ss$Cc),     Ctrough_mgpL = ss$Cc[which.max(ss$time)],     AUC_mghpL    = sum(diff(ss$time) *                        (head(ss$Cc, -1) + tail(ss$Cc, -1)) / 2) * 24   ) }  sim_typ_75_st0  <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical,                                                  events = ev_typ(75,  dose_days_q2w, typical_cov(0)))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalkm', 'etalogitfdepot' sim_typ_75_st1  <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical,                                                  events = ev_typ(75,  dose_days_q2w, typical_cov(1)))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalkm', 'etalogitfdepot' sim_typ_150_st0 <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical,                                                  events = ev_typ(150, dose_days_q2w, typical_cov(0)))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalkm', 'etalogitfdepot' sim_typ_150_st1 <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical,                                                  events = ev_typ(150, dose_days_q2w, typical_cov(1)))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalkm', 'etalogitfdepot'  typ_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(   ss_metrics(sim_typ_75_st0,  "75 mg Q2W | no statin"),   ss_metrics(sim_typ_75_st1,  "75 mg Q2W | + statin"),   ss_metrics(sim_typ_150_st0, "150 mg Q2W | no statin"),   ss_metrics(sim_typ_150_st1, "150 mg Q2W | + statin") )  # Paper's reference AUC_0-336h was 2170 mg*h/L for 75 mg Q2W, 50-100 kg patients # (Results §3.3). The phase III pooled population is ~80% statin-coadministered; # a population-weighted typical AUC of 0.8 * (75 mg + statin) + 0.2 * (75 mg no # statin) should bracket the reported 2170 mg*h/L value. wt_75 <- 0.8 * typ_tbl$AUC_mghpL[typ_tbl$treatment == "75 mg Q2W | + statin"] +          0.2 * typ_tbl$AUC_mghpL[typ_tbl$treatment == "75 mg Q2W | no statin"]  published <- tibble::tibble(   comparator = "75 mg Q2W, 50-100 kg, population-weighted (80% statin)",   AUC_paper_mghpL = 2170,   AUC_sim_mghpL   = wt_75,   pct_diff        = 100 * (wt_75 - 2170) / 2170 )  knitr::kable(typ_tbl, digits = 2,   caption = "Typical-patient steady-state exposures (IIV zeroed) at 82.9 kg / 60 y / FPCSK9 = 72.9 ng/mL with / without concomitant statin.") knitr::kable(published, digits = 1,   caption = "Comparison vs. Martinez 2019 Results §3.3 reference AUC(0-336h) = 2170 mg*h/L for 75 mg Q2W in 50-100 kg phase III patients (population-weighted 80% statin coadministration).")

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Martinez 2019 reports 28-29% decrease steady-state AUC(0-336h) statins coadministered typical patient, 75 mg 150 mg dose levels. Simulated steady-state AUC(0-336h) /without concomitant statin. Paper: ~28-29% decrease dose levels.
code: statin_tbl <- tibble::tibble(   dose = c("75 mg Q2W", "150 mg Q2W"),   AUC_no_statin = c(     typ_tbl$AUC_mghpL[typ_tbl$treatment == "75 mg Q2W | no statin"],     typ_tbl$AUC_mghpL[typ_tbl$treatment == "150 mg Q2W | no statin"]   ),   AUC_statin    = c(     typ_tbl$AUC_mghpL[typ_tbl$treatment == "75 mg Q2W | + statin"],     typ_tbl$AUC_mghpL[typ_tbl$treatment == "150 mg Q2W | + statin"]   ) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     pct_decrease_sim = 100 * (1 - AUC_statin / AUC_no_statin),     pct_decrease_pub = 28.5   ) knitr::kable(statin_tbl, digits = 1,   caption = "Simulated steady-state AUC(0-336h) with/without concomitant statin. Paper: ~28-29% decrease at both dose levels.")

title: Martinez_2019_alirocumab
text: Vm units. paper’s Table 2 header lists Vm “mg.h/L” main-text prose states “Vm (mg/h)”. Dimensional analysis comparison reported steady-state AUC support main-text interpretation (Vm rate mass elimination mg/h). table header treated typesetting error. recorded -file source-trace comment lvmax documented comparison table . Km units Figure 2 commentary. Section 3.2 states Km = 7.73 ng/mL FPCSK9 = 0 Km = 4.82 ng/mL FPCSK9 = 392 ng/mL. inconsistent Table 2’s Km mg/L; numerical values correct (7.73 4.82) units mg/L, ng/mL. model uses mg/L uniformly, matching Table 2. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Body weight drawn N(82.9, 18) kg truncated [45, 150]; age N(60, 12) truncated [18, 90]; STATIN = Bernoulli(0.8) (phase III pooled prevalence); FPCSK9 N(72.9, 120) ng/mL truncated [0, 400] approximate 5th-95th percentile range 0-392 ng/mL. paper release subject-level distributions; assumptions approximations. FPCSK9 treated covariate, state. Alirocumab binds depletes free PCSK9 (true TMDD feedback mechanism), reality free PCSK9 dynamically coupled alirocumab concentration. paper short-circuits treating time-varying free PCSK9 exogenous covariate Km (Martinez 2019 §3.2, first paragraph). simulation purposes means user must supply FPCSK9 time course; holding FPCSK9 constant 72.9 ng/mL (done typical-patient comparison) approximates steady-state population median capture transient early-dose suppression free PCSK9. Phase III statin prevalence. 80% weighting used population-weighted AUC comparison estimate — phase III ODYSSEY studies (COMBO II, FH , LONG TERM, MONO) mix statin-treated (COMBO II, FH , LONG TERM) statin-free (MONO) populations. Exact pooled prevalence published Martinez 2019 dataset. IV dosing. phase single-dose IV study (0.3-12 mg/kg) exercised vignette. Users can dose cmt = "central" bypass depot, F, lag IV comparisons. IV/SC bioavailability. typical F = 0.862 logit-space IIV apply SC administration . IV doses bypass depot (see previous bullet). unit conversion concentration. Dose units mg volume units L, central / vc yields mg/L directly. PKNCA uses mg/L concentration day time, matching units model file.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Citation: Masters JC, Khandelwal , di Pietro , Dai H, Brar S. Model-informed drug development supporting approval avelumab flat-dose regimen patients advanced renal cell carcinoma. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2022;11(4):458-468. doi:10.1002/psp4.12771 Description: Two-compartment population PK model avelumab (anti-PD-L1 IgG1) time-dependent clearance patients advanced solid tumors (Masters 2022) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12771 Avelumab fully human anti-PD-L1 IgG1 monoclonal antibody. Masters 2022 paper supported approval 800 mg Q2W flat-dose regimen simulating exposure historic 10 mg/kg Q2W weight-based regimen using population PK model inherited Wilkins 2019 (Ref. 20 paper), re-estimated pooled dataset dominated JAVELIN Renal 100 / 101 advanced-renal-cell-carcinoma (aRCC) combination-arm population. Structure: linear two-compartment IV model time-dependent clearance via Hill-type function time since first dose: CL(t)=CLbase⋅(1+Imax⋅tγT50γ+tγ) \mathrm{CL}(t) = \mathrm{CL}_{\text{base}} \cdot   \left( 1 + I_{\max}\cdot \dfrac{t^\gamma}{T_{50}^{\gamma} + t^\gamma} \right) Imax=−0.08533I_{\max} = -0.08533 (fractional decrease t≫T50t \gg T_{50}) allometric weight scaling reference weight 80 kg.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: final-model population pooled dataset 2,315 subjects across five studies (Masters 2022 Results, Population PK analysis): 488 aRCC patients JAVELIN Renal 100 (Phase Ib, avelumab + axitinib) JAVELIN Renal 101 (Phase III, avelumab + axitinib combination arm) trials. 1,827 solid-tumor monotherapy patients carried prior Wilkins 2019 dataset (metastatic Merkel cell carcinoma, advanced urothelial carcinoma, advanced solid tumors). Demographics aRCC sub-population (Tables S2 / S3 supplementary material): weight range 44.2–143.0 kg, median 81.5 kg; prior monotherapy sub-population: weight range 30.4–204 kg, median 70.6 kg. regimens dosed avelumab 10 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks, 800 mg flat dose simulated alternative. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Masters_2022_avelumab")$population.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Masters_2022_avelumab.R. table collects one place review. Equations: d/dt(central) d/dt(peripheral1): standard two-compartment IV micro-constant form (kel = cl/v1, k12 = q/v1, k21 = q/v2). Hill-type time-dependent CL: Masters 2022 Methods / Results Wilkins 2019 precedent.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose weight distribution approximates aRCC sub-population reported Masters 2022 (range 44.2–143.0 kg, median 81.5 kg). Two dosing regimens compared: weight-based 10 mg/kg Q2W approved flat 800 mg Q2W, given 13 cycles 180 days.
code: set.seed(2022) n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(81.5), 0.22), 44.2), 143.0) ) dose_interval_d <- 14 n_doses         <- 13 dose_times_d    <- seq(0, by = dose_interval_d, length.out = n_doses) obs_times_d     <- sort(unique(c(dose_times_d, seq(0, 180, by = 1))))  build_events <- function(pop, regimen) {   amt_per_subject <- if (regimen == "10 mg/kg Q2W") pop$WT * 10 else rep(800, nrow(pop))   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(AMT = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times_d) |>     mutate(EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 1 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = regimen)   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_d) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,            DV = NA_real_, treatment = regimen)   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events_flat    <- build_events(cohort, "800 mg Q2W") events_wtbased <- build_events(cohort, "10 mg/kg Q2W")

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Masters_2022_avelumab") sim_flat    <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_flat,    returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_wtbased <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_wtbased, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_flat,    treatment = "800 mg Q2W"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_wtbased, treatment = "10 mg/kg Q2W") )

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Masters 2022 Figure 2 Figure S1 compare predicted exposure flat-dose weight-based regimens. figure reproduces comparison (median 5–95% prediction interval per regimen):
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Avelumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Simulated avelumab PK: 800 mg flat vs 10 mg/kg Q2W",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ",                       n_subj, " virtual aRCC patients per arm)"),     caption = "Model: Masters 2022 CPT:PSP 11(4):458-468"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Masters 2022 reports fractional decrease CL time (Imax=−0.08533I_{\max} = -0.08533, T50=99.24T_{50} = 99.24 days, γ=2.086\gamma = 2.086). typical-value CL profile reproduces Hill-type time course reference 80 kg subject (deterministic, etas = 0):
code: t_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5) events_cl <- data.frame(   ID = 1, WT = 80,   TIME = c(0, t_grid),   AMT  = c(800, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   EVID = c(1, rep(0, length(t_grid))),   CMT  = "central",   DUR  = c(1 / 24, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   DV   = NA_real_ ) sim_cl <- rxSolve(   mod, events = events_cl,   params = c(WT = 80, etalcl = 0, etalvc = 0, etalvp = 0, etalImax = 0),   omega  = NA,   returnType = "data.frame" ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_cl <- sim_cl[sim_cl$time > 0, ]  ggplot(sim_cl, aes(time, cl / cl_base)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "CL(t) / CL(0)",     title = "Time-dependent clearance in Masters 2022",     subtitle = "Typical 80 kg subject; asymptotes at 1 + Imax = 0.915"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Compute NCA parameters third dosing interval (steady-state approximation mAb ~7 day half-life): Simulated NCA parameters (3rd dosing interval, days 28-42)
code: interval_start <- dose_times_d[3] interval_end   <- dose_times_d[4]  sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc),                 time >= interval_start,                 time <= interval_end) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - interval_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, Cc)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  dose_df <- data.frame(   id        = rep(cohort$ID, 2),   treatment = rep(c("800 mg Q2W", "10 mg/kg Q2W"), each = n_subj),   time_rel  = 0,   amt       = c(rep(800, n_subj), cohort$WT * 10) ) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = dose_interval_d,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    43% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         80% |  ETA:  2s knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),              caption = "Simulated NCA parameters (3rd dosing interval, days 28-42)")

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Table 1 θ_Q typo (0.3352 → 0.03352 L/h): paper’s Table 1 prints point estimate θQ\theta_Q 0.3352 L/h, listed RSE (12.24%) 95% CI (0.02548–0.04157 L/h) internally consistent θ_Q = 0.03352 L/h (missing leading zero printed point estimate). 0.03352 L/h also value inherited Wilkins 2019 base structural model expected range mAb inter-compartmental clearance. corrected value used annotated source-trace comment Masters_2022_avelumab.R. erratum posted publisher site 2026-04-20. Reference weight: Masters 2022 explicitly state reference weight used allometric scaling, 800 mg flat-dose rationale built around approximate 80 kg median body weight, 80 kg used allometric reference CL, V1, V2, Q. Imax parameterization: ImaxI_{\max} always negative source (−0.08533-0.08533). keep every individual Imax⁡,iI_{\max,} strictly negative log-normal IIV, model stores log⁡|Imax|\log|I_{\max}| applies negative sign model() block: Imax_i <- -exp(lImax + etalImax). equivalent log-normal distribution magnitude decrease. ω²(V2) = 1.204 (RSE 109.7%): large log-normal variance (IIV V2 ≈ 135% CV) poor identifiability, internally consistent reported 95% CI (0.9567–1.451) used reported. Downstream NCA estimates driven V2 (e.g., terminal-phase volumes) therefore show wide -subject spread. Residual error convention: sigma column Table 1 standard deviation (variance) proportional (0.1742) additive (2.168 µg/mL) residual components, matching NONMEM’s default reporting. Used directly propSd addSd. Virtual cohort: Demographics simulated log-normal weight distribution anchored aRCC-subpopulation median (81.5 kg) truncated reported range (44.2–143.0 kg). paper tabulate baseline covariates final model body weight enters final structural model. Time units: source reports CL Q L/h T50 days. packaged model keeps time days consistency T50; CL Q converted via ×24.

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: Citation: Melhem M, Hanze E, Lu Z, Venkatakrishnan K, Gupta N, Vugmeyster Y. Population pharmacokinetics exposure-response anti-programmed cell death protein-1 monoclonal antibody dostarlimab advanced solid tumours. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;88(9):4142-4154. doi:[10.1111/bcp.15339](https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.15339) Description: Two-compartment population PK model dostarlimab (anti-PD-1 IgG4) time-dependent (sigmoid I_max) clearance adults advanced solid tumours. Modality: Therapeutic monoclonal antibody (IgG4 hinge-stabilized), IV infusion. Dostarlimab anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody. Melhem 2022 paper developed population PK model Phase 1 GARNET trial (NCT02715284) supporting recommended therapeutic dose regimen 500 mg IV Q3W four cycles followed 1000 mg IV Q6W. Structure: linear two-compartment IV model time-dependent clearance via sigmoid I_max function time since first dose: CL(t)=CLbase⋅exp⁡(Imax⋅tHillT50Hill+tHill)   \mathrm{CL}(t) = \mathrm{CL}_{\text{base}} \cdot   \exp\!\left(I_{\max}\cdot \dfrac{t^{\mathrm{Hill}}}   {T_{50}^{\mathrm{Hill}} + t^{\mathrm{Hill}}}\right) Imax=−0.161I_{\max} = -0.161 (fractional decrease log-CL t≫T50t \gg T_{50}; 1−exp⁡(Imax)=0.1491 - \exp(I_{\max}) = 0.149, matching paper’s “maximum reduction 14.9%”), T50=108T_{50} = 108 days, Hill=5.29\mathrm{Hill} = 5.29. Allometric weight scaling uses reference weight 70 kg (exponent 0.470 CL, 0.419 Vc Vp).

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: final-model population 546 patients 4783 PK observations (exclusion 21 records absolute conditional weighted residual > 5) drawn single multinational Phase 1 trial across multiple expansion cohorts (Melhem 2022 Tables 1-2): 23.4% mismatch repair-deficient (dMMR/MSI-H) endometrial cancer (EC), 29.3% mismatch repair-proficient (MMRp/MSS) EC, 12.3% non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC), 28.8% non-EC dMMR/MSI-H POLE-mutant tumours, ovarian-cancer cohort. Demographics: median age 64 years (range 24-86), 77.3% female, median weight 71.4 kg (range 34-182), median albumin 39 g/L, median ALT 17 U/L. Hepatic renal impairment present baseline (mild--moderate 11% 61% patients respectively) affect dostarlimab PK. ADAs ever-positive 18.5% patients impact clearance. reference patient used published forest plot (Methods, PopPK model development, last paragraph) female, 70 kg, 64 years old, baseline albumin 39 g/L ALT 18 U/L. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Melhem_2022_dostarlimab")$population.

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Melhem_2022_dostarlimab.R. table collects one place review. Equations: d/dt(central) d/dt(peripheral1): standard two-compartment IV micro-constant form (kel = cl/vc, k12 = q/vc, k21 = q/vp). Source: Melhem 2022 Results, equation block (“final PopPK model mathematically described following equations”). Time-dependent CL via exp-Hill: equation block. Covariate effects CL Vc: power form (cov / ref)^theta continuous covariates, multiplicative (1 + theta * SEXM) sex.

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics (Melhem 2022 Table 2). recommended therapeutic dose (RTD) 500 mg IV Q3W four cycles followed 1000 mg IV Q6W. simulation runs one year dosing.
code: set.seed(2022) n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble(   ID   = seq_len(n_subj),   WT   = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(71.4), 0.27), 34),  182),  # Table 2: median 71.4 kg, range 34-182   AGE  = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj,  62.5, 11.0),       24),  86),  # Table 2: mean 62.5, range 24-86   ALB  = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj,  38.2, 5.1),        19,        51)),  # Table 2: mean 38.2 g/L, range 19-51   ALT  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(17), 0.5),     2.9),      120),  # Table 2: median 17, range 2.9-120   SEXF = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.773)                                       # Table 2: 77.3% female ) # RTD schedule (days from first dose). loading_doses_d <- seq(0, by = 21, length.out = 4)              # 500 mg Q3W x 4 maint_start_d   <- max(loading_doses_d) + 21                    # first 1000 mg dose 21 d after the 4th 500 mg maint_doses_d   <- seq(maint_start_d, by = 42, length.out = 12) # 1000 mg Q6W  obs_times_d <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 365, by = 1), loading_doses_d, maint_doses_d)))  build_events <- function(pop) {   d_load <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = loading_doses_d) |>     mutate(AMT = 500, EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 0.5 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = "RTD 500 mg Q3W x4 -> 1000 mg Q6W")   d_maint <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = maint_doses_d) |>     mutate(AMT = 1000, EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 0.5 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = "RTD 500 mg Q3W x4 -> 1000 mg Q6W")   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_d) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,            DV = NA_real_, treatment = "RTD 500 mg Q3W x4 -> 1000 mg Q6W")   dplyr::bind_rows(d_load, d_maint, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events <- build_events(cohort)

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Melhem_2022_dostarlimab") sim <- rxSolve(mod, events = events, returnType = "data.frame")

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: Median 5-95th percentile envelope across virtual cohort recommended therapeutic dose:
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Dostarlimab serum concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated dostarlimab PK at the recommended therapeutic dose",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ",                       n_subj, " virtual patients)"),     caption = "Model: Melhem 2022 Br J Clin Pharmacol 88(9):4142-4154"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: Melhem 2022 reports time-dependent clearance sigmoid I_max function time since first dose. typical-value CL profile reproduces time course reference patient (deterministic, etas = 0):
code: t_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5) events_cl <- data.frame(   ID   = 1, WT = 70, AGE = 64, ALB = 39, ALT = 18, SEXF = 1,   TIME = c(0, t_grid),   AMT  = c(500, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   EVID = c(1,  rep(0, length(t_grid))),   CMT  = "central",   DUR  = c(0.5 / 24, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   DV   = NA_real_ ) sim_cl <- rxSolve(   mod, events = events_cl,   params = c(WT = 70, AGE = 64, ALB = 39, ALT = 18, SEXF = 1,              etalcl = 0, etalvc = 0, etalImax = 0),   omega  = NA,   returnType = "data.frame" ) sim_cl <- sim_cl[sim_cl$time > 0, ]  ggplot(sim_cl, aes(time, cl / cl_base)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_hline(yintercept = exp(-0.161), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "CL(t) / CL(0)",     title = "Time-dependent clearance in Melhem 2022",     subtitle = "Reference patient; asymptotes at exp(I_max) = 0.851 (14.9% reduction)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: NCA computed third 21-day dosing interval (2nd-3rd 500 mg dose window, days 21-42 first dose) simulated reference cohort: Simulated NCA parameters (3rd dosing interval, days 21-42 first dose)
code: interval_start <- 21 interval_end   <- 42  sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc),                 time >= interval_start,                 time <= interval_end) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel  = time - interval_start,                 treatment = "RTD 500 mg Q3W x4 -> 1000 mg Q6W") |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, Cc)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  dose_df <- data.frame(   id        = cohort$ID,   treatment = "RTD 500 mg Q3W x4 -> 1000 mg Q6W",   time_rel  = 0,   amt       = 500 ) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = 21,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                  50% |  ETA:  2s knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),              caption = "Simulated NCA parameters (3rd dosing interval, days 21-42 after first dose)")

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: Melhem 2022 Table 4 reports per-patient cycle-1 (Day 21) exposure summary statistics efficacy ER subset (N = 362, RTD Part 2B arm): Note published AUC reported mgh/L computed 21-day cycle 1 interval; simulated auclast PKNCA table mgday/L simulation time variable days. Multiplying simulated auclast 24 converts mg*h/L comparison. Simulated cycle-1 Cmax 150-200 mg/L range (single 500 mg dose ~3 L Vc, small fraction redistributed peripherally day 1) consistent published Table 4 mean 177 mg/L.

title: Melhem_2022_dostarlimab
text: Residual error convention. Melhem 2022 Table 3 reports proportional residual variability 0.133 (unitless) additive residual variability 2.79 mg/L. additive entry carries units observation (mg/L, mg2/L2), indicating table column standard deviation, variance. entries therefore used directly propSd addSd nlmixr2’s combined add(addSd) + prop(propSd) form. 13.3% CV consistent mAb assay residuals reported comparable anti-PD-1 papers. Imax parameterization. I_max always negative source (-0.161). keep every individual I_max strictly negative log-normal IIV, model stores log|I_max| applies negative sign model() block: Imax_i <- -exp(lImax + etalImax). table’s omega^2_Imax = 0.537 maps omega = sqrt(0.537) = 0.733, paper labels “73.3% CV” — .e., omega reported CV without log-normal CV = sqrt(exp(omega^2) - 1) correction. packaged model uses 0.537 directly variance etalImax, preserving paper’s table value cost ~10 percentage- point divergence “labelled CV” “true log-normal CV”; parameterization used Masters_2022_avelumab.R. Albumin units typo Methods. Methods text describes reference patient “ALB 39 g dL-1”, Table 2 lists albumin g/L (median 39, range 19-51). 39 g/dL physiologically impossible (normal serum albumin 35-50 g/L = 3.5-5 g/dL). model uses g/L canonical unit 39 g/L reference, consistent Table 2 range reported elsewhere paper. Time variable. packaged model uses rxode2’s t (simulation time days) time-dependent CL term. Simulations must therefore start t = 0 first dose time--CL profile aligns source’s “time since first dose” convention. Time-varying covariates. ALB ALT enter published model time-varying covariates. simulations vignette hold subject’s ALB ALT fixed baseline value dosing window. Modelling -treatment ALB/ALT trajectories scope packaged structural model; users longitudinal lab data can pass time-varying ALB / ALT columns rxSolve directly via event table. Race / ethnicity cohort. simulated cohort include explicit race / ethnicity columns race enter final population PK model Melhem 2022. race distribution published analysis set (Table 2: 75.1% White, 3.5% Black, 2.4% Asian, 16.3% reported) recorded model’s population metadata users need stratify simulations. Race covariates declared. RACE_* covariate registered covariateData structural model race effect. Erratum. 15 June 2022 publisher correction issued copyright line ; parameter, equation, unit changed.

title: Mo_2018_olaratumab
text: Simulate olaratumab (anti-PDGFRα human IgG1 monoclonal antibody) serum concentrations using final two-compartment population PK model Mo et al. (2018). pooled analysis included 1501 serum concentration observations 171 patients across four phase II studies advanced metastatic cancer: soft tissue sarcoma (STS, n = 95), nonsmall cell lung cancer (NSCLC, n = 50), gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST, n = 19), glioblastoma multiforme (GBM, n = 7). structural model two-compartment IV model linear clearance. final covariates body weight (CL V1, power) baseline tumor size (CL, linear deviation population median). Target- mediated drug disposition tested Michaelis-Menten approximation rejected due model instability; treatment-emergent anti-drug antibodies (TE-ADA, 5% patients) effect CL included. 57:355-365. Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40262-017-0562-0

title: Mo_2018_olaratumab
text: See inst/references/covariate-columns.md canonical register.

title: Mo_2018_olaratumab
text: Approximate Mo 2018 Table 2 distributions pooled 171-patient cohort (medians, ranges, marginal counts — paper publish individual-level covariate data).
code: set.seed(2018) n_subj <- 200  # Weight (kg): log-normal around median 79.7, clipped to observed range 37.3-151 WT <- pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(79.7), 0.27), 37.3), 151)  # Tumor size (mm): log-normal around median 86.5, clipped to observed range 12-571. # Table 2 n=164 with tumor size (7 GBM patients had none); in simulation every subject # gets a value, which is conservative for the CL covariate effect. TUMSZ <- pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(86.5), 0.9), 12), 571)  pop <- data.frame(   ID    = seq_len(n_subj),   WT    = WT,   TUMSZ = TUMSZ )

title: Mo_2018_olaratumab
text: Two regimens simulated span dose range used analysis: 15 mg/kg days 1 8 21-day cycle (STS NSCLC studies), ≥ 8 cycles. 20 mg/kg every 14 days (GBM GIST studies). Infusion duration taken 60 min (STS-study protocol); time hours throughout.
code: hour_per_day   <- 24 cycle_h_21     <- 21 * hour_per_day cycle_h_14     <- 14 * hour_per_day n_cycles       <- 6 inf_h          <- 1   # 60-min IV infusion  dose_times_15 <- as.numeric(outer(   c(0, 7 * hour_per_day),             # days 1 and 8 within a cycle   seq_len(n_cycles) - 1,              # cycle index 0..n_cycles-1   FUN = function(doy, cy) doy + cy * cycle_h_21 ))  dose_times_20 <- seq(0, (n_cycles - 1) * cycle_h_14, by = cycle_h_14)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 7 * hour_per_day, by = 4),              # rich through day 7   seq(7 * hour_per_day, cycle_h_21, by = 12),    # pre/post dose through cycle 1   seq(cycle_h_21, n_cycles * cycle_h_21, by = 24),   seq(n_cycles * cycle_h_21,       n_cycles * cycle_h_21 + 60 * hour_per_day, by = 24) )))  make_cohort <- function(dose_mg_per_kg, dose_times, cohort_label,                         id_offset, pop_sub) {   n <- nrow(pop_sub)   d_dose <- pop_sub |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(       AMT     = dose_mg_per_kg * WT,       EVID    = 1,       CMT     = "central",       DUR     = inf_h,       DV      = NA_real_,       cohort  = cohort_label,       ID      = id_offset + ID     )   d_obs <- pop_sub |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(       AMT     = NA_real_,       EVID    = 0,       CMT     = "central",       DUR     = NA_real_,       DV      = NA_real_,       cohort  = cohort_label,       ID      = id_offset + ID     )   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) }  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   make_cohort(15, dose_times_15, "15 mg/kg q1w x2 / 21-day cycle",               id_offset = 0L,   pop_sub = pop),   make_cohort(20, dose_times_20, "20 mg/kg q2w",               id_offset = 1000L, pop_sub = pop) ) |>   dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>   as.data.frame()  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(events[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")]))

title: Mo_2018_olaratumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Mo_2018_olaratumab") set.seed(20180301) sim <- rxSolve(mod, events,                keep = c("cohort"),                returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Mo_2018_olaratumab
text: Replicates shape Figure 2 Mo et al. (2018) — full time course olaratumab serum concentrations first dose rich cycle-1 sampling. simulated median 90% prediction interval capture typical Cmax near end--infusion, rapid distribution, sustained exposure across repeat dosing.
