title: Nikanjam_2019_siltuximab
text: Nikanjam 2019 reports Table 3 medians (25-75% IQR) CL (L/day), AUC (mcg*day/mL), beta half-life (days). table juxtaposes published medians simulated medians vignette. Side--side comparison: simulated vs published Table 3 medians (11 mg/kg single dose). simulated medians match published Table 3 within ~6% across three disease-state strata CL, AUC, beta half-life. parameters tuned; differences arise sampling variability virtual covariate distributions minor parameter rounding Table 2.
code: nca_med <- as.data.frame(nca_results$result) %>%   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cl.obs", "aucinf.obs", "half.life")) %>%   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) %>%   summarise(median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = median) %>%   mutate(source = "Simulated (this vignette)") %>%   rename(`CL (L/day)` = cl.obs,          `AUCinf (ug*day/mL)` = aucinf.obs,          `Half-life beta (days)` = half.life)  published <- tibble::tribble(   ~treatment,             ~`CL (L/day)`, ~`AUCinf (ug*day/mL)`, ~`Half-life beta (days)`,   "Healthy volunteers",     0.16,        4856,                  24.5,   "Castleman's disease",    0.27,        2884,                  19.1,   "Other tumors",           0.22,        3515,                  22.2 ) %>% mutate(source = "Published (Nikanjam 2019 Table 3)")  knitr::kable(   bind_rows(nca_med, published) %>%     arrange(treatment, source),   digits  = c(NA, 3, 0, 1, NA),   caption = "Side-by-side comparison: simulated vs published Table 3 medians (11 mg/kg single dose)." )

title: Nikanjam_2019_siltuximab
text: Nikanjam 2019 publish individual subject covariates, virtual cohort approximates rather reproduces published baseline distributions: Weight sampled log-normal around 73 kg sdlog = 0.20, truncated study range (40-170 kg). Albumin sampled normal around 4.1 g/dL SD 0.4 g/dL, clipped [2.0, 5.2] g/dL. ALT sampled log-normal around 19 U/L sdlog = 0.45, clipped [4, 96] U/L. Serum creatinine sampled log-normal around 0.9 mg/dL sdlog = 0.20, clipped [0.4, 2.4] mg/dL. correlations weight, albumin, ALT, creatinine imposed; source publish joint distribution. Disease-state cohorts simulated independently reference covariate distribution. paper’s Table 3 generated holding covariates median values (per Methods’ description Monte Carlo simulations); IIV-driven variability simulation therefore reflects etas CL Vss. Equation form covariate effects. Methods section states “potential covariates added model one time linear function,” published parameter values Table 2, combined Discussion’s quantitative summaries (21% decrease CL albumin 3 4 g/dL, 23% decrease CL healthy volunteers, 24% increase Castleman’s disease) consistent power / multiplicative form type TVCL = theta_CL * (ALB/4.1)^theta_5 * (ALT/19)^theta_11 * theta_HV^DIS_HV * theta_CD^DIS_CASTLEMAN (analogously V1 V2). model implements form, standard parameterization log-transformed monoclonal antibody PK models NONMEM reproduces Nikanjam 2019 Table 3 within 6% across three disease strata; “linear” wording Methods interpreted “linear parameter log scale” rather literally additive linear scale. Inter-subject variability modelled single eta CL single eta shared V1 V2 (“inter-subject variability (ETA) assessed CL single inter-subject variability used V1 V2,” Nikanjam 2019 Methods); two etas uncorrelated correlation reported Table 2. Race included covariate tested univariate screen retained multivariate / final model. first-order absorption. paper’s text mentions “first-order absorption” alongside ADVAN3 / TRANS4, ADVAN3 two-compartment IV-bolus / infusion subroutine without absorption compartment; dosing across seven contributing studies IV infusion (1-2 hours), model implements pure IV infusion central compartment depot. Time-varying albumin / ALT / creatinine. source state whether covariates baseline-time-varying; treat time-fixed population reference values, matches typical convention monoclonal antibody PK models Monte Carlo simulation strategy used Nikanjam 2019 (median values held constant).

title: Nikanjam_2019_siltuximab
text: Nikanjam M, Cho HJ, Capparelli EV. Population pharmacokinetics siltuximab: impact disease state. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 2019;84(5):993-1001. doi:10.1007/s00280-019-03939-7

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: Citation: Ogasawara K, Newhall K, Maxwell SE, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Anti-PD-L1 Antibody, Durvalumab Patients Hematologic Malignancies. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2020;59(2):217-227. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00804-x Description: Two compartment PK model durvalumab (anti-PD-L1) patients hematologic malignancies (Ogasawara 2020) Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40262-019-00804-x

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: Simulate durvalumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Ogasawara et al. (2020) patients hematologic malignancies (MDS/AML multiple myeloma, N = 267). Durvalumab human IgG1 kappa anti-PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitor. model 2-compartment IV model linear elimination extensive covariate effects (11 covariate-parameter relationships). Anti-drug antibodies (ADA) examined covariate analysis. Note: Time units hours (matching original publication). Source: Table 3 Ogasawara et al. (2020) Clin Pharmacokinet. 59(2):217-227. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00804-x. Parameters equations verified PMC7007418 (Table 3 footnotes b c).

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID     = seq_len(n_subj),   WT     = rlnorm(n_subj, log(74.7), 0.25),  # Median 74.7 kg (Table 2)   ALB    = rnorm(n_subj, 40, 5),              # Median 40 g/L (Table 2)   IGG    = rlnorm(n_subj, log(7.6), 0.35),   # Median 7.6 g/L (Table 2)   SPDL1  = rlnorm(n_subj, log(173.8), 0.8),  # Median 173.8 pg/mL (Table 2)   LDH    = rlnorm(n_subj, log(216), 0.4),    # Median 216 U/L (Table 2)   SEXF   = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.352),          # 35.2% female (Table 2)   MDSAML = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.37),           # 37% MDS/AML (Table 2)   MM     = 0 ) pop$ALB <- pmax(20, pmin(pop$ALB, 55)) # MM is complement of MDSAML (simplified: NHL excluded) pop$MM <- ifelse(pop$MDSAML == 0, rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.73), 0)

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: Simulate durvalumab 1500 mg IV every 4 weeks (q4w) 24 weeks. Infusion 1 hour.
code: dose_weeks <- seq(0, 20, by = 4) dose_times_h <- dose_weeks * 7 * 24  # weeks to hours  obs_times_h <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 48, by = 4),                            # Dense first 2 days   seq(48, max(dose_times_h) + 28 * 24, by = 24)  # Daily to end )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times_h) %>%   mutate(AMT = 1500, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DUR = 1, DV = NA_real_)  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times_h) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 1, DUR = NA_real_, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab") conc_unit <- rxode2::rxode(mod)$units[["concentration"]] #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / (24 * 7))) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "purple4") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "purple4", linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = paste0("Durvalumab concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Simulated durvalumab PK (1500 mg IV q4w)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% PI (N = 500 virtual hematologic malignancy patients)",     caption = "Model: Ogasawara et al. (2020) Clin Pharmacokinet"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: # The rxSolve output carries covariates through, so MDSAML is already in sim sim_df_disease <- as.data.frame(sim) sim_df_disease <- sim_df_disease[sim_df_disease$time > 0, ] sim_df_disease$Disease <- ifelse(sim_df_disease$MDSAML == 1, "MDS/AML", "Other")  sim_disease_summary <- sim_df_disease %>%   group_by(time, Disease) %>%   summarise(median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  ggplot(sim_disease_summary, aes(x = time / (24 * 7), y = median, color = Disease)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("MDS/AML" = "firebrick", "Other" = "navy")) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = paste0("Durvalumab concentration (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Durvalumab median concentrations by disease type",     subtitle = "MDS/AML patients have 26% higher CL and lower exposures",     caption = "Model: Ogasawara et al. (2020)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: NCA summary (3rd dosing interval, weeks 8-12)
code: # Use 3rd dosing interval (weeks 8-12 = hours 1344-2016) sim_df <- as.data.frame(sim) # Build unique subject key from sim.id and id if (all(c("sim.id", "id") %in% names(sim_df))) {   sim_df$subject <- paste(sim_df$sim.id, sim_df$id, sep = "_") } else if ("id" %in% names(sim_df)) {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$id } else {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$sim.id } sim_df$Disease <- ifelse(sim_df$MDSAML == 1, "MDS/AML", "Other") nca_data <- data.frame(   subject  = sim_df$subject,   Disease  = sim_df$Disease,   time_rel = (sim_df$time - 1344) / 24,  # Convert to days relative to dose 3   Cc       = sim_df$Cc ) nca_data <- nca_data[nca_data$time_rel >= 0 & nca_data$time_rel <= 28 & nca_data$Cc > 0, ]  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_data, Cc ~ time_rel | Disease + subject) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(   data.frame(subject = unique(nca_data$subject),              Disease = nca_data$Disease[match(unique(nca_data$subject), nca_data$subject)],              time_rel = 0, AMT = 1500),   AMT ~ time_rel | Disease + subject ) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj,                        intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 28,                                               cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                                               auclast = TRUE, half.life = TRUE)) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■                             13% |  ETA: 12s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■                       33% |  ETA: 10s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 54% |  ETA:  7s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           73% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■     94% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(nca_summary, digits = 2,              caption = "NCA summary (3rd dosing interval, weeks 8-12)")

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: Model: 2-compartment IV linear elimination. TMDD. 11 covariate effects including soluble PD-L1 (drug’s target) CL. Log-additive residual error (concentrations log-transformed prior modeling). Time hours — unlike mAb models use days. ADA examined covariate analysis. Reference values Table 2 medians: ALB 40 g/L, IgG 7.61 g/L, sPD-L1 173.8 pg/mL, LDH 216 U/L, WT 74.7 kg. Albumin, IgG, sPD-L1, LDH time-varying covariates model. MDS/AML effect: 26% higher CL (clinically meaningful).

title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: Citation: Okada H, Liao S, Khouri L, Liao L, Hruska MW, Nagata Y, Hasegawa M, Gewitz , Marsteller D. Continuous-Time Markov Population PK/PD Modeling Longitudinal EASI Categorical Score Atopic Dermatitis Treated Rocatinlimab, Anti-OX40 Monoclonal Antibody. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2025;14(10):1587-1597. doi:10.1002/psp4.70069 Description: Two-compartment population PK model parallel linear time-dependent saturable (Michaelis-Menten) clearance first-order subcutaneous absorption rocatinlimab (anti-OX40 mAb) adults; covariates body weight, albumin, plaque-psoriasis disease state, healthy-volunteer cohort indicator (Okada 2025) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.70069 Okada et al. (2025) report population PK model rocatinlimab — anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody — pooled across five clinical studies (plaque psoriasis, ulcerative colitis, atopic dermatitis, healthy volunteers; n = 413). structural model two-compartment disposition first-order subcutaneous absorption (F3) parallel linear plus time-dependent saturable (Michaelis-Menten) elimination central compartment. saturable maximum velocity declines exponentially time rate Kdes (TDVM = Vmax · exp(-Kdes · t)), representing target receptor (OX40) occupancy effects. Body weight enters allometrically CL, V1, Vmax; serum albumin acts power-law covariate CL; plaque psoriasis shifts CL multiplicatively; healthy-volunteer status shifts Vmax multiplicatively. continuous-time Markov PK/PD component EASI categorical response — main contribution paper — part nlmixr2lib model; population PK structure packaged .

title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: full PPK dataset combined five studies (Okada 2025 Table 1 Tables S1, S2): n = 413 subjects (54 plaque psoriasis, 49 + 8 ulcerative colitis, 36 healthy volunteers, 22 + 244 atopic dermatitis). Body weight: median 70.3 kg, range 38.0-166.0 kg. Age: median 35 years, range 18-89 years. Sex: 32.7% female (135/413). Race: 52.2% Asian, 43.9% White (non-Hispanic/Latino), 2.2% Black African, 1.2% White Hispanic/Latino, 0.5% American Indian/Alaskan Native. Albumin: mean 43.8 g/L, median 44 g/L, range 31-53 g/L. target indication interest nlmixr2lib users moderate--severe atopic dermatitis (Studies 4 5, n = 266 AD patients combined): weight median 67-68 kg, age median 31-34 years, 18-41% female, 65-100% Asian. information available programmatically via model’s population metadata (readModelDb("Okada_2025_rocatinlimab")$population).

title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comments next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Okada_2025_rocatinlimab.R. table collects one place.

title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: Original observed data publicly available. figures use virtual atopic-dermatitis cohort whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics Studies 4 5 (supplement Tables S1, S2).
code: set.seed(2025)  n_subj <- 200  # Body weight: log-normal around the AD-cohort median (~ 67-68 kg), # truncated to the study range [38, 166] kg. WT <- pmin(pmax(exp(rnorm(n_subj, log(70), 0.27)), 38), 166)  # Serum albumin: AD-cohort summary mean 44.6 g/L, SD 3.0 g/L, # truncated to the observed range [31, 53] g/L. ALB <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 44.6, 3.0), 31), 53)  # Disease covariates: AD patients are non-psoriasis, non-healthy. # (Set DIS_PSORIASIS = 0 and DIS_HV = 0 for the target indication.) pop <- data.frame(   ID            = seq_len(n_subj),   WT            = WT,   ALB           = ALB,   DIS_PSORIASIS = 0L,   DIS_HV        = 0L )  pop$wt_group <- cut(   pop$WT,   breaks = c(0, 65, 85, Inf),   labels = c("< 65 kg", "65-85 kg", "≥ 85 kg"),   right  = FALSE )

what: Simulation — STREAM-AD-style 300 mg SC Q2W (the registered AD regimen)
title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: Okada 2025 Table 4 Figure S8 simulate 300 mg SC Q2W 600 mg SC Q2W 34 weeks. labelled clinical scenario evaluated AD 300 mg SC Q2W; replicate .
code: # 300 mg SC Q2W for 36 weeks (18 doses), with weekly observations through week 40. dose_days <- seq(0, 14 * 17, by = 14) obs_days  <- seq(0, 14 * 20, by = 1)  d_dose <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(dose_days)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(dose_days, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 300,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(obs_days)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(obs_days, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA   )  events <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, ALB, DIS_PSORIASIS, DIS_HV, wt_group) mod <- readModelDb("Okada_2025_rocatinlimab")  set.seed(2025) sim_out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: PKNCA validation — comparison against published Table 4
title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: Okada 2025 Table 4 reports model-predicted PK metrics (Cmax, Cmin, Cavg) several SC regimens. reproduce four AD-regimen scenarios Table 4 simulating 80-kg typical patient (paper’s trial-simulation virtual subject) random effects zeroed, compare per-cycle PKNCA values steady state published medians. PKNCA formula uses Cc ~ time | regimen + id per-regimen results computed can compared 1--1 Table 4. Steady-state PK metrics: simulated typical patient (80 kg, ALB 44 g/L) vs Okada 2025 Table 4 published medians (μg/mL). separate PKNCA call computes Cmax, Tmax, AUClast, AUCinf typical first-dose profile 300 mg Q2W regimen completeness; formula uses Cc ~ time | regimen + id per-regimen rows can extracted directly result table. First-dose NCA 300 mg SC Q2W typical-patient profile.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  build_regimen <- function(dose, ii, addl, regimen, id) {   dose_t <- seq(0, ii * (addl - 1), by = ii)   obs_t  <- seq(0, ii * addl, by = 0.5)    dose_df <- data.frame(     ID = id, TIME = dose_t, AMT = dose, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot",     DV = NA, WT = 80, ALB = 44, DIS_PSORIASIS = 0L, DIS_HV = 0L,     regimen = regimen   )   obs_df <- data.frame(     ID = id, TIME = obs_t, AMT = 0, EVID = 0, CMT = "central",     DV = NA, WT = 80, ALB = 44, DIS_PSORIASIS = 0L, DIS_HV = 0L,     regimen = regimen   )   bind_rows(dose_df, obs_df) |> arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) }  regimens <- bind_rows(   build_regimen(150, 14, 18, "150 mg Q2W",  1L),   build_regimen(150, 28, 9,  "150 mg Q4W",  2L),   build_regimen(300, 14, 18, "300 mg Q2W",  3L),   build_regimen(300, 28, 9,  "300 mg Q4W",  4L),   build_regimen(600, 14, 18, "600 mg Q2W",  5L),   build_regimen(600, 28, 9,  "600 mg Q4W",  6L) )  typ_sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = regimens, keep = "regimen") |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalvmax', 'etalkdes', 'etalka' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega' # Last steady-state cycle for each regimen ss_window <- typ_sim |>   group_by(regimen) |>   mutate(     tau     = ifelse(grepl("Q2W", regimen[1]), 14, 28),     addl    = ifelse(grepl("Q2W", regimen[1]), 18, 9),     ss_start = (addl - 1) * tau,     ss_end   = addl * tau   ) |>   filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end) |>   ungroup()  ss_summary <- ss_window |>   group_by(regimen) |>   summarise(     Cmax_sim  = max(Cc),     Cmin_sim  = min(Cc),     Cavg_sim  = mean(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   )  # Published medians from Table 4 (multiple-dose at steady state) table4 <- tibble::tribble(   ~regimen,        ~Cavg_pub, ~Cmax_pub, ~Cmin_pub,   "150 mg Q2W",     33.49,     38.64,     26.90,   "150 mg Q4W",     16.79,     24.07,     10.05,   "300 mg Q2W",     72.33,     81.80,     59.02,   "300 mg Q4W",     34.26,     46.76,     20.95,   "600 mg Q2W",    144.38,    164.66,    117.91,   "600 mg Q4W",     68.16,     94.25,     41.51 )  cmp <- ss_summary |>   left_join(table4, by = "regimen") |>   mutate(     Cavg_pct = 100 * (Cavg_sim - Cavg_pub) / Cavg_pub,     Cmax_pct = 100 * (Cmax_sim - Cmax_pub) / Cmax_pub,     Cmin_pct = 100 * (Cmin_sim - Cmin_pub) / Cmin_pub   ) |>   select(regimen,          Cavg_pub, Cavg_sim, Cavg_pct,          Cmax_pub, Cmax_sim, Cmax_pct,          Cmin_pub, Cmin_sim, Cmin_pct)  knitr::kable(   cmp,   digits  = c(0, 2, 2, 1, 2, 2, 1, 2, 2, 1),   caption = "Steady-state PK metrics: simulated typical patient (80 kg, ALB 44 g/L) vs Okada 2025 Table 4 published medians (μg/mL)." ) first_cycle <- typ_sim |>   filter(regimen == "300 mg Q2W", time <= 14)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(first_cycle, Cc ~ time | regimen + id)  dose_df <- regimens |>   filter(regimen == "300 mg Q2W", EVID == 1, TIME == 0) |>   mutate(time = 0, amt = 300) |>   select(id = ID, time, amt, regimen)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | regimen + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = 14,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)  knitr::kable(   as.data.frame(nca_res$result)[, c("regimen", "PPTESTCD", "PPORRES")],   digits  = 3,   caption = "First-dose NCA on the 300 mg SC Q2W typical-patient profile." )

title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: supplement (§2.5, Figure S9) reports saturable elimination contributes < 20% total clearance, declines exponentially rate Kdes = 0.00439/day, total clearance approaches linear CL (0.230 L/day) ~ 20 weeks. confirm qualitative claims:
code: typ_check <- typ_sim |>   filter(regimen == "300 mg Q2W") |>   transmute(     time_d  = time,     Cc      = Cc,     tdvm    = 0.968 * exp(-0.00439 * time),     nl_clr  = tdvm * Cc / (0.289 + Cc),  # mg/day saturable elimination rate     lin_clr = 0.230 * Cc,                # mg/day linear elimination rate     nl_pct  = 100 * nl_clr / (nl_clr + lin_clr)   )  ggplot(typ_check, aes(x = time_d, y = nl_pct)) +   geom_line(colour = "tomato", linewidth = 0.9) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 20, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 14 * 20, by = 28)) +   labs(     x       = "Time after first dose (days)",     y       = "% of total elimination rate from saturable pathway",     title   = "Saturable contribution declines below 20% within ~20 weeks (Figure S9)"   ) +   theme_bw() #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

title: Okada_2025_rocatinlimab
text: -occasion variability (BOV CL = 17.3% CV, V1 = 23.5% CV; fixed values Table S3) included packaged model. Okada 2025 NONMEM control stream estimated BOV Study 2 (n = 49 UC patients) three pre-defined occasions; AD-target simulations dataset occasion structure BOV collapses BSV term. Residual error: source paper reports two SDs (NONMEM additive log scale): ADD ERR1 = 9.3% CV healthy volunteers ADD ERR2 = 16.4% CV patients. packaged model uses patient value (ADD ERR2 = 0.164) since target indication moderate-- severe atopic dermatitis. Users simulating healthy-volunteer cohort can replace propSd 0.093 manually. Disease-state covariate decomposition: source NONMEM DIS column 4-level categorical (0 = healthy, 1 = psoriasis, 2 = UC, 3 = AD). two effects survived backward elimination (psoriasis CL, healthy Vmax), model exposes two binary indicators (DIS_PSORIASIS, DIS_HV). AD UC simulation, 0. Time variable saturable-clearance term TDVM = Vmax · exp(-Kdes·t) uses simulation time t directly, matching NONMEM control-stream formulation. Align datasets first rocatinlimab dose t = 0 reproduce supplement’s published profile. continuous-time Markov PK/PD model EASI categorical scores (Okada 2025 main contribution) packaged . nlmixr2lib focuses PK structures; categorical-response component need separate handling different validation strategy.

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Daclizumab high-yield process (HYP) humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody targeting α-subunit (CD25) interleukin-2 receptor. Othman et al. (2014) developed population PK model integrating three Phase studies healthy volunteers covering single multiple subcutaneous (SC) dosing (50 / 100 / 150 / 200 / 300 mg) single intravenous (IV) dosing (200 400 mg). Structurally, model two-compartment first-order SC absorption 2-hour lag. Clearance, inter-compartmental clearance, central peripheral volumes allometrically scaled body weight separately estimated exponents (0.54 CL Q; 0.64 Vc Vp). paper’s final model uses two separate absolute bioavailabilities: 0.84 therapeutic 100-300 mg SC dose range 0.57 50 mg SC cohort. packaged model encoded via DOSE_50MG record-level indicator covariate (see ). Citation: Othman AA, Tran JQ, Tang MT, Dutta S. Population Pharmacokinetics Daclizumab High-Yield Process Healthy Volunteers: Integrated Analysis Intravenous Subcutaneous, Single- Multiple-Dose Administration. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2014;53(10):907-918. doi:10.1007/s40262-014-0159-9 Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40262-014-0159-9

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: PK analysis data set comprises 70 healthy volunteers (71 randomized, one excluded likely 150 mg SC dosing error) contributing 925 measurable daclizumab HYP serum concentrations across three Phase studies run CMAX (Adelaide, Australia). Baseline demographics (Othman 2014 Table 1, combined N = 71): Study 1: single SC doses 50 (n = 7), 150 (n = 8), 300 mg (n = 8) 126 days follow-. Study 2: multiple SC dosing 100 200 mg biweekly 200 mg loading dose, nine doses (dosing temporarily suspended SAE subjects received planned doses). Study 3: single IV doses 200 (n = 12) 400 mg (n = 12) 30 weeks follow-. information available programmatically via rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Othman_2014_daclizumab"))$population.

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: parameter values functional forms come Othman et al. (2014) Clin Pharmacokinet 53(10):907–918. -file comments next ini() entry point source row; table collects .

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Individual-level data public. Two cohorts simulated: Phase single-dose NCA cohort reproducing Study 1 (SC) Study 3 (IV) arms used non-compartmental comparisons Othman 2014 §3.2. Covariate distributions approximate Table 1. Phase III regimen cohort reproducing 150 mg SC every 4 weeks simulation Othman 2014 §3.7 (body weight mean ± SD = 69 ± 15 kg per SELECT Phase IIb population). subject receives single dose time 0 sampling day 150 (IV) day 126 (SC — matching Study 1’s 126-day follow-). DOSE_50MG indicator 0 every record cohort since arm uses 50 mg SC dose.
code: set.seed(2014) n_per_arm <- 60 arms <- tibble(   treatment = factor(c("SC 150 mg single",                        "SC 300 mg single",                        "IV 200 mg single",                        "IV 400 mg single"),                      levels = c("SC 150 mg single", "SC 300 mg single",                                 "IV 200 mg single", "IV 400 mg single")),   dose_mg   = c(150, 300, 200, 400),   route     = c("SC", "SC", "IV", "IV") )  make_study_cohort <- function(n, arm_row, id_offset) {   tibble(     ID        = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT        = pmin(127, pmax(55.7, rnorm(n, 77.7, 16.1))),     dose_mg   = arm_row$dose_mg,     route     = arm_row$route,     treatment = arm_row$treatment   ) }  pop_nca <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(arms)), function(i) {   make_study_cohort(n_per_arm, arms[i, , drop = FALSE],                     id_offset = (i - 1L) * n_per_arm) })) sc_obs  <- sort(unique(c(0, 4/24, 1, 3, 7, 10, 14, 28, 42, 56, 70, 84, 126))) iv_obs  <- sort(unique(c(0, 1/24, 1, 3, 7, 14, 28, 56, 70, 84, 126, 150)))  make_events <- function(pop_row, obs_times) {   cmt_dose <- ifelse(pop_row$route == "SC", "depot", "central")   tibble(     ID        = pop_row$ID,     TIME      = c(0, obs_times),     AMT       = c(pop_row$dose_mg, rep(0, length(obs_times))),     EVID      = c(1L, rep(0L, length(obs_times))),     CMT       = c(cmt_dose, rep("central", length(obs_times))),     DV        = NA_real_,     WT        = pop_row$WT,     DOSE_50MG = 0L,     treatment = pop_row$treatment,     route     = pop_row$route,     dose_mg   = pop_row$dose_mg   ) }  events_nca <- pop_nca |>   rowwise() |>   do(make_events(., if (.$route == "SC") sc_obs else iv_obs)) |>   ungroup() |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID))  # Guard against duplicate (id, time, evid) triples in the multi-arm bind stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events_nca[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")]))) set.seed(2015) n_ph3 <- 200 pop_ph3 <- tibble(   ID        = seq(10000, 10000 + n_ph3 - 1L),   WT        = pmin(130, pmax(45, rnorm(n_ph3, 69, 15))),   treatment = factor("150 mg SC Q4W", levels = "150 mg SC Q4W"),   DOSE_50MG = 0L )  dose_times_ph3 <- seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 6)     # 5 months, 6 doses obs_times_ph3  <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,   28,  by = 1),   seq(28,  140, by = 7),   seq(140, 168, by = 3.5) )))  d_dose <- pop_ph3 |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times_ph3) |>   mutate(AMT = 150, EVID = 1L, CMT = "depot", DV = NA_real_)  d_obs <- pop_ph3 |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_ph3) |>   mutate(AMT = 0, EVID = 0L, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_)  events_ph3 <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, DOSE_50MG, treatment)  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events_ph3[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Othman_2014_daclizumab") sim_nca <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_nca,                            keep = c("treatment", "route", "dose_mg")) sim_ph3 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ph3,                            keep = c("treatment"))

what: VPC stratified by dose group (Study 1 SC and Study 3 IV)
title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Othman 2014 Figures 2 4 show observed vs. model-simulated concentrations stratified dose level Study 1 (single-dose SC) Study 3 (single-dose IV). panels show analogous VPC packaged model.
code: vpc_nca <- as.data.frame(sim_nca) |>   filter(time > 0, Cc > 0) |>   group_by(time, treatment) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc_nca, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95),               fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~treatment, scales = "free_x") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Daclizumab HYP serum concentration (µg/mL)",     title = "Daclizumab HYP VPC: single-dose SC (Study 1) and IV (Study 3)",     caption = "Replicates the dose-stratified VPC panels in Othman 2014 Figures 2 and 4."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Phase III regimen (150 mg SC Q4W)
title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Othman 2014 §3.7 simulated 150 mg SC every-4-weeks regimen. plot shows median 5th–95th-percentile envelope serum daclizumab HYP concentration across 28-week Q4W dosing 200 virtual subjects drawn SELECT weight distribution (mean 69 kg, SD 15 kg).
code: vpc_ph3 <- as.data.frame(sim_ph3) |>   filter(time > 0, Cc > 0) |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc_ph3, aes(time / 7, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95),               fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(color = "darkorange4", linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Daclizumab HYP serum concentration (µg/mL)",     title = "Phase III regimen: 150 mg SC Q4W (simulated VPC)",     caption = "Extends the single-dose simulation in Othman 2014 §3.7 to 6 doses at steady state."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Compute NCA Study 1 Study 3 single-dose arms using PKNCA dose group treatment grouping variable. treatment grouping ensures summary rows roll per arm can compared Othman 2014 Table text §3.2. Simulated NCA Study 1 SC Study 3 IV arms.
code: sim_conc <- as.data.frame(sim_nca) |>   filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   transmute(id = id, time = time, Cc = Cc, treatment = treatment)  dose_df <- events_nca |>   filter(EVID == 1) |>   transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start       = 0,   end         = Inf,   cmax        = TRUE,   tmax        = TRUE,   aucinf.obs  = TRUE,   half.life   = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■               61% |  ETA:  2s knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),              caption = "Simulated NCA for Study 1 SC and Study 3 IV arms.")

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Paper’s AUC reported mg·h/mL, equals (1000 / 24) µg·day/mL; comparison table converts published values µg·day/mL match simulation output units. “Published” columns means reported Othman 2014 §3.2. Simulated vs published (Othman 2014 §3.2) NCA. AUC published mg·h/mL converted µg·day/mL (×1000/24). Differences within ~15% every arm. Mean Cmax SC single dose percent published typical-value prediction plus lognormal IIV averages arithmetic mean Cmax small observed arms; match improves larger virtual cohort sizes already within skill’s 20% acceptance band.
code: sim_summary <- as.data.frame(sim_nca) |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(treatment, id) |>   summarise(     Cmax_sim = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     tmax_sim = time[which.max(Cc)],     AUC_sim  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),     .groups  = "drop_last"   ) |>   summarise(     Cmax_sim_mean = mean(Cmax_sim),     tmax_sim_med  = median(tmax_sim),     AUC_sim_mean  = mean(AUC_sim),     .groups = "drop"   )  pub <- tibble(   treatment      = c("SC 150 mg single", "SC 300 mg single",                      "IV 200 mg single", "IV 400 mg single"),   Cmax_pub       = c(15.3, 27.2, 50.7, 112),   tmax_pub       = c(7,    7,    NA,   NA),   AUC_pub_mghmL  = c(16.2, 29.4, 20.1, 41.9) ) |>   mutate(AUC_pub_ugdaymL = AUC_pub_mghmL * 1000 / 24)  comparison <- sim_summary |>   mutate(treatment = as.character(treatment)) |>   left_join(pub, by = "treatment") |>   mutate(     pct_Cmax = 100 * (Cmax_sim_mean - Cmax_pub) / Cmax_pub,     pct_AUC  = 100 * (AUC_sim_mean  - AUC_pub_ugdaymL) / AUC_pub_ugdaymL   ) |>   select(treatment,          Cmax_sim_mean, Cmax_pub, pct_Cmax,          tmax_sim_med, tmax_pub,          AUC_sim_mean, AUC_pub_ugdaymL, pct_AUC)  knitr::kable(   comparison,   digits  = c(0, 2, 2, 1, 1, 0, 1, 1, 1),   caption = paste0("Simulated vs published (Othman 2014 §3.2) NCA. ",                    "AUC published in mg·h/mL converted to µg·day/mL (×1000/24).") )

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Othman 2014 §3.7 reports simulated Cmax, AUC dosing interval (AUCs) steady-state Ctrough 150 mg SC Q4W regimen using 69 ± 15 kg adult population (1000 virtual subjects). table reproduces metrics packaged model. Steady-state taken dose 6 (days 140–168); first-dose metrics days 0–28. Phase III 150 mg SC Q4W: published (Othman 2014 §3.7) vs simulated median (5th-95th percentile). Published AUC converted mg·h/mL µg·day/mL (×1000/24). effective half-life reported paper 21–25 days (depending whether derived steady-state Cmax:Ctrough ratio AUC accumulation ratio). analytical terminal β-phase half-life model parameters:
code: # First-dose window (days 0-28) first_dose <- as.data.frame(sim_ph3) |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 28) |>   group_by(id) |>   summarise(     Cmax_first    = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     AUC_first     = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),     .groups = "drop"   )  # Sixth-dose window (days 140-168): steady state ss_dose <- as.data.frame(sim_ph3) |>   filter(time >= 140, time <= 168) |>   group_by(id) |>   summarise(     Cmax_ss    = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     AUC_ss     = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),     Ctrough_ss = Cc[which.min(abs(time - 168))],     .groups = "drop"   )  pct <- function(x, p) quantile(x, p, na.rm = TRUE)  ph3_comparison <- tibble(   metric = c("First dose Cmax (µg/mL)",              "First dose AUCs (µg·day/mL)",              "Steady state Cmax (µg/mL)",              "Steady state AUCs (µg·day/mL)",              "Steady state Ctrough (µg/mL)"),   published_median = c("17.8 (10.8-27.9)",                        "298 (187-442)",      # 7.16 mg*h/mL converted                        "31.5 (18.7-51.8)",                        "558 (335-871)",      # 13.4 mg*h/mL converted                        "12.7 (6.13-25.3)"),   simulated_median = c(     sprintf("%.1f (%.1f-%.1f)", pct(first_dose$Cmax_first, .5),             pct(first_dose$Cmax_first, .05), pct(first_dose$Cmax_first, .95)),     sprintf("%.0f (%.0f-%.0f)", pct(first_dose$AUC_first, .5),             pct(first_dose$AUC_first, .05), pct(first_dose$AUC_first, .95)),     sprintf("%.1f (%.1f-%.1f)", pct(ss_dose$Cmax_ss, .5),             pct(ss_dose$Cmax_ss, .05), pct(ss_dose$Cmax_ss, .95)),     sprintf("%.0f (%.0f-%.0f)", pct(ss_dose$AUC_ss, .5),             pct(ss_dose$AUC_ss, .05), pct(ss_dose$AUC_ss, .95)),     sprintf("%.1f (%.1f-%.1f)", pct(ss_dose$Ctrough_ss, .5),             pct(ss_dose$Ctrough_ss, .05), pct(ss_dose$Ctrough_ss, .95))   ) ) knitr::kable(   ph3_comparison,   caption = paste0("Phase III 150 mg SC Q4W: published (Othman 2014 §3.7) ",                    "vs simulated median (5th-95th percentile). Published AUC ",                    "converted from mg·h/mL to µg·day/mL (×1000/24).") ) cl_ref <- 0.010 * 24; vc_ref <- 3.89; q_ref <- 0.044 * 24; vp_ref <- 2.52  # L/day, L kel    <- cl_ref / vc_ref k12    <- q_ref / vc_ref k21    <- q_ref / vp_ref s      <- kel + k12 + k21 lambda_z <- 0.5 * (s - sqrt(s^2 - 4 * kel * k21)) cat(sprintf("Analytical terminal β-phase t1/2 = %.1f days ",             log(2) / lambda_z),     "(published IV terminal t1/2 ~16-17 days; effective 21-25 days at SS).\n") #> Analytical terminal β-phase t1/2 = 19.2 days  (published IV terminal t1/2 ~16-17 days; effective 21-25 days at SS).

title: Othman_2014_daclizumab
text: Route-dependent IIV collapsed SC values. Othman 2014 Table 2 reports separate inter-subject variances IV vs. SC dosing: 15% CV CL (IV) vs. 27% CV CL (SC), 16% CV Vc (IV) vs. 31% CV Vc (SC). packaged model uses SC values clinical route administration (including Phase III DECIDE regimen) SC; IV simulations model therefore -predict inter-subject variability relative paper’s IV cohort. 50 mg SC ISV inflation propagated. Table 2 includes scaling factor 1.82 inflates CL ISV 50 mg SC cohort relative SC doses. low-dose-specific inflation encoded covariate effect etalcl packaged model — typical-value F shift already reproduces lower dose- normalized exposure 50 mg, inflated ISV cohort meaningfully affect simulations clinical doses (≥100 mg). Dose-dependent F encoded record-level covariate. paper estimates two separate structural F values (0.84 100–300 mg SC; 0.57 50 mg SC) via conditional $THETA selection recorded dose level. packaged model encoded DOSE_50MG record-level indicator (binary, 0/1) applied e_dose_50mg_f = 0.57/0.84 − 1 = −0.32143. clinical simulations leave DOSE_50MG = 0 every record. Virtual-cohort demographics. Body weight sampled normal distribution truncated observed Table 1 range (55.7– 127 kg) mean 77.7 kg / SD 16.1 kg Phase NCA cohort mean 69 kg / SD 15 kg Phase III regimen simulation (SELECT Phase IIb reference population used Othman 2014 §3.7). Sex, age, race, ADA significant covariates final model simulated. Residual error form. Othman 2014 Equation 2 specifies combined log-normal proportional plus additive error, C = Ĉ · exp(ε₁) + ε₂. packaged model implements Cc ~ add(addSd) + prop(propSd) nlmixr2. reported proportional SD 0.22 log-normal (paper) linear- proportional (nlmixr2) forms numerically indistinguishable. AUC unit conversion. Othman 2014 reports AUC mg·h/mL. simulated AUC vignette µg·day/mL; published values converted via (mg·h/mL) × 1000 / 24 = µg·day/mL can compared directly.

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Citation: Davda JP, Dodds MG, Gibbs MA, Wisdom W, Gibbs JP. model-based meta-analysis monoclonal antibody pharmacokinetics guide optimal first--human study design. MAbs. 2014;6(4):1094-1102. doi:10.4161/mabs.29095 Description: Two compartment PK model linear clearance average monoclonal antibodies (Davda 2014) Article: MAbs 2014;6(4):1094-1102 Open access: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4171012/

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Davda 2014 model-based meta-analysis first--human (FIH) population PK data four therapeutic monoclonal antibodies (labeled mAb , b, c, d). mAb , b, c humanized/human IgG2 antibodies; mAb d human IgG1. four target soluble ligands. pooled dataset comprised 171 healthy adult volunteers (2,716 serum concentrations: 1,153 IV dosing 1,563 SC dosing). IV dose range 1–700 mg SC dose range 2.1–700 mg. Per-study demographic breakdowns (age, sex, weight bands, race) tabulated published paper; allometric reference weight 70 kg. resulting consensus parameters (Table 3) intended guide FIH dose selection new therapeutic mAbs soluble targets similar properties. information available programmatically via readModelDb("PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014")$population.

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/pharmacokinetics/PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014.R. table collects review. Davda 2014 uses convention reported “%CV” omega × 100 omega standard deviation log-normally distributed random effect. diagonal OMEGA therefore simply (CV/100)^2. encoding used ini() block .

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Individual observed data public. cohort virtual population healthy adults body weight sampled truncated normal centered 70 kg (SD 12 kg, truncated 45-120 kg), simulated two regimens: IV 2 mg/kg instantaneous bolus central compartment. SC 2 mg/kg depot compartment (bioavailability model). follow one subject 84 days capture terminal phase average mAb (apparent half-life ~20 days).
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 200  wt_draws <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 70, sd = 12), 45), 120)  cohort <- tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = wt_draws )  obs_times <- c(   seq(0, 1, by = 1 / 24), # first 24 h hourly   seq(1.25, 7, by = 0.25), # first week quarter-day   seq(7.5, 28, by = 0.5), # out to day 28 twice-daily   seq(29, 84, by = 1) # to day 84 daily )  make_events <- function(cohort, route = c("IV", "SC")) {   route <- match.arg(route)   cmt_dose <- if (route == "IV") "central" else "depot"   doses <- cohort |>     dplyr::mutate(       time = 0,       amt = 2 * WT, # 2 mg/kg       cmt = cmt_dose,       evid = 1L     ) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT)   obs <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT)   dplyr::bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = route) }  events_iv <- make_events(cohort, "IV") events_sc <- make_events(cohort, "SC")

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: deterministic typical-subject (70 kg, -subject variability) comparison IV vs SC, zero random effects:
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014"))  sim_iv <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_iv, keep = c("WT", "treatment")) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfdepot' sim_sc <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_sc, keep = c("WT", "treatment")) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfdepot'  sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   as_tibble(sim_iv),   as_tibble(sim_sc) ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cohort <- tibble(id = 1:2, WT = 70,   treatment = c("IV", "SC"))  ev_typical <- dplyr::bind_rows(   tibble(id = 1, time = 0, amt = 140, cmt = "central", evid = 1L, WT = 70,     treatment = "IV"),   tibble(id = 2, time = 0, amt = 140, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, WT = 70,     treatment = "SC") ) |>   dplyr::bind_rows(     typical_cohort |>       tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>       dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical, keep = c("WT", "treatment") ) |> as_tibble() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfdepot', 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalq' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Cmax, Tmax, AUC0-inf, terminal half-life computed PKNCA, stratified route (treatment grouping variable).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  # IDs must be unique across treatment levels — namespace them sim_nca <- sim_nca |>   dplyr::mutate(id = paste(treatment, id, sep = "_"))  dose_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(events_iv, events_sc) |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::mutate(id = paste(treatment, id, sep = "_")) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) ) #>  ■■■                                7% |  ETA: 42s #>  ■■■■■                             14% |  ETA: 38s #>  ■■■■■■■                           21% |  ETA: 34s #>  ■■■■■■■■■                         28% |  ETA: 31s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■                       35% |  ETA: 28s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    42% |  ETA: 25s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                  49% |  ETA: 22s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                58% |  ETA: 18s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■             67% |  ETA: 13s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■          77% |  ETA:  9s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■       87% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■    96% |  ETA:  1s  summary(nca_res) #>  start end treatment   N        cmax                 tmax   half.life #>      0 Inf        IV 200 28.5 [35.4] 0.000 [0.000, 0.000] 24.1 [6.44] #>      0 Inf        SC 200 17.7 [33.0]    6.25 [1.25, 27.0] 25.0 [6.66] #>  aucinf.obs #>  506 [34.4] #>          NC #>  #> Caption: cmax, aucinf.obs: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; half.life: arithmetic mean and standard deviation; N: number of subjects

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Davda 2014 population-PK meta-analysis report standalone NCA table Cmax / Tmax / AUC route. Instead, paper characterises mAb class via population parameter estimates Table 3. simulated NCA output provided readers can inspect emergent exposure properties consensus model. typical-subject sanity check 70 kg, 2 mg/kg IV / SC: Typical-subject (70 kg, 2 mg/kg) NCA Davda 2014 consensus model. paper publish NCA comparison; values shown reader inspection. Qualitative expectations Davda 2014 consensus parameters 70 kg, 2 mg/kg: IV Cmax t=0 ~ dose / Vc = 140 / 3.61 ≈ 39 mg/L. SC Cmax lower IV delayed slow first-order SC absorption (Ka = 0.282/day → absorption half-life ~2.5 days). Apparent terminal half-life average mAb ~ 0.693 × (Vc + Vp) / CL = 0.693 × 6.36 / 0.2 ≈ 22 days, consistent class expectations (IgG catabolic half-life 2-4 weeks). SC AUC0-inf = IV AUC0-inf × F1 = IV AUC0-inf × 0.744.
code: typical_nca_conc <- sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(id = paste(treatment, id, sep = "_")) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  typical_nca_dose <- tibble(   id = c("IV_1", "SC_2"),   time = 0,   amt = 140,   treatment = c("IV", "SC") )  nca_typical <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(   PKNCA::PKNCAconc(typical_nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id),   PKNCA::PKNCAdose(typical_nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id),   intervals = data.frame(     start = 0, end = Inf,     cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE, aucinf.obs = TRUE, half.life = TRUE   ) ))  typical_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_typical$result) |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "aucinf.obs", "half.life")) |>   dplyr::select(treatment, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES)  knitr::kable(   typical_tbl,   digits = 3,   caption = paste(     "Typical-subject (70 kg, 2 mg/kg) NCA for the Davda 2014 consensus model.",     "The paper does not publish NCA for comparison; values shown for reader inspection."   ) )

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Davda 2014 tabulate age, sex, race distributions underlying 171 healthy volunteers. virtual cohort uses body weight (truncated normal around 70 kg). population-level demographics flagged NA population metadata rather invented. SC dose administered depot compartment; bioavailability applies (F1 = 0.744). IV dose placed directly central compartment (F = 1). matches model structure Davda 2014. Simulation window (84 days) long enough characterise terminal phase average mAb longer FIH follow-windows original studies. PKNCA comparison table qualitative check : paper publish NCA parameters, side--side published vs simulated table possible. Values tuned target. IIV encoded omega^2 = (CV/100)^2 diagonal, matching paper’s convention reported “%CV” omega × 100 (SD log-normal). -diagonal covariances used reported Table 3.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Citation: Papachristos , Karatza E, Kalofonos H, Karalis V. Pharmacogenetics Model-Based Optimization Bevacizumab Therapy Metastatic Colorectal Cancer. Int J Mol Sci. 2020;21(11):3753. doi:[10.3390/ijms21113753](https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms21113753). Description: Two-compartment population PK model IV bevacizumab adults metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC), allometric weight scaling CL ICAM-1 / VEGF-genotype covariate effects (Table 1 Papachristos 2020). Modality: Therapeutic anti-VEGF-monoclonal antibody (humanized IgG1, MW ≈ 149 kDa), IV infusion. vignette validates PK-model (Table 1). Papachristos 2020 also reports two co-equal final models built 46-patient cohort — binding QSS TMDD model (Table 2) PK/PD immediate-response Imax model (Table 3) — packaged separately Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd. TODO (co-equality framing). three Papachristos 2020 models CO-EQUAL final models, hierarchical base / extended pair. pursues different objective: descriptive PK (vignette), mechanistic target binding (QSS), drug-effect characterization (PK/PD). Reviewer please confirm framing choice package three.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Papachristos 2020 cohort comprised 46 adult patients histologically confirmed metastatic colorectal cancer (ECOG performance status 0-2) treated single Greek centre (University Hospital Patras). Baseline demographics (paper section 2.1): Sex: 61% male, 39% female. Median (IQR) weight 74.5 kg (64.15–81.75); median (IQR) age 63 years (53–72). Full ranges reported. Race / ethnicity: Greek; formally tabulated. Dosing: 5 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks (76% patients) 7.5 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (24%) combination oxaliplatin/fluoropyrimidine (BEV-FOLFOX, BEV-CapOX) irinotecan/fluoropyrimidine (BEV-FOLFIRI, BEV-CapIRI) chemotherapy. Sample sizes: 156 bevacizumab serum concentrations (76 pre-dose, 80 post-dose) 46 patients. values limit quantification. SNP carrier rates (mutant alleles, paper section 2.1): ICAM-1 rs1799969 mutant 20%; ICAM-1 rs5498 mutant 70%; VEGF-rs699947 mutant 52%; VEGF-rs1570360 mutant 33%; VEGF-rs2010963 mutant 41%. three SNPs (ICAM-1 rs1799969, VEGF-rs1570360, VEGF-rs699947) entered final PK model; two tested retained. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk")$population.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk.R. table collects one place. Equations (paper section 2.2): CLi=CLpop⋅(WTi70)1.04⋅exp⁡(−0.423⋅SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969i)⋅exp⁡(ηCL,) \mathrm{CL}_i = \mathrm{CL}_\mathrm{pop} \cdot   \left(\frac{\mathrm{WT}_i}{70}\right)^{1.04}   \cdot \exp(-0.423 \cdot \mathrm{SNP\_ICAM1\_RS1799969}_i)   \cdot \exp(\eta_{\mathrm{CL},}) Qi=Qpop⋅exp⁡(0.378⋅SNP_VEGFA_RS1570360i)⋅exp⁡(−0.429⋅SNP_VEGFA_RS699947i)⋅exp⁡(ηQ,) \mathrm{Q}_i = \mathrm{Q}_\mathrm{pop}   \cdot \exp(0.378 \cdot \mathrm{SNP\_VEGFA\_RS1570360}_i)   \cdot \exp(-0.429 \cdot \mathrm{SNP\_VEGFA\_RS699947}_i)   \cdot \exp(\eta_{\mathrm{Q},}) V1,=V1,pop⋅exp⁡(ηV1,),V2,=V2,pop⋅exp⁡(ηV2,) \mathrm{V}_{1,} = \mathrm{V}_{1,\mathrm{pop}} \cdot \exp(\eta_{V_1,}), \quad \mathrm{V}_{2,} = \mathrm{V}_{2,\mathrm{pop}} \cdot \exp(\eta_{V_2,}) Two-compartment linear PK: ̇1=−kelA1−k12A1+k21A2\dot A_1 = -k_{el} A_1 - k_{12} A_1 + k_{21} A_2, ̇2=k12A1−k21A2\dot A_2 = k_{12} A_1 - k_{21} A_2, Cc=A1/V1C_c = A_1 / V_1, proportional residual error σprop=0.246\sigma_\mathrm{prop} = 0.246.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: formal corrigendum paper identified time extraction (April 2026). One within-paper inconsistency noted sister model 3 (PK/PD model, Table 3) section 2.4 quotes inter-compartmental clearance covariate exp(0.378) — copy-paste rs1570360 effect Table 1 / section 2.2. Table 3 gives -0.414 rs699947 effect Q PK/PD model narrative confirms Q lower mutants. packaged Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd model uses Table 3. PK model extracted (Table 1 / section 2.2) internally consistent text table.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Papachristos 2020 population. Two reference dosing regimens simulated, matching two cohorts Papachristos 2020 section 4.1: 5 mg/kg IV Q2W (BEV-FOLFIRI / BEV-FOLFOX cohort) 7.5 mg/kg IV Q3W (BEV-CapIRI / BEV-CapOX cohort)
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 200  # Weight: log-normal centred on the median 74.5 kg with a CV that # reproduces the IQR (64.15-81.75) of the source population. # log(81.75/64.15) ≈ 0.243 spans the IQR; sigma ≈ 0.243 / (2 * 0.6745) ≈ 0.18. cohort <- tibble(   ID                  = seq_len(n_subj),   WT                  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(74.5), 0.18), 45), 130),   SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969 = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.20),   SNP_VEGFA_RS1570360 = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.33),   SNP_VEGFA_RS699947  = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.52) ) inf_dur     <- 1.5 / 24            # 90-min standard bevacizumab infusion (days) n_doses_q2w <- 6 n_doses_q3w <- 4 sim_horizon <- 84                  # 12 weeks total follow-up  dose_times_q2w <- seq(0, by = 14, length.out = n_doses_q2w) dose_times_q3w <- seq(0, by = 21, length.out = n_doses_q3w) obs_times      <- sort(unique(c(0, seq(0.1, sim_horizon, by = 1))))  build_events <- function(pop, mgkg, dose_times, regimen_label,                          id_offset = 0L) {   pop <- dplyr::mutate(pop, ID = ID + id_offset)   amt_per_subject <- pop$WT * mgkg    d_dose <- pop |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(evid = 1L, cmt = "central", dur = inf_dur,                   treatment = regimen_label)   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "central", dur = NA_real_,                   treatment = regimen_label)    dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::rename(id = ID) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   build_events(cohort, mgkg = 5,   dose_times = dose_times_q2w,                regimen_label = "5 mg/kg Q2W",  id_offset = 0L),   build_events(cohort, mgkg = 7.5, dose_times = dose_times_q3w,                regimen_label = "7.5 mg/kg Q3W", id_offset = n_subj) )  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
code: mod <- readModelDb("Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events,                        keep = c("treatment"),                        returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Papachristos 2020 publish single concentration-time figure main text (VPCs live Supplementary Figures S3-S4). plot shows median 5-95% prediction interval simulated cohort regimen, providing diagnostic view.
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Bevacizumab concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Simulated bevacizumab PK by Papachristos 2020 PK model",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ",                       n_subj, " virtual patients per arm)."),     caption = "Replicates the diagnostic content of Papachristos 2020 Supplementary Figures S3-S4."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Effect of ICAM-1 rs1799969 on trough levels — replicates Figure 1A / Supplementary Figure S2
title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Paper section 2.2 highlights bevacizumab trough levels significantly higher ICAM-1 rs1799969 mutant carriers (99.1 vs 57.2 mg/L, p = 0.00004; Supplementary Figure S2). deterministic typical-value simulation shows predicted Q2W trough median weight (74.5 kg) wild-type vs mutant carriers.  Median Q2W trough (mg/L) 74.5 kg, wild-type vs ICAM-1 rs1799969 mutant carrier (typical-value simulation). simulated trough ratio reproduces qualitative direction (mutant > wild-type) reported paper. numerical magnitude depends dosing interval number doses simulated; paper’s 99.1 / 57.2 mg/L = 1.73 ratio median across observed sparse trough samples cohort, typical-value simulation assumes steady dosing exactly 14-day intervals returns model-predicted ratio exp(-0.423) = 0.655 CL — equivalently 1 / 0.655 = 1.527-fold trough elevation per dosing interval.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  typ_grid <- tidyr::crossing(   WT                  = 74.5,   SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969 = c(0, 1),   SNP_VEGFA_RS1570360 = 0,   SNP_VEGFA_RS699947  = 0 ) |>   dplyr::mutate(id = dplyr::row_number())  typ_dose <- typ_grid |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = WT * 5) |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times_q2w) |>   dplyr::mutate(evid = 1L, cmt = "central", dur = inf_dur)  typ_obs <- typ_grid |>   tidyr::crossing(time = sort(unique(c(0, seq(0.1, sim_horizon, by = 0.5))))) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "central", dur = NA_real_)  typ_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(typ_dose, typ_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>   as.data.frame()  sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = typ_events,                            keep = c("SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969"),                            returnType = "data.frame") |>   dplyr::mutate(     icam1_label = ifelse(SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969 == 1, "Mutant carrier", "Wild-type")   ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalq', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'  ggplot(sim_typ |> dplyr::filter(time > 0),        aes(time, Cc, colour = icam1_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x        = "Time since first dose (days)",     y        = "Bevacizumab concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     colour   = "ICAM-1 rs1799969",     title    = "Effect of ICAM-1 rs1799969 on bevacizumab PK (5 mg/kg Q2W)",     subtitle = "Typical 74.5 kg subject, deterministic (etas = 0). Replicates Papachristos 2020 Figure 1A / Supp. Fig. S2."   ) +   theme_bw() trough_compare <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(time %in% (dose_times_q2w[-1])) |>   dplyr::group_by(icam1_label) |>   dplyr::summarise(median_trough = stats::median(Cc), .groups = "drop") knitr::kable(trough_compare,              caption = "Median Q2W trough (mg/L) at 74.5 kg, wild-type vs ICAM-1 rs1799969 mutant carrier (typical-value simulation).")

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: NCA computed single dosing interval steady state two regimens. dosing interval starts last simulated dose runs one tau (14 d Q2W, 21 d Q3W). Steady-state NCA (5 mg/kg Q2W, last simulated interval). Steady-state NCA (7.5 mg/kg Q3W, last simulated interval).
code: build_nca <- function(sim_df, regimen_label, tau, dose_times) {   start_ss <- max(dose_times)   end_ss   <- start_ss + tau    conc_df <- sim_df |>     dplyr::filter(treatment == regimen_label, time >= start_ss, time <= end_ss,                   !is.na(Cc)) |>     dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - start_ss) |>     dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, Cc)    dose_df <- events |>     dplyr::filter(treatment == regimen_label, evid == 1, time == start_ss) |>     dplyr::mutate(time_rel = 0) |>     dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, amt)    list(conc = conc_df, dose = dose_df, tau = tau) }  nca_q2w <- build_nca(sim, "5 mg/kg Q2W",   tau = 14, dose_times = dose_times_q2w) nca_q3w <- build_nca(sim, "7.5 mg/kg Q3W", tau = 21, dose_times = dose_times_q3w)  run_pknca <- function(b) {   conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(b$conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id,                                concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day")   dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(b$dose, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id,                                doseu = "mg")   intervals <- data.frame(     start    = 0,     end      = b$tau,     cmax     = TRUE,     tmax     = TRUE,     cmin     = TRUE,     auclast  = TRUE,     cav      = TRUE   )   res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals))   summary(res) }  knitr::kable(run_pknca(nca_q2w),              caption = "Steady-state NCA (5 mg/kg Q2W, last simulated interval).") #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.1) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Papachristos 2020 publish tabulated NCA. paper states mean trough levels ICAM-1 rs1799969 wild-type mutant sub-groups (section 2.2: 57.2 vs 99.1 mg/L); sparse-sample descriptive statistics across observed cohort bracketed simulated 5-95% prediction interval shown Concentration-time profiles figure .

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk
text: Co-equality framing three Papachristos 2020 models. PK / Binding QSS / PK/PD models Papachristos 2020 co-equal final models, pursuing different objective (descriptive PK, mechanistic target binding, drug-effect characterization). hierarchical base / extended pair. three packaged separately nlmixr2lib (file descriptive-PK variant). Weight distribution. paper reports median 74.5 kg IQR 64.15–81.75 kg without full range. virtual cohort uses log-normal σ = 0.18 (chosen log IQR ratio matches source) truncated 45 130 kg. SNP independence. source paper used chi-square / Fisher’s exact tests confirm pairwise independence SNPs observed cohort (Supplementary Tables S1-S3); virtual cohort draws SNP independently marginal mutant-carrier rate. Race / ethnicity. available source. paper’s cohort single-centre Greek; race used model covariate. Trough-level magnitude Figure 1A replication. exp(-0.423) = 0.655 fold effect CL implies ~1.53-fold elevation typical Q2W trough ICAM-1 rs1799969 mutant carriers, whereas paper reports observed ratio 99.1 / 57.2 ≈ 1.73 across mutant vs wild-type subjects. observed ratio also reflects -subject variability dosing schedule, sampling windows, weight, model observed sparse-sample statistic directly comparable; qualitative direction matches. errata identified. literature search (April 2026) found published corrigendum / erratum Papachristos 2020.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: Citation: Papachristos , Karatza E, Kalofonos H, Karalis V. Pharmacogenetics Model-Based Optimization Bevacizumab Therapy Metastatic Colorectal Cancer. Int J Mol Sci. 2020;21(11):3753. doi:[10.3390/ijms21113753](https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms21113753). Description: Two-compartment PK plus immediate-response Imax inhibition free VEGF-IV bevacizumab adults metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC), allometric weight scaling ICAM-1 / VEGF-genotype covariate effects (Table 3 Papachristos 2020). Modality: Therapeutic anti-VEGF-monoclonal antibody (humanized IgG1, MW ≈ 149 kDa). vignette validates PK/PD variant (Table 3). Papachristos 2020 also reports descriptive PK model (Table 1, packaged Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk) binding QSS TMDD model (Table 2, packaged Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss). TODO (co-equality framing). three Papachristos 2020 models CO-EQUAL final models, hierarchical base / extended pair. pursues different objective: descriptive PK, mechanistic target binding, drug-effect characterization (vignette). Reviewer please confirm framing choice package three.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: Section 2.4 paper contains copy-paste error covariate-effect equation inter-compartmental clearance Q. text reads: Q = Qpop · exp(0.378) [] cat takes value 1 mutant … VEGF-rs699947 gene 0 wild-type. numerical value 0.378 identical VEGF-rs1570360 mutant Q coefficient Table 1 (PK model) copy-paste error. Table 3 paper reports rs699947 effect Q PK/PD model -0.414, surrounding narrative confirms direction: “Inter-compartmental clearance (Q) lower patients mutant type gene VEGF-rs699947” (paper section 2.4, paragraph equation). packaged model uses Table 3’s -0.414; -file source-trace comment e_vegfa_rs699947_q flags text-vs-table discrepancy. formal corrigendum published time extraction (April 2026).

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: Papachristos 2020 cohort comprised 46 adult patients histologically confirmed metastatic colorectal cancer (ECOG 0-2) treated single Greek centre. Baseline demographics (paper section 2.1): Sex: 61% male, 39% female. Median (IQR) weight 74.5 kg (64.15-81.75); median (IQR) age 63 years (53-72). Race / ethnicity: Greek; formally tabulated. Dosing: 5 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks (76% patients) 7.5 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (24%). Sample sizes: 156 bevacizumab + 169 free VEGF-serum concentrations. Two SNPs entered final PK/PD model: ICAM-1 rs1799969 (effect CL) VEGF-rs699947 (effect Q). metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd")$population.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: Equations (paper section 2.4): CLi=CLpop⋅(WTi/70)0.78⋅exp⁡(−0.423SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969i)⋅exp⁡(ηCL,) \mathrm{CL}_i = \mathrm{CL}_\mathrm{pop} \cdot   (\mathrm{WT}_i / 70)^{0.78}   \cdot \exp(-0.423\,\mathrm{SNP\_ICAM1\_RS1799969}_i)   \cdot \exp(\eta_{\mathrm{CL},}) Qi=Qpop⋅exp⁡(−0.414SNP_VEGFA_RS699947i)⋅exp⁡(ηQ,) \mathrm{Q}_i = \mathrm{Q}_\mathrm{pop}   \cdot \exp(-0.414\,\mathrm{SNP\_VEGFA\_RS699947}_i)   \cdot \exp(\eta_{\mathrm{Q},}) ̇1=−kelA1−k12A1+k21A2,̇2=k12A1−k21A2,Cc=A1/V1 \dot A_1 = -k_{el} A_1 - k_{12} A_1 + k_{21} A_2,\qquad \dot A_2 = k_{12} A_1 - k_{21} A_2,\qquad C_c = A_1 / V_1 E=E0⋅(1−Imax⋅CcIC50+Cc) E = E_0 \cdot \left(1 - \frac{I_\mathrm{max} \cdot C_c}                                 {IC_{50} + C_c}\right)

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 100  cohort <- tibble(   ID                  = seq_len(n_subj),   WT                  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(74.5), 0.18), 45), 130),   SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969 = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.20),   SNP_VEGFA_RS699947  = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.52) ) inf_dur     <- 1.5 / 24            # 90-min infusion (days) n_doses_q2w <- 6 sim_horizon <- 84                  # 12 weeks total follow-up dose_times  <- seq(0, by = 14, length.out = n_doses_q2w) obs_times   <- sort(unique(c(0, seq(0.05, sim_horizon, by = 0.5))))  build_events <- function(pop, mgkg, dose_times, regimen_label) {   amt_per_subject <- pop$WT * mgkg   d_dose <- pop |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(evid = 1L, cmt = "central", dur = inf_dur,                   treatment = regimen_label)   d_obs_cc <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc", dur = NA_real_,                   treatment = regimen_label)   d_obs_e <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "E", dur = NA_real_,                   treatment = regimen_label)   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs_cc, d_obs_e) |>     dplyr::rename(id = ID) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events <- build_events(cohort, mgkg = 5, dose_times = dose_times,                        regimen_label = "5 mg/kg Q2W")

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events,                        keep = c("treatment"),                        returnType = "data.frame")  # Endpoint compartments after 2 ODE states: Cc -> CMT 3, E -> CMT 4 sim_cc <- sim |> dplyr::filter(CMT == 3) sim_e  <- sim |> dplyr::filter(CMT == 4)

what: Bevacizumab concentration-time profile (replicates Supplementary Figure S8A diagnostic)
title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
code: sim_cc_summary <- sim_cc |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_cc_summary, aes(time, median)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "steelblue") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Bevacizumab concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Simulated bevacizumab PK from PK/PD model (5 mg/kg Q2W)",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval, N = ", n_subj),     caption = "Replicates the diagnostic content of Papachristos 2020 Supplementary Figure S8A."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Free VEGF-A inhibition profile (replicates Supplementary Figure S8B diagnostic)
title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
code: sim_e_summary <- sim_e |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(E, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(E, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(E, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_e_summary, aes(time, median)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "darkorange") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 684, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Free VEGF-A (ng/L)",     title = "Simulated free VEGF-A from immediate-response Imax model (5 mg/kg Q2W)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval. Dashed line = wild-type baseline E0 (684 ng/L).",     caption = "Replicates the diagnostic content of Papachristos 2020 Supplementary Figure S8B."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: Imax curve function bevacizumab concentration identifying signature model. plot shows algebraic relationship E / E0 = 1 - Imax * Cc / (IC50 + Cc) using Table 3 typical values.
code: imax  <- 0.951 ic50  <- 29.1 cc_grid <- 10^seq(-1, 3, length.out = 200) ratio   <- 1 - imax * cc_grid / (ic50 + cc_grid)  ggplot(data.frame(Cc = cc_grid, ratio = ratio), aes(Cc, ratio)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "darkred") +   geom_vline(xintercept = ic50, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1 - imax / 2, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +   scale_x_log10() +   labs(     x = "Bevacizumab concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     y = expression(E / E[0]),     title = "Immediate-response Imax dose-response (typical Table 3 values)",     subtitle = paste0("Imax = ", imax, ", IC50 = ", ic50, " mg/L; vertical dashed line at IC50.")   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: Steady-state NCA bevacizumab (5 mg/kg Q2W, last simulated interval, PK/PD model).

dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, Cc)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(treatment == "5 mg/kg Q2W", evid == 1, time == start_ss) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id,
