what: Comparison of PK parameters across the three models
title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: three Papachristos 2020 models share data use different structural assumptions; typical PK parameter estimates differ accordingly. descriptive-PK model (Table 1) account target-mediated clearance yields smaller V1 CL; binding QSS PK/PD models, include drug-target binding effects (explicit QSS, implicit PK/PD via Imax mechanism), yield larger V1 (PK/PD case) larger CL.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd
text: Co-equality framing three Papachristos 2020 models. PK / Binding QSS / PK/PD models Papachristos 2020 co-equal final models, pursuing different objective (descriptive PK, mechanistic target binding, drug-effect characterization [vignette]). hierarchical base / extended pair. three packaged separately nlmixr2lib. Section 2.4 text-vs-Table 3 inconsistency Q’s rs699947 effect. text equation section 2.4 prints exp(0.378) (clear copy-paste PK model section 2.2 / Table 1), Table 3 paper gives -0.414 rs699947 mutant effect Q PK/PD model, surrounding paragraph confirms negative direction (“Q … lower”). packaged model uses Table 3’s -0.414. See Errata note section -file e_vegfa_rs699947_q source-trace comment. Imax distributional assumption. Per paper section 4.6, Imax modelled logit-normal distribution constrained (0, 1) fitting. Table 3 reports ω_Imax random-effect SD, packaged model treats Imax fixed-effect-typical value (0.951). matches published parameter set; future validation requires per-subject Imax variability, model file needs updated. Immediate-response equilibrium free VEGF-. PD output computed algebraically current Cc, kinetic delay. exactly paper’s “immediate response Imax model without sigmoidicity” specification (section 2.4); transduction compartment Hill coefficient added. Weight distribution. paper reports median 74.5 kg, IQR 64.15-81.75, full range. Virtual cohort uses log-normal σ = 0.18 truncated 45-130 kg. errata identified. literature search (April 2026) found published corrigendum / erratum Papachristos 2020.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: Citation: Papachristos , Karatza E, Kalofonos H, Karalis V. Pharmacogenetics Model-Based Optimization Bevacizumab Therapy Metastatic Colorectal Cancer. Int J Mol Sci. 2020;21(11):3753. doi:[10.3390/ijms21113753](https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms21113753). Description: Two-compartment quasi-steady-state target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD QSS) model IV bevacizumab free VEGF-adults metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC), allometric weight scaling ICAM-1 / VEGF-genotype covariate effects (Table 2 Papachristos 2020). Modality: Therapeutic anti-VEGF-monoclonal antibody (humanized IgG1, MW ≈ 149 kDa) bound soluble target (free VEGF-, MW ≈ 45 kDa back-calculated paper’s BM0 dual-unit reporting). vignette validates binding-QSS variant (Table 2). Papachristos 2020 also reports descriptive PK model (Table 1, packaged Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pk) PK/PD immediate-response Imax model (Table 3, packaged Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd). TODO (co-equality framing). three Papachristos 2020 models CO-EQUAL final models, hierarchical base / extended pair. pursues different objective: descriptive PK, mechanistic target binding (vignette), drug-effect characterization (PK/PD). Reviewer please confirm framing choice package three.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: Papachristos 2020 cohort comprised 46 adult patients histologically confirmed metastatic colorectal cancer (ECOG 0-2) treated single Greek centre. Baseline demographics (paper section 2.1): Sex: 61% male, 39% female. Median (IQR) weight 74.5 kg (64.15-81.75); median (IQR) age 63 years (53-72). Race / ethnicity: Greek; formally tabulated. Dosing: 5 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks (76% patients) 7.5 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (24%). Sample sizes: 156 bevacizumab + 169 free VEGF-serum concentrations. Two SNPs entered final binding QSS model: ICAM-1 rs1799969 (effect CL) VEGF-rs699947 (effects K_ss BM0). metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss")$population.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: Equations (paper section 2.3 + Gibiansky et al. 2008 QSS-TMDD form): ̇1=−CLCfree−QCfree+Q(A2/V2)−kintRcV1 \dot A_1 = -\mathrm{CL}\,C_\mathrm{free} - Q\,C_\mathrm{free}             + Q\,(A_2/V_2) - k_\mathrm{int}\,R_c\,V_1 ̇2=QCfree−Q(A2/V2) \dot A_2 = Q\,C_\mathrm{free} - Q\,(A_2/V_2) Ṫtot=kin−koutTfree−kintRc \dot{T}_\mathrm{tot} = k_\mathrm{}             - k_\mathrm{}\,T_\mathrm{free}             - k_\mathrm{int}\,R_c QSS algebraic relation Rc=12(Ctot+Ttot+Kss−(Ctot−Ttot)2+2Kss(Ctot+Ttot)+Kss2) R_c = \tfrac{1}{2}\!\left(C_\mathrm{tot} + T_\mathrm{tot} + K_{ss}    - \sqrt{(C_\mathrm{tot} - T_\mathrm{tot})^2             + 2\,K_{ss}(C_\mathrm{tot} + T_\mathrm{tot}) + K_{ss}^2}\right) Cfree=Ctot−RcC_\mathrm{free} = C_\mathrm{tot} - R_c, Tfree=Ttot−RcT_\mathrm{free} = T_\mathrm{tot} - R_c, Ctot=A1/V1C_\mathrm{tot} = A_1/V_1, kin=koutBM0k_\mathrm{} = k_\mathrm{}\,\mathrm{BM_0}, kint=CL/V1k_\mathrm{int} = \mathrm{CL}/V_1 (paper section 2.3). bevacizumab observation total (free + complex) drug mg/L; free-VEGF-observation TfreeT_\mathrm{free} ng/L. residual error models proportional.

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: formal corrigendum identified time extraction (April 2026). One within-paper inconsistency sister model 3 (PK/PD model, Table 3) documented Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_pkpd vignette; affect binding QSS model presented .

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 100  cohort <- tibble(   ID                  = seq_len(n_subj),   WT                  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(74.5), 0.18), 45), 130),   SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969 = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.20),   SNP_VEGFA_RS699947  = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.52) ) inf_dur     <- 1.5 / 24            # 90-min infusion (days) n_doses_q2w <- 6 sim_horizon <- 84                  # 12 weeks total follow-up dose_times  <- seq(0, by = 14, length.out = n_doses_q2w) obs_times   <- sort(unique(c(0, seq(0.05, sim_horizon, by = 0.5))))  build_events <- function(pop, mgkg, dose_times, regimen_label) {   amt_per_subject <- pop$WT * mgkg   d_dose <- pop |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(evid = 1L, cmt = "central", dur = inf_dur,                   treatment = regimen_label)   d_obs_cc <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc", dur = NA_real_,                   treatment = regimen_label)   d_obs_cv <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cv", dur = NA_real_,                   treatment = regimen_label)   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs_cc, d_obs_cv) |>     dplyr::rename(id = ID) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events <- build_events(cohort, mgkg = 5, dose_times = dose_times,                        regimen_label = "5 mg/kg Q2W")

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events,                        keep = c("treatment"),                        returnType = "data.frame")  # Endpoint compartments: Cc -> CMT 4, Cv -> CMT 5 (after 3 ODE states) sim_cc <- sim |> dplyr::filter(CMT == 4) sim_cv <- sim |> dplyr::filter(CMT == 5)

what: Bevacizumab concentration-time profile (replicates Supplementary Figure S5A diagnostic)
title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
code: sim_cc_summary <- sim_cc |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_cc_summary, aes(time, median)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "steelblue") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Bevacizumab concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     title = "Simulated bevacizumab PK from binding QSS model (5 mg/kg Q2W)",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval, N = ", n_subj),     caption = "Replicates the diagnostic content of Papachristos 2020 Supplementary Figure S5A."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Free VEGF-A profile and rebound (replicates Supplementary Figure S5B diagnostic)
title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
code: sim_cv_summary <- sim_cv |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cv, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cv, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cv, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_cv_summary, aes(time, median)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "darkorange") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 616.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Free VEGF-A (ng/L)",     title = "Simulated free VEGF-A from binding QSS model (5 mg/kg Q2W)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval. Dashed line = wild-type baseline (616.5 ng/L).",     caption = "Replicates the diagnostic content of Papachristos 2020 Supplementary Figure S5B."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Effect of VEGF-A rs699947 on Kss and baseline (replicates Figure 2C/2D)
title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: Paper section 2.3 highlights two rs699947 effects: lower baseline free VEGF-(BM0, Figure 2C) higher -vivo affinity (higher K_ss, Figure 2D). deterministic typical-value simulation isolates genotype contrast fixed 74.5 kg / wild-type-ICAM1 reference subject.  Pre-dose baseline free VEGF-rs699947 genotype (typical-value simulation, ng/L).
code: mod_typical <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss")) |>   rxode2::zeroRe() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  typ_grid <- tidyr::crossing(   WT                  = 74.5,   SNP_ICAM1_RS1799969 = 0,   SNP_VEGFA_RS699947  = c(0, 1) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(id = dplyr::row_number())  build_typ_events <- function(typ_grid, mgkg, dose_times) {   d_dose <- typ_grid |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = WT * mgkg) |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(evid = 1L, cmt = "central", dur = inf_dur)   d_obs_cc <- typ_grid |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc", dur = NA_real_)   d_obs_cv <- typ_grid |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cv", dur = NA_real_)   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs_cc, d_obs_cv) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     as.data.frame() }  typ_events <- build_typ_events(typ_grid, 5, dose_times) sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = typ_events,                            keep = c("SNP_VEGFA_RS699947"),                            returnType = "data.frame") |>   dplyr::mutate(     geno = ifelse(SNP_VEGFA_RS699947 == 1, "rs699947 mutant", "rs699947 wild-type")   ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalq', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalbm0' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'  ggplot(sim_typ |> dplyr::filter(CMT == 5),        aes(time, Cv, colour = geno)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Free VEGF-A (ng/L)",     colour = "VEGF-A rs699947",     title = "Effect of rs699947 on free VEGF-A baseline and rebound",     subtitle = "Typical 74.5 kg, wild-type ICAM-1, 5 mg/kg Q2W. Replicates the time-course basis of Papachristos 2020 Figure 2C-D."   ) +   theme_bw() baseline_compare <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(CMT == 5, time == 0) |>   dplyr::select(geno, Cv) knitr::kable(baseline_compare,              caption = "Pre-dose baseline free VEGF-A by rs699947 genotype (typical-value simulation, ng/L).")

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: binding-target / turnover model — PKNCA VEGF-appropriate (VEGF-endogenous, dosed). two checks verify model correctness: Pre-dose baseline free VEGF-matches BM0. drug administered, T_free equal BM0 = 0.0137 nM × 45000 g/mol = 616.5 ng/L wild-type rs699947 subject BM0 × exp(-0.851) = 264 ng/L mutant carrier. Pre-dose free VEGF-: simulated vs algebraic expectation (ng/L). Long-term recovery baseline. dosing stopped last simulated infusion, free VEGF-rebound genotype-specific BM0 half-lives target turnover (1/k_out ≈ 8.6 days). End--simulation free VEGF-vs genotype-specific BM0 (ng/L). Last dose day 70; observed day 84 (~14 d ≈ 1.6 × 1/k_out post-dose).
code: expected_bm0 <- c(   `rs699947 wild-type` = 0.0137 * 45000,   `rs699947 mutant`    = 0.0137 * 45000 * exp(-0.851) ) predose <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(CMT == 5, time == 0) |>   dplyr::select(geno, observed_predose = Cv) |>   dplyr::mutate(expected_bm0 = expected_bm0[geno]) knitr::kable(predose,              caption = "Pre-dose free VEGF-A: simulated vs algebraic expectation (ng/L).") last_obs_predose <- sim_typ |>   dplyr::filter(CMT == 5, time >= 84) |>   dplyr::group_by(geno) |>   dplyr::summarise(final_Cv = dplyr::last(Cv), .groups = "drop") |>   dplyr::mutate(expected_bm0 = expected_bm0[geno],                 pct_recovered = round(100 * final_Cv / expected_bm0, 1)) knitr::kable(last_obs_predose,              caption = "End-of-simulation free VEGF-A vs genotype-specific BM0 (ng/L). Last dose at day 70; observed at day 84 (~14 d ≈ 1.6 × 1/k_out post-dose).")

title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: completeness, single-dose NCA recipe used descriptive PK model can applied QSS-model bevacizumab output. two output streams unified using cmt markers event table; dose-mass conservation differs slightly descriptive PK model total drug includes bound complex. Steady-state NCA bevacizumab (5 mg/kg Q2W, last simulated interval, binding QSS model).

dplyr::mutate(time_rel = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, amt)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(conc_df, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id,                              concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = 14,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: title: Papachristos_2020_bevacizumab_qss
text: Co-equality framing three Papachristos 2020 models. PK / Binding QSS / PK/PD models Papachristos 2020 co-equal final models, pursuing different objective (descriptive PK, mechanistic target binding [vignette], drug-effect characterization). hierarchical base / extended pair. three packaged separately nlmixr2lib. Bevacizumab molecular weight (MW_BEV = 149,000 g/mol). Required convert mg input doses molar units (nM) binding equations; reported numerically Papachristos 2020. 149 kDa value standard reference marketed antibody documented model assumption model file’s model() block. Free VEGF-effective molecular weight (MW_VEGF ≈ 45,000 g/mol). Back-calculated paper’s BM0 dual-unit reporting (0.0137 nM ≡ 616.5 ng/L → MW = 45 kDa, consistent homodimeric VEGF-A165 form). Documented model assumption. 1:1 stoichiometric drug-target binding. paper states “assuming 1:1 molecular interaction” (section 2.3). packaged QSS model uses 1:1 binding, consistent paper. Bevacizumab observation = total drug (free + complex). paper’s ELISA assay (section 4.4) described free-drug assay; total-drug ELISA typical default. Flagged interpretation assumption. k_int = CL / V1. Per paper section 2.3: “elimination clearance bevacizumab-VEGF-complex… set equal CL free bevacizumab.” Implemented exactly stated. errata identified. literature search (April 2026) found published corrigendum / erratum Papachristos 2020.

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: Citation: Papathanasiou T, Strougo , Roy , Vakkalagadda B, Stein , Jewell RC, Boer J, Dahmane E. Population pharmacokinetics belantamab mafodotin monotherapy combination therapies patients relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2025;64(6):925-942. doi:[10.1007/s40262-025-01508-1](https://doi.org/10.1007/s40262-025-01508-1) Description: Two-compartment population PK model antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) belantamab mafodotin patients relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM), sigmoidal time-varying clearance covariate effects body weight, BMI, serum albumin, soluble BCMA (sBCMA), serum IgG, race, combination-therapy status. Modality: Antibody-drug conjugate (humanized anti-BCMA IgG1 conjugated monomethyl auristatin F via cysteine maleimidocaproyl linker), IV infusion. Belantamab mafodotin (BLENREP) anti-BCMA ADC development relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma. Papathanasiou 2025 analysis pools 977 patients across six DREAMM trials (DREAMM-2/-3/-6/ -7/-12/-14) externally validates final model DREAMM-8 dataset (n = 150, belantamab mafodotin + pomalidomide + dexamethasone). final ADC model linear two-compartment disposition model time-varying clearance described sigmoid function time since first dose: CLi(t)=CLbase,⋅exp⁡(ImaxitγTI50iγ+tγ),Imaxi=θIMAX⋅θIMAX,COMBOCOMBO⋅(IGG/15)θIMAX,IGG⋅(SBCMA/50)θIMAX,SBCMA+ηIMAX,\mathrm{CL}_{}(t) \;=\; \mathrm{CL}_{\mathrm{base},} \cdot   \exp\!\left( \dfrac{\mathrm{Imax}_{}\, t^{\gamma}}                      {\mathrm{TI50}_{}^{\gamma} + t^{\gamma}} \right), \qquad \mathrm{Imax}_{} \;=\; \theta_{\mathrm{IMAX}}   \cdot \theta_{\mathrm{IMAX,COMBO}}^{\mathrm{COMBO}}   \cdot (\mathrm{IGG}/15)^{\theta_{\mathrm{IMAX,IGG}}}   \cdot (\mathrm{SBCMA}/50)^{\theta_{\mathrm{IMAX,SBCMA}}}   + \eta_{\mathrm{IMAX},} typical Imax = -0.403 (fractional CL decrease 1−e−0.403≈33.2%1 - e^{-0.403} \approx 33.2\% t≫TI50t \gg \mathrm{TI50}, monotherapy reference), TI50 = 66.4 days, Gamma = 2.87, combination-therapy multiplier 1.44 Imax (giving 1−e−0.580≈44.0%1 - e^{-0.580} \approx 44.0\% reduction combination regimens). vignette implements ADC moiety Papathanasiou 2025 analysis. companion cys-mcMMAF (payload) sub-model included — see Assumptions deviations .

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: PopPK estimation dataset comprised 977 patients RRMM contributing 8,880 measurable ADC concentrations across six trials (Papathanasiou 2025 Methods, Sect. 2.1 Table 1): DREAMM-2 (NCT03525678; n = 218), DREAMM-3 (NCT04162210; n = 217), DREAMM-6 (NCT03544281; n = 152), DREAMM-7 (NCT04246047; n = 242), DREAMM-12 (NCT04398745; n = 23), DREAMM-14 (NCT05064358; n = 125). Treatment: monotherapy 59.7%, bortezomib + dexamethasone 35.7%, lenalidomide + dexamethasone 4.6%. Belantamab mafodotin dose: 2.5 mg/kg IV every 21 days (pivotal labeled regimen) cycle-1 reference dose used exposure simulations paper. Baseline demographics (Table 1): Age 66.0 (range 32-89) years; body weight 74.0 (range 37-170) kg; 56.4% male. Race: 78.1% White, 13.6% Asian, 6.2% Black/African American, 0.8% , 1.2% Missing. Body mass index 26.7 (14.0-48.4) kg/m^2. Serum albumin 39.0 (19.0-57.0) g/L; serum IgG 13.1 (0.350-119) g/L; sBCMA 56.0 (2.08-2030) ng/mL; β2 microglobulin 297 (94.9-5190) nmol/L. ECOG performance status 0 40.0%, 1 51.0%, ≥2 8.9%. Renal function: normal eGFR 31.9%, mild impairment 42.0%, moderate 23.0%, severe 2.8%, end-stage 0.3%. External validation used DREAMM-8 dataset (n = 150 patients belantamab mafodotin + pomalidomide + dexamethasone; 1,221 measurable ADC concentrations). metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab")$population.

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab.R. table collects one place review. Equations: structural two-compartment micro-constant form time-varying CL; per-parameter covariate equations sigmoidal CL_Time formula taken verbatim “PK parameter estimation” block Papathanasiou 2025 Table 2. Reference covariates (Papathanasiou 2025 Methods Sect. 2.3): 65-year-old male, 75 kg WT, 27 kg/m^2 BMI, sBCMA 50 ng/mL, IgG 15 g/L, albumin 40 g/L, monotherapy regimen, non-Asian / non-Black/African American race.

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose marginal demographics approximate pooled Papathanasiou 2025 population (Table 1). Continuous covariates drawn log-normal distributions anchored reported median approximate spread; binary / categorical covariates match reported marginal proportions. Joint covariate correlations (e.g. WT BMI, IgG sBCMA) modeled. cycle-1 reference dose used Papathanasiou 2025 exposure simulations 2.5 mg/kg IV every 21 days. vignette compares two regimens dose: 2.5 mg/kg q3w monotherapy (COMBO_BELAMAF = 0). 2.5 mg/kg q3w combination (COMBO_BELAMAF = 1), pooling Bor-Dex / Len-Dex / Pom-Dex backbones tested DREAMM-6/-7/-8.
code: set.seed(2025) n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble(   ID            = seq_len(n_subj),   WT            = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(74.0), 0.22), 37), 170),   BMI           = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(26.7), 0.20), 14), 48),   ALB           = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 39.0, 4.5), 19), 57),   IGG           = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(13.1), 0.6), 0.5), 100),   SBCMA         = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(56.0), 1.2), 2), 2000),   RACE_ASIAN    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.136),   RACE_BLACK    = 0L,   COMBO_BELAMAF = 0L ) # Mutually-exclusive Black indicator: only assign Black to subjects not # already flagged as Asian, with marginal probability 0.062 / (1 - 0.136). non_asian        <- which(cohort$RACE_ASIAN == 0) flag_black       <- non_asian[   rbinom(length(non_asian), 1, 0.062 / (1 - 0.136)) == 1 ] cohort$RACE_BLACK[flag_black] <- 1L dose_interval_d <- 21 n_doses         <- 12   # ~36 weeks of follow-up to capture time-varying CL dose_times_d    <- seq(0, by = dose_interval_d, length.out = n_doses) obs_times_d     <- sort(unique(c(   dose_times_d,   seq(0,                         24, by = 0.5),    # cycle-1 dense sampling   seq(0,  dose_interval_d * n_doses, by = 1) )))  build_events <- function(pop, mgkg, combo_flag) {   amt_per_subject <- pop$WT * mgkg   pop2 <- pop   pop2$COMBO_BELAMAF <- combo_flag   d_dose <- pop2 |>     dplyr::mutate(AMT = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times_d) |>     dplyr::mutate(EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 1 / 24, DV = NA_real_,                   treatment = if (combo_flag == 1)                     paste0(mgkg, " mg/kg q3w combo")                   else                     paste0(mgkg, " mg/kg q3w mono"))   d_obs <- pop2 |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_d) |>     dplyr::mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central",                   DUR = NA_real_, DV = NA_real_,                   treatment = if (combo_flag == 1)                     paste0(mgkg, " mg/kg q3w combo")                   else                     paste0(mgkg, " mg/kg q3w mono"))   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events_mono  <- build_events(cohort, 2.5, 0) events_combo <- build_events(cohort, 2.5, 1)

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab") sim_mono  <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_mono,  returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_combo <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_combo, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_mono,  treatment = "2.5 mg/kg q3w mono"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_combo, treatment = "2.5 mg/kg q3w combo") )

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: Papathanasiou 2025 Figure 2 shows visual predictive check 2.5 mg/kg dose, dose-normalized. figure reproduces median 5-95% prediction interval packaged model first dosing interval (cycle 1), time-varying clearance yet appreciably reduced CL.
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0, time <= dose_interval_d) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Belantamab mafodotin ADC concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     title = "Cycle 1 ADC PK: 2.5 mg/kg q3w monotherapy vs combination",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ", n_subj,                       " virtual patients per arm). Replicates Papathanasiou 2025 Figure 2 (ADC panel)."),     caption = "Model: Papathanasiou 2025 Clin Pharmacokinet 64(6):925-942"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: Papathanasiou 2025 reports sigmoid decrease CL baseline CLbase⋅e−0.403≈0.668\mathrm{CL}_\mathrm{base} \cdot e^{-0.403} \approx 0.668 baseline (33.2% reduction) t≫TI50t \gg \mathrm{TI50} monotherapy, e−0.580≈0.560e^{-0.580} \approx 0.560 baseline (44.0% reduction) combination therapy. typical-value CL(t) / CL(0) profile reproduces time course reference subject (deterministic, etas = 0).
code: t_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5) build_cl_events <- function(combo_flag) {   data.frame(     ID            = 1,     WT            = 75, BMI = 27, ALB = 40, IGG = 15, SBCMA = 50,     RACE_ASIAN    = 0, RACE_BLACK = 0,     COMBO_BELAMAF = combo_flag,     TIME          = c(0, t_grid),     AMT           = c(75 * 2.5, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),     EVID          = c(1, rep(0, length(t_grid))),     CMT           = "central",     DUR           = c(1 / 24, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),     DV            = NA_real_   ) } mod_typ      <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_cl_mono  <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = build_cl_events(0),                                 returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etaimax', 'etalti50' sim_cl_combo <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = build_cl_events(1),                                 returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etaimax', 'etalti50' sim_cl <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_cl_mono,  treatment = "Monotherapy"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_cl_combo, treatment = "Combination") ) |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0)  ggplot(sim_cl, aes(time, cl / cl_base, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1,           linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = exp(-0.403), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = exp(-0.580), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "CL(t) / CL(0)",     title = "Time-varying ADC clearance",     subtitle = "Reference subject; asymptotes at exp(-0.403) = 0.668 (mono) and exp(-0.580) = 0.560 (combo)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: Compute cycle-1 NCA parameters across 0-21 day window regimens. Papathanasiou 2025 Table 4 reports typical-patient exposure values 2.5 mg/kg dose: ADC C_max 44.2 ug/mL, ADC C_avg 7.86 ug/mL, ADC C_tau 2.05 ug/mL. simulated medians agree within ~20%. Simulated cycle-1 NCA parameters 2.5 mg/kg q3w (monotherapy vs combination).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc),                 time >= 0,                 time <= dose_interval_d) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time, Cc)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id)  dose_df <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time == 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(.groups = "drop") |>   dplyr::left_join(cohort |> dplyr::select(id = ID, WT), by = "id") |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt  = WT * 2.5,     time = 0   ) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time, amt)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = dose_interval_d,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■             68% |  ETA:  1s knitr::kable(   summary(nca_res),   caption = "Simulated cycle-1 NCA parameters at 2.5 mg/kg q3w (monotherapy vs combination)." )

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: Papathanasiou 2025 Table 4 reports typical-patient cycle-1 exposure estimates 2.5 mg/kg dose. Cycle 1 cleanest comparison time-varying CL yet meaningfully changed (TI50 = 66.4 days; t = 21 days CL multiplier approximately e−0.403⋅212.87/(66.42.87+212.87)≈e−0.403⋅0.039≈0.984e^{-0.403 \cdot 21^{2.87}/(66.4^{2.87} + 21^{2.87})} \approx e^{-0.403 \cdot 0.039} \approx 0.984, ~1.6% deviation initial CL). Differences within ~20% expected virtual cohort approximates exactly reproduce source-paper marginal covariate distributions. reference-subject closed-form values agree <1% paper, confirming packaged equations parameter values faithful Table 2.

title: Papathanasiou_2025_belantamab
text: ADC moiety . Papathanasiou 2025 reports two coupled population PK models: 2-compartment ADC model (file) 2-compartment cys-mcMMAF (payload) model whose input rate governed proteolytic ADC degradation modulated exponentially-declining drug--antibody ratio (DAR). cys-mcMMAF sub-model requires initial DAR (DAR0) precise functional form input rate, neither given main paper text (published NONMEM control streams supplement). avoid guessing values, packaged model implements ADC moiety; cys-mcMMAF sub-model can added future revision supplement disk. restricts scope packaged model ADC concentrations (Cc); cys-mcMMAF concentrations predicted file. Omitted IgG--TI50 covariate effect. Papathanasiou 2025 Table 2 PK estimation block lists equation TI50i=θTI50⋅(IGG/15)θTI50,IGG⋅exp⁡(ηTI50)\mathrm{TI50}_{} = \theta_{\mathrm{TI50}} \cdot (\mathrm{IGG}/15)^{\theta_{\mathrm{TI50,IGG}}} \cdot \exp(\eta_{\mathrm{TI50}}), Table 4 lists IgG → ADC TI50 one covariate effects cycle-1 exposure. However, value appears Table 2 θTI50,IGG\theta_{\mathrm{TI50,IGG}} (every theta equation block numeric estimate). packaged model therefore omits (IGG/15)θTI50,IGG(\mathrm{IGG}/15)^{\theta_{\mathrm{TI50,IGG}}} factor. IgG effect preserved explicit IgG--CL exponent (0.170) IgG--Imax exponent (0.192). net effect cycle-1 ADC exposure varying IgG across 5th-95th percentile range dominated CL Imax pathways already implemented; quantitative agreement paper’s IgG-effect row Table 4 checked stochastic-virtual-cohort exposures reproduced. Time origin time-varying CL. source uses “Time” since first dose sigmoidal CL function. packaged model uses rxode2’s t (simulation time, starting first event), identical first dose t = 0. Multi-cycle simulations reset Time dose — matches Papathanasiou 2025. Race encoding. source dataset uses single categorical RACE column (“White” / “Asian” / “Black/African American” / “” / “Missing”). packaged model decomposes canonical RACE_ASIAN RACE_BLACK indicator columns (one-hot), reference category 0 = White / / Missing. two indicators mutually exclusive source dataset. sBCMA units. Papathanasiou 2025 Table 1 reports sBCMA μg/L typical-patient definition (Methods, Sect. 2.3) reference value (50) stated ng/mL; two units numerically equivalent (1 ng/mL = 1 μg/L). packaged model documents canonical SBCMA column ng/mL. Combination-therapy pooling. source pools Bor-Dex (DREAMM-6/-7, 35.7%) Len-Dex (DREAMM-6, 4.6%) single binary combination indicator Imax parameter estimation; Pom-Dex backbone (DREAMM-8, n = 150) held external validation . packaged COMBO_BELAMAF indicator follows pooling convention; per-backbone effects modeled. Residual error model. Papathanasiou 2025 reports residual error additive natural-log scale variance 0.0633 (log(ng/mL))^2 (Y = ln(IPRED) + ε). operationally equivalent proportional residual linear scale SD = sqrt(0.0633) = 0.2516 small errors, packaged model encodes (Cc ~ prop(propSd)). IIV Imax. Papathanasiou 2025 footnote: “normal distribution CV% = SQRT(Ω) / θ × 100”, indicating additive IIV linear Imax scale omega computed reported 29.0% CV around typical Imax -0.403, giving omega = (0.290 × 0.403)^2 = 0.01366. reported magnitude, small fraction stochastic-cohort draws may yield Imax > 0 (.e., slight CL increase time); feature additive parameterization, coding change. Inter-occasion variability (IOV) propagated. source’s cys-mcMMAF model includes IOV terms (κ CL_MMAF cys-mcMMAF Vc). ADC model IOV terms reported, none implemented . Virtual cohort. Demographics simulated match marginal distributions reported Papathanasiou 2025 Table 1. Continuous covariates (WT, BMI, ALB, IGG, SBCMA) drawn log-normal / normal distributions anchored median approximate spread; binary / categorical covariates (RACE_ASIAN, RACE_BLACK, COMBO_BELAMAF) match reported proportions. Joint covariate structure (e.g., correlations sBCMA, IgG, albumin RRMM patients) simulated. --scope covariates. Sex retained final model collinearity body weight; ethnicity, geographic region, prior treatments, prior anti-CD38 therapy, mild--severe renal impairment (eGFR / NCI-ODWG), mild--moderate hepatic impairment (NCI-ODWG), age, pharmacogenomic transporter activity tested retained. IV infusion duration. simulations use 1-hour infusion (DUR = 1/24 day), consistent labelled belantamab mafodotin administration regimen.

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: Citation: Perez-Ruixo C, Liu L, Galpern WR, Perez-Ruixo JJ. Mechanistic Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Model Tau-Targeted Antibody Posdinemab Healthy Participants Participants Alzheimer’s Disease. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2026;119(4):979-990. doi:10.1002/cpt.70173 Description: Mechanism-based population PK-PD model full TMDD anti-tau monoclonal antibody posdinemab serum, CSF, ISF (Perez-Ruixo 2025): two-compartment serum disposition linear elimination, distribution CSF compartment downstream ISF compartment, explicit second-order binding free posdinemab free p217+tau CSF tau seeds ISF, internalization free target drug-target complex, Alzheimer’s-disease-vs-healthy effect baseline p217+tau. Article DOI: https://doi.org/10.1002/cpt.70173 Pérez-Ruixo et al. (2025) developed mechanism-based population PK-PD model full target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD) anti-tau monoclonal antibody posdinemab (JNJ-63733657). model couples two-compartment serum disposition (linear elimination central, peripheral distribution) slow distribution CSF compartment downstream ISF compartment. Free posdinemab CSF binds free p217+tau (phosphorylated-tau biomarker) posdinemab ISF binds tau seeds; complexes cleared common first-order rate constant (kint), also subject dissociation back free target. CSF ISF p217+tau / tau-seed compartments zero-order endogenous production first-order elimination (kc). Allometric scaling PK parameters 70 kg reference subject applied fixed exponents 0.75 (clearances) 1.00 (volumes). Alzheimer’s disease status retained PK-PD covariate; shifts baseline free p217+tau CSF (R0) factor 7.56 (0.793 → 5.995 pmol/L).

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: Single first--human Phase 1 dose-escalation study (NCT03375697, Galpern 2024) 69 participants: 56 healthy adults (81.2 %) 13 adults Alzheimer’s disease (18.8 %). Median age 67 years (range 55-78), median body weight 75 kg (range 51-106), median height 169 cm (range 150-192), 46.4 % female, 98.6 % White (98.5 % Caucasian, 1.5 % Hispanic White) 1.4 % Asian. 53 participants received active posdinemab (single ascending 1, 3, 10, 30, 60 mg/kg IV; multiple ascending 5, 15, 30, 50 mg/kg IV every 28 days 13 weeks); 16 received placebo. Active subjects contributed 907 serum 169 CSF posdinemab concentration observations, 69 contributed 294 CSF p217+tau observations (free + total combined). Estimation FOCE NONMEM 7.3.0.
code: str(rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab"))$meta$population) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> List of 12 #>  $ n_subjects    : int 69 #>  $ n_studies     : int 1 #>  $ study         : chr "Phase 1 first-in-human dose-escalation, NCT03375697 (Galpern 2024)" #>  $ age_range     : chr "55-78 years (median 67)" #>  $ height_range  : chr "150-192 cm (median 169)" #>  $ weight_range  : chr "51-106 kg (median 75)" #>  $ sex_female_pct: num 46.4 #>  $ race_ethnicity: chr "98.6% White (98.5% Caucasian, 1.5% Hispanic of White), 1.4% Asian (Table 1)" #>  $ disease_state : chr "Healthy adults (n = 56, 81.2%) and adults with Alzheimer's disease (n = 13, 18.8%)" #>  $ dose_range    : chr "Single ascending dose 1, 3, 10, 30, 60 mg/kg IV (healthy); multiple ascending dose 5, 15, 30, or 50 mg/kg IV ev"| __truncated__ #>  $ regions       : chr "Multinational Phase 1 (Galpern 2024)" #>  $ notes         : chr "53 active-treatment participants contributed 907 serum and 169 CSF posdinemab concentration observations; all 6"| __truncated__

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: Original individual data publicly available. figure-replication simulations use typical-value (zeroRe) deterministic runs three body-weight strata (40, 80, 120 kg, matching paper’s Figure 5 sensitivity analysis) AD healthy R0 baselines.
code: mw_da     <- 148000           # posdinemab MW (Discussion) ref_wt_kg <- 80               # paper's reference body weight for fixed-dose simulations single_dose_mg_per_kg <- 30   # one of the SAD dose levels  # Convert mg/kg to absolute mg for an 80 kg subject dose_mg_30mgkg <- single_dose_mg_per_kg * ref_wt_kg  # Q4W repeat doses q4w_doses_mg <- c(`12.5_mgkg` = 12.5 * ref_wt_kg,                   `25_mgkg`   = 25   * ref_wt_kg,                   `37.5_mgkg` = 37.5 * ref_wt_kg)

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
code: modf <- nlmixr2lib::readModelDb("PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab") mod  <- modf() mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod)  # Run a single 30 mg/kg IV bolus in an 80 kg healthy subject; sample states # every 8 hours out to day 180. times_h <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 24, by = 1),                          seq(24, 24 * 28, by = 4),                          seq(24 * 28, 24 * 180, by = 24))))  build_ev <- function(dose_mg, dis_ad, weight_kg) {   ev <- et(id = 1) |>     et(amt = dose_mg, cmt = "central", time = 0, id = 1) |>     et(time = times_h, cmt = "Cc", id = 1) |>     et(time = times_h, cmt = "Ccsf", id = 1) |>     et(time = times_h, cmt = "FreeTau", id = 1) |>     et(time = times_h, cmt = "TotalTau", id = 1)   list(ev = ev,        iCov = data.frame(id = 1, WT = weight_kg, DIS_AD = dis_ad)) }  run_typ <- function(dose_mg, dis_ad, weight_kg) {   pk <- build_ev(dose_mg, dis_ad, weight_kg)   s  <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, pk$ev, iCov = pk$iCov, returnType = "data.frame")   s[!duplicated(s$time), ] |> mutate(day = time / 24) }  sim_hv30  <- run_typ(dose_mg_30mgkg, dis_ad = 0, weight_kg = ref_wt_kg) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint' sim_ad30  <- run_typ(dose_mg_30mgkg, dis_ad = 1, weight_kg = ref_wt_kg) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint'

what: Figure 2 — concentration-time profiles after a single IV dose (SAD)
title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: Figure 2 Pérez-Ruixo 2025 (panel ) shows posdinemab concentrations serum CSF free / total p217+tau CSF SAD cohorts. reproduce typical-value time courses 30 mg/kg cohort healthy 80 kg subject.
code: sad_long <- sim_hv30 |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Ccsf, FreeTau, TotalTau),                names_to = "panel", values_to = "value") |>   mutate(panel = recode(panel,                         Cc       = "Serum posdinemab (pmol/L)",                         Ccsf     = "CSF posdinemab (pmol/L)",                         FreeTau  = "CSF free p217+tau (pmol/L)",                         TotalTau = "CSF total p217+tau (pmol/L)"),          panel = factor(panel, levels = c(            "Serum posdinemab (pmol/L)",            "CSF posdinemab (pmol/L)",            "CSF total p217+tau (pmol/L)",            "CSF free p217+tau (pmol/L)")))  ggplot(sad_long |> filter(day <= 84), aes(day, value)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, colour = "steelblue") +   facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x = "Days since dose",     y = NULL,     title  = "Replicates Figure 2(a), 30 mg/kg cohort, of Pérez-Ruixo 2025",     caption = "Typical 80 kg healthy participant; deterministic run with zero IIV."   )

what: Figure 4 — Q4W multiple-dose simulation in AD participants
title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: Figure 4 Pérez-Ruixo 2025 shows deterministic simulations weight-based Q4W IV regimens AD participants. reproduce time-course serum posdinemab CSF p217+tau target engagement 25 mg/kg Q4W regimen.
code: q4w_dose_amount_mg <- 25 * ref_wt_kg   # 2000 mg q4w_interval_h <- 28 * 24 n_doses <- 12  mad_obs_t <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, q4w_interval_h * n_doses, by = 24),   q4w_interval_h * (0:(n_doses - 1)),   q4w_interval_h * (0:(n_doses - 1)) + 1 )))  build_mad <- function(dis_ad) {   ev <- et(id = 1)   for (k in 0:(n_doses - 1)) {     ev <- et(ev, amt = q4w_dose_amount_mg, cmt = "central",              time = k * q4w_interval_h, id = 1)   }   ev <- et(ev, time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "Cc",       id = 1) |>         et(time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "Ccsf",     id = 1) |>         et(time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "FreeTau",  id = 1) |>         et(time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "TotalTau", id = 1)   pk <- list(ev = ev,              iCov = data.frame(id = 1, WT = ref_wt_kg, DIS_AD = dis_ad))   rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, pk$ev, iCov = pk$iCov,                   returnType = "data.frame")[!duplicated(rxode2::rxSolve(     mod_typ, pk$ev, iCov = pk$iCov, returnType = "data.frame")$time), ] |>     mutate(day = time / 24) }  mad_ad <- build_mad(dis_ad = 1) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint' mad_hv <- build_mad(dis_ad = 0) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint'  # Pct of p217+tau bound to posdinemab in CSF: complex / (free + complex) mad_long <- bind_rows(   mad_ad |> transmute(day, Cc, Ccsf, FreeTau, TotalTau,                        pct_bound = (TotalTau - FreeTau) / TotalTau * 100,                        group = "AD"),   mad_hv |> transmute(day, Cc, Ccsf, FreeTau, TotalTau,                        pct_bound = (TotalTau - FreeTau) / TotalTau * 100,                        group = "Healthy") )  ggplot(mad_long, aes(day, Cc, colour = group)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Days", y = "Serum posdinemab (pmol/L, log scale)",        colour = NULL,        title = "Q4W 25 mg/kg IV multiple-dose; serum posdinemab",        caption = "Reproduces the serum-PK panel from Figure 4(a) of Pérez-Ruixo 2025.") ggplot(mad_long, aes(day, pct_bound, colour = group)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   ylim(0, 100) +   labs(x = "Days", y = "% CSF p217+tau bound to posdinemab",        colour = NULL,        title  = "Target engagement in CSF after Q4W 25 mg/kg IV",        caption = "Reproduces the binding panel from Figure 4(b) of Pérez-Ruixo 2025.")

what: Figure 5 — body-weight sensitivity at fixed-dose 1000 mg Q4W
title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: Figure 5 shows body-weight differences (40-120 kg) within 90 % prediction interval typical 80 kg subject fixed-dose regimens. reproduce deterministic typical-value lines 1000 mg Q4W across three body weights AD participants.
code: q4w_fixed_dose_mg <- 1000 n_doses_fixed <- 12  build_fixed <- function(weight_kg) {   ev <- et(id = 1)   for (k in 0:(n_doses_fixed - 1)) {     ev <- et(ev, amt = q4w_fixed_dose_mg, cmt = "central",              time = k * q4w_interval_h, id = 1)   }   ev <- et(ev, time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "Cc",       id = 1) |>         et(time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "Ccsf",     id = 1) |>         et(time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "FreeTau",  id = 1) |>         et(time = mad_obs_t, cmt = "TotalTau", id = 1)   pk <- list(ev = ev,              iCov = data.frame(id = 1, WT = weight_kg, DIS_AD = 1))   s  <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, pk$ev, iCov = pk$iCov,                         returnType = "data.frame")   s[!duplicated(s$time), ] |> mutate(day = time / 24, weight = weight_kg) }  fixed_df <- bind_rows(build_fixed(40), build_fixed(80), build_fixed(120)) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalq', 'etalvp', 'etalqcsf', 'etalvcsf', 'etalvisf', 'etalr0', 'etalkc', 'etalkint'  ggplot(fixed_df, aes(day, Cc, colour = factor(weight))) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Days", y = "Serum posdinemab (pmol/L, log scale)",        colour = "Body weight (kg)",        title  = "Fixed-dose 1000 mg Q4W IV — sensitivity to body weight (AD)",        caption = "Reproduces Figure 5(a) of Pérez-Ruixo 2025.")

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: paper publish formal NCA table, report (Discussion) serum α-phase half-life 38.2 hours, serum terminal half-life 20.6 days (494 hours), CSF terminal half-life 36.5 days (876 hours). perform self-consistency NCA typical-value single-dose simulation. PKNCA summary single 30 mg/kg IV bolus (typical 80 kg healthy participant; serum posdinemab pmol/L).
code: # Use the SAD 30 mg/kg simulation already run above. nca_conc <- sim_hv30 |>   filter(time > 0, Cc > 0) |>   transmute(id = 1L, time = time, Cc,             treatment = "30 mg/kg IV SD, healthy, 80 kg")  dose_df <- data.frame(id = 1L, time = 0, amt = dose_mg_30mgkg,                       treatment = "30 mg/kg IV SD, healthy, 80 kg")  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(start = 0, end = Inf,                         cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                         aucinf.obs = TRUE, half.life = TRUE) nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (1) #> is not allowed knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_res), digits = 3,              caption = "PKNCA summary for a single 30 mg/kg IV bolus (typical 80 kg healthy participant; serum posdinemab in pmol/L).")

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: algebraic-derivation cross-checks (kd, kss, ksyn, Vss) match paper’s Discussion-text values 3 significant figures. quantitative discrepancy time-course-derived CSF/serum ratio reflects structural ambiguity printed Equations 4 7 (see Errata section ).

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: supplement’s printed Equations 4 7 contain notation slips violate strict mass balance inconsistent across two equations . key issues, identified cross-checking methods narrative parameter values’ physical interpretation, : Eq. 4 binding-term unit mismatch. supplement prints dACSF/dt = ... - kon · R · (ACSF/VCSF) + koff · RC, ACSF stated drug amount (pmol). R RC concentrations (pmol/L), printed binding term units concentration·time⁻¹, amount·time⁻¹, appear dACSF/dt (amount-rate) equation. mass-balanced form ... - kon · R · ACSF + koff · RC · VCSF. Equation 5 (dRC/dt concentration-rate equation) dimensionally consistent printed. Eq. 4 outflow term — VISF VCSF expected. supplement prints ... - (QCSF/VCSF + QISF/VISF) · ACSF + (QISF/VISF) · AISF. standard inter-compartmental clearance (mass rate = clearance × source concentration), outflow CSF→ISF QISF · Ccsf = QISF/VCSF · ACSF, QISF/VISF · ACSF. mass-balanced form used ... - (QCSF + QISF)/VCSF · ACSF + QISF/VISF · AISF. Eq. 7 inflow term — QCSF QISF expected. supplement prints dAISF/dt = (QCSF/VCSF) · ACSF - (QISF/VISF) · AISF - .... methods narrative explicitly states QCSF connects central↔︎CSF QISF connects CSF↔︎ISF; inflow term dAISF/dt therefore must use QISF, QCSF. implement dAISF/dt = QISF/VCSF · ACSF - QISF/VISF · AISF - kon_ISF · RISF · AISF + koff_ISF · RCISF · VISF. Eq. 7 binding-term unit mismatch. issue item 1 ISF binding term: printed kon_ISF · RISF · (AISF/VISF) concentration- rate; amount-rate form kon_ISF · RISF · AISF, corresponding unbinding term koff_ISF · RCISF · VISF. Eq. 6 / Eq. 7 — single ksyn symbol two implicit values. supplement uses one symbol ksyn CSF (Eq. 3) ISF (Eq. 6) free-target equations. methods describe ksyn = R0 · kc (single value), prescribed initial condition RISF(0) = 10 · R(0) steady-state baseline ksyn_ISF = 10 · R0 · kc = 10 · ksyn_CSF. implement two distinct production rates (ksyn_csf = kc · R0 ksyn_isf = kc · 10 · R0) system steady state t = 0. Table 2 unit label kon. Table 2 displays kon units “nmol × mL⁻¹ × hour⁻¹”, concentration-rate dimension, inverse-concentration-time dimension required second-order association rate constant. Discussion’s algebraic derivation kd = koff / kon = 848.5 pmol/L works kon (nmol·mL⁻¹)⁻¹·hour⁻¹ = mL·(nmol·hour)⁻¹. treat Table 2 unit label typo use inverse-concentration-time interpretation, converting (pmol/L)⁻¹·hour⁻¹ internal consistency R0 values reported pmol/L. Table 2 description QISF. Table 2 describes QISF “Intercompartmental clearance central compartment ISF”, methods narrative supplement equations consistently use QISF CSF↔︎ISF clearance. follow methods narrative (CSF↔︎ISF) since equations encode. Methods text — “linear pathway (kint)” free posdinemab CSF. methods state “free posdinemab CSF eliminated via linear pathway (kint) binding free p217+tau”. supplement’s Eq. 4 include -kint · ACSF term, including term directly kint = 0.299/h (= 2.32 h half-life) inconsistent Discussion’s reported CSF terminal half-life 36.5 days. interpret methods phrase describing indirect elimination path free posdinemab via complex formation (kon · R · ACSF) subsequent complex internalization (kint · RC · VCSF), consistent printed equations reported half-life.

title: PerezRuixo_2025_posdinemab
text: ISF binding decomposition. paper assumes “free posdinemab ISF binds tau seeds 20-fold higher affinity (lower kd) compared binding p217+tau monomers CSF” specify whether achieved reducing koff increasing kon. adopt kon_ISF = kon (mAb-target -rates typically diffusion-limited near 10⁵-10⁶ M⁻¹s⁻¹) koff_ISF = koff / 20; preserves stated kd_ISF = kd_CSF / 20 keeping -rate physical ceiling. Alternative implementations (e.g., kon_ISF = 20 · kon, koff_ISF = koff) yield equilibrium binding fraction long times different transient kinetics. estimated IIV correlations. paper states “Correlations random effects explored incorporated model necessary.” Table 2 reports diagonal IIV entries (-diagonals), implementation uses independent (diagonal) IIV matrix. Allometric exponents fixed. Fixed 0.75 clearances 1.00 volumes per paper’s note estimating exponents improve MOFV (∆MOFV = -6.87, df = 8, p > 0.05). Residual error scale. Table 2 reports σ values bare numbers (8.73, 16.4, 11.2, 13.3) without explicit units; interpret percentages CV (consistent IIV column convention) store proportional residual standard deviations linear-space concentrations. Quantitative time-course discrepancy CSF/serum ratio. model implemented (mass-balanced form Equations 4 7) reproduces every algebraic derivation Discussion (kd, kss, ksyn, Vss, serum half-lives) predicts CSF/serum concentration ratio Day 14 approximately 0.77 % (typical 80 kg healthy participant) versus paper’s reported model-based value 0.26 % observed geometric mean 0.28 % (95 % CI 0.22-0.35 %). Re-implementing Equation 4 printed (QISF/VISF) · ACSF outflow term reduces Day 14 ratio 0.47 % (still published 0.26 %) variant Cisf becomes 5× larger Ccsf, contradicting Discussion’s claim ISF concentration 67 % lower CSF. two narrative claims (Day 14 CSF/serum ≈ 0.26 % Cisf < Ccsf steady state) simultaneously satisfiable either straightforward equation interpretations explored without access original NONMEM control stream. retain mass-balanced interpretation (Errata items 1-4) physically defensible report discrepancy explicitly . Compartment naming. mechanistic compartments (ACSF, AISF, R, RC, RISF, RCISF) follow paper’s notation directly. triggers warnings nlmixr2lib::checkModelConventions canonical PK-compartment list (central, peripheral1, …). mechanistic terminology preserved traceability paper. Dosing unit convention. units$dosing "mg" model internally tracks drug amounts pmol; conversion performed via f(central) <- 1e9 / mw_da (= 6757 pmol/mg, MW = 148 kDa). triggers dosing_concentration warning checkModelConventions dosing-unit dimension (mass) declared concentration-unit dimension (pmol/L, molar) differ. bioavailability factor acting unit-conversion factor spirit Hood_2021_medi7836.R.

what: Romiplostim PopPK/PD model for adults with immune thrombocytopenia
title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: Petrov 2024 paper modifies previously developed healthy-volunteer PopPK/PD model romiplostim (Makarenko 2024, reference 20 paper) platelet dynamics match patients chronic immune thrombocytopenia (ITP). PK side 1-compartment subcutaneous model (first-order absorption elimination) PD side sigmoid Emax stimulation platelet precursor production 4-compartment Friberg-style transit chain feeds circulating platelets, first-order eliminated kdeg. paper modifies platelet production constant kin degradation constant kdeg (IIV) match published platelet kinetic differences healthy subjects ITP patients (Ballem 1987 / Stoll 1985, references 31–32 Petrov 2024) reproduce baseline platelet count distribution romiplostim-ref pivotal trial (Kuter 2008, reference 13). Petrov 2024 (paper): Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev 14(2):116–126 (PMID 39702972). PK/PD backbone (healthy volunteers): Makarenko 2024, Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev (PMID 38168134).
code: mod <- readModelDb("Petrov_2024_romiplostim") ui  <- rxode2::rxode(mod) cat(ui$reference, sep = "\n") #> Petrov A, Makarenko I, Sokolov V, Drai R, Bondareva I, Sigaev V, Stuchkov M, Galustyan A, Stepanenko I, Lebedev V, Mishchenko A. Optimization of Romiplostim Biosimilar Efficacy Trial Using In Silico Clinical Trial Approach for Patients With Immune Thrombocytopenia. Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev. 2025 Feb;14(2):116-126. doi:10.1002/cpdd.1494 (PMID 39702972). PK/PD backbone (healthy volunteers): Makarenko I, Petrov A, Sokolov V, Drai R, Mishchenko A, Bondareva I, Galustyan A, Sigaev V. Population Pharmacokinetic and Pharmacodynamic Modeling of Romiplostim Biosimilar GP40141 and Reference Product in Healthy Volunteers to Evaluate Biosimilarity. Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev. 2024. doi:10.1002/cpdd.1367 (PMID 38168134; reference 20 in Petrov 2024).

title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: Petrov 2024 develops ITP modification combining two prior published datasets (none appear current paper patient-level data): PK/PD parameters inherited Makarenko 2024, healthy-volunteer comparative biosimilarity study romiplostim-ref vs GP40141. ITP-specific platelet kinetics modifications come Ballem 1987 Stoll 1985, baseline platelet distribution targets pivotal romiplostim-ref efficacy trial (Kuter 2008, NCT00102323 + NCT00102336; 41 non-splenectomized + 42 splenectomized adults). packaged model represents adults chronic ITP. simulated cohort Petrov 2024 (Methods) used body weight drawn normal distribution mean 77 kg CV 20%, 5% rate NAB-positive subjects, matching romiplostim-ref pivotal-trial demographics. default parameter set packaged model non-splenectomized, ITP mechanism 1 (increased platelet degradation ) subpopulation. See “subpopulations” three remaining variants.

Default parameter set: non-splenectomized, ITP mechanism 1 ("| __truncated__ #>  $ dose_range    : chr "Subcutaneous romiplostim with weekly dose-titration algorithm targeting platelets 50-200 x 10^9/L (validation s"| __truncated__ #>  $ nab_pos_pct   : num 5 #>  $ regions       : chr "Russian Federation (clinical development of GP40141 biosimilar, Makarenko 2024 healthy-volunteer source data).
