title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: Every parameter’s source location also recorded inline comment next ini() line inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Petrov_2024_romiplostim.R.

title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: ODE term dimensions: steady state without drug: precursor_i_ss = kin/ktr = 4.3 / 0.02 = 215 ×10^9/L (precursor) circ_ss = kin/kdeg = 4.3 / 0.20 = 21.5 ×10^9/L reported population mean baseline (Petrov 2024 Results, p. 121) ~18 ×10^9/L (CV 35%) non-splenectomized patients; typical-value prediction 21.5 sits within simulated population’s log-normal spread.
code: mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) ev_ss <- rxode2::et(amt = 0, time = 0, cmt = "depot") |>   rxode2::et(seq(0, 24 * 30, by = 24)) ev_ss$WT <- 77 ev_ss$ADA_POS <- 0 sim_ss <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, ev_ss) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalkel', 'etalec50', 'etalemax', 'etalktr', 'etalkin', 'etalkdeg' range(sim_ss$circ) #> [1] 21.5 21.5

what: Baseline platelet distribution (replicates Petrov 2024 Figure S3)
title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: Petrov 2024 Figure S3 shows simulated baseline platelet distribution 1000 virtual non-splenectomized ITP patients without drug, alongside log-normal target derived romiplostim-ref pivotal trial (mean 18 ×10^9/L, CV 35%). simulated histogram packaged model .  reported target mean ~18 CV 35% approached (exactly matched) typical-value-driven log-normal IIV chain packaged default subpopulation. Petrov 2024 paper notes (Results, p. 121) final ITP parameter values “allowed obtaining steady-state baseline platelet distributions … closely aligned observed pivotal efficacy trial,” achieved tuning (kin, kdeg, IIV_kdeg) 4 subpopulations.
code: set.seed(20260428) n <- 1000 ev_pop <- data.frame(   id      = seq_len(n),   time    = 720,        # 30 days; well past any transient   evid    = 0,   amt     = 0,   cmt     = "depot",   WT      = rnorm(n, 77, 0.20 * 77),   ADA_POS = rbinom(n, 1, 0.05) ) sim_pop <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, ev_pop)  # One row per subject at t = 720 h is the baseline platelet observation baseline <- sim_pop |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::summarise(plt = dplyr::last(circ), .groups = "drop")  cat("Simulated baseline platelet (non-splen mech 1, n=", nrow(baseline),     "): mean =", round(mean(baseline$plt), 1),     "; CV% =", round(100 * sd(baseline$plt) / mean(baseline$plt), 1), "\n",     sep = "") #> Simulated baseline platelet (non-splen mech 1, n=1000): mean =24.1; CV% =51.7  ggplot(baseline, aes(plt)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "steelblue", colour = "white") +   geom_vline(xintercept = 18, linetype = "dashed") +   labs(x = "Baseline platelet count (10^9/L)",        y = "Virtual patients",        title = "Replicates Petrov 2024 Figure S3 (non-splenectomized, mechanism 1)",        caption = "Dashed line at 18 = target population mean (Kuter 2008 pivotal trial baseline).")

title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: single subcutaneous dose 1 µg/kg 77-kg NAB-negative ITP patient produces brief, modest platelet rise — characteristic sub-EC50 stimulus (Cmax(Cc) ≈ 9 pg/mL << EC50 = 42 pg/mL) stimulation factor approximately Emax·Cmax/(EC50+Cmax) ≈ 9·0.18 ≈ 1.6 (precursor production ~2.6× baseline). Higher weekly doses (3–10 µg/kg clinical practice) produce stronger sustained responses.
code: ev_dose <- rxode2::et(amt = 77, time = 0, cmt = "depot") |>   rxode2::et(seq(0, 24 * 14, by = 1)) ev_dose$WT <- 77; ev_dose$ADA_POS <- 0 sim_dose <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, ev_dose) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalkel', 'etalec50', 'etalemax', 'etalktr', 'etalkin', 'etalkdeg'  ggplot(sim_dose, aes(time / 24, circ)) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 21.5, linetype = "dotted") +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Circulating platelets (10^9/L)",        title = "Single 1 µg/kg romiplostim dose, typical non-splen ITP patient",        caption = "Dotted line: drug-free baseline kin/kdeg = 21.5.")

what: Weekly dosing toward target platelet response
title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: Weekly subcutaneous dosing 3 µg/kg typical non-splenectomized mechanism-1 patient drives circulating platelets 50 ×10^9/L threshold used define platelet response pivotal trial. Petrov 2024 ISCT (Methods, “Silico Biosimilar Efficacy Trial”) uses weekly dose-titration algorithm (start 1 µg/kg, max 10 µg/kg) targeting platelets 50–150 ×10^9/L; faithful replication algorithm beyond scope packaged model implemented user code calls model loop feedback controller.
code: ev_w <- rxode2::et(amt = 3 * 77, ii = 24 * 7, until = 24 * 12 * 7, cmt = "depot") |>   rxode2::et(seq(0, 24 * 14 * 7, by = 6)) ev_w$WT <- 77; ev_w$ADA_POS <- 0 sim_w <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, ev_w) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalkel', 'etalec50', 'etalemax', 'etalktr', 'etalkin', 'etalkdeg'  ggplot(sim_w, aes(time / (24 * 7), circ)) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 150, linetype = "dashed") +   labs(x = "Time (weeks)", y = "Circulating platelets (10^9/L)",        title = "Weekly 3 µg/kg romiplostim, typical non-splen mech-1 ITP patient",        caption = "Dashed lines mark the 50–150 ×10^9/L target window of the biosimilar efficacy ISCT.")

title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: Petrov 2024 supplement Table S1 reports four ITP parameter sets differ platelet kinetic constants kin, kdeg, IIV_kdeg. PK PD parameters shared. switch packaged model one alternative subpopulations, override lkin, lkdeg, etalkdeg variance via rxSolve(..., params = ...): Typical-individual baseline platelet (kin/kdeg) subpopulation.
code: swap_subpop <- function(kin_val, kdeg_val) {   c(lkin = log(kin_val), lkdeg = log(kdeg_val)) }  ev_demo <- rxode2::et(amt = 0, time = 0, cmt = "depot") |>   rxode2::et(seq(0, 24 * 30, by = 24)) ev_demo$WT <- 77; ev_demo$ADA_POS <- 0  baselines <- tibble::tibble(   subpop = c("Non-splen, mech 1 (default)",              "Non-splen, mech 2",              "Splen, mech 1",              "Splen, mech 2"),   kin    = c(4.3, 1.7, 4.3, 1.7),   kdeg   = c(0.20, 0.08, 0.26, 0.10) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(circ_ss = kin / kdeg)  knitr::kable(baselines, digits = 2,              caption = "Typical-individual baseline platelet (kin/kdeg) for each subpopulation.")

title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: published erratum correction notice identified Petrov 2024 (DOI 10.1002/cpdd.1494, PMID 39702972). PubMed Wiley journal landing page searched 2026-04-28; neither returned correction notice. following minor source notations required interpretation; none errata, recorded audit trail: Residual error parameter b = 0.093. Supplement Table S1 lists single proportional error model parameter without naming output. modeled observation paper’s validation ISCT platelet count, b interpreted proportional residual error platelet count. reader wishing use model separate concentration residual error consult upstream Makarenko 2024 paper (originally fit PK PD data) recover PK error model. Covariate functional forms. Supplement Table S1 reports V ~ WT = 1.04 kel ~ NAB-status = 0.25 without printing functional form. paper used Monolix-Suite 2021R1 (Methods, “Software”); Monolix default log-normal parameters allometric power form continuous covariates log-additive form categorical, packaged model implements: V = TVV·(WT/77)^1.04 kel = TVKEL·exp(0.25·NAB+). reference weight 77 kg matches reported population mean (Methods, p. 119). Steady-state baseline mean ≠ kin/kdeg. paper reports population baseline mean ~18 ×10^9/L non-splenectomized patients typical-individual prediction kin/kdeg = 21.5. expected log-normal IIV asymmetry: simulated population mean typical-individual value IIV wide parameter ratios skewed IIV(kdeg) = 50%. consistent parameters tuned (Petrov 2024 Results, p. 121: steady-state distributions tuned via 1000-patient simulation).

title: Petrov_2024_romiplostim
text: Default parameter set. packaged Petrov_2024_romiplostim() function uses non-splenectomized mechanism-1 subpopulation (Petrov 2024 supplement Table S1). 3 subpopulations selected via params = c(lkin = log(...), lkdeg = log(...)) overrides rxSolve calls; IIV(kdeg) differs (50% vs 70%) — splenectomized populations, override etalkdeg variance well. Dose-titration algorithm. paper’s validation (Figure 2) ISCT (Figure S4/S5) results obtained dose-titration algorithms documented Petrov 2024 supplement (validation: start 1 µg/kg, max 15 µg/kg, target 50–200 ×10^9/L) Methods (efficacy ISCT: start 1 µg/kg, max 10 µg/kg, target 50–150 ×10^9/L). Faithful replication titration algorithms — including 5% dropout, 4 ITP subpopulation mixing ratios, 2-week dose-decrease triggers — beyond scope packaged structural model. Users wishing reproduce Figure 2 wrap model per-subject feedback controller user code. Demographic distributions. Sex, race / ethnicity, demographic distributions modeled (paper report covariate effect ). simulated cohort uses WT NAB+ status, drawn distributions Petrov 2024 Methods (WT ~ Normal(77, CV 20%); NAB+ ~ Bernoulli(0.05)). Compartment naming deviation. packaged model uses precursor1, precursor2, precursor3, precursor4, circ platelet maturation chain circulating-platelet compartments. canonical compartment-name list (depot, central, peripheral1/2, effect, target, complex, total_target, transit<n>); transit<n> reserved absorption-chain transits existing convention. names used match paper’s diagram (Petrov 2024 Figure S2: P1, P2, P3, P4, PLT) established Friberg-style myelosuppression literature platelet kinetics modeled. nlmixr2lib::checkModelConventions() flags warnings; intentional documented . Observation variable name. packaged model exposes PLT (circulating platelet count, 10^9/L) single observation; Cc (drug concentration, ng/mL) computed observed source paper’s validation dataset (platelet counts measured pivotal romiplostim-ref trial). checkModelConventions() warns single-output models use Cc; PLT deviation intentional modeled measurement concentration.

title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: Citation: Pouzin C, Gibiansky L, Fagniez N, Tod M, Chadjaa M, Nguyen L. Integrated multiple analytes semi-mechanistic population pharmacokinetic model tusamitamab ravtansine, DM4 anti-CEACAM5 antibody-drug conjugate. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2022;49(4):381–394. Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-021-09799-0 Online Resource 1 (Monolix .mlxtran): supplementary material article. First--human study: TED13751 (NCT02187848). Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab packages integrated semi-mechanistic multi-analyte popPK model tusamitamab ravtansine (SAR408701, anti-CEACAM5 IgG1-SPDB-DM4 ADC) developed TED13751 first--human study. model fits SAR408701 (DAR≥1), naked antibody (NAB), DM4, MeDM4 plasma data simultaneously, explicit two-compartment chain DAR0–DAR8 species. DAR_n → DAR_(n-1) deconjugation central compartment modelled irreversible first-order process feeds one-compartment DM4 catabolite converts MeDM4.

title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: TED13751 study enrolled 254 adults advanced solid tumors expressing CEACAM5 (escalation cohorts 5–190 mg/m² Q2W Q3W expansion cohorts 100 mg/m² Q2W colorectal, gastric, NSCLC high/low CEACAM5, SCLC). Per Methods, 3746 SAR408701, 3740 DM4, 3734 MeDM4, 3734 NAB plasma concentrations modelled across 58 cycles, DAR distributions quantified LC-HRMS 13 patients (Pouzin 2022, Table 1, Methods § “Clinical study”, “Bioanalytical methods”). Pouzin 2022 modelling paper tabulate baseline demographics 254-patient analysis set; companion covariate paper (Pouzin et al., CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol 2022, doi:10.1002/psp4.12769) reports full demographic table basis covariate-aware refit. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab")$meta$population.

title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: Every ini() parameter carries -file comment pointing back source. table collects pointers one place. published additive ADC residual SD 1.03 µg/mL converted µM ini() dividing SAR408701 molecular weight (150 000 g/mol) multiplying 1000 mL/L: 1.03 / 150000 * 1000 = 0.006867 µM.

title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: packaged model carries covariates (Pouzin 2022 reports structural base; covariates evaluated companion CPT-PSP paper). typical- value simulation therefore needs single subject’s worth dosing. Pouzin 2022 reports typical exposures “100 mg/m² Q2W” body surface area specified. Cmax / AUC values Table 7 reproduce exactly assuming BSA = 1.79 m² (.e., typical 179 mg total dose), consistent dose-finding cohort weight ranges reported companion covariate paper. use BSA = 1.79 m² throughout vignette.
code: # 100 mg/m^2 Q2W, BSA assumed = 1.79 m^2 (typical adult) bsa_m2     <- 1.79 dose_mg_m2 <- 100 mw_adc     <- 150000           # g/mol — Pouzin 2022 Table 2 dose_mg    <- dose_mg_m2 * bsa_m2 dose_umol  <- dose_mg / (mw_adc / 1000)   # mg / (mg/µmol) sprintf("dose = %.1f mg of ADC = %.4f µmol total antibody", dose_mg, dose_umol) #> [1] "dose = 179.0 mg of ADC = 1.1933 µmol total antibody" # Build a 9-row IV dose event for one administration: each chain receives # the *total* antibody dose, and the model multiplies by f<chain> # (= F_DARi / SUM) inside model() to deposit only that chain's fraction. ChainsCentral <- c("dar1_central", "dar2_central", "dar3_central",                    "dar4_central", "dar5_central", "dar6_central",                    "dar7_central", "dar8_central", "central_nab")  build_q2w_dosing <- function(dose_umol, n_cycles, chains = ChainsCentral) {   ev <- rxode2::et()   for (cycle in seq_len(n_cycles)) {     t0 <- (cycle - 1) * 14     for (cmt in chains) {       ev <- rxode2::et(ev, amt = dose_umol, cmt = cmt, time = t0)     }   }   ev }  obs_grid <- c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75,               seq(1, 14 * 6, by = 0.25))   # 6 Q2W cycles, dense early ev_typ <- build_q2w_dosing(dose_umol, n_cycles = 6) |>   rxode2::et(obs_grid, cmt = "Cc")  mod    <- readModelDb("Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab") mod0   <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim    <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod0, events = ev_typ, atol = 1e-12, rtol = 1e-10) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalq', 'etalcladc', 'etalclnab', 'etalkdec1', 'etalcldm4', 'etalclmedm4', 'etalfrmedm4', 'etalfrdar1', 'etalfrdar2', 'etalfrdar3', 'etalfrdar4', 'etalfrdar5', 'etalfrdar6', 'etalfrdar7', 'etalfrnab' df     <- as.data.frame(sim)

what: Replicate Figure 3 — typical SAR408701, NAB, DM4 and MeDM4 profile
title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: Pouzin 2022 Figure 3 shows typical concentration-time profile four analytes 100 mg/m² Q2W dosing. Replicates Figure 3 Pouzin 2022 — typical SAR408701, NAB, DM4 MeDM4 profiles 100 mg/m^2 Q2W.
code: sim_long <- df |>   select(time, Cc, Cc_nab, Cc_dm4, Cc_medm4) |>   pivot_longer(c(Cc, Cc_nab, Cc_dm4, Cc_medm4), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "C_uM") |>   mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,                           Cc       = "SAR408701 (DAR>=1)",                           Cc_nab   = "NAB (DAR0)",                           Cc_dm4   = "DM4",                           Cc_medm4 = "MeDM4"),          analyte = factor(analyte,                           levels = c("SAR408701 (DAR>=1)", "NAB (DAR0)", "DM4", "MeDM4")))  ggplot(sim_long, aes(time, C_uM, colour = analyte)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 84, by = 14)) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Concentration (µM)", colour = NULL,        title = "Tusamitamab ravtansine — typical four-analyte profile",        caption = "Replicates Figure 3 of Pouzin 2022 (100 mg/m² Q2W, BSA = 1.79 m²).")

what: Replicate Figure 4 — typical average DAR profile
title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: Pouzin 2022 Figure 4 shows average DAR ranges 1 ≈ 3.3 across cycles decreases slightly across repeat doses (3.3 → 2.8) DAR0 DAR1 accumulate. Replicates Figure 4 Pouzin 2022 — typical average DAR profile across 6 Q2W cycles.
code: ggplot(df, aes(time, DARavg)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, colour = "#1f78b4") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 84, by = 14)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Average DAR (mol DM4 / mol total antibody)",        title = "Average DAR across 6 Q2W cycles",        caption = "Replicates Figure 4 of Pouzin 2022 (100 mg/m² Q2W, BSA = 1.79 m²).")

what: PKNCA validation — Cycle 1 NCA per analyte
title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: published Table 7 reports cycle-1 typical Cmax AUC_TAU four analytes. use PKNCA cycle-1 single-dose simulation (0–14 days) analyte, compare side--side published values. PKNCA cycle-1 NCA typical-value simulation (BSA = 1.79 m²).
code: ev_cycle1 <- build_q2w_dosing(dose_umol, n_cycles = 1) |>   rxode2::et(c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, seq(1, 14, by = 0.25)),              cmt = "Cc") sim_c1 <- as.data.frame(   rxode2::rxSolve(mod0, events = ev_cycle1, atol = 1e-12, rtol = 1e-10) ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalq', 'etalcladc', 'etalclnab', 'etalkdec1', 'etalcldm4', 'etalclmedm4', 'etalfrmedm4', 'etalfrdar1', 'etalfrdar2', 'etalfrdar3', 'etalfrdar4', 'etalfrdar5', 'etalfrdar6', 'etalfrdar7', 'etalfrnab'  # Total ADC mg dosed in one cycle (single subject) dose_one <- data.frame(id = 1L, time = 0, amt = dose_mg, treatment = "100 mg/m² Q2W") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_one, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = 14,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  run_nca <- function(label, conc) {   d <- data.frame(id = 1L, time = sim_c1$time, conc = conc, treatment = "100 mg/m² Q2W")   d <- d[is.finite(d$conc) & d$conc > 0, ]   conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(d, conc ~ time | treatment + id)   res      <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals))   s        <- as.data.frame(res$result)   out <- list(     analyte   = label,     cmax_uM   = s$PPORRES[s$PPTESTCD == "cmax"][1],     auclast   = s$PPORRES[s$PPTESTCD == "auclast"][1],     half_life = s$PPORRES[s$PPTESTCD == "half.life"][1]   )   out }  nca_rows <- list(   run_nca("SAR408701 (DAR>=1)", sim_c1$Cc),   run_nca("NAB (DAR0)",         sim_c1$Cc_nab),   run_nca("DM4",                sim_c1$Cc_dm4),   run_nca("MeDM4",              sim_c1$Cc_medm4) ) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.001) is not allowed nca_tbl <- do.call(rbind, lapply(nca_rows, as.data.frame)) knitr::kable(nca_tbl, digits = 5,              caption = "PKNCA cycle-1 NCA on the typical-value simulation (BSA = 1.79 m²).")

previous_headings: PKNCA validation — Cycle 1 NCA per analyte
title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: Side--side: simulated vs. published Pouzin 2022 Table 7. AUC % difference calculated µg·d/mL interpretation published value (see Errata). four Cmax values agree Table 7 within ≈ 1 %, four AUC values agree within ≈ 1 % published AUC unit interpreted µg·day/mL ADC mass equivalent rather µM·day stated column header (see next section).
code: # Pouzin 2022 Table 7: Cmax in µM (free-species molar concentration); # AUC values are reported in the same column header (lM.day) but their # magnitude only reconciles with the model when interpreted as # µg·d/mL of antibody-mass equivalent (see "Errata / unit ambiguity" # section below). For direct comparison we therefore tabulate both the # model AUC in µM·d AND its mass-equivalent AUC × MW_ADC / 1000. mw_adc_mg_per_umol <- 150  # 150 000 g/mol = 150 mg/µmol table7 <- tibble::tribble(   ~analyte,            ~pub_cmax_uM, ~pub_auc_per_table7,   "SAR408701 (DAR>=1)", 0.326,        250,   "NAB (DAR0)",         0.0249,       32.5,   "DM4",                0.00208,      1.00,   "MeDM4",              0.00610,      8.69 )  cmp <- nca_tbl |>   left_join(table7, by = "analyte") |>   mutate(     sim_auc_uM_d   = auclast,     sim_auc_ug_d_per_mL = auclast * mw_adc_mg_per_umol,     cmax_pct_diff  = 100 * (cmax_uM - pub_cmax_uM) / pub_cmax_uM,     auc_pct_diff_against_ug = 100 * (sim_auc_ug_d_per_mL - pub_auc_per_table7) / pub_auc_per_table7   ) |>   select(analyte,          sim_cmax_uM = cmax_uM, pub_cmax_uM, cmax_pct_diff,          sim_auc_uM_d, sim_auc_ug_d_per_mL, pub_auc_per_table7,          auc_pct_diff_against_ug) knitr::kable(cmp, digits = 4,              caption = "Side-by-side: simulated vs. published Pouzin 2022 Table 7. AUC % difference is calculated against the µg·d/mL interpretation of the published value (see Errata).")

what: Errata / unit ambiguity in Pouzin 2022 Table 7
title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: PDF Pouzin 2022 reports Table 7 AUC column (µM·day) (PDF text-extraction renders µ-glyph l, giving (lM.day)), cycle-1 values 250, 32.5, 1.00 8.69 SAR408701, NAB, DM4 MeDM4 respectively. Cmax reported (µM) values 0.326, 0.0249, 0.00208 0.00610. literal reading values internally inconsistent: 250 µM·day / 0.326 µM ≈ 767 days, far longer 14-day τ AUC_TAU defined. simulation vignette reproduces four published Cmax values within ≈ 1 % BSA = 1.79 m², confirming structural model parameter values correct. also reproduces four published AUC values within ≈ 1 % AUC values divided SAR408701 molecular weight (150 000 g/mol) — .e., published AUC numbers µg·day/mL ADC mass equivalent, µM·day. consistent Methods note “DM4, MeDM4 NAB concentrations converted ADC molar equivalent (normalization SAR408701 molecular mass)”: AUC values generated µg·day/mL ADC-mass-equivalent units, column header (lM.day) (= µM·day) published table typesetting error. corresponding erratum published 2026-04-28; operator/PR reviewer treat discrepancy -paper unit-label mistake use µg·day/mL interpretation comparing future implementations Table 7.

title: Pouzin_2022_tusamitamab
text: BSA = 1.79 m² assumed typical-value simulation; paper reports doses 100 mg/m² without specifying BSA used generate Table 7. choice inferred reproducing Cmax ≈ 1 % across four analytes. V_DM4 = V_MeDM4 = 1 L fixed, matching Methods note formation-limited kinetics prevent simultaneous identification V FR_MeDM4. CL_DM4 (240 L/day) CL_MeDM4 (0.256 L/day) therefore apparent clearances conditional V = 1 L. Dose splitting across nine compartments. rxode2 / nlmixr2 natively redistribute single dose row across several compartments via bioavailability; vignette’s build_q2w_dosing() helper therefore emits one cmt-tagged dose row per ADC chain, total antibody amount, model applies per-chain f<chain> = F_DAR_i / SUM normalisation internally. mathematically equivalent Monolix supplement’s iv(cmt = …, p = F_DARi) declarations. Population demographics modelling paper. Pouzin 2022 tabulate 254-patient demographics; population metadata records cohort structure (Table 1) study-level descriptors leaves age/weight/sex/race fields “tabulated”. companion Pouzin et al. 2022 CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol covariate paper (PMID 35191618) source subsequent covariate-aware refit. AUC unit Table 7 — see Errata section . Outputs included. model exposes Cc (SAR408701, sum DAR1–DAR8), Cc_nab (DAR0/NAB), Cc_dm4, Cc_medm4 derived DARavg. individual DAR-proportions (Online Resource 1 prop_ADCi, prop_NAB) computable per-chain compartment amounts Vc exported separate outputs packaging — paper excludes DAR8 proportion fitting due BLQ. checkModelConventions() deviations (justified). model uses 20 mechanism-specific compartment names — dar1_central … dar8_central, dar1_peripheral1 … dar8_peripheral1, central_nab, peripheral1_nab, central_dm4, central_medm4 — instead canonical central / peripheral1 set. follows precedent Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_mechanistic load-bearing: explicit per-DAR chain structural mechanism paper proposes. dosing unit (umol antibody) concentration unit (uM) reported dimensionally incompatible simple-string parser, fact molar — long parenthetical descriptor units$dosing confuses parser.

what: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: Simulate trastuzumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Quartino et al. (2019) patients metastatic breast cancer (MBC), early breast cancer (EBC), advanced gastric cancer (AGC), solid tumors. source analysis pooled 26,040 trastuzumab serum concentrations 1,582 patients across 18 phase -III trials using innovator trastuzumab product Herceptin. final model two-compartment model parallel linear Michaelis-Menten (nonlinear) elimination central compartment: dcentraldt=−kelcentral−VmaxCcKm+Cc−k12central+k21peripheral1,dperipheral1dt=k12central−k21peripheral1, \frac{d\,central}{dt} = -k_{el}\,central                         - \frac{V_{\max}\,C_c}{K_m + C_c}                         - k_{12}\,central + k_{21}\,peripheral_1, \qquad \frac{d\,peripheral_1}{dt} = k_{12}\,central - k_{21}\,peripheral_1, Cc=central/VcC_c = central/V_c. Concentrations carried inside ODEs mg/L (= ug/mL) dose mg volumes L; VmaxV_{\max} mg/day KmK_m mg/L elimination fluxes mg/day. maintenance dosing linear pathway dominates (total CL 0.173-0.337 L/day depending tumor type, per paper’s Table 2). nonlinear MM pathway visible low concentrations single dose washout tail. Article (open access via SpringerLink): https://doi.org/10.1007/s00280-018-3728-z PubMed (PMID 30467591): https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30467591/

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: model estimated 1,582 patients 26,040 trastuzumab serum concentrations pooled across 18 phase -III trials using innovator trastuzumab product Herceptin (biosimilar data). patients MBC (810/1582), followed EBC (391), AGC (274), non-small cell lung cancer solid tumors (107), healthy volunteers (6). Median age 53 years, median body weight 66 kg, 82.7% female, 83.4% non-Asian. (94.5%) ECOG performance status 0 1. Patients received trastuzumab either single agent (n = 1,188) combination anthracyclines, docetaxel, paclitaxel, cisplatin, chemotherapy, weekly (qw; 4 mg/kg loading + 2 mg/kg maintenance) every-3-weeks (q3w; 8 mg/kg loading + 6 mg/kg maintenance) schedule (Quartino 2019 Results “Patient population” Online Resource 6). information available programmatically:
code: readModelDb("Quartino_2019_trastuzumab")$meta$population

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: source paper publish per-subject baseline covariates (Online Resource 6 gives aggregate summaries ). cohort pragmatic approximation simulation, centred reference-subject predictions reproduce Table 1 typical values.
code: set.seed(2019)  # Virtual cohort per-arm constructor. id_offset keeps subject IDs disjoint # across the three treatment arms so rxSolve does not collapse duplicate # IDs into single (wrong) subjects. make_cohort <- function(n, tumor_type, id_offset = 0L) {   tibble::tibble(     id         = id_offset + seq_len(n),     tumor_type = tumor_type,     TUMTP_GC   = as.integer(tumor_type == "AGC"),     TUMTP_OTH  = as.integer(tumor_type == "Others"),     WT         = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n, 66, 13), 40), 120),          # kg; centred at 66 kg (reference)     AST        = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n, log(24), 0.5), 8), 200),    # IU/L     ALB        = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n, 4.0, 0.4), 2.5), 5.5),       # g/dL     LMET       = rbinom(n, 1, 0.30)                              # prevalence approx per MBC cohorts   ) }  cohort <- dplyr::bind_rows(   make_cohort(200, "BC",     id_offset =   0L),   make_cohort(200, "AGC",    id_offset = 200L),   make_cohort(100, "Others", id_offset = 400L) )  # Sanity: IDs are disjoint across cohorts stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(cohort$id))

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: two labelled trastuzumab regimens simulated: q3w: 8 mg/kg IV loading dose day 0, 6 mg/kg IV q3w (day 21, 42, …). qw: 4 mg/kg IV loading dose day 0, 2 mg/kg IV qw. Eighteen q3w cycles (loading + 17 maintenance doses; 378 days) simulated — far beyond 12 weeks Quartino 2019 reports 90% steady state — NCA can run final (17th maintenance) dosing interval.
code: # Dose rows are expanded explicitly (one row per dose event) rather than via # rxode2's ii/addl shortcut so that PKNCA can locate the final dose at # the exact steady-state interval boundary. n_cycles <- 18L   # loading + 17 maintenance doses => 18 cycles dose_times <- c(0, seq_len(n_cycles - 1L) * 21)  make_events_q3w <- function(pop) {   dose_rows <- dplyr::tibble(     id     = rep(pop$id, each = length(dose_times)),     time   = rep(dose_times, times = nrow(pop)),     cycle  = rep(seq_len(n_cycles), times = nrow(pop)),     wt     = rep(pop$WT, each = length(dose_times))   ) |>     dplyr::mutate(       amt  = ifelse(cycle == 1L, 8 * wt, 6 * wt),  # 8 mg/kg load, 6 mg/kg maint       cmt  = "central",       evid = 1L     ) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid)    obs_times <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 21, length.out = 30),    # dense first cycle                              seq(21, 21 * n_cycles, length.out = 150))))  # sparse follow-up   obs_rows <- dplyr::tibble(     id   = rep(pop$id, each = length(obs_times)),     time = rep(obs_times, times = nrow(pop)),     amt  = NA_real_,     cmt  = NA_character_,     evid = 0L   )    dplyr::bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  events_q3w <- make_events_q3w(cohort)  # Attach covariates (keyed by id) events_q3w_cov <- dplyr::left_join(   events_q3w,   cohort |> dplyr::select(id, tumor_type, TUMTP_GC, TUMTP_OTH, WT, AST, ALB, LMET),   by = "id" )

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Quartino_2019_trastuzumab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod,   events = events_q3w_cov,   keep   = c("tumor_type") ) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim$time <- as.numeric(sim$time)

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors > Simulate the q3w regimen
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: Figure 4 Quartino 2019 shows model-predicted concentration-time profiles typical patient BC (MBC/EBC) AGC 8 mg/kg loading + 6 mg/kg q3w regimen. replicate typical-subject curves tumor types using rxode2::zeroRe() suppress -subject variability. Replicates Figure 4 Quartino 2019 — typical-subject concentration-time profiles 66 kg patient MBC/EBC vs AGC 8 mg/kg + 6 mg/kg q3w, first 6 cycles.
code: mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  ref_subject <- function(tgc, tot, id = 1L) {   dplyr::bind_rows(     data.frame(id = id, time = 0,  amt = 8 * 66, cmt = "central",                evid = 1, ii = 0,  addl = 0),     data.frame(id = id, time = 21, amt = 6 * 66, cmt = "central",                evid = 1, ii = 21, addl = 17),     data.frame(id = id,                time = seq(0, 21 * 6, length.out = 200),                amt = 0, cmt = NA, evid = 0, ii = 0, addl = 0)   ) |>     dplyr::mutate(WT = 66, AST = 24, ALB = 4, LMET = 0,                   TUMTP_GC = tgc, TUMTP_OTH = tot,                   tumor_type = dplyr::case_when(tgc == 1 ~ "AGC",                                                 tot == 1 ~ "Others",                                                 TRUE     ~ "BC")) }  ref_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   ref_subject(0, 0, id = 1L),  # BC (MBC/EBC)   ref_subject(1, 0, id = 2L)   # AGC )  sim_ref <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ref_events,                            keep = c("tumor_type")) |> as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalkm' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega' sim_ref$time <- as.numeric(sim_ref$time)  ggplot(sim_ref |> dplyr::filter(time > 0),        aes(time, Cc, color = tumor_type, group = interaction(id, tumor_type))) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c(BC = "steelblue", AGC = "firebrick",                                 Others = "darkgreen"),                      name = "Tumor type") +   labs(x = "Time (days)",        y = "Trastuzumab Cc (ug/mL)",        title = "Typical-subject trastuzumab profile, 8 mg/kg + 6 mg/kg q3w",        subtitle = "Reference subject: 66 kg, AST 24 IU/L, ALB 4 g/dL, no liver metastases",        caption = "Replicates Figure 4 of Quartino 2019 (typical-subject Cc vs time by tumor type).") +   theme_bw()

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors > Simulate the q3w regimen
what: VPC-style summary across the virtual cohort
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: VPC-style summary (median 5-95% prediction interval) stratified tumor type. Patterned Quartino 2019 Figure 1.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_bin = cut(time, breaks = seq(0, max(time), by = 3.5),                                include.lowest = TRUE, labels = FALSE)) |>   dplyr::group_by(tumor_type, time_bin) |>   dplyr::summarise(time   = mean(time),                    median = median(Cc),                    lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05),                    hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95),                    .groups = "drop")  ggplot(vpc, aes(time)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi, fill = tumor_type), alpha = 0.20) +   geom_line(aes(y = median, color = tumor_type), linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (days)",        y = "Trastuzumab Cc (ug/mL)",        title = "Simulated trastuzumab concentration-time profiles by tumor type",        subtitle = "8 mg/kg loading + 6 mg/kg q3w; 500 virtual patients",        caption = "Median and 5-95% prediction interval; patterned after Quartino 2019 Figure 1.") +   theme_bw()

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: Run PKNCA final (18th) dosing interval approximation steady state (well beyond 12-week 90%-SS reported Results / Table 2). Results grouped tumor type per-group NCA values can compared paper’s Table 2. Steady-state NCA (q3w, interval 17) tumor type: simulated median (5-95% PI).
code: tau        <- 21 start_ss   <- 21 * 17                       # final q3w dose end_ss     <- start_ss + tau  sim_ss <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= start_ss, time <= end_ss, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::transmute(id, time, Cc, tumor_type)  dose_ss <- events_q3w_cov |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == start_ss) |>   dplyr::transmute(id, time, amt, tumor_type)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_ss, Cc ~ time | tumor_type + id,                              concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_ss, amt ~ time | tumor_type + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = start_ss,   end     = end_ss,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_data   <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_result <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (1.83221) is not allowed title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: Simulated (virtual cohort) vs. Quartino 2019 Table 2 medians. Cmax AUCss values track paper’s Table 2 medians within roughly 5-20%; Cmin values AGC run higher paper’s median remain within paper’s 95% prediction interval. residual offset reflects three modelling choices rather parameter discrepancy: MM (nonlinear) elimination pathway’s contribution trough-exposure statistics highly asymmetric population: subjects lower typical CL drop regime Cc approaches Km = 8.92 ug/mL MM term accelerates elimination, pulling population-median Cmin typical-subject Cmin. published 95% PI Table 2 incorporates large Km IIV (139% CV, 44% shrinkage) load-bearing low-concentration tail; virtual-cohort approximation includes variability exact covariate-distribution imbalance source analysis dataset. LMET prevalence simulation cohort (30% assumption) match per-tumor-type prevalence Quartino 2019 (published per subgroup).
code: published <- tibble::tribble(   ~tumor_type, ~metric,  ~paper_median, ~paper_q05, ~paper_q95,   "BC",  "Cmax (ug/mL)",       182,        126,        260,   "BC",  "Cmin (ug/mL)",       45.8,       4.56,       85.5,   "BC",  "AUCss (ug*day/mL)",  1790,       727,        2760,   "AGC", "Cmax (ug/mL)",       119,        77.9,       173,   "AGC", "Cmin (ug/mL)",       25.2,       6.37,       52.7,   "AGC", "AUCss (ug*day/mL)",  1120,       596,        1840 )  sim_wide <- nca_med |>   dplyr::filter(tumor_type %in% c("BC", "AGC")) |>   dplyr::mutate(metric = dplyr::case_when(     PPTESTCD == "cmax"    ~ "Cmax (ug/mL)",     PPTESTCD == "cmin"    ~ "Cmin (ug/mL)",     PPTESTCD == "auclast" ~ "AUCss (ug*day/mL)"   )) |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(metric)) |>   dplyr::select(tumor_type, metric, sim_median = median,                 sim_q05 = q05, sim_q95 = q95)  comparison <- dplyr::left_join(published, sim_wide, by = c("tumor_type", "metric")) |>   dplyr::mutate(pct_diff = 100 * (sim_median - paper_median) / paper_median)  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 1,              caption = "Simulated (virtual cohort) vs. Quartino 2019 Table 2 medians.")

title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: “Assessment impact identified covariates PK exposure” section Quartino 2019 reports , compared 66 kg BC patient, typical linear CL decreases 27% 46 kg patient increases 43% 98 kg patient. AGC / BC Cmin,ss difference reported 30.5% lower AGC. code verifies sensitivity numbers packaged coefficients. Typical linear CL sensitivities reproduced packaged parameters. BC reference CL (0.127), AGC reference CL (0.176), Others reference CL (0.148) match Table 1 three significant digits. WT = 46 kg WT = 98 kg values correspond 27% decrease 43% increase linear CL respectively (numbers quoted paper’s sensitivity narrative).
code: q <- list(   cl_bc   = 0.127, cl_agc = 0.176, cl_oth = 0.148,   e_wt_cl = 0.967, e_ast_cl = 0.205, e_alb_cl = -0.998, e_lmet_cl = 0.152,   vc_bc = 2.62, vc_agc = 3.63 )  cl_typ <- function(tumor = "BC", WT = 66, AST = 24, ALB = 4, LMET = 0) {   cl0 <- switch(tumor, BC = q$cl_bc, AGC = q$cl_agc, Others = q$cl_oth)   cl0 *     (WT / 66)^q$e_wt_cl *     (AST / 24)^q$e_ast_cl *     (ALB / 4)^q$e_alb_cl *     exp(q$e_lmet_cl * LMET) }  sensitivity <- tibble::tribble(   ~Scenario,                               ~`Simulated CL (L/day)`, ~`Paper target`,   "BC, reference (66 kg)",                 cl_typ(),                "0.127",   "BC, WT = 46 kg",                        cl_typ(WT = 46),         "27% decrease",   "BC, WT = 98 kg",                        cl_typ(WT = 98),         "43% increase",   "BC, SGOT = 50 IU/L",                    cl_typ(AST = 50),        "elevated (CL increases with SGOT)",   "BC, ALB = 3 g/dL",                      cl_typ(ALB = 3),         "elevated (CL scales as ~1/ALB)",   "BC, LMET = 1",                          cl_typ(LMET = 1),        "+16% (exp(0.152))",   "AGC, reference",                        cl_typ(tumor = "AGC"),   "0.176",   "Others, reference",                     cl_typ(tumor = "Others"),"0.148" ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     `Ratio to BC reference` = round(`Simulated CL (L/day)` / cl_typ(), 3)   )  knitr::kable(sensitivity, digits = 3,              caption = "Typical linear CL sensitivities reproduced from the packaged parameters.")

title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: Quartino 2019 publish per-subject PK covariate data; Online Resource 6 gives aggregate baseline demographics . virtual cohort approximates source cohort follows: Body weight ~ Normal(66, 13) kg clipped 40-120 kg. Median matches reference 66 kg; spread chosen 5th-95th percentile range approximately brackets Table 1 reference subject. AST (SGOT) ~ log-Normal(log 24, 0.5) IU/L clipped 8-200. Median matches reference 24 IU/L. ALB ~ Normal(4.0, 0.4) g/dL clipped 2.5-5.5. Centred reference. LMET ~ Bernoulli(0.30). paper publish per-tumor-type liver-metastasis prevalence; 30% pragmatic value consistent published prevalence MBC literature. Tumor-type indicators: three cohorts 200/200/100 simulated; stratified sub-histology within “Others” group. Sex race covariates final model (primary tumor type, baseline WT, AST, ALB, LMET exhausted clinically relevant set), modelled . IIV Vp Km: retained per Table 1 despite 22.9% / 44.0% shrinkage reported Results. load-bearing low- concentration VPC tail. Residual-error interpretation: Table 1 reports sigma1 = 19.7% sigma2 = 1.38 ug/mL, footnote d confirming SDs (RSE column relative sigma^2, sigma). Stored propSd = 0.197 + addSd = 1.38 combined additive+proportional nlmixr2 convention. Time units: time days throughout. Quartino 2019 already reports CL L/day Vmax mg/day, time-unit conversion needed. Concentration units: mg/L (= ug/mL) inside ODE. Km = 8.92 mg/L equivalent 8.92 ug/mL. Observation-grid simplification build speed. simulation grid uses 30 points first 21-day cycle 150 points remaining 17 cycles (80 + 500 = 580), VPC subplot grid uses 200 points 6 cycles (700). published figures reproduced faithfully; reduction cuts pkgdown build time ~12 min ~2 min visible change plot. Errata search: author correction erratum located Cancer Chemotherapy Pharmacology landing page, PubMed, Google Scholar DOI 10.1007/s00280-018-3728-z time extraction. reference field amended later correction surfaces. Shed-antigen ECD-HER2 (SHED) mentioned Quartino 2019 Results exploratory (post-hoc) analysis , part final model; intentionally implemented .

previous_headings: Trastuzumab (Herceptin) population PK in solid tumors
title: Quartino_2019_trastuzumab
text: Structure: two-compartment PK model parallel linear Michaelis-Menten nonlinear elimination central compartment. IV dosing directly central. Typical linear CL: 0.127 L/day (MBC/EBC/HV), 0.176 L/day (AGC), 0.148 L/day (Others); Vc 2.62 L (non-AGC) 3.63 L (AGC); Q 0.544 L/day; Vp 2.97 L. Vmax 8.81 mg/day, Km 8.92 mg/L. Total CL range steady state (per Table 2): 0.173-0.283 L/day BC 0.189-0.337 L/day AGC; linear CL dominates maintenance concentrations MM pathway becomes visible low concentrations (e.g., washout tail early dosing history). Clinically relevant covariates: baseline WT AST increase linear CL; ALB decreases linear CL approximately proportionally (exponent -0.998); LMET (liver metastases) increases linear CL ~16%; AGC patients ~39% higher typical linear CL ~39% higher typical Vc BC patients. Half-life considerations: effective elimination half-life maintenance dosing range ~15-20 days (linear-pathway dominated), shortening low-concentration washout regime. Quartino 2019 reports 7-month (~188 day) washout < 1 ug/mL 95% BC patients following 12 q3w cycles.

title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: Replicates key figures parameter reference values Robbie et al. (2012), “Population Pharmacokinetics Palivizumab, Humanized Anti-Respiratory Syncytial Virus Monoclonal Antibody, Adults Children” (Antimicrob Agents Chemother 56:4927-4936; erratum AAC 56:5431 adds Anderson/Allegaert/Holford 2006 maturation-model reference). Parameter reference values: reproduce Table 2 (adult, 70 kg, 40-week PAGE) Table 3 (pediatric reference: 4.5 kg, 12.3-month PAGE). Figure 3 (corrected per erratum): simulated concentration-time profiles 5 monthly 15 mg/kg IM doses vs. abbreviated 3 monthly dose regimen. Figure 5 (corrected per erratum): group median concentration-time profiles stratified gestational age + postnatal age cohorts. PKNCA exposure summary: steady-state Cmax / Cmin / AUC0-tau label 5-monthly regimen. original study data publicly available, figures show simulated prediction intervals virtual infant populations whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics (Robbie 2012 Table 1). Body weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0-24 months).

title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: Baseline demographics reproduced Robbie 2012 Table 1: PK-analysis dataset totals 1,800 subjects across 22 clinical studies (9 adult: 7 healthy-volunteer + 2 transplant; 13 pediatric); Table 1 reports 1,883 enrolled. Adult cohort dosed IV IM across studies; pediatric cohort dosed IM. Dose range 3-30 mg/kg; label regimen 5 monthly doses 15 mg/kg IM.

title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0-24 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). Formula: weight (kg) = M * (1 + L * S * z)^(1/L).

what: Load model and confirm reference parameter values
title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: Sanity-check reference values paper’s Tables 2 3: single typical subject (-subject variability, dose) yield published per-subject CL, Vc, Vp, Q reference. Reference parameter reproduction Robbie 2012 Tables 2 3. Small deviations adult reference (~1%) arise adult reference Table 2 reported without maturation multiplier; model output evaluates full CL expression typical-adult PAGE.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Robbie_2012_palivizumab") mod0 <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  ref_ev <- function(WT, PAGE) {   data.frame(     ID = 1, TIME = c(0, 1e-6),     AMT = c(1, 0),     EVID = c(1, 0),     CMT = c("depot", "central"),     DV = NA,     WT = WT, PAGE = PAGE,     RACE_BLACK = 0, RACE_HISPANIC = 0, RACE_ASIAN = 0, RACE_OTHER = 0,     CLD_PREM = 0, ADA_TITER = 0   ) }  adult <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod0, events = ref_ev(70, 12 * 30 + 9))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' ped   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod0, events = ref_ev(4.5, 12.3))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc'  ref_table <- data.frame(   parameter = c("CL (mL/day)", "Vc (mL)", "Vp (mL)", "Q (mL/day)"),   paper_adult  = c(198, 4090, 2230, 879),   model_adult  = round(c(adult$cl[1] * 1000, adult$vc[1] * 1000,                           adult$vp[1] * 1000, adult$q[1] * 1000), 1),   paper_pediatric = c(11.0, 263, 143, 112),   model_pediatric = round(c(ped$cl[1] * 1000, ped$vc[1] * 1000,                              ped$vp[1] * 1000, ped$q[1] * 1000), 1) ) knitr::kable(ref_table,   caption = "Reference parameter reproduction against Robbie 2012 Tables 2 and 3.")

what: Helper: build a virtual pediatric cohort
title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
code: make_cohort <- function(n, ga_range, pna0_range, max_day,                         cld_prob = 0, ada_titer_fn = function(n) rep(0, n),                         obs_days = seq(0, max_day, by = 7),                         race_probs = c(White = 0.54, Black = 0.20,                                        Hispanic = 0.18, Asian = 0.02, Other = 0.06),                         n_doses = 5, dose_mg_per_kg = 15,                         id_offset = 0L) {   # id_offset lets callers stack multiple cohorts with non-overlapping IDs.   # rxSolve treats ID as the subject key, so two rows with ID=1 and   # different parameters would be merged into a single (wrong) subject.   GA <- runif(n, ga_range[1], ga_range[2])   PNA_0 <- runif(n, pna0_range[1], pna0_range[2])   wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n, 0, 1)))   WT_0 <- who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   CLD_PREM <- as.integer(runif(n) < cld_prob)   ADA_T <- ada_titer_fn(n)   race <- sample(names(race_probs), n, replace = TRUE, prob = race_probs)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = seq_len(n) + id_offset, GA, PNA_0, wt_z, WT_0,     RACE_BLACK    = as.integer(race == "Black"),     RACE_HISPANIC = as.integer(race == "Hispanic"),     RACE_ASIAN    = as.integer(race == "Asian"),     RACE_OTHER    = as.integer(race == "Other"),     CLD_PREM = CLD_PREM, ADA_TITER = ADA_T   )    # Dose records: n_doses at 30-day intervals, 15 mg/kg of current weight   dose_times <- (0:(n_doses - 1)) * 30   d_dose <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(dose_times)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(dose_times, times = n),       EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA,       PNA = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z),       AMT = dose_mg_per_kg * WT     )    # Observation records: weekly sampling   d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(obs_days)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_days, times = n),       EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA, AMT = 0,       PNA = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z)     )    bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PAGE,       RACE_BLACK, RACE_HISPANIC, RACE_ASIAN, RACE_OTHER,       CLD_PREM, ADA_TITER, WT_0, GA, PNA_0) }

title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: Replicates erratum-corrected Figure 3: simulated concentration-time profiles abbreviated 3 monthly 15 mg/kg IM dose regimen compared label 5 monthly doses, typical pediatric cohort.
code: set.seed(22802243) # PMID seed n_f3 <- 300 obs_days_f3 <- seq(0, 240, by = 2)  ev_5 <- make_cohort(n_f3, ga_range = c(26, 41), pna0_range = c(0, 6),                     max_day = 240, cld_prob = 0.2,                     obs_days = obs_days_f3, n_doses = 5,                     id_offset = 0L) |>   mutate(regimen = "5 monthly doses (label)") ev_3 <- make_cohort(n_f3, ga_range = c(26, 41), pna0_range = c(0, 6),                     max_day = 240, cld_prob = 0.2,                     obs_days = obs_days_f3, n_doses = 3,                     id_offset = n_f3) |>   mutate(regimen = "3 monthly doses (abbreviated)")  sim_f3 <- bind_rows(ev_5, ev_3)  out_f3 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = sim_f3, keep = "regimen") |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  d_f3 <- out_f3 |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) ggplot(d_f3, aes(x = time, y = Q50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x = 235, y = 42, hjust = 1, vjust = 0,            label = "40 ug/mL target", size = 3, colour = "grey40") +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 240, by = 30)) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Palivizumab serum concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Figure 3 (erratum-corrected) - 3 vs 5 monthly 15 mg/kg IM doses",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated median (line) and 90% prediction interval (shaded) in ",       n_f3, " virtual infants per regimen.\n",       "Dashed line: 40 ug/mL nominal target."     ),     colour = NULL, fill = NULL   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: Figure 5 (erratum-corrected) - profiles stratified by postnatal age
title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: erratum-corrected legend specifies “Group median concentration-time profiles palivizumab 15 mg/kg stratified gestational age postnatal age cohorts, 0 6 months (), 7 12 months (B), 13 24 months (C).” Replicate three cohorts differing postnatal age first dose.
code: set.seed(4927) # first journal page cohorts <- list(   list(label = "A. Postnatal 0 to 6 mo",   pna = c(0,  6)),   list(label = "B. Postnatal 7 to 12 mo",  pna = c(7, 12)),   list(label = "C. Postnatal 13 to 24 mo", pna = c(13, 24)) )  obs_days_f5 <- seq(0, 180, by = 2) n_per_cohort <- 200  sim_f5 <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_along(cohorts), function(i) {   co <- cohorts[[i]]   # Each cohort's IDs are offset by (i-1)*n_per_cohort so bind_rows does   # not produce multiple rows with the same ID (which would collapse   # distinct subjects into one inside rxSolve).   make_cohort(n_per_cohort, ga_range = c(26, 41), pna0_range = co$pna,               max_day = 180, cld_prob = 0.2,               obs_days = obs_days_f5, n_doses = 5,               id_offset = (i - 1L) * n_per_cohort) |>     mutate(cohort = co$label) }))  # `keep = "cohort"` carries the source-table `cohort` column through # rxSolve's output without needing a post-hoc left_join — avoids the # duplicate-ID merge trap altogether. out_f5 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = sim_f5, keep = "cohort") |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  d_f5 <- out_f5 |>   group_by(cohort, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) ggplot(d_f5, aes(x = time, y = Q50, colour = cohort, fill = cohort)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 180, by = 30)) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Palivizumab serum concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Figure 5 (erratum-corrected) - Profiles by postnatal age cohort at first dose",     caption = "Simulated median (line) and 90% prediction interval (shaded). N = 200 per cohort, 5 monthly 15 mg/kg IM doses.",     colour = NULL, fill = NULL   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: PKNCA exposure summary for the 5-monthly regimen
title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: Compute steady-state Cmax, Cmin, AUC0-tau last dosing interval Figure 3 5-dose cohort, using PKNCA (inline trapezoidal integration) convention validation vignettes. grouping variable keeps adult vs pediatric separation possible future multi-group comparisons; carry single treatment level. Steady-state NCA (final dosing interval) 5-monthly 15 mg/kg IM regimen virtual pediatric infants. paper tabulate individual pediatric NCA results, table serves forward-prediction steady-state exposure label regimen; reviewers can compare qualitatively 40 ug/mL target cited paper’s discussion.
code: tau <- 30 last_dose_time <- 4 * tau  conc_df <- out_f3 |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(regimen == "5 monthly doses (label)", !is.na(Cc)) |>   mutate(treatment = "pediatric_5dose_15mpk") |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- ev_5 |>   filter(EVID == 1) |>   mutate(treatment = "pediatric_5dose_15mpk") |>   select(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(conc_df, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = last_dose_time,   end     = last_dose_time + tau,   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) nca_df <- as.data.frame(res$result)  nca_summary <- nca_df |>   group_by(PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     median = round(median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), 2),     q05    = round(quantile(PPORRES, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), 2),     q95    = round(quantile(PPORRES, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE), 2),     .groups = "drop"   ) knitr::kable(nca_summary,   caption = "Steady-state NCA (final dosing interval) of the 5-monthly 15 mg/kg IM regimen in virtual pediatric infants.")

title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: Erratum eq. 10 numerical correction (6,900 -> 16,900). 2012 erratum corrects “6,900” “16,900” equation 10. Equation 10 NONMEM $PRIOR matrix adult fit used inform pediatric fit (THETA prior means diagonal OMEGA prior covariance); 16,900 prior variance adult Vp (consistent adult fit’s reported %RSE 5.39% Vp = 2,410 mL: SE = 130 mL, SE^2 = 16,900). model file uses post-fit final parameter estimates Table 2, priors equation 10, erratum’s numerical correction propagate ini() value. erratum corrections (renaming equation 4 “1a”, adding Anderson/Allegaert/Holford 2006 reference 1a, Figure 3 replacement, Figure 5 legend rewording) reflected model file’s reference field source trace . Maturation parameterization. paper’s eq. 4 / 1a reads TVCL = theta_CL * (1 - beta * exp(...)) reported beta = 0.411 TCL = 62.3 months reproduce pediatric Table 3 reference (CL = 11.0 mL/day 4.5 kg, 12.3-mo PAGE) abstract’s 10.2 11.9 mL/day PAGE 7-18 month range equation taken (1 - (1-beta) * exp(...)), .e., beta = fraction adult CL term = 0.411. Anderson / Allegaert / Holford (2006) parameterization added 2012 erratum reference 1a. model file uses form documents choice ini() block model() comments. ADA titer step function. Robbie 2012 modelled ADA titer four separate multiplicative bins (10, 20, 40, >=80) rather continuous trend. implementation derives bin indicators inside model() continuous canonical ADA_TITER column users supply single column. Values 0 (ADA-negative) map reference theta = 1. Hispanic race Vc. Hispanic effect Vc (1.06, CI included unity) reported alongside CL race effects; included structural fidelity. Residual variance. paper reports sigma^2_prop = 0.0639 proportional-error CV 23.4%. model uses propSd = sqrt(0.0639) = 0.2528 (25.3%); 23.4% figure appears derived summary rather fundamental estimate. Race weight virtual cohort. Race distribution matches Robbie 2012 Table 1 pediatric proportions (54/20/18/2/6 White/Black/ Hispanic/Asian/); weight uses combined-sex LMS infant’s z-score held constant. Population metadata corrected revisit. original extraction ran PMC abstract stub contain Methods text. pediatric n_pediatric count 1,767 (derived Table 1 enrolled total 1,883 minus 116 adults) replaced paper’s PK-analysis dataset count 1,684 (“932 boys 752 girls”); n_subjects realigned 1,883 enrolled 1,800 PK dataset; sex_female_pct denominator corrected 1,660 1,684 (giving 44.7% rather 45.3% female). Original observed data available. figures show simulated prediction intervals .

title: Robbie_2012_palivizumab
text: Robbie GJ, Zhao L, Mondick J, Losonsky G, Roskos LK. Population Pharmacokinetics Palivizumab, Humanized Anti-Respiratory Syncytial Virus Monoclonal Antibody, Adults Children. title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: Citation: Rosario M, Dirks NL, Gastonguay MR, Fasanmade AA, Wyant T, Parikh , Sandborn WJ, Feagan BG, Reinisch W, Fox . Population pharmacokinetics-pharmacodynamics vedolizumab patients ulcerative colitis Crohn’s disease. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2015;42(2):188-202. doi:10.1111/apt.13243 (PMID 25996351). corrigendum (doi:10.1111/apt.15571; PMC6885991) corrects unit typo text (ng/mL -> ug/mL) change parameter value. Description: Two-compartment population PK model vedolizumab (humanised anti-alpha4-beta7 integrin IgG1 monoclonal antibody) parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination adults moderately--severely active ulcerative colitis Crohn’s disease healthy volunteers (Rosario 2015). Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/apt.13243 Corrigendum: https://doi.org/10.1111/apt.15571 — text-unit-typo fix (ng/mL → µg/mL) parameter-value changes.

title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: model developed 2700 evaluable PK subjects pooled across six vedolizumab clinical studies: C13009 (phase 1, healthy volunteers, single IV doses 0.2–10 mg/kg), C13002 (phase 2, UC, multiple IV 2.0/6.0/10.0 mg/kg), GEMINI 1 (phase 3, UC, 300 mg Q4W/Q8W maintenance), GEMINI 2 GEMINI 3 (phase 3, CD, 300 mg Q4W/Q8W maintenance), C13004 (phase 2, CD). Baseline demographics (Rosario 2015 Table 1 / Appendix S1 Table S1): age 18–78 years (median 39 y IBD cohort), body weight 29.4–156 kg (median 70 kg), 48% female, predominantly White (87%), 51% CD / 43% UC / 6% healthy volunteers, 50% prior anti-TNF exposure, 4% ADA- positive time analysis. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Rosario_2015_vedolizumab")$population.

title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Rosario_2015_vedolizumab.R. table collects one place. Reference patient (Rosario 2015 Table 2 footnote / Figure 5 caption): 70 kg, 40 years old, albumin 4 g/dL, faecal calprotectin 700 mg/kg, CDAI 300 (CD), partial Mayo 6 (UC), UC diagnosis Vc, concomitant therapy, ADA-negative, TNF-naive.

title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual populations whose covariate distributions approximate published phase 3 demographics (Rosario 2015 Table 1 Appendix S1 Table S1).
code: make_cohort <- function(n,                         n_doses = 8,                         dosing_interval_days = 56,   # Q8W maintenance                         obs_days_per_dose = seq(0, 56, by = 7),                         amt_mg = 300,                         ibd_cd_prob = 0.55,          # 55% CD, 45% UC (Table 1)                         seed = 13243) {   set.seed(seed)    # Baseline covariates approximating Rosario 2015 Table 1   WT      <- pmax(30, pmin(160, rlnorm(n, log(70), 0.22)))   AGE     <- pmax(18, pmin(80,  rnorm(n, 39, 13)))   ALB     <- pmax(2.0, pmin(5.5, rnorm(n, 4.0, 0.4)))        # g/dL   CALPRO  <- pmax(10,  exp(rnorm(n, log(700), 1.1)))         # mg/kg   IBD_CD  <- rbinom(n, 1, ibd_cd_prob)   # Disease-activity scores: supply CDAI for CD, PMAYO for UC; the gating in   # the model sets the unused score's exponent to zero, so the supplied value   # is irrelevant for the other cohort. We set them to the reference value to   # make the columns self-consistent.   CDAI    <- ifelse(IBD_CD == 1,                     pmax(150, pmin(600, rnorm(n, 300, 75))),                     300)   PMAYO   <- ifelse(IBD_CD == 0,                     pmax(2, pmin(9, round(rnorm(n, 6, 1.2)))),                     6)   PRIOR_TNF    <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.50)   ADA_POS      <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.04)   CONMED_AZA   <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.15)   CONMED_MP    <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.05)   CONMED_MTX   <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.05)   CONMED_AMINO <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.45)    dose_times <- seq(0, (n_doses - 1) * dosing_interval_days,                     by = dosing_interval_days)    pop <- data.frame(     ID  = seq_len(n),     WT, AGE, ALB, CALPRO, CDAI, PMAYO, IBD_CD,     PRIOR_TNF, ADA_POS, CONMED_AZA, CONMED_MP, CONMED_MTX, CONMED_AMINO   )    # IV infusion records: rate implements a 30-min infusion (0.0208 day).   d_dose <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(dose_times)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(dose_times, times = n),       AMT  = amt_mg,       EVID = 1,       CMT  = "central",       RATE = amt_mg / (30 / (60 * 24)),       DV   = NA_real_     )    # Observation records: per-dose relative sample times, across all doses   obs_grid <- as.vector(outer(obs_days_per_dose, dose_times, "+"))   obs_grid <- sort(unique(obs_grid))    d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(obs_grid)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_grid, times = n),       AMT  = 0,       EVID = 0,       CMT  = "central",       RATE = 0,       DV   = NA_real_     )    bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, RATE, DV,            WT, AGE, ALB, CALPRO, CDAI, PMAYO, IBD_CD,            PRIOR_TNF, ADA_POS, CONMED_AZA, CONMED_MP, CONMED_MTX, CONMED_AMINO) } mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Rosario_2015_vedolizumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: Phase 3 maintenance dosing: 300 mg Q8W IV × 8 doses, weekly sampling 56-week treatment period.
code: events_vpc <- make_cohort(n = 300) sim_vpc <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_vpc) |> as.data.frame()

what: Figure 4 analogue: concentration–time VPC (phase 3 maintenance, 300 mg Q8W)
title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
code: d_vpc <- sim_vpc |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(Cc, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(Cc, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(d_vpc, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q10), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q90), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 8 * 56, by = 56),                      labels = seq(0, 8 * 56, by = 56) / 7) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = expression("Vedolizumab concentration (" * mu * "g/mL)"),     title = "Figure 4 analogue — simulated concentration VPC",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated 10th/50th/90th percentiles, N = 300 virtual subjects, ",       "300 mg IV Q8W × 8 doses.\n",       "Replicates the shape of Figure 4 (phase 3 maintenance) of Rosario 2015."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 5 analogue: covariate effects on CLL
title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: Figure 5 Rosario 2015 covariate forest plot showing multiplicative effect steady-state linear clearance covariate moves reference 5th/95th percentile (continuous) 0 → 1 (categorical). compute equivalent ratios model’s coefficients.
code: ini <- mod$theta get <- function(nm) unname(ini[nm])  # Continuous covariate sensitivity at 5th / 95th population percentiles. # Percentiles approximated from the virtual cohort above. forest_cont <- tribble(   ~covariate,            ~low,   ~ref,   ~high,  ~exponent,   "Weight (kg)",         49,     70,     100,    get("e_wt_cl"),   "Albumin (g/dL)",      3.2,    4.0,    4.8,    get("e_alb_cl"),   "Calprotectin (mg/kg)", 50,    700,    5000,   get("e_calpro_cl"),   "Age (years)",         22,     40,     68,     get("e_age_cl"),   "CDAI (CD only)",      150,    300,    500,    get("e_cdai_cl"),   "Partial Mayo (UC only)", 3,   6,      9,      get("e_pmayo_cl") ) |>   mutate(     ratio_low  = (low  / ref)^exponent,     ratio_high = (high / ref)^exponent   )  forest_cat <- tribble(   ~covariate,                       ~ratio_low, ~ratio_high,   "Prior TNF-α (vs naive)",         1,           get("e_priortnf_cl"),   "ADA-positive (vs negative)",     1,           get("e_ada_cl"),   "Concomitant AZA",                1,           get("e_conmed_aza_cl"),   "Concomitant 6-MP",               1,           get("e_conmed_mp_cl"),   "Concomitant MTX",                1,           get("e_conmed_mtx_cl"),   "Concomitant aminosalicylate",    1,           get("e_conmed_amino_cl"),   "Diagnosis CD (vs UC)",           1,           exp(get("lcl_cd") - get("lcl")) )  forest_all <- bind_rows(   forest_cont |> select(covariate, ratio_low, ratio_high),   forest_cat ) |>   mutate(covariate = factor(covariate, levels = rev(covariate)))  ggplot(forest_all, aes(y = covariate)) +   geom_segment(aes(x = ratio_low, xend = ratio_high, yend = covariate),                colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 1.0) +   geom_point(aes(x = ratio_low),  colour = "#4682b4", size = 2) +   geom_point(aes(x = ratio_high), colour = "#4682b4", size = 2) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   scale_x_log10(breaks = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 3.0)) +   labs(     x = "Multiplicative effect on linear clearance (CLL)",     y = NULL,     title = "Figure 5 analogue — covariate effects on CLL",     caption = paste0(       "Ratios computed at the 5th / 95th covariate percentiles vs the ",       "reference.\n",       "Reference: 70 kg, 40 y, albumin 4 g/dL, calprotectin 700 mg/kg, UC, ",       "TNF-naive, ADA-negative, no concomitant medication."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: Compute NCA simulated typical-patient profile single 300 mg IV dose steady state (dose 8 Q8W regimen). Rosario 2015 Table 4 reports following typical exposure metrics 300 mg Q8W phase 3 maintenance regimen: Steady-state trough (week 54, pre-dose): ~9 µg/mL UC CD. Steady-state Cmax (week 54, end--infusion): ~98 µg/mL UC CD. Terminal half-life: ~25 days (vedolizumab prescribing information derived model). Single-dose NCA typical reference-patient profile. Steady-state NCA 8th (final) dosing interval.
code: events_single <- make_cohort(   n = 1, n_doses = 1,   obs_days_per_dose = c(0.02, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 7, 14, 21, 28,                         42, 56, 70, 84, 112, 140, 168) ) |>   mutate(     WT = 70, AGE = 40, ALB = 4, CALPRO = 700,     CDAI = 300, PMAYO = 6, IBD_CD = 0,     PRIOR_TNF = 0, ADA_POS = 0,     CONMED_AZA = 0, CONMED_MP = 0, CONMED_MTX = 0, CONMED_AMINO = 0   )  mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  sim_single <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events_single) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(id = 1L, treatment = "single_300mg") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvmax'  sim_nca_single <- sim_single |>   filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_single <- events_single |>   filter(EVID == 1) |>   transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = "single_300mg")  conc_single <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_single, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_single_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_single, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_single <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_single <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_single, dose_single_obj, intervals = intervals_single) ) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement #> (0.02) is not allowed knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_single$result),              caption = "Single-dose NCA on the typical reference-patient profile.") # Phase 3 maintenance steady state: 300 mg Q8W, extract the final (8th) # dosing interval and scale time to 0 at that dose. events_ss_base <- make_cohort(   n = 1, n_doses = 8,   obs_days_per_dose = c(0, 0.02, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 7, 14, 21, 28,                         35, 42, 49, 56),   dosing_interval_days = 56 ) |>   mutate(     WT = 70, AGE = 40, ALB = 4, CALPRO = 700,     CDAI = 300, PMAYO = 6, IBD_CD = 0,     PRIOR_TNF = 0, ADA_POS = 0,     CONMED_AZA = 0, CONMED_MP = 0, CONMED_MTX = 0, CONMED_AMINO = 0   )  final_dose_day <- 7 * 56   # 392 days, start of the 8th dose extra_obs <- data.frame(   ID = 1L,   TIME = final_dose_day + c(70, 84, 112, 140, 168),   AMT = 0, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", RATE = 0, DV = NA_real_,   WT = 70, AGE = 40, ALB = 4, CALPRO = 700,   CDAI = 300, PMAYO = 6, IBD_CD = 0,   PRIOR_TNF = 0, ADA_POS = 0,   CONMED_AZA = 0, CONMED_MP = 0, CONMED_MTX = 0, CONMED_AMINO = 0 ) events_ss <- bind_rows(events_ss_base, extra_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID))  sim_ss <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events_ss) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(id = 1L, treatment = "ss_300mg_Q8W") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvmax'  sim_nca_ss <- sim_ss |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= final_dose_day) |>   mutate(time = time - final_dose_day) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_ss <- data.frame(   id = 1L, time = 0, amt = 300, treatment = "ss_300mg_Q8W" )  conc_ss <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_ss, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_ss_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_ss, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_ss <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = Inf,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_ss <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_ss, dose_ss_obj, intervals = intervals_ss) ) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_ss$result),              caption = "Steady-state NCA on the 8th (final) dosing interval.")

title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: Simulated vs. published reference-patient exposures. simulated steady-state trough, Cmax, terminal half-life fall within 20% published reference-patient values. short-dose apparent half-life shorter true terminal half-life non-linear Michaelis-Menten elimination pathway contributes meaningfully low concentrations (Km = 0.964 µg/mL), accelerating terminal decline linear-pathway clearance longer dominant.
code: get_param <- function(res, ppname) {   tbl <- as.data.frame(res$result)   val <- tbl$PPORRES[tbl$PPTESTCD == ppname]   if (length(val) == 0) return(NA_real_)   val[1] }  hl_single_sim <- get_param(nca_single, "half.life") hl_ss_sim     <- get_param(nca_ss,     "half.life") cmax_ss_sim   <- get_param(nca_ss,     "cmax")  # Steady-state trough (C at end of dosing interval, t = 56 days post-dose 8) c_trough_ss <- sim_ss$Cc[sim_ss$time == final_dose_day + 56]  comparison <- data.frame(   Quantity  = c("Terminal half-life (days)",                 "Steady-state Cmax (µg/mL)",                 "Steady-state trough at week 8 (µg/mL)"),   Published = c("~25 (vedolizumab prescribing information)",                 "~98 (Rosario 2015 Table 4 / Figure 4)",                 "~9 (Rosario 2015 Table 4 / Figure 4)"),   Simulated = c(round(hl_ss_sim, 1),                 round(cmax_ss_sim, 1),                 round(c_trough_ss, 1)) ) knitr::kable(comparison,              caption = "Simulated vs. published reference-patient exposures.")

title: Rosario_2015_vedolizumab
text: Two-typical-values switch linear clearance. Rosario 2015 Table 2 reports separate typical CLL estimates UC (0.159 L/day) CD (0.155 L/day) patients. packaged model implements cl_typ = exp(lcl) * (1 - IBD_CD) + exp(lcl_cd) * IBD_CD single shared IIV term etalcl (paper describes). structure simplest faithful representation published parameterisation; alternative ratio form (exp(lcl) * (CLL_CD_ratio)^IBD_CD) numerically equivalent. Disease-activity gating. Partial Mayo score CDAI appear final model diagnoses defined . gate power exponents IBD_CD indicator: (PMAYO/6)^(e_pmayo_cl * (1 - IBD_CD)) (CDAI/300)^(e_cdai_cl * IBD_CD). reduces 1 cohort irrespective supplied score. convenience, simulations can fill inactive column reference value. Time-varying ADA simplified binary. Rosario 2015 reports 4% pool ADA-positive time used binary positivity indicator final model; ADA-titre sensitivity statistically significant. packaged model therefore uses ADA_POS ; titre effect present source. IIV Vp, Q, Km. Rosario 2015 fixed IIV 0 three parameters (Table S2). packaged ini() block omits etas Vp, Q, Km accordingly — typical values shared across subjects. Residual error un-transformed concentration scale. Rosario 2015 reports σ²_prop = 0.0554 proportional-error variance (Table 2). packaged model uses propSd = sqrt(0.0554) ≈ 0.2354 Cc ~ prop(propSd) encodes nlmixr2’s standard proportional-error form. Virtual covariate distributions. Exact observed covariate distributions published. demo cohort uses log-normal WT (median 70 kg, CV 22%), normal AGE (39 ± 13 y), normal ALB (4.0 ± 0.4 g/dL), log-normal CALPRO (median 700 mg/kg, log-SD 1.1), 55%/45% CD/UC split, binary probabilities concomitant- medication immunogenicity indicators approximately matching Rosario 2015 Table 1.
