title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: Citation: Sanghavi K, Zhang J, Zhao X, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Ipilimumab Combination Nivolumab Patients Advanced Solid Tumors. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2020;9(1):29-39. doi:10.1002/psp4.12477 Description: Two-compartment population PK model intravenous ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4 IgG1) time-varying clearance via sigmoid Emax function patients advanced solid tumors receiving ipilimumab alone combination nivolumab (Sanghavi 2020) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12477 Ipilimumab fully human anti-CTLA-4 IgG1 monoclonal antibody. Sanghavi 2020 analysis refines earlier ipilimumab population PK model (Feng 2014, reference 17 paper) much larger combination-therapy dataset sigmoid-Emax description time-varying clearance. Structural form: linear two-compartment IV model first-order elimination multiplicative time--CL term: CLi(t)=CL0,⋅exp⁡(Emaxi⋅tHILLT50HILL+tHILL) \mathrm{CL}_i(t) = \mathrm{CL}_{0,} \cdot \exp\!\left(\mathrm{Emax}_i \cdot \frac{t^{\,\mathrm{HILL}}}{T_{50}^{\,\mathrm{HILL}} + t^{\,\mathrm{HILL}}}\right) Emaxi\mathrm{Emax}_i negative typical patient combination therapy (CL decreases time), T50≈106T_{50} \approx 106 days, HILL=7.43\mathrm{HILL} = 7.43 gives sharp sigmoid switch baseline steady-state CL. Baseline body weight (BBWT) scales CL Q (BBWT/80)0.694(\mathrm{BBWT}/80)^{0.694} VC VP (BBWT/80)0.600(\mathrm{BBWT}/80)^{0.600}.

title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: final-model population pooled 3,411 patients advanced solid tumors across 16 clinical trials (12,545 ipilimumab serum concentrations). breakdown (Sanghavi 2020 Table 1): Tumor type: melanoma 50.4%, non-small cell lung cancer 17.2%, renal cell carcinoma 13.1%, small cell lung cancer 5.2%, hepatocellular carcinoma 3.8%, colorectal cancer 3.6%. Treatment: ipilimumab monotherapy 26.2% (N = 893); ipilimumab combination nivolumab 73.8% (N = 2,518) across five nivolumab regimens (0.3 mg/kg Q3W, 1 mg/kg Q2W, 1 mg/kg Q3W, 3 mg/kg Q2W, 3 mg/kg Q3W). Demographics: body weight median 76.8 kg (range 36.8–181); baseline LDH median 217 U/L (range 74–6,245); baseline albumin median 4.1 g/dL (range 1.8–5.3); baseline tumor size median 6.29 cm. Performance status 0 57.3%, 1 41.3%, ≥ 2 1.4%. Trials: two phase , two phase /II, eight phase II, three phase III, one phase IIIb/IV (multinational). metadata available programmatically via rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab"))$population.

title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab.R. table collects one place review. Equations (paper’s “Final model” subsection Results): cl0 = CL0_REF · (BBWT/80)^CL_BBWT · (log(BLDH)/log(217))^CL_logBLDH · exp(CL_SCLC·I_SCLC) · exp(CL_LINE·I_1L) · exp(CL_N1Q3W·I_N1Q3W) · exp(CL_N3Q2W·I_N3Q2W) · exp(η_CL) Emax_i = Emax_REF + Emax_COMBO·I_COMBO + η_Emax cl(t)  = cl0_i · exp(Emax_i · t^HILL / (T50^HILL + t^HILL)) vc = VC_REF · (BBWT/80)^V_BBWT · exp(η_VC) q  = Q_REF  · (BBWT/80)^CL_BBWT vp = VP_REF · (BBWT/80)^V_BBWT

title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual melanoma cohort whose body-weight distribution approximates pooled trial population (median 76.8 kg, range 36.8–181 kg), reference covariates fixed paper’s reference patient values (LDH 217 U/L, melanoma, 2L+) time--CL component dominant driver -cohort differences. Model Application section Sanghavi 2020 simulates approved combination induction: ipilimumab 3 mg/kg Q3W plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg Q3W, four doses. Two arms compared : Combination: ipilimumab 3 mg/kg + nivolumab 1 mg/kg Q3W × 4 (COMBO_NIVO = 1, NIVO_1Q3W = 1). Monotherapy: ipilimumab 3 mg/kg Q3W × 4 alone (COMBO_NIVO = 0, NIVO_1Q3W = 0).
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_subj),   WT  = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(76.8), 0.27), 36.8), 181),   LDH = 217,   TUMTP_SCLC = 0L,   # melanoma reference (SCLC indicator off)   LINE_1L    = 0L    # 2L+ reference ) make_arm <- function(pop, regimen, id_offset = 0L) {   combo  <- as.integer(regimen == "Combination")   n1q3w  <- combo   ipi_amt <- pop$WT * 3   # 3 mg/kg   dose_t <- seq(0, by = 21, length.out = 4)   obs_t  <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 168, by = 1))))  # daily; 168 pts vs 672 (every 6 h)    d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(ID = id_offset + ID) |>     crossing(TIME = dose_t) |>     mutate(AMT = rep(ipi_amt, length(dose_t)),            EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 1.5 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = regimen,            NIVO_1Q3W = n1q3w, NIVO_3Q2W = 0L, COMBO_NIVO = combo)   d_obs <- pop |>     mutate(ID = id_offset + ID) |>     crossing(TIME = obs_t) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,            DV = NA_real_, treatment = regimen,            NIVO_1Q3W = n1q3w, NIVO_3Q2W = 0L, COMBO_NIVO = combo)   bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |> arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |> as.data.frame() }  events <- bind_rows(   make_arm(cohort, "Combination", id_offset = 0L),   make_arm(cohort, "Monotherapy", id_offset = n_subj) ) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))

title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: deterministic typical-value replication model’s random effects can zeroed:
code: mod <- readModelDb("Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, returnType = "data.frame",                        keep = c("treatment", "WT")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' events_ref <- data.frame(   ID = 1L, TIME = c(seq(0, 168, by = 0.5)),   AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0L, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,   DV = NA_real_, WT = 80, LDH = 217, TUMTP_SCLC = 0L, LINE_1L = 0L,   NIVO_1Q3W = 1L, NIVO_3Q2W = 0L, COMBO_NIVO = 1L ) events_ref <- bind_rows(   data.frame(ID = 1L, TIME = seq(0, by = 21, length.out = 4),              AMT = 240, EVID = 1L, CMT = "central", DUR = 1.5 / 24,              DV = NA_real_, WT = 80, LDH = 217,              TUMTP_SCLC = 0L, LINE_1L = 0L,              NIVO_1Q3W = 1L, NIVO_3Q2W = 0L, COMBO_NIVO = 1L),   events_ref ) |> arrange(TIME, desc(EVID))  sim_ref <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events_ref,                            returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etaEmax'

what: Time-varying clearance (Figure 3, left side: monotherapy vs. combination)
title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: Sanghavi 2020 Figure 3 reports model-estimated CL trajectories treatment arm. plot shows typical-individual CL ratio CL(t)/CL0\mathrm{CL}(t)/\mathrm{CL}_0 two reference scenarios (80 kg melanoma 2L+ patient, LDH 217 U/L, nivolumab regimen indicator baseline CL):
code: t_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5) make_typical_arm <- function(combo) {   ev <- data.frame(     ID = 1L,     TIME = c(seq(0, by = 21, length.out = 4), t_grid),     AMT = c(rep(240, 4), rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),     EVID = c(rep(1L, 4), rep(0L, length(t_grid))),     CMT = "central",     DUR = c(rep(1.5 / 24, 4), rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),     DV = NA_real_,     WT = 80, LDH = 217, TUMTP_SCLC = 0L, LINE_1L = 0L,     NIVO_1Q3W = combo, NIVO_3Q2W = 0L, COMBO_NIVO = combo   ) |> arrange(TIME, desc(EVID))   res <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev, returnType = "data.frame")   res$treatment <- if (combo == 1) "Combination (nivo 1 mg/kg Q3W)" else "Monotherapy"   res[res$time > 0, ] } cl_traj <- bind_rows(make_typical_arm(0L), make_typical_arm(1L)) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etaEmax' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etaEmax'  ggplot(cl_traj, aes(time, cl / cl0, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(x = "Time since first dose (days)",        y = "CL(t) / CL(0)",        title = "Time-varying clearance trajectory (typical 80 kg patient)",        subtitle = "Replicates the qualitative pattern of Figure 3 of Sanghavi 2020",        colour = NULL) +   theme_bw() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(time, treatment) |>   summarise(median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),             lo = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),             hi = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),             .groups = "drop")  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Ipilimumab Cc (μg/mL)",        title = "Simulated ipilimumab PK: 3 mg/kg Q3W induction × 4 doses",        subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval, N = ",                          n_subj, " virtual subjects per arm"),        caption = "Model: Sanghavi 2020 CPT:PSP 9(1):29-39") +   theme_bw()

title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: Compute NCA parameters first dosing interval (Day 0 Day 21) combination arm, Sanghavi 2020 Table 4 reports geometric-mean Day-1 peak (62.6 μg/mL) Day-21 trough (10.4 μg/mL). Simulated NCA parameters (combination arm, days 0-21).
code: sim_combo <- sim |>   filter(treatment == "Combination", !is.na(Cc),          time >= 0, time <= 21) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment) |>   filter(time > 0)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_combo, Cc ~ time | treatment + id)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(EVID == 1, treatment == "Combination", TIME == 0) |>   transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = treatment) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start = 0, end = 21,   cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE, half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (1) is not title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: Sanghavi 2020 Table 4 reports geometric-mean ipilimumab exposures N = 618 patients melanoma receiving ipilimumab 3 mg/kg + nivolumab 1 mg/kg Q3W induction. table compares typical-individual predictions packaged model geometric means. Day 1 peak Day 21 trough match published geometric means within ~5%. Day 84 trough ~20% lower typical-value simulation; consistent geometric mean N = 618 heterogeneous-covariate cohort slightly higher typical-80-kg-patient prediction (lighter patients lower CL (BBWT/80)^0.694 contribute disproportionately trough geometric mean). Day 105 prediction (6.5 vs. 9.3 μg/mL) reflects population-vs-typical effect inherent sensitivity late-elimination predictions inter-individual variability — see Assumptions deviations .

title: Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab
text: Reference covariates. Sanghavi 2020 Figure 1 defines reference patient 80 kg BBWT, 217 U/L BLDH, melanoma, ipilimumab monotherapy, 2L+. reference values hard-coded packaged model’s covariate centering (e.g., (WT/80)^e_wt_cl_q); user supplying different cohort must verify covariate centering matches. BLDH covariate form. paper’s final-model equation writes BLDH effect (log(BLDH_i) / log(BLDH_REF))^CL_log-BLDH — literally power ratio logs, conventional (BLDH/ref)^θ form. literal form implemented conventional form predict > 100% CL changes across reported BLDH range (74–6,245 U/L), inconsistent paper’s narrative BLDH effect < 20% clinically meaningful. See -file comment near e_logldh_cl Sanghavi_2020_ipilimumab.R numerical justification. Time-varying CL uses simulation time t. packaged model uses rxode2’s built-t symbol time--CL function, equals time since start simulation. dosing schedule begins time = 0 equivalent Sanghavi 2020’s “time first dose”; users shift dosing time series must either re-zero time substitute derived “time since first dose” covariate. Tumor-type indicators. TUMTP_SCLC = 0 reference group collapses non-SCLC tumor types (melanoma, NSCLC, RCC, CRC, HCC) reference. Effects NSCLC, CRC, HCC, RCC vs. melanoma tested full model survive backward elimination, collapse faithful published final model. Nivolumab-regimen indicators. surviving final-model indicators (NIVO_1Q3W, NIVO_3Q2W baseline CL, plus COMBO_NIVO Emax) exposed. tested regimens (0.3 mg/kg Q3W, 1 mg/kg Q2W, 3 mg/kg Q3W) collapse reference 0 group per-regimen indicators still trigger COMBO_NIVO = 1 time--CL Emax effect. Residual error convention. “Proportional” (0.223) “Additive” (0.607 μg/mL) entries Sanghavi 2020 Table 2 treated standard deviations rather variances, matching convention used avelumab (Masters 2022) tislelizumab (Budha 2023) models package. Time units. source reports CL Q mL/h T50 hours; packaged model carries time days (CL × 0.024 L/day; T50 / 24 days) consistency units$time = "day". Virtual cohort weight distribution. log-normal weight distribution median 76.8 kg sdlog = 0.27 truncated published range (36.8–181 kg) used paper’s Table 1 reports median range BBWT, full distribution. Adjust replacing cohort chunk different weight prior required. explicit age covariate. Sanghavi 2020 Table 1 tabulate age main text, age final model. virtual cohort therefore omits age; users need age-stratified simulations add passive covariate (enter structural model). Observation-grid simplification build speed. simulation grid uses daily time points (= 1 day, 168 points per subject) instead every-6-hour points (= 0.25 day, 672 points). 400 total subjects (200 per arm) reduces event dataset ~270 k rows ~68 k rows. time-varying CL curve concentration profiles faithfully reproduced daily resolution; build time drops ~7 min ~1–2 min.

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Citation: Sathe AG, Singh , Singh P, Diderichsen PM, Wang X, Chang P, Taqui , Phan S, Girish S, Othman AA. Population Pharmacokinetics Sacituzumab Govitecan Patients Metastatic Triple-Negative Breast Cancer Solid Tumors. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2024;63(5):669-681. Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40262-024-01366-3 Open-access PMC version: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11106201/ Supplement (NONMEM control streams, Section 6): https://static-content.springer.com/esm/art%3A10.1007%2Fs40262-024-01366-3/MediaObjects/40262_2024_1366_MOESM1_ESM.pdf Sacituzumab govitecan (SG) antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) anti-Trop-2 humanized monoclonal antibody (hRS7) covalently linked topoisomerase 1 inhibitor SN-38 via hydrolyzable linker (average drug--antibody ratio = 8). model jointly describes three analyte profiles SG infusion: SG (ADC) — output Cc model. Two-compartment PK body-weight allometric scaling baseline-albumin power covariate CL. Free SN-38 (released payload) — output Cc_sn38. Two-compartment PK generated sequentially SG via first-order release rate KREL. Apparent central peripheral volumes fixed literature values (Klein et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther 2002;72:638-647). Total antibody (tAB) — output Cc_tab. Two-compartment PK time-dependent CL, IIV CL V1 covariance, covariates baseline albumin (CL), tumor type (CL), sex (V1).

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: pooled analysis included 529 patients phase /II IMMU-132-01 (NCT01631552, n = 276) phase III ASCENT (NCT02574455, n = 253) studies. Patients metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (mTNBC, n = 277), metastatic urothelial cancer (mUC, n = 36), HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer (n = 32), solid tumors (n = 184; epithelial cancers including small-cell non-small-cell lung, colorectal, esophageal, pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma). Median (range) age 58 years (27-88), body weight 70 kg (37-140), serum albumin 38 g/L (19-50). 78% female 84% White (Sathe 2024 Section 3.1 Table S2). full population metadata available programmatically:
code: mod_meta <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Sathe_2024_sacituzumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' str(mod_meta$population, max.level = 1) #> List of 16 #>  $ n_subjects             : num 529 #>  $ n_studies              : num 2 #>  $ age_range              : chr "27-88 years" #>  $ age_median             : chr "58 years" #>  $ weight_range           : chr "37-140 kg" #>  $ weight_median          : chr "70 kg" #>  $ sex_female_pct         : num 78 #>  $ race_ethnicity         : Named num [1:4] 84 7 2 6 #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:4] "White" "Black_or_African_American" "Asian" "Other" #>  $ disease_state          : chr "Metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (mTNBC, n = 277), metastatic urothelial cancer (mUC, n = 36), HR+/HER2"| __truncated__ #>  $ dose_range             : chr "Phase I/II (IMMU-132-01): 8, 10, 12, or 18 mg/kg IV on days 1 and 8 of 21-day cycles. Phase III (ASCENT): 10 mg"| __truncated__ #>  $ regions                : chr "Multinational pooled analysis (IMMU-132-01 + ASCENT)" #>  $ studies                : chr "IMMU-132-01 (NCT01631552, n = 276 of which mTNBC = 24, mUC = 36, HR+/HER2- mBC = 32, other = 184) and ASCENT (N"| __truncated__ #>  $ baseline_albumin_median: chr "38 g/L (range 19-50)" #>  $ baseline_clcr_median   : chr "91 mL/min (range 22-262); 51% normal (>= 90), 38% mild impairment (60 to < 90), 11% moderate impairment (30 to "| __truncated__ #>  $ notes                  : chr "Sacituzumab govitecan is an ADC of an anti-Trop-2 humanized monoclonal antibody (hRS7) covalently linked to the"| __truncated__ #>  $ dosing_note            : chr "Each SG infusion event generates inputs to both the SG compartment and the tAB compartment. To simulate, provid"| __truncated__

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulation uses virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Sathe 2024 Table S2: median body weight 70 kg (range 37-140), median baseline albumin 38 g/L (range 19-50), 78% female, tumor-type composition matching pooled analysis (52% mTNBC, 7% mUC, 6% HR+/HER2- mBC, 35% solid tumors).
code: set.seed(20240405)  n_subj <- 200 make_cohort <- function(n) {   # Body weight: log-normal-ish around 70 kg, clamped to the source range.   wt  <- pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n, mean = 70, sd = 16), 1), 37), 140)   # Baseline albumin: normal around 38 g/L, clamped to the source range.   alb <- pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n, mean = 38, sd = 5), 1), 19), 50)   # Sex 78% female.   sexf <- rbinom(n, 1, prob = 0.78)   # Tumor type: 35% "Other" (per Sathe 2024 Table S2: 184/529).   tumor_oth <- rbinom(n, 1, prob = 184/529)   data.frame(     id        = seq_len(n),     WT        = wt,     ALB       = alb,     SEXF      = sexf,     TUMTP_OTH = tumor_oth,     cohort    = "10 mg/kg q1q8 of 21-day cycle"   ) }  cov_df <- make_cohort(n_subj)  # Build the event table: 10 mg/kg as a 30-min IV infusion on days 1 and 8 of a # 21-day cycle, two cycles. Each SG infusion needs TWO simultaneous dose events # (cmt = central and cmt = central_tab) so that both compartments receive the # same input mass at the same time (model file's documented dosing convention). infusion_dur <- 0.5  # hours dose_times_h <- c(0, 7 * 24, 21 * 24, 21 * 24 + 7 * 24)  # days 1, 8, 22, 29  events <- cov_df |>   rowwise() |>   do({     rows <- .     amt_dose <- 10 * rows$WT  # mg per dose at 10 mg/kg     rate_dose <- amt_dose / infusion_dur     dose_rows <- expand.grid(time = dose_times_h, cmt = c("central", "central_tab"),                              stringsAsFactors = FALSE)     dose_rows$id   <- rows$id     dose_rows$amt  <- amt_dose     dose_rows$rate <- rate_dose     dose_rows$evid <- 1     obs_rows <- data.frame(       id = rows$id,       time = c(0.001, seq(0.5, 21 * 24 + 7 * 24 + 14 * 24, length.out = 220)),       cmt = "Cc", amt = NA_real_, rate = NA_real_, evid = 0     )     out <- dplyr::bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows)     out$WT        <- rows$WT     out$ALB       <- rows$ALB     out$SEXF      <- rows$SEXF     out$TUMTP_OTH <- rows$TUMTP_OTH     out$cohort    <- rows$cohort     out   }) |>   ungroup() |>   arrange(id, time, evid)

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Typical-value simulation (IIV / RUV) replicating paper’s per-figure medians:
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Sathe_2024_sacituzumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = c("cohort", "WT")) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cov <- data.frame(   id = 1, WT = 70, ALB = 38, SEXF = 1, TUMTP_OTH = 0 ) typical_events <- expand.grid(   time = dose_times_h, cmt = c("central", "central_tab"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) typical_events$id  <- 1 typical_events$amt <- 700 typical_events$rate <- 700 / infusion_dur typical_events$evid <- 1 typical_obs <- data.frame(   id = 1,   time = c(0.001, seq(0.5, 21 * 24 + 7 * 24 + 14 * 24, length.out = 600)),   cmt = "Cc", amt = NA_real_, rate = NA_real_, evid = 0 ) typical_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(typical_events, typical_obs) typical_events$WT        <- 70 typical_events$ALB       <- 38 typical_events$SEXF      <- 1 typical_events$TUMTP_OTH <- 0  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = typical_events) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalkrel', 'etalcl_sn38', 'etalcl_tab', 'etalvc_tab'

what: Figure 2a — SG concentrations versus time after last dose
title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Sathe 2024 Figure 2a shows prediction-corrected VPC SG. virtual covariates observed data show simulated 5/50/95 percentiles versus time. Multiplying model output (Cc, ug/mL) 1000 converts paper’s reporting unit ng/mL.
code: sim_sg <- sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   mutate(SG_ng_per_mL = Cc * 1000)  sim_sg_summary <- sim_sg |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(SG_ng_per_mL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(SG_ng_per_mL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(SG_ng_per_mL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_sg_summary, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "SG (ng/mL)",        title = "Figure 2a — simulated SG concentrations",        caption = "Replicates Figure 2a of Sathe 2024 (median + 5-95th percentile envelope).")

what: Figure 2b — Free SN-38 concentrations versus time
title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
code: sim_sn38 <- sim |>   filter(time > 0) |>   mutate(SN38_ng_per_mL = Cc_sn38 * 1000)  sim_sn38_summary <- sim_sn38 |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(SN38_ng_per_mL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(SN38_ng_per_mL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(SN38_ng_per_mL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_sn38_summary, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Free SN-38 (ng/mL)",        title = "Figure 2b — simulated free SN-38 concentrations",        caption = "Replicates Figure 2b of Sathe 2024 (median + 5-95th percentile envelope).")

what: Figure 2c — Total antibody concentrations versus time
title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
code: sim_tab <- sim |>   filter(time > 0)  sim_tab_summary <- sim_tab |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc_tab, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc_tab, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc_tab, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_tab_summary, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Total antibody (ug/mL)",        title = "Figure 2c — simulated total antibody concentrations",        caption = "Replicates Figure 2c of Sathe 2024 (median + 5-95th percentile envelope).")

what: Figure 3a — body-weight effect on SG AUC
title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Sathe 2024 Figure 3a/b show body weights 5th (49 kg) 95th (110 kg) percentiles produce SG AUC 0.84 (95% CI 0.81-0.86) 1.25 (95% CI 1.21-1.29) relative 70 kg typical patient, constant 10 mg/kg mg/kg dosing. Reproduce typical-value model: Body-weight effect first-cycle AUC relative 70 kg typical patient. simulated SG AUC ratios 49 kg 110 kg bracket roughly 0.84 1.25 respectively (Sathe 2024 Figure 3a). Total antibody AUC scales slightly body weight dosing regimen mg/kg-based allometric exponents tAB CL/V less 1.
code: bw_grid <- c(49, 70, 110) out_bw <- lapply(bw_grid, function(wt) {   ev <- expand.grid(time = dose_times_h, cmt = c("central", "central_tab"),                     stringsAsFactors = FALSE)   ev$id  <- 1   ev$amt <- 10 * wt   ev$rate <- (10 * wt) / infusion_dur   ev$evid <- 1   obs <- data.frame(id = 1, time = c(0.001, seq(0.5, 21 * 24, length.out = 250)),                     cmt = "Cc", amt = NA_real_, rate = NA_real_, evid = 0)   ev <- bind_rows(ev, obs)   ev$WT <- wt; ev$ALB <- 38; ev$SEXF <- 1; ev$TUMTP_OTH <- 0   s <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev)   data.frame(WT = wt, time = s$time, Cc = s$Cc, Cc_tab = s$Cc_tab) }) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalkrel', 'etalcl_sn38', 'etalcl_tab', 'etalvc_tab' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalkrel', 'etalcl_sn38', 'etalcl_tab', 'etalvc_tab' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalkrel', 'etalcl_sn38', 'etalcl_tab', 'etalvc_tab' out_bw <- bind_rows(out_bw)  # AUC over the first 21-day treatment cycle via trapezoidal rule trapz <- function(x, y) sum(diff(x) * (head(y, -1) + tail(y, -1)) / 2)  auc_first_cycle <- out_bw |>   filter(time <= 21 * 24) |>   group_by(WT) |>   summarise(     AUC_SG_ug_h_per_mL  = trapz(time, Cc),     AUC_TAB_ug_h_per_mL = trapz(time, Cc_tab),     .groups = "drop"   )  ref_70 <- auc_first_cycle |> filter(WT == 70) auc_relative <- auc_first_cycle |>   mutate(     SG_AUC_rel  = AUC_SG_ug_h_per_mL  / ref_70$AUC_SG_ug_h_per_mL,     TAB_AUC_rel = AUC_TAB_ug_h_per_mL / ref_70$AUC_TAB_ug_h_per_mL   )  knitr::kable(auc_relative, digits = 3,              caption = "Body-weight effect on first-cycle AUC relative to a 70 kg typical patient.")

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Per Sathe 2024 Section 3.5, simulated first-cycle AUC Cmax compared across body-weight strata. Run PKNCA separately analyte; carry weight-band stratification keep.
code: sim_first_cycle <- sim |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 21 * 24) |>   mutate(     weight_band = case_when(       WT < 60  ~ "Low (<60 kg)",       WT > 90  ~ "High (>90 kg)",       TRUE     ~ "Mid (60-90 kg)"     )   )  dose_first_cycle <- events |>   filter(evid == 1, cmt == "central", time < 21 * 24) |>   mutate(weight_band = case_when(     WT < 60  ~ "Low (<60 kg)",     WT > 90  ~ "High (>90 kg)",     TRUE     ~ "Mid (60-90 kg)"   )) |>   select(id, time, amt, weight_band)

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Simulated first-cycle SG Cmax (ug/mL) AUClast (ug*h/mL) body-weight band.
code: sim_sg_nca <- sim_first_cycle |>   filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   select(id, time, Cc, weight_band)  conc_sg <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_sg_nca, Cc ~ time | weight_band + id,                             concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "h") dose_sg <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_first_cycle, amt ~ time | weight_band + id,                             doseu = "mg")  intervals_sg <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = 21 * 24,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE )  nca_sg <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_sg, dose_sg, intervals = intervals_sg)) #> Warning: Requesting an title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Sathe 2024 abstract reports population-typical CL 0.133 L/h Vss 3.68 L SG, 0.0164 L/h / 4.26 L tAB. body-weight allometric scaling 70 kg, infinite-time AUC single 700 mg dose simplifies dose / CL. Predicted single-dose AUCinf 70 kg / 38 g/L typical covariate values: dose / CL.
code: auc_inf_predicted <- data.frame(   analyte = c("SG", "tAB"),   CL_pop_L_per_h = c(0.133, 0.0164),   Dose_mg = 700,   AUCinf_predicted_ug_h_per_mL = c(700 / 0.133, 700 / 0.0164) ) knitr::kable(auc_inf_predicted, digits = 1,              caption = "Predicted single-dose AUCinf at 70 kg / 38 g/L typical covariate values: dose / CL.")

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Sathe 2024 Table 3 reports maximum 16.7% relative reduction tAB CL half-time approximately 48 days (rate constant 6.08e-4 1/h). Reproduce trajectory:
code: maxRed <- 16.7 keff   <- 6.08e-4 t_grid <- seq(0, 21 * 6 * 24, length.out = 200)  # 6 cycles = ~126 days cl_factor <- 1 - (maxRed / 100) * (1 - exp(-keff * t_grid))  half_time_h <- log(2) / keff half_time_days <- half_time_h / 24  ggplot(data.frame(time_days = t_grid / 24, cl_factor = cl_factor),        aes(time_days, cl_factor)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1 - maxRed / 100, linetype = "dashed") +   geom_vline(xintercept = half_time_days, linetype = "dotted") +   labs(x = "Time since first dose (days)",        y = "tAB CL relative to baseline",        title = "Time-dependent reduction of tAB CL",        caption = sprintf(          "Asymptote = 1 - %.1f%% = %.3f; half-time = %.1f days.",          maxRed, 1 - maxRed / 100, half_time_days        ))

title: Sathe_2024_sacituzumab
text: Dosing convention. SG infusion event represented event table TWO simultaneous dose rows (one cmt = "central", one cmt = "central_tab") SG tAB compartments receive input mass time. matches source paper’s setup, SG tAB models fit independently using SG dose directly. tAB CL / V “true” vs. model values. Sathe 2024 Methods 2.3 reports “true” tAB CL volume parameters model estimates multiplied 0.92 (mass ratio naked antibody SG). values stored model model-fit estimates intended used SG dose directly, 0.92-scaled “true” values. Multiply 0.92 comparing per-mass--antibody PK estimates sources. Compartment names. Multi-output ADC simulation requires named compartments (central_sn38, peripheral1_sn38, central_tab, peripheral1_tab) outside canonical central/peripheral1 set defined naming-conventions.md. nlmixr2lib::checkModelConventions flags compartments warnings; design multi-analyte models (see also Bender_2014_trastuzumabEmtansine_mechanistic). Time--last-dose effect residual error. Sathe 2024 Tables 1 2 report small TALD effect scales residual variance SG (+0.005 per hour TALD W^2) free SN-38 (+0.011 per hour). effects dropped simulation residual model proportional SD constant baseline values 0.204 (SG) 0.357 (SN-38). Including primarily widen prediction envelope late post-dose times; median trajectory unaffected. Study-indicator effect residual error. Sathe 2024 Tables 2 3 report study-IMMU-132-01 indicator RUV (-0.230 free SN-38 -0.250 tAB W). also dropped simulation; simulated cohort treated single pooled population. Race covariate. Race tested retained three final models (Sathe 2024 Section 3.6); appear model required simulation event table. Tumor-Trop-2 expression, UGT1A1 genotype, ECOG status, renal/hepatic function, ADA, age, prior treatment, prior lines therapy. Tested retained final covariates model (Sathe 2024 Section 3.6). Virtual cohort distribution. body-weight, baseline-albumin, sex, tumor-type distributions simulation cohort reconstructed Sathe 2024 Table S2 summary statistics (medians, ranges, percentages). individual-level covariate joint distribution published. Time reference time-dependent CL. expression 1 - maxRed_tab/100 * (1 - exp(-keff_tab * t)) uses rxode2 simulation time variable t, referenced first dose t = 0. Match simulation start start treatment reproducing time- decline trajectory.

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Citation: Soehoel , Larsen MS, Timmermann S. Population Pharmacokinetics Tralokinumab Adult Subjects Moderate Severe Atopic Dermatitis. Clinical Pharmacology Drug Development. 2022;11(8):910-921. doi:10.1002/cpdd.1113 Description: Two-compartment population PK model tralokinumab (Soehoel 2022) adults moderate--severe atopic dermatitis, SC first-order absorption allometric body-weight effects. Article: Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev. 2022;11(8):910-921 (open-access via PMC9796478)

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: published analysis pooled 2561 subjects (13,361 quantifiable serum concentrations) across 10 clinical trials: 3 phase 3 trials (ECZTRA 1/2/3), 4 phase 2 trials (including diluted-formulation trial D2213C00001), 3 phase 1 trials, enrolling atopic-dermatitis patients (n = 2066, 81%), asthma patients (n = 441, 17%), healthy volunteers (n = 54, 2%). Baseline demographics Soehoel 2022 Table 1: age median 38 years (range 18-92, 131 subjects >= 65 years); body weight median 74.5 kg (range 36-165); 55% male / 45% female; race 67% White, 22% Asian, 7% Black/African American; ethnicity 9% Hispanic/Latino. Baseline EASI score AD subjects: median 27.5 (range 12-72). Subcutaneous tralokinumab doses ranged single-dose phase 1 exposures labelled atopic-dermatitis regimen 600 mg SC loading followed 300 mg SC every 2 weeks. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab")$population.

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken directly Soehoel 2022 Table 2. reference weight 75 kg; nonECZTRA = 0 dilution = 0 define ECZTRA / undiluted reference. Table 2 footnote (d) Soehoel 2022 states IIV reported sqrt(exp(omega^2) - 1), .e., log-normal CV convention. inverse relation omega^2 = log(1 + CV^2) used convert reported 40.1% / 31.3% CVs variance/covariance triple stored ~ c(...) form ini(). previous release stored sqrt(.) values (standard deviations), -estimated IIV simulation time; vignette depends corrected form.

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Soehoel 2022 Table 1 demographics restricted adult atopic-dermatitis subpopulation (.e., regimen-relevant population): body weight truncated normal centered reported median (74.5 kg, SD ~18 kg) limits 36-165 kg; 45% female; nonECZTRA = 0 (ECZTRA-trial reference) dilution = 0 (undiluted reference) labelled regimen. Sex race columns retained documentation enter Soehoel 2022 model.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id        = seq_len(n_subj),   WT        = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 74.5, sd = 18), 36), 165),   SEXF      = as.integer(runif(n_subj) < 0.45),   nonECZTRA = 0L,   dilution  = 0L )  # Labelled AD regimen: 600 mg SC loading dose on day 0, 300 mg SC Q2W # (every 14 days) for 12 additional doses => study window 0-182 days. # By doses 10+ the profile is at steady state (terminal half-life ~3 weeks at # typical parameters). load_dose <- 600 maint_dose <- 300 tau <- 14 n_maint <- 12 dose_days <- c(0, seq(tau, tau * n_maint, by = tau)) amt_vec <- c(load_dose, rep(maint_dose, n_maint))  ev_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time) |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_vec, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L) |>   dplyr::ungroup()  obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, tau * (n_maint + 1), by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3 )))  ev_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,     WT, SEXF, nonECZTRA, dilution)

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab")) conc_unit <- mod$units[["concentration"]] sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = events,   keep = c("WT", "SEXF", "nonECZTRA", "dilution") )

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Soehoel 2022 Figure 3 (prediction-corrected VPC) presents 5th/50th/95th percentile prediction bands observed data. Original data publicly available; figure shows simulated 5/50/95 percentile bands virtual AD cohort labelled 600-mg-load--300-mg-Q2W regimen across approximately 13 dosing cycles (steady-state reached cycle 8-10).
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#4682b4") +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = dose_days, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey70") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = paste0("Tralokinumab Cc (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile VPC; 600 mg SC load then 300 mg SC Q2W",     caption = "Virtual AD cohort (N = 400); WT median 74.5 kg; nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Zoomed-view final Q2W cycle (days 168-182) isolate steady-state peak, trough, AUC_tau used NCA .
code: ss_start <- tau * n_maint # day 168 (time of dose 13) ss_end <- ss_start + tau # day 182  ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes(time - ss_start, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#b4464b") +   geom_line(colour = "#b4464b", linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time after final dose (days)",     y = paste0("Tralokinumab Cc (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Steady-state Q2W cycle (dose 13 of 13)",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval over one 14-day dosing interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis steady-state Q2W interval (days 168-182). Compute Cmax, Cmin (Ctrough end tau), AUC_tau, average concentration per simulated subject, summarise across cohort.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start,     treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = maint_dose, treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = tau,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) summary(nca_res) #>  start end    treatment   N     auclast       cmax       cmin        cav #>      0  14 300mg_Q2W_SS 400 2110 [41.5] 163 [40.7] 130 [43.4] 151 [41.5] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against published typical steady-state exposure
title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Soehoel 2022 tabulate point estimates steady-state Cmax / Cmin / AUC_tau main text; Figure 3 (prediction-corrected VPC) Discussion (Predicting success reduced dosing frequency, 2024 follow-analyses) imply typical steady-state trough concentrations approximately 98 ug/mL typical average concentrations near 130 ug/mL 75-kg adult 600-mg-load + 300-mg-Q2W SC. typical-value (“population-typical”) prediction IIV zeroed provides direct comparison: Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg, nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0; IIV zeroed).
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id        = 1L,   WT        = 75,   SEXF      = 0L,   nonECZTRA = 0L,   dilution  = 0L )  ev_typical <- events |>   dplyr::filter(id == 1L) |>   dplyr::mutate(WT = 75)  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical,   keep = c("WT", "SEXF", "nonECZTRA", "dilution") ) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalcl'  ss_typical <- sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc))  typical_summary <- tibble::tibble(   metric = c("Cmax (ug/mL)", "Cmin/Ctrough (ug/mL)",     "Cavg (ug/mL)", "AUC_tau (day*ug/mL)"),   typical_value = c(     max(ss_typical$Cc),     min(ss_typical$Cc),     mean(ss_typical$Cc),     sum(diff(ss_typical$time) *       (ss_typical$Cc[-length(ss_typical$Cc)] +         ss_typical$Cc[-1]) / 2)   ) ) knitr::kable(typical_summary, digits = 2,   caption = "Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg, nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0; IIV zeroed).")

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: pooled-analysis population spans AD, asthma, healthy subjects across 10 trials. virtual cohort restricted AD-relevant reference combination (nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0) simulated profile mirrors labelled 600-mg-load + 300-mg-Q2W SC atopic-dermatitis regimen. Body weight drawn truncated normal approximating Table 1 (median 74.5 kg, range 36-165 kg). paper reports overall pooled median range; per-indication WT summary given, approximation unavoidable. Sex retained annotation column; covariate Soehoel 2022 final model. SEXF, nonECZTRA, dilution column names preserve original paper’s mixed-case spellings per nlmixr2lib covariate-columns register model; future models use canonical SEXF / NON_ECZTRA / DILUTION forms. IIV variances back-calculated reported CV% using log-normal convention Table 2 footnote (omega^2 = log(1 + CV^2)). variance-covariance triple passed ini() (0.148971, 0.071977, 0.093459). Soehoel 2022 publish numerical Cmax / Cmin / AUC_tau steady state; PKNCA summary therefore self-consistency check implemented model rather back--paper numerical comparison. Qualitative agreement Figure 3 (prediction-corrected VPC) intended validation. unit conversion required dosing amount (mg) concentration unit (ug/mL) mg / L = ug / mL.

what: Risankizumab population PK in healthy volunteers and plaque psoriasis
title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: Simulate risankizumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Suleiman et al. (2019) integrated phase -III clinical-trial programme healthy volunteers patients moderate--severe plaque psoriasis. source paper pooled 13,123 plasma concentrations 1899 subjects (67 healthy volunteers 1832 psoriasis patients) across seven studies. model two-compartment structure first-order subcutaneous absorption linear elimination (NONMEM ADVAN4 TRANS4). Typical parameters 70 kg reference subject (median albumin, creatinine, high-sensitivity C-reactive protein [hs-CRP], ADA titer < 128, phase III drug supply) CL = 0.243 L/day, Vc = 4.86 L, Vp = 4.25 L, ka = 0.229 /day, Q = 0.656 L/day, F = 0.890. final covariate set body weight CL, Vc, Vp; baseline serum albumin, serum creatinine, hs-CRP CL; time-varying ADA titer threshold effect CL (+43% titer ≥ 128). Citation: Suleiman AA, Khatri , Minocha M, Othman AA. Population Pharmacokinetics Risankizumab Healthy Volunteers Subjects Moderate Severe Plaque Psoriasis: Integrated Analyses Phase -III Clinical Trials. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(10):1309-1321. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00759-z Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40262-019-00759-z

title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: Phase III clinical regimen: 150 mg SC weeks 0 4, every 12 weeks (q12w) thereafter. Simulate week 52 match study-design window UltIMMa-1 / UltIMMa-2 Fig. 2 paper.
code: dose_weeks <- c(0, 4, 16, 28, 40) dose_times <- dose_weeks * 7  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,  28,  by = 0.5),   seq(28, 52 * 7, by = 2) )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 150,       # mg SC     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 1,         # depot     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: median 90% prediction interval simulated concentration-time profile 150 mg SC q12w regimen week 52. Replicates VPC layout Fig. 2 (UltIMMa-1 UltIMMa-2 combined, phase III clinical regimen).
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x        = "Time (weeks)",     y        = "Risankizumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title    = "Simulated risankizumab PK (150 mg SC weeks 0, 4, q12w)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual psoriasis patients)",     caption  = "Replicates Fig. 2 of Suleiman 2019 (UltIMMa-1 / UltIMMa-2 layout)."   ) +   theme_bw()

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in healthy volunteers and plaque psoriasis
title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: Compute Cmax Ctrough first three maintenance dosing intervals (weeks 4-16, 16-28, 28-40). paper reports steady-state plasma exposures approached week 16 dosing (Abstract Sect. 3.3), intervals starting week 16 onward reflect steady-state. Simulated per-interval exposures (Cmax/Ctrough ug/mL; AUCtau ug*day/mL).
code: intervals <- tribble(   ~label,       ~start_wk, ~end_wk,   "2 (4-16)",    4,  16,   "3 (16-28)",  16,  28,   "4 (28-40)",  28,  40 )  ivl_summary <- intervals %>%   rowwise() %>%   do({     row <- .     sub <- sim %>%       filter(time >= row$start_wk * 7, time <= row$end_wk * 7, Cc > 0)     per_id <- sub %>%       group_by(id) %>%       summarise(         Cmax    = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),         Ctrough = Cc[which.max(time)],         AUCtau  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),         .groups = "drop"       )     tibble(       interval       = row$label,       Cmax_median    = median(per_id$Cmax),       Ctrough_median = median(per_id$Ctrough),       AUCtau_median  = median(per_id$AUCtau)     )   }) %>%   bind_rows()  knitr::kable(   ivl_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = "Simulated per-interval exposures (Cmax/Ctrough in ug/mL; AUCtau in ug*day/mL)." )

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in healthy volunteers and plaque psoriasis
title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: Run PKNCA steady-state maintenance interval (weeks 16-28 phase III 150 mg SC q12w regimen). expected terminal elimination half-life ~28 days typical 90 kg psoriasis patient (Abstract). PKNCA summary steady-state maintenance interval (weeks 16-28). Expected terminal half-life ~28 days typical 90 kg psoriasis subject (Suleiman 2019, Abstract).
code: nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= 16 * 7, time <= 28 * 7, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = time - 16 * 7,     treatment = "risankizumab_150mg_q12w"   ) %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 150,     treatment = "risankizumab_150mg_q12w"   ) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 12 * 7,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■                           22% |  ETA: 14s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■                      38% |  ETA: 11s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 55% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■            71% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■       87% |  ETA:  2s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste(     "PKNCA summary for the steady-state maintenance interval (weeks 16-28).",     "Expected terminal half-life ~28 days for a typical 90 kg psoriasis subject",     "(Suleiman 2019, Abstract)."   ) )

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in healthy volunteers and plaque psoriasis
what: Typical-subject comparison against the published abstract
title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: abstract states CL ≈ 0.31 L/day, Vss ≈ 11.2 L, terminal half-life ≈ 28 days typical 90 kg psoriatic subject median covariates. Reproduce using packaged model - subject variability zeroed :
code:
mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  typ_pop <- tibble(   ID        = 1L,   WT        = 90,   ALB       = 44,   CREAT     = 76,   CRP       = 2.8,   ADA_TITER = 0 )  typ_dose <- typ_pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = 150, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DV = NA_real_)  typ_obs <- typ_pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)  typ_events <- bind_rows(typ_dose, typ_obs) %>%   arrange(TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()  sim_typ <- rxSolve(mod_typ, typ_events, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalogitfdepot'  # Typical-subject parameter values cl_typ <- 0.243 * (90/70)^0.933 vc_typ <- 4.86  * (90/70)^1.17 vp_typ <- 4.25  * (90/70)^0.377 q_typ  <- 0.656 kel_typ <- cl_typ / vc_typ vss_typ <- vc_typ + vp_typ  # True terminal-phase half-life from the 2-cpt rate-constant eigenvalues k12 <- q_typ / vc_typ k21 <- q_typ / vp_typ sum_k <- kel_typ + k12 + k21 lambda_z <- (sum_k - sqrt(sum_k^2 - 4 * kel_typ * k21)) / 2 t_half_terminal <- log(2) / lambda_z  cat(sprintf(   paste0(     "Typical 90 kg psoriasis subject (vs paper abstract):
",     "  CL  = %.3f L/day (paper: 0.31)
",     "  Vc  = %.2f L    (paper: 6.52)
",     "  Vp  = %.2f L    (paper: 4.67)
",     "  Vss = %.2f L    (paper: 11.2)
",     "  t1/2 terminal = %.1f days (paper: 28)
",     "  F (phase III) = %.3f    (paper: 0.890)
"   ),   cl_typ, vc_typ, vp_typ, vss_typ, t_half_terminal,   exp(2.09) / (1 + exp(2.09)) )) #> Typical 90 kg psoriasis subject (vs paper abstract): #>   CL  = 0.307 L/day (paper: 0.31) #>   Vc  = 6.52 L    (paper: 6.52) #>   Vp  = 4.67 L    (paper: 4.67) #>   Vss = 11.19 L    (paper: 11.2) #>   t1/2 terminal = 27.6 days (paper: 28) #>   F (phase III) = 0.890    (paper: 0.890)  ggplot(sim_typ %>% filter(time > 0), aes(time / 7, Cc)) +   geom_line(colour = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x        = "Time (weeks)",     y        = "Risankizumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title    = "Typical 90 kg psoriasis patient (zeroed random effects)",     caption  = "Reproduces the 'approaches steady-state by week 16' behaviour from Abstract."   ) +   theme_bw()

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in healthy volunteers and plaque psoriasis
title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: paper reports patients ADA titer ≥ 128 (~1.5% phase III ADA-evaluable subjects) ~43% increased CL ~30% decreased AUC 150 mg SC q12w regimen (Sect. 3.3). Simulate typical ADA-positive subject compare AUC steady state.
code:
typ_pop_ada <- typ_pop %>% mutate(ADA_TITER = 256)  # above threshold 128  typ_events_ada <- bind_rows(   typ_pop_ada %>% crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%     mutate(AMT = 150, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DV = NA_real_),   typ_pop_ada %>% crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_) ) %>%   arrange(TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()  sim_typ_ada <- rxSolve(mod_typ, typ_events_ada, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalogitfdepot'  # AUC over interval 3 (weeks 16-28) for both typical profiles auc_window <- function(df) {   d <- df %>% filter(time >= 16 * 7, time <= 28 * 7, Cc > 0) %>% arrange(time)   sum(diff(d$time) * (head(d$Cc, -1) + tail(d$Cc, -1)) / 2) } auc_ref <- auc_window(sim_typ) auc_ada <- auc_window(sim_typ_ada) cat(sprintf(   paste0(     "Typical-subject AUCtau (weeks 16-28):
",     "  ADA-negative:       %.1f ug*day/mL
",     "  ADA titer >= 128:   %.1f ug*day/mL  (%.1f%% change vs negative; paper: -30%%)
"   ),   auc_ref, auc_ada, 100 * (auc_ada - auc_ref) / auc_ref )) #> Typical-subject AUCtau (weeks 16-28): #>   ADA-negative:       449.8 ug*day/mL #>   ADA titer >= 128:   307.9 ug*day/mL  (-31.5% change vs negative; paper: -30%)

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in healthy volunteers and plaque psoriasis
title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: Suleiman 2019 publish individual-level PK per-subject covariate values, virtual population approximates -subject demographics Table 2 rather reproducing : Weight: sampled log-normal around 87 kg median (Table 2 - subject median) SD 0.23 log scale, clipped 42-200 kg. Serum albumin: normal around 44 g/L (-subject median), SD 3, clipped 34-58 g/L (observed range). Serum creatinine: normal around 76 umol/L (-subject median), SD 16, clipped 35-200 umol/L (observed range). hs-CRP: log-normal around 2.8 mg/L median (-subject median), SD 1.0 log scale. hs-CRP highly right-skewed. ADA titer: set 0 (negative) main virtual cohort ~98.5% phase III ADA-evaluable subjects titers < 128. separate typical-subject simulation ADA titer = 256 demonstrates -threshold effect. correlation continuous covariates imposed; paper report joint distributions. Sex, age, race, country, baseline PASI, bilirubin, AST, ALT, NAb status evaluated paper retained final model part packaged model. Phase -II drug supply (F = 0.710). packaged model’s default F uses phase III drug-supply logit (2.09 → F = 0.890), approved clinical regimen uses phase III drug supply. Users wishing simulate early-phase formulation can override logitfdepot 0.896 constructing model object. Terminal half-life 28 days. abstract’s ~28-day terminal half-life derives full bi-exponential eigenvalue 2-compartment system; rough Vss / CL back--envelope - estimates days.

title: Suleiman_2019_risankizumab
text: Suleiman AA, Khatri , Minocha M, Othman AA. Population Pharmacokinetics Risankizumab Healthy Volunteers Subjects Moderate Severe Plaque Psoriasis: Integrated Analyses Phase -III Clinical Trials. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(10):1309-1321. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00759-z

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: Citation: Suri , Mould DR, Liu Y, Jang G, Venkatakrishnan K. Population PK Exposure-Response Relationships Antibody-Drug Conjugate Brentuximab Vedotin CTCL Patients Phase III ALCANZA Study. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2018;104(5):989-999. Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/cpt.1037 (PMID 29377077) Open-access PMC copy: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6220930/ Supplements: ten supplementary files (PMID_29377077_supplement_{1..10}.docx). popPK final-model parameter values come supplements 6 (Table S1, ADC) 8 (Table S3, MMAE); structural-model methodology, IIV / residual error / covariate-effect equations come supplement 1; covariate exploration study-design details come supplements 7, 9, 10 (Tables S2, S4, S5). Suri 2018 paper reports coupled population-PK model brentuximab vedotin (BV) antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) released payload monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE), pooled across six clinical studies (380 patients CD30-positive malignancies). population PK model fit full pooled cohort; exposure-response work focuses 66 cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL) patients enrolled phase III ALCANZA study (NCT01578499). Final-model parameter values come supplementary Tables S1 (ADC) S3 (MMAE); structural-model textual description comes main paper (Results section, page 991 onward) plus Mould-lab ADC-modeling framework supplement explicitly inherits (“previously developed models used structural base models analysis,” supplement 1 introductory paragraph). ALFM / Klag / Kd / FM mechanism used related pediatric model Zhou_2025_brentuximab (also Mould-lab publication).

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: pooled analysis dataset comprised 380 patients across six clinical studies: Phase (NCT00430846, Younes 2010, n = 45) — relapsed/refractory CD30-positive hematologic malignancies; dose-ranging 0.1-1.2 mg/kg every 3 weeks (older ADA assay). Phase (NCT00649584, Fanale 2012, n = 44) — relapsed/refractory CD30-positive hematologic malignancies; dose-ranging 0.4-1.8 mg/kg weekly 3 weeks 4-week cycle (older ADA assay). Phase II (NCT00848926, Younes 2012, n = 102) — Hodgkin lymphoma; 1.2 1.8 mg/kg every 3 weeks (older ADA assay). Phase II (NCT00866047, Pro 2012, n = 58) — relapsed/refractory systemic ALCL; 1.2 1.8 mg/kg every 3 weeks (older ADA assay). Phase II (NCT01990534, Walewski 2016, n = 60) — relapsed/refractory HL; 1.8 mg/kg every 3 weeks (newer ADA assay). Phase III ALCANZA (NCT01578499, Prince 2017, n = 66) — previously treated CD30-positive CTCL (mycosis fungoides pcALCL); 1.8 mg/kg every 3 weeks (newer ADA assay). Median (range) baseline characteristics Suri 2018 Table 1 (overall population, n = 380): age 37 (12-87) y; weight 76.5 (39-168) kg; BSA 1.865 (1.264-2.858) m2; serum albumin 36.81 (17-53) g/L; total bilirubin 7.78 (2-123) umol/L; serum creatinine 72.4 (35-159) umol/L. Sex 45% female; race 83% White / 6% Black / 8% Asian / 2% . Disease type distribution: HL 64.7% (n = 246), sALCL 17.4% (n = 66), MF 13.2% (n = 50), pcALCL 4.2% (n = 16), 0.5% (n = 2). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Suri_2018_brentuximab")$meta$population.

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Suri_2018_brentuximab.R. table collects source locations one place. Tables S1, S3, S5 references Suri 2018 supplementary tables (supplements 6, 8, 10 respectively); Table 1 main paper’s overall-population baseline characteristics table.

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: main-paper Results page 994 prints MMAE rate constants units “L/h” — binding rate constant Kd (0.00069), ADC->MMAE conversion rate ALFM (2.64), lag-compartment rate constant Klag (15.7). supplement Table S3 column header (supplement 8) abbreviations list Figure 1b caption report 1/h (.e., first-order rate constants), unit dimensionally consistent ODE structure (equations require dA/dt terms like Klag * A_lag, yields amount/time Klag 1/time). packaged model uses supplement / Figure-1b unit (1/hr); treat main-paper “L/h” labels typographical error rather separate unit convention. published erratum corrigendum located PubMed Wiley Online Library landing page doi:10.1002/cpt.1037 2026-04-28.

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: Original participant-level data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate published pooled-population demographics (Suri 2018 Table 1, overall column). cohort split two regimen labels — licensed 1.8 mg/kg Q3W (matching dosing four largest contributing studies ALCANZA) small 1.2 mg/kg Q3W cohort (matching lower-dose arm phase II HL sALCL studies recommended dose-reduction regimen hepatic / severe-renal impairment).
code: set.seed(29377077L)  # Brentuximab vedotin and MMAE molecular weights — used to convert mg doses to # umol (the model amount unit, matching the Mould-lab NONMEM convention also # used in Zhou_2025_brentuximab). MW_BV_kDa  <- 153.4 # ADC molar mass approx 153.4 kg/mol MW_MMAE_Da <- 718.0 # payload molar mass approx 718 g/mol  cycle_h_q3w <- 21 * 24 # every 3 weeks n_cycles    <- 4L  # Helper builds one cohort. id_offset shifts subject IDs so multiple cohorts # can be bind_rows()-ed without colliding. make_cohort <- function(n, dose_mg_per_kg, regimen, wt, bsa, alb, tbili, creat,                         tumtp_pcalcl = 0L, ada_posnew = 0L, ada_posold = 0L,                         ada_missing = 0L, dose_cap_mg = 180, id_offset = 0L) {   ids <- id_offset + seq_len(n)   dose_mg  <- pmin(dose_mg_per_kg * wt, dose_cap_mg)   amt_umol <- dose_mg / MW_BV_kDa    obs_t <- c(seq(0, 6, by = 0.5),                       # dense first 6 h              seq(8, 24, by = 4),                        # tail of first day              seq(48, cycle_h_q3w, by = 24),             # daily through cycle 1              seq(cycle_h_q3w + 4, n_cycles * cycle_h_q3w, by = 12))   obs_t <- sort(unique(obs_t))    doses <- tibble::tibble(     id   = ids,     time = 0,     amt  = amt_umol,     cmt  = "central",     evid = 1L,     ii   = cycle_h_q3w,     addl = n_cycles - 1L,     dur  = 0.5,     regimen = regimen   )    obs <- tidyr::expand_grid(id = ids, time = obs_t) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = NA_real_, cmt = "Cc", evid = 0L,                   ii = 0, addl = 0L, dur = NA_real_, regimen = regimen)    cov <- tibble::tibble(     id           = ids,     BSA          = bsa,     ALB          = alb,     TBILI        = tbili,     CREAT        = creat,     TUMTP_PCALCL = as.integer(tumtp_pcalcl),     ADA_POS   = as.integer(ada_posnew),     ADA_POSOLD   = as.integer(ada_posold),     ADA_MISSING  = as.integer(ada_missing)   )    events <- dplyr::bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::left_join(cov, by = "id")   events }  n_per_cohort <- 80L  # Sample covariates from log-normal distributions whose medians and CV match # the Table 1 overall-population summary statistics. Pediatric patients (12 y) # in the original cohort are not represented; the virtual cohort approximates # the adult majority (median age 37 y, 75 kg). sample_cov <- function(n) {   list(     wt    = exp(rnorm(n, log(76.5),  0.27)),     bsa   = exp(rnorm(n, log(1.865), 0.16)),     alb   = exp(rnorm(n, log(36.81), 0.18)),     tbili = exp(rnorm(n, log(7.78),  0.45)),     creat = exp(rnorm(n, log(72.4),  0.28))   ) }  cohort_a_cov <- sample_cov(n_per_cohort) cohort_a <- make_cohort(   n              = n_per_cohort,   dose_mg_per_kg = 1.8,   regimen        = "1.8 mg/kg Q3W",   wt             = cohort_a_cov$wt,   bsa            = cohort_a_cov$bsa,   alb            = cohort_a_cov$alb,   tbili          = cohort_a_cov$tbili,   creat          = cohort_a_cov$creat,   ada_posnew     = 0L,   id_offset      = 0L )  cohort_b_cov <- sample_cov(n_per_cohort) cohort_b <- make_cohort(   n              = n_per_cohort,   dose_mg_per_kg = 1.2,   regimen        = "1.2 mg/kg Q3W",   wt             = cohort_b_cov$wt,   bsa            = cohort_b_cov$bsa,   alb            = cohort_b_cov$alb,   tbili          = cohort_b_cov$tbili,   creat          = cohort_b_cov$creat,   ada_posnew     = 0L,   id_offset      = n_per_cohort )  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(cohort_a, cohort_b) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: deterministic typical-value simulation (reproducing Figure 3 panels c-d paper), zero random effects:
code: mod <- readModelDb("Suri_2018_brentuximab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = "regimen") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_df <- as.data.frame(sim)  # Convert ADC concentration umol/L -> ug/mL  (= umol/L * MW_kDa) # Convert MMAE concentration umol/L -> ng/mL  (= umol/L * MW_Da) sim_df <- sim_df |>   dplyr::mutate(     Cc_ugmL    = Cc    * MW_BV_kDa,     Cmmae_ngmL = Cc_mmae * MW_MMAE_Da,     day        = time / 24   ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events, keep = "regimen")

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: Suri 2018 paper plots VPCs ADC MMAE versus time-since-last-dose (Figure 3a-b) simulated typical-value concentration-time profiles 1.8 mg/kg Q3W three cycles (Figure 3c-d). simulated cohorts reproduce regimen; per-time medians 5th / 95th percentiles across virtual cohort analogues Figure 3.
code: sim_df |>   dplyr::filter(regimen == "1.8 mg/kg Q3W") |>   dplyr::group_by(day) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = stats::quantile(Cc_ugmL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = stats::quantile(Cc_ugmL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = stats::quantile(Cc_ugmL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "ADC concentration (ug/mL)",        title = "Figure 3a/c analogue — ADC VPC at 1.8 mg/kg Q3W",        caption = "Replicates Figure 3 (panels a, c) of Suri 2018.") #> Warning in scale_y_log10(): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. sim_df |>   dplyr::filter(regimen == "1.8 mg/kg Q3W") |>   dplyr::group_by(day) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = stats::quantile(Cmmae_ngmL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = stats::quantile(Cmmae_ngmL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = stats::quantile(Cmmae_ngmL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::filter(Q50 > 0) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pmax(Q05, 1e-3), ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "MMAE concentration (ng/mL)",        title = "Figure 3b/d analogue — MMAE VPC at 1.8 mg/kg Q3W",        caption = "Replicates Figure 3 (panels b, d) of Suri 2018.")

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: PKNCA NCA single steady-state interval (cycle 4, day 1 day 21) provides quantitative cross-check simulated ADC MMAE exposures. PKNCA formula carries regimen grouping per-cohort summaries can compared paper’s per-cohort exposure summaries. Simulated cycle-4 ADC NCA parameters regimen. Simulated cycle-4 MMAE NCA parameters regimen.
code: sim_adc <- sim_df |>   dplyr::mutate(     in_cyc4 = time >= 3 * cycle_h_q3w & time < 4 * cycle_h_q3w   ) |>   dplyr::filter(in_cyc4) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_in_cycle = time - 3 * cycle_h_q3w) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_in_cycle, conc = Cc_ugmL, regimen) |>   dplyr::arrange(regimen, id, time)  conc_obj_adc <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_adc, conc ~ time | regimen + id)  doses_pknca <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1L, time == 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, regimen) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(doses_pknca, amt ~ time | regimen + id)  intervals_adc <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = cycle_h_q3w,   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE )  nca_data_adc <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_adc, dose_obj,                                  intervals = intervals_adc) title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: Suri 2018 Results pages 991-993 report cycle-3 (steady-state) ADC MMAE geometric-mean AUC values model-based simulation 1.8 mg/kg Q3W regimen, tumor type ADA status: simulated 1.8 mg/kg Q3W cohort vignette draws covariates overall pooled-population distribution (n = 380 Suri 2018 Table 1) set ADA-negative non-pcALCL throughout, simulated cycle-4 ADC AUC land near published 2,772 ug*h/mL “non-pcALCL, ADA-negative” geometric mean. Deviations >20% target prompt re-checking covariate distributions, BSA reference value, dose-cap rule rather tuning model. simulated MMAE AUC match published 600 ngh/mL value. underlying unit convention used original NONMEM run MMAE submodel — specifically bilinear term Kd * Target * central proteolytic flux FM * exp(-ALFM * tad) * K10 * central scaled ADC--MMAE stoichiometry — stated supplement, published Kd / ALFM / Klag / V_M parameters uniquely determine absolute MMAE concentration scale. molar-amount dosing (1.8 mg/kg = 0.88 umol ADC 75 kg subject), model produces MMAE Cmax ~0.2 ng/mL AUC ~50 ngh/mL — 12-fold published exposure, roughly-DAR-sized stoichiometric scaling factor (brentuximab vedotin’s average drug-antibody ratio ~4) missing bilinear flux terms. direct mg-amount dosing (135 mg) model overshoots ~4-fold (Cmax ~28 ng/mL, AUC ~2400 ngh/mL). Neither pure unit convention reproduces published absolute MMAE concentrations. ADC submodel unaffected ambiguity (ODE term whose absolute parameter value depends amount unit binding flux MMAE), shape* simulated MMAE concentration-time profile matches paper’s Figure 3d (Tmax ~1-2 days, slow elimination). original NONMEM control stream available disambiguate amount-unit convention DAR-related scaling, treat simulated MMAE absolute concentrations informative shape ; use absolute exposure projections. simulated ADC concentrations exposures reliable shape absolute magnitude. Note AUC values reported Suri 2018 paper’s “ug-h/mL” / “ng-h/mL” notation; PKNCA auclast output shares units input concentrations ug/mL ng/mL.

title: Suri_2018_brentuximab
text: following modeling choices made Suri 2018 paper / supplement specify , reflect formatting quirks source documentation needed re-derivation. Reference values Table 1 means. supplement 1 “Covariate model development” paragraph states continuous covariates “normalized population mean,” population means come Table 1’s overall column (BSA 1.865 m2, albumin 36.81 g/L, total bilirubin 7.78 umol/L, serum creatinine 72.4 umol/L). packaged model uses exact Table 1 means normalization references rather rounded values (1.8 m2, 37 40 g/L, etc.) used related Mould-lab models Zhou_2025_brentuximab. downstream simulation needs rounded references, override wrapper rather editing model file. Compartment naming. model names seven ODE states central, peripheral1, peripheral2, central_mmae, peripheral1_mmae, target, lag. parent (ADC) compartments use canonical central / peripheral1 / peripheral2 set; MMAE compartments use canonical metabolite-suffix convention (central_mmae, peripheral1_mmae). precedent applies Li_2017_brentuximab, Lu_2014_trastuzumabemtansine, Zhou_2025_brentuximab. canonical target compartment naming-conventions.md reused irreversibly-depletable hypothetical-target binding pool described paper page 994. Source-paper unit error Kd / ALFM / Klag. main paper’s Results page 994 prints three rate constants units L/h; supplement Table S3 column header Figure 1b abbreviations list use 1/h (dimensionally consistent ODE structure). packaged model uses supplement / Figure 1b unit. See Errata section . Three-state ADA encoding. Suri 2018 splits binary ADA-positivity effect three mutually exclusive indicators (ADA-positive newer- assay study, ADA-positive older-assay study, ADA-missing) older newer assays differed substantially sensitivity drug tolerance. packaged model uses three mutually exclusive indicators: ADA_POS (positive modern/newer assay — general-scope canonical used models), ADA_POSOLD (positive older assay), ADA_MISSING (ADA result recorded). ADA-negative reference (three indicators 0). Future BV models run single assay generation just use standard ADA_POS. MMAE submodel coupled, sequential. Suri 2018 Methods page 993 notes “PK model MMAE included link ADC elimination using individual parameter estimates ADC model predict ADC concentrations MMAE model” — .e., MMAE model fit individual ADC parameters held fixed (sequential / IPP estimation). estimation convenience change simulation: packaged model ADC MMAE compartments integrated together single ODE system, subject’s ADC etas (etalcl, etalvc) MMAE etas (etalcl_mmae, etalvc_mmae) sampled independently. Dose-capping 180 mg. licensed regimen caps per-dose mass 180 mg patients weighing >100 kg (Suri 2018 Methods, “Tables descriptive statistics… using brentuximab vedotin 1.8 mg/kg (maximum 180 mg patients weighing >100 kg)”). vignette honors via pmin(dose_mg_per_kg * wt, 180) make_cohort(). Doses converted umol. Mould-lab framework — also used Zhou_2025_brentuximab — uses molar amounts (AMT UM -> umol; DV UM -> umol/L). binding term kd * target * central ADC -> MMAE conversion equation sensitive amount unit switching mg without rescaling kd corrupt conversion-flux magnitude. mg-based doses converted vignette via amt_umol = dose_mg / MW_BV_kDa (MW_BV approx 153.4 kDa). Absolute MMAE scale unverified. discussed “Comparison published exposures” section , model implements Suri 2018’s parameter values Mould-lab framework ODE structure faithfully, absolute simulated MMAE concentration scale match paper’s reported 600 ngh/mL cycle-3 AUC. underlying ambiguity whether paper’s Kd * Target * central term intended operate amount (umol ADC) mass (mg ADC), whether DAR-sized stoichiometric factor implicit. Neither unit convention reproduces absolute MMAE exposure. ADC submodel shape* MMAE profile (Tmax ~1-2 days, slow elimination matching paper’s Figure 3d) correctly reproduced. Resolving requires access original Suri 2018 NONMEM control stream, supplements disk. -diagonal IIV. Suri 2018 supplement Table S1 / S3 report -diagonal correlations IIV terms, supplement 1 states “highest feasible number variance terms added OMEGA matrix. possible, diagonal elements describing correlation added well.” packaged model uses fully diagonal IIV block four estimated etas (etalcl, etalvc, etalcl_mmae, etalvc_mmae); add block correlations source NONMEM control stream later obtained. Cycle-4 NCA steady-state comparison. paper presents steady-state AUC simulations 3 dosing cycles (Methods, “Tables descriptive statistics AUC cycle, three cycles, created”). vignette PKNCA block computes quantity cycle 4 (interval start = 3 * cycle_h_q3w, end = 4 * cycle_h_q3w) — cycle 3 - 4 system steady state ADC MMAE. pediatric simulation. pooled cohort includes small minority pediatric patients (youngest age 12 phase II HL study), typical-value covariates virtual cohort overall Table 1 means, adult (median age 37, weight 76.5 kg). Pediatric predictions use dedicated Zhou_2025_brentuximab model instead.

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: Citation: Takahashi T, Al-Kofahi M, Jaber M, Bratrude B, Betz K, Suessmuth Y, Yu , Neuberg DS, Choi SW, Davis J, Duncan C, Giller R, Grimley M, Harris AC, Jacobsohn D, Lalefar N, Farhadfar N, Pulsipher MA, Shenoy S, Petrovic , Schultz KR, Yanik GA, Blazar BR, Horan JT, Watkins B, Langston , Qayed M, Kean LS. Higher abatacept exposure transplant decreases acute GVHD risk without increasing adverse events. Blood. 2023 Aug 24;142(8):700-710. doi:10.1182/blood.2023020035 Description: Two-compartment IV population PK model abatacept (CTLA4-Ig Fc-fusion) pooled across 685 adult/pediatric patients rheumatoid arthritis polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis adult/pediatric patients receiving allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation ABA2 trial (Takahashi 2023). Linear elimination, allometric weight scaling CL/Vc/Vp/Q estimated exponents, three-level cohort categorical (RA/JIA reference, ABA2 HLA 7/8, ABA2 HLA 8/8) CL Vc. Article: Blood 142(8):700-710

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: Takahashi 2023 fit population PK model 4872 abatacept serum concentrations 685 patients pooled across eight studies (Supplemental Tables 1 2): Seven adult rheumatoid arthritis (RA) one pediatric polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis (pJIA) studies (n = 570; studies IM103002, IM101100, IM101101, IM101102, IM101029, IM101031, IM101033). Adult RA Q4W maintenance dosing approximated 10 mg/kg 500 / 750 / 1000 mg vial-rounded weight tiers; pJIA study used 10 mg/kg every 4 weeks (lead-dosing). ABA2 hematopoietic-cell-transplant (HCT) trial (IM101311 / NCT01743131) HLA 7/8 (one-allele-mismatched donor) cohort (n = 42). ABA2 HLA 8/8 (allele-matched donor) cohort (n = 73). ABA2 patients >= 6 years old received IV abatacept 10 mg/kg (maximum 1000 mg) 1 hour days -1, +5, +14, +28 relative graft. Median age 45 years (range 6 - 84), median weight 67.9 kg (range 14.4 - 186.8), 67% female, 85% White (Supplemental Table 1). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Takahashi_2023_abatacept")$population.
