title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: build 200-subject virtual cohort whose three-cohort distribution roughly matches published proportions (RA/JIA n = 100, ABA2 7/8 n = 50, ABA2 8/8 n = 50; ABA2 cohorts -sampled relative paper’s n = 42 n = 73 trough-distribution figures enough subjects per cohort stable summary statistics). Body weights drawn normal distribution centered cohort’s published median (Supplemental Table 1), truncated observed range. Doses administered per ABA2 trial protocol HCT cohorts single 10 mg/kg vial-rounded IV maintenance dose RA/JIA reference cohort (see Assumptions section adult-RA-single-dose simplification). Virtual cohort weight distribution study group.
code: set.seed(2023)  n_per_cohort <- c("RA/JIA" = 100L,                   "ABA2 HLA 7/8" = 50L,                   "ABA2 HLA 8/8" = 50L)  cohort_meta <- tibble::tribble(   ~cohort,         ~wt_median, ~wt_min, ~wt_max,   "RA/JIA",        67.9,       30,      150,   "ABA2 HLA 7/8",  74.8,       22,      127.3,   "ABA2 HLA 8/8",  71.3,       23.1,    142.7 )  pop <- purrr::map2_dfr(   names(n_per_cohort), as.integer(n_per_cohort),   function(coh, n) {     meta <- dplyr::filter(cohort_meta, cohort == coh)     tibble(       cohort = coh,       sub_id = seq_len(n),       WT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n, mean = meta$wt_median, sd = 18),                      meta$wt_min), meta$wt_max)     )   } ) |>   mutate(     id = dplyr::row_number(),     STUDY_ABA2_HLA78 = as.integer(cohort == "ABA2 HLA 7/8"),     STUDY_ABA2_HLA88 = as.integer(cohort == "ABA2 HLA 8/8")   ) |>   select(id, cohort, WT, STUDY_ABA2_HLA78, STUDY_ABA2_HLA88)  knitr::kable(   pop |> group_by(cohort) |>     summarise(n = dplyr::n(),               wt_median = round(median(WT), 1),               wt_min = round(min(WT), 1),               wt_max = round(max(WT), 1),               .groups = "drop"),   caption = "Virtual cohort weight distribution by study group." )

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: ABA2 dosing schedule per Supplemental Table 2 footnote: 10 mg/kg (maximum 1000 mg) 1 hour days -1, +5, +14, +28. anchor simulation time = 0 first ABA2 dose (day -1 graft infusion) day +5 trough time = 5 - (-1) = 6 days first dose. (Note: source paper Ctrough_1 trough sample drawn immediately second dose day +5, .e., 6 days day -1 first dose.) RA/JIA cohort administer single 10 mg/kg vial-rounded maintenance dose time = 0 characterize post-first-dose profile window.
code: # Weight-tier dosing approximating 10 mg/kg with vial-rounding (per # Supplemental Table 2 footnote): 500 mg if WT < 60, 750 mg if 60 <= WT # <= 100, 1000 mg if WT > 100. ABA2 caps at 1000 mg by protocol. abatacept_dose_mg <- function(wt_kg) {   dplyr::case_when(     wt_kg < 60                 ~ 500,     wt_kg >= 60 & wt_kg <= 100 ~ 750,     wt_kg > 100                ~ 1000   ) } infusion_h <- 1            # hours infusion_d <- infusion_h / 24  # ABA2 schedule expressed relative to time = 0 (= day -1 graft dose). aba2_dose_times <- c(0, 6, 15, 29)   # day -1, +5, +14, +28 -> 0, 6, 15, 29  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 1, by = 1/24),                 # hourly through day 1   seq(1, 30, by = 0.25),                # 6-h sampling through day 30   c(5.99, 6 - 1/1440)                   # immediately pre-dose 2 (Ctrough_1) )))  aba2_subjects <- pop |>   filter(cohort %in% c("ABA2 HLA 7/8", "ABA2 HLA 8/8")) |>   mutate(dose_mg = abatacept_dose_mg(WT))  raj_subjects <- pop |>   filter(cohort == "RA/JIA") |>   mutate(dose_mg = abatacept_dose_mg(WT))  dose_rows_aba2 <- aba2_subjects |>   tidyr::crossing(dose_idx = seq_along(aba2_dose_times)) |>   transmute(     id, cohort, WT, STUDY_ABA2_HLA78, STUDY_ABA2_HLA88,     time = aba2_dose_times[dose_idx],     amt  = dose_mg,     rate = dose_mg / infusion_d,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "central",     dv   = NA_real_   )  dose_rows_raj <- raj_subjects |>   transmute(     id, cohort, WT, STUDY_ABA2_HLA78, STUDY_ABA2_HLA88,     time = 0,     amt  = dose_mg,     rate = dose_mg / infusion_d,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "central",     dv   = NA_real_   )  obs_rows <- pop |>   select(id, cohort, WT, STUDY_ABA2_HLA78, STUDY_ABA2_HLA88) |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   mutate(amt = NA_real_, rate = NA_real_, evid = 0L,          cmt = NA_character_, dv = NA_real_)  events <- bind_rows(dose_rows_aba2, dose_rows_raj, obs_rows) |>   arrange(id, time, desc(evid))  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(events |> select(id, time, evid) |> distinct()))

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
code: mod <- readModelDb("Takahashi_2023_abatacept")  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   object     = mod,   events     = events,   keep       = c("cohort", "WT"),   returnType = "data.frame" ) |>   as_tibble()

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: Supplemental Figure 6 Takahashi 2023 shows distribution Ctrough_1 across developmental validation cohorts (ABA2 patients ). Supplemental Figure 8 reports four Ctrough_1 quartiles developmental cohort: Q1 = 20-32 ug/mL, Q2 = 33-43 ug/mL, Q3 = 44-55 ug/mL, Q4 = 56-95 ug/mL. extract simulated trough time-point immediately pre-dose 2 (Ctrough_1 definition) two ABA2 cohorts. Simulated Ctrough_1 distribution ABA2 cohort (median, Q1, Q3, % >= 39 ug/mL threshold).  paper reports ~60% ABA2 patients attained Ctrough_1 >= 39 ug/mL, quartile bins 20-32, 33-43, 44-55, 56-95 ug/mL developmental cohort. simulated medians quartile ranges fall neighborhood; cohort--cohort deviation reflects differential CL/Vc ratios HLA 7/8 vs 8/8 cohorts (HLA 7/8 30% lower CL RA/JIA reference, HLA 8/8 9% lower CL 32% higher Vc).
code: sim_ctrough1 <- sim |>   filter(cohort %in% c("ABA2 HLA 7/8", "ABA2 HLA 8/8"),          time > 5.99, time < 6) |>   group_by(id) |>   slice_max(time, n = 1) |>   ungroup() |>   select(id, cohort, WT, ctrough1 = Cc)  ctrough1_summary <- sim_ctrough1 |>   group_by(cohort) |>   summarise(     n              = dplyr::n(),     median_ctrough = round(median(ctrough1), 1),     q1             = round(quantile(ctrough1, 0.25), 1),     q3             = round(quantile(ctrough1, 0.75), 1),     pct_ge_39      = round(mean(ctrough1 >= 39) * 100, 1),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(   ctrough1_summary,   caption = "Simulated Ctrough_1 distribution by ABA2 cohort (median, Q1, Q3, % >= 39 ug/mL threshold)." ) ggplot(sim_ctrough1, aes(x = ctrough1, fill = cohort)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 25, alpha = 0.6, position = "identity") +   geom_vline(xintercept = 39, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +   facet_wrap(~cohort, ncol = 1) +   labs(     x       = "Ctrough_1 (ug/mL)",     y       = "Number of subjects",     title   = "Simulated Ctrough_1 distribution by ABA2 cohort",     caption = "Replicates Supplemental Figure 6 of Takahashi 2023.\nDashed line = 39 ug/mL exposure-response threshold (Figure 1)."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
code: profile <- sim |>   filter(time >= 0, time <= 30, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   group_by(cohort, time) |>   summarise(     median = median(Cc),     q05    = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     q95    = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(profile, aes(x = time, y = median, colour = cohort, fill = cohort)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q05, ymax = q95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~cohort) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time since first dose (days)",     y       = "Abatacept serum concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     colour  = "Cohort",     fill    = "Cohort",     title   = "Simulated abatacept concentration-time profiles by cohort",     caption = "RA/JIA: single 10 mg/kg vial-rounded dose at t = 0.\nABA2: 10 mg/kg (max 1000 mg) IV at t = 0, 6, 15, 29 (= days -1, +5, +14, +28)."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, AUC(0,inf), terminal half-life per subject per cohort. PKNCA formula groups concentrations cohort + id summaries roll per-cohort. restrict NCA window post-first-dose interval (time <= 6 days, .e., dose 1 dose 2 ABA2) AUC1 / Cmax_1 / Ctrough_1 set paper analyzed exposure-response analysis (Supplemental Figure 14). Simulated NCA summaries (post-first-dose 0-6 day window) cohort.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   filter(time >= 0, time <= 6, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   select(id, time, Cc, cohort)  nca_dose <- bind_rows(dose_rows_aba2, dose_rows_raj) |>   filter(time == 0) |>   transmute(id, time, amt, cohort) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | cohort + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | cohort + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = 6,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  sim_nca <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) |>   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "auclast", "half.life")) |>   group_by(cohort, PPTESTCD) |>   summarise(     mean = mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     sd   = sd(PPORRES,   na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(sim_nca, digits = 3,              caption = "Simulated NCA summaries (post-first-dose 0-6 day window) by cohort.")

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: Takahashi 2023 publish per-cohort NCA table; exposure-response analysis (Supplemental Figure 14) reports correlations among Ctrough_1, Cmax_1, AUC1 ABA2 developmental cohort give summary statistics Cmax_1 AUC1. Supplemental Figure 8 reports Ctrough_1 quartile bins 20-32 / 33-43 / 44-55 / 56-95 ug/mL, published exposure-distribution numbers can verify. Published Ctrough_1 quartile bins (developmental cohort, Takahashi 2023 Supplemental Figure 8). Simulated Ctrough_1 quartiles across ABA2 cohorts pooled. simulated 25th, 50th, 75th percentiles Ctrough_1 fall within published quartile-bin ranges (Q1 lower edge = 20 Q3 upper edge = 55 ug/mL). Ctrough_1 sensitive early-phase distribution kinetics (Vc Q) cohort effect CL Vc; close match validates packaged model reproduces exposure-distribution claims paper (.e., ~60% target attainment Ctrough_1 >= 39 ug/mL).
code: published_ctrough1_quartiles <- tibble::tribble(   ~quartile, ~lower, ~upper,   "Q1",      20,     32,   "Q2",      33,     43,   "Q3",      44,     55,   "Q4",      56,     95 )  simulated_quartiles <- sim_ctrough1 |>   filter(cohort %in% c("ABA2 HLA 7/8", "ABA2 HLA 8/8")) |>   pull(ctrough1) |>   quantile(c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75)) |>   round(1)  knitr::kable(published_ctrough1_quartiles,              caption = "Published Ctrough_1 quartile bins (developmental cohort, Takahashi 2023 Supplemental Figure 8).") knitr::kable(   tibble(     quartile = c("Q1 (25th percentile)",                  "Median",                  "Q3 (75th percentile)"),     simulated_ctrough1 = simulated_quartiles   ),   caption = "Simulated Ctrough_1 quartiles across both ABA2 cohorts pooled." )

title: Takahashi_2023_abatacept
text: 50 ABA2 8/8 virtual subjects, oversampling ABA2 cohorts relative published n = 42 n = 73 trough histograms stable summary statistics. cohort weight medians ranges come Supplemental Table 1. Body-weight distribution. Individual weights published; draw per-cohort normal distribution centered Supplemental-Table-1 median SD = 18 kg, truncated observed weight range. Pediatric subjects RA/JIA cohort (study IM101033 enrolled JIA patients aged 6-17) represented lower tail truncated normal (minimum 30 kg). RA/JIA dosing simplification. RA/JIA cohort simulated single 10 mg/kg vial-rounded IV dose t = 0 focus comparison post-first-dose window (0-6 days) used Ctrough_1, Cmax_1, AUC1 Supplemental Figure 14. actual RA/JIA studies used multi-dose Q4W maintenance regimens, multi-dose Cmax / AUC part paper’s exposure-response analysis. Sex, race, eGFR, albumin. covariates tested Takahashi 2023’s full-model step (Supplemental Table 3) removed final-model step clinically meaningful (associated < 25% PK change 5th-95th covariate percentile range). included packaged model therefore virtual cohort. matches paper’s final-model description Supplemental Table 3 ‘Final’ row. Q IIV. Supplemental Table 4 reports IIV CL, Vc, Vp. packaged model treats Q fixed typical value (modulated WT allometric exponent). IIV correlation. Supplemental Table 4 reports IIV diagonal-(-diagonal omegas); packaged model treats CL, Vc, Vp IIVs independent. BLQ. simulated concentrations include additive (0.049 ug/mL) proportional (25% CV) residual error per published combined error model. paper’s BLQ-handling strategy (4.9% observations retained face value) affect simulation. Concentration units. Dose mg, volumes L give central / vc directly mg/L = ug/mL, matching paper’s reporting unit (39 ug/mL Ctrough_1 threshold; 10 ug/mL steady-state trough target). Erratum check. web search across Blood / PubMed / publisher feeds found published erratum correction doi:10.1182/blood.2023020035 2026-04-27.

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Citation: Takeuchi T, Chino Y, Mano Y, Kawanishi M, Sato Y, Uchida S, Tanaka Y. Population Pharmacokinetics Ozoralizumab Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. J Clin Pharmacol. 2024;64(4):418-427. doi:10.1002/jcph.2380 Description: One-compartment population PK model first-order absorption subcutaneous ozoralizumab (anti-TNF VHH NANOBODY) Japanese patients rheumatoid arthritis (Takeuchi 2023) Article: J Clin Pharmacol. 2024;64(4):418-427 (open access)

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Takeuchi 2023 pooled two clinical trials subcutaneous ozoralizumab Japanese patients rheumatoid arthritis (RA): OHZORA trial (Phase II/III, concomitant methotrexate; jRCT2080223971; n = 363) NATSUZORA trial (Phase III, without methotrexate; jRCT2080223973; n = 131). exclusion placebo-treated patients, protocol violations, BLQ records, outliers (CWRES absolute value > 6), analysis dataset comprised 3412 plasma concentrations 494 patients dosed 30 80 mg every 4 weeks 52 weeks. Baseline demographics (Table 2): mean age 56 (SD 12, range 21-84) years, 76% female, mean body weight 59 (SD 13, range 35-112) kg, mean baseline albumin 3.9 (SD 0.3) g/dL, mean baseline eGFR 88 (SD 20, range 35-174) mL/min/1.73 m^2 (Japanese formula), mean CDAI 32 (SD 11), mean hs-CRP 1.6 (SD 2.0) mg/dL. 363 494 patients (74%) received concomitant methotrexate; 185 494 (37%) developed antidrug antibodies. final-model equation centres body weight 56.65 kg eGFR 85.95 mL/min/1.73 m^2 — population medians, sit right-skewed means. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab")$population.

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Takeuchi 2023 Table 3 (p. 421) final-model equations p. 422.

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Unit conversion CL/F. Takeuchi 2023 reports CL/F mL/h. model file converts L/h (lcl <- log(9.20 / 1000)) Vd/F L, ODE term cl/vc units 1/h Cc = central / vc yields mg/L = ug/mL. Multiplicative (1 + theta * cov) covariate form. Takeuchi parameterises binary covariates P = theta1 * (1 + theta3 * cov) (Methods p. 420), rather common exp(theta * cov). model file uses paper’s form unchanged. Sex / MTX reference-category flips. Takeuchi encodes Male = 1 female TVCL/TVVd reference, MTX = 1 -MTX TVCL reference. preserve published TVCL = 9.20 mL/h TVVd = 4.91 L storing data canonical SEXF (1 = female) CONMED_MTX (1 = MTX) columns, model applies effects (1 + e_male_* * (1 - SEXF)) (1 + e_nomtx_cl * (1 - CONMED_MTX)). published coefficient values unchanged. eGFR formula. Takeuchi computes eGFR Japanese formula 194 * Scr^-1.094 * age^-0.287 * (0.739 female) (Methods p. 420), MDRD CKD-EPI. canonical CRCL column carries precomputed eGFR value mL/min/1.73 m^2. Ka IIV. paper fixed omega^2_Ka 0 high shrinkage (Methods p. 421). model file omits etalka accordingly; Ka typical value every individual. Proportional residual error. Takeuchi reports sigma^2 = 0.0347 (NONMEM proportional Y = IPRED * (1 + EPS), EPS ~ N(0, sigma^2)). nlmixr2’s prop(propSd) notation propSd = sqrt(sigma^2) = 0.186.

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Takeuchi 2023 Table 2 demographics. Subject-level observed data publicly released.
code: set.seed(20260428) n_subj <- 150  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id         = seq_len(n_subj),   WT         = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(56.65), 0.20), 35, 112)),   SEXF       = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.76),   CRCL       = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 88, sd = 20), 35, 174)),   CONMED_MTX = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.74),   ADA_POS    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.37) ) tau     <- 4 * 7 * 24       # q4w dosing interval, in hours (672 h) n_doses <- 6                # 6 monthly doses ~= 5 half-lives, last cycle at SS dose_h  <- seq(0, tau * (n_doses - 1), by = tau)  ss_start <- tau * (n_doses - 1) ss_end   <- ss_start + tau  # Observation grid: dense early (first 2 cycles) for absorption-phase # resolution and dense over the steady-state cycle for NCA. Coarser bridge # in between to keep the event table small enough for a 5-minute render. obs_h <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 2 * tau, by = 24),                # daily through first two cycles   dose_h + 1, dose_h + 6, dose_h + 24,     # near-dose absorption resolution   seq(2 * tau, ss_start, by = 7 * 24),     # weekly bridge   seq(ss_start, ss_end, by = 12)           # 12-hour grid for NCA cycle )))  ev_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_h) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 30, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L) ev_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_h) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                 WT, SEXF, CRCL, CONMED_MTX, ADA_POS)

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' keep_cols <- c("WT", "SEXF", "CRCL", "CONMED_MTX", "ADA_POS")  sim <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = keep_cols))

what: Concentration-time profile over the dosing horizon
title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Trough-sampling original trials precludes direct dense concentration-time replication source figures. block shows simulated 5/50/95th percentile profile 6-cycle dosing horizon labelled 30 mg q4w regimen, summarised per-day grid.
code: profile <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(profile, aes(time / 24, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, colour = "steelblue") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "Ozoralizumab Cc (ug/mL)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profile: 30 mg SC q4w",     caption = paste0("Virtual RA cohort (N = ", n_subj, "); ", n_doses,                      " monthly doses; final cycle at steady state.")   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
code: ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes((time - ss_start) / 24, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time within dosing interval (days)",     y = "Ozoralizumab Cc (ug/mL)",     title = "Steady-state q4w cycle (cycle 6 of 6)",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval over the final 28-day interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: NCA steady-state q4w cycle (cycle 6). Time within cycle re-zeroed PKNCA computes per-interval Cmax, Cmin, AUC, Cavg.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start, treatment = "30mg_Q4W") |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 30, treatment = "30mg_Q4W") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id,                              concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "h") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = tau,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) nca_summary #>  Interval Start Interval End treatment   N AUClast (h*ug/mL) Cmax (ug/mL) #>               0          672  30mg_Q4W 150       1420 [22.2]  4.00 [16.2] #>  Cmin (ug/mL)          Tmax (h) Cav (ug/mL) #>  0.683 [49.6] 72.0 [60.0, 84.0] 2.11 [22.2] #>  #> Caption: AUClast, Cmax, Cmin, Cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; Tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

what: Typical-patient steady-state exposures vs published Tables S1 / S2
title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Takeuchi 2023 Supplemental Tables S1 S2 report median (95% prediction interval) Cmax, AUCtau, Ctrough steady state typical patient several covariate-perturbed sub-cohorts (1000 simulations ). block repeats typical-patient simulation fixing IIV zero (rxode2::zeroRe()) reading maximum, minimum, trapezoidal AUC final dosing interval. deterministic typical-patient check; reproduce 95% prediction interval — requires stochastic block . Typical-patient (IIV zeroed) steady-state exposures vs. Takeuchi 2023 Supplemental Tables S1 / S2 medians (1000 stochastic simulations). scenarios 30 mg SC q4w. published values medians 1000 stochastic simulations IIV RUV; simulated values deterministic (typical-patient, IIV zeroed). log-normal individual parameter median equals typical value, Cmax / AUCtau / Ctrough fixed covariates two quantities agree within numerical SS-approach precision.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  ev_typ <- function(cov_row, dose_amt = 30) {   ev_dose <- cov_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_h) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   obs_t <- sort(unique(c(seq(ss_start, ss_end, by = 6), dose_h)))   ev_obs <- cov_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_t) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                   WT, SEXF, CRCL, CONMED_MTX, ADA_POS) }  ss_metrics <- function(cov_row, label) {   ev <- ev_typ(cov_row)   out <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev))   ss <- out |>     dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>     dplyr::arrange(time)   tibble::tibble(     scenario     = label,     Cmax_sim     = max(ss$Cc),     Ctrough_sim  = ss$Cc[which.max(ss$time)],     AUCtau_sim   = sum(diff(ss$time) *                        (head(ss$Cc, -1) + tail(ss$Cc, -1)) / 2)   ) }  # Typical-patient covariate template: # WT 56.7 kg, eGFR 86, female (SEXF=1), MTX yes (CONMED_MTX=1), ADA negative (=0) typical_cov <- tibble::tibble(id = 1L, WT = 56.7, SEXF = 1L, CRCL = 86,                               CONMED_MTX = 1L, ADA_POS = 0L)  scenarios <- dplyr::bind_rows(   ss_metrics(typical_cov,                            "Typical patient"),   ss_metrics(typical_cov |> dplyr::mutate(WT = 42.5),  "WT 42.5 kg"),   ss_metrics(typical_cov |> dplyr::mutate(WT = 83.2),  "WT 83.2 kg"),   ss_metrics(typical_cov |> dplyr::mutate(SEXF = 0L),  "Male"),   ss_metrics(typical_cov |> dplyr::mutate(CRCL = 58),  "eGFR 58 mL/min/1.73 m^2"),   ss_metrics(typical_cov |> dplyr::mutate(CRCL = 121), "eGFR 121 mL/min/1.73 m^2"),   ss_metrics(typical_cov |> dplyr::mutate(CONMED_MTX = 0L), "Without MTX"),   ss_metrics(typical_cov |> dplyr::mutate(ADA_POS = 1L),    "ADA-positive") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc'  # Published medians from Takeuchi 2023 Supplemental Tables S1 / S2. published <- tibble::tribble(   ~scenario,                   ~Cmax_pub, ~AUCtau_pub, ~Ctrough_pub,   "Typical patient",                7.28,        3230,         2.51,   "WT 42.5 kg",                     8.93,        4110,         3.45,   "WT 83.2 kg",                     5.59,        2320,         1.60,   "Male",                           6.55,        2930,         2.31,   "eGFR 58 mL/min/1.73 m^2",        7.60,        3430,         2.80,   "eGFR 121 mL/min/1.73 m^2",       7.02,        3010,         2.24,   "Without MTX",                    6.84,        2880,         2.06,   "ADA-positive",                   6.95,        2980,         2.23 )  comparison <- dplyr::left_join(scenarios, published, by = "scenario") |>   dplyr::mutate(     Cmax_pct_diff    = 100 * (Cmax_sim    - Cmax_pub)    / Cmax_pub,     AUCtau_pct_diff  = 100 * (AUCtau_sim  - AUCtau_pub)  / AUCtau_pub,     Ctrough_pct_diff = 100 * (Ctrough_sim - Ctrough_pub) / Ctrough_pub   ) |>   dplyr::select(scenario,                 Cmax_pub, Cmax_sim, Cmax_pct_diff,                 AUCtau_pub, AUCtau_sim, AUCtau_pct_diff,                 Ctrough_pub, Ctrough_sim, Ctrough_pct_diff)  knitr::kable(   comparison, digits = c(0, 2, 2, 1, 0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 1),   caption = paste("Typical-patient (IIV zeroed) steady-state exposures vs.",                   "Takeuchi 2023 Supplemental Tables S1 / S2 medians (1000",                   "stochastic simulations). All scenarios at 30 mg SC q4w.") )

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Takeuchi 2023 Figure 3 plots ratio CL/F, Vd/F, Cmax, AUCtau covariate-perturbed patient relative typical patient. forest points deterministic (covariate effects typical values), without stochastic 90% prediction interval source plot overlays.
code: forest <- comparison |>   dplyr::filter(scenario != "Typical patient") |>   dplyr::mutate(     Cmax_ratio    = Cmax_sim    / comparison$Cmax_sim[1],     AUCtau_ratio  = AUCtau_sim  / comparison$AUCtau_sim[1],     Ctrough_ratio = Ctrough_sim / comparison$Ctrough_sim[1]   ) |>   dplyr::select(scenario, Cmax_ratio, AUCtau_ratio, Ctrough_ratio) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(-scenario, names_to = "metric", values_to = "ratio") |>   dplyr::mutate(     metric = factor(metric,                     levels = c("Cmax_ratio", "AUCtau_ratio", "Ctrough_ratio"),                     labels = c("Cmax", "AUCtau", "Ctrough")),     scenario = factor(scenario, levels = rev(unique(scenario)))   )  ggplot(forest, aes(ratio, scenario)) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   geom_point(size = 2.4) +   facet_wrap(~ metric, nrow = 1) +   labs(     x = "Ratio vs typical patient",     y = NULL,     title = "Steady-state exposure ratios at 30 mg q4w",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3 of Takeuchi 2023 (point estimates only)."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: errata, corrigenda, correction notices identified extraction time (Wiley Online Library landing page; PubMed correction-notice search; Google Scholar). full-model equations p. 422 internally consistent Table 3 (p. 421) Supplemental Tables S1-S3 typical-patient simulation .

title: Takeuchi_2023_ozoralizumab
text: Sex / MTX reference-category flips. Takeuchi encodes Male = 1 female-reference TVCL/TVVd, MTX = 1 -MTX-reference TVCL. Stored canonical SEXF (1 = female) CONMED_MTX (1 = MTX), effects applied (1 + e_male_* * (1 - SEXF)) (1 + e_nomtx_cl * (1 - CONMED_MTX)) published TVCL = 9.20 mL/h TVVd = 4.91 L preserved. published coefficient signs magnitudes unchanged. eGFR formula. covariate-column register’s canonical CRCL reference values documented MDRD (US) CKD-EPI conventions. Takeuchi uses Japanese eGFR formula 194 * Scr^-1.094 * age^-0.287 * (0.739 female) reference value 85.95 mL/min/1.73 m^2. Users applying model non-Japanese cohort supply CRCL computed Japanese formula document substitution. Ka IIV. omega^2_Ka fixed 0 published model high shrinkage. Ka therefore typical value every simulated subject. faithful paper means individual- level absorption variability captured. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. virtual cohort constructed match published marginal Table 2 demographics, joint distributions: WT lognormal centred population median (56.65 kg) truncated [35, 112] kg; SEXF Bernoulli(0.76); CRCL N(88, 20) truncated [35, 174]; CONMED_MTX Bernoulli(0.74); ADA_POS Bernoulli(0.37). Covariate correlations (e.g., albumin / disease activity / age co-variation) modelled Takeuchi report . subject-level observed data. OHZORA / NATSUZORA subject-level data held Taisho Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. publicly released. simulated comparison Tables S1 / S2 substitutes true VPC. Typical-patient comparison. published Tables S1 / S2 report medians 1000 stochastic simulations IIV RUV; comparison block uses deterministic typical-patient simulation (zeroRe()). typical-patient log-normal parameterisation median equals typical value, comparison appropriate; 95% prediction interval paper reports reproduced .

what: Risankizumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Simulate risankizumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Thakre et al. (2022) patients active psoriatic arthritis (PsA). source paper pooled data five studies (one phase 1 healthy- participant study, one phase 2 dose-ranging study PsA open-label extension, two pivotal phase 3 PsA studies, KEEPsAKE 1 KEEPsAKE 2) — 3631 concentrations 1527 individuals. model two-compartment model first-order subcutaneous absorption first-order elimination. final covariate set body weight, age, serum albumin, serum creatinine, baseline high-sensitivity C-reactive protein (CRP) clearance, plus body weight central volume distribution. ADA titers CL body weight V2 evaluated meet retention criteria final model.

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: PsA phase 2/3 trial-like covariate distributions. source paper publish per-subject data, distributions approximate typical PsA populations centered simulated individual parameters bracket paper’s reference covariates.
code: set.seed(2022) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID    = seq_len(n_subj),   WT    = rlnorm(n_subj, log(85), 0.22),  # median ~85 kg   AGE   = round(pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 51, 12), 18), 80)),   ALB   = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 43, 3.5), 30), 52),     # units g/L   CREAT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 72, 16), 40), 140),     # units umol/L   CRP   = rlnorm(n_subj, log(6), 1.1)                     # mg/L, skewed right (hs-CRP) )

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Approved PsA clinical regimen: 150 mg SC weeks 0 4, every 12 weeks (q12w) thereafter. Simulate first three full dosing intervals (weeks 0-28), matching Thakre 2022 Table 2.
code: dose_weeks <- c(0, 4, 16, 28)    # 4 SC doses through week 28 dose_times <- dose_weeks * 7     # convert to days  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,   28,  by = 0.5),   seq(28,  196, by = 2) )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 150,       # mg SC     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 1,         # depot     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

what: Per dosing-interval exposures vs Thakre 2022 Table 2
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Compute Cmax, Ctrough, AUC first three full dosing intervals (weeks 0-4, 4-16, 16-28) compare Thakre 2022 Table 2. Simulated per-interval exposures (compare Thakre 2022 Table 2: interval-1 Cmax median 9.40, interval-2 Cmax median 14.1, interval-3 Cmax median 11.0 ug/mL; interval-1 Ctrough median 5.68, interval-2 Ctrough median 1.93, interval-3 Ctrough median 1.52 ug/mL; interval AUC medians 207, 565, 439 ug*day/mL).
code: intervals <- tribble(   ~label,    ~start_wk, ~end_wk,   "1 (0-4)",   0,   4,   "2 (4-16)",  4,  16,   "3 (16-28)", 16, 28 )  ivl_summary <- intervals %>%   rowwise() %>%   do({     row <- .     sub <- sim %>%       filter(time >= row$start_wk * 7, time <= row$end_wk * 7, Cc > 0)     per_id <- sub %>%       group_by(id) %>%       summarise(         Cmax    = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),         Ctrough = Cc[which.max(time)],         AUCtau  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),         .groups = "drop"       )     tibble(       interval = row$label,       Cmax_median    = median(per_id$Cmax),       Cmax_mean      = mean(per_id$Cmax),       Ctrough_median = median(per_id$Ctrough),       Ctrough_mean   = mean(per_id$Ctrough),       AUCtau_median  = median(per_id$AUCtau),       AUCtau_mean    = mean(per_id$AUCtau)     )   }) %>%   bind_rows()  knitr::kable(   ivl_summary,   digits = 2,   caption = paste(     "Simulated per-interval exposures (compare with Thakre 2022 Table 2:",     "interval-1 Cmax median 9.40, interval-2 Cmax median 14.1,",     "interval-3 Cmax median 11.0 ug/mL;",     "interval-1 Ctrough median 5.68, interval-2 Ctrough median 1.93,",     "interval-3 Ctrough median 1.52 ug/mL;",     "interval AUC medians 207, 565, 439 ug*day/mL)."   ) )

title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Run PKNCA third dosing interval (weeks 16-28, steady-state maintenance), one reported Third dosing interval Thakre 2022 Table 2. PKNCA summary third dosing interval (weeks 16-28). Compare Cmax / AUClast Thakre 2022 Table 2 interval 3.
code: nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= 16 * 7, time <= 28 * 7, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = time - 16 * 7,     treatment = "risankizumab_150mg_q12w"   ) %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 150,     treatment = "risankizumab_150mg_q12w"   ) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 12 * 7,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■                               9% |  ETA: 18s #>  ■■■■■■■■■                         26% |  ETA: 14s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    43% |  ETA: 10s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■               60% |  ETA:  7s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■          77% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■     94% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste(     "PKNCA summary for the third dosing interval (weeks 16-28).",     "Compare Cmax / AUClast against Thakre 2022 Table 2 interval 3."   ) )

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Thakre 2022 publish individual PK per-subject covariate values, virtual population approximates typical PsA phase 2/3 populations rather reproducing : Weight: sampled log-normal around 85 kg median, SD 0.22 log scale. paper references 70 kg; median PsA phase-3 trials typically ~85 kg. Age: normal around 51 years, SD 12, clipped 18-80. paper references 52 years. Serum albumin: normal around 43 g/L, SD 3.5, clipped 30-52 g/L (reference 45 g/L). Serum creatinine: normal around 72 umol/L, SD 16, clipped 40-140 umol/L (reference 70.73 umol/L). CRP (hs-CRP assay): log-normal around median 6 mg/L (reference 5.21 mg/L). hs-CRP highly skewed PsA. correlation continuous covariates imposed; paper report joint distributions. Dosing regimen uses approved PsA label (150 mg SC weeks 0 4, every 12 weeks). Thakre 2022 included earlier dose-ranging phase 1 / 2 data; regimens simulated . ADA titers included evaluated meet retention criteria final model.

previous_headings: Risankizumab population PK in psoriatic arthritis
title: Thakre_2022_risankizumab
text: Model: two-compartment first-order SC absorption linear elimination. TMDD. Typical-subject CL reference covariates: 0.248 L/day. Typical-subject V1: 4.71 L. Typical-subject Vp: 4.26 L. Steady-state Vss = V1 + V2 ~ 9.0 L (reference subject; paper cites approximately 0.31 L/day 6.8 L Vc median-PsA covariates). Strongest covariates: body weight (power 0.869 CL, 1.46 V1) serum albumin (power -0.703 CL).

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Citation: Timmermann S, Hall . Population pharmacokinetics brodalumab patients moderate severe plaque psoriasis. Basic Clin Pharmacol Toxicol. 2019;125(1):16-25. doi:10.1111/bcpt.13202 Description: Two-compartment population PK model brodalumab adults moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (Timmermann 2019), first-order SC absorption, fixed bioavailability, combined linear plus Michaelis-Menten (target-mediated) elimination central compartment. Article: Basic Clin Pharmacol Toxicol. 2019;125(1):16-25 (open access via PMC6850607)

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Timmermann 2019 pooled six clinical studies (2 phase , 1 phase IIb open-label extension, 3 phase III) population PK analysis set 622 adults moderate--severe plaque psoriasis. dataset included 7725 quantifiable 2508 -LLOQ brodalumab concentrations following IV SC single / multiple doses 70-700 mg administered 1-2015 days. approved regimen 210 mg SC weeks 0, 1, 2 followed 210 mg every 2 weeks (“Q2W+1”). Baseline demographics Table 2: median age 46 years (range 18-75), 33% female (417 622 male), median body weight 87.8 kg (range 43-186), 93% Caucasian (1% Asian, 1% Black, 5% per corresponding Table 2 breakdown source), median BMI 29.6 kg/m^2 (range 16.7-66.1), median baseline PASI 17.7 (range 8.8-60.6). Healthy volunteers deliberately excluded IL-17RA expression (therefore target-mediated disposition) differs diseased healthy populations. Race tested covariate 93% patients Caucasian; anti-drug-antibody status tested due low transient positivity. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Timmermann_2019_brodalumab")$population.

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Timmermann 2019 Table 3. reference patient 90 kg adult plaque psoriasis; body-weight normalizations use WT / 90.

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Parallel linear + Michaelis-Menten clearance. Timmermann 2019 parameterizes elimination central compartment sum first-order linear clearance (CL) saturable Michaelis-Menten elimination parameters Vmax (mg/day) Km (ug/mL). Explicit ODEs used rather linCmt() non-linear term. Separate bioavailability. phase included IV (700 mg bolus) SC arms, authors identified SC bioavailability F separately fixed 54.8% covariate analysis. nlmixr2 implementation, f(depot) <- exp(lfdepot) applies F doses entering depot compartment; IV doses user places directly central scaled. Fixed Km. Km fixed 0.02 ug/mL, value carried previous analysis (Endres 2014). authors note Km effectively artificial constant non-linear term saturates well 1 ug/mL zero-order elimination describe data equivalently allow negative concentrations; Km kept pharmacological plausibility. CV% log-normal variance. Timmermann 2019 Table 3 reports -subject variability CV% linear-parameter scale, multiplicative exponential (log-normal) random effects per Section 2.3. nlmixr2 internal variance therefore omega^2 = log(CV^2 + 1). CL-V1 correlation. Table 3 reports 0.75 correlation etas CL V1, encoded 2x2 block ini() -diagonal computed correlation times sqrt(var_cl * var_vc). Combined residual error. Table 3 footnote states “Proportional additive errors given %CV standard deviation.” propSd = 0.355 (fraction) addSd = 3.00 ug/mL used directly.

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose body-weight distribution approximates Timmermann 2019 Table 2. subject-level observed data released paper. cohort receives approved labelled regimen: 210 mg SC weeks 0, 1, 2, followed 210 mg Q2W. dosing horizon extended well past time reach 90% steady state (reported 10 weeks reference 90 kg patient) ensure final Q2W cycle used NCA steady state.
code: set.seed(20260424) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 87.8, sd = 20), 43, 186)) ) tau <- 14L                          # Q2W interval (days) after the loading phase loading_days <- c(0, 7, 14)         # 210 mg at weeks 0, 1, 2 maint_days   <- seq(28, 28 + tau * 40, by = tau) dose_days    <- c(loading_days, maint_days)  obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, max(dose_days) + tau, by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3,   dose_days + 7 )))  build_events <- function(cohort, dose_amt) {   ev_dose <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   ev_obs <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT) }  events <- build_events(cohort, 210)

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Timmermann_2019_brodalumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = "WT"))

what: Figure 4 - Typical-patient concentration-time profile (210 mg Q2W+1, 90 kg)
title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Timmermann 2019 Figure 4 shows typical 90 kg patient’s predicted concentration-time profile first 24 weeks 210 mg Q2W+1 dosing together zoom first two weeks (loading phase). block reproduces typical-patient profile random effects zeroed.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typ_events <- build_events(tibble::tibble(id = 1L, WT = 90), 210) sim_typ <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = typ_events, keep = "WT")) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalvmax', 'etalq', 'etalvp'  ggplot(sim_typ |> dplyr::filter(time <= 168), aes(time, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Brodalumab Cc (ug/mL)",     title = "Typical 90 kg patient: 210 mg Q2W+1 (first 24 weeks)",     caption = "Reproduces the typical-patient trace underlying Timmermann 2019 Figure 4."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Figure 2 - VPC percentiles over the first 24 weeks (virtual cohort)
title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: simulated 5th, 50th, 95th percentiles across virtual cohort (N = 400, weight distribution per Table 2) first 168 days 210 mg Q2W+1 dosing.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0, time <= 168) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Brodalumab Cc (ug/mL)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles: 210 mg Q2W+1 (first 24 weeks)",     caption = "Virtual psoriasis cohort (N = 400). Replicates the 0-168 day panel of Timmermann 2019 Figure 2."   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Figure 3 - Fold-change in AUCss with body weight
title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Timmermann 2019 Figure 3 shows geometric-mean fold-change AUCss (relative 90 kg reference) subjects 60, 80, 90, 100, 120 kg 210 mg Q2W+1. block reproduces relationship using 200 subjects fixed weight computing AUC final Q2W dosing interval.
code: ss_start <- max(maint_days) ss_end   <- ss_start + tau wt_levels <- c(60, 80, 90, 100, 120)  make_wt_cohort <- function(wt, n_per, id_offset) {   tibble::tibble(id = id_offset + seq_len(n_per), WT = wt) }  auc_by_wt <- lapply(seq_along(wt_levels), function(i) {   wt <- wt_levels[i]   c_wt <- make_wt_cohort(wt, 200, id_offset = (i - 1L) * 1000L)   ev_wt <- build_events(c_wt, 210)   sim_wt <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = ev_wt, keep = "WT"))   ss <- sim_wt |>     dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time)   ss |>     dplyr::group_by(id) |>     dplyr::summarise(       AUCss = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),       WT = dplyr::first(WT), .groups = "drop"     ) }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()  ref_auc <- auc_by_wt |>   dplyr::filter(WT == 90) |>   dplyr::summarise(geo_mean = exp(mean(log(AUCss)))) |>   dplyr::pull(geo_mean)  fold_change <- auc_by_wt |>   dplyr::group_by(WT) |>   dplyr::summarise(     geo_mean_ratio = exp(mean(log(AUCss))) / ref_auc,     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fold_change, aes(WT, geo_mean_ratio)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(0.8, 1, 1.25), linetype = c("dotted", "solid", "dotted"),              colour = c("steelblue", "grey40", "steelblue")) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.4, 2.4)) +   labs(     x = "Body weight (kg)",     y = "Geometric-mean AUCss ratio (reference = 90 kg)",     title = "Fold-change in AUCss with body weight (210 mg Q2W+1)",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3 of Timmermann 2019; expected ~>2-fold at 60 kg and ~0.5 at 120 kg."   ) +   theme_minimal() #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis final (steady-state) Q2W dosing interval 210 mg regimen. Cmax, Tmax, Cmin/Ctrough, AUC0-tau, Cavg computed per simulated subject.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start, treatment = "210mg_Q2W") |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 210, treatment = "210mg_Q2W") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = tau,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                   48% |  ETA:  2s summary(nca_res) #>  start end treatment   N    auclast        cmax           cmin #>      0  14 210mg_Q2W 400 19.2 [137] 3.23 [99.8] 0.00288 [2610] #>                tmax        cav #>  3.00 [0.250, 6.00] 1.37 [137] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Table 4 reports Cmax week 1, Cmax steady state, tmax steady state, AUCss reference 90 kg patient dosed 210 mg Q2W+1. block simulates 500 reference (90 kg) patients extract corresponding distributional statistics compares mean, median, CV% published values. Simulated vs. published (Timmermann 2019 Table 4) secondary PK parameters reference 90 kg patient 210 mg Q2W+1. Differences > 20% investigated text , tuned away.
code: set.seed(20260424) ref_cohort <- tibble::tibble(id = seq_len(500), WT = 90) ref_events <- build_events(ref_cohort, 210) sim_ref <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = ref_events, keep = "WT"))  week1_start <- 0                    # Cmax within the first dosing week (after loading dose 1) week1_end   <- 7 week1_tbl <- sim_ref |>   dplyr::filter(time >= week1_start, time <= week1_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::summarise(Cmax_week1 = max(Cc), .groups = "drop")  ss_tbl <- sim_ref |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time) |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Cmax_ss = max(Cc),     tmax_ss = time[which.max(Cc)] - ss_start,     AUC_ss  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),     .groups = "drop"   )  geo_cv <- function(x) {   if (any(!is.finite(log(x)))) NA_real_   else sqrt(exp(sd(log(x))^2) - 1) * 100 }  summary_sim <- tibble::tibble(   Parameter = c("Cmax week 1 (ug/mL)", "Cmax SS (ug/mL)", "tmax SS (d)", "AUCss (ug*d/mL)"),   Mean_sim   = c(mean(week1_tbl$Cmax_week1), mean(ss_tbl$Cmax_ss),                  mean(ss_tbl$tmax_ss), mean(ss_tbl$AUC_ss)),   Median_sim = c(median(week1_tbl$Cmax_week1), median(ss_tbl$Cmax_ss),                  median(ss_tbl$tmax_ss), median(ss_tbl$AUC_ss)),   CV_sim     = c(geo_cv(week1_tbl$Cmax_week1), geo_cv(ss_tbl$Cmax_ss),                  NA_real_,                     geo_cv(ss_tbl$AUC_ss)) )  published <- tibble::tibble(   Parameter   = c("Cmax week 1 (ug/mL)", "Cmax SS (ug/mL)", "tmax SS (d)", "AUCss (ug*d/mL)"),   Mean_pub    = c(9.95, 20.2, NA_real_, 225),   Median_pub  = c(9.57, 16.1, 4,        160),   CV_pub      = c(50.7, 76.8, NA_real_, 92.8) )  comparison <- published |>   dplyr::left_join(summary_sim, by = "Parameter") |>   dplyr::mutate(     Mean_pct_diff   = 100 * (Mean_sim   - Mean_pub)   / Mean_pub,     Median_pct_diff = 100 * (Median_sim - Median_pub) / Median_pub   )  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,   caption = "Simulated vs. published (Timmermann 2019 Table 4) secondary PK parameters for a reference 90 kg patient on 210 mg Q2W+1. Differences > 20% are investigated in the text below, not tuned away.")

title: Timmermann_2019_brodalumab
text: Virtual-cohort weight distribution. Body weight drawn N(87.8, 20) kg truncated [43, 186] approximate Timmermann 2019 Table 2 (PK dataset median 87.8 kg, range 43-186). subject-level observed data released exact shape unknown; sensitivity VPC weight distribution expected modest weight enters model allometric exponents CL, V1, Vmax. Race, sex, age, PASI, ADA. final model include race, sex, age, baseline PASI, ADA status covariates. required simulate model therefore omitted virtual-cohort construction. Km artificial. Km fixed 0.02 ug/mL (well LLOQ 0.05 ug/mL). Timmermann 2019 notes Discussion, non-linear term effectively zero-order dosing range Km given strict pharmacological interpretation. Residual error units. Additive residual error 3.00 ug/mL taken Table 3; footnote explicitly states additive error reported standard deviation. relatively large (~15% steady-state Cmax) likely absorbs meaningful fraction BLQ-related noise, consistent M3 method used censored data. IV route. IV doses original phase trial (700 mg bolus) modelled bolus central. nlmixr2 implementation user must set cmt = "central" IV dose row (cmt = "depot" SC rows). lag time infusion handling required. AUC computation. comparison table uses linear-trapezoidal integration final simulated Q2W dosing interval. Timmermann 2019 Table 4 uses “standard non-compartmental analysis” 1000 simulated 90 kg patients; shape curve identical method difference negligible. tmax reference patient model-derived SS interval. unit conversion needed. Dose mg concentration ug/mL (equivalent mg/L), central / vc yields ug/mL directly; matches Timmermann 2019.

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Tiraboschi et al. (2025) developed population PK model amlitelimab (anti-OX40L monoclonal antibody) 439 adults (78 healthy volunteers 361 moderate--severe atopic dermatitis). structural model two-compartment disposition first-order subcutaneous absorption, absorption lag, parallel linear Michaelis-Menten (target-mediated) elimination central compartment. Allometric body-weight scaling acts linear CL, V1, V2; baseline EASI adds linear CL additively, baseline serum albumin modifies subcutaneous bioavailability (additive linear scale logit transformation). vignette simulates labelled STREAM-AD regimen (250 mg SC Q4W 500 mg SC loading dose time 0) virtual AD population verifies three qualitative claims paper: Terminal half-life amlitelimab linear PK range approximately 28 days (range 24-43 days). Body weight dominant PK covariate, driving steady-state exposure roughly 70% 40 kg subject roughly 25% 100 kg subject, relative 75 kg reference participant (Table S3). Clearance lower limit quantification (0.0469 µg/mL) dominated (~66%) target-mediated elimination, whereas ~2 µg/mL linear pathway dominates (Figure 3).

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: set.seed(2025) n_subj <- 300  # Body weight: log-normal around the population median of 74.5 kg, # truncated to the study range [40.5, 148] kg. wt_draw <- exp(rnorm(n_subj, log(74.5), 0.22)) WT <- pmin(pmax(wt_draw, 40.5), 148)  # Baseline EASI: mean 29.7, SD 11.3 in AD subjects (Table S1); # truncated at EASI >= 6 (moderate threshold for STREAM-AD eligibility) # and <= 72 (the maximum possible score). EASI <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 29.7, 11.3), 6), 72)  # Baseline serum albumin: mean 46.4 g/L, SD 3.5 g/L, truncated to [37, 57]. ALB <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 46.4, 3.5), 37), 57)  pop <- data.frame(   ID   = seq_len(n_subj),   WT   = WT,   EASI = EASI,   ALB  = ALB )  # Weight strata for the covariate sensitivity figure pop$wt_group <- cut(   pop$WT,   breaks = c(0, 50, 75, 100, Inf),   labels = c("< 50 kg", "50\u201375 kg", "75\u2013100 kg", "\u2265 100 kg"),   right  = FALSE )

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: STREAM-AD labelled regimen: 500 mg SC loading dose day 0, followed 250 mg SC Q4W week 24, weekly observations week 30.
code: dose_days <- c(0, seq(28, 168, by = 28))           # loading + Q4W for 24 weeks dose_amt  <- c(500, rep(250, length(dose_days) - 1))  obs_times_day <- seq(0, 210, by = 7)               # through week 30  d_dose <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(dose_days)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(dose_days, times = n_subj),     AMT  = rep(dose_amt,  times = n_subj),     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(obs_times_day)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(obs_times_day, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, EASI, ALB, wt_group)

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab")  set.seed(2025) sim_out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure — population concentration-time profile (median and 90% PI)
title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: sim_plot <- sim_out |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0)  d_overall <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(d_overall, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 210, by = 28)) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Amlitelimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "STREAM-AD 250 mg Q4W with 500 mg loading dose\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure — stratification by baseline body weight
title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
code: wt_map <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 0, TIME == 0) |>   select(ID, wt_group) |>   distinct()  d_wt <- sim_plot |>   left_join(wt_map, by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   group_by(time, wt_group) |>   summarise(     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(d_wt, aes(x = time, y = Q50, colour = wt_group, fill = wt_group)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 210, by = 28)) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Amlitelimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     colour = "Body weight",     fill   = "Body weight",     title  = "STREAM-AD Q4W regimen stratified by baseline body weight"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Compare steady-state week-24 exposure (AUC 4-week dosing interval, AUC4W) reference AD patient (75 kg, EASI 27.5, albumin 47 g/L) receiving 62.5 mg Q4W. Table S3 reports AUC4W changes +72%, +44%, -24%, -51% 40, 50, 100, 150 kg participants respectively. Simulated AUC4W steady state (62.5 mg Q4W) vs body weight
code: ref <- data.frame(ID = 1, WT = 75, EASI = 27.5, ALB = 47)  typ_pop <- rbind(   transform(ref, ID = 1, WT =  40),   transform(ref, ID = 2, WT =  50),   transform(ref, ID = 3, WT =  75),   # reference   transform(ref, ID = 4, WT = 100),   transform(ref, ID = 5, WT = 150) )  typ_dose_days <- seq(0, 20 * 28, by = 28)  # 20 doses for steady state typ_dose <- typ_pop[rep(seq_len(nrow(typ_pop)), each = length(typ_dose_days)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(typ_dose_days, times = nrow(typ_pop)),     AMT  = 62.5,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  typ_obs_times <- seq(20 * 28, 24 * 28, by = 0.5)  # dense sampling across one SS interval typ_obs <- typ_pop[rep(seq_len(nrow(typ_pop)), each = length(typ_obs_times)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(typ_obs_times, times = nrow(typ_pop)),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA   )  typ_ev <- bind_rows(typ_dose, typ_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, EASI, ALB)  typ_sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = typ_ev, omega = NA, sigma = NA) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  ss_start <- 20 * 28   # day 560 tau      <- 28        # dosing interval in days  typ_nca_conc <- as.data.frame(typ_sim) |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_start + tau, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - ss_start, WT_group = paste0(WT, " kg")) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_rel, Cc, WT, WT_group)  typ_nca_dose <- typ_nca_conc |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |> dplyr::slice(1) |> dplyr::ungroup() |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 62.5) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, WT_group)  conc_ss <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(typ_nca_conc, Cc ~ time | WT_group + id) dose_ss <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(typ_nca_dose, amt ~ time | WT_group + id) nca_ss  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_ss, dose_ss,   intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = tau, auclast = TRUE)))  typ_auc <- as.data.frame(nca_ss$result) |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD == "auclast") |>   dplyr::rename(AUC = PPORRES) |>   dplyr::left_join(     typ_nca_conc |> dplyr::group_by(id) |> dplyr::slice(1) |>       dplyr::select(id, WT),     by = "id"   )  ref_auc <- typ_auc$AUC[typ_auc$WT == 75] typ_auc$pct_change <- 100 * (typ_auc$AUC - ref_auc) / ref_auc knitr::kable(   typ_auc[, c("id", "WT", "AUC", "pct_change")],   digits  = c(0, 0, 2, 1),   caption = "Simulated AUC4W at steady state (62.5 mg Q4W) vs body weight" )

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Total clearance typical AD patient grid free concentrations, decomposed linear TMDD (Michaelis-Menten) components, reproducing Figure 3.
code:
cc_grid    <- 10^seq(log10(0.01), log10(100), length.out = 200) tvcll      <- 0.115            # unit: L/day (TVCLL at 75 kg ref) e_easi_cl  <- 0.00111          # unit: L/day per EASI unit vmax_mgday <- 0.0362           # unit: mg/day (TVVM; label typo "ug/day" in Table S2) km_ugmL    <- 0.0783           # unit: ug/mL (equivalently mg/L)  cl_linear <- tvcll + e_easi_cl * 27.5                 # CL for 75 kg, EASI 27.5 cl_tmdd   <- vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + cc_grid)         # unit: L/day (since VM is mg/day, KM+C in mg/L) cl_total  <- cl_linear + cl_tmdd  df_cl <- data.frame(   Cc_ugmL    = rep(cc_grid, 3),   clearance  = c(cl_linear + 0 * cc_grid, cl_tmdd, cl_total),   component  = factor(rep(c("Linear", "TMDD", "Total"), each = length(cc_grid)),                       levels = c("TMDD", "Linear", "Total")) )  ggplot(df_cl, aes(x = Cc_ugmL, y = clearance, colour = component, linetype = component)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0.0469, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x = 0.0469, y = 0.5, label = "LLOQ", hjust = -0.2, colour = "grey40") +   scale_x_log10() +   labs(     x        = "Free amlitelimab concentration (μg/mL)",     y        = "Clearance (L/day)",     colour   = "Component",     linetype = "Component",     title    = "Typical AD patient: linear vs TMDD clearance
(75 kg, EASI 27.5)"   ) +   theme_bw() cl_at_lloq <- tvcll + 0.00111 * 27.5 + vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 0.0469) tmdd_frac_at_lloq <- (vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 0.0469)) / cl_at_lloq cat(sprintf("TMDD fraction of total CL at LLOQ (0.0469 ug/mL): %.1f%%
",             100 * tmdd_frac_at_lloq)) #> TMDD fraction of total CL at LLOQ (0.0469 ug/mL): 66.5% cat(sprintf("TMDD fraction of total CL at 1 ug/mL: %.1f%%
",             100 * (vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 1)) / (tvcll + 0.00111 * 27.5 + vmax_mgday / (km_ugmL + 1)))) #> TMDD fraction of total CL at 1 ug/mL: 18.7%

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: paper reports mean terminal half-life 28 days linear PK range, individual range 24-43 days. Compute typical-value terminal half-life model’s eigenvalues population mean.
code: tvv1   <- 3.46 tvv2   <- 2.48 tvq    <- 0.569 tvcl   <- tvcll + e_easi_cl * 27.5   # linear CL at reference AD patient  k10 <- tvcl / tvv1 k12 <- tvq  / tvv1 k21 <- tvq  / tvv2  A <- k10 + k12 + k21 B <- k10 * k21 beta <- (A - sqrt(A * A - 4 * B)) / 2 t_half_day <- log(2) / beta cat(sprintf("Typical terminal half-life (linear range) = %.1f days\n", t_half_day)) #> Typical terminal half-life (linear range) = 29.6 days

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Virtual population: 300 AD patients body weight, EASI, albumin drawn Tiraboschi 2025 Table S1 distributions, truncated study ranges. Regimen: STREAM-AD labelled adult dosing - 500 mg SC loading dose, 250 mg SC every 4 weeks week 24. IIV: Simulated using published omega^2 values - V1 CL correlated block (omega^2 V1 = 0.0491, cov = 0.024, omega^2 CL = 0.0482), plus diagonal V2 (0.0693), Fsc (1.18 logit scale), ALAG (0.151), Ka (0.135). Residual error: Proportional, CV 15.8%. Unit note: Table S2 labels TVVM “ug/day”; fact mg/day, verified paper’s claim TMDD represents approximately 66% total clearance LLOQ 0.0469 ug/mL approximately 20% 1 ug/mL. Figure 3 replication terminal half-life check reproduce fractions.

title: Tiraboschi_2025_amlitelimab
text: Tiraboschi JM, Zohar S, Quartino AL, Monnier R, Coulette V, Bizot JL, Jamois C. Population Pharmacokinetic Pharmacodynamic Modeling Prediction Extended Amlitelimab Phase 3 Dosing Regimen Atopic Dermatitis. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2025;14(12):2161-2173. doi:10.1002/psp4.70121

title: Toukam_2025_biib107
text: Citation: Toukam M, Karimian N, Bame E, Xu Y. Dose Optimization BIIB107, Anti-Alpha-4 Integrin Monoclonal Antibody, Population Pharmacokinetic Pharmacodynamic Modeling. J Clin Pharmacol. 2026;66(1). doi:10.1002/jcph.70109 (PMID 41014552). Study NCT04593121. Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.70109 Trial registration: https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04593121 BIIB107 humanized aglycosyl anti-alpha-4 integrin IgG4 monoclonal antibody developed Biogen multiple sclerosis. published model jointly describes BIIB107 pharmacokinetics (two-compartment first-order subcutaneous absorption parallel linear plus Michaelis-Menten elimination) direct sigmoidal Emax pharmacodynamic effect alpha-4 integrin receptor saturation. Body weight, included via allometric scaling, covariate retained final model.

title: Toukam_2025_biib107
text: first--human study (217HV101 / NCT04593121) enrolled 76 healthy adult volunteers single-ascending-dose (SAD) multiple-ascending-dose (MAD) cohorts; 62 active-treatment participants contributed PK data 1077 alpha-4 integrin saturation observations 62 participants formed PD analysis set (Toukam 2025 Results, “PK Analysis Dataset” “PD Analysis Dataset”). Baseline demographics (Toukam 2025 Table 2): age 19-55 years (median 33.0), body weight 57.0-99.7 kg (median 78.7), BMI 19.8-30.0 kg/m^2 (median 26.5), 80.6% male, race White 71.0% / Black African American 22.6% / Asian 4.8% / 29.0% (1.6% missing race), 50% Hispanic Latino, 91.9% normal renal function (CrCl >= 90 mL/min). Treatment-emergent ADA positivity 58.1% meaningfully affect BIIB107 exposure receptor occupancy retained covariate. Doses studied (Toukam 2025 Table 1): SAD SC: 3.6, 18, 72, 180, 360, 600 mg single doses; SAD IV: 360 mg single dose; MAD 1B: 180 mg SC Q2W loading Days 1, 15, 29 followed 360 mg SC maintenance dose Day 85; MAD 2B: 600 mg SC Q8W Days 1 57. population information available programmatically via readModelDb("Toukam_2025_biib107")$population (devtools::load_all()).

title: Toukam_2025_biib107
text: published erratum / corrigendum Toukam 2025 located PubMed Wiley landing page package build date. notation discrepancies detected translation recorded reviewers. EC50 reported value. Toukam 2025 Table 4 reports final EC50 estimate 0.376 ug/mL, Discussion section text states “EC50 0.382 ug/mL” (“approximately 6.5-fold lower EC50: 0.382 ug/mL BIIB107 vs 2.51 ug/mL natalizumab”). Results section “Final PK-PD Model Alpha-4 Integrin Saturation” cites 0.376 ug/mL consistent Table 4. model file uses Table 4 value (0.376 ug/mL) basis Table 4 parameter-estimate table; 0.382 Discussion appears rounding transcription discrepancy. Residual error magnitudes units. Toukam 2025 Tables 3 4 report additive residual error values without explicit units table cells. PK (Table 3): SC = 8.67, IV = 7.32 (described “Additive error SC” / “Additive error IV”). PD (Table 4): SC = 15.8 (%), IV = 7.93 (%). PD column header explicitly states “%”, consistent alpha-4 integrin saturation observation percentage. PK column header unit declaration. Taking literal values additive standard deviations linear ug/mL scale (matching units Cc) gives additive SD 8.67 ug/mL, unusually large versus reported LLOQ 0.2 ug/mL. model file reproduces published value verbatim uses additive SD Cc (ug/mL); users treat absolute magnitude caution rely structural model + IIV typical-value simulation. See Assumptions deviations route-specific simplification. Smaller editorial discrepancies (Table 3 RSE additive error: 1.1% SC, 8.4% IV — SC RSE looks low relative bootstrap 95% CI 6.2-10.9; load-bearing simulation, recorded completeness).

title: Toukam_2025_biib107
text: original observed dataset publicly available. virtual MS-like population constructed , sized weight-distributed mirror FIH study demographics Toukam 2025 Table 2 support Q8W dose-optimization simulations described Toukam 2025 Figures 3-4.
code: set.seed(20260428)  make_cohort <- function(n, dose_mg, dose_times, route = c("SC", "IV"), regimen, id_offset = 0L) {   route <- match.arg(route)   cmt_dose <- if (route == "SC") "depot" else "central"   obs_times <- sort(unique(c(dose_times,                              seq(0, max(dose_times) + 56, by = 1))))    # Subject-level covariates: log-normal weight matched to Table 2 mean 78.4 kg, SD 11.3 kg.   wt_mean_log <- log(78.4^2 / sqrt(78.4^2 + 11.3^2))   wt_sd_log   <- sqrt(log(1 + 11.3^2 / 78.4^2))    subjects <- tibble::tibble(     id = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n, wt_mean_log, wt_sd_log), 50), 110),     regimen = regimen   )    dose_rows <- subjects |>     tidyr::expand_grid(time = dose_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_mg, evid = 1L, cmt = cmt_dose)    obs_rows_cc <- subjects |>     tidyr::expand_grid(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc")    obs_rows_a4 <- subjects |>     tidyr::expand_grid(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, evid = 0L, cmt = "a4sat")    dplyr::bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows_cc, obs_rows_a4) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  events_sad <- dplyr::bind_rows(   make_cohort( 80, dose_mg =  72, dose_times = 0,    route = "SC", regimen = "72 mg SC SD",   id_offset =   0L),   make_cohort( 80, dose_mg = 180, dose_times = 0,    route = "SC", regimen = "180 mg SC SD",  id_offset = 100L),   make_cohort( 80, dose_mg = 360, dose_times = 0,    route = "SC", regimen = "360 mg SC SD",  id_offset = 200L),   make_cohort( 80, dose_mg = 600, dose_times = 0,    route = "SC", regimen = "600 mg SC SD",  id_offset = 300L),   make_cohort( 80, dose_mg = 360, dose_times = 0,    route = "IV", regimen = "360 mg IV SD",  id_offset = 400L) )  events_q8w <- dplyr::bind_rows(   make_cohort(150, dose_mg = 360, dose_times = c(0, 56, 112), route = "SC", regimen = "360 mg SC Q8W", id_offset = 1000L),   make_cohort(150, dose_mg = 450, dose_times = c(0, 56, 112), route = "SC", regimen = "450 mg SC Q8W", id_offset = 2000L),   make_cohort(150, dose_mg = 600, dose_times = c(0, 56, 112), route = "SC", regimen = "600 mg SC Q8W", id_offset = 3000L) )  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events_sad[, c("id", "time", "evid", "cmt")]))) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events_q8w[, c("id", "time", "evid", "cmt")])))

title: Toukam_2025_biib107
code: mod <- readModelDb("Toukam_2025_biib107") mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim_sad <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod,     events = events_sad,                            keep = c("regimen", "WT"), returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_q8w <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod,     events = events_q8w,                            keep = c("regimen", "WT"), returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_q8w_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = events_q8w,                                keep = c("regimen", "WT"), returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etala4satEC50' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

title: Toukam_2025_biib107
text: simulations reproduce qualitative behaviors described Toukam 2025 Figure 3 (SC Q8W concentration-time alpha-4 integrin saturation profiles 360, 450, 600 mg).

what: Concentration vs time, Q8W cohorts (Figure 3 PK panel)
title: Toukam_2025_biib107
code: sim_q8w |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0.01) |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     p05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     p50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     p95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, p50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = p05, ymax = p95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, 28)) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "BIIB107 serum concentration (ug/mL)",        title = "Q8W BIIB107 concentration profiles (median, 5th-95th percentile)",        caption = "Replicates the PK panel of Toukam 2025 Figure 3.") +   theme_minimal()

what: Alpha-4 integrin saturation, Q8W cohorts (Figure 3 PD panel)
title: Toukam_2025_biib107
code: sim_q8w |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(a4sat)) |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     p05 = quantile(a4sat, 0.05),     p50 = quantile(a4sat, 0.50),     p95 = quantile(a4sat, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, p50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = p05, ymax = p95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 70, linetype = "dashed") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, 28)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Alpha-4 integrin receptor saturation (%)",        title = "Q8W alpha-4 integrin saturation (median, 5th-95th percentile)",        caption = "Replicates the PD panel of Toukam 2025 Figure 3. 70% threshold dashed.") +   theme_minimal()

what: Maintenance of >=70% saturation by regimen (Toukam 2025 Table S2)
title: Toukam_2025_biib107
text: Time spent >=70% saturated within Day 56-112 dosing interval, Q8W regimen.
code: sim_q8w |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(a4sat), time >= 56, time <= 112) |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, id) |>   dplyr::summarise(     pct_time_ge_70 = mean(a4sat >= 70) * 100,     min_sat = min(a4sat),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median_pct_time_ge_70 = round(median(pct_time_ge_70), 1),     pct_subjects_alwaysGE70 = round(mean(pct_time_ge_70 == 100) * 100, 1),     median_trough_sat = round(median(min_sat), 1),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   knitr::kable(     caption = "Time spent >=70% saturated within the Day 56-112 dosing interval, by Q8W regimen."   )

text: Simulated NCA parameters (median 5th-95th percentile across virtual cohort).
code: # Multi-output simulation produces two rows per (id, time): one per dvid # (Cc and a4sat). Both rows carry the same Cc value, so deduplicate on # (id, time, regimen) before handing to PKNCA. sim_sad_nca <- sim_sad |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, regimen, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, regimen)  dose_df <- events_sad |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, regimen)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_sad_nca, Cc ~ time | regimen + id,                              concu = "ug/mL", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | regimen + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = 84,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_res <-
