title: Wang_2024_sugemalimab
text: Wang 2024 tabulate Cmax / Cmin / AUC values directly, Supplementary Tables S1–S4 report steady-state geometric-mean exposure metrics six 1200 mg Q3W doses various covariate subgroups. simulated cycle-8 NCA values can cross-checked overall-population row Wang 2024 Table S1 (row labelled Age <= 65 (n = 1165) essentially typical population >65 row differs ~1 %): simulated cycle-8 metrics differ Wang 2024 Table S1 ~20 %, model file re-checked Wang 2024 Table 3 rather tuned (per skill). side--side numerical comparison computed . Cycle-8 simulated geometric-mean NCA vs. Wang 2024 Table S1 (Age <= 65 subgroup, 1200 mg Q3W).
code: nca_df <- as.data.frame(nca_ss$result)  simulated_summary <- nca_df |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "cmax", "cmin")) |>   dplyr::group_by(PPTESTCD) |>   dplyr::summarise(     geomean = exp(mean(log(PPORRES[PPORRES > 0]), na.rm = TRUE)),     .groups = "drop"   )  published <- tibble::tibble(   PPTESTCD     = c("auclast", "cmax", "cmin"),   metric       = c("AUCss (μg*day/mL)",                    "Cmax,ss (μg/mL)",                    "Cmin,ss (μg/mL)"),   published_gm = c(6951, 571, 221) )  comparison <- dplyr::left_join(published, simulated_summary, by = "PPTESTCD") |>   dplyr::mutate(diff_pct = 100 * (geomean - published_gm) / published_gm)  knitr::kable(   comparison,   digits  = 1,   caption = "Cycle-8 simulated geometric-mean NCA vs. Wang 2024 Table S1 (Age <= 65 subgroup, 1200 mg Q3W)." )

title: Wang_2024_sugemalimab
text: Wang 2024 publish individual PK covariate tables, virtual population coarse approximation Table 2 distributions. Body weight, albumin, tumour burden: log-normal / normal centered Table 2 medians, clipped reported ranges. actual distributions covariate correlations published. Tumour-type indicators: sampled independently per subject Table 2 marginal proportions. Lung cancer treated reference category (four TUMTP_* indicators = 0). Missing tumour burden (14.7 % source subjects per Wang 2024 Table 2 footnote b) represented virtual cohort; paper state missing tumour burden imputed model fitting. Cycle-8 vs. cycle-6 steady-state: Wang 2024 reports steady-state exposure metrics sixth 1200 mg Q3W dose (Methods section 2.4 Supplementary Tables S1–S4); simulation vignette runs eight cycles computes NCA cycle-8 dosing interval. T50=53.6T_{50} = 53.6 days λ=2.60\lambda = 2.60, CL trajectory reached ~92 % EmaxE_{\max} day 105 (start cycle 6) ~96 % day 147 (start cycle 8); cycle-8 metrics therefore slightly larger cycle-6 source paper. comparison table flags differences > 20 %. Albumin SD: Wang 2024 publish SD baseline albumin. 3.5 g/L used , consistent typical oncology cohorts. Race / ECOG: included final Wang 2024 model, generated virtual cohort. Q IIV: per Wang 2024 Table 3, IIV term retained Q. model file reflects ; Q typical-value parameter . Emax additive eta linear scale: Wang 2024 parameterizes Emax random effect additive linear scale (Table 3 footnote: Emax_i = theta5 + eta_Emax,). variance ~0.00955 (SD ~0.098) typical Emax -0.528, allows individual Emax values range roughly −0.72-0.72 −0.34-0.34 95 % level (asymptotic CL ratio ranges ∼0.49\sim 0.49 ∼0.71\sim 0.71 baseline). subject parameterization expected exhibit Emax>0E_{\max} > 0 (CL increase), unlike Zhang 2019 nivolumab parameterization IIV broader. PKNCA reporting unit: AUC computed auclast 21-day dosing interval reported μ\mug⋅\cdotday/mL (matching Wang 2024 Table S1’s units μ\mug⋅\cdotday/mL).

title: Wang_2024_sugemalimab
text: errata corrigenda located Wang K, Pan C, Xu F, Tse , Sheng Y. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2025;91(3):748–760 time extraction. publisher’s article landing page checked correction notices PubMed / Google Scholar search "Wang sugemalimab" erratum returned none.

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Citation: Wojciechowski J, Purohit VS, Nawarskas R, Marshall S, Charnas L, Bhattacharya . Population pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic analysis domagrozumab pediatric patients Duchenne muscular dystrophy. Clin Transl Sci. 2022;15(12):2939-2952. doi:10.1111/cts.13418 Description: Quasi-steady-state TMDD population PK/PD model domagrozumab (anti-myostatin IgG1) healthy adult volunteers pediatric patients Duchenne muscular dystrophy (Wojciechowski 2022): two-compartment IV/SC drug disposition parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination, synthesis-degradation total-myostatin compartment drug-mediated internalization, study-population covariate (DIS_DMD) shifting myostatin baseline turnover. Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/cts.13418 Domagrozumab humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody binds neutralizes myostatin, negative regulator skeletal muscle growth. Wojciechowski 2022 re-fits quasi-steady-state target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD) PK/PD model jointly free-drug total-myostatin observations Phase 1 study healthy adult volunteers (NCT01616277, n = 73) Phase 2 B5161002 study ambulatory boys Duchenne muscular dystrophy (NCT02310763, n = 120). structural model unchanged prior healthy-adult fit; pediatric DMD differences manifest study-population covariate (SPOP, encoded canonically DIS_DMD) shifts baseline myostatin (BASE) joint myostatin turnover axis (kdeg/kint).

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Pooled analysis (N = 193): 73 healthy adult volunteers (HV; Phase 1) 120 ambulatory boys Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD pediatric; Phase 2). HV ages 19–61 years (median 37, mean 37.7), weights 56.8–99.8 kg (median 76.9, mean 76.9), 90.4 % male; DMD pediatric ages 6–15 years (median 9, mean 8.7), weights 14.8–86.4 kg (median 28.9, mean 31.8), 100 % male. Combined race distribution 68.4 % White, 12.4 % Black, 9.3 % Asian, 9.8 % . HV cohort received single IV doses 1, 3, 10, 20, 40 mg/kg 2-h infusion; one cohort received single SC 3 mg/kg; one cohort received 10 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks 3 doses. DMD pediatric patients received ascending sequence 5, 20, 40 mg/kg IV every 4 weeks 16 weeks per dose level (Period 1), continued highest tolerated dose 48 weeks (Period 2). Free domagrozumab assayed validated ELISA (LLOQ 0.2 nM Phase 1, 2.67 nM Phase 2); total myostatin assayed validated immunoprecipitation LC-MS/MS (LLOQ 0.04 nM Phase 1, 0.008 nM Phase 2). Anti-drug antibody samples negative limit quantification across studies, ADA tested covariate. Full demographics appear Table 1 Wojciechowski 2022. information available programmatically via model’s population metadata:
code: str(rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab"))$meta$population) #> List of 14 #>  $ n_subjects              : int 193 #>  $ n_studies               : int 2 #>  $ age_range               : chr "6-61 years (healthy adults 19-61; DMD pediatric 6-15)" #>  $ age_median              : chr "37.0 years (HV); 9.0 years (DMD)" #>  $ weight_range            : chr "14.8-99.8 kg (HV 56.8-99.8; DMD 14.8-86.4)" #>  $ weight_median           : chr "76.9 kg (HV); 28.9 kg (DMD)" #>  $ sex_female_pct          : num 3.6 #>  $ race_ethnicity          : Named num [1:4] 68.4 12.4 9.3 9.8 #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:4] "White" "Black" "Asian" "Other" #>  $ disease_state           : chr "Pooled healthy adult volunteers (Phase 1, NCT01616277, n = 73) and ambulatory boys with Duchenne muscular dystr"| __truncated__ #>  $ dose_range              : chr "Healthy adults: single IV doses 1, 3, 10, 20, 40 mg/kg over 2-h infusion; single SC 3 mg/kg; or 10 mg/kg IV eve"| __truncated__ #>  $ regions                 : chr "Multi-regional Phase 1 and Phase 2 trials (Pfizer-sponsored)." #>  $ n_observations_drug     : int 5181 #>  $ n_observations_myostatin: int 8001 #>  $ notes                   : chr "Demographics from Wojciechowski 2022 Table 1 (combined HV + DMD pediatric column). 5.79% of free domagrozumab a"| __truncated__

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab.R. table collects one place review. representative IgG1 molecular weight 145,000 g/mol used inside model file convert mg-based dosing (rxode2 amount convention, volumes L) paper’s nM concentration scale. paper report domagrozumab molecular weight; documented deviation .

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. Wojciechowski 2022 Figures 3 4 depict typical-value--percentile VPCs free domagrozumab total myostatin pediatric DMD patients; Figure 5a simulated typical-value plus 95 % PI myostatin coverage. build virtual DMD pediatric population approximates Phase 2 demographics Table 1 (n = 120, ages 6-15 years, weights 14.8-86.4 kg, male). Body weight sampled log-normal distribution mean SD reported Table 1 (28.9 kg median, 11.1 SD), truncated Phase 2 weight range.
code: set.seed(20260426)  n_dmd <- 500 make_dmd_cohort <- function(n, id_offset = 0L) {   # Log-normal weights matched approximately to Wojciechowski 2022 Table 1   # Phase 2 column (median 28.9 kg, mean 31.8 kg, SD 11.1 kg, range 14.8-86.4).   wt <- rlnorm(n, meanlog = log(28.9), sdlog = 0.32)   wt <- pmin(pmax(wt, 14.8), 86.4)   tibble(     id      = id_offset + seq_len(n),     WT      = wt,     DIS_DMD = 1L   ) }  dmd_cohort <- make_dmd_cohort(n_dmd) summary(dmd_cohort$WT) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   14.80   22.79   28.94   30.73   36.97   86.40

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Wojciechowski 2022 reports myostatin coverage three Phase 2 dose levels (5, 20, 40 mg/kg every 4 weeks). simulate dose level 16 weeks (4 doses) record free domagrozumab, total myostatin, predicted myostatin coverage predose, 2 hours postdose, 6 hours postdose, matching paper’s evaluation schedule.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab")  # Simulation horizon: 16 weeks of Q4W dosing (4 doses) weeks       <- 16 ndose       <- 4 dose_int_h  <- 4 * 7 * 24      # 4 weeks in hours horizon_h   <- weeks * 7 * 24 samp_times  <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, horizon_h, by = 24),                                   # daily   rep(seq(0, by = dose_int_h, length.out = ndose), each = 3) +     c(0.001, 2, 6)                                              # predose, +2 h, +6 h )))  dose_times <- seq(0, by = dose_int_h, length.out = ndose)  build_events <- function(cohort, dose_mg_per_kg) {   doses <- cohort |>     tidyr::expand_grid(time = dose_times) |>     mutate(       evid = 1L, cmt = "central", amt = dose_mg_per_kg * WT, dv = NA_real_     )   obs_cc <- cohort |>     tidyr::expand_grid(time = samp_times) |>     mutate(evid = 0L, cmt = "Cc",  amt = NA_real_, dv = NA_real_)   obs_myo <- cohort |>     tidyr::expand_grid(time = samp_times) |>     mutate(evid = 0L, cmt = "Myo", amt = NA_real_, dv = NA_real_)   bind_rows(doses, obs_cc, obs_myo) |>     arrange(id, time, desc(evid)) |>     select(id, time, amt, evid, cmt, WT, DIS_DMD) }  sim_dose <- function(cohort, dose_mg_per_kg, label) {   ev <- build_events(cohort, dose_mg_per_kg)   sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = ev, returnType = "data.frame")   # Myostatin coverage (Eq. 10): MyoCov = 1 - Myo * kSS/(kSS + Cc) / BMyo   # where BMyo is the subject-specific baseline myostatin (here, the   # typical-value DMD-pediatric baseline; per-subject etas perturb both   # actual baseline and Myo identically, so the typical-value reference   # is appropriate for evaluating Eq. 10 across the cohort).   kss      <- 7.76   bmyo_dmd <- 0.156 * (1 + (-0.641))   sim |>     mutate(       regimen    = label,       MyoCov_pct = 100 * (1 - (Myo * kss / (kss + Cc)) / bmyo_dmd)     ) }  sim_5  <- sim_dose(dmd_cohort,  5, "5 mg/kg Q4W") sim_20 <- sim_dose(dmd_cohort, 20, "20 mg/kg Q4W") sim_40 <- sim_dose(dmd_cohort, 40, "40 mg/kg Q4W")  sim_all <- bind_rows(sim_5, sim_20, sim_40) |>   mutate(regimen = factor(regimen,                           levels = c("5 mg/kg Q4W", "20 mg/kg Q4W", "40 mg/kg Q4W")))

what: Figure 3 — VPC of free domagrozumab in DMD pediatric patients
title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Wojciechowski 2022 Figure 3 shows median 5th/95th-percentile VPC ribbons free domagrozumab vs. time--first-dose pediatric DMD patients across three Phase 2 dose levels combined. reproduce typical-value--percentile envelope simulation.
code: sim_all |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   filter(time > 0) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Q50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Free domagrozumab (nM)",     title = "Figure 3 — Free domagrozumab VPC in DMD pediatric patients",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3 of Wojciechowski 2022 (median / 5th-95th-percentile envelope)."   )

what: Figure 4 — VPC of total myostatin in DMD pediatric patients
title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Wojciechowski 2022 Figure 4 shows analogous VPC total myostatin. signature feature sustained increase total myostatin treatment, drug-myostatin complex internalized far slower free myostatin (kint << kdeg) — total myostatin (free + bound) accumulates drug binds protects degradation.
code: sim_all |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Myo, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Myo, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Myo, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   filter(time >= 0) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Q50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.20, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Total myostatin (nM)",     title = "Figure 4 — Total myostatin VPC in DMD pediatric patients",     caption = "Replicates Figure 4 of Wojciechowski 2022."   )

what: Figure 5a — Simulated myostatin coverage by dose level
title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Wojciechowski 2022 Figure 5a shows median 95 % PI myostatin coverage following 4 doses 5, 20, 40 mg/kg every 4 weeks. Equation 10 defines coverage fraction baseline myostatin bound drug given time:
code: MyoCoverage(t) = 1 - Myo(t) * kSS / (kSS + Cc(t)) / BMyo sim_all |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(regimen, time) |>   summarise(     Q025 = quantile(MyoCov_pct, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50  = quantile(MyoCov_pct, 0.50,  na.rm = TRUE),     Q975 = quantile(MyoCov_pct, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Q50, colour = regimen, fill = regimen)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q025, ymax = Q975), alpha = 0.20, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   ylim(0, 100) +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Myostatin coverage (%)",     title = "Figure 5a — Simulated myostatin coverage by dose level (DMD pediatric)",     caption = "Replicates Figure 5a of Wojciechowski 2022."   )

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Wojciechowski 2022 (Results, “Simulation myostatin coverage”) reports following median (95 % PI) values myostatin coverage DMD pediatric patients dose level. compute analogous summary statistics simulated cohort across post-dose sampling times. Myostatin coverage DMD pediatric patients: simulated vs. Wojciechowski 2022 Results. Differences > 20 % simulated published values indicate translation error flagged investigation. simulated coverage incorporates -subject variability propagated 5x5 OMEGA block SD-ratio scaling eta_kint eta_kdeg, sampling baseline parameters jointly per subject.
code: sim_summary <- sim_all |>   filter(time > 0) |>   group_by(regimen) |>   summarise(     sim_median = quantile(MyoCov_pct, 0.50,  na.rm = TRUE),     sim_lo     = quantile(MyoCov_pct, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),     sim_hi     = quantile(MyoCov_pct, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(simulated = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f, %.1f)", sim_median, sim_lo, sim_hi)) |>   select(regimen, simulated)  published <- tibble(   regimen   = c("5 mg/kg Q4W", "20 mg/kg Q4W", "40 mg/kg Q4W"),   published = c("86.9 (69.1, 92.9)", "96.6 (93.8, 98.2)", "98.3 (96.8, 99.1)") )  comparison <- left_join(published, sim_summary, by = "regimen") knitr::kable(comparison,              col.names = c("Regimen", "Published median (95 % PI)",                            "Simulated median (95 % PI)"),              caption = "Myostatin coverage in DMD pediatric patients: simulated vs. Wojciechowski 2022 Results.")

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: NCA simulate single 5 mg/kg IV dose representative DMD pediatric subject (28.9 kg, cohort median weight) compute standard PK parameters free-domagrozumab profile. paper publish formal NCA table domagrozumab, self-consistency check — Cmax, AUC, half-life consistent 2-compartment mAb cleared 0.0000982 L/hour/kg early linear range TMDD becomes saturating. PKNCA summary single 5 mg/kg IV dose (typical-value DMD pediatric, 28.9 kg). implied terminal half-life linear PK micro-constants log(2) * V1 / CL = log(2) * 0.0415 / 0.0000982 = 293 hours = 12.2 days, consistent typical IgG mAb. PKNCA half.life value order; TMDD-driven saturation low concentrations can lengthen apparent terminal slope.
code: mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod)  nca_times <- sort(unique(c(   0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12,   seq(24, 4 * 7 * 24, by = 24) ))) ev_sd <- et(id = 1) |>   et(amt = 5 * 28.9, cmt = "central", time = 0, id = 1) |>   et(time = nca_times, cmt = "Cc", id = 1) ev_sd$WT      <- 28.9 ev_sd$DIS_DMD <- 1L  sim_sd <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, ev_sd, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvmax', 'etalbase', 'etalkdeg_kint'  sim_nca <- sim_sd |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 0) |>   mutate(id = 1L, treatment = "5 mg/kg IV SD, DMD pediatric") |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- data.frame(   id = 1L, time = 0, amt = 5 * 28.9,   treatment = "5 mg/kg IV SD, DMD pediatric" )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_res), digits = 3,              caption = paste("PKNCA summary for a single 5 mg/kg IV dose",                              "(typical-value DMD pediatric, 28.9 kg)."))

title: Wojciechowski_2022_domagrozumab
text: Domagrozumab molecular weight = 145,000 g/mol assumed (representative humanized IgG1 mAb mass). paper report domagrozumab MW. MW enters unit bridge mg-dosing paper’s nM concentration scale; parameter values come paper unchanged. user access regulatory MW (typically published Pfizer briefing document) can override MW_DOMA model body. Body weight enters multiplicative scaling per-kg PK parameters (CL, V1, Q, V2, Vmax) exponent = 1, matching paper’s per-kg parameterization. paper presents alternative allometric model (CL/Q/Vmax exponent 0.75; V1/V2 exponent 1; reference 70 kg) comparison Table 2; allometric model final model packaged . Users prefer allometric form can edit model file use second column Wojciechowski 2022 Table 2. (1 + theta * DIS_DMD) covariate form multiplicative-additive linear scale, specified Equations 7-8 paper, rather common theta^DIS_DMD exponential form. theta_SPOP_(kdeg,kint) = -0.900 produces 90 % reduction affected parameter DMD patients, exponential parameterization give different magnitude (exp(-0.900) = 0.407, 59 % reduction) — preserving paper’s form therefore load-bearing reproducing Figures 3-5. single etalkdeg_kint random effect drives kdeg kint, kint scaled structural ratio e_ratio_kdegkint = -0.295. matches Wojciechowski 2022 Table 2 (“Ratio SD eta_kint relative eta_kdeg” row); IIV name follows shared-eta convention etal<param1>_<param2> analogous shared-exponent covariate-effect form e_<cov>_<param1>_<param2>. Concentration units nM, matching paper, rather common ug/mL (mass-per-volume) unit used nlmixr2lib mAb models. total-myostatin observation naturally nM, drug-myostatin binding constant kSS nM, working molar units throughout avoids mixed-unit arithmetic; convention check warning missing / units$concentration known consequence documented . virtual cohort holds DIS_DMD = 1 subjects (pediatric DMD patients ). Setting DIS_DMD = 0 recovers healthy-adult-volunteer parameter set: BASE = 0.156 nM, kdeg = 0.0381 1/hour, kint = 0.00716 1/hour (verifiable re-running simulation DIS_DMD = 0). errata corrigenda found Wojciechowski 2022 time extraction (2026-04-26); search journal landing page PubMed returned published corrections. correction later issued, affected parameter values updated model file’s ini() block reference field amended cite erratum alongside main publication.

what: Inotuzumab ozogamicin population PK in pediatric and adult B-cell precursor ALL / NHL
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: Simulate inotuzumab ozogamicin (InO) concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Wu et al. (2024). model developed reanalyzing Garrett 2019 adult InO population PK model pooled adult + pediatric dataset (8924 serum InO concentrations 818 patients across 12 studies — 531 adult B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL), 234 adult B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (BCP-), 53 pediatric BCP-participants ITCC-059) adding pediatric-specific covariate effects. structural model two-compartment disposition IV input total clearance sums linear (steady-state) component (CL_SS, paper’s CL1) time-decaying target-mediated component (CL_t = CL_TIME * exp(-kdes * time), paper’s CL2 arm). CLtotal(t)=CLSS+CLTIMEe−kdest   CL_{\text{total}}(t) = CL_{\text{SS}} + CL_{\text{TIME}} \, e^{-k_{\text{des}}\, t} Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40262-024-01386-z PubMed (PMID 38907948): https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38907948/

previous_headings: Inotuzumab ozogamicin population PK in pediatric and adult B-cell precursor ALL / NHL
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: pooled analysis included three cohorts: Adult B-cell NHL (n = 531; 5609 observations): age 18–92 y (median 65 y), LBM 24.4–87.4 kg (median 53.6 kg), albumin 2.20–5.20 g/dL. Adult BCP-(n = 234; 2752 observations): age 20–79 y (median 46 y), LBM 29.4–95.9 kg (median 54.4 kg), peripheral-blood blasts median 4.00% (absolute counts median 0.82 x 10^9). phase II): age 1–17 y (median 9 y), body weight 12.7–147 kg (median 34.3 kg), LBM 10.9–69.0 kg (median 29.5 kg), peripheral-blood blasts median 3.00%; 67.9% male; 13 patients dose level 1 (1.4 mg/m^2/cycle), 40 dose level 2 / RP2D (1.8 mg/m^2/cycle). Demographics summarized Wu 2024 Table 2 ESM Table 3. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Wu_2024_inotuzumab")$population.

previous_headings: Inotuzumab ozogamicin population PK in pediatric and adult B-cell precursor ALL / NHL
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: Wu 2024 publish individual baseline covariates. cohort approximates ITCC-059 pediatric BCP-population centered medians reported Wu 2024 Table 2.
code: set.seed(2024) n_subj <- 200  pop <- data.frame(   ID           = seq_len(n_subj),   AGE          = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_subj, 9, 4.5)), 1), 17),   LBM          = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 29.5, 11), 10.9), 69.0),   DIS_BCPALL   = 1L,   CONMED_RITUX = 0L,   # BLSTABL is highly skewed in the pediatric ITCC-059 cohort   # (median 0 with a long upper tail extending to 32 x 10^9 counts).   # For a power covariate centered at 0.352 we cannot use 0 directly;   # draw from a log-normal centered on the BCP-ALL reference 0.352 to   # avoid singularities in the (BLSTABL/0.352)^exp term while preserving   # the right-skewed character.   BLSTABL      = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(0.352), 1.6), 0.01), 32) )  # Compute body weight implied by the pediatric Peters et al. LBM relationship is # circular; instead approximate BSA from a Mosteller-like surrogate using # (heuristic) BSA ~ LBM-scaled body weight for children. We only need BSA to # convert mg/m^2 doses to mg, so use the published Wu 2024 cohort relationship # (median LBM 29.5 -> median BSA 1.18) and scale linearly with LBM. pop$BSA <- pop$LBM * (1.18 / 29.5)

previous_headings: Inotuzumab ozogamicin population PK in pediatric and adult B-cell precursor ALL / NHL
what: Dosing dataset — pediatric RP2D (1.8 / 1.5 mg/m^2/cycle, IV)
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: Cycle 1 (21 days): 0.8 + 0.5 + 0.5 mg/m^2 days 1, 8, 15. Cycles 2–6 (28-day cycles): 0.5 + 0.5 + 0.5 mg/m^2 days 1, 8, 15. dose 60-minute IV infusion. simulate three full cycles (1 cycle 21 days + 2 cycles 28 days = 77 days = 1848 h).
code: infusion_dur_h <- 1   # 60-minute IV infusion (Wu 2024 Methods 2.1)  # Cycle / fraction schedule (mg/m^2 per fractioned dose). schedule <- tibble::tribble(   ~cycle, ~day, ~mg_m2,   1L,      1L,    0.8,   1L,      8L,    0.5,   1L,     15L,    0.5,   2L,     22L,    0.5,   2L,     29L,    0.5,   2L,     36L,    0.5,   3L,     50L,    0.5,   3L,     57L,    0.5,   3L,     64L,    0.5 )  doses <- tidyr::crossing(pop, schedule) %>%   mutate(     TIME = (day - 1) * 24,         # day 1 -> hour 0     AMT  = mg_m2 * BSA,            # mg     RATE = AMT / infusion_dur_h,   # mg/h (60-min infusion)     EVID = 1L,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, RATE, EVID, CMT, DV,          AGE, LBM, DIS_BCPALL, CONMED_RITUX, BLSTABL)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 24, by = 1),                   # day 1 hourly   seq(24, 24 * 21, by = 12),            # cycle 1 every 12 h   seq(24 * 21, 24 * 49, by = 24),       # cycle 2 daily   seq(24 * 49, 24 * 77, by = 24)        # cycle 3 daily )))  obs <- tidyr::crossing(pop, TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT = NA_real_, RATE = NA_real_,     EVID = 0L, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, RATE, EVID, CMT, DV,          AGE, LBM, DIS_BCPALL, CONMED_RITUX, BLSTABL)  events <- bind_rows(doses, obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()  # Disjoint-ID guard for the bind_rows (single cohort here, but cheap regression check). stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))

code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc),     lo10   = quantile(Cc, 0.10),     hi90   = quantile(Cc, 0.90),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 24)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo10, ymax = hi90), alpha = 0.20, fill = "darkgreen") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Inotuzumab ozogamicin (ng/mL)",     title = "Simulated pediatric BCP-ALL inotuzumab ozogamicin PK",     subtitle = sprintf("RP2D (1.8 then 1.5 mg/m^2/cycle, IV; N = %d virtual pediatric patients)", n_subj),     caption = "Replicates Figure 8 of Wu 2024 (pediatric panel; median + 10th-90th percentile)."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Adult BCP-ALL comparison (replicates Figure 8 of Wu 2024 — adult panel)
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: adult cohort re-use dosing schedule (also adult approved regimen) draw covariates adult BCP-population (Wu 2024 Table 2): median LBM 54.4 kg, median age 46 y.
code: set.seed(2024) pop_adult <- data.frame(   ID           = seq_len(n_subj) + n_subj,                # disjoint IDs   AGE          = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_subj, 46, 14)), 20), 79),   LBM          = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 54.4, 12), 29.4), 95.9),   DIS_BCPALL   = 1L,   CONMED_RITUX = 0L,   BLSTABL      = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(0.82), 1.5), 0.01), 254) ) pop_adult$BSA <- pop_adult$LBM * (1.86 / 54.4)            # adult BCP-ALL median BSA / LBM  doses_a <- tidyr::crossing(pop_adult, schedule) %>%   mutate(     TIME = (day - 1) * 24,     AMT  = mg_m2 * BSA,     RATE = AMT / infusion_dur_h,     EVID = 1L, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, RATE, EVID, CMT, DV,          AGE, LBM, DIS_BCPALL, CONMED_RITUX, BLSTABL)  obs_a <- tidyr::crossing(pop_adult, TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, RATE = NA_real_,          EVID = 0L, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, RATE, EVID, CMT, DV,          AGE, LBM, DIS_BCPALL, CONMED_RITUX, BLSTABL)  events_adult <- bind_rows(doses_a, obs_a) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events_adult[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))  sim_adult <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events_adult, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' both <- bind_rows(   sim       %>% mutate(cohort = "Pediatric BCP-ALL"),   sim_adult %>% mutate(cohort = "Adult BCP-ALL") ) %>%   filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) %>%   group_by(cohort, time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc),     lo10   = quantile(Cc, 0.10),     hi90   = quantile(Cc, 0.90),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(both, aes(x = time / 24, color = cohort, fill = cohort)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo10, ymax = hi90), alpha = 0.18, color = NA) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("Pediatric BCP-ALL" = "darkgreen",                                 "Adult BCP-ALL"     = "firebrick")) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pediatric BCP-ALL" = "darkgreen",                                "Adult BCP-ALL"     = "firebrick")) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Inotuzumab ozogamicin (ng/mL)",     color = NULL, fill = NULL,     title = "Simulated InO PK at the RP2D — pediatric vs adult BCP-ALL",     caption = "Replicates Figure 8 of Wu 2024 (adult vs pediatric overlay)."   ) +   theme_bw()

previous_headings: Inotuzumab ozogamicin population PK in pediatric and adult B-cell precursor ALL / NHL
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: Compute steady-state cumulative AUC end cycle 1 (per Wu 2024 Section 3.4 / Table 4: median 26.1 x 10^3 ng*h/mL among pediatric responders). PKNCA window full 21-day cycle 1 (time 0 504 h) cohorts. Simulated NCA cycle 1 (0-504 h) pediatric adult BCP-cohorts.
code: nca_conc <- bind_rows(   sim       %>% filter(time <= 504, Cc > 0) %>%     transmute(ID = id, time, Cc, treatment = "Pediatric BCP-ALL"),   sim_adult %>% filter(time <= 504, Cc > 0) %>%     transmute(ID = id, time, Cc, treatment = "Adult BCP-ALL") )  nca_dose <- bind_rows(   doses   %>% transmute(ID, time = TIME, AMT, treatment = "Pediatric BCP-ALL"),   doses_a %>% transmute(ID, time = TIME, AMT, treatment = "Adult BCP-ALL") ) %>%   filter(time <= 504)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time | treatment + title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: Cycle-1 cumulative AUC0-504h: simulated vs. published Wu 2024 Table 5 model-based simulation values. simulated medians expected track published Wu 2024 Table 5 values within ~10–20%. Residual differences come virtual-population covariate distributions (especially BLSTABL right-skew, BSA--LBM heuristic, finite N = 200 vs paper’s 1000-subject simulation), differences model parameters .
code: # Cumulative AUC at end of cycle 1 from Wu 2024 (Tables 4 and 5): #   - Table 4 (pediatric trial-data-driven, model-based AUC): responders 26.1 #     [18.9-35.0], non-responders 10.1 [9.19-16.1]; combined median 19.7 #     (informally) x 10^3 ng*h/mL. #   - Table 5 (model-based simulation at RP2D): #       Adult BCP-ALL:     19.7 [12.1-30.3] x 10^3 ng*h/mL #       Pediatric BCP-ALL: 26.6 [17.9-37.0] x 10^3 ng*h/mL sim_auc <- bind_rows(   sim       %>% mutate(cohort = "Pediatric BCP-ALL"),   sim_adult %>% mutate(cohort = "Adult BCP-ALL") ) %>%   filter(time <= 504, !is.na(Cc)) %>%   arrange(cohort, id, time) %>%   group_by(cohort, id) %>%   summarise(     AUC0_504 = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),     .groups  = "drop"   )  q <- function(x) {   c(median   = median(x),     p25      = quantile(x, 0.25, names = FALSE),     p75      = quantile(x, 0.75, names = FALSE)) }  published <- tibble::tribble(   ~Cohort,                ~Source,         ~`Median AUC0-504 (10^3 ng*h/mL)`, ~`IQR (10^3 ng*h/mL)`,   "Adult BCP-ALL",        "Wu 2024 Table 5",                          19.7,  "12.1-30.3",   "Pediatric BCP-ALL",    "Wu 2024 Table 5",                          26.6,  "17.9-37.0" )  simulated <- sim_auc %>%   group_by(cohort) %>%   summarise(     `Median AUC0-504 (10^3 ng*h/mL)` = round(median(AUC0_504) / 1000, 1),     `IQR (10^3 ng*h/mL)`             = sprintf("%.1f-%.1f",                                                 quantile(AUC0_504, 0.25) / 1000,                                                 quantile(AUC0_504, 0.75) / 1000),     Source = "This vignette (simulated)",     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   rename(Cohort = cohort) %>%   select(Cohort, Source,          `Median AUC0-504 (10^3 ng*h/mL)`,          `IQR (10^3 ng*h/mL)`)  knitr::kable(   bind_rows(published, simulated) %>% arrange(Cohort, Source),   caption = paste(     "Cycle-1 cumulative AUC0-504h: simulated vs. published Wu 2024",     "Table 5 model-based simulation values."   ) )

title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: completeness, simulate adult NHL cohort. NHL patients BLSTABL AGE effect kdes; supply BLSTABL = 0.352 (BCP-reference) gated power term evaluates 1.
code: set.seed(2024) pop_nhl <- data.frame(   ID           = seq_len(n_subj) + 2L * n_subj,   AGE          = pmin(pmax(round(rnorm(n_subj, 65, 12)), 18), 92),   LBM          = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 53.6, 12), 24.4), 87.4),   DIS_BCPALL   = 0L,                     # NHL   CONMED_RITUX = 0L,   BLSTABL      = 0.352                   # gated off for NHL ) pop_nhl$BSA <- pop_nhl$LBM * (1.83 / 53.6)  doses_n <- tidyr::crossing(pop_nhl, schedule) %>%   mutate(     TIME = (day - 1) * 24,     AMT  = mg_m2 * BSA,     RATE = AMT / infusion_dur_h,     EVID = 1L, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_   ) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, RATE, EVID, CMT, DV,          AGE, LBM, DIS_BCPALL, CONMED_RITUX, BLSTABL)  obs_n <- tidyr::crossing(pop_nhl, TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, RATE = NA_real_,          EVID = 0L, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_) %>%   select(ID, TIME, AMT, RATE, EVID, CMT, DV,          AGE, LBM, DIS_BCPALL, CONMED_RITUX, BLSTABL)  events_nhl <- bind_rows(doses_n, obs_n) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()  stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events_nhl[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))  sim_nhl <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events_nhl, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

previous_headings: Inotuzumab ozogamicin population PK in pediatric and adult B-cell precursor ALL / NHL
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: Wu 2024 publish individual baseline covariates, virtual populations approximate paper’s reported medians ranges: LBM: drawn population-specific normal distributions centered medians Wu 2024 Table 2 clipped published min/max. AGE: pediatric BCP-drawn N(9, 4.5) clipped 1–17 y; adult BCP-N(46, 14) clipped 20–79 y; adult NHL N(65, 12) clipped 18–92 y, matching Wu 2024 Table 2. BLSTABL: drawn log-normal centered reference 0.352 x 10^9 avoid zero-singularity (BLSTABL/0.352)^exponent power covariate. pediatric ITCC-059 cohort true median 0; simulation use population reference Wu 2024 notes BLSTABL clinically relevant InO disposition treatment duration. BSA: approximated LBM via per-cohort linear scaling (ratio cohort-median BSA / cohort-median LBM Wu 2024 Table 2). exact BSA required convert mg/m^2 doses mg; model’s PK parameters depend LBM, BSA, BSA distribution errors propagate dose magnitude, clearance. Race / sex distributions: retained covariates Wu 2024, simulated. Residual error: packaged ini() defaults pediatric BCP-log-scale SD 0.452. simulate adult NHL adult BCP-published residuals, override via mod <- ini(mod, expSd = 0.444) (adult NHL) mod <- ini(mod, expSd = 0.612) (adult BCP-). expSd parameter naming: deviates canonical propSd/addSd names (flagged checkModelConventions()). Wu 2024 models residual additive log-transformed concentrations (NONMEM Y = LOG(IPRED) + EPS(1) EPS(1) ~ N(0, sigma^2)), maps Cc ~ lnorm(SD) nlmixr2 — proportional additive linear-scale form. expSd name follows Cirincione_2017_exenatide precedent repo log-scale residual SDs documented justified deviation. Concentration units: dosing mg, concentration ng/mL (Cc = (central / vc) * 1000). paper reports bioanalytical LLOQ 1.0 ng/mL, ng/mL natural reporting unit; checkModelConventions() flags mg/ng magnitude difference informational note, conversion explicitly applied model code. Time: hours (matches Wu 2024 Table 3 unit kdes 1/h CL L/h). Half-life AUC values reported paper quoted h / h*ng/mL days; convert needed. Time-dependent CL time semantics: time cl_time * exp(-kdes * time) integration time start simulation (= time first dose event datasets begin t = 0). patients whose first observation partway treatment course (e.g., cycle-2 ), build event table relative first dose time-decay term correct clock.

previous_headings: Inotuzumab ozogamicin population PK in pediatric and adult B-cell precursor ALL / NHL
title: Wu_2024_inotuzumab
text: Structure: 2-compartment IV PK linear / steady-state (CL_SS, paper’s CL1) plus time-decaying target-mediated (CL_t = CL_TIME * exp(-kdes * time), paper’s CL2 arm) clearance components. depot. Population: pooled adult B-cell NHL + adult BCP-+ pediatric BCP-(n = 818 across 12 studies; 8924 InO concentrations). Reference subject (NHL adult, LBM 52.7 kg, AGE 60 y, BLSTABL 0.352, rituximab): CL_SS 0.130 L/h, Vc 6.49 L, CL_TIME 0.569 L/h, kdes 0.0577 1/h, Q 0.0437 L/h, Vp 4.74 L. Terminal half-life ≈ 285 h (~11.9 d) adult BCP-, ≈ 423 h (~17.6 d) pediatric BCP-(Wu 2024 Section 3.5). Strongest covariates: LBM CL_SS (1.05), Vc (0.977), CL_TIME (0.687); effect (DIS_BCPALL) CL_SS (-0.767), CL_TIME (-0.362), kdes (-0.924); AGE kdes (-0.296, BCP-).

title: Xie_2019_agomelatine
text: Citation: Xie F, Vermeulen , Colin P, Cheng Z. semiphysiological population pharmacokinetic model agomelatine metabolites Chinese healthy volunteers. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2019 May;85(5):1003-1014. doi: 10.1111/bcp.13902. Epub 2019 Mar 21. PMID: 30761579; PMCID: PMC6475681. Description: semiphysiological population pharmacokinetic model agomelatine metabolites Chinese healthy volunteers Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.13902

what: Recreate figure 4 from the original paper
title: Xie_2019_agomelatine
text: dose single 25 mg dose based methods section reference.    figure BQL 3-hydroxy-agomelatine original paper. added completeness.
code:
Xie_2019_agomelatine <- nlmixr(readModelDb("Xie_2019_agomelatine")) #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: etaiov_k13_1, etaiov_k13_2, etaiov_k13_3, etaiov_k13_4, etaiov_alag2_1, etaiov_alag2_2, etaiov_alag2_3, etaiov_alag2_4, etaiov_k23_1, etaiov_k23_2, etaiov_k23_3, etaiov_k23_4, etaiov_clint_1, etaiov_clint_2, etaiov_clint_3, etaiov_clint_4, etaiov_fpop_1, etaiov_fpop_2, etaiov_fpop_3, etaiov_fpop_4 #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line conc_unit <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Xie_2019_agomelatine"))$units[["concentration"]] #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: etaiov_k13_1, etaiov_k13_2, etaiov_k13_3, etaiov_k13_4, etaiov_alag2_1, etaiov_alag2_2, etaiov_alag2_3, etaiov_alag2_4, etaiov_k23_1, etaiov_k23_2, etaiov_k23_3, etaiov_k23_4, etaiov_clint_1, etaiov_clint_2, etaiov_clint_3, etaiov_clint_4, etaiov_fpop_1, etaiov_fpop_2, etaiov_fpop_3, etaiov_fpop_4 #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line  d_sim_dosing <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     EVID = 1,     AMT = 25, # mg     TIME = 0,     CMT = c("depot", "depot2")   ) d_sim_obs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     EVID = 0,     AMT = 0,     TIME = seq(0, 8, by = 0.1),     CMT = "lcalmt"   ) d_sim_prep <- rbind(d_sim_dosing, d_sim_obs) d_sim_prep$WT <- 60 # kg, based on table 1 from the paper d_sim_prep$ooc1 <- 1 d_sim_prep$ooc2 <-   d_sim_prep$ooc3 <-   d_sim_prep$ooc4 <- 0 d_sim_pop <- nlmixr2(Xie_2019_agomelatine, data = d_sim_prep, est = "rxSolve", control = list(nStud = 1000)) #> unhandled error message: EE:[lsoda] 70000 steps taken before reaching tout #>  @(lsoda.c:748 #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): some ID(s) could not solve the ODEs correctly; #> These values are replaced with 'NA' d_plot <-   d_sim_pop |>   group_by(time) |>   summarize(     Q05_calmt = quantile(calmt, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50_calmt = quantile(calmt, probs = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95_calmt = quantile(calmt, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     prob_blq_calmt = sum(calmt < 0.046, na.rm = TRUE) / n(),      Q05_c3oh = quantile(c3oh, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50_c3oh = quantile(c3oh, probs = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95_c3oh = quantile(c3oh, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     prob_blq_c3oh = sum(c3oh < 0.460, na.rm = TRUE) / n(),      Q05_c7dm = quantile(c7dm, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50_c7dm = quantile(c7dm, probs = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95_c7dm = quantile(c7dm, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     prob_blq_c7dm = sum(c7dm < 0.137, na.rm = TRUE) / n()   ) |>   ungroup() ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_calmt, ymin = Q05_calmt, ymax = Q95_calmt)) +   geom_ribbon(colour = NA, linetype = "63", fill = "gray", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.046, linetype = "63", colour = "gray") +   scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.046, labels = "LLOQ")) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = paste0("Plasma agomelatine concentration (", conc_unit, ")
Median and 90% prediction intervals")   ) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.046, : log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = prob_blq_calmt)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Probability of BLQ plasma agomelatine concentration"   ) ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_c3oh, ymin = Q05_c3oh, ymax = Q95_c3oh)) +   geom_ribbon(colour = NA, linetype = "63", fill = "gray", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.460, linetype = "63", colour = "gray") +   scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.460, labels = "LLOQ")) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = paste0("Plasma 3-hydroxy-agomelatine concentration (", conc_unit, ")
Median and 90% prediction intervals")   ) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.46, : log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = prob_blq_c3oh)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Probability of BLQ plasma 3-hydroxy-agomelatine concentration"   ) ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_c7dm, ymin = Q05_c7dm, ymax = Q95_c7dm)) +   geom_ribbon(colour = NA, linetype = "63", fill = "gray", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.137, linetype = "63", colour = "gray") +   scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.137, labels = "LLOQ")) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = paste0("Plasma 7-desmethyl-agomelatine concentration (", conc_unit, ")
Median and 90% prediction intervals")   ) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = identity, breaks = 0.137, : log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. ggplot(d_plot, aes(x = time, y = prob_blq_c7dm)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (hours)",     y = "Probability of BLQ plasma 7-desmethyl-agomelatine concentration"   )

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Citation: Xu Z, Bouman-Thio E, Comisar C, Frederick B, Van Hartingsveldt B, Marini JC, Davis HM, Zhou H. Pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics safety human anti-IL-6 monoclonal antibody (sirukumab) healthy subjects first--human study. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2011;72(2):270-281. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2125.2011.03964.x Description: Two-compartment population PK model sirukumab (anti-IL-6 human IgG1 kappa monoclonal antibody, CNTO 136) healthy adults following single intravenous infusion, first-order elimination central compartment allometric body-weight scaling (Xu 2011). Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol 72(2):270-281

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Simulate sirukumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Xu et al. 2011. Sirukumab (CNTO 136) human anti-IL-6 monoclonal antibody developed inflammatory autoimmune diseases. Xu 2011 study double-blind, placebo-controlled, ascending single-dose first--human (FIH) trial healthy adult volunteers (study C0524T01). two-compartment model zero-order IV input first-order elimination central compartment adequately described serum concentration-time data pooled across dose cohorts.

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Xu 2011 Table 1: 45 healthy adults enrolled 34 received sirukumab across six cohorts (0.3, 1, 3, 10 mg/kg mixed-sex cohorts six subjects except 10 mg/kg cohort n = 4, separate 6 mg/kg cohorts six male six female subjects evaluate sex). Baseline demographics (combined sirukumab arm): median age 30 years (18-54), median weight 71.3 kg (49-99), 16% female, 71% White / 16% Black / 9% Asian / 4% . population PK dataset comprised 34 sirukumab-treated subjects. subject developed antibodies sirukumab 20-week follow-. information available programmatically readModelDb("Xu_2011_sirukumab") (returned function’s body holds population list literal).

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: per-subject body weights demographics released paper. build 34-subject virtual cohort whose cohort sizes match Table 1 exactly whose weights sampled log-normal distribution bounded published 49-99 kg range median 71.3 kg.
code: set.seed(2011)  cohorts <- tribble(   ~treatment,          ~dose_mgkg, ~n,   "0.3 mg/kg",            0.3,      6,   "1 mg/kg",              1.0,      6,   "3 mg/kg",              3.0,      6,   "6 mg/kg (male)",       6.0,      6,   "6 mg/kg (female)",     6.0,      6,   "10 mg/kg",            10.0,      4 )  pop <- cohorts %>%   group_by(treatment, dose_mgkg) %>%   summarise(     ID = list(seq_len(n[1])),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   tidyr::unnest(ID) %>%   mutate(     ID = dplyr::row_number(),     WT = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(dplyr::n(), meanlog = log(71.3), sdlog = 0.15), 49), 99),     amt = dose_mgkg * WT   )  pop #> # A tibble: 34 × 5 #>    treatment dose_mgkg    ID    WT   amt #>    <chr>         <dbl> <int> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1 0.3 mg/kg       0.3     1  64.6  19.4 #>  2 0.3 mg/kg       0.3     2  71.0  21.3 #>  3 0.3 mg/kg       0.3     3  69.3  20.8 #>  4 0.3 mg/kg       0.3     4  62.3  18.7 #>  5 0.3 mg/kg       0.3     5  86.8  26.0 #>  6 0.3 mg/kg       0.3     6  63.0  18.9 #>  7 1 mg/kg         1       7  68.6  68.6 #>  8 1 mg/kg         1       8  73.9  73.9 #>  9 1 mg/kg         1       9  66.9  66.9 #> 10 1 mg/kg         1      10  64.8  64.8 #> # ℹ 24 more rows

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Sirukumab given single 10-15 min IV infusion. use 15 min infusion (dur = 15/60/24 day) doses delivered time 0 central compartment. Sampling times track paper’s PK-profile design: dense sampling first 48 h, 140 days (20 weeks) follow-.
code: obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0,   1,   by = 1/24),           # hourly on day 1   seq(1,   7,   by = 0.5),            # twice daily through day 7   seq(7,  28,   by = 1),              # daily through day 28   seq(28, 140,  by = 7)               # weekly through week 20 )))  infusion_dur <- 15 / (60 * 24)          # 15 min in days  dose_rows <- pop %>%   transmute(     ID, treatment, dose_mgkg, WT,     time = 0,     amt,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "central",     dur  = infusion_dur,     dv   = NA_real_   )  obs_rows <- pop %>%   select(ID, treatment, dose_mgkg, WT) %>%   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) %>%   mutate(     amt  = NA_real_,     evid = 0L,     cmt  = NA_character_,     dur  = NA_real_,     dv   = NA_real_   )  events <- bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) %>%   arrange(ID, time, desc(evid))

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Simulate -subject variability spread across virtual cohort matches paper’s individual variability.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Xu_2011_sirukumab")  events_sim <- events %>% rename(id = ID) # `treatment` and `dose_mgkg` are already on every row of `events_sim`. # Carry them through rxSolve via `keep = ...` rather than a fragile # post-hoc left_join from `pop`. sim <- rxSolve(object = mod, events = events_sim, returnType = "data.frame",                keep = c("treatment", "dose_mgkg")) %>%   as_tibble() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Figure 3: concentration-time profiles by dose
title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Figure 3 Xu 2011 shows mean (SD) sirukumab serum concentrations vs. time dose cohort log-linear scale following single IV dose.
code: fig3 <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) %>%   group_by(treatment, time) %>%   summarise(     mean_Cc = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     sd_Cc   = sd(Cc,   na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig3, aes(x = time, y = mean_Cc, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time since dose (days)",     y       = "Sirukumab concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     colour  = "Cohort",     title   = "Figure 3 replication - simulated sirukumab exposure by dose cohort",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3 of Xu et al. 2011 (mean across virtual cohort; cohort sizes match Table 1)."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Compute non-compartmental Cmax, Tmax, AUC(0,inf), terminal half-life per subject per cohort compare published NCA values (Xu 2011 Table 3). PKNCA formula groups concentrations treatment + id summaries per-cohort.
code: # Keep t = 0 (Cc = 0 before infusion starts) so PKNCA can integrate AUC from the # dose time; filter only true NA values. nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= 0, !is.na(Cc)) %>%   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   transmute(id = ID, time = 0, amt, treatment) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  sim_nca <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) %>%   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "aucinf.obs", "half.life")) %>%   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) %>%   summarise(     mean = mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     sd   = sd(PPORRES,   na.rm = TRUE),     med  = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  sim_nca #> # A tibble: 18 × 5 #>    treatment        PPTESTCD      mean      sd     med #>    <chr>            <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 0.3 mg/kg        aucinf.obs   49.4    5.85    48.5  #>  2 0.3 mg/kg        cmax          5.85   0.517    6.03 #>  3 0.3 mg/kg        half.life    21.4    7.75    19.2  #>  4 1 mg/kg          aucinf.obs  201.    39.7    194.   #>  5 1 mg/kg          cmax         24.9    5.64    23.8  #>  6 1 mg/kg          half.life    23.1    6.73    22.1  #>  7 10 mg/kg         aucinf.obs 2330.   746.    2081.   #>  8 10 mg/kg         cmax        212.    34.7    224.   #>  9 10 mg/kg         half.life    22.1    6.24    21.2  #> 10 3 mg/kg          aucinf.obs  641.   309.     588.   #> 11 3 mg/kg          cmax         57.5    7.67    60.4  #> 12 3 mg/kg          half.life    20.8    9.10    22.0  #> 13 6 mg/kg (female) aucinf.obs 1332.   315.    1251.   #> 14 6 mg/kg (female) cmax        119.    19.3    109.   #> 15 6 mg/kg (female) half.life    28.6   13.6     22.3  #> 16 6 mg/kg (male)   aucinf.obs 1300.   318.    1284.   #> 17 6 mg/kg (male)   cmax        129.    24.8    131.   #> 18 6 mg/kg (male)   half.life    22.1    8.16    18.7

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Table 3 Xu 2011 reports (mean +/- SD Cmax AUC(0,inf); median [range] terminal half-life): Simulated NCA vs. Xu 2011 Table 3: Cmax (ug/mL), AUC(0,inf) (ug*day/mL), terminal t1/2 (days). Dose-proportionality holds exactly construction packaged model (linear clearance, absorption nonlinearity), mean Cmax mean AUC scale linearly mg/kg dose across six cohorts. paper noted observed median terminal half-life ranged 18.5 29.6 days across cohorts despite mechanistic dose dependence; cohort-level variation consistent - subject variability small n rather structural effect, simulated median half-life falls within reported range.
code: published <- tribble(   ~treatment,          ~cmax_pub_mean, ~cmax_pub_sd, ~auc_pub_mean, ~auc_pub_sd, ~thalf_pub_median,   "0.3 mg/kg",           7.9,            1.4,          82.1,          20.4,        20.9,   "1 mg/kg",            19.2,            1.8,         167.3,          23.6,        19.9,   "3 mg/kg",            60.1,           14.0,         540.2,         175.2,        18.5,   "6 mg/kg (male)",    116.3,           11.6,        1225.0,         378.2,        29.6,   "6 mg/kg (female)",  118.6,           19.2,        1262.0,         307.0,        25.0,   "10 mg/kg",          248.8,           61.7,        2164.7,         658.5,        21.0 )  sim_cmax <- sim_nca %>% filter(PPTESTCD == "cmax") %>%   transmute(treatment, cmax_sim_mean = mean, cmax_sim_sd = sd) sim_auc  <- sim_nca %>% filter(PPTESTCD == "aucinf.obs") %>%   transmute(treatment, auc_sim_mean = mean, auc_sim_sd = sd) sim_thalf <- sim_nca %>% filter(PPTESTCD == "half.life") %>%   transmute(treatment, thalf_sim_median = med)  compare <- published %>%   left_join(sim_cmax,  by = "treatment") %>%   left_join(sim_auc,   by = "treatment") %>%   left_join(sim_thalf, by = "treatment") %>%   mutate(     cmax_pct_diff = 100 * (cmax_sim_mean - cmax_pub_mean) / cmax_pub_mean,     auc_pct_diff  = 100 * (auc_sim_mean  - auc_pub_mean)  / auc_pub_mean   )  knitr::kable(compare,              digits  = 2,              caption = "Simulated NCA vs. Xu 2011 Table 3: Cmax (ug/mL), AUC(0,inf) (ug*day/mL), terminal t1/2 (days).")

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Weight distribution. Xu 2011 publishes summary statistics (median 71.3 kg, range 49-99 kg). draw weights log-normal distribution median 71.3 kg log-scale SD 0.15, clipped reported range. Sex. 6 mg/kg cohorts stratified sex paper evaluate sex effects, sex covariate final PK model. simulation treat “6 mg/kg (male)” “6 mg/kg (female)” cohort labels ; structural parameters identical. Time-varying body weight. paper describes weight baseline covariate allometric scaling. hold WT constant 20-week follow-. Residual error magnitude. Xu 2011 Table 4 lists “Additive error” units ug/L value 0.0228. preserve value converting model concentration units (ug/mL): 0.0228 ug/L = 2.28e-5 ug/mL. additive term therefore numerically negligible compared 21.7% proportional component across observed concentration range, overall residual dominated proportional term. ADA. subjects developed antibodies sirukumab study, ADA model covariate. Populations ADA incidence non-trivial (e.g., patients chronic dosing) outside validated scope model.

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Structural model: 2-compartment zero-order IV input central, first-order elimination central, allometric weight scaling CL Q (exponent 0.75) V1 V2 (exponent 1) relative 70 kg reference. IIV: diagonal matrix CL, V1, Q, V2; -diagonal terms reported source. Terminal half-life predicted structural parameters typical 70 kg subject approximately 21 days (derived CL, V1, Q, V2), consistent 18.5-29.6 day range observed cohort-median half-lives reported Table 3.

title: Xu_2011_sirukumab
text: Xu Z, Bouman-Thio E, Comisar C, Frederick B, Van Hartingsveldt B, Marini JC, Davis HM, Zhou H. Pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics safety human anti-IL-6 monoclonal antibody (sirukumab) healthy subjects first--human study. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2011;72(2):270-281. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2125.2011.03964.x

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: Citation: Xu C, Su Y, Paccaly , Kanamaluru V. Population Pharmacokinetics Sarilumab Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(11):1455-1467. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00765-1 Description: Two-compartment population PK model sarilumab adults rheumatoid arthritis (Xu 2019), first-order SC absorption parallel linear plus Michaelis-Menten (target-mediated) elimination central compartment. Article: Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(11):1455-1467 (open access via PMC6856490)

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: Xu 2019 pooled 12 clinical studies (7 phase , 1 phase II, 4 phase III) final population PK dataset 1770 adults moderate--severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA) inadequate response methotrexate, TNF inhibitors, DMARDs. dataset contained 7676 serum sarilumab concentrations SC doses spanning 50-200 mg single repeated administrations (weekly every-2-weeks). marketed regimen 200 mg SC every 2 weeks (Q2W), reduction 150 mg Q2W available safety management. Baseline demographics Table 2: median age 53 years (range 18-87), 83% female, median body weight 71.0 kg (range 31.5-176.9), 88% White / 6% Asian / 3% Black / 3% , median baseline serum albumin 38 g/L, median creatinine clearance 104.8 mL/min, median baseline CRP 14.2 mg/L, ADA-positive 18%, concomitant methotrexate 91%. Drug-product formulations: DP1 4%, DP2 20%, DP3 (commercial) 76%. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Xu_2019_sarilumab")$population.

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Xu 2019 Table 3. reference covariate values 71 kg female, ADA-negative, non-DP2 formulation, albumin/ULN ratio 0.78 (.e. 38 g/L typical ULN 48.7 g/L), 1.73 x CrCl / BSA 100 mL/min/1.73 m^2, baseline CRP 14.2 mg/L.

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: Michaelis-Menten plus linear clearance. Xu 2019 parameterizes elimination central compartment sum first-order linear clearance (CL/F) saturable Michaelis-Menten elimination apparent parameters Vm (mg/day) Km (mg/L). ODE therefore implemented explicitly rather via linCmt(). CV% log-normal variance. Xu 2019 Table 3 reports -subject variability CV% linear-parameter scale. nlmixr2 convention log-normal IIV log-transformed parameter; conversion omega^2 = log(CV^2 + 1) applied ini(). Vm / CLO/F correlation. Table 3 reports -0.566 correlation etas Vm CLO/F, encoded 2x2 block ini() -diagonal computed r times sqrt(var_vmax * var_cl). Log-additive residual error. Xu 2019 fit log-transformed concentrations additive residual error log scale (NONMEM log-EPS, sigma^2 = 0.395). linear scale maps proportional residual error propSd = sqrt(0.395) = 0.6285. SEX encoding. Xu 2019 codes SEX=1 female final CLO/F equation reports male patients higher CL lower AUC0-14d. gives multiplicative effect 0.846 SEXF=1 (female), operator-confirmed model extraction (see SEXF entry covariateData). canonical SEXF column nlmixr2lib uses 1 = female, effect applied e_sexf_cl^SEXF. CRCL canonical column. Xu 2019 defines Vm covariate term (1.73 * CrCl / BSA / 100)^theta13 CrCl mL/min BSA m^2. canonical CRCL column carries precomputed 1.73 * CrCl / BSA value (units mL/min/1.73 m^2) reference value 100.

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Xu 2019 Table 2 demographics. subject-level observed data released paper. Two regimens simulated parallel: labelled 200 mg SC Q2W adult RA dose dose-reduced 150 mg SC Q2W option. dosing horizon extended well past nominal half-life (~8-10 days linear range) ensure final Q2W cycle steady state.
code: set.seed(20260419) # Cohort size: 200 subjects is enough to stabilise the 5/50/95 percentile # bands in the VPC (Figure 3 reproduction) and the PKNCA distribution # summaries. Doubling to 400 did not visibly change the published bands # but made the simulation the largest in the package (~28 min on a single # core), which caps pkgdown's parallel-article build via Amdahl's law. n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id       = seq_len(n_subj),   WT       = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 71, sd = 17),   40, 165)),   SEXF     = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.83),   ADA_POS  = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.18),   FORM_DP2 = 0L,                          # commercial DP3 formulation for labelled regimen   ALBR     = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 0.78, sd = 0.08), 0.50, 1.05)),   CRCL = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 100,  sd = 25),   40,  200)),   CRP    = pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(14.2) - 0.5 * 0.9^2, 0.9), 0.5) ) tau <- 14                # Q2W dosing interval (days) # Dose count: the linear-range half-life is ~8-10 days, so the 4-5 # half-lives needed for >95% steady-state are reached by dose 7-8. Twelve # Q2W doses (168 days, ~17 half-lives) puts the last cycle safely at SS # with no signal from earlier cycles, while the previous value of 50 # doses (700 days) was a 10x oversample that dominated run time. n_doses <- 12 dose_days <- seq(0, tau * (n_doses - 1), by = tau)  build_events <- function(cohort, dose_amt, treatment) {   ev_dose <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L,                   treatment = treatment)   # Observation grid: dense only where the published figures need   # resolution. Days 0-84 (six cycles) reproduce the Xu 2019 Figure 3   # VPC; the final 14-day interval (ss_start to ss_end, days 154-168)   # feeds the steady-state plot and the PKNCA computations. Between   # those two windows a weekly grid is enough to keep the ODE solver   # moving without contributing to any figure. The earlier uniform   # daily-plus-4-per-dose grid over all 700 days produced ~900   # observations per subject per arm, which (x 400 subjects x 2 arms)   # pushed the event table past 750k rows and drove most of the cost.   ss_start <- tau * (n_doses - 1)   ss_end   <- ss_start + tau   early_doses <- dose_days[dose_days <= 84]   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(     seq(0, 84, by = 1),            # daily through the VPC window     early_doses + 0.25,            # keep fine near-dose structure     early_doses + 1,               #   so the SC absorption peak is     early_doses + 3,               #   resolved in Figure 3     early_doses + 7,     seq(84, ss_start, by = 7),     # coarse bridge between windows     seq(ss_start, ss_end, by = 0.5) # dense SS cycle for NCA / plot   )))   ev_obs <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L,                   treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, treatment,                   WT, SEXF, ADA_POS, FORM_DP2, ALBR, CRCL, CRP) }  events_200 <- build_events(cohort, 200, "200mg_Q2W") events_150 <- build_events(cohort, 150, "150mg_Q2W") events <- dplyr::bind_rows(events_200, events_150)

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Xu_2019_sarilumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' conc_unit <- mod$units[["concentration"]] keep_cols <- c("WT", "SEXF", "ADA_POS", "FORM_DP2",                "ALBR", "CRCL", "CRP", "treatment")  sim <- lapply(split(events, events$treatment), function(ev) {   out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = ev, keep = keep_cols)   as.data.frame(out) }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: Xu 2019 Figure 3 shows model-predicted mean +/- SD serum concentrations first dosing cycles 150 mg 200 mg Q2W SC regimens. block reproduces comparison using 5th/50th/95th percentile bands first 84 days (6 dosing cycles).
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0, time <= 84) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = paste0("Sarilumab Cc (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profiles: 150 vs 200 mg SC Q2W",     caption = "Virtual RA cohort (N = 200); first 6 dosing cycles."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: final dosing interval simulation window (days 154 168) used steady-state NCA .
code: ss_start <- tau * (n_doses - 1) ss_end   <- ss_start + tau  ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(treatment, time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes(time - ss_start, Q50, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time within dosing interval (days)",     y = paste0("Sarilumab Cc (", conc_unit, ")"),     title = "Steady-state Q2W cycle",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval over the final 14-day interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis final (steady-state) Q2W dosing interval. Compute Cmax, Cmin/Ctrough, AUC0-tau, Cavg per simulated subject treatment.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- dplyr::bind_rows(   cohort |> dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 200, treatment = "200mg_Q2W"),   cohort |> dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 150, treatment = "150mg_Q2W") ) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = tau,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■        84% |  ETA:  1s summary(nca_res) #>  start end treatment   N     auclast        cmax         cmin              tmax #>      0  14 150mg_Q2W 200 60.2 [55.5] 8.80 [46.1] 0.565 [91.5] 3.00 [1.50, 5.00] #>      0  14 200mg_Q2W 200  120 [47.9] 15.3 [39.4]   1.36 [135] 3.50 [1.50, 5.50] #>          cav #>  4.30 [55.5] #>  8.59 [47.9] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: compare published Table 4 mean exposures block simulates typical patient (IIV zeroed, reference covariate values: 71 kg female, ADA-negative, DP3 formulation, ALBR = 0.78, CRCL = 100, CRP = 14.2) extracts Cmax, Ctrough, AUC0-14d steady state. Typical-patient steady-state exposures (IIV zeroed) vs. Xu 2019 Table 4 mean values. differences within ~10%.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 71, SEXF = 1L, ADA_POS = 0L, FORM_DP2 = 0L,   ALBR = 0.78, CRCL = 100, CRP = 14.2 )  ev_typ <- function(dose) {   ev_dose <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   obs_times <- sort(unique(c(     seq(ss_start, ss_end, by = 0.05),     dose_days   )))   ev_obs <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                   WT, SEXF, ADA_POS, FORM_DP2, ALBR, CRCL, CRP) }  sim_typ_200 <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(200))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka' sim_typ_150 <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(150))) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka'  ss_metrics <- function(sim_df, label) {   ss <- sim_df |>     dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>     dplyr::arrange(time)   tibble::tibble(     treatment   = label,     Cmax_sim    = max(ss$Cc),     Ctrough_sim = ss$Cc[which.max(ss$time)],     AUC_sim     = sum(diff(ss$time) *                       (head(ss$Cc, -1) + tail(ss$Cc, -1)) / 2)   ) }  typ_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(   ss_metrics(sim_typ_200, "200 mg Q2W"),   ss_metrics(sim_typ_150, "150 mg Q2W") )  published <- tibble::tibble(   treatment   = c("200 mg Q2W", "150 mg Q2W"),   Cmax_pub    = c(35.6, 20.0),   Ctrough_pub = c(16.5, 6.35),   AUC_pub     = c(395,  202) )  comparison <- published |>   dplyr::left_join(typ_tbl, by = "treatment") |>   dplyr::mutate(     Cmax_pct_diff    = 100 * (Cmax_sim - Cmax_pub) / Cmax_pub,     Ctrough_pct_diff = 100 * (Ctrough_sim - Ctrough_pub) / Ctrough_pub,     AUC_pct_diff     = 100 * (AUC_sim - AUC_pub) / AUC_pub   )  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,   caption = paste("Typical-patient steady-state exposures (IIV zeroed) vs.",                   "Xu 2019 Table 4 mean values. All differences within ~10%."))

title: Xu_2019_sarilumab
text: SEX encoding (operator-confirmed). Xu 2019 reports SEX covariate multiplier theta17 = 0.846 CLO/F equation narrates male patients higher apparent clearance (lower AUC0-14d). theta17 < 1 reduces clearance, SEX final-model equation must indicate female (SEX=1=female). interpretation confirmed operator extraction applied via canonical SEXF covariate (1 = female). Supplement reviewed. Clinical Pharmacokinetics electronic supplementary material (NONMEM control stream supplementary tables) downloaded extraction time (journal’s CDN returned JS-gated “preparing download” page rather DOCX). parameters model therefore sourced published main text (Tables 2-4 covariate-model equations p. 1458-1459). supplement exposes different digits precision, update may warranted. CRCL pre-computation. Xu 2019 writes renal covariate term (1.73 * CrCl / BSA / 100)^theta13. canonical CRCL column nlmixr2lib carries pre-computed 1.73 * CrCl / BSA value mL/min/1.73 m^2, reference 100. ALBR reference. typical-patient reference ALBR = 0.78 corresponds median serum albumin 38 g/L typical laboratory ULN 48.7 g/L (per Xu 2019 Table 2 Vm covariate-model narrative). Users applying model cohort different reference ULN supply ALBR = observed albumin / ULN directly. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Body weight drawn N(71, 17) kg truncated [40, 165]; albumin ratio N(0.78, 0.08) truncated [0.50, 1.05]; CRCL N(100, 25) truncated [40, 200]; baseline CRP log-normal median 14.2 mg/L geometric SD exp(0.9) ~ 2.5x. ranges approximate Xu 2019 Table 2 drawn observed subject-level data, publicly released. Typical-patient NCA. comparison table uses typical-patient simulation (IIV zeroed ) Table 4 Xu 2019 reports mean exposures labelled / dose-reduced typical patient rather distribution. full-cohort PKNCA block documents subject-level variability aggregates via median +/- 90% interval. unit conversion needed. Dose units mg concentration units mg/L; central / vc therefore yields mg/L directly, matching Xu 2019.

what: Zolbetuximab population PK in gastric / GEJ adenocarcinoma
title: Yamada_2025_zolbetuximab
text: Simulate zolbetuximab concentration-time profiles using final time-dependent-clearance (TDC) population PK model Yamada et al. (2025) patients locally advanced unresectable metastatic gastric gastroesophageal junction (G/GEJ) adenocarcinoma. source paper pooled 5066 serum concentrations 714 subjects across eight clinical studies (three phase 1, three phase 2 — MONO, FAST, ILUSTRO — two phase 3 — SPOTLIGHT GLOW). Zolbetuximab IgG1 monoclonal antibody claudin 18.2 (CLDN18.2). model two-compartment structure zero-order IV input time-dependent total clearance CL(t)=CLss+CLTexp⁡(−Kdecayt),   CL(t) = CL_{ss} + CL_{T}\,\exp(-K_{\text{decay}}\,t), tt time first dose. Covariate effects enter power models continuous covariates additive fractional-change dummy-variable effects categorical covariates, matching NONMEM parameterization reported Yamada 2025 Table 1 (final TDC model). Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/cts.70280 PubMed (PMID 40604351): https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40604351/

title: Yamada_2025_zolbetuximab
text: source paper reports population summary statistics publish per-subject baseline covariates. cohort approximates typical G/GEJ adenocarcinoma phase 3 population centered simulated parameters bracket Yamada 2025 Figure 1 reference subject.
code: set.seed(2025) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID       = seq_len(n_subj),   BSA      = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 1.70, 0.18), 1.20), 2.40),   ALB      = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 39.1, 4.5), 25), 52),   # g/L   HGB      = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, 118,  16),  70), 170),  # g/L   TBILI    = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(0.45), 0.45), 0.10), 2.00), # mg/dL   SEXF     = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.34),          # ~34% female in phase 3 SPOTLIGHT/GLOW   PRIOR_GAST     = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.30),          # ~30% prior gastrectomy   COMB_EOX = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.04)           # EOX used in a small subset )

previous_headings: Zolbetuximab population PK in gastric / GEJ adenocarcinoma
title: Yamada_2025_zolbetuximab
text: Phase 3 regimen: 800 mg/m^2 IV loading (cycle 1 day 1), 600 mg/m^2 IV every 3 weeks. Infusion duration typically 2 hours. Simulate eight 3-week cycles (168 days follow-).
code: infusion_dur_hr <- 2 infusion_dur_day <- infusion_dur_hr / 24  # Per-subject doses by BSA. d_load <- pop %>% mutate(   TIME = 0,   AMT  = 800 * BSA,        # mg   RATE = AMT / infusion_dur_day,   EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_ )  d_maint <- tidyr::crossing(pop, TIME = seq(21, 21 * 7, by = 21)) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = 600 * BSA,     RATE = AMT / infusion_dur_day,     EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_   )  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 21, by = 0.25),   seq(21, 168, by = 0.5) )))  d_obs <- tidyr::crossing(pop, TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, RATE = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_)  d_sim_q3w <- bind_rows(d_load, d_maint, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Yamada_2025_zolbetuximab
text: Compute NCA parameters steady-state 42-day window reported Yamada 2025 Table 2. Q3W regimen covers cycles 6-7 (doses days 105 126 follow-, observation window days 105-147); Q2W regimen covers three cycles 14 days (days 112-154). group regimen subject. PKNCA summary steady-state 42-day window. Compare Cmax, Cmin, AUClast ratios (Q2W / Q3W) Yamada 2025 Table 2 GMRs (0.792, 1.192, 1.000).
code: ss_q3w <- sim_q3w %>%   filter(time >= 105, time <= 147, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(time_rel  = time - 105, treatment = "Q3W_800_600") %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  ss_q2w <- sim_q2w %>%   filter(time >= 112, time <= 154, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(time_rel  = time - 112, treatment = "Q2W_800_400") %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_conc <- bind_rows(ss_q3w, ss_q2w)  nca_dose <- bind_rows(   pop %>% transmute(     ID,     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 600 * BSA,     treatment = "Q3W_800_600"   ),   pop %>% transmute(     ID,     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 400 * BSA,     treatment = "Q2W_800_400"   ) )  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID)  data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start   = 0,     end     = 42,     cmax    = TRUE,     tmax    = TRUE,     cmin    = TRUE,     auclast = TRUE   ) )  nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    42% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■        84% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste(     "PKNCA summary for the steady-state 42-day window.",     "Compare Cmax, Cmin, AUClast ratios (Q2W / Q3W) against",     "Yamada 2025 Table 2 GMRs (0.792, 1.192, 1.000)."   ) )
