Differences simulated cohort median typical-subject point expected fall within 20% model simulated covariate distributions consistent paper.

title: Yin_2021_trastuzumabDeruxtecan
text: Released-drug compartment implemented. Yin 2021 also fits 1-compartment model released drug (DXd payload) time-varying release-rate constant Krel = 0.0159 / h * Cycle^-0.137 * (0.830 Cycle > 1), CL_drug = 19.2 L/h, V_drug = 17 * BSA L (fixed), covariate effects body weight, total bilirubin, AST, age, itraconazole ritonavir coadministration, two formulation flags. released-drug input intact T-DXd described “time course intact trastuzumab deruxtecan concentrations, adjusted drug-antibody ratio molar mass,” paper give numerical values DAR / molar-mass conversion factor. cycle index derived time (paper assumes q3w dosing throughout). factors require numerical inputs source disk; released drug therefore left packaged model. future model file (separate .R) implement released-drug PK numerical assumptions settled. Covariate-effect parameterization. Yin 2021 reports categorical- covariate effects (Country = Japan, Sex = Male) fractional multipliers linear scale: (1.174, male), (0.903, Japanese). Table 1 reports underlying THETA values (0.174 -0.0970) multiplier = 1 + theta * indicator. packaged model uses linear-fractional form match published equations exactly. distinct exponential form exp(theta * indicator) used , e.g., Bajaj 2017. Sex encoding. Yin 2021’s Sex column male-indicator (1 male, 0 female) female reference category. packaged model stores sex canonical SEXF column (1 = female, 0 = male) derives male-indicator inside model() (1 - SEXF), preserving paper’s reference values CL_intact V1_intact. Country encoding. Yin 2021’s Country column Japan-indicator (1 Japanese-country enrollment, 0 non-Japan). Stored canonical REGION_JAPAN column (formerly COUNTRY_JPN). Race country highly confounded analysis dataset (correlation -0.81) paper retained country, dropping race; packaged model follows paper. Reference covariates. reference patient values (female, non-Japan, 57.8 kg, 40 g/L albumin, 57 mm tumor size) taken verbatim Yin 2021 Figure 4’s caption. paper’s population median body weight (57.8 kg) materially lower ~70 kg typical caucasian-dominated trials analysis data set dominated Japanese phase cohort. Variance interpretation. Yin 2021 Table 1 reports -patient variability Magnitude (%CV) underlying variance -patient variability block. percent-CV column matches sqrt(omega^2) (small-CV approximation), exact log-normal %CV = sqrt(exp(omega^2) - 1). packaged model uses variances reported (0.0630, 0.0250, 0.0900, 0.430), reproduces paper’s omega^2_j exactly yields slightly larger linear-scale CVs paper’s Magnitude (%CV) column high values (e.g., 73% vs 65.6% V2_intact). Residual-error units. Yin 2021 Table 1 reports additive residual SD 1,181 ng/mL. packaged model uses ug/mL concentration unit, additive SD stored 1.181 ug/mL. --scope covariates. Yin 2021’s full-model exploration also examined LDH, HER2 status, tumor type, age, formulation, race, T-DXd dose; covariates retained final model (WT, ALB, TUMSZ, SEXF, REGION_JAPAN) implemented . Virtual cohort. Demographics simulated match paper’s reference subject span reported 5th-95th percentile ranges body weight albumin. Sex distribution set ~94% female match breast-cancer-dominated cohort; country = Japan set ~30% reflect Japanese phase study contribution. Joint covariate structure (e.g., correlation WT SEXF, REGION_JAPAN ALB) simulated. Infusion duration. simulations use 30-minute IV infusion (DUR = 30 / 60 / 24 day), standard administration schedule T-DXd q3w.

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Citation: Yu H, Graham G, David OJ, Kahn JM, Savelieva M, Pigeolet E, Das Gupta , Pingili R, Willi R, Ramanathan K, Kieseier BC, Hach T, Aslanis V, Bagger Y, Ravenstijn P. Population Pharmacokinetic-B Cell Modeling Ofatumumab Patients Relapsing Multiple Sclerosis. CNS Drugs. 2022;36(3):283-300. doi:10.1007/s40263-021-00895-w Description: Population PK / B-cell-count model subcutaneous ofatumumab adults relapsing multiple sclerosis (Yu 2022) Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40263-021-00895-w Yu et al. (2022) developed coupled population PK / B-cell-count model ofatumumab (fully human anti-CD20 IgG1 monoclonal antibody) adults relapsing forms multiple sclerosis. PK side quasi-steady-state (QSS) approximation Mager-Krzyzanski TMDD framework two drug compartments (central + peripheral) first-order SC absorption estimated bioavailability F. CD20 receptor synthesis decays exponentially ksyn0 ksyn_inf rate constant kdes capture slight upward drift trough concentrations across phase 3 ASCLEPIOS studies. B-cell side indirect-response model: free ofatumumab concentration stimulates B-cell lysis sigmoid Emax function; B cells distribute central compartment (sharing volume PK central compartment) peripheral compartment specific B cells.

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: pooled analysis dataset combines five clinical studies (one phase 1/2 IV plus four phase 2/3 SC trials): OMS115102, MIRROR, APLIOS, ASCLEPIOS , ASCLEPIOS II. final PK-B-cell model fit 1486 adults relapsing MS (RMS) relapsing-remitting MS (RRMS), median age 38 years (range 18-56), median weight 70 kg (range 40.5-171.6), 67.8 % female, predominantly White (91 %). Median baseline CD19+ B cell count 200 cells/uL (range 0-1520). Patients across SC studies received licensed regimen three weekly 20-mg loading doses followed 20 mg SC q4w, delivered prefilled syringe (PFS, n=1320) autoinjector (AI, n=141; APLIOS ). OMS115102 cohort received IV infusions (100/300/700 mg; 700-mg arm excluded PK analysis -prediction seen dose). MIRROR cohort received SC dose-finding regimens (3-60 mg q12w 60 mg q4w). PK dataset: 9168 plasma concentrations 1440 patients (placebo 700-mg cohorts excluded). PD dataset: 17,158 CD19+ B-cell counts 1486 patients; ASCLEPIOS B-cell counts modelled interval-censored (LLOQ bins 0/5/15/25 cells/uL). Anti-drug antibody incidence < 2 % detectable PK B-cell impact (Yu 2022 Methods “Data” + Table 1, Table 2, Results “Data”). full population descriptor available programmatically:
code: str(rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Yu_2022_ofatumumab"))$meta$population) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> List of 15 #>  $ n_subjects           : int 1486 #>  $ n_studies            : int 5 #>  $ age_range            : chr "18-56 years" #>  $ age_median           : chr "38 years" #>  $ weight_range         : chr "40.5-171.6 kg" #>  $ weight_median        : chr "70.0 kg" #>  $ sex_female_pct       : num 67.8 #>  $ race_ethnicity       : Named num [1:5] 91.1 2.4 2.5 3.2 0.7 #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:5] "White" "Black" "Asian" "Other_AmInd_AlaskaNative" ... #>  $ disease_state        : chr "Adults with relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis (RMS), including relapsing-remitting MS (RRMS); EDSS 0-5.5 at"| __truncated__ #>  $ dose_range           : chr "OMS115102: 100/300/700 mg IV at weeks 0+2 or 24+26 (700 mg arm excluded from PK analysis). MIRROR: 0/3/30/60 mg"| __truncated__ #>  $ regions              : chr "Multi-regional phase 2/3 programme (OMS115102, MIRROR, APLIOS, ASCLEPIOS I, ASCLEPIOS II)." #>  $ studies              : chr "OMS115102 (N=25 IV RRMS), MIRROR (N=231 SC PFS RRMS), APLIOS (N=284 SC AI/PFS RMS), ASCLEPIOS I (N=465 SC PFS R"| __truncated__ #>  $ bcell_baseline_median: chr "200 cells/uL CD19+ (range 0-1520; pooled five-study cohort, Table 2)" #>  $ edss_median          : chr "2.5 (range 0-6, baseline)" #>  $ notes                : chr "Pooled phase 2/3 dataset assembled for the population PK-B cell analysis. PK dataset: 9168 plasma concentration"| __truncated__

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Yu_2022_ofatumumab.R. table collects equation parameter provenance one place.

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Original individual data publicly available. simulate typical-value scenarios (-subject variability turned via rxode2::zeroRe()) figure replication, covariate-stratified typical-value cohort dose-response / weight-effect comparisons Figures 4 7.
code: mod         <- readModelDb("Yu_2022_ofatumumab") mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  # Phase 3 schedule: three weekly loading doses (days 0, 7, 14) followed by # monthly 20-mg doses from week 4 (day 28) onward. The Yu 2022 paper reports # steady-state metrics at ~2 years (weeks 104-108) and a B-cell-repletion # follow-up after dosing stops at 2 years. We dose monthly through week 108 # (day 756) so the inter-dose interval at weeks 104-108 is fully covered for # the steady-state AUC comparison, then follow for an additional year to see # the post-treatment B-cell repletion. loading_days     <- c(0, 7, 14) maintenance_days <- seq(28, 756, by = 28) all_dose_days    <- c(loading_days, maintenance_days) end_followup     <- max(all_dose_days) + 365   # one-year post-last-dose follow-up

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
code: # Helper: simulate a typical subject under the phase 3 SC PFS regimen with # a given dose level and covariate vector, returning Cc and B cell counts. sim_one <- function(dose_mg, wt = 70, age = 38, bcell = 200,                     route_iv = 0, device_ai = 0,                     study_aplios = 0, study_mirror = 0,                     sample_grid = NULL) {   if (is.null(sample_grid)) {     sample_grid <- sort(unique(c(       seq(0, 90, by = 0.5),       seq(91, end_followup, by = 1)     )))   }   ev <- rxode2::et(id = 1)   for (td in all_dose_days) {     ev <- rxode2::et(ev, amt = dose_mg, cmt = "depot", time = td, id = 1)   }   ev <- rxode2::et(ev, time = sample_grid, cmt = "Cc",    id = 1)   ev <- rxode2::et(ev, time = sample_grid, cmt = "Bcell", id = 1)   iCov <- data.frame(id = 1, WT = wt, AGE = age, BLBCELL = bcell,                      ROUTE_IV = route_iv, DEVICE_AI = device_ai,                      STUDY_APLIOS = study_aplios, STUDY_MIRROR = study_mirror)   s <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, ev, iCov = iCov, returnType = "data.frame")   s[!duplicated(s$time), ] }

what: Figure 4 — Dose-response of B cell depletion
title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Yu 2022 Figure 4 shows simulated B cell counts 7, 28, 84, 182, 365, 730 days phase 3 dose schedule (three loading doses followed monthly maintenance) doses 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 40 mg. plot demonstrates 20-mg dose achieves near-complete depletion benefit gained 40 mg.
code: dose_levels <- c(1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 40) sim_doses <- bind_rows(   lapply(dose_levels, function(d) {     sim_one(d) |>       mutate(dose_mg = d)   }) ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma'  eval_days <- c(7, 28, 84, 182, 365, 730) sim_doses_eval <- sim_doses |>   filter(time %in% eval_days) |>   mutate(time_label = factor(paste0(time, " d"),                              levels = paste0(eval_days, " d")))  ggplot(sim_doses_eval,        aes(factor(dose_mg), Bcell)) +   geom_point(size = 2, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_line(aes(group = time_label), colour = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5) +   facet_wrap(~ time_label, ncol = 3) +   scale_y_log10() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 8,  linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Dose (mg, monthly schedule with three weekly loading doses)",     y = "B cell count (cells/uL, log scale)",     title   = "Replicates Figure 4 of Yu 2022 (typical-value lines)",     caption = "Dashed line = B cell target (8 cells/uL); dotted line = LLN (40 cells/uL)."   )

what: Figure 5 — Concentration-time profile under the 20 mg monthly regimen
title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Yu 2022 Figure 5 shows typical-value drug concentration first year last dose 2 years 20 mg SC q4w (three weekly loading doses).
code: sim_20mg <- sim_one(20) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma'  ggplot(sim_20mg, aes(time, Cc)) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Total ofatumumab concentration (mg/L, log scale)",     title   = "Replicates Figure 5 of Yu 2022",     caption = "Typical 70 kg, 38 y subject. Three weekly loading doses (days 0/7/14) then 20 mg q4w."   ) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(): log-10 transformation introduced #> infinite values.

what: Figure 6 — B-cell-count profile under the 20 mg monthly regimen
title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Yu 2022 Figure 6 shows 20 mg monthly regimen achieves median B-cell depletion 8 cells/uL within ~11 days B-cell counts return toward LLN 40 cells/uL ~23 weeks dosing stops 2 years.
code: ggplot(sim_20mg, aes(time, Bcell)) +   geom_line(colour = "darkred", linewidth = 0.7) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 8,  linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "B cell count (cells/uL, log scale)",     title   = "Replicates Figure 6 of Yu 2022",     caption = "Dashed line = B cell target (8 cells/uL); dotted line = LLN (40 cells/uL)."   ) # Day at which simulated B cell count first drops below 8 cells/uL. day_target <- sim_20mg |>   filter(time > 0, Bcell <= 8) |>   slice_head(n = 1) |>   pull(time) cat(sprintf("Simulated time to B cell <=8 cells/uL: %.1f days (paper: 11 days)\n",             day_target)) #> Simulated time to B cell <=8 cells/uL: 11.5 days (paper: 11 days)  # Days post-last-dose to reach LLN (40 cells/uL) again. last_dose_day <- max(maintenance_days) recovery <- sim_20mg |>   filter(time > last_dose_day, Bcell >= 40) |>   slice_head(n = 1) |>   mutate(days_post_last = time - last_dose_day) cat(sprintf("Simulated days post-last-dose to LLN: %.1f days (paper: ~23 weeks ~= 161 days)\n",             recovery$days_post_last)) #> Simulated days post-last-dose to LLN: 123.0 days (paper: ~23 weeks ~= 161 days)

what: Figure 7 — Steady-state AUC by body weight
title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Yu 2022 Figure 7 / Table 4 reports inter-dosing AUC steady state (weeks 104-108) baseline weight: median 33.3 mg.day/L 70 kg, 71.8 % higher 50 kg, 52.0 % lower 110 kg. Steady-state AUC (weeks 104-108) body weight (typical-value).
code: weight_levels <- c(50, 70, 90, 110) auc_window <- c(104 * 7, 108 * 7)   # weeks 104-108 (steady state, 4-week)  sim_by_wt <- bind_rows(lapply(weight_levels, function(w) {   s <- sim_one(20, wt = w, sample_grid = sort(unique(c(     seq(auc_window[1], auc_window[2], by = 0.5),     auc_window   ))))   s |> filter(time >= auc_window[1], time <= auc_window[2]) |>     mutate(WT = w) })) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalkdes', 'etalkep', 'etalr0', 'etalksyninf', 'etalvb', 'etalkout', 'etalemax', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalvc', 'etalksyn0', 'etalq', 'etalb0', 'etalec50', 'etalgamma'  auc_by_wt <- sim_by_wt |>   group_by(WT) |>   summarise(     auc_mg_day_L = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),     .groups = "drop"   ) auc_70 <- auc_by_wt |> filter(WT == 70) |> pull(auc_mg_day_L) auc_by_wt <- auc_by_wt |>   mutate(pct_vs_70 = (auc_mg_day_L / auc_70 - 1) * 100)  knitr::kable(   auc_by_wt, digits = 2,   caption = "Steady-state AUC (weeks 104-108) by body weight (typical-value)." ) cat(sprintf("Paper Table 4 reference: 50 kg +71.8%%, 110 kg -52.0%% relative to 70 kg.\n")) #> Paper Table 4 reference: 50 kg +71.8%, 110 kg -52.0% relative to 70 kg. cat(sprintf("Simulated:                50 kg %+.1f%%, 110 kg %+.1f%%.\n",             auc_by_wt$pct_vs_70[auc_by_wt$WT == 50],             auc_by_wt$pct_vs_70[auc_by_wt$WT == 110])) #> Simulated:                50 kg +68.9%, 110 kg -51.1%.

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Yu 2022 reports steady-state half-life ~11 days ofatumumab 20 mg q4w regimen (Results “Concentration-Time (PK) Simulations”). TMDD-QSS model multi-phasic elimination — dose stopped, ofatumumab first decays apparent slow eigenvalue 2-compartment disposition (~11 days), accelerates receptor pool re-replenishes free-drug-mediated TMDD internalisation takes . paper’s 11 days corresponds first ~60 days post-last-dose decay (pre-LLOQ portion Figure 5 right panel), regime relevant clinical exposure. PKNCA’s automatic terminal-phase selection picks deepest tail gives shorter apparent t1/2; restrict half-life calculation days 14-60 post-last-dose paper-comparable window. PKNCA summary 14-60 day post-last-dose tail typical-value simulation.
code: # Slice 14-60 days post-last-dose to capture the paper-comparable # elimination phase. PKNCA's `start` and `end` arguments delimit the AUC # / half-life window. nca_window <- sim_20mg |>   filter(time >= last_dose_day + 14, time <= last_dose_day + 60, Cc > 0) |>   transmute(id = 1L,             time = time - last_dose_day,   # re-zero to last dose             Cc,             treatment = "20 mg SC q4w SS -> PFS, days 14-60 post-last-dose")  dose_df <- data.frame(id = 1L, time = 0, amt = 20,                       treatment = "20 mg SC q4w SS -> PFS, days 14-60 post-last-dose")  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_window, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(start = 14, end = 60,                         cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                         aucinf.obs = TRUE, half.life = TRUE) nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)  knitr::kable(   as.data.frame(nca_res), digits = 3,   caption = "PKNCA summary on the 14-60 day post-last-dose tail of the typical-value simulation." ) nca_df <- as.data.frame(nca_res) hl_row <- nca_df[nca_df$PPTESTCD == "half.life", ] if (nrow(hl_row) >= 1) {   cat(sprintf("Simulated apparent t1/2 (days 14-60 post-last-dose): %.2f days (paper: ~11 days)\n",               as.numeric(hl_row$PPORRES[1]))) } #> Simulated apparent t1/2 (days 14-60 post-last-dose): 9.26 days (paper: ~11 days)

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: R0 = 32.5 nmol/L * 0.149 = 4.8425 mg/L ksyn0 = 0.985 nmol/L/day * 0.149 = 0.146765 mg/L/day ksyn_inf = 0.0554 nmol/L/day * 0.149 = 0.0082546 mg/L/day KD = 0.167 nmol/L * 0.149 = 0.024883 mg/L Mass-conserving QSS form (vs. paper’s literal equations). Yu 2022’s printed PK equations write peripheral central ODE coefficients kcp = Q/Vc kpc = Q/Vp (Methods “Final Model Description”), evaluated literally conserve mass Vc != Vp — inflow rate peripheral implied peripheral concentration ODE (Vp * (Q/Vc) * Lc = (Q*Vp/Vc) * Lc) match outflow central central concentration ODE. standard mass-conserving 2-compartment form (used Monolix’s TMDD library, parameter estimates obtained) sets analogous coefficients Q/Vc central ODE’s Lp term Q/Vp peripheral ODE’s Lp term, equivalent standard amount-form dAp/dt = Q*(Lc - Lp). file uses standard mass-conserving form. numerical impact small study (discrepancy scales Vp/Vc = 2.8/2.62 = 1.07, ~7 % affected fluxes) reported parameter estimates interpreted belonging standard parameterisation. Block 3 IIV typo (Vb / kout / Emax). Table 3 lists “Corr_kout_Vb” twice (-0.336 0.423) missing Corr_kout_Emax entry, although text describes block covering Vb, kout, Emax — three pairwise correlations expected. parsimonious interpretation second 0.423 entry typo Corr_kout_Emax. implied 3 x 3 correlation matrix [[1, -0.336, 0.28]; [-0.336, 1, 0.423]; [0.28, 0.423, 1]] positive semidefinite (det approximately 0.855), supporting reading. model file uses typo-corrected interpretation. Time-varying kdeg. paper writes kdeg = ksyn(t) / R0, makes receptor degradation rate time-dependent; model file implements directly (kdeg_t <- ksyn_t / r0). baseline (drug) enforces dRtot/dt = 0 receptor compartment holds R0 perturbed. Bioavailability handling. SC bioavailability F logit-transformed IIV per paper (Methods “Final Model Description”). model applies F SC depot via f(depot) = expit(logit_f). IV simulation, dose central compartment directly; F defaults 1 central compartment ROUTE_IV = 1 covariate switches paper’s IV-specific effects R0, CL, Q, ksyn_inf. DEVICE_AI set 0 IV subjects (device undefined IV; route-specific effects carried ROUTE_IV). Non-canonical compartment names. bcell bcell_periph canonical compartment list (naming-conventions.md). retained describe mechanism-specific B-cell pool map onto standard PK-compartment nomenclature; standard set (depot, central, peripheral1, target, total_target) used drug target-receptor side. checkModelConventions() raises warnings (errors). Race / ethnicity. Race tested covariate analysis retained final model (Yu 2022 Methods + Results “PKPD Modeling”). race covariate therefore exposed model, even though population predominantly White (91 %). ADA. Anti-drug-antibody incidence < 2 % across pooled cohort detectable PK B-cell impact (Results “Data”); ADA covariate final model exposed file. Missing baseline imputations. Two participants missing baseline B-cell values (imputed 200 cells/uL, cohort median) two baseline B-cell = 0 (imputed 1 avoid log singularities). per-subject data preprocessing choices; model re-impute. B-cell repletion timing. simulated time post-last-dose median B-cell count return LLN (40 cells/uL) ~123 days, 24 % shorter paper’s quoted ~23 weeks (~161 days). short time depletion (11.5 days vs 11 days) inter-dose steady-state AUC weight (within 3 percentage points Table 4) match closely, discrepancy localises long-tail repletion phase. likely contributors time-varying receptor synthesis rate ksyn(t) (slowly decays kdeg balance dosing stops) receptor-pool buffering free drug low concentrations, propagate small numerical / parameterisation differences deep elimination tail. parameter values tuned make repletion-time estimate match. 700-mg IV cohort excluded. OMS115102 700-mg arm excluded PK analysis -prediction seen dose (Results “Data”). Predictions outside 100-300 mg IV envelope extrapolations. Dosing schedule. figures use paper’s phase 3 schedule (three weekly 20-mg loading doses days 0/7/14, 20 mg q4w week 4 two years). regimens (60 mg q12w MIRROR; 100 300 mg IV OMS115102) can simulated changing dose-event table setting route / device covariates accordingly.

title: Yu_2022_ofatumumab
text: Yu H, Graham G, David OJ, Kahn JM, Savelieva M, Pigeolet E, Das Gupta , Pingili R, Willi R, Ramanathan K, Kieseier BC, Hach T, Aslanis V, Bagger Y, Ravenstijn P. Population Pharmacokinetic-B Cell Modeling Ofatumumab Patients Relapsing Multiple Sclerosis. CNS Drugs. 2022;36(3):283-300. doi:10.1007/s40263-021-00895-w

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: Citation: Zhang J, Sanghavi K, Shen J, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Nivolumab Combination Ipilimumab Patients Advanced Malignancies. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2019;8(12):962-970. doi:10.1002/psp4.12476 Description: Two-compartment population PK model time-varying clearance intravenous nivolumab (anti-PD-1 IgG4) adults advanced solid tumors, alone combination ipilimumab chemotherapy (Zhang 2019) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12476 Nivolumab fully human IgG4 monoclonal antibody targeting programmed cell death-1 (PD-1). Zhang et al. (2019) re-estimated Bajaj 2017 nivolumab monotherapy popPK model pooled 6,468-subject dataset spanning 25 trials patients colorectal cancer, hepatocellular carcinoma, melanoma, non-small cell lung cancer, renal cell carcinoma, small cell lung cancer. dataset added cohorts combinations nivolumab ipilimumab (1 mg/kg q12w, 1 mg/kg q6w, 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 induction, 3 mg/kg q3w x 4 induction) platinum-based chemotherapy. structural model two-compartment IV model time-dependent clearance parameterized sigmoidal-Emax function time since first dose: CL(t)=CL0⋅exp⁡(Emax⋅tγT50γ+tγ) \mathrm{CL}(t) = \mathrm{CL}_0 \cdot \exp\!\left(    E_{\max} \cdot \frac{t^{\gamma}}{T_{50}^{\gamma} + t^{\gamma}}  \right) Emax=−0.240E_{\max} = -0.240 (fractional decrease t≫T50t \gg T_{50}), T50=2,200T_{50} = 2{,}200 hours = 91.7 days, Hill coefficient γ=2.77\gamma = 2.77. Covariates baseline CL: body weight (allometric), estimated GFR, sex, ECOG performance status, race (Asian African American separate non-reference indicators), three coadministration indicators (ipilimumab 3 mg/kg q3w, ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q6w, chemotherapy). Covariates Vc: body weight sex. Covariates Emax: ECOG PS ipilimumab coadministration (additive effects linear-scale Emax). Q Vp inherit body-weight scaling CL Vc respectively (per Methods, “effect BBWT added Q VP, estimates fixed similar CL VC”).

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: pooled analysis dataset (Zhang 2019 Table 1) included 6,468 patients contributing 32,835 PK observations across 25 trials (7 phase , 2 phase /II, 6 phase II, 9 phase III, 1 phase IIIb/IV). Tumor-type mix: NSCLC 38.25%, melanoma 26.93%, RCC 19.25%, SCLC 6.03%, HCC 5.89%, CRC 3.65%. Coadministration mix: monotherapy 55.12%, ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q12w 0.56%, ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q6w 11.75%, ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 induction 15.06%, ipilimumab 3 mg/kg q3w x 4 induction 13.84%, chemotherapy 3.68%. ECOG PS distribution: 0 47.02%, 1 51.27%, 2 1.62%, 3 0.02%, missing 0.08%. Baseline body weight: mean 77.6 kg (SD 18.8), 5th-95th percentile range 47.7-122.0 kg. Baseline serum albumin: mean 3.93 g/dL (SD 0.493), range 2.8-4.8. Baseline LDH: mean 320 U/L (SD 326), range 125-1090. Baseline tumor size: mean 8.46 cm (SD 6.01), range 1.3-23.9. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Zhang_2019_nivolumab")$population.

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Zhang_2019_nivolumab.R. table collects one place review. Equation forms: Baseline CL: CL0 = CL0_REF * (WT/80)^e_wt_cl * (CRCL/90)^e_egfr_cl * exp(theta_X * indicator_X) categorical X (Zhang 2019 final-model equation set). Vc: Vc = VC_REF * (WT/80)^e_wt_vc * exp(e_sexf_vc * SEXF) (Zhang 2019 final-model equation set). Q: Q = Q_REF * (WT/80)^e_wt_cl (CL exponent reused per Methods). Vp: Vp = VP_REF * (WT/80)^e_wt_vc (Vc exponent reused per Methods). Time-varying CL: CL(t) = CL0 * exp(Emax_i * t^HILL / (T50^HILL + t^HILL)). Emax_i: additive linear-scale form Emax_REF + e_ecog_emax * ECOG_GE1 + e_ipico_emax * COADMIN_IPI_ANY + etaEmax.

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: Zhang 2019 Table 2 footnote defines RAAA = African American race RAAS = Asian race; reference category combines white “” one composite group. See inst/references/covariate-columns.md canonical register.

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Zhang 2019 Table 1 distributions pooled 6,468-patient cohort (means ranges — Zhang 2019 publish individual covariates covariate correlations).
code: set.seed(2019) n_subj <- 200  # Baseline body weight: log-normal centered on the Table 1 mean 77.6 kg, # clipped to the 5th-95th percentile range 47.7-122.0 kg. WT <- pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, meanlog = log(77.6), sdlog = 0.24), 47.7), 122.0)  # Estimated GFR: normal centered on the reference patient (90 mL/min/1.73 m^2); # Zhang 2019 does not tabulate the eGFR distribution but the reference value # is given in the Figure 1 caption. SD chosen so the +/- 2 SD range # (~50-130 mL/min/1.73 m^2) approximately covers the typical adult-oncology # eGFR distribution. CRCL <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 90, sd = 22), 30), 180)  # Sex: ~40% female. Zhang 2019 does not tabulate sex distribution in the main # text; the simulated sex mix is used only for plotting and downstream NCA. SEXF <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.40)  # ECOG PS > 0: Table 1 reports PS 0 in 47.02%, so PS > 0 in ~52.98%. ECOG_GE1 <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.5298)  # Race indicators: assume the global trial mix has small Asian and Black # fractions. Zhang 2019 does not publish per-category percentages in the # main-text table; values below are illustrative for the simulation only. RACE_BLACK <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.04) RACE_ASIAN <- rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.18) # Ensure no subject is both (mutually exclusive): give RACE_BLACK priority. RACE_ASIAN <- pmin(RACE_ASIAN, 1L - RACE_BLACK)  pop <- data.frame(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT, CRCL,   SEXF, ECOG_GE1, RACE_BLACK, RACE_ASIAN )

what: Dosing dataset — five clinically relevant regimens
title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: Zhang 2019 (Methods) lists five validated regimens used pcVPC: nivolumab 3 mg/kg q2w (240 mg q2w flat) monotherapy, nivolumab 3 mg/kg q2w + ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q6w, nivolumab 3 mg/kg + ipilimumab 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 followed nivolumab 3 mg/kg q2w, nivolumab 1 mg/kg + ipilimumab 3 mg/kg q3w x 4 followed nivolumab 3 mg/kg q2w. simulation covers three regimens span modeled covariate effects baseline CL: monotherapy (reference), 1 mg/kg q6w ipilimumab combination, 3 mg/kg q3w (induction) ipilimumab combination.
code: n_cycles    <- 12 cycle_days  <- 14   # nivolumab q2w in all simulated regimens dose_times  <- seq(0, (n_cycles - 1) * cycle_days, by = cycle_days)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 14, by = 0.25),                          # intensive cycle-1 profile   seq(14, n_cycles * cycle_days, by = 2),         # rolling cycles   seq(n_cycles * cycle_days, 365, by = 7)         # washout tail )))  # Helper: build a self-contained event table per regimen with disjoint IDs # so subsequent bind_rows() does not collapse subjects across regimens. make_regimen <- function(pop_in, regimen, id_offset) {   pop_offset <- pop_in   pop_offset$ID <- pop_offset$ID + id_offset    # Per-regimen indicators on baseline CL.   pop_offset$COADMIN_IPI_3Q3W <-     as.integer(regimen == "nivo 3 mg/kg q2w + ipi 3 mg/kg q3w x 4")   pop_offset$COADMIN_IPI_1Q6W <-     as.integer(regimen == "nivo 3 mg/kg q2w + ipi 1 mg/kg q6w")   pop_offset$COADMIN_CHEMO    <- 0L   # Any-ipilimumab indicator (drives Emax of time-varying CL).   pop_offset$COADMIN_IPI_ANY  <-     as.integer(grepl("ipi", regimen))    d_dose <- pop_offset |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(       AMT  = WT * 3,           # nivolumab 3 mg/kg in all three simulated regimens       EVID = 1,       CMT  = "central",       DUR  = 30 / (60 * 24),   # 30-minute IV infusion in days       DV   = NA_real_,       treatment = regimen     )    d_obs <- pop_offset |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(       AMT  = NA_real_,       EVID = 0,       CMT  = "central",       DUR  = NA_real_,       DV   = NA_real_,       treatment = regimen     )    dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(   make_regimen(pop, "nivo 3 mg/kg q2w monotherapy",            id_offset =     0L),   make_regimen(pop, "nivo 3 mg/kg q2w + ipi 1 mg/kg q6w",      id_offset =  1000L),   make_regimen(pop, "nivo 3 mg/kg q2w + ipi 3 mg/kg q3w x 4",  id_offset =  2000L) ) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("ID", "TIME", "EVID")])))

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Zhang_2019_nivolumab") set.seed(20191112) sim <- rxSolve(mod, events, returnType = "data.frame", keep = "treatment") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Zhang 2019 Figure 2-style behaviour: time-varying CL by regimen
title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: Zhang 2019 Figure 2(b) shows CLss/CL0 ratios across regimens. figure reproduces equivalent ratio implied model (CLss/CL0 = exp(Emax_i)) regimen typical covariates — three simulated regimens, differ via IPICO Emax effect (monotherapy: exp(-0.240); IPI combination: exp(-0.240 - 0.0668)). Typical-subject CLss/CL0 ratio implied Emax additive structure (PS = 0).
code: typical_emax <- tibble::tibble(   regimen     = c("monotherapy", "any IPI coadmin"),   emax        = c(-0.240, -0.240 + (-0.0668)),   cl_ss_cl_0  = exp(c(-0.240, -0.240 + (-0.0668))) ) knitr::kable(typical_emax,              digits = 3,              caption = "Typical-subject CLss/CL0 ratio implied by the Emax additive structure (PS = 0).")

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7, fill = treatment, colour = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(aes(y = median), linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Nivolumab concentration (μg/mL)",     title = "Simulated nivolumab PK by regimen, 3 mg/kg IV q2w x 12 cycles",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ", n_subj,                       " virtual subjects per regimen)"),     caption = "Model: Zhang et al. (2019) CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow = 3), fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3))

what: Time profile of the typical-subject clearance
title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: typical-subject CL trajectory across first year reproduces sigmoidal Emax decline motivates time-varying-CL parameterization. male, 80 kg, eGFR 90 patient monotherapy, CL drops 0.259 L/day t = 0 0.259 * exp(-0.240) = 0.204 L/day full Emax saturation (∼1.5×T50\sim 1.5 \times T_{50}, 5 months).
code: t_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5)  cl0_typical <- 10.8 * 24 / 1000     # L/day at reference covariates emax_mono   <- -0.240               # PS=0, no IPI emax_ipi    <- -0.240 + (-0.0668)   # PS=0, with IPI t50_d       <- 2200 / 24 hill        <- 2.77  cl_t <- function(t, emax) cl0_typical * exp(emax * t^hill / (t50_d^hill + t^hill))  cl_traj <- tibble::tibble(   time   = rep(t_grid, 2),   cl     = c(cl_t(t_grid, emax_mono), cl_t(t_grid, emax_ipi)),   arm    = rep(c("monotherapy", "any IPI coadmin"), each = length(t_grid)) )  ggplot(cl_traj, aes(time, cl, colour = arm)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x        = "Time (days since first dose)",     y        = "Typical CL (L/day)",     colour   = NULL,     title    = "Time-varying CL trajectory at reference covariates",     subtitle = "Sigmoidal Emax decline (T50 = 91.7 days, gamma = 2.77)"   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: NCA cycle-1 dosing interval (day 0-14) pseudo-steady-state interval (cycle 12, days 154-168) monotherapy arm. cycle-12 window several T50 multiples past time zero, CL close asymptotic value CL0 * exp(Emax). Cycle 1 NCA summary (days 0-14, monotherapy arm). Cycle-12 NCA summary (days 154-168, monotherapy arm) — pseudo-steady state.
