knitr::kable(   summary(nca_ss),   digits  = 2,   caption = "Cycle-12 NCA summary (days 154-168, monotherapy arm) — pseudo-steady state." )

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: Zhang 2019 publish tabulated NCA summary (validation paper pcVPC, NCA table). simulated cycle-1 Cmax cycle-12 Cmax/Cmin orders magnitude can however cross-checked broader nivolumab clinical-pharmacology literature: Single 3 mg/kg dose Cmax: published values around 50-72 ug/mL adults; simulated cycle-1 Cmax fall range. Steady-state Cmax/Cmin (q2w): published values around 130-180 ug/mL Cmax 50-80 ug/mL Cmin cycle 12+; simulated cycle-12 NCA values approach range. Effective half-life: ~25 days steady state. slow approach steady state model visible concentration-time figure. simulated values sit outside ranges ~20%, model file re-checked rather tuned (per skill).

title: Zhang_2019_nivolumab
text: Zhang 2019 publish individual PK covariate tables, virtual population coarse approximation Table 1 distributions. Body weight: log-normal, mean 77.6 kg (Table 1), clipped reported 5th-95th percentile range 47.7-122.0 kg. Estimated GFR: tabulated Zhang 2019. Sampled N(90, 22^2) clipped 30-180 mL/min/1.73 m^2; mean matches reference patient defined Figure 1 caption. Sex: tabulated Zhang 2019 main text. 40% female assumed virtual cohort. ECOG PS > 0: 52.98% (1 - PS=0 fraction Table 1). Race: tabulated percentages Zhang 2019 main text. virtual cohort uses 4% RACE_BLACK 18% RACE_ASIAN (mutually exclusive) illustrative values; per Figure 1 plot covariate effects, per-individual influence race CL small (3-4% shifts) dominated covariates. eGFR formula: stated Zhang 2019. Bajaj 2017 base model inherits uses MDRD-estimated GFR; model file documents inheritance covariateData$CRCL$notes. eGFR reference value: 90 mL/min/1.73 m^2, taken Figure 1 caption defines reference patient. Sex column: Zhang 2019 specify whether dataset’s SEX column coded 1=female / 0=male reverse. Figure 1 caption says reference patient male, CL_SEX = -0.181 produces ~17% female-vs-male CL reduction commonly observed mAb popPK literature, canonical SEXF (1 = female, 0 = male) used. Coadministration regimens beyond three simulated : model file supports four ipilimumab schedules described Zhang 2019 combining COADMIN_* indicators (unmodeled 1 mg/kg q3w x 4 induction 1 mg/kg q12w schedules collapse reference group baseline CL, still set COADMIN_IPI_ANY = 1 contribute IPICO additive effect Emax). vignette simulates three regimens keep figures readable. eta_Q eta_Vp variances: per Zhang 2019 Methods, IIV Q Vp follows distribution IIV CL Vc respectively. model file encodes separate (independent) etas variances pinned omega^2_CL omega^2_VC. alternative interpretation — using eta realization (NONMEM $ETA ) Q CL, Vp Vc — induce additional correlation parameters; paper disambiguate two parameterizations simulation impact small. Emax additive eta linear scale: Zhang 2019 parameterizes Emax random effect additive linear scale (log-normal magnitude). omega^2 = 0.0874 (SD ~0.296) typical Emax -0.240, allows roughly 21% simulated subjects Emax > 0 (.e., increasing CL time) — known feature paper’s parameterization, consistent Zhang 2019 Figure 2 shows subjects CLss/CL0 > 1.

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Citation: Zheng B, Yu X-Q, Greth W, Robbie GJ. Population pharmacokinetic analysis sifalimumab clinical phase IIb trial systemic lupus erythematosus patients. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2016;81(5):918-928. doi:10.1111/bcp.12864 Description: Two-compartment population PK model sifalimumab (anti-IFN-alpha human IgG1 monoclonal antibody) adults systemic lupus erythematosus following repeat fixed intravenous doses (Zheng 2016). Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol 81(5):918-928

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Simulate sifalimumab serum concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Zheng et al. 2016. Sifalimumab human IgG1 kappa monoclonal antibody interferon-alpha (IFN-alpha) developed systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). Zheng 2016 analysis used data phase IIb trial (MI-CP180) 298 SLE patients received fixed intravenous doses 200, 600, 1200 mg every 4 weeks 52 weeks (14 doses total, additional loading dose day 15). two-compartment model first-order elimination central compartment adequately described serum concentration-time data pooled across dose cohorts. Body weight, baseline 21-gene type interferon signature score, dose, baseline steroid use identified statistically significant covariates CL V1 accounted < 10% PK variability.

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Zheng 2016 Table 1: 298 SLE patients received sifalimumab (102 200 mg, 102 600 mg, 94 1200 mg) 431 enrolled. Baseline demographics (combined sifalimumab arm): median age 40 years (18-73), median weight 64.3 kg (39-131.3), 92% female, 59% White / 15% Asian / 7% African American / 4% Native American / 15% . 85% patients baseline (concomitant) corticosteroids. Median baseline 21-gene IFN signature score 12.04 (range 0.32-38.59); median baseline SLEDAI-2K score 10 (range 6-27). population PK dataset comprised 3961 quantifiable serum concentrations. information available programmatically readModelDb("Zheng_2016_sifalimumab") (returned function’s body holds population list literal).

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: per-subject covariates released paper. build 298-subject virtual cohort whose cohort sizes match Table 1 exactly (102/102/94 200/600/1200 mg) whose body weights baseline 21-gene IFN signature scores sampled log-normal distributions bounded published ranges (39-131.3 kg 0.32-38.59 respectively). Baseline steroid use drawn Bernoulli variable p = 0.85 match published 85% prevalence.
code: set.seed(2016)  cohorts <- tribble(   ~treatment,    ~dose_mg, ~n,   "200 mg",         200,   102L,   "600 mg",         600,   102L,   "1200 mg",       1200,    94L )  pop <- cohorts %>%   rowwise() %>%   do(tibble(treatment = .$treatment, dose_mg = .$dose_mg,             subj = seq_len(.$n))) %>%   ungroup() %>%   mutate(     ID         = dplyr::row_number(),     WT         = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(dplyr::n(), meanlog = log(64.3), sdlog = 0.22),                            39), 131.3),     BGENE21    = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(dplyr::n(), meanlog = log(12.04), sdlog = 0.8),                            0.32), 38.59),     STEROID = as.integer(runif(dplyr::n()) < 0.85),     DOSE       = dose_mg   ) %>%   select(ID, treatment, dose_mg, WT, BGENE21, STEROID, DOSE)  pop #> # A tibble: 298 × 7 #>       ID treatment dose_mg    WT BGENE21 STEROID  DOSE #>    <int> <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <int> <dbl> #>  1     1 200 mg        200  52.6    5.19       1   200 #>  2     2 200 mg        200  80.1    5.96       1   200 #>  3     3 200 mg        200  63.5   15.9        1   200 #>  4     4 200 mg        200  68.6   15.9        1   200 #>  5     5 200 mg        200  39     18.9        0   200 #>  6     6 200 mg        200  60.4   35.5        1   200 #>  7     7 200 mg        200  54.4    8.85       0   200 #>  8     8 200 mg        200  55.3   10.2        1   200 #>  9     9 200 mg        200  69.7   37.3        1   200 #> 10    10 200 mg        200  66.9    4.27       1   200 #> # ℹ 288 more rows

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Sifalimumab given 30-60 minute IV infusion every 4 weeks 52 weeks, additional loading dose day 15. full schedule 14 IV doses days 1, 15, 29, 57, 85, 113, 141, 169, 197, 225, 253, 281, 309, 337. use 30-minute infusion simulation. Observations scheduled give dense coverage first dose, trough values dosing visit, full profile final (14th) dose, follow-day 450 capture terminal elimination.
code: dose_days <- c(1, 15, 29, 57, 85, 113, 141, 169, 197, 225,                253, 281, 309, 337) - 1  # model time starts at 0 on day 1 infusion_dur <- 30 / (60 * 24)  # 30 min in days  # Observation times: peri-dose window on dose days, dense after last dose peri_dose <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(dose_days, function(d) {   d + c(-0.01, 0, 0.25, 1, 3, 7)  # pre-dose, end-of-infusion-ish, day 1, 3, 7 post })))) peri_dose <- peri_dose[peri_dose >= 0]  terminal <- seq(from = max(dose_days), to = max(dose_days) + 112, by = 3)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(peri_dose, terminal)))  dose_rows <- pop %>%   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) %>%   transmute(     ID, treatment, WT, BGENE21, STEROID, DOSE,     time,     amt  = dose_mg,     evid = 1L,     cmt  = "central",     dur  = infusion_dur,     dv   = NA_real_   )  obs_rows <- pop %>%   select(ID, treatment, WT, BGENE21, STEROID, DOSE) %>%   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) %>%   mutate(     amt  = NA_real_,     evid = 0L,     cmt  = NA_character_,     dur  = NA_real_,     dv   = NA_real_   )  events <- bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) %>%   arrange(ID, time, desc(evid))

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Simulate -subject variability spread across virtual cohort matches paper’s reported variability.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Zheng_2016_sifalimumab")  events_sim <- events %>% rename(id = ID) # `treatment` is already on every row of `events_sim` (carried from `pop` # via the dose_rows / obs_rows construction above). Carry it through # rxSolve via `keep = ...` rather than a fragile post-hoc left_join # from `pop`. `dose_mg` is taken from `pop` directly when building the # PKNCA dose table below, so it does not need to ride through `sim`. sim <- rxSolve(object = mod, events = events_sim, returnType = "data.frame",                keep = "treatment") %>%   as_tibble() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Figure 2 Zheng 2016 shows representative individual sifalimumab serum concentration-time profiles patients three dose cohorts, overlaying observed model-predicted concentrations log-linear scale across 52-week dosing interval. plot simulated median (5th-95th percentile) concentrations dose cohort.
code: profile <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0, !is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) %>%   group_by(treatment, time) %>%   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  profile$treatment <- factor(profile$treatment,                             levels = c("200 mg", "600 mg", "1200 mg"))  ggplot(profile, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~ treatment) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x       = "Time since first dose (days)",     y       = "Sifalimumab serum concentration (ug/mL, log scale)",     title   = "Figure 2 replication - simulated concentration-time profiles by cohort",     caption = "Replicates Figure 2 of Zheng et al. 2016 (median with 5th-95th percentile band)."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Compute non-compartmental Cmax, Cmin/Ctrough, AUC(tau), terminal half-life per subject per cohort steady state (dosing interval 13th 14th doses, .e., days 309-337) compare published exposure summary. PKNCA formula groups concentrations treatment + id summaries per-cohort. Steady-state NCA summary (13th 14th doses) simulated 298-subject virtual cohort: Cmax Cmin ug/mL, AUC(tau=28 d) ug*day/mL.
code: # Steady-state interval: between the last two doses ss_start <- dose_days[length(dose_days) - 1]  # day of 13th dose ss_end   <- dose_days[length(dose_days)]      # day of 14th dose (last)  nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) %>%   mutate(time_in_interval = time - ss_start) %>%   select(id, time = time_in_interval, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   transmute(id = ID, time = 0, amt = dose_mg, treatment) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start       = 0,   end         = 28,   cmax        = TRUE,   cmin        = TRUE,   tmax        = TRUE,   auclast     = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  sim_nca <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) %>%   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "cmin", "auclast")) %>%   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) %>%   summarise(     mean = mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     sd   = sd(PPORRES,   na.rm = TRUE),     med  = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     min  = min(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     max  = max(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  sim_nca$treatment <- factor(sim_nca$treatment,                             levels = c("200 mg", "600 mg", "1200 mg")) sim_nca <- sim_nca %>% arrange(treatment, PPTESTCD)  knitr::kable(sim_nca,              digits  = 2,              caption = "Steady-state NCA summary (between 13th and 14th doses) from the simulated 298-subject virtual cohort: Cmax and Cmin in ug/mL, AUC(tau=28 d) in ug*day/mL.")

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Zheng 2016 publish tabulated steady-state NCA dose cohort. Instead paper reports (Results, “Steady-state exposures” paragraph): Peak serum concentrations (Cmax,ss) range 117-562 ug/mL across three cohorts combined. Cmax,ss, Ctrough,ss AUC(0,tau) increased dose-proportional manner across 200-1200 mg. Typical clearance 0.184 L/day implies typical terminal half-life roughly 24 days given V1 = 2.82 L, V2 = 1.88 L, Q = 0.34 L/day structural parameters. simulated Cmax,ss range across 298-subject virtual cohort span 117-562 ug/mL envelope scale approximately linearly across three dose cohorts. Simulated Cmax,ss cohort (ug/mL). Overall min-max across cohorts bracket Zheng 2016 reported 117-562 ug/mL envelope. Dose-proportionality holds exactly construction packaged model (linear elimination, non-linear saturation terms); small (DOSE/600)^0.06 covariate V1 introduces weak deviation (+/-5% across 200-1200 mg range), consistent paper’s description covariate accounts < 10% PK variability.
code: cmax_range <- sim_nca %>%   filter(PPTESTCD == "cmax") %>%   transmute(treatment,             cmax_mean = mean,             cmax_med  = med,             cmax_min  = min,             cmax_max  = max) %>%   arrange(treatment)  knitr::kable(cmax_range,              digits  = 1,              caption = "Simulated Cmax,ss by cohort (ug/mL). Overall min-max across cohorts should bracket the Zheng 2016 reported 117-562 ug/mL envelope.")

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Weight distribution. Zheng 2016 publishes summary statistics (median 64.3 kg, range 39-131.3 kg). draw weights log-normal distribution median 64.3 kg log-scale SD 0.22, clipped published range. BGENE21 distribution. median (12.04) range (0.32-38.59) published. draw values log-normal distribution median 12.04 log-scale SD 0.8, clipped published range. Steroid use correlation weight. 85% steroid-use prevalence sampled independently weight BGENE21; paper describe joint distributions. Time-varying weight. Body weight treated time-fixed baseline covariate simulation. paper discuss intra-subject weight trajectories 52-week treatment period. Inter-occasion variability (IOV) V1. published final model included 26% CV IOV V1 first seven infusions . Occasion-specific random effects outside scope typical single-eta subject-level library model, IOV implemented. consequence individual peri-dose fluctuations simulation somewhat tighter observed data; steady-state exposure summaries (Cmax,ss, AUC(tau), Ctrough,ss) across full cohort materially affected. Infusion duration. Zheng 2016 specifies 30-60 min IV infusion; use 30 min. choice numerically immaterial given 28-day dosing interval ~24-day half-life. Dose covariate V1. source uses continuous (Dose/600)^0.06 covariate V1 driven subject’s assigned fixed dose level. preserve parameterisation exactly; subject’s DOSE covariate equals regimen dose (200, 600, 1200 mg).

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Structural model: 2-compartment IV input central, first-order elimination central, explicit depot absorption compartment. IIV: diagonal matrix CL V1; BSV Q V2. Covariates: WT, BGENE21, STEROID CL; WT, DOSE, STEROID V1. Collectively accounted < 10% PK variability published final model. Typical terminal half-life predicted structural parameters typical 64.3 kg patient BGENE21 = 12.04, DOSE = 600 mg, STEROID = 0 approximately 24 days (dominated central-compartment elimination rate CL/V1 = 0.184/2.82 = 0.0652 day^-1 plus small peripheral redistribution term), consistent Results narrative.

title: Zheng_2016_sifalimumab
text: Zheng B, Yu X-Q, Greth W, Robbie GJ. Population pharmacokinetic analysis sifalimumab clinical phase IIb trial systemic lupus erythematosus patients. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2016;81(5):918-928. doi:10.1111/bcp.12864

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Citation: Zhong R, Maxwell K, Passarell J, Murthy B, Aras U, Williams D. Model-Informed Abatacept Dose Recommendation Pediatric Patients Acute Graft-Versus-Host Disease. J Clin Pharmacol. 2026;66(2):[-issue]. doi:10.1002/jcph.70156 Description: Two-compartment population PK model abatacept (CTLA4-Ig Fc-fusion) pooled across 9 phase 2/3 studies (Zhong 2026): adults rheumatoid arthritis, patients aged 2-17 years polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis, patients aged 6+ years hematologic malignancies receiving HLA-matched unrelated-donor HSCT (ABA2 trial). Final model zero-order IV infusion, first-order SC absorption, first-order linear elimination, additive plus proportional residual error, allometric weight CL/VC/VP, hepatic (AST) renal (cGFR) markers CL, sex CL VC, two HSCT cohort indicators (7--8 8--8 HLA-matched URD) CL/VC, logit-scale SC bioavailability sub-model weight, age, pJIA-disease covariates fixed previously developed internal JIA PPK model (values match Gandhi 2021). Article: J Clin Pharmacol. 2026;66(2) (PMID 41684189)

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Zhong 2026 pooled nine phase 2/3 abatacept clinical studies final population PK dataset 904 patients (6 355 abatacept serum concentrations): 386 adults rheumatoid arthritis (RA, 42.7 %), 403 patients aged 2-17 years polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis (pJIA, 44.6 %), 115 patients aged 6-76 years hematologic malignancies (HM, 12.7 %) receiving HLA-matched unrelated-donor (URD) hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) ABA2 trial (Study IM101311, NCT01743131). HM cohort decomposed 7--8 HLA-mismatched stratum (HSCT_URD_7OF8 = 1, n = 41) 8--8 HLA-matched stratum (HSCT_URD_8OF8 = 1, n = 74); patients RA pJIA studies indicators set 0. -LLOQ samples accounted 2.9 % collected samples excluded. Zhong 2026 used PPK model simulate abatacept exposure 10 000 virtual pediatric patients aged 2 < 6 years inform dosing regimen prevention acute graft-versus-host disease (aGvHD) URD HSCT. selected regimen — 15 mg/kg loading dose Day -1 followed 12 mg/kg Days 5, 14, 28 — chosen delivered abatacept exposures comparable recommended adult older-pediatric regimen 10 mg/kg days, supported FDA approval abatacept prevention acute GvHD patients aged 2 years older. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Zhong_2026_abatacept")$population.

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Zhong 2026 Table 2 (final population PK model fitted pooled RA + pJIA + HM dataset nine studies) Supplementary Table S2 (lists IIV variances directly “Final Model Study IM101301” column). Reference covariate values come Figure 1 caption: male reference patient, baseline body weight 67.9 kg, baseline AST 20 U/L, calculated GFR 103 mL/min/1.73 m², HSCT cohort 7/8 8/8. F1 (SC bioavailability) sub-model covariate effects fixed Zhong 2026 final estimates previously developed internal abatacept JIA PPK model (Methods, Final model paragraph; supplement page 6); values match Gandhi 2021 published estimates exactly, F1 sub-model’s reference age (49 years) inherited Gandhi 2021. 0.1 kg discrepancy Zhong 2026 structural-reference WT (67.9 kg) Gandhi 2021 F1-reference WT (68 kg) documented Errata ; consistency model file uses 67.9 kg uniformly. numerical impact F1 predictions < 0.1 % across model’s covariate range.

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Time-unit conversion. Zhong 2026 reports CL Q L/h KA 1/h. nlmixr2lib convention time days, values multiplied 24 inside log(...) ini(). VC, VP, F, IIV variances, residual-error magnitudes carry unchanged. writes F1 covariate model F1_TV = F1_TV,ref · (BWT/BWT_ref)^F1_BWT · (AGE/AGE_ref)^F1_AGE · exp(JIA·F1_JIA), .e. multiplicative form F1 logit value. model file implements F1 additive logit scale: logit_F = logit_F_TV + e_wt_f · log(WT/67.9) + e_age_f · log(AGE/49) + DIS_PJIA · e_jia_f. two forms algebraically identical F1 logit can principle negative; additive form preserves sign-changing flexibility inverse-logit link. Per source-paper context (F1 sub-model fixed previously developed internal JIA PPK model; published Gandhi 2021 model author group closest match implemented additive logit Gandhi_2021_abatacept.R), additive interpretation used . See Errata . KA > kel flip-flop constraint. Zhong 2026 reports typical KA alongside prior models’ values (Discussion: “inclusion data patients GVHD resulted 35.3 % increase KA (0.00521 0.00705 1/h)”), 0.00521 Gandhi 2021 KA value. BMS abatacept-author-group convention (Li 2019 Eq. S2, Gandhi 2021 Eq. S2) reparameterises KA KA_i = KA_TV · exp(etaKA) + k_el,k_el,= CL_i / VC_i prevent flip-flop, Zhong 2026 directly compares KA value Gandhi 2021’s, model file uses parameterisation (ka <- exp(lka + etalka) + kel). typical-value parameters (KA_TV · 24 = 0.169 1/day, k_el = 0.552 / 3.19 ≈ 0.173 1/day), effective absorption rate constant ≈ 0.342 1/day, giving SC absorption half-life ≈ 2.0 days single-dose Tmax near 4 days, consistent abatacept SC label values. Independent IIVs (full block). Zhong 2026 reports ETA’s variance shrinkage independently full-block covariance reported (Q IIV per Table 2 ‘NE’). model file treats IIVs independent. F1 fixed Zhong 2026. F1, IIV--F1, F1-covariate effects FIXED Zhong 2026 final estimates previously developed internal abatacept JIA PPK model (Methods, Final model). values match Gandhi 2021’s published Table 2 exactly, suggesting internal model published Gandhi 2021 model closely related variant. model file uses fixed values verbatim, including 100 % shrinkage F1 noted Table 2.

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Zhong 2026 study populations (Table 1 stratified--study demographics). Subject-level observed data released paper. pediatric (2 < 6 years) HM cohort population Zhong 2026 derives dose recommendation; virtual covariates follow paper Methods (Pharmacokinetic Simulations Virtual Pediatric Patients section): age uniform [2, 6) years, weight CDC growth charts, sex 50/50, baseline cGFR 158.97 mL/min/1.73 m² AST 29 U/L set median values 6--13-year-old patients IM101311, cohort assignment 36 % 7/8 / 64 % 8/8 matching IM101311 demographics.
code: set.seed(20260429)  # Adult RA cohort -- pooled across IM103002, IM101029, IM101031, IM101100, # IM101101, IM101102 per Table 1. n_ra <- 200 ra <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq_len(n_ra),   AGE             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 53, sd = 12), 20, 85)),   WT              = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 78, sd = 20), 40, 187)),   AST             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 21, sd = 9),   7,  85)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_ra, mean = 90, sd = 28), 37, 250)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_ra, 1, 0.71),       # RA cohorts ~70-77 % female   DIS_PJIA        = 0L,   HSCT_URD_7OF8   = 0L,   HSCT_URD_8OF8   = 0L,   cohort          = "RA" )  # pJIA pediatric cohort spanning 2-17 years (IM101033 + IM101301). n_pjia <- 200 pjia <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq.int(from = n_ra + 1, length.out = n_pjia),   AGE             = runif(n_pjia, 2, 17),   WT              = pmax(pmin(8 + (AGE - 2) * 4 + rnorm(n_pjia, 0, 6), 100), 8),   AST             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_pjia, mean = 23, sd = 12),  9, 180)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_pjia, mean = 160, sd = 50), 65, 580)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_pjia, 1, 0.74),     # pJIA cohorts skew female   DIS_PJIA        = 1L,   HSCT_URD_7OF8   = 0L,   HSCT_URD_8OF8   = 0L,   cohort          = "pJIA" ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     pjia_dose = dplyr::case_when(       WT <  25 ~ 50,       WT <  50 ~ 87.5,       TRUE     ~ 125     )   )  # Adult HM cohort 8/8 (n_pop_8 = 74 in the paper; here we simulate # enough subjects to give stable percentile estimates). n_hm8 <- 100 hm8 <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq.int(from = n_ra + n_pjia + 1, length.out = n_hm8),   AGE             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm8, mean = 39, sd = 21), 6, 76)),   WT              = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm8, mean = 73, sd = 25), 22, 143)),   AST             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm8, mean = 28, sd = 15), 8, 85)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm8, mean = 125, sd = 49), 51, 315)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_hm8, 1, 0.42),   DIS_PJIA        = 0L,   HSCT_URD_7OF8   = 0L,   HSCT_URD_8OF8   = 1L,   cohort          = "HM_8of8" )  # Adult HM cohort 7/8 (n_pop_7 = 41 in the paper). n_hm7 <- 60 hm7 <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq.int(from = n_ra + n_pjia + n_hm8 + 1, length.out = n_hm7),   AGE             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm7, mean = 39, sd = 21), 6, 76)),   WT              = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm7, mean = 73, sd = 25), 22, 143)),   AST             = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm7, mean = 28, sd = 15), 8, 85)),   CRCL            = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_hm7, mean = 125, sd = 49), 51, 315)),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_hm7, 1, 0.42),   DIS_PJIA        = 0L,   HSCT_URD_7OF8   = 1L,   HSCT_URD_8OF8   = 0L,   cohort          = "HM_7of8" )  # Pediatric (2 to <6 years) HM virtual cohort -- the dose-recommendation # population of the paper. Demographics are taken from the paper Methods: # age uniform on [2, 6) years; weight from CDC growth charts (approximated # here by a 2-y-old ~12 kg, 6-y-old ~21 kg linear interpolation +/- noise); # sex 50/50; baseline cGFR 158.97 (median of 6-13 y subjects in IM101311); # baseline AST 29 (same source). The paper assigns the cohort distribution # 36 % 7/8 / 64 % 8/8, matching Study IM101311 demographics. n_ped <- 300 ped_age <- runif(n_ped, 2, 6) ped <- tibble::tibble(   id              = seq.int(from = n_ra + n_pjia + n_hm8 + n_hm7 + 1,                             length.out = n_ped),   AGE             = ped_age,   WT              = pmax(pmin(12 + (ped_age - 2) * (21 - 12) / 4 +                                 rnorm(n_ped, 0, 1.5), 30), 8),   AST             = rep(29, n_ped),   CRCL            = rep(158.97, n_ped),   SEXF            = rbinom(n_ped, 1, 0.5),   DIS_PJIA        = 0L,   HSCT_URD_7OF8   = rbinom(n_ped, 1, 0.36),   cohort          = "ped_HM" ) |>   dplyr::mutate(HSCT_URD_8OF8 = 1L - HSCT_URD_7OF8)

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Zhong_2026_abatacept")) keep_cols <- c("WT", "AGE", "AST", "CRCL", "SEXF", "DIS_PJIA",                "HSCT_URD_7OF8", "HSCT_URD_8OF8", "cohort", "treatment")  # (a) Adult RA SC 125 mg QW for typical SC absorption check build_sc_events <- function(cov_df, dose_days, treatment, dose_col = NULL,                             fixed_amt = NULL, obs_step = 7) {   ev_dose <- cov_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(       amt = if (!is.null(fixed_amt)) fixed_amt else .data[[dose_col]],       cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment     )   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, max(dose_days) + 7, by = obs_step),                             dose_days + 0.25, dose_days + 1, dose_days + 3)))   ev_obs <- cov_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L, treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  # (b) HM IV 10 mg/kg on Days -1, 5, 14, 28 -- the adult/older-pediatric # regimen used as the exposure benchmark. build_aba2_events <- function(cov_df, treatment, dose_per_kg = 10) {   dose_days <- c(0, 6, 15, 29)   # Day -1, 5, 14, 28 from anchor of Day 0 = Day -1   ev_dose <- cov_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(       amt = WT * dose_per_kg,       rate = amt * 24,           # 1-h IV infusion -> rate in mg/day       cmt = "central", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment     )   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 56, by = 1),                             dose_days + 0.04, dose_days + 0.1)))   ev_obs <- cov_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L,                   treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  # (c) Pediatric (2 to <6) HM with the paper's recommended regimen: # 15 mg/kg loading on Day -1, then 12 mg/kg on Days 5, 14, 28. build_ped_recommended_events <- function(cov_df, treatment) {   ev_load <- cov_df |>     dplyr::mutate(time = 0,  amt = WT * 15, rate = amt * 24,                   cmt = "central", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment)   ev_maint <- cov_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = c(6, 15, 29)) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = WT * 12, rate = amt * 24,                   cmt = "central", evid = 1L, treatment = treatment)   obs_days <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 56, by = 1),                             c(0, 6, 15, 29) + 0.04,                             c(0, 6, 15, 29) + 0.1)))   ev_obs <- cov_df |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, rate = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L,                   treatment = treatment)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_load, ev_maint, ev_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) }  events_ra_sc        <- build_sc_events(ra, dose_days = seq(0, 12 * 7, by = 7),                                        treatment = "RA_SC_125_QW",                                        fixed_amt = 125, obs_step = 7) events_pjia_sc      <- build_sc_events(pjia, dose_days = seq(0, 12 * 7, by = 7),                                        treatment = "pJIA_SC_weight_tiered",                                        dose_col = "pjia_dose", obs_step = 7) events_hm8_iv10     <- build_aba2_events(hm8,       "HM_8of8_IV_10mgkg") events_hm7_iv10     <- build_aba2_events(hm7,       "HM_7of8_IV_10mgkg") events_ped_iv10     <- build_aba2_events(ped,       "ped_HM_IV_10mgkg") events_ped_iv12     <- build_aba2_events(ped,       "ped_HM_IV_12mgkg",                                          dose_per_kg = 12) events_ped_iv15     <- build_aba2_events(ped,       "ped_HM_IV_15mgkg",                                          dose_per_kg = 15) events_ped_recom    <- build_ped_recommended_events(ped, "ped_HM_15_then_12")  sim_ra_sc      <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ra_sc,                                                 keep = keep_cols)) sim_pjia_sc    <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_pjia_sc,                                                 keep = keep_cols)) sim_hm8_iv10   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_hm8_iv10,                                                 keep = keep_cols)) sim_hm7_iv10   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_hm7_iv10,                                                 keep = keep_cols)) sim_ped_iv10   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ped_iv10,                                                 keep = keep_cols)) sim_ped_iv12   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ped_iv12,                                                 keep = keep_cols)) sim_ped_iv15   <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ped_iv15,                                                 keep = keep_cols)) sim_ped_recom  <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_ped_recom,                                                 keep = keep_cols))

what: Adult RA single SC 125 mg — typical SC absorption profile
title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: typical adult RA reference patient (67.9 kg, 49 y, AST 20 U/L, cGFR 103 mL/min/1.73 m², male, HSCT cohort) receiving single 125 mg SC dose. simulated Tmax (≈ 4 days) shape match abatacept SC label.
code: mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) typ_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 67.9, AGE = 49, AST = 20, CRCL = 103,   SEXF = 0L, DIS_PJIA = 0L, HSCT_URD_7OF8 = 0L, HSCT_URD_8OF8 = 0L ) ev_single_sc <- typ_cov |>   tidyr::crossing(time = c(0, seq(0.25, 28, by = 0.5))) |>   dplyr::mutate(     amt  = ifelse(time == 0, 125, 0),     cmt  = ifelse(time == 0, "depot", NA_character_),     evid = ifelse(time == 0, 1L, 0L)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) sim_single_sc <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = ev_single_sc)) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalka', 'etalogitfdepot'  tmax_day  <- sim_single_sc$time[which.max(sim_single_sc$Cc)] cmax_mgL  <- max(sim_single_sc$Cc)  ggplot(sim_single_sc, aes(time, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = tmax_day, linetype = "dashed", colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     x = "Time after single SC dose (days)",     y = "Abatacept Cc (mg/L = ug/mL)",     title = sprintf("Typical adult RA single 125 mg SC: Tmax = %.1f days, Cmax = %.2f mg/L",                     tmax_day, cmax_mgL)   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Pediatric HM exposure: 10 vs 12 vs 15 mg/kg fixed-dose IV (replicates Figure 3)
title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Zhong 2026 Figure 3 plots AUC1, Cmax1, AUCL (last dose) fixed IV dose regimens 10, 12, 15, 20 mg/kg 2--< 6-year-old virtual pediatric population, IM101311 older-pediatric (6-12 y, 13-17 y) adult (≥ 18 y) reference exposures 10 mg/kg. cohorts overlap pediatric dose 12 15 mg/kg, supporting choice two doses consideration.
code: auc_simpson <- function(time, conc) {   ord <- order(time)   time <- time[ord]   conc <- conc[ord]   sum(diff(time) * (head(conc, -1) + tail(conc, -1)) / 2) }  per_subject_first_dose <- function(sim_df, regimen) {   sim_df |>     dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= 6) |>     # first dose interval is Days -1 to 5 -> 6 days     dplyr::group_by(id) |>     dplyr::summarise(       regimen = regimen,       AUC1 = auc_simpson(time, Cc),       Cmax1 = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),       .groups = "drop"     ) }  per_subject_last_dose <- function(sim_df, regimen) {   sim_df |>     dplyr::filter(time >= 29, time <= 56) |>    # last dose at Day 28 -> 28-day window     dplyr::group_by(id) |>     dplyr::summarise(       regimen = regimen,       AUCL = auc_simpson(time, Cc),       .groups = "drop"     ) }  ped_first_10 <- per_subject_first_dose(sim_ped_iv10, "ped 10 mg/kg") ped_first_12 <- per_subject_first_dose(sim_ped_iv12, "ped 12 mg/kg") ped_first_15 <- per_subject_first_dose(sim_ped_iv15, "ped 15 mg/kg") hm_first_812 <- per_subject_first_dose(sim_hm8_iv10  |>                                        dplyr::filter(AGE >= 6, AGE <= 12),                                        "IM101311 6-12 y (10 mg/kg)") #> Warning: There was 1 warning in `dplyr::summarise()`. #> ℹ In argument: `Cmax1 = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE)`. #> Caused by warning in `max()`: #> ! no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf hm_first_1317 <- per_subject_first_dose(sim_hm8_iv10 |>                                         dplyr::filter(AGE > 12, AGE <= 17),                                         "IM101311 13-17 y (10 mg/kg)") #> Warning: There was 1 warning in `dplyr::summarise()`. #> ℹ In argument: `Cmax1 = max(Cc, na.rm = TRUE)`. #> Caused by warning in `max()`: #> ! no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf hm_first_adult <- per_subject_first_dose(sim_hm8_iv10 |>                                          dplyr::filter(AGE >= 18),                                          "IM101311 >=18 y (10 mg/kg)")  first_dose_long <- dplyr::bind_rows(   ped_first_10, ped_first_12, ped_first_15,   hm_first_812, hm_first_1317, hm_first_adult )  ggplot(first_dose_long, aes(regimen, AUC1)) +   geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.6) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = NULL,     y = "AUC1 (mg*day/L, log scale)",     title = "Replicates Figure 3a (AUC1 across pediatric IV dose levels)",     caption = "Pediatric 2-<6 y virtual cohort vs IM101311 6-12 y / 13-17 y / >=18 y subgroups."   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

what: Loading + maintenance regimen Cmin1 (replicates Supplementary Figure S7)
title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Zhong 2026 Supplementary Figure S7 shows recommended pediatric regimen (15 mg/kg loading dose Day -1, 12 mg/kg Days 5, 14, 28) achieves median Cmin1 50 µg/mL, exceeding Ctrough_1 ≥ 39 µg/mL favorable-aGvHD threshold reported ABA2 exposure-response analysis. replication computes Cmin1 virtual pediatric cohort recommended regimen. Pediatric (2 <6 y) Cmin1 recommended 15+12 mg/kg regimen. Zhong 2026 Suppl. Fig S7 reports median Cmin1 50 ug/mL.
code: ped_cmin1 <- sim_ped_recom |>   dplyr::filter(time == 6) |>          # Cmin1 = trough just before Day 5 dose (= 6 d after Day -1)   dplyr::summarise(     median_Cmin1 = median(Cc),     p25 = quantile(Cc, 0.25),     p75 = quantile(Cc, 0.75),     pct_ge_39 = 100 * mean(Cc >= 39),     pct_ge_50 = 100 * mean(Cc >= 50)   )  knitr::kable(ped_cmin1, digits = 2,   caption = "Pediatric (2 to <6 y) Cmin1 under the recommended 15+12 mg/kg regimen. Zhong 2026 Suppl. Fig S7 reports median Cmin1 above 50 ug/mL.")

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Non-compartmental analysis first dose interval pediatric HM (recommended 15 + 12 mg/kg) adult HM 8/8 (10 mg/kg) cohorts. PKNCA computes per-subject Cmax1, Cmin1, AUC1. Two PKNCA blocks run (one per regimen-cohort grouping) formula carries id/treatment per skill’s PKNCA recipe.
code: nca_conc_ped <- sim_ped_recom |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= 6, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_ped <- ped |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = WT * 15, treatment = "ped_HM_15_then_12") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_ped <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_ped, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_ped <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_ped, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_iv <- data.frame(start = 0, end = 6,                            cmax = TRUE, cmin = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                            auclast = TRUE, cav = TRUE) nca_ped <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_ped, dose_obj_ped,                                           intervals = intervals_iv)) summary(nca_ped) #>  start end         treatment   N    auclast       cmax cmin #>      0   6 ped_HM_15_then_12 300 504 [16.7] 180 [21.8]   NC #>                   tmax         cav #>  0.100 [0.0400, 0.100] 84.0 [16.7] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects nca_conc_hm8 <- sim_hm8_iv10 |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 0, time <= 6, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose_hm8 <- hm8 |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = WT * 10, treatment = "HM_8of8_IV_10mgkg") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj_hm8 <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc_hm8, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj_hm8 <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose_hm8, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  nca_hm8 <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_hm8, dose_obj_hm8,                                           intervals = intervals_iv)) summary(nca_hm8) #>  start end         treatment   N    auclast       cmax cmin #>      0   6 HM_8of8_IV_10mgkg 100 638 [11.0] 205 [16.2]   NC #>                  tmax        cav #>  0.100 [0.100, 0.100] 106 [11.0] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against the Zhong 2026 Cmin1 benchmarks
title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Zhong 2026 references two Cmin1 benchmarks ABA2 exposure-safety analysis: Cmin1 ≥ 39 µg/mL associated favorable Grade 2-4 aGvHD risk (paper Discussion paragraph) recommended pediatric regimen achieves median Cmin1 50 µg/mL (Supplementary Figure S7). fraction--threshold check verifies virtual pediatric cohort recommended regimen meets benchmarks. Cmin1 (Day 5, just second dose) regimen. Zhong 2026 references Cmin1 >= 39 ug/mL favorable-aGvHD threshold; recommended pediatric regimen achieves median Cmin1 > 50 ug/mL (Suppl. Fig S7).
code: fractions <- list(   "ped_HM_15_then_12 (this paper's recommendation)" = sim_ped_recom,   "ped_HM_IV_15mgkg fixed"                          = sim_ped_iv15,   "ped_HM_IV_12mgkg fixed"                          = sim_ped_iv12,   "ped_HM_IV_10mgkg fixed"                          = sim_ped_iv10,   "HM_8of8_IV_10mgkg (adult/older-ped reference)"   = sim_hm8_iv10 )  frac_table <- purrr::imap_dfr(fractions, function(df, lbl) {   cmin1 <- df |>     dplyr::filter(time == 6) |>     dplyr::pull(Cc)   tibble::tibble(     regimen = lbl,     N = length(cmin1),     median_Cmin1_mgL = median(cmin1),     pct_ge_39 = 100 * mean(cmin1 >= 39),     pct_ge_50 = 100 * mean(cmin1 >= 50)   ) })  knitr::kable(frac_table, digits = 1,   caption = "Cmin1 (Day 5, just before second dose) by regimen. Zhong 2026 references Cmin1 >= 39 ug/mL as the favorable-aGvHD threshold; the recommended pediatric regimen achieves median Cmin1 > 50 ug/mL (Suppl. Fig S7).")

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: Time-unit rescaling. Zhong 2026 reports CL, Q L/h KA 1/h; model file converts per-day factor 24 match nlmixr2lib units$time = "day" convention. Logit-F additive interpretation. Zhong 2026 Methods writes F1 covariate model multiplicative form F1 logit value (F1_TV = F1_TV,ref · ...). model file implements F1 additive logit scale, matching existing Gandhi_2021_abatacept.R implementation nlmixr2lib (F1 sub-model fixed Zhong 2026 previously developed internal JIA PPK model whose published equivalent — Gandhi 2021 — uses additive--logit). See Errata. F1 reference-WT discrepancy. Zhong 2026 Figure 1 caption sets structural reference WT 67.9 kg, state F1 reference WT. F1 piece fixed previously developed JIA PPK model whose Gandhi 2021 published reference 68 kg. model file uses 67.9 kg uniformly structural F1 references; numerical impact F1 predictions across model’s covariate range < 0.1 %. See Errata. F1 reference age (49 y) inherited. Zhong 2026 state AGE reference F1 sub-model. model file uses 49 years, inherited Gandhi 2021’s published reference, since F1 piece fixed internal model. documented inheritance, fitted value. KA > kel flip-flop constraint. model file uses BMS abatacept-author-group flip-flop reparameterization (ka <- exp(lka + etalka) + kel) per Li 2019 Eq. S2 Gandhi 2021 Eq. S2; Zhong 2026 explicitly state parameterization directly compares KA value (0.00705 1/h) Gandhi 2021’s (0.00521 1/h), strongly implying convention. Independent IIVs. Zhong 2026 reports ETA’s variance shrinkage independently full-block covariance reported. model treats IIVs independent. Q IIV (paper Table 2 ‘NE’); F1 IIV FIXED 0.516 (variance) per Suppl. Table S2 / Table 2 (SD 0.718 FIXED). Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Zhong 2026 Table 1 provides per-study summary statistics (mean ± SD, min/max) individual-level data. virtual cohorts approximate Table 1 pooled means Gaussian dispersions. pediatric (2 < 6 years) HM cohort follows paper Methods (CDC growth-chart weight, sex 50/50, baseline cGFR 158.97 mL/min/1.73 m² AST 29 U/L 6-13 year IM101311 subgroup, cohort 36 % 7/8 / 64 % 8/8). IM101311 1-h IV infusion. Zhong 2026 administers IV abatacept 1-h infusion (paper Methods); simulation uses rate = amt * 24 mg/day give 1-h infusion time--days units. subject-level observed data. Zhong 2026 release individual subject data; figures reported pure forward simulation published final-model parameters virtual cohort. PKNCA simulated data . PKNCA run simulated concentration-time data ; per-subject Cmax1, Cmin1, AUC1 computed summarised tables benchmark comparison. subject-level NCA source paper exists direct side--side comparison; benchmarks (Cmin1 ≥ 39 µg/mL, median Cmin1 ≥ 50 µg/mL recommended regimen) numerical exposure references paper publishes.

title: Zhong_2026_abatacept
text: published Zhong 2026 paper contains several notational choices reviewer re-reading source alongside model aware : F1 covariate model: multiplicative--logit vs additive--logit. Zhong 2026 Methods (page 6) writes F1 covariate model F1_TV = F1_TV,ref · (BWT/BWT_ref)^F1_BWT · (AGE/AGE_ref)^F1_AGE · exp(JIA·F1_JIA), applying multiplicative form F1 (logit-scale parameter) structural parameters CL, VC, VP (positive parameters multiplicative form unambiguous). F1 specifically, multiplicative form forces F1_TV retain sign F1_TV,ref (positive), contradicts general inverse-logit link logit can negative. published Gandhi 2021 paper uses multiplicative--logit notation existing Gandhi_2021_abatacept.R implementation nlmixr2lib (presumably underlying NONMEM control stream) uses additive logit, matching standard NONMEM convention logit-scale parameters. model file follows additive--logit interpretation consistency Gandhi 2021. numerical difference two interpretations small WT, AGE, JIA range supported dataset, future reader access Zhong 2026 NONMEM control stream verify intended form. VC ~ Cohort 8 vs Cohort 8/8 wording. Zhong 2026 Table 2 labels VC covariate effect “Exponent GVHD cohort 8 effect” (slash), whereas parallel CL effect labeled “Exponent GVHD cohort 8/8 effect” Supplementary Table S2 labels “Cohort 8/8”. model file treats Cohort 8/8 (indicator parallel Cohort 7/8 3-level cohort decomposition); consistent paper’s narrative description Figure 1 forest plot legend. Reference WT slight discrepancy (67.9 vs 68 kg). Zhong 2026 Figure 1 caption sets structural reference body weight 67.9 kg, F1 sub-model’s fixed parameters inherited Gandhi 2021 JIA PPK model whose published reference 68 kg. model file uses 67.9 kg uniformly; numerical impact < 0.1 %. F1 reference age stated. Zhong 2026 state AGE reference value F1 covariate effect ((AGE/AGE_ref)^F1_AGE). model file uses 49 years, inherited Gandhi 2021. future reader access Zhong 2026 control stream verify. Residual error column reports variance. Zhong 2026 Table 2 footnote gives residual-variability formula (SQRT(0.0724·F^2 + 5.26E-04)/F)·100 makes 0.0724 5.26E-04 unambiguously variances. model file converts SDs via sqrt() nlmixr2’s add() / prop() conventions: propSd = sqrt(0.0724) ≈ 0.269 addSd = sqrt(5.26E-04) ≈ 0.0229 mg/L. formal erratum. search PubMed J Clin Pharmacol corrections feed 2026-04-29 returned published corrections.

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: Citation: Zhou L, Lee S, Zhu L, Roy , Zhou H, Yang H. Prediction Belimumab Pharmacokinetics Chinese Pediatric Patients Systemic Lupus Erythematosus. Drugs R D. 2021;21(4):407-417. doi:10.1007/s40268-021-00363-2 Description: Linear two-compartment IV population PK model belimumab Chinese non-Chinese adult pediatric patients systemic lupus erythematosus (Zhou 2021) Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s40268-021-00363-2
code: mod <- readModelDb("Zhou_2021_belimumab") mod_meta <- rxode2::rxode(mod)

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: model estimated pooled population PK dataset comprising 9650 serum belimumab concentrations 1783 patients enrolled nine clinical studies (Zhou 2021, Methods Table 1): Adult patients (n = 1730): systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) healthy volunteers; median age 38 years (range 18–80); median weight 65.7 kg (range 35.8–165.4); median fat-free mass 41.08 kg (range 24.32–87.93); 93.4 % female; 13.4 % North East Asian (Chinese, Japanese, Korean heritage). Pediatric patients (n = 53): SLE; median age 14 years (range 6–18); median weight 52.5 kg (range 17–85.5); median fat-free mass 34.45 kg (range 12.56–57.16); 92.5 % female; 5.7 % North East Asian. Drawn PLUTO study (NCT01649765). Studies pooled (9): BEL114055 (PLUTO), LBSL01, LBSL02, BEL113750 (BLISS-NEA), BEL116119, BEL110751 (BLISS-76), BEL110752 (BLISS-52), 200909, BEL114448. two early-phase studies LBSL01 LBSL02 used ELISA-based assay; seven later studies used electrochemiluminescence-based assay. model carries STDY_LBSL indicator adjusts CL V1 two early studies. Disease state: active autoantibody-positive SLE (healthy participants early-phase studies); median baseline serum IgG 14.7 g/L (adults) 14.5 g/L (pediatric); median baseline serum albumin 40 g/L (adults) 43 g/L (pediatric). Dosing validation regimen: belimumab 10 mg/kg IV every 4 weeks (Q4W). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Zhou_2021_belimumab")$population.

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: Per-parameter origins recorded -file comments next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Zhou_2021_belimumab.R. table collects one place review. values final-model column (“run613”) Zhou 2021 Table 2 unless otherwise noted. published abstract reports typical adult steady-state volume distribution 4915 mL = 4.915 L, equals θ2 + θ4 = 2.597 + 2.318.

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort virtual population whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics Table 1. validation focus steady-state average concentration (Cavg,ss) labelled regimen belimumab 10 mg/kg IV every 4 weeks, can compared Zhou 2021 Table 3.
code: set.seed(34628605)  # Helper: sample fat-free mass directly from the published Table 1 ranges # rather than re-deriving it from height/weight/sex (height was not # tabulated, so a Janmahasatian re-computation is not possible). make_cohort <- function(n,                         age_range, age_median,                         wt_range,  wt_median,                         ffm_range, ffm_median,                         igg_median, alb_median,                         sex_female_pct,                         race_neas_pct,                         stdy_lbsl_pct = 0,                         id_offset = 0L) {   # Triangle-ish distributions anchored on published median + min + max   rtri <- function(n, lo, mid, hi) {     u <- runif(n)     out <- ifelse(       u < (mid - lo) / (hi - lo),       lo  + sqrt(u                * (hi - lo) * (mid - lo)),       hi  - sqrt((1 - u)          * (hi - lo) * (hi - mid))     )     pmax(lo, pmin(hi, out))   }   AGE <- rtri(n, age_range[1], age_median, age_range[2])   WT  <- rtri(n, wt_range[1],  wt_median,  wt_range[2])   FFM <- rtri(n, ffm_range[1], ffm_median, ffm_range[2])   IGG <- rlnorm(n, meanlog = log(igg_median), sdlog = 0.30)   ALB <- pmin(55, pmax(20, rnorm(n, mean = alb_median, sd = 4)))   data.frame(     id        = id_offset + seq_len(n),     AGE       = AGE,     WT        = WT,     FFM       = FFM,     IGG       = IGG,     ALB       = ALB,     SEXF      = as.integer(runif(n) < sex_female_pct / 100),     RACE_NEAS = as.integer(runif(n) < race_neas_pct / 100),     STDY_LBSL = as.integer(runif(n) < stdy_lbsl_pct / 100)   ) }  adult_cohort <- make_cohort(   n              = 500,   age_range      = c(18, 80),   age_median     = 38,   wt_range       = c(35.8, 165.4),   wt_median      = 65.7,   ffm_range      = c(24.32, 87.93),   ffm_median     = 41.08,   igg_median     = 14.7,   alb_median     = 40,   sex_female_pct = 93.35,   race_neas_pct  = 13.41,   id_offset      = 0L ) |> mutate(cohort = "All adult")  ped_cohort <- make_cohort(   n              = 200,   age_range      = c(6, 18),   age_median     = 14,   wt_range       = c(17, 85.5),   wt_median      = 52.5,   ffm_range      = c(12.56, 57.16),   ffm_median     = 34.45,   igg_median     = 14.5,   alb_median     = 43,   sex_female_pct = 92.45,   race_neas_pct  = 5.66,   id_offset      = 1000L ) |> mutate(cohort = "All pediatric")  neas_adult_cohort <- make_cohort(   n              = 500,   age_range      = c(18, 80),   age_median     = 38,   wt_range       = c(35.8, 165.4),   wt_median      = 65.7,   ffm_range      = c(24.32, 87.93),   ffm_median     = 41.08,   igg_median     = 14.7,   alb_median     = 40,   sex_female_pct = 93.35,   race_neas_pct  = 100,   id_offset      = 2000L ) |> mutate(cohort = "North East Asian adult")  cohort_all <- bind_rows(adult_cohort, ped_cohort, neas_adult_cohort) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(cohort_all$id))

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: Belimumab 10 mg/kg IV every 4 weeks 24 weeks (six doses; reaches steady state approximately week 16 per published profile). dose bolus central model extravascular compartment.
code: make_events <- function(cohort_df, n_doses = 6, tau = 28, max_day = 24 * 7) {   obs_days <- seq(0, max_day, by = 1)   dose_days <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)   d_dose <- cohort_df[rep(seq_len(nrow(cohort_df)), each = length(dose_days)), ] |>     mutate(       time = rep(dose_days, times = nrow(cohort_df)),       evid = 1L,       cmt  = "central",       amt  = 10 * WT          # 10 mg/kg IV     )   d_obs <- cohort_df[rep(seq_len(nrow(cohort_df)), each = length(obs_days)), ] |>     mutate(       time = rep(obs_days, times = nrow(cohort_df)),       evid = 0L,       cmt  = "central",       amt  = 0     )   bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(id, time, desc(evid)) }  events <- make_events(cohort_all)  sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod,   events = events,   keep   = c("cohort", "WT") ) |> as.data.frame()

what: Figure 4 — Visual predictive check by adult vs pediatric (first dose)
title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: published figure shows 5%, 50%, 95% percentiles belimumab concentrations first 10 mg/kg IV dose adults pediatric patients (Zhou 2021 Figure 4). simulated equivalent implementation; adults pediatric “” cohorts shown.
code: sim_first_cycle <- sim |>   filter(cohort %in% c("All adult", "All pediatric"),          time >= 0, time <= 28)  f4 <- sim_first_cycle |>   group_by(cohort, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(f4, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.9) +   facet_wrap(~cohort) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time after first dose (days)",     y = "Belimumab serum concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Figure 4 — Simulated VPC after first 10 mg/kg IV dose",     caption = paste(       "Replicates Figure 4 of Zhou 2021 (90% prediction intervals).",       "Lines: 5%/50%/95% percentiles. Original observed data not available."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 5 — Steady-state exposure: pediatric vs adult and NEAS vs non-NEAS
title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: Replicates four-panel exposure-comparison figure Zhou 2021 condensed form: simulated Cavg,ss distributions cohort.
code: # Last dosing interval (week 20-24) is fully at steady state for belimumab ss_window <- sim |> filter(time >= 20 * 7, time <= 24 * 7)  # Mean of dense daily samples is a close approximation to AUC0-tau / tau over # the final dosing interval; PKNCA below produces the authoritative value. cavg_ss <- ss_window |>   group_by(id, cohort) |>   summarise(Cavg_ss = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  ggplot(cavg_ss, aes(x = cohort, y = Cavg_ss, fill = cohort)) +   geom_violin(alpha = 0.4, scale = "width") +   geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA, fill = "white") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(     x = NULL,     y = expression(C[avg*","*ss] ~ "(mg/L)"),     title = "Figure 5 — Simulated steady-state Cavg,ss by population",     caption = paste(       "Replicates Zhou 2021 Figure 5 (PK exposure comparison).",       "10 mg/kg IV Q4W; weeks 20-24 averaged."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: PKNCA used compute steady-state average concentration final dosing interval (Cavg,ss = AUC0-tau / tau). treatment grouping variable cohort per skill template.
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   filter(time >= 20 * 7, time <= 24 * 7, !is.na(Cc)) |>   select(id, time, Cc, cohort)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(evid == 1, time == 20 * 7) |>   select(id, time, amt) |>   left_join(distinct(cohort_all, id, cohort), by = "id")  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | cohort + id,                              concu = "mg/L", timeu = "day") dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | cohort + id,                              doseu = "mg")  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 20 * 7,   end      = 24 * 7,   cmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■■■■                          23% |  ETA:  7s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 54% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■       86% |  ETA:  1s nca_tbl  <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result)

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: Zhou 2021 Table 3 reports first third quartiles Cavg,ss (mg/L) population. simulated values PKNCA summarised . Cavg,ss (mg/L) steady state 10 mg/kg IV Q4W: simulated vs Zhou 2021 Table 3. Differences within roughly ±20 % expected given virtual covariate distributions reconstructed published medians ranges (height, individual subject-level data, time-varying covariates available replication).
code: sim_q <- nca_tbl |>   filter(PPTESTCD == "cav") |>   group_by(cohort) |>   summarise(     Q1_sim = round(quantile(PPORRES, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 1),     Q3_sim = round(quantile(PPORRES, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 1),     .groups = "drop"   )  published <- tibble::tibble(   cohort = c("All adult", "North East Asian adult", "All pediatric"),   Q1_pub = c(81.0, 75.8,  78.2),   Q3_pub = c(111.2, 98.0, 105.8) )  comparison <- published |>   left_join(sim_q, by = "cohort") |>   mutate(     Q1_pct_diff = round(100 * (Q1_sim - Q1_pub) / Q1_pub, 1),     Q3_pct_diff = round(100 * (Q3_sim - Q3_pub) / Q3_pub, 1)   )  knitr::kable(   comparison,   caption = "Cavg,ss (mg/L) at steady state under 10 mg/kg IV Q4W: simulated vs Zhou 2021 Table 3." )

title: Zhou_2021_belimumab
text: Fat-free mass distribution — Sampled directly published Table 1 ranges triangle distribution anchored median. Janmahasatian formula re-applied height reported source paper. IgG albumin distributions — IgG sampled log-normal distribution centered published median (CV 30%). Albumin sampled Normal centered published median σ = 4 g/L, truncated [20, 55] g/L. paper reports median range, parametric form working assumption. Race study-indicator distributions — RACE_NEAS STDY_LBSL sampled Bernoulli published prevalences; STDY_LBSL defaults 0 % validation cohort 10 mg/kg Q4W exposure-comparison analysis (Zhou 2021 Table 3) driven later, primary-assay studies. IIV residual error — Zhou 2021 Table 2 reports inter-individual variance 0.186 proportional residual error (η5, shrinkage 7.8 %). -subject component residual variability implemented packaged model (typical-value propSd = 0.247 addSd = 0.221 carried). omission narrows simulated prediction bands modestly relative published VPC. Theta-13 labelling — Zhou 2021 Table 2 prints V1 race effect θ-index V1 study-indicator effect (“× θ12 RAC4”). labelling typo published table; value 1.07 distinct parameter value 1.26 (study indicator V1). -file comment notes transparency. High V1 IIV shrinkage — Zhou 2021 Table 2 reports 35.8 % shrinkage η2 (V1). documented transparency; variance retained published. Dosing scheme validation — Six 10 mg/kg Q4W IV doses simulated reach steady state. paper’s Cavg,ss values generated either post-hoc empirical Bayes estimates simulations using regimen.

title: Zhou_2025_brentuximab
text: Citation: Zhou X, Mould DR, Gore L, Bai X, Gupta N. Optimizing Brentuximab Vedotin Dosing Pediatric Patients Advanced Hodgkin Lymphoma: Population Pharmacokinetic Exposure-Response Analysis. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2025;117(6):1803-1810. Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/cpt.3629 (PMID 40095373) Open-access PMC copy: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12087681/ Supplement: NONMEM code ADC MMAE population-PK models, plus Tables S1 S2 final-model parameter values (open-access supplement file CPT-117-1803-s001.docx linked publisher PMC pages ). Zhou 2025 paper reports coupled population-PK model brentuximab vedotin (BV) antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) released payload monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE) pediatric patients classical Hodgkin lymphoma (HL) systemic anaplastic large-cell lymphoma (sALCL). Final-model parameter values come supplementary Tables S1 (ADC) S2 (MMAE); structural ODEs come NONMEM control streams supplement.

title: Zhou_2025_brentuximab
text: pooled analysis dataset comprised 95 pediatric patients across two open-label phase /II studies: Study 1 (NCT01492088, C25002, n = 36) — children adolescents 7-18 years relapsed refractory sALCL HL, treated single-agent BV 1.4-1.8 mg/kg every 3 weeks. Study 2 (NCT02979522, C25004, n = 59) — children 5 <18 years advanced-stage CD30+ newly diagnosed classical HL, treated BV 48 mg/m2 IV every 2 weeks combined adriamycin (doxorubicin) 25 mg/m2, vinblastine 6 mg/m2, dacarbazine 375 mg/m2 (+AVD regimen). Median (range) baseline characteristics Zhou 2025 Table 1: age 14 (5-18) y; weight 49 (18.8-87.0) kg; BSA 1.52 (0.79-2.03) m2; serum albumin 39-40.5 (23-51) g/L; creatinine clearance (Cockcroft) 132-165 (85.5-301) mL/min; sum--tumor-area 1581-1639 (455-9792) mm2. Sex 41% female, race 68% White / 13% Black / 5% Asian / 14% . information available programmatically via readModelDb("Zhou_2025_brentuximab")$meta$population.

title: Zhou_2025_brentuximab
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Zhou_2025_brentuximab.R. table collects source locations one place. Tables S1 / S2 references Zhou 2025 Supplementary Tables; NONMEM control streams fix structural ODE form reproduced Zhou 2025 Supplementary Methods.

title: Zhou_2025_brentuximab
text: Original participant-level data publicly available. simulations use virtual pediatric cohort whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics (Zhou 2025 Table 1, pooled across studies).
code: set.seed(40095373L)  # Brentuximab vedotin and MMAE molecular weights (used to convert mg # doses to umol — the model's amount unit, matching Zhou 2025's NONMEM # dataset convention "AMT IN UM"). MW_BV_kDa   <- 153.4   # ADC molar mass approx 153.4 kg/mol MW_MMAE_Da  <- 718.0   # payload molar mass approx 718 g/mol  cycle_h_q3w <- 21 * 24 # study 1: every 3 weeks cycle_h_q2w <- 14 * 24 # study 2: every 2 weeks n_cycles    <- 4L  # Helper builds one cohort. id_offset shifts subject IDs so multiple # cohorts can be bind_rows()-ed without colliding. make_cohort <- function(n, dose_mg, cycle_h, n_cycles, regimen,                         wt, bsa, alb, creat, tumsz, tumtp_chl,                         ada_pos = 0L, coadmin_avd = 0L, id_offset = 0L) {   ids <- id_offset + seq_len(n)   amt_umol <- dose_mg / MW_BV_kDa   obs_t    <- seq(0, n_cycles * cycle_h, length.out = 200)    # Single deterministic dose schedule applied to every subject in the cohort.   doses <- tibble::tibble(     id   = rep(ids, each = 1L),     time = 0,     amt  = amt_umol,     cmt  = "central",     evid = 1L,     ii   = cycle_h,     addl = n_cycles - 1L,     dur  = 0.5,     regimen = regimen   )    # One observation row per time point — rxSolve returns Cc and Cc_mmae as   # model variables at every output, so we don't need separate rows per   # output variable.   obs <- tidyr::expand_grid(id = ids, time = obs_t) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = NA_real_, cmt = "Cc", evid = 0L,                   ii = 0, addl = 0L, dur = NA_real_, regimen = regimen)    cov <- tibble::tibble(     id          = ids,     BSA         = bsa,     ALB         = alb,     CREAT       = creat,     TUMSZ       = tumsz,     TUMTP_CHL   = as.integer(tumtp_chl),     ADA_POS     = as.integer(ada_pos),     COADMIN_AVD = as.integer(coadmin_avd)   )    events <- dplyr::bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::left_join(cov, by = "id")   events }  # Approximate per-cohort summary stats from Zhou 2025 Table 1. # Sample BSA / albumin / creatinine from log-normal distributions whose # medians and CV match the per-study summary statistics; tumor size from # the pooled distribution. n_per_cohort <- 60L  study1_dose_mg_per_kg <- 1.6   # midpoint of 1.4-1.8 mg/kg study-1 range study1_wt             <- exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(49.9), 0.32)) study1 <- make_cohort(   n           = n_per_cohort,   dose_mg     = study1_wt * study1_dose_mg_per_kg,   cycle_h     = cycle_h_q3w,   n_cycles    = n_cycles,   regimen     = "Study 1: BV 1.6 mg/kg Q3W",   wt          = study1_wt,   bsa         = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(1.52), 0.20)),   alb         = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(40.5), 0.13)),   creat       = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(50),   0.30)),   tumsz       = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(41),   0.40)),   tumtp_chl   = rbinom(n_per_cohort, 1, 0.85),   ada_pos     = 0L,   coadmin_avd = 0L,   id_offset   = 0L )  study2_bsa <- exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(1.52), 0.18)) study2_dose_mg <- 48 * study2_bsa study2 <- make_cohort(   n           = n_per_cohort,   dose_mg     = study2_dose_mg,   cycle_h     = cycle_h_q2w,   n_cycles    = n_cycles,   regimen     = "Study 2: BV 48 mg/m^2 + AVD Q2W",   wt          = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(49), 0.30)),   bsa         = study2_bsa,   alb         = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(39), 0.13)),   creat       = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(50), 0.30)),   tumsz       = exp(rnorm(n_per_cohort, log(41), 0.40)),   tumtp_chl   = 1L, # study 2 is HL only   ada_pos     = 0L,   coadmin_avd = 1L,   id_offset   = n_per_cohort )  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(study1, study2) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid")])))

title: Zhou_2025_brentuximab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Zhou_2025_brentuximab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = "regimen") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_df <- as.data.frame(sim)  # Convert ADC concentration umol/L -> ug/mL (ug/mL = umol/L * MW_kDa). # Convert MMAE concentration umol/L -> ng/mL (ng/mL = umol/L * MW_Da). sim_df <- sim_df |>   dplyr::mutate(     Cc_ugmL    = Cc    * MW_BV_kDa,     Cmmae_ngmL = Cc_mmae * MW_MMAE_Da,     day        = time / 24   )

title: Zhou_2025_brentuximab
text: Zhou 2025 paper plots prediction-corrected VPCs ADC MMAE versus time-since-last-dose (Figure S1). simulated cohorts reproduce dosing regimens; per-time medians 5th / 95th percentiles analogues pcVPC.
code: sim_df |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, day) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = stats::quantile(Cc_ugmL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = stats::quantile(Cc_ugmL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = stats::quantile(Cc_ugmL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~regimen, scales = "free_x") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "ADC concentration (ug/mL)",        title = "Figure S1A analogue — ADC VPC by regimen",        caption = "Replicates Figure S1 (panel A) of Zhou 2025.") #> Warning in scale_y_log10(): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. sim_df |>   dplyr::group_by(regimen, day) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = stats::quantile(Cmmae_ngmL, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = stats::quantile(Cmmae_ngmL, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = stats::quantile(Cmmae_ngmL, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::filter(Q50 > 0) |>   ggplot(aes(day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pmax(Q05, 1e-3), ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~regimen, scales = "free_x") +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (day)", y = "MMAE concentration (ng/mL)",        title = "Figure S1B analogue — MMAE VPC by regimen",        caption = "Replicates Figure S1 (panel B) of Zhou 2025.")

title: Zhou_2025_brentuximab
text: PKNCA NCA single steady-state interval (cycle 4, day 1 day 14 study 2; cycle 4, day 1 day 21 study 1) provides quantitative cross-check simulated ADC MMAE exposures. PKNCA formula carries regimen grouping per-cohort summaries can compared paper’s per-study cycle-4 exposure summaries. Simulated cycle-4 ADC NCA parameters regimen. Simulated cycle-4 MMAE NCA parameters regimen.
code: # Pull cycle-4 (3rd repeated dose interval starting after dose 4) by # regimen-specific cycle length. Use ADC observations only. cycle4_window <- function(cycle_h) c(start = 3 * cycle_h, end = 4 * cycle_h)  sim_adc <- sim_df |>   dplyr::mutate(     cycle_h  = ifelse(regimen == "Study 1: BV 1.6 mg/kg Q3W", cycle_h_q3w, cycle_h_q2w),     in_cyc4  = time >= 3 * cycle_h & time < 4 * cycle_h   ) |>   dplyr::filter(in_cyc4) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_in_cycle = time - 3 * cycle_h) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_in_cycle, conc = Cc_ugmL, regimen)  # PKNCA expects monotone time within (id, group). Sort defensively. sim_adc <- sim_adc |> dplyr::arrange(regimen, id, time)  conc_obj_adc <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_adc, conc ~ time | regimen + id)  doses_pknca <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1L, time == 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, regimen) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(doses_pknca, amt ~ time | regimen + id)  intervals_adc <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = c(cycle_h_q3w, cycle_h_q2w),   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE,   cl.last    = TRUE )  nca_data_adc <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_adc, dose_obj,                                  intervals = intervals_adc) nca_res_adc  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data_adc) #> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (7.59799) is not allowed #> Requesting an AUC range starting
